Content:

Again, please quote where did Parshurama said, he gave all his weapon to Karna. Didn't he already gave them to Drona? And where did Parshuram said he gave Pashupata to Karna? Please share reference.

Did you know Shiva said no God or human have knowledge of Pashupata. Was he lying?

If Parshuram had Pashupatastra (assuming Shiva to be a liar) he must have given it to Drona, and then Drona to Arjuna. So why did Arjuna went to Shiva to seek it again?

If Karna had Pashpatatra, why did he explicitly mentioned to Shalya, that Arjuna has Pashupata and no one has any weapon to compete? Was he too lying?

Or is it that more likely that Shiva stated the fact that no one has Pashupata and Karna confirmed the same.

What is with you guys that you think yo build you narrative you can contradict both Shiva and Karna?

If this is logic, what is illogic?

  • Shiva said no one has Pashupata
  • Parshuram never said he has Pashupata
  • Karna said no one has Pashupata.

And you think you have logic! Wow.

But you still didn't answer my question. Can you answer my previous questions?

Answer Comment

Content:

I have asked several questions in each comment and you ignored all of them. It is difficult for me to compile all those questions together. But let me get you a few to get started —

  • If he really fought for Duryodhana, why did he abstain from the battle for first ten days? Did his loyalty start from eleventh day?
  • If he really cared about Duryodhana why did he promised Kunti that he would spare the life of Bhima? Kunti never asked? He could have made an exception for Bhima rather than Arjuna or may be both. He knew Bhima took an oath to kill his best friend.
  • Did he ever told Duryodhana that he had promised Kunti to spare the life of his enemies? Is this faithfulness? Truthfulness?
  • If he was faithful to Duryodhana why did he deserted him during Gandharva battle. He had his Kavacha as I already proved by giving reference and showing that you didn’t know that.
  • Why he let Kauravas brothers get killed to protect him from Bhima. Why didn’t he stop Bhima?
  • Why didn’t he imprison Yudhishthira? He had taken an oath not to kill him, but he could have protected his friend by imprisoning's Yudhishthira and win the war for him.
  • Where did Balram praised Karna?
  • Why would you not call a person evil who tried to throw a bride in fire because he failed?
  • Why would you not hate an evil who tries to disrobe a woman in public?
  • Why would you not call a warrior coward who hunts a lone warrior in pack?

Creation time: May 01, 2021 11:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Sure. Will wait to see where Parshurama said, he gave Pashupata. Because as per Shiva, the real owner of Pashupata, no one had — not even Parshuram.

Yes, Krishna said Karna had Pashupata. But he also said at the exact same point that Karna is going to kill Yudhisthir. That means Karna lied to Kunti. He wanted to kill Yudhisthir and obviously failed. Do you agree?

But why did Karna say, he doesn't have anything equivalent to Arjuna's weapon?

And who said Brahmastra was like a nuclear weapon? It's incomparable.

So do quote exact phrase.

Content:

Where does it mention Pashupata? Don’t you see all implies all that Parshurama was left with. Remember he donated all his weapons to Drona earlier.

Now cross this reference to two other points to get full picture —

  • Shiva himself said explicitly — No one Gods or human know this weapon. Do you mistrust Shiva?
  • Karna too said, Arjuna obtained Pashupata by pleasing Mahadeva and he has weapons that have no measure. Was Karna too lying?
  • There is no reference where it is said that Parshurama gave Pashupata to Karna.
    • Had Parshurama got Pashupata, he would have given it to Drona as he gave all his weapons to him. And Drona would have passed it to Arjuna.
    • Why did then Arjuna took so much pain to please Shiva if anyone can get it handed over?

As you can see you don’t have a valid reference. Try again!

Creation time: May 02, 2021 02:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I must say, now you are spamming! This is not related to the current question by any stretch of imagination.

Your first reference is a classic definition of heresy and way beyond the scope of current discussion — No comments.

Bhargavastra?

The second reference is the most favorite of Karna’s fan. But what does that prove? Is it a praise for Karna or Bhargava weapon? Read again to verify.

Do you think Bhargava weapon was more powerful than Brahmsira weapon or Pashupata? Tell me then, why didn’t it win the battle for Kauravas? Why didn’t it kill any warrior of repute? Do you have any answer?

I will tell you. Check out what Shiva, Drona and Indra said about divine weapons — It shouldn’t be used on a weaker opponent and shouldn’t be used recklessly. Karna didn’t use it against Arjuna, he used it against ordinary soldiers thus violating the ethics of Divyastra. Arjuna saw no way of countering that weapon without stooping to the level of Karna and manifesting a higher weapon.

But Krishna knew a way out — ignore the weapon and let Karna suffer his foolishness. He says Karna would be exhausted.

The quote proves for Arjuna, Dharma was more important than his egoistic vows. He could have countered it with higher weapon, but he still adhered to Krishna’s advise and let go of his own vows. That makes someone great — when you fight for something bigger than your ego.

I know you have only select few reference. But tell me this —

  • Does epic mentions Arjuna evil?
  • Doesn’t Dhritrashtra and Krishna calls Arjuna sinless?
  • Doesn’t Krishna himself says — He who hates Arjuna, hates me!

Tell me why do you hate Krishna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 03:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Again. You are simply quoting what others said. And there is enough evidence to suggest that many things said in the epic are contradictory and exaggerated. Many ordinary warriors are called Indra among men. Now everyone can’t be Indra.

So focus on facts.

Is there any reference that Parshurama had Pashupata?

If you insist on saying that because Krishna say and therefore there is no room for argument then you agree —

  • Shiva was liar as he said no one has Pashupata.
  • Karna was a liar as he said no one has Pashupata.
  • Karna lied to his mother that he would not kill Yudhishtira, because Krishna said Karna is going to kill Yudhisthira.
  • Yudhishtira was stronger than Shalya was stronger than Karna therefore Karna never spared Yudhisthira’s life. Karna only fooled everyone.
  • Duryodhana was the king of all the world including Panchala, Matasya and Dwarka

Now please answer all these question and say you agree with all these statements because Krishna said.

Answer Comment

Content:

The focus of discussion is Mahabharata only. The two events are thousands of years apart and there can be any number of explanation why Parshurama lost Pashupata during this period. Remember he lost his divinity too during Ramayana period.

So stay focused, and show where Parshurama has Pashupata during Dwapara yuga where Shiva categorically mentions that no one has Pashupata — no human or God.

Karna himself claimed he doesn’t have it.

So reference from only Mahabharata is acceptable.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 05:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I never said Krishna’s references are invalid. I said it shouldn’t contradict the fact. But do you really not understand the difference between the two?

Let me highlight —

KRISHNA’S STATEMENTS THAT ARE SAID TO MOTIVATE AND NOT AS A MATTER OF FACT

Krishna says Karna can’t be defeated with his Kavach and Kundala. But it can’t be a fact as Karna had already been defeated despite of having Kavacha and Kundala during Swyamvara battle and Gandharva battle.

Now you can also view this statement as difference between theory and practice. Since Kavacha was divine theortically Karna couldn’t be killed. But then, Kavacha can’t protect you from getting afraid and running away — as Karna did on both occasion.

Take another example — Krishna said Karna had Pashupata and he would kill Yudhishthira. It was essentially Krishna’s attempt to create a sense of urgency. In fact there is no cross reference that Karna had Pashupata and it was explicitly confirmed by Shiva and Karna. If you still can’t see the truth, it’s your choice.

KRISHNA’S DIVINE WISDOM

Krishna said ignore Bhargavastra and Karna would get exhausted. And He did get exhausted. Bhargavastra was not countered by anyone and yet it got exhausted without destroying Pandavas.

Any comments, why?

Krishna Arjuna connection

Krishna repeatedly not only said He who hates Arjuna hates me. He always acted in favor of Arjuna, always regarded him his best friend till the end of his days even beyond Kurukshetra battle. So there is a cross reference.

While Krishna called Karna evil on multiple occasion, but didn’t call Arjuna evil even once. No only that he protected Arjuna in all possible ways and became his guide.

And not only Krishna, but even Dhritrahstra called Arjuna Nishpaap.

I trust Krishna

And I understand him well enough to understand the difference between when he speaks his heart and when his great brain is playing tricks. You can understand too, if you stop hating him and surrender in true devotion.

Even Krishna said in Gita — act in devotion to me. When I am devoted, you want to call me blind fan? You think you insulted me? No, my friend, I wear this title with pride and honor!

Just ask a simple question to your self and you don’t have to answer me — Krishna, Shiva and Hanuman all of them supported Arjuna against Karna, There must be some reason.

Try exploring that reason, follow Krishna and you won’t have to justify evils.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 05:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Please do answer this question rather than answering on ten different thread with irrelevant references.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 05:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It actually means exactly that, unless you want to say Lord Shiva lied.

Shiva very clearly said no one knows this weapon —

‘The illustrious god said, “O Pandava! I will give you the great pashupata weapon, dear to me. You will be capable of bearing, releasing and withdrawing it. The great Indra, Yama, the king of the yakshas, 292 Varuna and Vayu do not know it. How can it be known to humans?

And remember after losing his divinity, Parshurama was a human.

And I consider no one, I mean absolutely no one, an authority, on the matter of Pashupata, over and above Shiva. If you want to dispute, say it loud. Now.

Again, Karna too said the same thing here —

He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds. The various guardians of the world assembled and gave him weapons that have no measure.

So, you want to say that Shiva was wrong, Karna was wrong , there is no explicit reference of Parshurama giving Karna Pashupata, and yet your perception is right?

If Karan Karna really had Pashupata…

So let me humor you. You believe, Shiva was stupid not to know Karna had Pashupata, and Arjuna was stupid enough to work so hard to please Shiva for a weapon he had already obtained from Drona, so answer these simple questions —

  • Do you mean to say the great Pashupata failed on Ghatotkatch?
    • There was an explicit condition on use of Pashupata — But if you unleash this invincible weapon in a fit of fury, when you possess other weapons, there is no doubt that it will descend on you.”
    • Karna had used all his divine weapons before using Vasvi Shakti — His divine weapon had been destroyed by the maya. He began to think about what should be the appropriate course of action.
  • Krishna was worried about Karna’s Kavach and Kundala. He was worried about his Vasvi Shakti. But he wasn’t worried about Pashupata weapon. He said — Because of Ghatotkacha, the spear has been used up. O Dhananjaya! Therefore, know that Karna has already been slain. Had Karna possessed the spear in his hand, no man in the world would have been able to stand before him.
  • Why was Karna not aware that he had Pashupata?

Can you answer these questions? I know like all other questions you would have no satisfactory answer.

Creation time: May 02, 2021 10:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

If you can’t answer you can stop. But please don’t keep jumping from one point to another. This is spamming.

Content:

Here is my list — Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Who are the top 10 warriors in Dwapara Yuga?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 10:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I have no doubt about Abhimanyu’s courage. He was the warrior of the highest order and that you could check if you read any of my answer in that regard.

The reason Arjuna is on the top of the list is —

  • Arjuna was no less in valour and courage than Abhimanyu. Remember how he faced Gandharvas or entire Kuru forces on his own.
  • To add to that Arjuna was also more experienced warrior with a number of feats to his credit such as —
    • Only warrior to have faced Mahadeva himself!
    • Arjuna faced all those warriors during Matasya battle, but then the table was turned.

That doesn’t mean I value Abhimanyu as a lesser warrior. But he lacked experience and stamina of Arjuna. He was still quite young with little war experience.

I must say I am as proud of Abhimanyu as you are.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 10:55 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No, I mean even after Karna’s death and knowing he was his brother, Bhima didn’t change his view about Karna. He said to the effect that Karna got what he deserved.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 10:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Satyaki

He was a brave warrior with a single blemish to his career — He killed Bhurisrava while he sat repenting helplessly. No doubt Bhurisrava was going to do the same thing — kill an unconscious Satyaki. We may call it tit-for-tat. But the problem with tit-for-tat is you are not a contender for the list of top most warrior. Not in my list.

Satyaki did defeat Karna on occasions nearly killing him and Karna too defeated Satyaki on multiple occasion. That too doesn’t speak volumes about Satyaki, if he was defeated by a warrior of the stature of Karna.

Summary: He was a brave warrior not a war monger. But not enough to make to my list.

Bhagdatta

He is one of the most overhyped warriors of Mahabharata — intrestingly more overhyped on social media. Arjuna defeated him during Rajsuy battle and he pleaded to Arjuna that he can fight him. Arjuna spared him. And how did he repay? By fighting against Pandavas!

Most of the feats done by Bhagdatta appears to be done by his elephant. I don’t see a great warrior there.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 11:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Many of the points you mentioned here may appear to be a crime if we look from todays perspective but were accepted protocol of the era and it would be cruel to judge someone based on those parameters.

Bhishma kidnapped Kashi Princess as he realized King of Kashi violated the norms by not inviting Hastinapur. Krishna himself said that marriage by abduction was a tradition. So can I blame him? You may notice I never condemned Karna for kidnapping bride for Duryodhana as it was a part of the norms. Was it correct? I can’t answer for a civilization dating five thousand years back based on my 21st century morality.

Bhishma apparently wasn’t aware of most of the earlier conspiracy as Vidura had supressed it’s public knowledge. He realized the extent of rift only after Lakshagrih andthen he agreed for a partition to maintain the peace.

Drauapdi’s humilation is a confirmatory evidence that social order was heading towards a moral degradation. The entire court couldn’t find fault with staking kindom, brothers and wives to gambling. But blaming Bhishma alone would be injustice. Even Yudhishthira, Drona or Kripacharya couldn’t find fault with Yudhisthira’s action. That implies that customs were getting confused for Dharma! And we needed to uproot the system.

I wish Bhishma should have done something, but can’t blame him for not doing anything when shastra’s scholars like Yudhishtira, Kripacharya couldn’t explain the problem. Even Vidura, who opposed the whole process explain the evil nature of Karna and Shakuni and how it would lead to disaster, but couldn’t invalidate the gambling.

In defense of Bhishma, his intentions were beyond question. He wasn’t a war monger. He was under his kings order and he had to attack Matasya. He never suggested to attack Matasya. But he had no option to go against the king of Hastinpura.

Bhishma avoided war as long as he could. When he fought he was expected to cause havoc of Pandavas army. That he did. And rest would be a repetition.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 02, 2021 11:18 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Creation time: May 02, 2021 11:22 AM PDT

Content:

As I said, I agree with you on the valour of Abhimanyu. No doubt there. Victory is immaterial.

But I didn't considered bravery alone. Purpose is another thing that makes a warrior and separates it from evils and warmongers.

Bhima fought battles for others as well and not only for his family. Again Abhimanyu would have. He didn't get a chance. But then that doesn't give us right to ignore those parameters.

Content:

Read this line — This discussion will not end until your perception about karna will not change

This is the definition of obsession!

  • You failed to answer any of my question.
  • You failed to provide proper references
  • You go against direct references where Karna was called evil multiple times.

And you expect me to chance my perception? I should begin to respect an evil who Shiva and Krishna wanted dead?

You can end the discussion without answering my questions. Because I won’t go against Shiva and Krishna. I won’t call a deceitful, arrogant, conniving, coward evil an honorable person.

You continue to find fault with epic, Shiva, Arjuna, Krishna everyone…

This is the end of discussion from my side as you have nothing to offer. And you never could answer my questions.

Don’t expect any further response unless you answer all my questions.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 03, 2021 07:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Here is the exact proof. The statement of Shiva. And there can be no greater proof in matters of Pashupata than the statement of Shiva —

‘The illustrious god said, “O Pandava! I will give you the great pashupata weapon, dear to me. You will be capable of bearing, releasing and withdrawing it. The great Indra, Yama, the king of the yakshas, 292 Varuna and Vayu do not know it. How can it be known to humans?

Here is what Karna said —

He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds. The various guardians of the world assembled and gave him weapons that have no measure.

If you understand English, you would understand it.

But stop asking me for references, which I have already given multiple times.

Unless you answer my questions, I won’t respond to you any further.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 03, 2021 07:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

I don’t want to argue with you. I didn’t react to your post. You did. I answered all your questions. You didn’t answer my questions because you are sleepy. I think you worry, If you answer my question, that would be a wakeup call.

So please ignore and live in your dream where an abuser of women, a coward, conspiring evil was an honorable man. And don’t wake up, lest you may end of seeing that face who Krishna, Shiva, Parshurama, Drona everyone condemned and Ved Vyas called trunk of a tree that was evil Duryodhana.

I won’t respond to your comments any further till unless you answer my questions.

So sleep and have sweet dreams —

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh! don’t bother about my English. Check who is the speaker. Do you want to say Shiva is confused? Do you think Shiva is not sure if humans have Pashupata or not? Do you think, he is wondering if some one has or not?

No dear, it’s you who need to perfect your English. Because you can’t understand when an authority speaks a statement, question mark becomes the answer.

How can it be known to humans? is a confirmatory assurance that no one has it. Shiva is almighty and all knowing not a fool in search of answer.

If you fail to understand this much, I must say, I wasted my time debating with you. You won’t understand. Be a prisioner of your perception.

I won’t debate on second hand knowledge! And you can’t find authentic reference when it doesn’t exist.

I have already posted my question two times. Not doing it a third time. Search though the post and find out if you care to answer.

But now I know your ability to understand English, I am not really interested in what you have to say, because I can see you have trouble understanding.

Regards,

Content:

Dear Lokesh,

thanks a lot for your review. I would really appreciate if you can write a slightly elaborate review and share with our space The Accursed God

Also share your details (email/phone) as a personal message to me so that I can add you to the list of recipients of ARC copy of my next book.

As to answer your confusion, I have answered this question recently — Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Where did Pitamaha Bhishma make a mistake for which he suffered for days on an arrow bed until he got salvation?

This is not based on my book, but on the epic itself. Please check and suggest, what do you think.

Creation time: May 03, 2021 11:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I said, you won’t understand. And I won’t debate with you because you can’t understand.

Shiva’s statement is clear. There is absolutely no contradiction of his statement anywhere in the epic.

Karna himself said he doesn’t have Pashupata. If he actually had it he couldn’t have Vasvi Shakti on Ghatotkatch without first using it.

All reference points to same thing without any doubt.

You guys simply try to create the doubt.

And I won’t ask you to provide reference of where Arjuna forgot Pashupata, because I know you again mis-understood simple English.

So Enjoy.

And please don’t respond without a valid reference to whatever you say. I have no time for your baseless logics.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 04, 2021 12:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

That is something that also makes me wonder. The logical answer is simple — Armours were better than anything mortals would get but not completely impenetrable. This is also evident from the fact that Karna managed to remove the armor using a dagger, which he won’t be able to if Kavacha was actually penetrable.

I believe Kundala was more important, that protected Karna from a certain death because of its Amrita.

As I see, Kavacha certainly reduced the force of impact and made attacks less fatal. Kundala ensured Karna event though mangled would not die and may be heal sooner.

But most certainly it was not something impenetrable. With a great warrior, it was a perfect winning combo. For Karna, it was the best protection there was.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-karn-never-defeated-Arjun-instead-he-was-defeated-by-many-warriors-including-Arjun-then-why-Krishna-always-warned-Arjun-that-karn-has-the-potential-to-change-the-scenario-of-war-he-has-potential-to-even-defeat/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 04, 2021 02:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I see, since Kundala was made of Amrita, he won't be dead, no matter how mortally he is wounded. Remember Rahu lives on with its head chopped off. May be Karna would have met same fate, had he not wisely exchanged his Kavacha. Remember Kavacha don't protect neck and Arjuna had severed his neck.

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you really know English? I doubt. Where is it written, Arjuna forgot? Did Krishna say so? Did Arjuna say so? Did Shiva say so? It is seeking Shiva's permission. But as you proved on occasions, you don't have respect for Shiva or Krishna.

Keeping distorting the epic.

Content:

So, since you don’t have references to your claims, you decided to spam the post with useless and irrelevant screenshots? Great!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 06, 2021 03:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Shri Krishna didn’t say you have forgotten. He said — had you known about it —

There is a difference between “not knowing” and “forgetting”. Again it is a English thing, which you may be confused about.

But Arjuna already knew. And Arjuna never said he forgot. Krishna never said he forgot.

Here Krishna was simply reminding Arjuna to remember Shiva and seek his permission.

Now you must understand seeking permission is so important for using Pashupata. Now think, how would someone have Pashupata who —

  • Never please Mahadeva once.
  • Who had a curse to forget everything.

Please don’t misquote. And if you want to quote be clear. Where Did Arjuna said he forgot? Where did Krishna said Arjuna forgot?

This is another proof of what I have been telling all the while. Don’t take Krishna word on face value. Try to understand deeper meaning. Here it is simply reminding that Arjuna could use Pashupata if he needs, something Arjuna had already resolved never to use.

Hope that helps you realize. And try to read English correctly.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 06, 2021 03:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No I don’t care about any achievement and talent of an evil.

Raavana was talented. Hirankashyap was powerful. Tarkasura was powerful. It doesn’t matter to me, if you used your power against Dharma.

Your power and talent is waste if it didn’t do humanity any good and if you brutalized those who are weak and inferior.

Why should I cherish someone's power if he used it for his selfish interests only?

And I am happy in my world — A world where I cherish those who became symbol of good. Who did something beyond their selfish interest. I am happy to cherish those who are called good by deities like Shiva, Krishna. I am happy to hate evils. That’s the learning I have from my great epic and civilization. I am happy.

And you say you don’t want to argue, but keep returning back. Funny. Isn’t. So let me ask you my questions back and that may stop you from coming back —

  • Tell me what great contribution Karna did to the society?
  • Did he try to protect a bride from those heinous Kings who were willing to throw her in fire?
  • Did he try to protect a woman whose modesty was being outraged?
  • Did he try to prevent an innocent family from a conspiracy of being burnt alive?
  • Did he stood by his friend in the need of hours as he, his brothers and family women were getting captured? Was he ready to die for his friend?
  • Did he try to prevent a war that was to consume a large portion of humanity?
  • Did he try to prevent an attempt to rob a kingdom in distress because his general was killed?
  • Why did he not fight for the first ten days of Kurukshetra war? Was it because he was loyal to his friend?
  • Why did he spare the life of Bhima? Was it because he was loyal to his friend?
  • Why didn’t he capture Yudhishthira? Was it because he was loyal to his friend? Had he taken any oath that he wouldn’t even capture Yudhishthira?
  • Why did he not spare Arjuna despite of knowing he was his own brother?

Are these the great qualities of Karna that I should cherish. Can you answer those question?

Since you can’t stay happy ignoring these questions. You are condemned to follow an evil person. Can you say the same for me?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 06, 2021 10:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You asked — why will he fight under a person who always insults him and calls him dog?

Who called Karna a dog and when? Read the epic. Bhima said you don’t deserve a kingdom in the same way a Dog doesn’t deserve yagna havi. This is a metaphor!

And this incident takes place during passing out ceremony. Karna was an archrival of Pandavas from long before from gurukul days. He was part of several conspiracies by Duryoudhana against Pandavas.

Can you justify his actions? Can you quote any reference that any Pandava had done anything wrong against him? Was he aware that someday Bhima would compare him to a Dog?

You asked — Yes he lied to both. But tell me one thing. If u were at Karna’s place could you kill your brothers?

First thing first. I would never be in Karna’s place. And If I were, I would do many things differently. As a friend, I would prevent my friend from committing crimes. I would try to protect him from himself.

As a brother I wouldn’t kill my brother. Not one of them. I won’t say my mother that I would kill one of my brothers and no one else. And I won’t kill my nephew.

And most certainly, I would not betray by friend. If I know my origin, I would tell my friend who trust me blindly and I would let him take a call. He may ask me to sit out of the battle or he may plan the battle so that I don’t have to confront my brothers. But I would never have robbed him of the truth and chance to make contingency plans. That is worst. I would rather be like Bhishma, clear in my intention from the beginning.

At least I would have divulged my truth to my brothers and urge them not to kill my best friend. At least that much I owed to my friend who didn’t call me a dog, but always supported me.

I would have accepted Krishna’s offer and handed down crown to my friend. None of my brothers would have objected.

But Karna didn’t do anything I would have done. Can you guess why he didn’t do any of these?

And Bhima was WRONG! even in his metaphor. Karna was no Dog. Dogs are loyal creatures.

You said — And what conspiracy. I've read answers of many unbiased writers who gave reference. In reality Pandavas did conspiracy by asking indra to cut his kavach.

So you trust your ‘unbiased’ writer who gave references against the authetnic epic? And that is why you called them unbiased. Great definition of being unbiased. Please share those unbiased references. I would like to see them. And I would also like to see the reference that Pandavas asked Indra to cut his kavacha. Can you provide that reference? Maybe some of your unbiased writers can give that reference. I don’t see it exists in Vyasa Mahabharata.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 08, 2021 08:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dharmo Rakshiti Rakshitah!

Krishna was bound to help Pandavas against all evils including Karna. Had Karna supported Dharma, he too would have its protection.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 08, 2021 08:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

We all are portions of Parambrahma — our soul is an extension of the divine. There was an Asura Jalandhara who was part of Shiva. And yet he was destroyed as he was evil.

Even Ashwatthama is considered as an Ansha of Shiva. He too was cursed and condemned. And that is the most important lesson of our Sanatana culture —

We all are born divine. We all are extension of same Parambrahma! But we are not just the pure soul. When the soul enters body it is driven by the bodily desires and our mind and comes under the control of three gunas — Satto, Rajo and Tamas. Gods don’t control our actions, our gunas control our actions and we pay for those actions. Our soul also acquires knowledge which is conditioned by our surroundings, our friends and our passion. With all our attributes we are polluted beings not the pure soul.

Do you think, Surya forced Karna to become evil? Did Surya ask him to conspire? Did Karna an extension of Surya even listened to the advice of the Surya himself? Answer is No.

Do soul fire weapons? Do soul care if they are considered the best. But most of the time the human existence prefers his body desires, him mind that is shaped by the society, his own morals and aspirations.

Consider this — the entire atmosphere is one single extension and not part. But same air smell differently in a garden and differently near a gutter. Why? Are they two different airs? No. it is the surrounding that influences the air that touches it.

Same is true for Karna. He was indeed part of Parambrahma just like all of us. But that doesn’t contradict the fact that he was an evil creature whom needed to be destroyed for the protection of Dharma — Krishna wanted him dead, Shiva and Brahma wanted him dead and Parshurama cursed him.

Karna was that portion of Surya that causes Surya grahan — evils sometimes eclipses the good.

And yes in a few scripture Surya is the deity. But Mahabharata is not one of those epics so not relevant for our discussion.

Hope that answers your question.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-being-a-better-warrior/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 08, 2021 10:22 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Why will he (Bhima) insult him? Karna was no way a part of lac house or poisoning.

Second part of you statement answers the first one. Are you sure Karna was not part of lac house or poisoning? Can you present any reference that explicitly says Karna wasn’t involved? Because it is mentioned in both BORI and KMG —

Here are few references from Adi Parva

‘“Thereupon, the evil-minded Duryodhana, with Karna and the son of Subala,13 tried to banish them and oppressed them in various ways. That evil son of Dhritarashtra gave poison to Bhima with his food, but the warrior Vrikodara14 digested it.

When the Pandavas were not killed through all these means, open and hidden, since they were protected by fate and destiny, he16 consulted his advisers: Vrisha, 17 Duhshasana and the others. With Dhritarashtra’s consent, he had a house of lac built. The Pandavas, of unlimited energy, were forced to live there and it was burnt down by fire when they18 least suspected it. Because of Vidura’s warning, a trench was dug and that gave them a wonderful means of escape and they were freed from danger.

Now please don’t tell that he consulted by Karna objected to the suggestion. There is no reference of his rejection. You may at best say Karna played along because he never tried anything to stop the conspiracy. That makes him accessary to the conspiracy. But that is not the end of the story. Here is what Krishna said in Karna Vadha Parva —

Karna is evil-minded and extremely injurious towards the Pandavas. … O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

He further clarfies —

O descendant of the Bharata lineage! Kill Karna today. In all the evil acts that Dhritarashtra’s son has done towards you, in all of these, the evil-souled and extremely wicked Karna has been present.

Now you want to convience me that Karna was no way involved you need to give me explcit reference of his no way involvement.

Do you still wonder why Bhima would insult Karna?

You said — Karna advised duryodhan to fight against Pandavas fairly

Can you quote a reference for this advise of fair-fight? Maybe while you check the reference of this fair fight you would understand Karna’s character better.

As far as I have read — Karna asked for a direct (not fair) fight because the conspiracies failed. And this too was a conspiracy when he suggested kill them while they are weak and armies of Vrishnis don’t join them. Please read the reference.

You asked — Why is Bhishma to insult Karna?

Bhishma never insulted Karna. In fact he supported him in Matasya battle despite his abusive words towards Kripacharya.

When he became the commander of Kuru forces he had right to analyze his subordinates and he did it fairly. He stated facts — Karna was arrogant, selfish and use to run away from battles. If your ‘facts’ are embarrassing can you blame others?

And Karna proved him right, by refusing to participate in the battle for his ego. For him his ego was bigger than Duryodhana’s needs.

You asked — Why lord krishna unleashed the truth before the war? He only wanted to weaken karna? Why?

Didn’t Krishna himself answered your question? He was trying to prevent an apocalyptic war and approached Karna, as he was in an unique position to help him. As I see he tried to empowered Karna and had Karna accepted his appeal, he would have emerged as one of the greatest hero — the savior of humanity! But evils always find sage advice unacceptable.

You asked — Karna himself told krishna that he'll be slain by arjun? Isn't these 2 words contradictory?

Yes, the are contradictory. But that is being human, isn’t it? The English word for it is inner-conflict. His brain knew he would be killed by mighty Arjuna. His heart was hoping against hope that he might win for once. You know such hopes exists in many hearts even today — Had this not happened, Karna might have…

But the fact is evil won’t win. Never have.

You said — If he wasn't loyal, he would have directly accepted bhagwan Krishna's offer and would have accepted throne.

Actually if he were loyal he would have done what was in the benefit of Duryodhana and entire humanity. He knew that his best friend would perish in war. He could have done so many things here —

  • He could have revealed his truth to Duryodhana and let him take the next call. At least he would have let him know that he wouldn’t kill his brothers. What he did was treachary towards his friend and treason towards his army.
  • He could have revealed his truth to Duryodhana and asked him to grant him (Karna) five village and pass it to Pandavas.
  • He could have become the King and handed over the kingdom to Duryodhana.

And in all these scenarios the war might have been prevented and humanity might have been saved.

So, why did he rejected Krishna offer? One, being the King was not part of his ambition, so certainly not a prize. Second, You have answered yourself and I would deal with it next to next section.

You asked — If karna was so evil. Limitless evil According to you! Why was he awarded aditya lok after death?

Even Duryodhana and Duhshasha attained Swargaloka after their death. Does that mean they too were not evil?

Karna’s soul was part of Surya (Aditya) and naturally submerged back to him. But that was just the soul. A person is not just a soul but human existence is a combination of — soul, body, mind and earthly knowledge. And bodily desire, your friends and beliefs corrupt soul. You become evil. Upon death the ever pure soul had tendency to merge back in divine.

And Karna was evil, not according to me. Even Krishna said so. Parshurama and Drona found him wicked. Shiva decreed him to die. There is a very long list!

And now let me go to your most important question.

You said — Karna was born for the war. Imagine duryodhan became king? What woudl have happened? He would have done adharma!

That is the most important part. Only evils and war mongers like terrorists think they are born for the war. Karna was one of them.

You guessed it correctly — Karna rejected Krishna and Kunti as following their advice would prevent the battle, something Karna never wanted.

But let us understand, a true warrior never seeks war. In Gita Krishna says — Among warriors (wielder of weapons) I am Rama!

Why Rama? Because a warrior’s first Dharma is to prevent the war. For him the war is the last resort —

  • Rama didn’t dry up the ocean right away. He pleaded the ocean to give way.
  • Rama crossed over to Lanka. He didn’t start the war immediately. He tried to prevent it and sent emissary.

Now that is what even Pandavas did.

  • Arjuna and Bhima too were warriors and yet requested Krishna to strive for peace.
  • Arjuna asked Krishna the necessity of war and refused to fight till Krishna gave him satisfying answers.

These qualities define true warrior. One who things he is born for battle and thus there should be a battle is a war-monger not a warrior. Consider those people evil. There is no greatness in battle for the sake of battle or to prove you are great.

The cost of your greatness can’t be innumerable innocent lives.

Now censure these questions in your wisdom and seek the truth yourself.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 08, 2021 09:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna was not weaponless. He went down fighting. Just before his death he fired Brahmastra on Arjuna. And anyone with the knowledge of Divyastra would know you can’t be unarmed!

And no one asked Karna to give up his arm. If you roam in battle field without your weapon, you can’t find fault with your opponents. Who asked Karna to give ups his arms? And where is it written that he left his bow on the stuck of chariot? Won’t that be stupid?

Cowardise is when you can’t defeat a warrior and so hunt him in a pack while he is alone and unarmed. Disarm him from behind because you can’t dare to fight face to face.

Karna wasn’t killed by a pack. He had his weapons with him and it doesn’t matter if he chose to lay them down to protect himself.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay/answer/Harish-Kumar-1195
Creation time: May 08, 2021 10:57 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Arjuna defeated Karna after Draupadi Swyamvara, where he retreated scared.

Ajuna defeated those Gandharvas, from whom Karna fled fearing for his life.

Arjuna again defeated Karna thrice during Matasya battle and robbed him of his cloths.

Karna failed to protect Jaidradh on 14th day.

Arjuna defeated Karna on 16th day.

What is the basis of your assumption that he can’t defeat karna with bow in his hand?

But let me bust your myth here —

Where did dharma go then?’ When Vasudeva addressed Radheya in this way, Pandava Dhananjaya remembered all this and was overcome by great rage. Energetic flames of anger seemed to issue out from all the pores on his body and it was extraordinary. ‘ “On seeing this, Karna again invoked brahmastra against Dhananjaya. He showered down arrows and tried to extricate his chariot. Pandava countered those weapons with his own weapons. Kounteya then released another weapon, beloved of the fire god, towards Karna. It blazed fiercely. Karna pacified the fire through a varuna weapon. He covered all the directions with clouds and it was as dark as a rainy day. Pandaveya was not frightened. While Radheya looked on, the valiant one used the vayavya weapon and dispelled all the clouds. The supreme one’s standard had the marks of an elephant’s housing. It was decorated with gold, pearls, jewels and diamonds. It had been crafted by excellent artisans over a long period of time. It was expensive and beautiful in form. It always inspired your soldiers and terrified and frightened the enemy. It was renowned in the world and blazed like the sun and the moon. Kiriti used a razor-sharp arrow that was gold-tufted and pointed. With that, he brought down the handsome and blazing standard of the great-souled maharatha, Adhiratha’s son. O venerable one! When that standard was uprooted, fame, dharma, victory and everything that was dear to the hearts of the Kurus also fell down. Great sounds of lamentation arose. ‘ “To ensure Karna’s death, Pandava took out an anjalika arrow.

—Karna Vadha Parva, BORI

If you notice, Karna did invoke Brahmastra, which was countered by Arjuna. That was the curse of Parshurama. But as you can see Karna showered arrows as he tried to extricate his wheel. This proves Karna had bow in his hand unless you are thinking he showered those arrows with hand.

And it was an stupid excuse that I wouldn’t fight because my chariot is stuck. Do you know Arjuna himself fought from ground on 14th day when his horses got tired. He didn’t ask anyone for recessess.

If you want to debate, please share valid reference and not just theories.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay/answer/Harish-Kumar-1195
Creation time: May 09, 2021 10:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Sarath,

Thanks for your question. The goal of my space “The Mahabharata Project” is to demythify the greatest tale ever told.

But this can’t be done at a single plane. Most people carry the knowledge of Mahabharata using TV Show and fictional books and you can’t force them to read your answers on quora, where there are so many contradictions and it becomes difficult for me to search my own answers. And at some point a non-fiction may become just montonous. Another problem I feel is people have too many biased perception and among those argument no one maybe sure about the truth.

So we need to take this effort at every plane. And when it comes to my novel The Accursed God, it is a fiction. There are several elements in the story that is not found in the authentic epic. This is to clarify certain points where unfortunately epic itself is silent. But I can tell you proudly that even in taking creative freedoms, I never betrayed the true spirit of the epic.

Unless we provide inquisitive readers a version of epic that doesn’t betray it’s true spirit, they are condemned to find truth in the fictions they have.

I also believe the fictional elements are part of our Shruti-Smriti tradition for centuries. It is not the fiction that we should be bothered, it is the intent that is the main concern. When I add a story, does it add value to the epic or it kills its core spirit? That said, I can’t trick readers in assuming that every part of my book is exactly based on authentic narratives only.

I have answered this in greater detail here — Is Accursed God accurately based on Mahabharata? in The Accursed God

Would you read that post and share your views?

Regards,

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/It-was-a-war-between-Kauravas-and-Pandavas-WHY-was-it-called-MAHABHARAT-ka-yudh-I-had-earlier-asked-a-question-It-3
Creation time: May 09, 2021 10:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Bhishma didn’t pledge. But he certainly made it clear that he don’t intend to kill Pandavas. And he did it before entering the battle.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Bheeshma-being-unloyal-to-Duryodhana-when-he-refused-to-kill-any-of-the-pandavas-and-further-assisted-pandavas-on-how-to-defeat-him-in-order-to-break-the-war-stalemate-Didnt-he-vow-that-he-would-serve-whoever-sits-on-the-kings-throne/answer/Aish-Nair
Creation time: May 09, 2021 10:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I quoted authentic epic. You are quoting baseless fictions. And as per the authentic reference Karna did fire Brahnastra.

And during Draupadi Swayamvara Arjuna defeated Karna without Gandiva. Gandharvas defeated him with his invincible armors. Abhimanyu defeated Karna and forced him to flee after seeing his brother killed. Abhimanyu had no Gandiva. Satyaki almost killed him. He too had no Gandiva.

What you guys don't understand is bows or armor aren't substitute for warriors valour and inner strength which Karna lacked.

And you have no reference to contradict me. Just excuses. You want to say that Karna could win only because of armour or bows or arrow, then what was his worth?

Anyways, I gave you a direct reference. It's your choice — either trust epic or any fiction of your choice.

Regards,

Vivek Dutta Mishra

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay/answer/Harish-Kumar-1195
Creation time: May 09, 2021 11:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh I heard the story of moving several steps back. Only problem, Ved Vyas never knew about that story. It is not part of authentic narrative. It is just a story to glorify Karna. You need such stories to over-glorify someone, when original epic doesn’t do that.

Krishna condemned Karna more time than he praised him.

Can you tell me which version says Abhimanyu defeating Karna as baseless? Can you provide any reference? You can’t.

Don’t quote absurd tales that have no reference. Unless you can validate your claim don’t make them.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay/answer/Harish-Kumar-1195
Creation time: May 10, 2021 10:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Shibashis Das You blocked me from replying in the hope that I couldn’t answer your question. If you can’t debate, you should not get into one.

You are confused about Krishna! I didn’t say he said lies. I said you must evaluate his wisdom with cross references.

Not only Krishna, but even Vaisampanya Das too said Karna was involved in conspiracy. That is cross-reference. On the top of it, there is not contradiction. Then again not only Krishna but everyone else referred Karna’s wicked nature including Drona and Parshurama. Shiva willed him to die.

And above all we can miss the facts. He had been evil in nature though out. Vaisampanya Das called him trunk of the tree called evil Duryodhana.

Since you say Narada said Karna’s purpose of life was war, that pronounces him a war monger. There have been other war mongers who pushed everything to war.

But you asked an interesting question — Why didn’t he disclose Karna’s secret to Pandavas. Simple. He realized that Karna too is evil beyond correction and he wasn’t willing to put the fate of Aryavart in the hand of an evil.

Karna himself admitted he was afraid of Arjuna. But his ego and ambition were greater than his fear. And therefore he fought.

You asked — what would Karna do if his bow is broken and chariot is broken?

First Karna’s bow wasn’t broken. Second had if got any opportunity to flee he would have. Unfortunately all warrior of his side had abandoned him by then. Remeber how he fled in earlier occasions against Gandharvas, Matasya battle and Abhimanyu. Unfortunately there was no escape this time. And do you really want to contradict authentic reference?

You said — Karna was alone during Gandharva battle Arjuna had his five brothers.

You are wrong again! Karna was with hundered Karuava brothers and a whole army of his own. He still fled. And he was first to flee. Even Duryodhana was braver — he refused to flee.

Pandavas were just four. No army. And barring Arjuna none of them had divine weapons or Kavacha like Karna. Karna fled nevertheless.

Finally you asked — where Rabindra Nath Tagore or Shivaji Sawant fools?

No they were not fools. They were scholars. But neither of them were Mahabharata scholar. And when it comes to chose and authority on Mahabharata their credentials are dwarfed in comparison to Ved Vyas. Neither of them can claim that extensive research on the subject as BORI.

And most important neither of them claimed that their work on Karna is authentic. They wrote fiction and took creative liberties.

And if you don’t have courage to debate, please don’t respond with option to reply blocked. If I see another such instance, I would delete them without warning.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 10, 2021 11:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I had decided not to be part of this question in anyway possible. But you made me rethink my decision!

You said only anti-Hindus like evil like Arjuna!

Do you really believe what you said? Do you want to say Shiva and Krishna are anti-Hindus? Do you find Krishna blind Arjuna fanatics?

Do you know Krishna said he who hates Arjuna hates me? And as per my understanding only anti-Hindus would hate Krishna. Only anti-Hindus would say Shiva was wrong to bless Arjuna and decree that Karna should be killed!

You status says Karna was the greatest warrior and greatest character of the epic. You failed to mention, which epic? Can you quote any reference from Mahabharata to substantiate your claim that he was the greatest warrior and greatest epic? Or you refer to another epic.

It is unfortunate that to keep your belief intact you would go against Shiva and Krishna. I would be happy to follow them blindly. So who is Anti-Hindu?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 11, 2021 08:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t answer my questions. Why did you call Arjuna fans (that includes Krishna and Shiva) as anti-Hindu?

Do you really know the epic? Here is two questions for you. Let me see if you have the courage you talked about in your question to answer truthfully.

  1. Among the below referred names tell me who the only person was who devas like Vishnu (a.k.a Krishna) and Shiva didn’t wanted killed — like Ravan,Tarkasur,Arjuna,Mahihshasura and Hiranyakashyapu, Karna? (HINT: There is only one name)
  2. Between Karna and Arjuna one has been referred as evil and wicked on multiple occasions; other has been referred Nishpaap (sinless). Can you guess whose who? (This will tell how much of epic do you know)

And if you can do try to answer the question from my previous post. And I am sure you are not coward to delete my comment, and would surely answer the three questions to the point.

If you want to contradict do so and put all your logics you like. But please respond to the three questions to the point, if you can.

If you can’t, you acknowledge either you don’t know the answer or dare not answer them.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 11, 2021 08:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Good you realized the mistake and edited your post. You are still calling Arjuna fan’s as anti-Hindu.

And as I predicted in my last comment you clearly can’t answer the questions I asked. Let me try once again. Here are my three original questions that you skipped.

  1. Among the below referred names tell me who the only person was who devas like Vishnu (a.k.a Krishna) and Shiva didn’t wanted killed — like Ravan,Tarkasur,Arjuna,Mahihshasura and Hiranyakashyapu, Karna? (HINT: There is only one name)
  2. Between Karna and Arjuna one has been referred as evil and wicked on multiple occasions; other has been referred Nishpaap (sinless). Can you guess whose who? (This will tell how much of epic do you know)
  3. You status says Karna was the greatest warrior and greatest character of the epic. You failed to mention, which epic? Can you quote any reference from Mahabharata to substantiate your claim that he was the greatest warrior and greatest epic? Or you refer to another epic.

The fact is Krishna himself said — He who hates Arjuna hates me! And your post clearly says you hate Arjuna! You have listed him with demons. So who is anti-Hindu? He who hates Krishna or he who loves him? I know you can’t answer this question, too.

But don’t worry, I have the courage to answer your question —

You said — I only targeted Arjun fans.And why are you calling Gods as fan of particular mortal😁😁?

I would recommend edit out your smiles. Yes, God to become fans of mortals. God of Gods Shiva became a fan of Rama, a mortal incarnation! Wasn’t Rama a fan of Hanuman? Don’t parent become fan of their children? Don’t you become fan of your own creations? And Krishna himself said, no one is dearer to me than Arjuna? Was Krishna an evil or a fool in your opinion?

You asked — Well now Krishna also wanted Bhishma and Drona to die in Kurukshetra War.Does it really mean Bhishma and Drona are evils??

Did Bhishma or Drona participate in multiple conspiracies? Are they mentioned as the trunk of the tree that is evil Duryodhana? Did Bhishma or Krishna provoked Duhasasana to disrobe Draupadi? Did Krishna ever call Bhishma or Drona evil?

No. Krishna wanted Bhishma and Drona to die because they sided with evil. But Karna himself was called evil. Can you understand the difference?

You said — Krishna has praised Karna multiple times in epic but you won't count that either.

Yes, Krishna did praise Karna on occasion. I counted them. But you must understand he called him evil on more occasions than he praised. And more important he wanted him dead!

You said — And even Karna was referred as sinless one in epic. Does that really mean Karna didn't do any wrong things??Well same applies for Arjuna.

No. Same doesn’t apply to Arjuna. Krishna called Karna evil. He never called Arjuna evil. Not once. Ved Vyas didn’t. Shiva didn’t. Parshurama didn’t. Bhishma didn’t. Drona Didn’t. Dhritrashtra didn’t. Even Karna didn’t. In fact no one did except few people like you.

Can you re-evaluate who is anti-Hindu here? And who is blind fan here?

I concede I am a blind fan! Because I am a blind follower of Shiva. And Krishna. I trust if they blessed Arjuna, I can blindly follow him.

Now tell me, what’s you excuse to blindly follow Karna? Who told he was the greatest warrior of the epic? Who told he was the greatest character of the epic? Can you provide a single reference to prove your assertions? If not, ask again — who is blind fan?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 11, 2021 10:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Great! You still couldn’t reply the direct question.

So let me answer those questions and contradict them if you can —

  1. Among the below referred names tell me who the only person was who devas like Vishnu (a.k.a Krishna) and Shiva didn’t wanted killed — like Ravan,Tarkasur,Arjuna,Mahihshasura and Hiranyakashyapu, Karna? (HINT: There is only one name)
    ANSWER: ARJUNA. His name can’t be used in the list of people divinity wanted dead to establish Dharma! So you can’t use his name along with Raavana, Tarkasura etc. Karna fits in, because he was evil as said by Krishna, Parshurama, Drona.
  2. Between Karna and Arjuna one has been referred as evil and wicked on multiple occasions; other has been referred Nishpaap (sinless). Can you guess whose who? (This will tell how much of epic do you know)
    Answer: Karna has been referred evil on multiple occasions. Arjuna has never been referred by evil.
  3. You status says Karna was the greatest warrior and greatest character of the epic. You failed to mention, which epic? Can you quote any reference from Mahabharata to substantiate your claim that he was the greatest warrior and greatest epic? Or you refer to another epic.
    ANSWER: NEVER. The grand epic Mahabharata including both ancient and BORI CE not once referred Karna as the greatest warrior of the greatest character of the epic. He is often referred as a coward who flee the battles and conspire.

Now time for your answers, again.

You again asked — I have already said that Krishna wanted trio of Bhishma Drona and Karna to die in Kurukshetra War. So this doesn't make them evil at all

But you didn’t bother to see, I already replied. All the three needed to die to establish Dharma. But Krishna and epic times and again referred only one of them evil. You know, even if you are unwilling to accept — Karna!

And that is the difference.

You said — Karna to die in Kurukshetra War. So this doesn't make them evil at all. And Karna was never in any conspiracies of Duryodhana like lac house.

Sorry to bust you myth. But Karna was involved in every conspiracy including lac house. It is present both in KMG and BORI and has cross-references. And you would have known it had you bothered to read the epic. You know it but you put a blind eye — that’s the only way someone could like Karna.

You said —dyut sabha incident everyone was culprit there.

Agree. Yudhishthira was guilty of putting his kingdom and family as a bait in what he thought was a family game. He lost himself before losing his wife and perhaps never thought he would be molested. And perhaps no one would have stooped to such depth till a man with dirty mind suggested to strip a woman in public. And that is the difference. Yudhishthira was foolish to trust his family. Karna was evil.

And that is what epic said. Krishna didn’t say Yudhishthira would be killed. But he did say Karna would be killed. Do you think you are more intelligent than Krishna?

You said — Lord Shiva and Lord Bramha called Karna a Hero just like Bhishma and Drona and said Karna deserves to be worshipped in heaven…

Have you read the epic? What was the conclusion of Shiva’s and Brahma’s statement? That Karna need to be killed. Shiva decreed it. Can you guess why? The message is given in epic. Krishna, Parshurama, Ved Vyas, Drona, Ahwatthama all of them said that — He was evil. Haven’t you read that? And where did you read that Shiva or Brahma said that Karna is a hero and should be worshipped? Here is what they said (Read carefully) —

Brahma and Ishana760 spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone. Karna, foremost in the worlds, is a bull among men. Vaikartana is brave and valiant. But let the two Krishnas be victorious. With Drona and Bhishma, let him762 obtain the great world of heaven and the worlds of the Vasus and the Maruts.’

  • So now tell me where did Shiva said he should be worshipped?
  • Where did Shiva said he isn’t evil?

Please don’t distort the facts! Shiva never called Karna sinless. He just called him brave and valiant and every warrior of Mahabharata ascended to heaven. Even Duryodhana and Duhsasana. So Karna was as sinless as Duryodhana and Duhsasasna.

You asked — by your logic you disrespected statement of Bramha and Shiva by calling Karna evil.

Not at all. Krishna said Karna was evil. Shiva and Brahma willed that he should be slain. Karna himself said he is the responsible for the destruction of the earth (due to war). And they didn’t say he was not evil.

Did Shiva ever blessed Karna? No. He willed him to die. Did Krishna call Karna his inseparable part? NO

Did Shiva himself blessed Arjuna? Did Shiva willed his victory? Did Krishna called Arjuna his inseparable part? YES

MAIN DIFFERENCE — KARNA AND ARJUNA

Karna was called evil on multiple occasion by multiple prominent people. Trinity wanted him to lose and die. No one ever called Arjuna an evil. And that is the difference.

You said — And indirectly comparing Arjun with Supreme Lord Rama comparing mortal with God 😅😅.

Do you understand comparisons? I also compared parent being proud and fan of his child and a person being fan of his own creation. I wanted to drive the idea by giving examples that it is possible that God would be a fan of his devotee and someone great. Remember Indra’s Vajra is made up of a mortals bones! So I didn’t compare Arjuna with Lord Rama. I gave examples. And I think if you revisit my answer (which I didn’t edit) you should understand it better. By the way read the above text to see what Shiva called Arjuna. Now I am sure you must have to plot something to condemn Shiva.

You asked — Yes, Karna is Greatest warrior and Greatest Character of epic. Karna was called hero by Supreme trinity(Bramha, Shiva and Krishna) so what's wrong if I call Karna a hero.

Do you understand the significance of ‘est’ suffix at the end of the word like great or brave? It is absolute superlative term. Did trinity called Karna greatest? No Shiva and Brahma praised him before condemning him to death sentence. And Krishna positively called him evil.

So let me ask you again — Where did you read he was the greatest warrior and the greatest character? Ok. We both know you can’t provide reference. So tell me how a warrior who fled battle after battle was the greatest warrior? How a person who tried to strip a woman in public became the greatest character? How a person who fled leaving his best friend and family women behind at the mercy of his enemies can either be termed as greatest warrior or greatest character — greater than even Krishna!

So you have no reference to prove your point. And you would like to contradict all the epic reference where he was called evil. Sure you have read the epic well!

I think this concludes our question answer, because you couldn’t answer a single question I asked and only repeated your points despite of the fact that I answered them all on the basis of actual epic reference.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 11, 2021 01:10 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Already replied to your points. But just in short.

Krishna called him evil.

His evil acts are well documented in the epic.

Shiva and Brahma very clearly willed him to die. And contrary to your claims none of them said he is a hero and deserved to be worshipped. Here is what they said —

Brahma and Ishana760 spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone. Karna, foremost in the worlds, is a bull among men. Vaikartana is brave and valiant. But let the two Krishnas be victorious. With Drona and Bhishma, let him762 obtain the great world of heaven and the worlds of the Vasus and the Maruts.’

Now where did Shiva say that Karna is a hero? Where did he say that he should be worhsipped? Please don’t distort the facts.

No divinity said — He who hates Karna hates me! So following Krishna won’t make someone anti-Hindu. Following Shiva decree that Karna must die isn’t anti-Hindu.

But I believe someone said a similar word about Arjuna. Guess who.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 11, 2021 01:19 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Mihir,

I am replying here because you have blocked me. And I get it you hate me and can understand the reason for it — I bust those myths that you want to hold on to.

And you hate me because you know I am right and always quote authentic reference and doesn’t take blind perceptions and authenticated version and you can’t refute those questions.

That said, I never responded to anyone with abusive words. I have always used civil language towards you and everybody.

So don’t you think, a difference in opinion shouldn’t be a reason to hate individual? If you hate people whom you don’t agree then maybe you shouldn’t be in a debate.

You called me misinterpreted — You know I never misinterpret. I give references.

And knowing your opinion and emotion against me, I would like to tell you that I still don’t hate you and hope someday you see the truth.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 12, 2021 01:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hey Sourav,

Do you know the context of your quote? Krishna was desperately trying to prevent the war! He was ready for compromise, even if unjust to ensure war doesn’t happen. Are you aware of that? Krishna knew compassionate Arjuna would agree to whatever Krishna proposes, even being Karna’s car driver. He also knew Arjuna wouldn’t consider it beneath his dignity to be a charioteer. After all Krishna too became one. They had no complex — Not for upholding Dharma!

Now you also ignore two important points —

  1. Krishna first approached Duryodhana and offered him kingship. Just demanded five village. Now tell me —
    1. Do you think he consider Duryodhana a better person than Karna? He offered first deal to him. Right?
    2. Had Duryodhana agreed for Krishna’s peace proposal, do you think Krishna would still have come to Karna?
  2. Do you find anywhere in the epic where Krishna said Karna would be dearer to him than anyone else? Do you find any reference where Krishna finds Karna his inseparable existence?

So understand this, Krishna would have loved Arjuna more than Karna even if Arjuna had driven his chariot.

Had Karna agreed to Krishna, he would have done something so worthy that his past sins might have washed off and he would have become worthy enough to have Arjuna has his companion, just like Krishna was to Arjuna.

Alas! Karna rejected Krishna and showed his true color. Krishna offer would be relevant only if Karna accepted it. Krishna didn’t say if you don’t switch over, we would still crown you!

Don’t you really see it right?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 12, 2021 10:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Again Dear Mihir,

I don’t remember a single thread where I was defeated. Not one. Because I don’t manipulate the truth.

And I don’t abuse anyone. You can’t find abusive words or slang in my posts.

I never concluded a post by telling that you are a fan of evil. I always waited for people to respond back with proper reference. They simply failed. Do you want an example. Here it is . You said following things in a post just above it —

  1. Lord Krishna never killed innocent nagar
  2. Never killed innocent nishadas
  3. Never forcefully abducted sister of his friend

Now do you have explicit reference to any of the points you made?

  • Who killed innocent nagar? What is that story? Did Ved Vyas say that Arjuna did something evil?
  • Who abducted sister for of his friend? Was he someone evil? And what would you say about someone who supported such an evil act? Do you have a reference it was an evil act? Have you read the epic? Do you know Arjuna abducted a smiling Subhadra? Or are you one of those who flash a screenshot by someone who translates Subhadra Harana as rape of Shubhadra? Have you heard the story of Rukmini Harana? Or Prithvi Raj Chauhan abducting Sanyogita? Both were abduction by force! Then do you know about Draupadi Harana? And Sita Harana? Who of these were smiling? Can you tell me who is misinterpreting here? You can hate me for that!
  • Who killed innocent Nishadas? Can you provide an explicit reference that either Arjuna or any Pandavas actually killed Nishadas? Can you prove any of them had slightest idea about Nishadas? Is there a single cross reference? Did anyone including Dhritrashtra or Duryodhana ever referred it? Anyone? And sure, I am mis-interpreting it! Yes, do hate me for this!

And I am done explaining myself. Please feel free to hate me. Your hate doesn’t define me. It defines you.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 12, 2021 10:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There is no exaggeration here — Krishna did said. Very clearly. He who hates him, hates me. He who loves him loves me.

I don’t see, how it is a mis-interpretation. Calling it exaggeration is interpretation.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 12, 2021 10:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sourav you contradicted yourself.

You asked — So for you Krishna now became an ordinary person. Krishna was ready to compromise.

What is a true difference between a warrior and a warmonger? It is valour. What is a valor without an ounce of compassion? If you are too eager to show your valour, chances are you have none!

Do you consider Rama a God? Did he attack Lanka without giving peace a chance? Didn’t he offer a compromise to Raavana? What if Raavana had returned Sita?

You asked — Didn’t Krishna believe in the Valours of Pandavas?

Have you read Bhagwat Yaan Parva? Do you know what Parshurama and Krishna said about Arjuna’s valor?

Have you read what Karna thought about Pandava’s valor at this point? What was his prediction? Who would win?

But it is not about exhibition of valor it was giving humanity a chance at survival.

This this way. Arjuna may not have valor. But was Krishna too void of valor? Why did he try for peace? Why did Rama do it?

Because you need to have supreme courage to strive for peace, specially when you know you are competent enough to destroy your opponent?

You said — Krishna offered duryodhana kingship ,no Duryodhana was already king at that time.

You must have read a different Mahabharata. Duryodhana wasn’t the king. Dhirtrashtra was.

And after the completion of the term of exile, Duryodhana was illegally occupying the territory of Pandavas. Krishna offered that entire territory in exchange of five village and long term peace.

Had Duryodhana agreed, and his friend not counselled him against accepting peace, Krishna may not have approached Karna. By the way can you tell me, why Karna wanted war so badly that he counselled Duryodhana to reject Krishna?

You asked — who was Karna why Krishna offered him kingdom?

Two reasons. Karna was one person what had greatest influence on Duryodhana. Karna had advised Duryodhana to reject peace proposal. Krishna knew and he explicitly said, Duryodhana was ready to wage the war because he trusted Karna. (We will come back to this trust later).

Krishna thought if he could convince Karna it would have multiple benefits —

  • The war would be avoided.
  • Karna would get back his family.

If you read properly, neither Krishna nor Karna had any confusion about the result of the war. Krishna was concerned about humanity. He did all he could. He counselled Duryodyaha. He counselled Karna. Rama too had tried to put sense in Raavana. But evils don’t see God’s grace.

You Pointed — Krishna said — “I am alike to all creatures; there is none hateful to me, none dear. They, however, who worship me with reverence are in me and I also am in them”

And this is the best quotes I have seen from you, ever. I am surprised you know this quote and still didn’t get the answer to your previous question.

Krishna isn’t hateful to anyone. He didn’t reject Duryodhana. He didn’t reject Karna. They rejected Krishna.

And now read the next line — They who worship me with reverence, are in me and I also am in them

Can you see the significance of this statement! Karna, in rejecting Karna sealed his doomed fate. God resides in them who follow them, who adhere to their advise.

Do you still wonder why Krishna said now one is dearer to me than Arjuna? Do you still wonder why he said Arjuna is half of his existence? Read the above line — again and again — till you get the idea.

You said — He saved all the pandavas from humiliation of serving a suta .

Do you mean Karna was a racist? Did Karna consider Suta beneath Arjuna? That is a serious allegation on Karna that even I never accused him of.

There is no where mentioned in Mahabharata that being charioteer was a disrespect. Krishna was a charioteer. Sanjay the charioteer was also an advisor to king and his ambassador.

Why you people consider charioteer bad?

And imagine someone let the whole humanity suffer because of his mental block — because he didn’t wanted Pandavas to be humiliated.
