Do you believe in youself? What did Karna did in Dyutshabha? Why did Karna planed the Ghosha yatra expedition? Read them. It was to humilate them.

You said — He made Yudhishthira king by rejecting the offer. His decision of rejecting the offer made a road for dharma to win

Oh! Dear. You finally accepted one of my point here, that I have been trying to explain Karna fans like you for a long long time — Karna was a traitor.

He was never loyal to Duryodhana. He wanted Yudhishthira to be the king. And his act can be considered as treason against his best friend. Well to be honest, this was not the first time and it wasn’t for the last time.

But there were better ways to make Yudhishthira win. There was easier way for Dharma to win. With almost no blood-shed. He could have simply revealed his identity to Duryodhahna. He could have refused to fight for him. Simple as that.

By doing that he would have saved his friend who was always kind to him. He could have refused to be the King and remained just the brother of King Yudhishthira and a bridge between Kauravas and Pandavas. That would have been Dharma.

So at the end, why did he backstabbed Duryodhana (you yourself accepted)? Why did he provoke him to fight (authentic reference)? Did he really wanted to get Duryodhana killed?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 12, 2021 11:30 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Mihir,

That is misinterpretation. Every point of it. And best part you didn’t bother to provide any reference. You know why? Because you have none.

YOU SAID — The same Lord Krishna in gita said slaughter of animal is biggest sin vaisampayana didn't call it bad does that mean it's not bad.

Perhaps you don’t know but Gita is eighteen chapter and not just one line. It also includes another line that says —

brahmany adhaya karmani
sangam tyaktva karoti yah
lipyate na sa papena
padma-patram ivambhasa

—Gita Chapter 5, Shloka 10

Do you know what it says?

One who performs his duty without attachment, surrendering the results unto the Supreme God, is not affected by sinful action, as the lotus leaf is untouched by water.

Now remember, why did Arjuna burn down Khandava. Not because he wanted to but because supreme lord wanted to.

Want more proof? Krishna himself was involved in Khandava Daha. Does that make him a sinner?

You know why Vaisampanyadas didn’t call it bad? Because he understood Gita. And those who understand Gita don’t call it bad. Do you?

But since you yourself accepted Vaisampanya didn’t call it bad, if someone is misinterpreting here. it is you. Not me.

You said — Again misinterpreted do you know what that line said it said the princess of smile was Abductied t doesn't say that she smiled and in rukmini harana by the way we both know goddess rukmini agreed on that herself she was Lord Krishna’s Divine consort.

Yes misinterpreted. But by whom? Let us check the exact extract —

Kunti’s son rushed at her and forced her onto his chariot. Abducting the sweet-smiling one, that tiger among men set out for his own city on his chariot, as if on air. On seeing Subhadra thus carried away, all the armed soldiers who were with her raised an alarm and dashed towards the city of Dvaraka.

Do you know present continuous tense? Hint - they end with ‘ing’. It is not a general statement that Subhadra used to smile. She was smiling during her abduction.

Ok. You don’t agree. Don’t worry. I may be misinterpreting. Time to show your reference —

  • Give one reference which says Shubhadra was tormented. You know like Draupadi or Sita!
  • Give one reference where she complained against Arjuna abducting him.
  • Do you think Balarama was a coward that he invited his sister’s abductor and offered his sister to him in marriage?

Do you have any reference for anything?

You said —They themselves agreed to put six dead bodies.

Yes. They agreed. But then in Matasya Parva Karna said that he would kill Arjuna and bring down the ape banner. Does that mean he actually did that?

  • Did they say they are going to kill Six Nishada family?
  • Did they say they would kill anyone? They are talking about the body not killing.
  • Is there any single mention that any Pandava were aware of their presence?
  • Bhima ensures Purochana is killed by starting fire in his room. But he doesn’t bother to ensure Nishada’s are dead. Why?
  • Yudhishthira, after escape, wonders if Purochana died or not. But he doesn’t even think about Nishada family. Why?
  • No one ever mentions Nishadas death either as glory or as gory anywhere? Why?
  • Now what epic does mention is this —

Let us escape, unobserved by anyone, after setting fire to the armoury, burning Purochana to death and leaving six bodies here.” ‘O king! On the occasion of giving alms, Kunti fed a large number of Brahmanas in the night. A number of women also came. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! They enjoyed themselves and ate and drank as much as they wished. When it was night, with Madhavi’s70 permission, they returned home. Driven by destiny and in search of food, a hunter woman also happened to come to the feast, accompanied by her five sons. All of them drank wine, until, with her sons, she was completely drunk. O ruler of men! She and her sons lost their senses and slept in that house, as if dead. When everyone was asleep and a violent storm started in the night, Bhima started a fire at the spot where Purochana was sleeping.

Note:

  1. intetion was to put Purochana to death. But not kill six person. Get Six bodies.
  2. The people who came were expected to return back. No plan to imprison them.
  3. Driven by destiny and not by conspiracy is what Vaisampanya said.

Here is what Yudhshitira said later —

We do not know if that evil Purochana has actually been burnt to death.

Even after setting fire at Purochana’s place Yudhishitra doesn’t know if he died. Why they don’t talk about other five people?

Now my question —

Do you have any reference to any of your point? Or you would keep debating on perceptions that are not present in Mahabharata and keep calling Vaisampanya biased and Krishna a sinner? Krishna was involved in Shubhadra’s abduction and Khandava daha.

Is this how I become mis-interpreter? Because, I contradict your fake claims by valid reference.

Is this how you decided you won the debate because you have no reference to offer?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 13, 2021 06:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No it wasn't Karna's fault that he wasn't nara maharishi. His fault was his arrogance and deceitful nature. The conspiracy, the treachery, was his fault. Attempt to disrobe a woman was his fault. Trying to throw someone in fire was his fault. And there is a long list of those faults documented in the epic. Do read it sometime.

Yes. It was Karna's fault that he couldn't gain Krishna's favour. Krishna had offered him his grace. But evils and wicked fail to see God's compassion.

Krishna offered Karna entire Aryavart. But didn't he offer the same first to Duryodhana? Does that make him more virtuous? Even Rama offered Ravana a chance to peace and keep his reign. What does this prove? Two things —

  1. God provides a chance to repent even to sinners and evils. But they because of their evil nature reject God.
  2. Krishna wasn't a war monger and tried to protect humanity. Karna derailed his efforts and proved the evil he was. Raavan did the same. They also met the same fate.

And you are again right, Krishna didn't call Bhima his inseparable existence. But did that stopped Bhima from doing good? Why didn't Karna, then, became good? Why didn't he try to stop bloodshed?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 13, 2021 11:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — karna was born for the war.

Shri Rama had incarnated to destroy evil. Yet he tried to avoid bloodshed. Yet he gave Lanka a chance of survival.

Shri Krishna had incarnated to establish righteousness. He was powerful to kill Jarasandha. Yet he chose to shift from Mathura to Dwarka. And he tried for the final peace between Kaurava and Pandavas.

Kartikeya is the war Lord. But he doesn’t keep finding opportunity for wars!

If Karna was born for war, he could have chosen the right wars to fight. He could have fought those heinous people desiring to throw a bride in fire. Instead he chose to be part of that heinous crowd!

He could have chosen to protect his friends and family woman against Gandhavans. Wasn’t he born for war? Why did then he flee? Why did he not fight first ten days at Kurukshetra?

There is a difference between warrior and war-monger. A warrior fights for a bigger cause and war itself is never the cause for them.

You asked — What deceitful nature?

Oh! there is a long list.

  • He conspired against Pandavas from the begining. Why did he do that? Pandavas had done him no harm? And this is not one off case.
  • Wasn’t he deceitful to Parshurama? Before you answer remember he himself accepted being that.
  • Didn’t he try to throw Draupadi into fire after her swyamvara?
  • Didn’t he ask Dussasana to strip Draupadi?
  • Didn’t he advise Duryodhana to kill Pandavas in war before Vrishni’s join them and while they were still weak?
  • Didn’t he seconded Susharmas suggestion to plunder Matasya when Keechak was killed?

You said — Krishna and Pandavas were cousins. So they had a good relationship. But what's the relationship between Kauravas and krishna,? Nothing.

Is there a point here? Are you blaming Krishna for being biased? Be specific.

By the way do you know whom was Duryodhana’s daughter, Laksmana married to?

You asked — Did karna know that bhagwan krishna was God. If he knew, he would have worshiped him like he did for lord Surya?

Did Karna not know about Krishna? Had he not seen moments ago how Krishna got released from his and Duryodhana’s plan to capture him?

Had he not grown up hearing the stories of his heroics?

Did he forget when Indra explicitly told that Krishna his Hari and invincible Narayana?

Was he calling him Madhusudhana and Hrishikesh without a reason?

Did Krishna followed Surya’s advise? Didn’t Surya explicitly asked him to follow Kunti’s directives? Did he follow that? Or Did he not know that Surya was a God?

Is it important, that you listen only to God? Had Karna any doubt that Krishna (God or not) was advising something that was in the welfare of man kind?

Karna knew who Krishna was. He knew what was good for humanity. Yet he chose to go against it. And, that makes him evil.

You said — You mentioned Ravana's case. Ravana was gatekeeper of vaikuntha and was cursed to be born on earth and be killed by lord vishnu.

I will skip the authenticity part here. But tell me this. Does this fact makes Raavana less evil? Should we worship Raavana? Or we continue to burn his effigy like we did for thousands and thousands of years?

This proves your past birth (Raavana) or lineage (Karna) is no prove that you would be good or evil. Your acts do.

And those acts make Raavana and Karna evil.

Your best question — First of all, who's Karna to stop what’s written by God? Outcome was written by bhagwan Krishna himself.

You carry false belief about the true nature of God as per Sanatana Sanskiriti!

God’s don’t micro-manage human affairs. If it were so, there would be nothing evil in the universe.

God let humans find their destiny on their own and incarnate only when humans have completely disbalanced the synergy of the universe. That is what Krishna said — Yada Yada his Dharmasya.

Did you notice, a brutal king Vishwarath got chance to become Vishwamitra and a robber Ratnakara became rishi Valmiki?

If Krishna was micro-managing human affair, why did he need to preach Gita to Arjuna? Why did Arjuna deviate from his plan?

And even if you think, God micro-manages human affair, who said it is monolithic. Our life is a sequence of decision making — we are here because of decision we made in our life. God has planned a blue print as to where one would reach when he takes a particular descision — You jump and gravitation would pull you down. But if you are man enough and play with God’s set rules (science) you can fly!

Maybe Krishna had created two endings of this story — one in which Karna rejected his offer and one in which he accepted his offer!

God gives opportunity to every one — multiple opportunities. That is his compassion. What you do with those opportunities makes you good or evil.

And if you agree with nothing I said and want to believe it was Krishna wish, then Karna was evil as per that wish! Why do you want to debate against Krishna’s wish?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 13, 2021 10:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why didn’t Rama returned back from Lanka without Sita? That would have prevented the war?

Pandavas were not on the wrong path. Karna was. Krishna tried to counsel him and hoped he could change his ways and follow the righteousness. Karna didn’t.

Now do you think, Krishna forgot his purpose of existence — It was to establish the righteousness — through peace or through war. He tried for peace. That attempt failed.

Now it was time for war. Aryavart couldn’t be left to be tormented in the hands of criminals and conspirators like Kauravas and Karna!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 13, 2021 10:11 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I take it your earlier confusions are now resolved as you didn’t raise those issues again. So let me answer your new issues —

You said — yes karna fled away. did he have any unbreakable chariot and bow like arjuna? arjuna was supported by 5 pandavs. was karna supported by anyone.

Are you talking about Karna or the divine artifacts?

Arjuna was supported by only three brothers, Karna had an entire army along with hundred Kaurava brothers. Can you count which is greater 3 or 100+army?

Nakula and Sahdeva had no divine weapons, no invincible chariot and no unbreakable bow. Did they run away?

Duryodhana had no divine chariot or invincible chariot. Did he run away?

During Draupadi’s Swaymvara when wicked kings tried to throw away Draupadi in fire (and Kara was one of them), Arjuna had no divine chariot and not Gandiva. Yet he Karna retreated scared.

During Matasya battle Karna himself says he has sufficient divine weapons that he could kill Arjuna and bring down his ape banner. And guess who once again ran away? And how many times?

Abhimanyu didn’t had invincible chariot or unbreakable bow and he was fighting alone. Guess who ran away once again?

Divine artifacts don’t make a warrior; character strength does.

You asked — Didn’t he return to save Duryodhana?

Well NO. He didn’t. He waited outside Hastinapur and met him in his tent. Don’t propagate baseless facts. Let me remind you the sequence of events —

  1. Karna Fled.
  2. Duryodhana was captured and he would have been transported to Indra’s place for his punishment. Karna is getting late for rescue.
  3. News reached Yudhishthira and it would have taken time.
  4. The brothers debate weather or not he should be saved.
  5. Arjuna goes to rescue. Now this was high time for Karna to return for rescue if he wanted.
  6. Arjuna tries to negotiate with Gandharvas. Negotiation fails.
  7. There is a terrible fight between Arjuna and Gandharvavs. Don’t tell me it ended in a second.
  8. Gandharvas try to trasnport Duryodhana. Here Karna would have missed any hope of rescuing Duryodhana.
  9. Arjun foils their plan. Not Karna.
  10. Chitrasena comes to fight Arjuna. Another fierce battle breaks. Karna hasn’t returned yet.
  11. Chitrasena is defeated and he tells the behind story to Arjuna. The debate.
  12. They reach Yudhishthira. Once again there is debate. Karna is no where to be seen.
  13. Yudhishtira frees Duryodhana and his army and ask them to return. Karna is still not visible.
  14. Duryodhana and his army starts there return journy.
  15. In the night Duryodhana camps in the forest. The entire night passes and Karna hasn’t returned yet.
  16. Karna returns back in the morning.

Now question for you —

  • How did you decide Karna was coming to rescue Duryodhana? Did he ever say that he was returning to rescue him?
  • Do you know what he said. Read it very carefully —

When night was over, Karna came to Duryodhana and spoke to him. “O son of Gandhari! It is fortunate that you are alive and fortunate that we have met again. It is fortunate that you have vanquished the gandharvas, who are capable of assuming any form at will. O descendant of the Kuru lineage! It is fortunate that I see all of your brothers. They are maharathas and have been victorious, triumphing over the enemies in battle. While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! I think it is extraordinary that I see you here, without harm and without injury, and with all your wives, riches and vehicles. You have freed yourself from that superhuman battle. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! O great king! There is no other man in this world who is like you, or can achieve what you have done in battle with your brothers.”

  • Not Karna confesses he ran away. He doesn’t say he was returning to save him. He just came back and started flattering them.
  • He didn’t imagine to see them again and finds it extraordinary that they are victorious.
  • He thinks no other man could have achieve what they (acutally Pandavas) achieved.

Which reference suggests that he was returning to protect him? He never said. And timeline doesn’t match. It was too late for any rescue.

You asked — Did he deceived parshuram bhagwan?

Do you want to say, he didn’t deceive Parhsurama? Are you sure? Here is what Karna said —

“In earlier times, when Rama found that I had obtained the brahmastra weapon from him under false pretences, he told me, ‘When your end has come, it will not appear before you. ’143 The maharshi possessed fierce energy and was capable of burning up the entire earth, along with her oceans. I had committed a grave crime. But even when the maharshi imposed that grave curse, he did it pleasantly.”
—Yaan Sandhi Parva, BORI

Why are you defending a deceitful person here?

You asked — Who tolerated the ultimate pain for not disturbing sleep?

Wow! do you consider him a great warrior because he tolerated an insect bite!

Sure I shouldn’t be surprised if Karna fled battle about battle. If someone greatest valor is tolerating and insect bite we shouldn’t expect greater deeds from him. Should we?

Tell me was this insect bite greater than his deceit?

You asked — who told this — bramhanya satyavadicha, tapasvi niyavrata, nipushvupi dayavanasya, tasmo karna vrisha ..

I think the same person, who said Karna is extremely wicked and confirmed Karna’s involvement in every conspiracy of Duryodhana. Yes, the same person who reminded Karna’s crime to Arjuna and persuaded Arjuna to kill him. I think he also said Karna was going to kill Yudhishthira. Am I right?

Do you know many small King were referred as Indra among men?

Does this shloka negate any of the evil acts of Karna?

So stop quoting a reference of what someone said. Please stay focused on what someone actually did. Here Krishna said he is compassionate towards enemy. Do you remember how he treated Bhima and Abhimanyu? Here it says Karna was Tapasvi. Can you give me a reference, when did Karna did Tapa? Can you provide a reference?

So why did he say so? You need to understand Krishna to understand what he said. He was just motivating Arjuna as there is no cross reference to whatever is said in the shloka. On the contrary, there are enough contradictions to it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 14, 2021 06:48 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Now I completely agree! And that is why Krishna persuaded Arjuna to take up arms and kill the evils. That’s where Gita comes in. Now we are on the same page.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 14, 2021 09:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You asked — Ohh really!!! Virat war he used any celestial weapon?

I think you neither read the epic nor my answers properly. I said Karna had divine artifacts. I said he claimed he would kill Arjuna today and bring down the ape banner. And all he did was flee before Arjuna made him unconscious and stripped him of the clothes the way he wanted for Draupadi all those years back.

You said — He could have used Vasvi Shakti.

Really. Do you know the precondition to use Vasvi Shakti? You must have used all your other weapons. But to use weapons you need to fight first. Karna was too busy running away, again and again.

You said — Because he was under the illusions of lord krishna.

can you please give an explicit reference that Karna was under illusions of Lord Krishna during Matasya battle. Till what level would you go to prove that evil a hero? He himself knew he is responsible for the destruction of earth!

You said — Karna retreated because he was afraid of his bramhanic energy.

Completely agree. Karna was afraid during Swyamvara. Then he was afraid during Gandharva battle (He himself said). Then he told Indra he was afraid of Arjuna. Then he was afraid during Matasya battle. Then he got afraid of Abhimanyu and fled when Abhimanyu killed his brother.

Yes. I know this theme. Karna got afraid. Karna fled.

Do you know who was that Brahmanic enery? Arjuna!

You said — Abhimanyu used his bramha kavach and rudra bow. He lost them? What else u want!

What I want you to realized that Brahma Kavacha wasn’t something great. It was easily cut down. And Abhimanyu died. That could mean two things — either there was nothing great about that Kavacha or if Brahma’s Kavacha could be cut down, so could Surya’s Kavacha!

What I want you to know that Karna made the right exchange — Vasvi Shakti for a Kavacha. Because anyway Kavacha didn’t prove too useful during Swaymavara battle or Gandharva battle.

What I want you to realize was Abhimanyu wasn’t beating Karna with his Kavacha, but with his arrows. There was no need to run away. He ran away because he was coward!

You asked — Why did arjuna save duryodhan????

Now for sure you didn’t read the epic otherwise you wont be asking this question. But don’t worry. I will tell you. Yudhishthira asked Arjuna to do that. But why did Yudhishthira wanted that?

Because Pandava’s were actually good people. And good people want to preserve the good. They would never have degrade themselves to the level of evils like Karna or Duryodhana to let someone humiliate the women folks of the royal family.

Do you know what Kauravas were doing in forest? They had come with their wives to mock Pandava’s miserey. It was Karna’s suggestion. He wanted to see Arjuna and Draupadi in tree barks. It would have given him the satisfaction.

But when they were attacked by Gandharvas, Karna fled. He didn’t care about his friend and certainly not about those women!

But Pandavas were not some Karna. They won’t let someone humiliate their family members. Not even evil Duryodhana.

They may have thought that it would change Duryodhana. But Alas…

You said — He (Krishna) told the same to inspire arjuna that karna was the most evil and not to show mercy on him.

You are right. Again! But then he gave reference to actual events that happened in the past. He reminded of Karna treachery in killing Abhimanyu. He reminded Arjuna of his role in humiliating Draupadi. He reminded about Karna’s role in every conspiracy against Pandavas.

Important point to note everything Krishna said about Karna’s evil nature was already discussed earlier in the epic. It was just a confirmatory proof. Karna role in Abhimanyu’s vadh or Draupadi’s humiliation or conspiracies to kill Pandavas is well documented in early part of the epic.

But whatever Krishna said about Karna’s valor or invincibility has no proof. And those glorifications are same as added for Eklavya, Jarasandha and Shishupala.

So Karna’s evil nature has sufficient proof. But is there any reference that —

  • Karna was kind to his enemies?
  • Karna had done any tapa? Had he pleased any divinity?
  • Krishna said he is truthful. Was he truthful to Duryodhana? Did he tell Duryodhana that he won’t kill Pandavas? Did he tell Duryodhana that he was Pandava’s brother?

All we know is he donated riches to the poor. I see many politicians doing that everyday.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 14, 2021 10:13 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why??? Why???

Because you don’t want to see the truth. Since Karna and Pandavas were not same, telling them the same thing won’t have same effect.

Pandavas were stationed in Dharma. And that is an explicit message of the epic which is contradicted only on social media through unverifiable and unsustainable claims — You have not given one reference against it.

On the other hand Karna had to redeem himself. Had he realized the truth, had he shown the compassion for humanity, had he sincerely repented and tried to atone for his sins by leaving the evil side, he would have proved himself a good candidate for becoming King — afterall he would have become the savior of humanity. But Karna refused to switch sides towards Dharma.

On the other hand, had Pandavas known the truth and given up their claims for someone who rejected being on the side of Dharma, Pandavas would have crossed over to the evil side.

You see there was a divide between Pandavas and Karna. On one side was Dharma and Krishna, while on other side Adharma and evil. Do you think both cross over would mean the same? No.

Karna crossing over would be Karna coming to the protection of Dharma. We have seen earlier Ratnakara crossing the line and becoming Valmiki. We have seen ruthless King Vishwarath crossing over to become Vishwamitra. And we have seen Daksha crossing over in the opposite direction.

Can you know see, why revealing the secret to Karna and Pandavas are so different from each other? In fact that would be exact opposite.

Is your ‘Why’ answered? Because I am sure you won’t answer any of my questions.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 15, 2021 08:24 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hey what's the point in answering your same question again and again when you don’t understand and don’t respond to questions that I asked?

You mistrust the epic narrative and live in your own world of delusion. How would you understand the epic message?

You said — Arjuna lost to robbers why because he was a adharmi.

Can you provide any reference supporting your claim? I dare you! And that shows your are delusional. You will make claims without reading the epic.

And since you can’t backup your claim, you now know who is distorting the epic.

You again said — In Mahabharata many people believed that suta was a lower caste. Bhima compared suta with dog.

Can you again provide any reference supporting your claim?

Do you understand English? Bhima used a metaphor! You have no right to throne just as a Dog has no right to yagna prasad!

And Bhima didn’t abuse him because he was a suta, but because he was undeserving. He was a murder conspirator!

Bhima didn’t insult Vidura or Sanjay. They were always respected. So caste baise exists in your head not in the epic.

You claim — Drona rejected brahmastra due to his caste.

Did Drona provide Brahmastra to Bhima? Or Duryodhana? Or Yudhisthira? Were they too lower caste in your opinion? Did you miss you when Drona saw his wicked nature? Did you miss out we had Varna not caste then?

You asked — Arjuna shows his valour during khandav forest burning. Where is the compassion at that time?

I would say exactly at same place where Rama’s compassion was when he killed Tadka, Subhahu, Khara and Dushana! He was doing his duty and was helped my Krishna himself. And do you know what Krishna said in Gita Chapter 5, Shloka 10?

ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति य: |
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा || 10||

Those who dedicate their actions to God, abandoning all attachment, remain untouched by sin, just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.

Why don’t you read before debating?

You asked (again) — Here Karna was not a king he couldn't stop the war. War was not going to stop by karna because war was not stopped when Karna was not fighting for ten days?

You are wrong! It’s not only Kings but also generals whom King trusts most decides the fate of the war. Karna was one of those who provoked Duryodhana to reject peace proposal. He surely could have stopped the war.

At least Krishna thought so. Had Karna left Duryodhana, he would have at least one less person to misguide towards certain doom.

As for why war didn’t stop during first ten days, is because Karna had already pushed Duryodhana towards the battle and now one was coward enough to back out after that point.

You said — No one praised Arjuna alone. Can you find somewhere where parshurama praised Arjuna alone.

Sure. Here it is —

O king! Therefore, discard your vanity and go to Dhananjaya82 before a weapon has been affixed to Gandiva, the best of bows. He possesses Kakudika, Shuka, Naka, Akshisamtarjana, Santana, Nartana, Ghora and Ajyamodaka as the eighth.83 All men who are pierced by these confront their death, or move around insane, or lose their senses and become unconscious, or go to sleep, or jump around, or vomit, or urinate, or incessantly cry and laugh. Partha’s qualities are innumerable and Janardana is superior to him.

If you read carefully you would see this praise is explicit to Arjuna. Krishna comes later.

But that is not all. Let me quote someone even superior.

Shiva

O bull among men! Accept a boon from me. What is it that you desire? You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

But I know you may find Shiva too biased. So let me quote one who is the only person for you who is beyond reproach.

Karna

First Karna says how afraid he is of Arjuna, when he asked Indra for Vasvi Shakti

Karna replied, “I wish to kill only one enemy37 in a great battle. He roars and blazes and is the source of fear for me.”

Then he admits in front of Shalya the day he met his maker —

He is firm and accomplished in the use of weapons. He is light in the use of his hands. There is no warrior who is Pandaveya’s equal. He grasps many arrows tufted with the feathers of herons. He shoots them as if they were but one. They descend at the distance of one krosha and do not deviate from their aim. Where is the warrior on earth who is his equal?

Now don’t tell Karna was asking a question.

You said — Karna was not a traitor .

Oh he was. And not just a traitor but a thankless man. Neither he cared for humanity nor for that friend who was always kind to him. He was traitor nevertheless —

  • He didn’t fight for his army for first ten days.
  • He didn’t reveal the fact that he had made a secret pact with opponents team and he would not kill their vital people
  • He knew his friend would die and did nothing to protect him. He played double game — persuaded Duryodhana to reject peace proposal and rejected Krishna’s proposal.

There is no better example of traitor.

Now you jumped to personal attacks — You know that quote and still think that god only love Arjuna and who loves Arjuna that's a surprise. Krishna was not hateful to anyone, It's you who is hateful to everyone who not love Arjuna .

No I am a Krishna devotee. I hate no one but evil. I don’t hate you. Same think I replied to Mihir in one of the comments here only. I don’t hate him and I don’t hate any Karna follower. You don’t deserve my hate. You deserve enlightenment. And that is what I strive.

You said — During Gita prava Krishna speaking like god and read it god is not bonded with any relationship. Arjuna was dearer to Krishna the human not dearer to the god.

So you want to say the Krishna who said — He who hates Arjuna hates me — wasn’t God Krishna? And if he wasn’t God he couldn’t have helped Arjuna win the war! But you are mistaken — like always. And I am sure you haven’t read Gita. Here is the shloka you claim to know but clearly don’t

समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रिय: |
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् || 29||

—Gita Chapter 9

This is what ‘The God Krishna said’ —

I am equally disposed to all living beings; I am neither inimical nor partial to anyone. But the devotees who worship Me with love reside in Me and I reside in them.

You are right Krishna wasn’t partial. It is mortal who are partial and they lose the touch of God. Those who are devotee reside in Krishna as his inseparable existence. Those who are evil and foolish they reject the God and are doomed. Can you guess you rejected Krishna? Duryodhana and Karna. Do you remember whom did Krishna explained this divine knowledge?

You said that — You reject every statement of Krishna when he praised Karna because for you Arjuna was god not krishna.

You are wrong. It’s another perception for which there is no proof. Can you quote a single reference from my answer where I said Arjuna is better than Krishna? Any single reference where I said Krishna is not good?

Arjuna is inseparable part of Krishna and only a fool would hate Krishna and claim to love Arjuna. And I am certainly not one of them.

I never rejected Krishna’s truths. I pointed out those statements of Krishna where he is aiming something much higher than truth — Dharma!

Krishna often said things which were said to achieve the objective of Dharma even if they weren’t factual. And you know it well.

Let us say Krishna said Karna was devoted to truth. But did he not said he was also an evil? But what was the ultimate goal of Krishna — to kill him. Right?

Krishan praises Karna in Drona Parva after Ghatotkatch’s death. But then he also praised Eklavya, Shishupal and Jarasandha. So you want to say Karna was as good as Jarasandha and Shishupala? Ok Let me agree.

But do you remember Krishna was extremely happy at this point. Do you remember why? Because now it was easier to kill Karna.

And do you rememer the purpose of Krishna incarnation as per gita — Partiranaya Sadhunaama, Vinashcyayta Duskritam — Protect Good, Destroy evil.

Now whom did he protect? And whom did he destroy?

Your final abuse — You didn't understand dharma. For you Arjuna is your dharma.

You can’t decide for me. Let me spell it out for you and you can check the same consistency in every of my answer.

I hate evils like Raavana and Karna. They rejected God and are doomed.

I don’t claim, I am an expert of Dharma, but certainly try with sincerity. I follow Shiva blindly. I follow Krishna blindly.

And finally for me Arjuna is not Dharma! He is proof of Dharma. Only Dharma could have made Shiva and Krishna side with him. He is an inspiration. He is a proof that God would be on your side if you are on side of Dharma

Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah!

And you would find me quoting that shloka times and again.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 15, 2021 10:48 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Pashupata is the only weapon capable of destroying the entire universe with all smaller Gods and Amrita. But you don’t fire missile to kill a mosquito.

Arjuna wouldn’t have used Pashupata on Karna. It was supposed to be used on a stronger opponent which Karna clearly wasn’t.

As far as Kavacha is concerned, Kara lost several battles which he had Kavacha.

Finally Kavacha wasn’t going to protect Karna’s head. Kundal would have kept his head alive though.

So if Karna had Kavacha we may have had an evil twin of Ketu — Karna and Ketu

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-karn-never-defeated-Arjun-instead-he-was-defeated-by-many-warriors-including-Arjun-then-why-Krishna-always-warned-Arjun-that-karn-has-the-potential-to-change-the-scenario-of-war-he-has-potential-to-even-defeat/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 15, 2021 10:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — A crime is a crime if it was done by a respected person also.

Agreed. But who would define what is a crime? You? Is killing a crime? Is killing of terrorists by a army official also a crime? Chandrashekhar Azad killed British officer. Was that a crime?

So who would decide it was a crime or a heroic? Mahabharata is placed in an era five thousand years ago. And it mentions Arjuna and Krishna act as heroic.

Does it matter, if you say Krishna was a criminal? What matter is what does Mahabharata say? Do you know what does Mahabharata say?

Can you reply this question honestly? I know you can’t.

You said — Pandavas killed nishad intentionally.

Can you provide an explicit reference that Pandavas killed Nishada intentionally.

Again you need to provide references and not your perceptions. Your perception doesn’t matter. The reference does.

  • Is it mentioned that Pandavas knew about their presence?
  • Is it mentioned that they set fire in the room where they were present? Bhima ensure Purochana was there when he set fire.
  • Is it mentioned that they made Nishada’s unconscious? Do you know someone who conspires hoping someone will come and become unconscious by destiny?
  • Did Yudhishthira wonder if Nishada people were dead or not? He certainly wondered if Purochana survived
  • Did anyone anywhere in Mahabharata condemned Pandavas for burning Nishada family?

Do you have absolutely any reference to back your claim? You said they killed them intentionally. Can you prove that?

If you can’t, then you know you are distorting the facts.

You said — During subhadra harana it was not about Krishna or Arjuna. It was about subhadra.

You are right. Subhadra Harana is not about Krishna or Arjuna. It was about Subhadra. Do you also realize it is not about what you think or what your perception is. It is about Subhadra. So tell me this —

  • Have you read Shubhadra was smiling (present continous tense). Which girls smiles during her adbuction? Did Sita smile? Did Draupadi smile?
  • Did Subhadra ever complain about her abduction? Did she ever resent? Did she held a grudge against Arjuna?

My simple quesiton — If Subhadra never complained against Krisna or Arjuna, who are you to complain and malign them?

You asked — Why they never asked devaki,vasudev and balarama .

Now tell me why should the ask Devaki, Vasudev or Balrama? You yourself said it is about Subhadra and not about Krishna. Why should it then be about Balarama or Devaki or Vasudeva?

Krishna too was his guardian. Now you tell me —

  • Did Devki or Vasudev resent Krishna descision?
  • Was Balarama a coward that he didn’t punish the abductor of his sister?

You asked — What they fear? It was written that Krishna fears that subhadra might love someone else.

Yes Krishna was afraid as he wasn’t sure whom she may choose —

O Partha! But that is uncertain if one doesn’t know the inclination. Those who are learned in the ways of dharma say that for kshatriyas, who are warriors, abduction for marriage is permissible. O Arjuna! Therefore, abduct my beautiful sister. Who knows what she might do in a svayamvara?”

But does this tell Krishna was sure she loved someone else? He was worried she may chose someone evil like Shishupala or Duryodhana or worse…

No brother would like that for her sister.

But does that prove Subhadra loved someone else? If Krishna wasn’t sure, how can you be sure? Isn’t it about Shubhdra?

So let me ask you again —

  • Did Subhadra ever resent or complain?
  • Why was she smiling during her abduction?
  • Did Devaki or Vasudeva complain?
  • Did Balarama punish Krishna or Arjuna?

Now if the incident doesn’t invite the ire of Krishna or Balrama and Subhadra never complained — Who are you to complain on her behalf? Who are you to condemn them? And what is the basis, other than your malicious opinion?

In all your answer you have just presented your views which have no foundation. Not one.

Can you answer any of my question with prover evidence about —

  • Khandava Dahana
  • Lakshagriha
  • Subhadra Harana

I would wait for your reply. And I see you have habit of not replying at the right thread but push your answer at some other place where it is difficult to search your answer.

If you have answer, reply here. I would wait.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: May 16, 2021 12:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry didn’t understand what you said. Can you please clarify? Did Duryodhana said so? Please quote the reference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Doryodhan-a-victim-of-injustice/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 16, 2021 01:22 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your inputs. I would just accept your words — you are no authority. And that is why you failed to find analysis and references in the answer.

Had you actually known the proper references from Mahabharata you would have seen both analysis and reference to incident as per actual epic.

And since you are no authority, I can understand if you miss the part that the question itself is seeking opinion based on preconceived notion against the authentic epic.

Yet, without exact knowledge or authority you decided that you have a right to your opinion, right to judge others as mad, and yet you feel other shouldn’t have it. Some may call it hypocrisy!

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-being-a-better-warrior/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 16, 2021 04:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Are you sure Karna rejected Krishna’s offer because of his oath? Then can you please elaborate a few points —

  • What was the exact oath of Karna towards Duryodhana? He tells Kirshna he knows Kauravas would be destroyed in the battle. So battle wasn’t in Duryodhana’s favour. Right? So what was his real oath?
  • Why didn’t Karna fight for the first ten days in Kurukeshetra? Do you think his oath had taken a leave for that period?
  • Why did Karna promised Kunti to spare the life of her four sons? Was that in favor of Duryodhana? Didn’t it interfere in his duty towards Duryodhana? Kunti never asked for such a boon. He could have declined Kunti flatly just like he did to Krishna. So why didnt’ he? If he could have made an exception for Arjuna, why not a similar exception for Bhima who was one true nemesis of all Kauravas?
  • Did Karna ever tell Duryodhana that he had promised Kunti to spare the life of all prominent members of Pandavas with sole exception to Arjuna who was his personal competitor?

Ask these questions to yourself and you would realize we are not comparing apples — we are comparing a selfish person to someone who always worked for humanity.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 16, 2021 12:37 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi Agree to most part of what you said, except a few.

You can’t compare Karna to Bhishma or Drona. Bhishma and Drona were honorable person who had to fight for Hastinapur. They didn’t participate in conspiracies'. Karna was part of all the conspiracy of Duryodhana and then some more where he was himself the master mind or prime instigator. And He was called wicked or evil by Krishna, Ashwatthama, Drona and Parshurama.

And you may find it laughable but that doesn’t change either of the facts — Karna was evil and a mediocre warrior. He lost many battles and fled most of them. Not only Arjuna defeated him but so did Gandharvas, Satyaki, Bhima and Abhimanyu. He did defeat Satyaki and Bhima on occasion. But his defeats confirms he wasn’t some great uncomparable warrior.

And as per Santana philosophy Gods don’t act as a puppeteer and pull human strings — else there would be no evil. We are responsible for our acts. Our guna controls our action, our action controls our destiny.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-being-a-better-warrior/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 16, 2021 12:44 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The above quote is true.

And this goes on to prove half truth may be deadlier than the lies. This is just half a quote. Here is the full quote —

When playing in the water, he would sometimes catch ten of them in his arms and hold them down in the water, letting them go only when they were about to drown. When they climbed a tree to gather fruit, Bhima would kick the tree with his feet and make it shake. Shaken by the force, the tree would whirl around and swiftly fall down on the ground with its fruit, frightening the princes. In fights, speed and martial exercises, the princes were never able to get the better of Vrikodara in any competition. Thus, in any competition, Dhritarashtra’s sons began to hate Vrikodara, even though he bore no ill will towards them, since he was only a child.

Important Notes as per authentic narrative

  • The narratives are related to Play and competitions.
  • Bhima was childish (and strong)
  • He bore no ill will towards them.
  • Bhima didn’t try to kill anyone.
  • Broken bones are common traits of outdoor play not only five thousand years ago, but even till our childhood. My own broken bones can be a testimony to that fact.

SO WHY DID DURYODHANA DID WHAT HE DID

‘Bhimasena’s great strength became well known. On learning this, Dhritarashtra’s powerful son320 revealed the evil side of his nature. He was wicked and inclined towards evil. Out of delusion and greed for riches, an evil thought occurred to him. “Kunti’s son Vrikodara, the second of the Pandavas, is the best in strength. I must find some trick so as to kill him. Then I will overpower his younger brother and his elder brother Yudhishthira. I will tie them up and reign as the sovereign of the earth.” Having made his mind up about this evil act, Duryodhana was always on the lookout for a chance to get at the great-souled Bhima.

More important than actions are the intentions —

  • Duryoudhana showed his evil and wicked side.
  • His actions had nothing to do with saving his brothers.
  • It was out of delusion, and greed for riches.
  • Even at that age he wanted to become the king.
  • He didn’t compete with Bhima. He tried to kill them by deceit.

There is a difference between childish play and deceitful greed. There is a difference between show or raw strength and wicked conspiracy. There is a difference between one person beating a hundered in a game vs few crooked people trying to kill a sleeping opponent by drowning or poisoning.

Same is mentioned in both BORI and KMG. So these people make up their own perspective by quoting half text.

What makes me choke is not as much the fact that Duryodhana was evil and wicked as the fact that people try to find justification of that act by contradicting the epic itself out of seer malice!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Doryodhan-a-victim-of-injustice/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 17, 2021 12:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I already responded to this in a previous answer to your exact same comment. Check in the above thread.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-being-a-better-warrior/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 17, 2021 01:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Pradeep,

Should I answer for myself of Karna? For I can assure you I would do things very differently from what Karna did. We are not same. Let me try what I would do and what Karna did.

Yes. I may go to war knowing my side would lose. For I may be fighting for a bigger cause — For Dharma. For friends. For family. For patriotism.

There are things greater than win and lose.

But I would be reluctant to fight on side of Adharma even to support my family and friend. I would like them to see the reason and try to prevent the destruction of my friend. I would refrain from fighting war for personal glory.

What did Karna fight for? Dharma? Patriotism? Family? Friends?

No.

He fought for his ego. Had he fought for Duryodhana —

  • He won’t be missing the first ten days of the battle.
  • He won’t have given Kunti an unrequested promise to spare all most all prominent members of enemy team including the nemesis of Duryodhana.
  • He won’t have kept Duryodhana in dark about his secret pact with Kunti.
  • He would have tried to protect Duryodhana from Bhima — by capturing him, if not killing him.

The problem with arrogant fools is the fact that they are blinded by their ego and self-glorification. For Karna the greatest ambition was to prove himself better than Arjuna. This desire was greater than anything — wealth, kingdom, friendship, dharma and even his own life.

He lived for it. He died for it. period.

Now you said — Don't tell me he was was a coward or good in finding ways to escape.

You want me to say Karna wasn’t coward and not good in finding ways to escape? On what basis should I say that —

  • Did he not retreated scared after Draupadi Swyamvara?
  • Did he not fled the battle against Gandharvas leaving his best friend and family behind?
  • Did he not flee Matasya battle?
  • Did he no flee Abhimanyu as his own brother was killed?

Finally you said — He was simply a great warrior on the wrong side of God ( dharma). And he had to die.

Now I won’t answer this question. Rather I would ask you a few here —

  • Was Karna just on the wrong side or he himself was wrong?
    • Was he not part of various conspiracy against Pandavas?
    • Did he not part of folks that tried to throw a bride to fire?
    • Was he not the prime instigator in Draupadi’s humilation?
    • Was it not his idea to mock Pandavas in exile, because of which Gandharva battle happen?
    • Wasn’t Karna the first to accept Shusharam’s propsal to plunder Matasya after Keechak’s death?
    • Didn’t Karna advised Duryodhana to rejected the peace proposal by Krishna? Was it just being on the side of Adharma or promoting Adharama?
    • Didn’t Karna used a snake in a battle between humans?

Finally tell me, what do you think makes Karna a great warrior —

  • How many battle did he fought for good of others and not for his own glory?
  • How many times he rose to defend someone else who were not linked to him by blood or friendship?

And finally tell me what was that great act of Karna that made you his fan, despite knowing he was the trunk of a tree that was evil Duryodhana?

By the way, I must congratulate you for giving your son an honorable name.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-being-a-better-warrior/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 17, 2021 02:11 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Perhaps you didn’t read the epic clearly. Can you check again if it mentions ‘fled’?

Krishna asked Arjuna to move on to a more important mission — ensure the welfare of the king. Krishna thought Bhargavastra wasn’t something worth wasting Arjunja’s time. It was fired in an irresponsible way and would soon tire out Karna. And guess he was right.

Now one neutralized Bhargavastra. So what happened to it? Did it win the war for Karna? Did it kill the entire army? Did it kill any warrior of repute? Why not?

Either talk logic or follow references. So can you show where is it mentioned that Arjuna fled because of Bhargavastra?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 17, 2021 08:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You are right! I hate evils. And I don’t find evils like Raavana, Hiranakashyap or Karna to be a warriors.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 19, 2021 10:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hey Soham,

No one. I repeat no one in the entire epic called Karna Daanveer. Yes, Krishna, Sanjaya and Yudhishthira did refer that Karna was a great in his charity. But does that make his Daaneveer? Was he greater than King Dilip, Sivi and Dadhichi? There is no reference.

And my dislike for Karna has nothing to do with weather or not he did charity or not. I see a lot of corrupt politicians today, who donate and give alms to poor on public money or wealth amassed by plundering others. I don’t like them either.

The epic doesn’t say you should love all the characters in it. It shows the victory of good over evil. The lesson is to recognize and condemn villain's and follow the ideals of evil. That’s the moral. It doesn’t ask us to follow and idolize people like Raavana, Hiranyakashyapa and Karna.

My dislike for Karna stems from the fact that he was an evil person. An epic five thousand years ago mentioned him wicked and evil. Five thousand years ago God incarnated to reestablish Dharma, to protect the good, to destroy the evil. And he wanted Karna to be destroyed. He did praise Karna on occasions, but gave reasons that couldn’t be verified against actual incidents. He censured Karna for his evil acts and gave evidence for each verifiable by the incident. Five thousand years ago, Ved Vyas described Karna as the trunk of evil tree Duryodhana. Five thousand years ago Shiva decreed Karna must be defeated. Five thousand years ago many such incidents were documented when Karna was involved in heinous deceitful acts which were condemned then, and are condemned today.

But now few people including you, born after five thousand years, trying to contradict all the epic incidents in favour of few selected quote and trying to portray him as not evil. I don’t know why would you contradict the core message of the epic? Last time we interacted, I asked you several questions which you couldn’t answer. Can you do it now?

  • Vyasji clearly says Karna was part of various conspiracy. Krishna seconded the idea. Ashwatthama, Drona reminded it. Karna himself repented. How could you not condemn a man who was so full of deceit and conspiracy?
  • What great thing did Karna did in his entire life for humanity that you would idolize him? Did he save a village from cannibles? Did he defeat any king and freed his prisoners? Did he help Gods in their mission?
  • Karna was a king of Anga. What was his achievement as a King of Anga? Go and read what Shalya says about Anga.
  • Why do you admire a person who wanted to throw a bride into fire because he failed at the swyamvara?
  • How can a person condone public humiliations of a woman instigated by Karna? When it was condemned by everyone that day and it is condemned by everyone today! Even Karna regretted. But how can a person yesterday, today or tomorrow not call him evil?
  • How can a person admire someone who wanted to see the anguish and torment of a family in exile and find nothing could give him greater happiness?
  • How can someone be loyal to him, who betrayed his own friend and fled the battle living his patron and friend and family women behind at the mercy of enemies?
  • How can someone call him brave who fled seeing his own brothers killed in the battle? What brave does that?
  • Why did Karna advice Duryodhana to reject peace proposal and jail Krishna? Why didn’t he strived for peace?
  • Why did he not fight the first ten days of battle? Was that his loyalty to Duryodhana?
  • Did he ever tell Duryodhana that he had a secret pact with the enemy and he won’t kill most of their key members including the one who has sworn to kill his friend, his king and all his brothers? How was it less than treason and back stabbing?

Can you answer why people five thousand years later start to patronize a villain based on few astray remark and ignore all his misdeeds? How can some people try to prove every part of epic that says bad about Karna as an interpolation? Why these people want to destroy and distort the whole epic for the sake of one person? Do you want to answer them?

And please don’t deviate from these questions by quoting unrelated incidents. I asked these questions again because —

  1. You wanted to know why I dislike Karna. You tried to portray that while Karna was a great donor, I hate him for no reason.
  2. You didn’t answer these questions during the last debate.

You may choose to answer my questions, or leave the debate. I am expecting point wise answer. Don’t expect further reply unless you can answer each of these questions.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-danveer-or-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 20, 2021 02:17 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I have never read any authentic reference from Chanakya about Karna. Can you share the exact source and reference. Similarly I would request you to share which Doha of Kabir Das says so. I would really appreciate that.

That said, trusting your words that there exists such a reference, I would like to proceed with my answer.

I don’t deny Karna donated and he was appreciated by Krishna himself for his charity. It is also a fact that Karna lost his divine protection Kavacha and Kundala because of his charitable nature. Had he not been famous for his charity, Indra may not have come seeking his Kavacha and Kundala in disguise. He also offered to spare the life of his four brothers during the Kurukshetra battle that was one of the reasons for Duryodhana’s loss (not that he would have won any way).

Second, donation wasn’t something associated with Karna alone. Didn’t Parshurama donated everything he had? Didn’t Sivi donated his own flesh? Didn’t Dadhchi donated his bones? Won’t it be a disrespect to all of them if I call Karna the greatest donor?

Third, can we really call it a donation if you seek something in exchange and refuse to give up unless your demand is met? Did Sivi or Dadhichi sought something in return to their own flesh and bones? Should there be a difference between the two? What does Kabir or Chanakya say about this?

Fourth, can we say it a donation, if I give something to myself? Whom did Karna donate the life of his own brothers? To Kunti? Were they not his own brothers? Is self donation also a donation? Also, tell me who paid the cost of this donation? Karna or Duryodhana? You steal something from someone and donate to other — Is that still a donation?

I am not denying Karna donated wealth to poor. And that was a great act. But was that great enough to atone for his sins?

Karna donated wealth to the poor. What was the source of those wealth? The royal treasury Or the wealth he plundered by vanquishing other kingdoms. Right? Can donation of wealth be considered greater than donating your life, your Dharma?

Karna said Surya that he won’t back out from donation, as it would bring him bad name. Can you see a tinge of ego here?

On the other hand Yudhishthira was ready to give up the heaven for the sake of a mere Dog? Do you call this ego or compassion? Is there a greater charity that giving up the chance of a swarga? Karna refused a mere Kingdom and he is great! Yudhishthira rejected swarga and there is no greatness! Who is Daanveer?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-danveer-or-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 20, 2021 08:24 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh no! I never claimed that. Forget Chanakya, even you can read it. And when you read it debate on authentic references rather than making personal attacks — all it proves is, you don't have contradictions.

When you read Ravindra Nath Tagore and Shivaji Sawant, just check if they called their work as fiction or authentic translation and you may have your answer. Sadguru talks about great warrior called Karna. But then he also talks about great warrior Barbarik. Should I trust him or Vyas Mahabharata, that doesn't have Barbarik?

Suthankar never said, Karna was not evil. He never said Karna didn't exchange his Kavach. He did say Shiva decreed that Karna would have to be killed for establishing Dharma. He did say Krishna was happy that Karna will be killed the way Eklavya and Jarsandha were killed.

So please tell what is your point? And Give reference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-danveer-or-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 20, 2021 12:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Good question.

Divyastras were nothing like the modern weaponry. As I understand, the most important difference being —

  • The worked not only on the energy and perseverance of the wielder but also comparative strength of the enemy. That is something told by Drona, Parshurama, Indra and above all Mahadeva Shiva himself. Remember Narayanastra withdrew when it found no target.
  • Divyastra had something like their souls or mind. Remember Vaishnavastra fired by Bhagdatta became a garland for Krishna.
  • They were not something like a projectile rather self guided one with target as accurate as fetus in a woman’s womb without actually damaging the woman.

Let us contrast it with ordinary weapons like the snake weapon that Karna fired on Arjuna. It had a projectile motion and Karna easily made it miss by thrusting down the chariot a bit.

Hope that answers your question.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Karna-had-Pashupatastra-as-there-is-a-verse-in-which-he-would-use-it-against-Yudhishthira-If-yes-when-did-he-get-it/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 24, 2021 10:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,
your answers have certain inaccuracies —

You said — Karna was never tested against Arjuna completely

What is the basis of this statement? There are number of occasions he was tested completed and routed.

  • Karna retreated scared from Arjuna after Draupadi’s swyamvara. There was no ambiguity in his defeat.
  • Karna was routed by Gandharvas who were routed by Arjuna at the same point of time, with Arjuna fighting lot more of them than Karna with fewer allies that Karna.
  • Karna retreated thrice during Matasya battle. He fled even as Arjuna killed his brother in front of him. Finally Karna was rendered unconscious. How much more complete defeated are you expecting?
  • Karna couldn’t stop Arjuna from killing Jaidradha.

I think if you still can’t conclude the relative strengths, you never would.

You said — Arjuna got his share of issues.. one with Ekalavya is a good example. On the whole both are equally at fault and equally good.

Can you please elaborate? Arjuna was jealous of Eklavya? But is it equal to actively conspiring against someone? Did Arjuna ask Drona to disable Eklavaya? Did he conspire to kill a innocent boy by poison? Did he try to burn a bride alive? Did he try to disrobe a woman in public? Did he want to mock and torment a family in exile? Did he want to ransack and kingdom whose general had recently died? Did he want to throw a peace emissary in prison?

How were both equally at fault and equally good?

Do you think, Krishna or Shiva were fool to support Arjuna and wish that Karna should be killed? Was Krishna, the hero, stupid to consider Arjuna his inseparable existence? If you trust Krishna, you would have your answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-real-hero-between-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Varun-V-199
Creation time: May 26, 2021 06:19 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree. This response is for those who cherish the notion that Arjuna's tryst with robbers can be considered as defeat. And trust me, many carry that illusion as hope.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 28, 2021 09:20 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What is fair is a relative term. Ulupi said, Vasus considered it unfair. Now when the death of a dear one ever been fair? When did we stop blaming someone or something for it.

What actually happened is a group attack on Bhishma. And Arjuna attacked Bhishma as Shikhandi obstructed Bhishma attack towards Arjuna. But that wasn't the first group attack of Kurukshetra. Even Bhishma participated in such group attacks.

What is important is Bhishma wasn't alone facing group attack. It was a group to group fight. Bhishma had his army with him.

He died because he wanted to and because of his whim of not attacking Shikhandi fatally.

Krishna too said Bhishma can't be defeated in a fair batlle. He included a bunch of other people when he said so. But then we don't have a verifiable evidence to that statement — Most of them were defeated by Arjuna in Matasya battle when later faced a group attack. I believe Krishna's statements have a deeper truth attached to them, which few don't want to see.

Krishna said we couldn't have defeated them fairly. Now is it believable that even [math]Krishna couldn't have? [/math]

That said Vasu's had their belief, and they cursed Arjuna. They themselves suggested the cure. And Ulupi helped Arjuna get that cure.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 28, 2021 09:38 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

WRONG.

Let us talk about Eklavaya never defeated Arjuna. Arjuna indeed found him a great archer. But if some one is a great archer, that doesn’t mean you are defeated.

In case of Karna you are perhaps hinting at the passing out ceremony. Once again there was no direct fight. Arjuna showcased some of his archery skills. Naturally you can’t show case your years of knowledge in a small span. Karna came next. He had seen what Arjuna exhibited. He followed the suite and exhibited few more. Once again they had no direct competition to conclude a victory or a defeat. Arjuna was ready to respond to Karna.

Based on all the evidence from Gurudakshina battle to Draupadi swyamvara to Gandharva battle, Matasya battle and Kurukshetra battle, it was Karna who was saved by Kripacharya that day.

You are once again, wrong to say Karna had no formal education. He was trained by Drona, Kripa and Parshurama.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 02, 2021 11:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I see, power or valor doesn’t make you a great human. Would you rather have NDA (National Defense Academy) train nuxals and terrorists?

Eklavya had potential. But did he have ethics? Why did he betrayed Drona by calling himself his student despite Drona explicitly forbidding him? And what was the ultimate fate of Eklavya? Why was he killed? As I see, Drona punished him wearing the same mantle Eklavya was misusing.

Krishna gave a projections. And projections are different from the facts. It was to emphasize that many powerful people were removed from equation even before Kurukshetra battle. You must notice here first he talks about Karna. Then about Ghatotkatch, Eklavya, Jarasandha and Shishupala. What was common between them? Did they all sided with Kauravas? No. Were they all evil? Yes.

You want more proof — Why didn’t he include Bhishma here?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-Dronachrya-take-the-thumb-of-Eklavya-and-did-unjustice/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 02, 2021 11:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Eklavya’s presence in Rajsuy yagna is explicitly mentioned in both BORI and KMG. You can’t call it FAKE.

Krishna killed Eklavya after Jarasandha was killed. It was sometimes between Rajsuy yagna and Kurukshetra battle.

The story that Eklavya was reborn as Dhristduymna is fake.

You obviously can’t provide any proof of whatever you said based on authentic incident.

Creation time: Jun 02, 2021 11:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Shibashis Das, I would delete any comment where I can’t reply back.

Karna has been mentioned as trunk of a tree that was Duryodhana. And Duryodhana was evil. And by that extension Karna was the trunk of “evil tree Duryodhana”

Lust has different forms. Karna and you may not lust on same thing. He had lust of recognition as the greatest warrior and that is why he rejected Krishna offer for the kingdom.

And Mahabharata has mentioned Karna as evil, deceitful, wicket on multiple occasions. He is also referred lustrous during Draupadi’s vastraharana.

By the way he did abduct a woman. G and read the epic.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-are-there-so-many-haters-for-Karna-Are-there-any-written-proofs-texts-to-show-he-is-really-an-Adharmi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 02, 2021 11:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You have too many questions and too many confusions.

But tell me this — Why only Karna fan’s critisize BORI.

BORI isn’t ancient. It is critical as it is a derivative based on ancient editons.

But for your satisfaction let us put BORI aside. Take any authentic version of Mahabharata —

  • Is there any version where Parshurama didn’t teach Bhishma?
  • Is there any version that says Karna was punished for being a suta and not for being deceitful?

I agree, when you say writing of works stared during Gupta period. But distortion started even more recently. Question is why are you distorting the facts — You want to contradict epic references based on you logic!

So let us check the logic. Karna decided to cheat of Parshurama. He didn’t just call him Brahman. He called himself a Bhargava Brahman. Can you guess why? Because Parshurama was a Bhargava. He thought this would give him and easy access. And his deceit was successful. He got both knowledge and respect.

The problem with wicked people is they think they are smartest and they would never be caught. Karna too suffered the same fate. You say why would Karna do something knowing the risk — has the fear ever stopped a robber from robbing people or a rapist from raping someone?

Now to answer your last para — there are not some experts, but just one person. And if you read that text it is full of “may be”, “perhaps” kind of words that we use when we have not verifiable evidence to support our statement. It is just to create a narrative which can’t be challenged as they are guarded by suppositions. It is unfortunate that people like you even bother to refer to those essays with such a trust that you don’t show for the ancient epics.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parshuram-incarnation-of-Narayana-decline-to-teach-Karna-when-he-got-to-know-that-he-was-a-soot-putra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 02, 2021 12:14 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your answer is not as per authentic versions, but based on popular modern depictions.

  1. Krishna did virtually nothing in the swyamver. He sided no one. advised no one on anything.
  2. Krishna didn’t interact with Draupadi during the swyamver. There is no evidence that they were friends at this stage.
  3. Draupadi never stopped Karna from participating. Karna participated. He failed.
  4. Arjuna won the swyamver on his own accord. Krishna recognized him. He was happy for him. But that is it.
  5. Krishna sided no one in the battle that followed soon after Draupadi swaymaver where once again Arjuna and Bhima vanquished the crowd of failed aspirants who tried to throw Draupadi back to yagna fire.
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Why-did-Shri-Krishna-make-Draupadi-Swayamvar-easier-for-Arjuna-3
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 09:53 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks. It was a typo. Updated it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-Dalit/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 09:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What it really means that ancient India followed Varna not caste.

Take this way Karna was raised by a charioteer, and no one knew his real identity. Yet, he became the King of Anga. And If it makes sense for Karna to remain a Suta, why did he become the King?

Now let us take the example of another abandoned kid — Krishna. His Prince father and princess mother abandoned him and he was raised by cowherds. Does that mean his biological father lost right over him? Did Krishna refused to accept them as their parent because long back they had abandoned him?

Finally, Kunti didn’t approach Karna claiming his right. She approached with a plea, that Karna could have considered, sided with Dharma and projected humanity.

It’s not a question of right and legality but ethics and compassion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-Dalit/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 10:06 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ok Let us see who is wrong —

I always said a young Arjuna was jealous of Eklavya. But it wasn’t a defeat — not even a mental one. It didn’t break Arjuna. It made him strive for becoming better. He goes to his guru. He praises him. He asks why is a warrior better than others? He doesn’t ask the guru to make that warrior weak. He wants to be better. That is NOT A DEFEAT.

Now coming to Karna

Can you provide any reference to prove your two points below —

  1. Karna was twice refused by the Gurus Drona and Kripa. Fortunately Duryodhana saw his archery and thought himself he could be equal to Arjuna and hence he compelled Durona and Kripa Acharyas to impel knowledge to him. Even then they didn't want to impart the knowledge to this “SudhPutra”. This SudhPutra tag, he carried this ugly tag till his death. He was always seen by the Acharyas as SudhPutra only and they shown partiality towards him.
  2. Again under the compulsion of Duryodhana only he went to Parasurama. It was not only against his wish but also against his own conscience.

None of the authentic references include either of these two points you mentioned above. This is what really happened —

  1. Drona never rejected Karna. Even Karna admits Guru had never been partial against him.
  2. Karna went to Parshurama on his own. He lied on his own. And his hate for Arjuna had so overwhelmed him that he was out of focus and was cursed by the Brahman for killing his cow. But that would be an interesting read to see what Brahamana said while cursing Karna.

You said — Finally all know how Krishna saved Arjuna in the battle.

Who is this “all”?

Karna was saved many times by many people. People wiled different shield, created army formation, designed chariots and armors and shields for protection. Protection doesn’t make a warrior weak and is not a pronouncement for his defeat. Krishna protected Arjuna from a divine weapon of Bhagdatta. In Arjuna Karna part, he never need protection. If you are talking about serpent weapon it was Karna’s treachery and Krishna smar maneuver as a charioteer. If a charioteer can’t protect his warrior from the projectile of an arrow, he is a worthless charioteer. That doesn’t raise question mark on warrior.

And Krishna was not involved in —

  • Draupadi Swyamvara battle where Arjuna defeated Karna.
  • Matasya battle where Karna fled thrice even as his brother was killed by Arjuna.
  • Day 14 of Kurukshetra battle
  • Day 16 of Kurkshetra battle.

You said — So totally I am against your 50–99 comparison.

Can you tell me where did I compare? My answer is about Arjuna. Not about Karna. It is you who jumped into the question and started comparing.

By the way, if I were to compare, I would never give a score as high as 50 to Karna for constantly fleeing all his battle and deploying all kind of deceits. For having a career as ‘always fleeing warrior’ maybe…

So unless you can provide reference to your own claims as I asked, we can safely conclude this discussion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 10:25 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

That is not the take away message from our epics.

Every epic, every purana highlights one core point — Dharma wins!

Krishna said the same thing in Gita — Dharma Sansthampnaya, Sambhavani Yugu Yuge.

That is the moral of Rama’s life.

And for Paritranaya Sadhunama, Vinaschya cha Duskritam, whenever there is war, there is a victory and there is a defeat.

And everytime a divinity incarnates on earth, they have a common message —

It is in the human purview to bring about the change.

Ram’s wife was abducted. He doesn’t act divine. If he had he could have prevented the abduction. He could have walked on the ocean bed. He could have saved himself and his brother from getting fatally wounded. He never needed an army or support. But he did all those things. Because he wanted to send us a message. You don’t need to be divine to be an agent of change.

Krishna didn’t fight the greatest war ever fought. He didn’t pick up the arms. Why? Because he wanted humans to be responsible for their own destiny.

And that is underlined once again in Gita. God doesn’t control our deeds. If it were so there would never have been a Raavana, Duryodhana or Karna. Our own gunas (sat-rajas-tamas) controls our actions.

God interferes only to restore the balance. And when it does, it is to destroy the evil, protect the good and reestablish the righteous society.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 10:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi Sheetal,

I agree with most part of your answer and you made a few very valid points like —

  • Karna after becoming a king could have become an agent of change. He could have done something for his tribe and could have created a legacy as a great teacher. But alas, he didn’t do any of those.
  • Most characters were trapped in their chakravyuha.
  • Ultimately goodness wins over evil.

But then there are points I have to disagree. Disagreements are the pillar of healthy debate, if we can do it politely and with mutual respect —

You said — Karna teaches us that if achieve something inspire of all the odds
