I don’t thinking using deceit to achieve your goal qualifies as a good candidate for a lesson for future generation. Karna always tried shortcuts. He didn’t try to impress Drona by hard work rather decided to demand divine weapon to become equal to the best student of the gurukul. Naturally Drona rejected. He again took a shortcut with Parshurama by deceiving him into believing he was a Brahman.

If you need an inspiration, why not look at Arjuna —

  • He practices archery even at night against the plan of his guru and wins his appreciation.
  • He saves his guru from death.
  • He becomes so great that he could defeat three of his guru in battle — Drona, Kripa and Chitrasena (Gandharva)
  • He is the only one to face Mahadeva as discussed in the original answer.
  • He, unlike others, passes his knowledge and becomes a guru to many worthy warriors — Isn’t it what you wished others would have done?

You said — Arjuna and Karna have equal achievements and equal failure.

I couldn’t agree with you. They are incomparable —

  • Arjuna won every single battle against Arjuna — directly and indirectly. How can there be an equal comparison.
  • Arjuna defeated Gods and Gandharvas. Karna fled Gandharvas — how can there be an equality?
  • Arjuna please all his gurus including Shiva. Duryodhana was rejected by his guru for his deceitful nature. Where is the comparison?
  • Karna tried to throw a bride in fire, Arjuna tried to protect her. Where is the comparison?
  • Karna fled Gandharvas leaving his friends and family woman behind. Arjuna fights without any support from women who were not his responsibility. He keeps fighting to protect them. Can you call their achievements equal?

Can you enumerate what were those achievements and failure where you find them equal?

You said — Arjuna was entrapped in his own kindness and compassion.

Here I only partially disagree. Kindness and compassions are not the chakravyuh but the armors. That is what wins the God’s grace.

As I see, Arjuna’s chakravyuha was his dedication towards his brother Yudhishthira. He never spoke against him.

But Arjuna breaks his chakravyuha, right before the final war. He drops his weapon. He was no more willing to follow anyone without proper reasoning. His compassion broke the chakravyuha. And the trophy was the divine dialog known as Gita. And that was a turning point.

Nice interacting with you.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-we-have-an-Arjuna-Award-for-the-best-runner-because-Arjuna-was-the-best-runner-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 11:07 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There is a difference in manuscript match and intent match.

Take for example. For same shloka of gita, if I write down it’s meaning and you write down it’s meaning there was be textual gap. That is what referred by scholars and manuscript gap.

But we must understand the meaning that I would write and you would write using different words would still have same meaning or essence.

So naturally any manuscript written by two person without in sync would differ in their shloka. But that doesn’t mean they differ in their meaning.

And based on meaning we can easily find out the distortions. For example, with whatever words if they mentioned Arjuna refused to fight in Kurukshetra, the essence would be same. One manuscript may mention few additional names other a few less. But that doesn’t distort the epic.

Take another example — some may say Krishna protected Draupadi others may say Dharma protected her. But baseline is when the evil tried to disrobe Draupadi she was protected by divine intervention. So a change is not necessarily a distortion.

Take another example — one script may say Karna called Draupadi a whore other may say he called her a prostitute. Now this would be case of manuscript mismatch. But what about the essence?

What is a distortion is a adding or deleting an incident that kills it essence. Some example of such modern distortion is —

  • Karna tried to protect Draupadi’s Vastraharana
  • Gandharva incident didn’t happen
  • Karna Arjuna fight in Matasya ended in a tie.

In Shruti-Smriti tradition it is common that different people would present narratives in their own words. What makes it still a wonderful tradition that with so many narratives no distortions can go un-noticed. And we have a verifiable way to weed out distortion — any regional narrative not present in other regional narratives can be safely called distortion.

And this becomes the basis of critical analysis.

On this note, distortions done in past few thousand years in nothing compared to those done in past century and especially last few decades.

Ignoring the difference between manuscript mismatch and essence mismatch would be the greatest distortion that would ridicule the epic and degrade it to a fable.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-there-is-so-much-distortion-or-differences-in-versions-of-Mahabharata-with-regard-to-Draupadi-Swayamvara-Was-there-no-need-to-bend-and-see-the-reflection-of-target-in-water-and-was-the-target-not-moving-as-per/answer/Sminu
Creation time: Jun 03, 2021 11:48 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You first comment wasn’t about conditions. It was an absolute statement — legally, as far as I know, parents who leave their children are tantamount to losing their rights over the child.

So legally it doesn’t matter, why a parent abandoned his child.

Second, as I already mentioned Kunti never approached Karna for her right over him, but to persuade him to ditch the evil side.

But if you insist on comparing the reasons of their abandonment, they are not as different as you think. Kunti abandoned Karna not for herself, but for her family and even for Karna. Just answer one question — do you think any dignified person would have married Kunti knowing that he had a son? Is there a precedence? Do you know even Satyavati hid his son’s identity from her husband? So what would have been Karna’s life had Kunti not abandoned him? Do you think, he would have a dignified existence?

On the other hand think about Krishna. Do you really think, Krishna’s life was at risk had Vasudeva not exported him out of prison? Do you really believe Kansa could have harmed him had he remained in prison? Could Kansa harm the baby girl who replaced Krishna in prison? Could Putna harm him? So, why did Vasudeva and Devaki abandoned him? Think!

Krishna accepted his parent because he could understand their turmoil. Ved Vyas never ditched his mother, because he understood her compulsions. Karna, well he was a different person. True we can’t compare.

You said — Of course, he received a lot of insults as a child from the lower class because of his hobby.

Can you quote a single reference that Karna was insulted as a child for his lower class? I would be interested in seeing that.

First thing first, a suta isn’t a lower caste. It is a child born out of intermarriage between Brahmin and Kshatriya. Second, Karna was the son of a prosperous family and his father was a close friend to Prince Dhritrashtra. Third he was accepted in Drona’s gurukul where he was a closed friend and confidant of Duryodhana. Which of these elements tell you he was insulted for his caste?

If you can quote a reference, do so. Just wondering why didn’t Karna carry such a resentment for his father or for that matter, why you never blamed him who forced Kunti to give birth to Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-Dalit/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 11:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I never debated on the right. Kunti never said, I have a right on you. She pleaded. She pleaded him to leave the evil side.

And parent or not, anyone can ask someone to leave the evil side. That is ethical thing to do. Don’t you agree?

While Karna had reservation is returning to the side of Dharma, he had no qualms in becoming a King. This could mean only two things — either Karna had duel standards or contrary to your belief there was no such rigid caste system. It was Varna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-a-Dalit/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 12:02 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think you must read what Ashwatthama said. He reminded the situation. He also reminded what Vidura said. And you are forgetting it is a cross-reference. One of the many pointing to the original incident. And original incident clearly points out what Karna said and did. It is present in both the ancient edition and critical edition.

The same incident is also referenced by Dronacharya and Krishna. And Krishna gave explicit statement of the abusive words spoken by Karna. You may not find reference of disrobing because you forget it never happened. But it doesn’t mean it was never attempted.

Karna himself regretted his words.

Now let me ask you simple questions —

  • Why in your opinion, Ved Vyas mentions Karna role explicitly in vastraharana?
  • What do you think Ashwatthama means when he said — you made them do the act? Which act was he referring?
  • Why did Krishna said in Kairata Parva that Duryodhana, Duhssasana, Shakuni and Karna shall be punished by death? What was Karna’s crime?
  • What do you think Drona meant when he said — In the assembly of the Kurus, with Shakuni and with Dhritrarashtra’s sanction, you excited the wrath of the Pandavas. Duhshasana supported you and Karna increased it — How did Karna increased it?
  • Why did Karna regretted his action? Why did he say that he along with Duryodhana, Duhsasana and Shakuni is responsible for everyone’s destruction?

You are ready to ignore so many direct references. Do you have any single reference to suggest Karna wasn’t involved in Druapadi’s humiliations? Can you provide a single reference? We both know the answer.

You said — By your logic bhisma and drona were also gulity of tolerating the incident. Why didn't they raise weapon.

Bhishma and Drona can be considered guilty of inaction. And there inaction was due to confusion with dharma and parampara.

Karna was guilty of a heinous action. I am surprised you can’t differentiate between the two acts.

You said — And if karna was such a shameless creature why did lord krishna say that draupadi will have her sixth husband as karna?

Karna was shameless. And Krishna did never offered Draupadi to him. That is a translation blunder. I would recommend check out the original Sanskrit verse.

If you apply little common sense, you can understand that. Draupadi got five husband because of Shiva’s blessing. And Shiva’s blessing had not provision for a sixth husband.

As far as the loyalty of his brothers, Krishna offered a similar proposal to even Duryodhana. That doesn’t make him lesser evil. All it means is Krishna tried to avoid the war.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 12:25 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you have a point here?

You said — Nowhere was karna defeated in swayamvar. He retreated and it was clear stalemate.

How did you deduce that? Arjuna challenged him to continue fighting. Karna retreated scared thinking Brahmana was invincible. He compared him with Indra, Vishnu, Parshurama, the personification of the knowledge of weapons. You still find it stalemate?

Ok tell me what was the fight about? Karna’s goal was to kill Drupad and his family and throw Draupadi in fire. Arjuna’s role was to protect them. Who succeeded in his goal?

Can you quote a reference that proves it was a stalemate? And why did Karna retreated scared?

You said — Could Drona capture yudhishthira despite making advanced war formations. Did bhisma do it??? Only Karna did in less than 2 days.

What happened next? Did Duryodhana win the war? Why did he let Yudhisthira go? On whose side was he fighting? Did he ever tell Duryodhana that he would not kill or capture Yudhisthira or his other Pandavas?

Bhishma certainly informed Duryodhana right before start and Drona tried his best.

What Karna did was back-stabbing Duryodhana — an act of treason. You want to applaud him. Go ahead.

You said — In reality karna cannot be killed. He can be defeated.

I agree. Karna can be defeated. And he was defeated again and again. Let us say he can’t be killed — so he would have lived on like a loser. Right! And he could have joined Ashwatthama in his journey to live a condemned life to eternity giving each other a company.

By the way. no one is immortal. But yes, we both agree, he was a loser.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 12:36 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It is on the eighteenth day of the battle soon after Arjuna killed Karna’s son and a fierce battle broke between the two. All Gods went to Brahma and Shiva and there they spoke these words —

Brahma and Ishana spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone. Karna, foremost in the worlds, is a bull among men. Vaikartana is brave and valiant. But let the two Krishnas be victorious. With Drona and Bhishma, let him762 obtain the great world of heaven and the worlds of the Vasus and the Maruts.’

Same was also mentioned by Indra —

Having heard the words spoken by those two gods of the gods and abiding by the instructions of Brahma and Ishana, the one with the one thousand eyes spoke these words to all the beings. ‘You have heard what the two illustrious ones have said for the welfare of the universe. It must happen that way and cannot be countered.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 12:42 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Here is what Krishna said —

O friend! O lord! I tell you truthfully that my limbs burn at the thought that even then, the evil-souled Karna attacked him. In that battle, he was unable to remain in front of Abhimanyu. He was mangled by the arrows of Subhadra’s son. He was unconscious and blood flowed from his body. He blazed and sighed in rage. However, afflicted by the arrows, he retreated. He retreated and lost all enterprise. He gave up all hope of remaining alive. He was supremely unconscious in that battle and exhausted because of the blows. Hearing the appropriate, but cruel, words of Drona in the battle then, Karna severed his bow.

If you notice the words, it won’t be difficult to understand —

  • The evil-souled Karna was unable to remain in front of Abhimanyu.
  • He retreated and lost all his enterprise.

It is interesting to note that the coward retreated even on seeing Abhimanyu killing his brother in the same way he had retreated when Arjuna had killed his other brother in the Matasya battle.

Now someone who is unable to stand in front of a warrior and retreats back and then attacks, where do you think he is attacking from? He has been mentioned retreating.

But do you have any reference to suggest that he came in front and attacked? Please share your reference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 04, 2021 12:50 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You know your words are not sufficient. Give a direct reference that Krishna said he helped Arjuna in Matasya battle. Can you?

Karna and Duryodhana both knew Krishna was a God. But they were evils and evils generally ignore Gods when they reach their doom. Daksha knew he was insulting God of Gods. Hiranyakashyap defined God. Raavana too knew Rama was no ordinary person. If only evil learn that their power is pale in comparision to God! Alas. They don’t.

You said — Karna supported duryodhan only due to friendship and loyalty. Why will he comply with his mother who left him

Karna didn’t need to comply with his mother. He needed to be on the side of Dharma. He didn’t do that.

Krishna too was abandoned by his mother. He didn’t carry his resentment.

Krishna didn’t abandon Karna. He didn’t comply with him either.

Was Karna loyal to his Duryodhana?

  • Why did he spare Bhima or Yudhishthira? If he refused to comply with Kunti, why did he promised her something she never asked? If he could make an exception for Arjuna, why not for Bhima as well? Kunti never asked either to spare his sons or make an exception.
  • Did he ever tell Duryodhana that he had a secret pact with the enemy? Did he ever tell him that while you are sacrificing your brother to save my life, I would let them and you die but not kill Bhima? Did he tell the truth? This is treason!
  • Where was his loyalty to Duryodhana during Gandharva battle?
  • Why didn’t he fight for first 10 days of the battle? Was his ego greater than his loyalty?

You said — Karna was never in lakshagraha or bhma poisoning. And bheema only tortured Kauravas so anger started.

Your statement proves you haven’t read the epic. It is clearly mentioned that Karna was involved in all those conspiracies with explicit mention to Lakshagrih. Krishna himself confirmed it as a cross-reference.

You may feel sympathetic for Duryodhana. But Duryodhana didn’t had anger. He had greed, jealousy and murderous intentions. Ved Vyas calls Bhima’s action as childish without malice, while Duryodhana’s action as evil.

If you have a reference to prove your point, give. If it’s your view. I dont’ care.

You said — t's given that karna feared bramhanic energy. But what's the meaning of circumspection? It means unwillingness. Karna feared bramhanas. He was already cursed.

Check out my next comment.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-some-Arjuna-fans-say-that-Karna-was-inferior-to-Arjuna-when-Lord-Krishna-himself-declared-that-Karna-was-superior-to-Arjuna/answer/Gobi-Subramaniam
Creation time: Jun 06, 2021 05:27 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — It's given that karna feared bramhanic energy. But what's the meaning of circumspection? It means unwillingness. Karna feared bramhanas. He was already cursed.

Have you read the epic and context of the circumspection? Have you checked what caused this unwillingness? Don’t worry, I will guide you —

Phase 1 — What was the objective of battle

The kings said, “We are assembled here and he passes us over like straw. He wishes to give Droupadi, supreme among women, to a Brahmana. This evil-hearted one does not respect us. Let us kill him. He does not deserve our respect or veneration because of qualities of age. On account of this, let us kill this wretch who insults kings and their sons. After inviting and entertaining in the proper fashion all the kings with food, he then shows them disrespect. In this assembly of kings, which is like a conclave of the gods, can he not find a single king who is his equal? The sacred texts clearly say that a svayamvara is for Kshatriyas; Brahmanas have no right in the choice of a husband. O kings! If this lady does not wish one of us as her husband, let us throw her into the fire and return to our kingdoms. Though that Brahmana has done injury to kings out of his impertinence and greed, he should not still be killed. After all, our kingdoms, lives, riches, sons, grandsons and all our other wealth exist for the sake of Brahmanas. But something must be done to prevent insult and to protect our own dharma, so that other svayamvaras do not end like this one.” Having said this, those tigers among kings, with arms like clubs, rushed at Drupada with diverse weapons, so as to kill him.

  • They wanted to kill Drupad and throw Draupadi in fire.
  • Though they decided Brahama shouldn’t be killed, they must be taught a lesson. This decision would soon overturn.

Phase 2 — Arjuna moves to protect Draupadi

Arjuna moves forward to protect Panchal family under attack —

Vaishampayana said, ‘Those bulls among the Brahmanas shook their deerskins and water pots29 and said, “Do not be frightened. We will fight with the enemies.” Arjuna smilingly told those Brahmanas, “Stand aside as spectators. I will repulse those kings, like poisonous snakes, with mantras, with showers of hundreds of sharp-pointed arrows.” Having said this, the maharatha took up the bow he had obtained as dowry and accompanied by his brother Bhima stood there like an immovable mountain. Seeing the Kshatriyas, with Karna leading them, furious to do battle, they fell upon them, like two elephants against hostile elephants. Eager to fight, those kings then said, “It is permissible to kill a Brahmana who is willing to fight.”

  • At this stage Kings changed their mind. Before the battle started they were willing to kill Brahmana.

At this stage all your theories that Karna didn’t want to kill Brahmana collapses.

Phase 3 — Karna Arjuna battle starts

Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant. Shalya, king of the Madras, rushed at Bhimasena.

Note carefully —

  • Karna rushed at Arjuna eager to fight.
  • He acts as an elephant rival over a female elephant.
  • Do you see the circumspection till this point?

Phase 4 — When did Karna became circumspect?

‘Seeing Vaikartana Karna rush towards him, the wise Arjuna stretched his mighty bow and pierced him with three arrows. The impact of these sharp arrows stunned Radheya31 and he approached with greater circumspection. Then those two invincible warriors fought violently, each eager to vanquish the other. Such was the speed that they became invisible.32

  • Arjuna pierced Karna with three arrows stunning him.
  • At this point arrogant Karna became fearful and approached with circumspection. His elephant attitude is gone.
  • But the fight continues.

Here it is clear, Karna knowing person to a brahma fought violently eager to vanquish him. Where is the excuse of respect or fear of Brahmana’s curse?

Phase 5 — How did the battle ended?

O supreme among Brahmanas! Are you Rama,33 the personification of the knowledge of weapons? 34 Or are you Harihaya35 himself? Or are you Achyuta Vishnu himself? Have you assumed the form of a Brahmana to disguise yourself and are now fighting strongly with me for self-preservation, mustering the strength of your arms? When I am angry in the field of battle, no one except Shachi’s husband36 and Pandava Kiriti37 can withstand me.” Hearing these words, Phalguni38 replied, “O Karna! I am not the science of weapons personified. Nor am I the powerful Rama. I am only a Brahmana, chief among warriors and supreme among those who have the knowledge of weapons. Through my preceptor’s grace, I have become skilled in the use of brahma and paurandara39 weapons. O warrior! Wait for a bit. I stand here today, to vanquish you in battle.” At these words, Radheya Karna withdrew from the battle, because the maharatha thought that Brahmana strength was invincible.

  • Karna compares Arjuna to Rama, Vishnu, Indra, the personification of knowledge of weapons. Who would compare a losing warrior to greatest warriors of all times?
  • Arjuna challenges him to stay but.
  • He knows Arjuna is one of those who can withstand him even when he is angry.
  • Karna withdrew. Why? Not because he thought he shouldn’t fight Brahmana. Because he thought Brahamana (Arjuna) is invincible.

Do we have more proof?

When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened and surrounded Vrikodara. They said, “These bulls among Brahmanas are supreme. Let us find out in what lineage they were born and where they live. Who can fight with Radheya Karna in battle except Rama, Drona, Sharadvata Kripa,41 Devaki’s son Krishna and Phalguni, the scorcher of enemies?

  • Karna withdrew scared.

Wasn’t the battle a draw?

Remember the objective of the battle. Karna wanted to destroy Drauap and throw Draupadi to fire. Arjuna wanted to protect them. Who won?

CONCLUSION

What really bothers me is not the need to prove that Karna retreated scared, but the mindset that wants to find excuse for his defeat. Do you know who are you protecting — An evil man who wanted to throw a bride in fire!

It is the legacy of these people that modern day acid thrower follow — If you can’t get a girl destroy her. Where is the valor in such a heinous act? Should you not be cheering the defeat of Karna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-some-Arjuna-fans-say-that-Karna-was-inferior-to-Arjuna-when-Lord-Krishna-himself-declared-that-Karna-was-superior-to-Arjuna/answer/Gobi-Subramaniam
Creation time: Jun 06, 2021 05:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I couldn’t understand much from your comment other than you want to call me biased twice. I plead guilty. I am biased towards dharma.

There is a difference between strong and brave and warrior. A Lion is strong, but he isn’t a warrior as he as no warrior ethics. Strength is a physical trait that can be augmented with weapons and armies. But to be brave you need to have character strength.

Ravaana may have been strong, but was he brave? He kidnapped Sita like a thieve ensuring Rama and Lakshaman are out of sight. Is this what you call bravery? He raped multiple women in his life. Is that the good quality you are referring to?

Similarly to be on the wrong side and being the wrong himself is two different things. Karna wasn’t just on the wrong side. He himself was evil, deceitful, conniving, coward. He was part of every Duryodhana’s conspiracy right from the beginning. He was one of the prime instigator of Draupadi’s humilation, Gosha Yastra, Goharana. It was on his suggestion, Duryodhana rejected Krishna’s proposal.

Unlike Bhishma or Drona, Karna was not an unwilling supporter of Duryodhana who supported him out of his loyalty or some compulsion, he was one of the prime conspirators who forced the war by rejecting every possible way to avoid the war!

Can you see the difference — Bhishma and Drona sided with evil, Karna and Duryodhana were evil.

Now if you understand the difference and still prefer to call them great, I must say you are one of those unbaised people who couldn’t differentiate between Dharma and Adharma.

And guess what, from where I see, being unbiased between good and evil is supporting evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 07, 2021 12:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Look carefully. I didn’t equate anyone. I grouped them together for what there were worth — evils and abuser of woman.

Either you didn’t read or didn’t understand — Shiva and Brahma decreed that Karna will have to die and attain heaven. Arjuna would remain undefeated. By the way do you know even Duryodhana and Duhasasana is said to have attained heaven. That is no proof that he wasn’t evil.

But Krishna did recount his evil deeds and was too happy that Karna would die. He was ready to even lend his Sudarshan to do it. Do you recall why Krishna incarnated — To protect good and destroy the evil!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 07, 2021 12:17 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree. It won’t be the way you or I want. So why not focus on the epic —

  • Krishna wanted Karna to die.
  • He called him evil and recounted his crimes repeatedly.
  • He ensured he is killed.
  • He was born to destroy evils.

Karna’s evil act included

  • Conspiracy against Pandavas
  • Cheating his guru
  • Ditching his own friend
  • Trying to burn down Draupadi
  • Trying to molest Draupadi
  • Ditching his own brother

And this is only a partial list.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 07, 2021 12:19 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You appear to be lost in your web of imagination and your answers don’t match that of the epic.

  1. Mahadeva vs Arjuna — Arjuna had to lose. No one can win against Mahadeva. I already explained what made his defeat more glorious than any defeat. Mahadeva himself praised.
  2. Burbruvahana — Arjuna clearly didn’t fight with all his might. It is very explicitly mentioned. The only way you could miss it is if you haven’t read it. And Arjuna was revived by Uulipi not Krishna.
  3. Robbers — Read again. Arjuna pushed them away. They fled. Not Arjuna. Had Arjuna fled, there would have been no women left. But epic clearly mentions Arjuna’s rehabilitation attempt for the families and that included most prominent wives of Krishna.
  4. Karna — Never defeated Arjuna. Conceded his defeat explicitly on day 16 about which you are talking. Even Dhritrashatra and Sanjaya explained Arjuna’s glorious victory.
  5. Nagaastra wasn’t a weapon, it was the proof of Karna’s treachary — using an snake to kill a human rival! Thanks to Krishna the snake missed and killed. Krishna’s smartness can’t be considered as Arjuna’s defeat. It is the responsibility of a charioteer to deflect attacks on the warrior. It is interesting to note that You consider even Karna’s miss as his victory.
  6. How did Arjuna get defeated by Vaisnavastra? Did he get a chance to counter it? Did he run away from it? Did he say he couldn’t counter it? Did it kill Arjuna? Please explain, how was it Arjuna’s defeat?

Please quote valid reference and refrain baseless answers.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 08, 2021 11:18 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh No,

I would never insult Lakshama by comparing him to Karna. I named Lakshamana as the epitome of loyalty and generosity and wanted to remind that there are many and truly great people whom you can’t ignore to when choosing the best or most generous man.

I didn’t compare any of those great people with Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 08, 2021 11:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

A five year kid knows many things, that he grows up to realize were not true — this is called maturity.

I guess you still have to reach that moment. No reason, why your learning can’t start today. This is what Karna says in Ratha-Atiratha Samiksha Parva —

O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the commander is credited with qualities, never the warriors. O king! I will never fight as long as Gangeya is alive. But once Bhishma has been slain, I will fight with all the maharathas.’”’

A five year old may not know this.

You said — No one asked karna his life or else he had given it.

I highly doubt that. Karna loved his life dearly and always fled to protect it. He fled leaving behind his best friend at Gandharva battle. Didn’t he pledged his life to him earlier? He fled two battles as his two brothers were slaughtered.

You said — How can a man forgive his mother who abonded her to river and who was the reason of his life long struggle.

Krishna certainly forgave her mother. So did Ved Vyas. Why was Karna an exception?

And can you highlight what was his life long struggle? As I see, he was raised by a wealthy family, was educated by best teachers of Aryavart and became a King at a very early age and lived in luxary, something admitted by Karna himself. So let me ask again, what was the struggle?

Now can you imagine what would have happened, had Kunti not abandoned Karna? Do you think she would still have the chance to become the queen of Hastinapur or anywhere else? Is there any reference that a lady became queen despite having a known pre-marital child? Didn’t even Satyavati concealed the identity of her child?

Now what do you think would have been the future of Karna with Kunti? Can you speculate?

You said —If she had said her in beginning then might the situation was changed?

What point was the beginning? Kunti first saw her during talent showcase event. But Karna was already actively involved in conspiracies against Pandavas from a very long time. There is no mention that Pandavas’ did any thing wrong with him. He was already a dear friend of Duryodhana who had schemed for killing Pandavas many times over. How would things have changed.

I am sure you must have learnt something today. No? Well, keep trying. One day you would…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 08, 2021 11:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you think Karna took Kavacha only because Surya asked. Read the chapter again. Surya also said not to give Kavacha. Surya also said accept Kunti’s proposal. So can you tell, why Karna refused to agree to is two out of three proposals?

Idea is simple, he followed what suited him.

You say Indra would have to pay for… But isn’t that for Indra to decide? Was Karna the arbitrator? But he did say I won’t give you Kavacha unless you give me something in exchange.

Can you guess, why did he ask for Vasvi Shakti? Go read it. Karna had explained this to Indra — He was afraid of someone. Now you may not be knowing, whom.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 08, 2021 11:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why do you think death isn’t a punishment? Why do you think criminals are hanged or awarded death penalty?

But yes, one death may not be sufficient punishment. After all Ravana to got only one death. So do you mean to say there is no penalty for being evil?

But we can have bigger punishment. Show there true picture to the world. But some try to glorify even heinous molesters, acid throwers and treacherous creatures also. What can we do?

When talking about a punishment would you consider these sufficient punishment — some one who committed himself to life long of sins and crimes just to be considered the greatest warriors, dies failing everyone and never reaching the aspirations he hoped to achieve using unethical means?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 08, 2021 12:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I did read your answer. Unfortunately they don’t match the authentic text.

Robbers

Good you accepted your mistake. Once again your assertions that he couldn’t save most of the wives and citizens. He actually saved most of them. Few robbers carried away and few went with them by their own. You can’t save those who don’t want to be saved. There is a detailed mention of various people Arjuna rehabliated at many places. But yes, I always maintain, he couldn’t save them all. Once you lose your divine weapons and you are a normal human, number matters. And more than that courage matters. That is something that makes Maharana Pratap and Chandrashekar Azar honorable.

Do you know even Shri Rama lost against Marich’s deceit and failed to protect Sita from abduction. Does that make him weak? Do you count it as his weakness or defeat? Why then double standard against Arjuna? Didn’t both tried their best. Do you know what the other warrior of that time did in a similar situation?

You said — Babruvahana it was gangas curse due to which arjun was killed arjun wasn’t testing his son

I don’t know what is the basis of your assertion which is in contradiction with what the authentic version say. Let me quote you some —

The bull among Pandavas laughed and killed them. The king became extremely angry and swiftly descended from his chariot. Enraged, he fought with his father, Pandava, on foot. The bull among the Pandus was delighted at his son’s valour. The son of the wielder of the vajra did not wish to oppress his son excessively. However, Babhruvahana thought that his father was no longer willing to fight.

—BORI, Ashwamedhika Parva

But the above reference clearly proves, Arjuna was fighting mildly not willing to hurt his son. Even his son realized this. Now coming to the testing part, here is what Uulipi mentions.

He is incapable of being defeated by men and not even by the gods, with Vasava. For the sake of causing pleasure to your illustrious father, Indra among men, I invoked the maya known as Mohini today. O son! Kourava desired to test your strength.

—BORI, Ashwamedhika Parva

And here you see, it is clearly mentioned that he was testing his son.

Hope that answers your question.

You said — I said he overpowered him that day (16th day) and arjuna was weak infront of Karna

Are you sure about it? Let me quote you what happened on 16th day.

Arjuna himself countered Karna’s weapons with his own weapons. He enveloped the directions, the sky and the earth with his showers of arrows. Those arrows descended like clubs and bludgeons. Some were like shataghnis and others were as fierce as the vajra. The soldiers, the foot soldiers, horses, rathas and elephants, were destroyed. They closed their eyes, uttered woes of lamentation and fled distractedly in different directions. In that battle, horses, men and elephants were destroyed. The soldiers were killed by the arrows and terrified, fled. ‘ “Thus did they engage in battle, desiring victory. The sun approached Mount Asta.278 O great king! In particular, there was darkness and dust. We could not see anything, favourable or unfavourable. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! The great archers were frightened of fighting in the night. With all their horses, they therefore withdrew.279 O king! When the Kouravas departed at the end of the day, the Parthas were delighted in their minds at having obtained victory and also left for their own camps.

— End of day 16

Can you check who won here? If you are confused let us see what Karuvas felt about the battle on day 16.

‘Dhritarashtra said, “Arjuna killed all of us easily, as he wished. I don’t think Yama would have escaped, had he attacked him in a battle. O fortunate one! Partha robbed single-handed. Single-handed, he satisfied Agni.280 He vanquished the earth single-handed and made all the powerful kings offer tribute.281 Single-handedly, using his divine bow, he killed the nivatakavacha s.282 He fought single-handedly with Sharva, who was in the form of a hunter.283 He protected the Bharatas single-handed and satisfied Bhava.284 Fierce in his energy, he single-handedly defeated all those on my side.

‘Sanjaya replied, “They were killed, wounded and shattered. They were deprived of their armour and the vehicles that bore them in war. Their voices were distressed. Those insolent ones grieved and were defeated by the enemy. The Kouravas went to their camps and sought counsel and advice from each other. They were like serpents that had been defanged and had lost their poison, having then been trod on by the foot.

Now if you don’t trust these eye witness account, look for what Karna said —

Karna angrily spoke to them, sighing like a snake. He rubbed one hand against another hand and glanced towards your son.286 ‘Arjuna is always firm, skilled and persevering. He understands and when the time is right, Adhokshaja287 instructs him. We were deceived by him today, because of that sudden shower of weapons. O lord of the earth! But tomorrow, I will destroy all his intentions.’288

Now if you think, Karna was overpowering Arjuna, think again. And please don’t forget to share the reference.

You said — Karna didnt use treachery he used nagastra he didnt knew abput aswasen in his arrow that isn’t treachery

Nagastra means an arrow with a snake sitting on it. And not only Karna knew about it’s presence, he had worshipped this snake for many years for this occasion. It is not only mentioned at the time Karna used it, but also is cross referenced at two more places in the epic. Even Bhishma and Duryodhana knew Karna’s association with Ashwasena.

And remember a simple idea — you can’t ditch a divine weapon by moving few inches away or by pushing you chariot down. Such rules apply to normal weapons that follow physics projectile. Divine weapon can be as accurate as hitting a child in the womb without actually causing damage to the mother.

So yes, Karna act was treacherous and heinous.

And as I said earlier, it is the duty of a charioteer to protect his warrior by deflecting attacks if possible. Why else do you need a great charioteer?

You said — An unfallible weapon of vishnu so arjuna cant counter it so Krishna himself saved him all was due to krishnas intellegence.

Did Arjuna said so that he can’t counter it? Did he become afraid? Did he try to run away? It was Krishna’s proactive decision. A warrior doesn’t lose even if he dies unless, he loses his enterprise and concedes the defeat. Abhimanyu died. But he was never defeated.

There were two possibilities here —

  1. Arjuna may have countered it with higher weapons like Pashupata. It would have been selfish and undesirable as it would cause more damage if two supreme weapons collide.
  2. Arjuna may have died. A victorious death. Not running away.

But in either case you can’t call it a defeat if you don’t give a chance to fight. Can you?

You said — In the entire mahabharat war he had the supreme lord krishna ss well ad hanuman on his side so none can defeat him

Agreed.

You started the debate trying to elaborate Arjuna’s defeat. You ended the debate suggesting none can defeat him because Gods sided him. What is it you exactly want to prove?

  1. Why do you relish that Arjuna got defeated, when you know even Gods supported him?
  2. You do you think God’s support is something that takes away Arjuna’s credit? You say it as if it makes Arjuna weaker.

Can you tell me what is a divyastra? What were Karna’s divine armors? They are extension of God’s grace. Is having them a bad things?

Here is an important consideration — Why did Shiva, Krishna and Hanuman sided with Arjuna? Do you think, gaining the grace of Gods is a trivial issue?

Shouldn’t this be the main question — Who was worthy of God’s grace and who did they willed to destroy? Why?

Do you know about Hiranyakashyap? He was powerful. He had a boon that made him almost immortal. He died anyway. Is there a takeaway? Is power without dharma even worth praising?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 09, 2021 12:02 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What is the role of a war charioteer in general? Isn’t it to maneuver the chariot in such a way to aid his warrior’s success? Isn’t it to protect him from a weapons projectile? Krishna did what was the part of his job description. Can you call it Arjuna’s defeat?

And should I consider a supposition to be a defeat? Wasn’t it an acceptance of defeat for Karna, that he tried to kill Arjuna using treachery? He used a snake instead of a weapon? Do you know what was was the snake weapon? It was an arrow on which Karna affixed a serpent — a sworn enemy of Arjuna — one who Karna had worshipped for many years. Had that snake killed Arjuna it would be the victory, would you consider that victory of one snake or two snakes?

What was wrong in foiling the deceitful trick of an evil warrior?

And finally I agree with you one one point — Krishna may have saved Arjuna. But do you see the underlying message in this statement? Whom had Krishna sworn to protect? Whom had he sworn to destroy? He had declared this here —

Paritranaya sadhunang vinashay cha dushkritam
Dharmasangsthapanarthay sambhabami yuge yuge

Don’t you think, by being good Arjuna won the grace of Arjuna and that is probably the greatest victory that would dwarf all defeats?

And to answer your question — No. You are not defeated unless you are afraid, unless in your fear you decide to run away and concede. Nothing like that happened in the incident that you are telling. Arjuna didn’t flee. He wasn’t afraid. He didn’t request his opponent to stop in the mid of the battle. He didn’t ask for time. He didn’t made excuses. He just fought.

Even if Arjuna were to be killed at that moment — it would have been his victory and glory and not that of the treacherous foe who tried to kill him with the help of a serpent.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 09, 2021 01:13 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,

I think you didn’t read my answer properly. Had you read —

  1. you would have seen I quoted you line by line before contradicting
  2. You would have not misquoted me.

So let us start now —

You said — I dont feel considering it is bad.

Sorry but I couldn’t make out the meaning of this sentence. Fail to understand what is bad and what you don’t feel. May be you can elaborate.

You said — I will like to tell yu that none of the epics are directed by actual ved vyas we are only reading the things which later acharyas wrote in accordance to their thoughts of the war sure they proved pandavas higher as they ere told that they were supporter of dharma and haf lors with him.

Your statement says that you don’t trust the epic. May I know what is the source of your knowledge? I know about Sruti and Smriti tradition. But what you should know is when many acharyas in different language, regions and time wrote their epic, they had a common cord — And that narrative is analysis is what is the accepted term.

If you don’t trust the epic, I don’t know what are you debating on?

You said — Karna overpowered him on 16th day its real acc. To BORI etc it might not be written but its real accordibg to texts related to that time

Oh! so Karna overpowered Arjuna on the 16th day is the real account. Wow, I am sure you would be able to provide me a reference for the same as I did to you in the last post. Please provide me with those real texts. Also let me know the source of of that real reference.

Do you know BORI CE is a document that is the outcome of over 40 years of hard work analyzing all the available ancient manuscript from Sanskrit, Nepali, Bengali, Sarda, Neelkantha, Maithili and Dakshinayat. And yet you mistrust them all! For what? To prove that an evil person can over power a hero momentarily?

You accused — Karna as yu say having divine armour is bad!

You certainly haven’t read my answer. I never said Karna having divine armor is bad. I asked you this question to point out a divine weapon is a divine blessing the same way divinities blessings. This was to emphasize that you can’t ridicule Arjuna for being worthy of divine grace of Shiva, Krishna and Hanuman.

You said — yu should also think that having permanent support of indra is also bad karna didnt had support of anyone.

Oh! so Arjuna had a permanent support of Indra? Do you know even Duryodhana rubbished this theory.

Did Indra help Arjuna in Lakshagrih consipiracy? Did he help him during Khandava Dahana? Did he helped him during the dice game? Did he gave him a free pass to swarga? Did he fight for him at Kurukshetra?

If you look carefully, Indra fought against Arjuna at Khandava Dahana and his army (Gandharva) fought against him during Ghosha yatra. In fact it was Arjuna who won Indra’s war against Nivatkavacha.

So tell me, how exactly did Indra permanently support Arjuna?

Now coming to Karna, can you tell me why he didn’t had support. Didn’t Surya warned him about Indra’s intended arrival? Didn’t he bestowed on him a divine Kavacha right at the birth? Didn’t he recommended him not to donate his Kavacha? Didn’t he suggest him to ask for Vasvi Shakti? Didn’t he suggested her to accept Kunti’s proposal?

Didn’t Krishna himself offered his support to him in exchange for averting the war?

The problem is evil people don’t know when they were given the divine bless and they waste it all.

You said — He was even neglected by his younger brothers was it good ?

Have you read the epic? Or just watched the TV Shows thinking they are the ancient words?

Pandavas never knew Karna was their brother. Where was the question of neglect? How was it good or bad?

You said — He had no other chance than to side with adharmis if he had given same chances as arjuna do yu think arjuna be ever even equal to him?

It was not a question of chance but choice. Karna was given the same chance as Arjuna. Where Karna failed was in decision making.

Karna and Arjuna both joined Drona’s gurukul. Initially guru didn’t preferred either over his own son. But Arjuna won his guru by his dedication and persistent practice. Why didn’t Karna do the same thing?

Why didn’t Karna became friend to Arjuna or Pandavas? Because he chose to be friend with Duryodhana. Mind it, it was a choice. Not a chance. How do you expect Pandavas to support someone who was constantly conspiring to kill them?

Karna got a better chance. He reached Parshurama. Arjuna didn’t. He could have been better. But then he chose to lie. Again his choice earned with the wrath of Parshurama.

You can’t be given the chance. You have to take it. Tell me, did Karna learn new arts after leaving Parshurama? Is there any mention that he met any other guru? No he led his life in luxary as a king.

No one had stopped him to go worship Shiva and earn his blessing. Did Karna event attempted to do it?

A man addicted to luxary can’t take the pain to learn knew art. He was rusty and fled Gandharvas, during Matasya battle, from Abhimanyu.

In short, it was a matter of choice and hardwork and not chance. Karna had the opportunity, that he wasted in arrogance and luxary.

And nothing changes the fact — Arjuna was better than Karna. Every single person accepted this including Karna himself.

Once again, I am not talking about TVShows.

You said — Karna didnt worshipped aswasen he was wulling to kill arjuna without any help.

All I can say is you don’t know. Period.

Here is what actually happened —

O king! The clash between Arjuna and Adhiratha’s son assumed a great and dreadful form. They struck each other with arrows, like two fierce elephants goring each other with their tusks. Karna affixed an extremely sharp and flaming arrow that was capable of slaying the enemy. This had a serpent in its mouth.786 That terrible arrow had been carefully preserved and washed well, protected well for Partha’s destruction. It had been worshipped and laid down on a bed of sandalwood paste. That immensely virulent weapon was lying down in a golden quiver. It was generated from the lineage of Airavata787 and flamed. Wishing to kill Phalguna in the battle, he aimed at his head.

—Karna Vadha Parva

It is very clearly mentioned, that Karna affixed the snake that he had worshipped for years.

Interestingly this incident has a clear cross reference at more than one point. This is what Bhishma said in Udyoga Parva —

Bhishma responded, “What are you saying? Your intelligence has been numbed by destiny. O Karna! Do you not know that once the foremost ones have been killed, Dhritarashtra’s sons will be dead? With Krishna as his second, Dhananjaya burnt down Khandava. Hearing about that should be sufficient to restrain your soul and those of your relatives. The great-souled, illustrious and great Indra, the lord of the thirty gods, bestowed a spear on you. In battle, you will see it shattered and reduced to ashes, struck down by Keshava’s chakra. There is a radiant arrow with the mouth of a serpent. 145 You have always honoured and protected it with the best of garlands. It will be struck by the arrows of Pandu’s sons. O Karna! Together with you, it will be destroyed. O Karna! Vasudeva, who killed Bana and Bhoumya,146 himself protects Kiriti. In tumultuous battles, he has killed many enemies who are your equal and superior.”

Yet, if you want to believe Karna wanted to kill Arjuna out of his merit, that would be you choice. But tell me this, if he really wanted to kill Arjuna with his own capability, why did he ask Indra for Vasvi Shakti? Why did he wanted to kill Arjuna using that divine weapon? I don’t see, Arjuna saving any weapon to kill Karna?

You said — Vaisnavastra none can counter vaisnavastra and ebwn pashupastra cant be used against vaisnavastra as it can destroy the universe he was about to be killed but krishna saved him it can be called duty or savior of dharma but truth is truth he cant counter it.

You contradict yourself and now saying what I already said. As per both Krishna and Shiva there is nothing in the universe that can’t be countered by Pashupata. The only condition was it shouldn’t be used on a weaker opponent. Now Vaisnavastra wasn’t a weak opponent.

But as I said earlier, and now you agree, Arjuna could have countered this weapon but that would have led to greater destruction. Krishna intervened to avert that destruction. That was Krishna’s choice.

But you can’t hold Arjuna responsible for Krishna’s choice. You can’t declare him defeated without giving him a chance.

Vaisnavastra was Krishna’s responsibility. He handled it. period. Arjuna doesn’t exist in the picture. Had Arjuna failed to counter and Krishna had then intervened your point may be worthy.

You said — Again to robbers maximum wives etc were captured by robbers some went on their own.

That’s your perception and not backed by either reference or common sense. As I said there is detailed reference of how Arjuna rehabilitated everyone in different parts of the country. The problem is you have chosen to believe someone and unwilling to see the reference that is you choice.

And as I said, even Rama couldn’t protect. And that wasn’t his defeat.

I saw your argument about Rama but couldn’t understand what you are trying to say? Would be great if you can explain —

You also said — Rama knew about marichis plan yet he didnt d9 anything why? It was because his fault was the main reason of his death

Whose fault was the main reason for whose death? Why would he let Raavan kidnap Sita? Are you saying Rama was a co-conspirator?

You said — you woyud have known that ravana was an incarnation of jaya a guard at vaikuntha who was curse by panchrishis in order to save them he took rama avatar to free him so he by himself live like a human and did all .

Krishna killed Kansa and Narkasura. But he didn’t allow them to kidnap his wife. Did he. Think and you would see the flaw in your logic.

You said — I think karnas death was quite more glorious than arjunas defeat. I hope yu agree it all i.

I couldn’t disagree more. Karna died like a true coward he really was.

Did you see Abhimanyu begging his enemy to stop attacking? Wasn’t Abhimanyu without weapons or kavacha or chariot? Did Karna remember the Dharma then?

Karna continued to attack unarmed chariotless Bhima on day 14. Did Bhima asked him to stop?

Arjuna fought without chariot on day 14, when his horses were tired. Did he ask opponents to stop fighting till he gets back to chariot?

No.

No self respecting warrior would have pleaded for recesses the way Karna did. He himself attacked Abhimanyu, Bhima, Satyaki etc. in the same way, but when it was his turn he started referring to Dharma.

Karna’s chariot had stuck in the mud. He wasn’t weaponless. He had option to fight like a brave warrior. He could have learnt from Abhimanyu, Bhima and Arjuna.

There was nothing glorious in his death. And it doesn’t matter if you agree or not.

You asked — read properly before replying

Hope you will find, I replied everything after reading yours. Now if you wish to reply, reply with proper reference. If you want to say epic is flawed and references are flawed, don’t bother to reply. I don’t debate with someone without reference or trust on the epic.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 09, 2021 06:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

So you think, Pandavas got punished and Kauravas were awarded? Is that your understanding?

Are you sure Kauravas weren’t sent to hell? Do you know the time difference between the demise of Kauravas and Pandavas?

Please clarify your statement.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 09, 2021 11:39 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As a mortal existence, we all are condemned to see our loved ones gone and we suffer their loss — we can do nothing about it. I believe all of us can relate to this specially after the current pandemic. But that is the law of nature. Not punishment.

For a selfish person, who is just thinking about his or her family, yes death is preferable. But to prefer death you need to be brave. A coward may bear the agony of seeing his brother and sons killed and yet prize his life so much that he can actually beg his enemy not to kill because he is no position to flee.

But for good people who dedicate life to a greater cause, they go on even at the cost of personal agony. They are put to test because they can bear the test. Remember to swallow the poison you need to be Shiva.

By the way in Kurukshetra both good and evil had to suffer the agony. Arjuna saw his son killed. Karna and Duryodhana too saw their brothers and sons killed in front of their eyes. But there was a difference —

Arjuna had solace that his sons were killed when he wasn’t around to protect and he did avenge their deaths adequately.

The real punishment is when you see the life drain out of your sons and brothers, and the last thing they see is you fleeing without attempting to save them. Sons and brothers of Karna and Duryodhana died in front of their eye and they could do nothing for him.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 09, 2021 11:50 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your post is not worth answering. Nothing is based on any authentic retellings. And you certainly don’t read my responses, just keep telling what you want. Have you provided any reference to any of your claims so far? NO.

Can you share what is your source other than BR Chopra’s Mahabharata? On what source you have made your claims?

Even if you ignore BORI and follow ancient Neelkantha or Dakshinayata recitations you would find the following —

  • Karna was a student of Drona. None of the ancient versions contradict this fact.
  • According to all ancient narrations including Neelkantha, Karna and Duryodhana were friend and co-conspirator. None of the ancient version says Karna met Duryodhana during talent show case.
  • It is a fake narrative that Parshurama taught only Brahmana. A clear proof his Bhishma, who too was his disciple without lying to Parshurama. He was cursed for his lies not for being not Brahmana. You don’t need to go to ancient narrative to understand this point.
  • According to ancient narratives Suta were not Shudra but cross between Brahmin and Kshatriya.
  • None of the ancient narrative say Karna was drunk during Gandharva attack. Not one. It was BR Chopra imagination. There is no ancient narrative attached to it.
  • Not even Neelkantha recitation includes Karna pushing the Krishna chariot back. It is another folk tale not an authentic narrative.
  • Arjuna also defeated Karna during Swyamvara battle by all accounts of ancient narratives.

Interestingly you said a lot of things but didn’t provide any proof of it or any of your reference. Now let me show you a flaw in your opinion —

You said — In some other books Arjuna fled away from robbers anyonne yu can say the situaton is same most of the or some of the women were taken away .

What are these other books in which Arjuna fled? Can you give me an example? Are they authentic version? Irony is you don’t trust BORI. You don’t trust ancient Neelkantha and base your opinion on TVShows and some other books of unknown origin! And yes, the drunk theory originates in BRChopra Mahabharata exclusively. There is no ancient reference to it.

You said — Karna overpowered arjuna on 16th day i again say the same see in texts carefully .

Once again, you didn’t read my answer at all. I gave you exact proof of Karna’s defeat. Badly. But you again base yourself on BRChopra Mahabharata and thing Karna overpowered Arjuna on 16th day. Can you provide a reference to support your point.

You said — Morwver he never fought with all hos might at virat he thoughta arjuna as a neuter gender so be didnt fought him bravely at the kurukshetr war he didnt fougjt bravely as he didnt want to fight hos brother at his hoghest abulity he was equal to arjuna

You are wrong. Karna clearly said he would kill Arjuna today. Everyone identified Gandiva. So neutral theory is baseless. Imagine a neutral gender kills your brother and you still doesn’t fight bravely. I would call this being coward. In Kurukshetra he fought with all his might because he had sworn to kill him. For Arjuna being brother didn’t matter. He did kill Abhimanyu and showed no remorse. So stop giving baseless arguments without any valid reference.

You said — None states karna as a coward not even once except you

Are you sure? Bhishma called him as such reminding he always use to run away from battle. Dorna seconded his opinion. Kripacharya and Ashwatthama called him boasting. Dhritrashtra lamented that Duryodhana thought Karna would win the battle but he keeps loosing.

Guess again, I am not alone. Again not talking about BR Chopra Mahabharata.

You said — he wanted his death at the hands of his brother.

Once again, I know you have no proof. Just tell me this, why did Karna beg for recesses if he wanted to die on his brothers hand? Why did he ask him to wait? Why didn’t he go down like a true warrior? Fact is Karna didn’t welcome death but tried to push it away by asking Arjuna to wait. He asked Arjuna to follow that Dhrama he never followed in his conducts.

You said — Karna also promised his mother kunti that he willnt kill any brother except arjuna as he knew he wil die at the result of the war as he only wanted to glorify himself not kill him after knowing himself as his elfer brother

Yes he wanted a glory for himself and that is why he back-stabbed Duryodhana and made a pact with his enemies. Did he ever tell Duryodhana about his secret pact? Foolish Duryodhana believed him and let his brothers die to save Karna.

Questions for you

  • Provide a reference that proves Drona didn’t teach Karna.
  • Provide a reference that Satyaki never defeated Karna.
  • Provide a reference that Arjuna fled and robbers fled. Apply your common sense. Had Arjuna fled, there would have been no one to protect. Robbers would have carried everyone — at least the prominent queens of Krishan — who were prized catches.
  • Karna overpowered Arjuna on 16th day
  • Karna was drunk during Gandharva battle.

Now, I may not respond to your questions unless you can provide references. If you next response like your previous responses are without reference we both would agree that you have none to offer and you just forward arguments based on TVShows.

Can you provide credible reference to your assertions? (I know the answer, but nevertheless like to hear from you)

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 01:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Great you read 20 books. Can you name which one you think is authentic?

As I can see your answers are based all on TVShows. But you would be surprised to know that they are not more authentic than BORI and ancient Neel kantha.

Perhaps you didn’t pay attention, but even these TVShows start with disclaimer that have take creative liberties, some of which includes creative freedom.

For example Karna Sangini is based on a fictional novel called Karna’s wife. There is absolutely no reference of Karna’s wife in original epic.

BR Chopra didn’t try to show Arjuna as a greater warrior. Here are some attempt to distort the true epic —

  • Drona’s rejection of Karna. Never happened. It was a cheap attempt to garner sympathy for Karna.
  • Draupadi calling Duryodhana Andhe ka Putra Andha — This story originated in a stage play Andha Yug created by famous author Dharma Veer Bharti. But doesn’t exist in Vyas Mahabharata.
  • Karna was drunk during Gandharva battle. It was BR Chopra’s to build excuse for his failure. NO authentic reference.
  • Karna stopping his arrow on 16th day. No authentic reference. Apply your common sense. Same Karna continued to fight at night on 14th days after Jaidradh’s death. Where was his eithics that day?

Now I would answer your two questions, in the hope that you may try to understand the truth.

About BORI and other ancient scripts

I am not sure what is your aversion to BORI and other authentic ancient scripts. But you are mistaken if you think, I am talking about just two books.

BORI CE is based over 40 years of research based on over 1259 ancient mansucripts from sanskrit, Nepali, Maithili, Benali, Southern and Sharda narrative. And that is way higher and accurate that twenty fictions you are talking about.

So weather you want to agree or not, don’t call BORI as just one book — it is the summary of scholary study of thousands of available manuscript and that is lot more than what you have read.

About Fictions and TVShows

What you prefer to believe are self proclaimed fictions that have taken creative liberties which would be evident if you compare them with each other — you would find a variation.

Many of these modern fictions have their foundation in BRChopra Mahabharata and each other and not in the authentic scripts.

For example, Andhe Ka Putra Andha was taken from Dharmaveer Bharti’s stage play — Andha Yuga.

Let me give another example — BR Chopra Mahabharata says Karna was drunk during Gandharva’s battle. But Suryaputra Karna doesn’t have Gandharva’s battle. So which one is true?

In BR Chopra Mahabharata Karna abused Druapadi in dice game, but in Suryaputra Karna, he tried to protect Draupadi. Who is correct? What does Karna Sangini says on that matter? You would find them contradicting each other.

Now many authors who don’t read authentic epic and still want to write a book, follow the TV Shows and say whatever they want to say. And this number will continue to rise. Today it is 20, tomorrow it may well be 100. But does this number matter? Do they have their research?

Take example of Ajaya. Author himself says he wrote an anti-hero book. Naturally he would distort the facts. Can you trust them?

Why would you have so much respect for books and TV Shows that themselves don’t claim authenticity and so little for those compiled by scholars after so many years of research? It always beats me!

If you really want to read a more researched fiction, I would recommend, read mine and that may help you understand the epic more clearly.

Think! Why was Krishna happy that Karna would die. Why did he support Arjuna? Was he biased or as he mentioned he incarnated to destroy evils and protect the good.

And since you accepted your references are based on non-verifiable source, there is no point in continuing the debate.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 06:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I never said Karna didn’t defeat anyone. He did defeat many, got defeated by many. That simply implies he was powerful. Doesn’t prove he was one the greatest.

You are misinformed if you think Karna was defeated only by Arjuna. He was badly defeated by Gandharvas, and Abhimanyu.

With Satyaki and Bheema he had a fierce battle where both defeated each other on multiple occasion and we can’t decide who finally won.

Now your questions about —

Bhishma

Bhishma declared his intentions of not killing Pandava brothers even before the battle started. He didn’t lie to Duryodhana. He had a blueprint of defeating Pandavas without killing them by destroying his army.

Drona

Tried his best to destroy Pandavas. He also did high damage — It was his plan that got Abhimanyu killed. He killed Drupad and Virata king. He continued the battle well into the night on the 14th night.

Neither of them lied to Duryodhana. There were always honest.

Karna

Did Karna declared his intention to Duryodhana ever? Can you see the difference?

It was Bheema’s oath to kill Duryodhana and Kaurava brothers. Arjuna didn’t kill any of them. So killing Bheema was more crucial than anyone. And anyway Karna had planned to kill Arjuna. Had he killed all of them, he could have saved Kauravas.

You say result would be same. WRONG.

Let me give you a scenario. Karna could have defeated and captured Yudhishthira and Bhima. Arjuna would never have killed Duryodhana while Bhima lived on. Hell he couldn’t have fought with his two brothers held in ransom. With the King Yudhishthira captured, the war would have ended and Kauravas would have won.He never promised Kunti that he wouldn’t capture them. He had only promised he won’t kill them. So why didn’t Karna captured them?

Think! And you would get the true reason!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 06:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No. I never said he never defeated anyone.

Here is my official stand — Karna was a strong and skilled archer trained by Drona, Kripacharya and Parshurama. He had defeated many rulers during his lifetime.

He had many defeats to blemish his career. Apart from Arjuna he was badly defeated by Gandharvas and Abhimanyu. He was also defeated by Satyaki and Bheema on several occasions although he also defeated Bhima and Satyaki in return.

But none of his feat made him a contender for the greatest warrior of the era — not even close — because of his lack of courage and a tendency to running away from the battles. This was pointed out on many occasions by Bhishma, Drona and Krishna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 06:33 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — No but nowhere it's written that karna went to hell. Do you have any evidence.

Your question proves that you haven’t read the epic. When Yudhishthira arrived at the hell to meet his brothers and Draupadi, Karna was still languishing in the hell. They all got freed from hell together. So Karna actually languished there together. Don’t worry, I am sharing the exact reference below after answering your other question.

You said — I don’t think lord Shiva would be lying.

No. Shiva doesn’t lie. But then Shiva praised Arjuna in a more glorified term. He called him along with Krishna as great souled and the creator of the universe. So how come Arjuna too was present in hell?

Well you would have understood the answer if you had bothered to read the epic more than just selective answers on quora.

Never mind, here is the exact extract from Swargarohan Parva

Swargarohan Parva

1.When Yudhisthira reaches Swarga he find Duryodhana present there alone

Important to note — Duryodhana has attained the swarga, but not Karna. Yudhishthira asks about Karna explicitly as he know Karna is his brother —

“Duryodhana was ignorant about dharma. He was wicked and destroyed the earth and his well-wishers. Yet, he has obtained the eternal world meant for heroes. It is because of him that the earth was destroyed, with its horses, chariots and elephants. It is because of him that we burnt with rage and were goaded towards enmity. My brave brothers were great-souled and great in their vows. They were valiant and truthful in speech and abided by truthful pledges. Which are the worlds they have obtained? I desire to see those. What about the great-souled Karna Kounteya, devoted to the truth?

—Swargarohan Parva

As you can see Karna is not in swarga.

Here is how Narada explained Duryodhana’s presence is Swarga —

“It is not like that. O Indra among kings! For those who reside in heaven, all enmity is destroyed.

Again note, no where it is said that their evil acts were not evil. It simply said that they don’t matter on heaven.

Where was Karna?

Here is the description of the hell —

There were many such distressed words, full of pain. O king! In that spot, he heard such voices from every direction. Hearing their words, he was distressed and filled with compassion. Yudhishthira remained there and exclaimed, “This is terrible suffering.” He heard those words repeatedly and these seemed to be voices he had heard before. They were full of pain and suffering, but Pandava did not recognize them. ‘Unable to understand, Yudhishthira, Dharma’s son, spoke these words. “Who are you and why are you here?” Having been thus addressed, all of them replied from every direction. “O lord! I am Karna. I am Bhimasena. I am Arjuna. I am Nakula. I am Sahadeva. I am Dhrishtadyumna. I am Droupadi. We are Droupadi’s sons.” These were the voices that were raised. O king! In that place, he heard such words. The king wondered, “What kind of contrary destiny is this? What tainted deeds have these great-souled ones, Karna, Droupadi’s sons and the slender-waisted Panchali, performed?

I believe now you got the evidence you were asking — Karna was in the hell along with Pandavas, but for 34 years longer.

So what is this mystery? Was Shiva wrong? NO. here is the exact answer of why Duryodhana was in heaven and others in hell —

‘Shakra, lord of the gods, was extremely prosperous. He comforted Yudhishthira and spoke these words to him. ‘O Yudhishthira! O mighty-armed one! The large numbers of gods are delighted with you. Come. O tiger among men! Come. O lord! You have obtained success. O king! You have obtained the eternal worlds. You should not have any anger. Listen to my words. O son!3 There is no doubt that all the kings must see hell. O bull among men! There are accumulations of good and bad deeds. A person who enjoys the good deeds earlier, goes to hell later. A person who goes to hell first, enjoys heaven later. A person who has committed many wicked deeds may enjoy heaven first.

  • Everyone sees heaven and hell.
  • A wicked person like Duryodhana may enjoy heaven for a short while and the live in eternal hell.
  • A person may go to hell first and then enjoy heaven.
  • Karna languished in hell for 34 years and Pandavas more a little while.

Guess that should answer all your doubts. If you still don’t understand, feel free to ask instead of trusting selective references which are factually incorrect and thinking Krishna sided with evils.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 08:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Once soul leaves the mortal body, it becomes part of the divinity — Parmatma. Sould in never impure — it is the body, mind and desire that makes it impure.

Those who are afflicted with ambition, lust and greed and ignore their soul are deemed evil in this world.

But there is no evil equal or parallel to God. So in the end they all merge to same divinity. This is also underlined in Gita.

That doesn’t mean they weren’t evil in their mortal existence.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2021 08:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You ‘but’ proves that you realized your mistake but still don’t want to admit it.

Even Duryodhana got to enjoy the fruits of his good deeds.

Every moment in hell is extremely tormenting as explained in the epic and imagine Karna stayed there for such a long time.

Before I answer the question, why did Karna got heaven, we must realize two things —

  • Yudhshithir never accepted the reason as to why Duryodhana should get heaven after all his misdeeds.
  • Neither Narada nor Indra disputed their deeds in as mortals. They were evil.
  • Krishna categorically called Karna evil and his evil acts are described in great deal in epic.

So why did Karna eventually got heaven —

  • Karna had done some good deeds in his life in form of donations to the poor. Just like for his evil deeds, he was punished, his good deeds too have to be rewarded.
  • He languished there for several decades.
  • Yudhishthira wasn’t willing to leave his brothers and family behind. And he considered Karna as his family, he wasn’t ready to leave him.
  • Gods put Yudhishthira in high esteem and relented to his demands as he couldn’t be punished and allowed to languish in hell.
  • As It is said in Gita, soul when enter mortal realm and unites with body, desire, lust, mind becomes impure. Once it is purified it is part of eternal parambrahma.
  • Karna was part of sun God. His evil actions needed to be purged. Once they were purged the soul had to return to infinite energetic Sun.
  • Duryodhana’s soul too, after a longer time in hell would eventually merge into divinity.

None of these things prove Karna wasn’t evil. And such a longer existence in hell certainly proves otherwise.

What I am not sure is why are trying to prove Karna to be a good person —

  • Didn’t he try to throw Draupadi in fire?
  • Didn’t he abused Draupadi during dyut shabha?
  • Didn’t he want to mock Drauapdi and Pandavas in exile?
  • Didn’t he leave his best friend and family behind and flee?
  • Wasn’t he part of cowardly killing of Abhimanyu?
  • Was he not the advisor or Duryodhana for rejecting Krishna’s peace proposal and an attempt to arrest him?
  • Did he not conspire against Pandavas?

Now tell me what are good deeds of Karna —

  • Did he do anything for the welfare of the society?
  • Did he protect a woman in distress whom others tried to burn?
  • Did he try to prevent the molestation of a woman in pubic?
  • Did he try to protect a warrior in distress whom others were trying to kill unethically?
  • Did he help Gods in their endeavor?
  • Did he try to prevent an apocalyptic war for the humanity?
  • Did he perform any penance to please any higher God with his caliber?

You only quote what someone said without validating the context. Can you give any evidence also of what actually happened?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 11, 2021 01:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,
