The problem as I repeated several times is you think fictional work higher than authentic works. What is the foundation of those non-authentic works?

You said — Bori might have some authentic parts and is based onnthe research and it may be mpre authentic than other

BORI is more authentic than the books your refer and not in just some parts. And I am not talking just about BORI. Other ancient editions such as Neelkantha, Sarda editin, Southern edition, they all say the same thing.

Where as the books/tv show you refer are self professed fictions. They don’t have the research. And they mostly refer each other to build narrative.

You said — The things which is i have is based on one of my school books also it had also the same written and ut was authentic

Was that a history book? NO. In India Ramayana and Mahabharata are not taught in history. What you may have read at best, is a chapter in your hindi/english/language papers. And you want to call that more authentic than researched works!

By the way didn’t you read the story of Panchatantra and the drama of william Shakespaer in you school? Does that make them authentic too? Think!

You said — I forgot the writers name of the book it just told karna lost on virat war and he lost to gandharvas.

Tell me about the book that you read with unknown title and by unknown author — how many pages and chapter it had? Gita Press Edition of Mahabharata has more than 6 volume each with over 400 pages. Do you think your book may have covered every part of Mahabharata? What did it say about Abhimanyu’s death?

You said — In Draupadi’s incident he abused her as she always used to call her sutputra

It is useless to explain you that Draupadi didn’t stop Karna from participation in swyamver and all authentic versions show Karna failed at the swaymver.

So let me ask you this — Let us assume Druapadi didn’t want to marry Karna. Was that not her right? You say she called her Sutaputra. Was he not a sutaputra? Is calling a Sutaputra, sutaputra an abuse? Even Dhritrashtra and Duryodhana called him Sutaputra.

Now you say she always used to call him sutaputra. What do you mean always? Were they friends? Were the neighbours? On how many occasion do you think Draupadi and Karna met before dice game?

And by your logic, am I correct to believe that you justify and respect acid-throwers who deface a girl because she rejected their advances? Are you ok with such people? Please clarify!

You said — it never mentioned arjuna victory on 16th day or his defeat over satyaki bheem other pandavas it showed the unmeaningful killing of abhimanyu by karna and others and their night atack on the call pf shakuni but it didnt mentioned even once that karna be freinded duryodhana at dronsabha or drona accepted to consider him as a student it never showed karna failed to ring the bowstring and he was defeated at the draupadi syambar

Isn’t it a good time to ask yourself, why do you trust a story which doesn’t cover most important part of the epic?

You said — You think reading bori c.e. and neelkanth is worthy, read kmg if yu have i shall tell yu that many of the fcts in bori are not there .

Ha…Ha…Ha... Funny! Do you even know what is KMG? KMG is short for Mahabharata translation by Kisari Mohan Ganguli. It was the first English translation of Mahabharata. Do you know which Sanskrit edition he translated — Neelkantha!

So yes, I have read both Hindi-Sanskrit version of Neelkantha and KMG Mahabharata.

So tell me what do you want to tell me from KMG? I would love to hear.

TV Shows

No comments. Even they know they are fictional. But if you want to know about TV Serials here is a comparision exposing their fake narratives — Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Some people claims on social media that Karna is overrated and backs up their claims by advicing to read original Mahabharata. Is Karna actually overrated?

I think this answer is explicitly for people like you. Maybe it can help you see the truth.

You said — Leave it yu keep on studyi g yur bori neelkanth and try to be in suspense in those books researched over 40 years i shall read my own ok good and b.. Bye

Ya Bye. I would stay with authentic narrative rather than baseless fables trying to abuse our epic. Can’t force you to change your mind. But my effort to weed out distortion would continue. May be someday you would understand it too…

You said — karna was too good to be arjuna

I can never understand how someone would say and abuser of a woman as a good person and some one who fought to protect them as a defeated warrior.

Indra came to Karna to take the Kavacha and gave him divine weapon in return. But same Indra came to Arjuna for protection from demons.

And God of Gods Shiva blessed Arjuna. Not Karna.

Krishna became a friend and a charioteer for Arjuna and never for Karna even if Suryaputra Karna tries to show a fake narrative. Did you see any such thing is BR Chopra or start plus Mahabharata?

Krishna wanted Karna dead. He could have done anything to protect Arjuna. Does that tell you who was good and who was bad.

Most important, it was Karna who wanted to prove himself. — Arjuna was a milestone for him. Arjuna never compared himself to Karna. He didn’t need to prove himself.

Believe whatever you want, but then don’t trust everything you believe

—Vivek Dutta Mishra

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 11, 2021 03:19 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — it is because of his greateness Indra had to give some thing.

This shows you haven’t read the epic but influenced by TVShows. Had you read the epic, you would have known that Karna explcitly asked for exchange and refused to give up his Kavacha and Kundala without exchange. And yes, none of the above five has done that — exchange!

And since you don’t know the epic, there is no point in debating with you.

But yes, you are right — I don’t like evils. But I don’t need to search for his mistakes that are abundant and you need to close your eyes in order to not see it.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2021 12:25 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Even God respected his mother which in a way was his creation as he was God.

But Karna’s mother was her creator and ultimate protector. Without her there would have been no Kavacha and Kundala! Karna should have shown higher respect to his mother.

Ved Vyas too didn’t resent his mother leaving him! Why was Karna the only exception?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2021 12:28 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Bye.

Not millions of years ago. Around five thousand years. But you are right. It is a long time for manuscript to exist. That is why we have Sruti - Smriti - Samhita — more reliable that manuscript.

You don’t want to respect 40 years of research… your choice.

You don’t want to respect the references materials of that 40 years of research, that is over thousands manuscripts written several centuries ago in different language, regions and religions… your choice.

You don’t want to trust Krishna and Gita… your choice.

But what overwhelms me is your other choice —

  • trusting what is self declared fiction!
  • trusting a tale which is hardly 5 decades old and known to be creative imagination.
  • trusting works which are more copied from each other than authentic epic!

But at the end of the day, it is your choice.

To conclude, I won’t respond to any of the point your raised in your last answer.

So, yes, Bye.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2021 12:39 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree.

Yet the defeat was by Arjuna parameter only… He couldn’t save all the dependents. But important point to note —

  • He didn’t flee out of fear. Many so called great warriors would have fled leaving everyone behind.
  • It was robbers who fled. Had Arjuna fled he won’t have been able to save everyone.
  • He was protecting many of those people who were not his responsibility. He saved them on humanitarian ground.

  3. Can act as bridge between original but lengthy epic and common people

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-more-powerful-Drona-or-Bhisma/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 15, 2021 11:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don’t see how your comment is relevant to either the question or the answer.

And while we are talking about the original epic Mahabharata, it contains an extremely important line —

Paritranaya Sadhunama, Vinashchyas Durskritam, Dharma Sansthamnarathya Sambhavi Yugu-Yuge.

It means — To protect the good, to destroy the evil and to establish the Dharma, I shall incarnate again and again.

This proclamation isn’t a latter addon or a twisted story, but the essence of the epic — While everyone can make a mistake — there were good people and bad people. It also emphasizes that even God helps those who are good and righteous.

And the narrative of epic is remphasis on another Sanatana wisdom — Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah!

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 20, 2021 10:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why didn’t you ever act on your advise? Read. Don’t just watch TV Shows. And when you would read, you would find —

  • Krishna, Drona, Bhishma, Parshurama, Kripacharya, Ashwatthama everyone called Karna evil or wicked at some point.
  • There are numerous incident to prove that.
  • Karna languished in hell till the time Yudhishthira freed him, that was more than three decades later.
  • Even Duryodhana attained Swarga — that doesn’t make him lesser evil.

But since you find Krishna doing wrong and Karna right, there isn’t much to discuss here.

Creation time: Jun 20, 2021 12:11 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

This is a Star Plus Mahabharata reimagination and can’t compensate original reading.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karn-offer-his-kavach-kundal-free-of-cost-to-Indra-or-charged-something-in-return-with-strong-evidence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 20, 2021 12:12 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Please read lest you expose your ignorance. Neither was Karna invincible nor weaponless when he died. He was badly beaten on a number of occasions, not only by Arjuna but also by Gandharvas, Abhimanyu, Satyaki and Bheema.

You feel even God did wrong deed and don’t see anything wrong with Karna. Now argument.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karn-offer-his-kavach-kundal-free-of-cost-to-Indra-or-charged-something-in-return-with-strong-evidence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 20, 2021 12:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Where did you get this rubbish? Nothing like this happened.

Karna died fighting. He certainly begged Arjuna to give him time to take out the wheel. Krishna declined reminding him his sins (not punya).

He started fighting back. Fired Brahmastra and Varunastara and finally died. Period.

You know many warriors in Mahabharata fought without chariot — Abhimanyu, Bheema, Drona and Arjuna. None of them pleaded their enemy to wait. Karna could have fought from the ground.

And is there a point in saying read the epic?

Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 09:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

So for you onetime rapist is alright. That is your perception.

You think saying that he would sell his wife is just an expression. Again your perception. But which gentleman would make such an expression?

But for your information Karna isn’t being judged for his one time estray remark. I would recommend to read —

  • What he said further at that point.
  • What he said about women
  • Read Karna’s dialog with Shalya and you would know what he thought about woman.

But I value actions more than words —

  • Why did Karna attacked Arjuna after the swaymvara? Because Arjuna was trying to protect Draupadi from him and his friends who were keen on throwing Draupadi to fire. Alive. Does this sound noble to you?
  • Do you know what happened after Gandharva battle. Karna fled. Do you know not only he deserted his best friend Duryodhana but also women of the family. Was that a respect?

And please don’t compare Karna with Krishna or Harishchandra. Quoting an action without intent is distortion —

  • Krishna expressed his trust in Arjuna when he said he could give his wives and son’s to Arjuna. He knew Arjuna to be of pure intention. Interestingly After Krishna left the human plane, Arjuna did inherit that responsibility of protecting them. Can you say, Karna’s statement was with similar motive?
  • Read Harishchandra’s stories before commenting. He had to offer his wives and son to follow his Dharma. Not same as Karna.

Summary —

  • Karna in general held low opinion about women which is expressed during Swyamvara and with Shalya.
  • He did try to throw Draupadi in fire.
  • He provoked Dussassana to disrobe her.
  • He himself deserted his family women to save his life.

Now I don’t know how do I know what Karna meant by his clear statement about his wife and child.

But Karna’s action against Draupadi alone is sufficient to condemn the heinous man he was.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-not-an-exemplary-man-of-honor-that-he-promised-Kunti-that-he-would-not-kill-any-of-her-sons-except-Arjuna-and-kept-that-promise/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 09:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for you input. Agree with your points. And you have already added your point here.

I have said the same thing at many places. But adding here would be diverting from the key topic.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 10:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

History is written by winners. That is why we never wrote history.

Had Mahabharata been history —

  • They wouldn’t have included the chapter that Yudhisthira gambled away his brothers and wife.
  • They wouldn’t have included what you may call unfair death of Duryodhana.
  • That Arjuna couldn’t defeat and destroy all Dwarka robbers.

But there inclusion proves it is not something written by winner. You are blessed, you are born in India and not is some western world. Here we do worship even those whom history may not call victorious—

  • Maharana Pratap
  • Bhagat Singh
  • Chandrashekhar Azad
  • Prithvi Raj Chauhan

Now can you tell me how is your comment relevant to either this question or my answer?

Did you see the question is neither about Karna nor about Eklavaya. Yet you bring them here. I don’t see a reason. Yet I would try to answer your question.

You said — Eklavaya would have defeated Arjuna

It is just your view without any supporting evidence. Without a proper guru Eklavaya would never have obtained Divyastra. And not to forget he was killed by Krishna because he allied with evils.

You said — Drona unfairly made him chop his thumb.

Didn’t Drona declined to teach Eklavaya? Didn’t he impersonated himself as his disciple. We know Eklavaya sided with evil and that led to his killing by Krishna. Why would Drona allow such element to misuse his name? Do you think Indian Milletary Academy would allow anyone to use their name and become and Army officer? What punishment do you think they would get?

You said — Everyone was unfair to Karna

Can you explain how?

  • Karna got the most powerful protection, thanks to his mother, who he always condemned. Krishna too was abandoned by his mother and so was Ved Vyas. They never blamed their mother. They never disrespected them. So how is it everyone was unfair to Karna and not otherwise?
  • Karna became the disciple of three best gurus of his time. He was cursed by Parshurma because of his own deceit. So who was unfair here?
  • Indra did ask Karna’s Kavacha. But did he just give it away? No. He asked a fair price for his kavacha and was just too happy with the exchange. How is that unfair.
  • Krishna asked Karna to switch sides towards Dharma. He offered him everything. Karna refused. Why? Had he really thought about welfare of Duryodhana he would have accepted Krishna’s offer and prevented the war. If he wished he could have given it to Duryodhana. But all he cared was a fight with Arjuna that would not be possible without the war. So he went for the war. If someone was unfair here, it was Karna.

You said — Arjuna and Krishna very unfairly as they could not defeat him through fair means

Let us see —

  1. Arjuna defeated Karna during Draupadi’s swyamvara. Karna fled scared.
  2. Karna fled Gandharvas. Arjuna defeated them.
  3. Arjuna defeated Karna thrice during Matasya battle. He killed his brother and Karna ran scared.
  4. Arjuna defeated Karna on 14th day while he sought Jaidradh.
  5. Arjuna defeated Karna again on 16th day.
  6. Arjuna Killed Karna’s son in front of him and he could do nothing.

And yes, you were saying that Arjuna couldn’t have defeated Karna through fair means. Where did you get that? And can you tell me what was that unfair means by which Arjuna killed Karna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 10:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You must understand —

  • Bhagwat Gita discourse happen during the war and wasn’t a drawing room discussion to be noted down by a secretary.
  • Gita is ‘knowledge’ not a book. A written book is just one form of knowledge. Problem with written media is it is bound by physical artifact such as paper and ink which has limited life span.

We recorded this greater wisdom on the first and primarily neural network that ever existed — human brain. And we transferred this wisdom across generations. A written work is just an auxiliary backup for that wisdom.

Take an example — what are the contemporary forms of a book today? written, and audio. Right? What is audio book? someone reading it for you. Does that make it lesser than book. Now follow the chain of thought farther — we started with audio book.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Where-is-the-actual-original-written-Shrimad-Bhagwad-Geeta-kept/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 08:51 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry you comment was collapsed and couldn’t see it earlier. Remember, Gita discourse between Arjuna and Krishna was explained by Sanjay to Dhirtrashtra and Sanjay was blessed by Ved Vyas. That is why world knows it.

But important point to understand is — being written — isn’t the most significant part of the knowledge. If a knowledge exists it may be written by someone, memorized by other.

Take an example — what are the contemporary forms of a book today? written, and audio. Right? What is audio book? someone reading it for you. Does that make it lesser than book. Now follow the chain of thought farther — we started with audio book.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Where-is-the-actual-original-written-Shrimad-Bhagwad-Geeta-kept/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 08:58 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

God watch over everything.

Karna while certainly not at par with Arjuna was indeed an overhyped warrior, he was still a warrior of repute. Imagine that than in an examination topper secures 95% marks and every one else under 50%. So a person who secures 40% would most likely be in 90 percentile (not percent). He would still be considered one of the best in class. But does that mean he is at par (or even closer to the topper)?

Another important reason — Karna had a large number of divine weapons, he obtained by deceit from Bhagwan Parshurama and misuse of such weapons could lead to world destruction.

Imagine a small weak nation of terrorists somehow gets hold of nuclear weapon. Don’t you think that would make even the USA worried? Don’t you think the world would be watching? Don’t you think the world would be worried? Does that make that nation brave? No it simply makes them scary and irresponsible.

A divine weapon in an irresponsible hand is always a matter or worry. Remember this could lead to greater destruction as they have lesser resolve.

(sorry for a late reply. Was out of network for a long period)

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-fans-and-Arjun-fans-fight-each-other-who-will-win/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 09:06 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Is there a question here? I don’t think this question related to Arjuna. It is about Drona and Eklavya.

I would recommend value actions over words. There are so many superlative narratives (cheerful and devoted to truth) that has no evidence. Same cheerful and devoted to truth person was killed by Krishna because he sided with big bad evil Jarasandha.

Arjuna was a kid and he certainly was happy. That was the lowest point in Arjuna’s otherwise unblemished character. Arjuna never showed any envy or jealousy for anyone. And remember he was just a kid. And he never asked Drona for Eklavaya’s thumb!

Hope that answers your query.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-Dronachrya-take-the-thumb-of-Eklavya-and-did-unjustice/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2021 09:12 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

He had qualities of all five Pandavas! Where did you get that? Can you quote a single reference from the authentic epic that says so?

  • Was Karna truthful like Yudhishthira? Did he try to save distressed women like Yudhishthira did? As I recall, Karna was cursed for being deceitful and he fled leaving his friend and distressed women behind. He also was the prime instigator in the molestation of Draupadi.
  • Was Karna a brother like Bhima? Did he fulfil his oath like Bhima? Did he rush like Bhima to protect distressed village from Bakasura? Did he do anything for the welfare of anyone other than his own selfish interest and his friend?
  • Was Karna an archer like Arjuna? Did he please Shiva by his persistence? Did he kill millions of demons? Who won every single time the two clashed? Who won against Gandharvas and who fled?
  • Was Karna obedient to his mother like Nakula or Sahdeva? Did he fight for Dharma? Did he suffered like any Pandavas?

You would have your answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/All-astra-including-Sudarshan-chakra-Brahmastra-were-totally-INCAPABLE-of-penetrating-armour-of-Karna-How-could-he-cut-his-armour-with-normal-knife-1/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 12, 2021 08:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your theories are, unfortunately, not backed by the epic references.

  1. God’s are not blind to chose the wrong person. Arjuna wasn’t lucky, but worthy. That is why Krishna sided him. More important, God don’t chose you, you chose God. Krishna approached Karna too. But he rejected God. He tried to imprision him. Arjuna chose him over a prosperous Army.
  2. Karna didn’t side with Duryodhana on the day of talent showcase, but from his gurukul days where he sided with Duryodhana to plot against Pandavas. Why did Karna interfered in the talent showcase that was to exclusively showcase the talent of Kuru Princess? It wasn’t an open show. It was Karna’s ego.
  3. Karna didn’t just chose wrong side. He was WRONG. It was he who instigated Duryodhana to disrobe Draupadi. It was he who instigated Duryodhana to mock Pandavas in exile leading to Gandharva war fiasco. You are right, these qualities were non-existent in other Pandavas.
  4. If Karna really had considered his duty to side with Duryodhana —
    1. He wouldn’t have fled Gandharvas leaving Duryodhana and family women behind.
    2. He wouldn’t have spared the life of Bhima and Yudhishthira. Kunti had never asked for it.
    3. He wouldn’t have abstained from the war for first ten days because he felt humiliated by Bhishma.

I agree with you not all heros wear victor’s crown. Abhimanyu is an excellent example, who lost at the hand of heinous coward hyenas. But now we have a reverse trend — villians like Raavan, Karna and Meghnad are being touted as heros for no reason.

Can you tell me what make Karna a hero —

  • Did he do anything for the welfare of the society?
  • Did he save a women from a mad crowd who wanted to throw her in fire (He was part of that crowd). Arjuna did try to save Draupadi.
  • Did he save a women in distress or tried to disrobe her? Pandavas certainly did it on multiple occasions.
  • Did he save a village from cannibal monster? Bhima certainly did it.
  • Did he help Gods? Arjuna certainly did it.
  • Did he try to prevent an apoloclyptic war when he had the opportunity? All Pandavas tried for it. Arjuna put down his bows.
  • Did he win favor of any God? Yudhishtira, Bhima and Arjuna did it by proving their worth and not because they were son of some God. Arjuna please Shiva himself.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2021 10:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It is true that Karna’s Kundal were made up of Amrita and that made un-killable not invincible.

You can’t die doesn’t mean you can’t be defeated. There are sufficient indication that he was pierced despite of Kavacha and Kundala. Karna was defeated on multiple occasions.

  1. After Draupadi’s swyamvara, Karna was part of the mob that tried to burn down Draupadi. Here Arjuna came forward to protect Draupadi and Karna Arjuna had a face-off. After a fierce battle Karna retreated “scared”. He had his Kavacha and Kundala. You can read detailed answer here — The First Arjuna-Karna Battle — Draupadi’s Swyamvara Part 2
  2. Karna proposed to mock Pandava’s in exile. Duryodhana and team went with a large army and family women to mock Pandavas. Here they got intercepted by Gandharvas and after a fierce battle, Karna fled the scene leaving Duryodhana and everyone behind. He later concedes that he fled after getting badly mangled by arrows.

Both these incidents prove —

  • A mighty artifact doesn’t make a mighty warrior. It can protect your life but doesn’t make you fearless.
  • You can lose if you lack moral courage.
  • Karna was defeated and mangled on multiple occasion.

Do you know the story of Ketu? He too had consumed Amrita. Now he lives on just as a head without body. Karna too could have same faith.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/All-astra-including-Sudarshan-chakra-Brahmastra-were-totally-INCAPABLE-of-penetrating-armour-of-Karna-How-could-he-cut-his-armour-with-normal-knife-1/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2021 10:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Mahabharata may appear complex but can be simplified by considering two simple issues —

  1. What is the core message of the epic? Who were good side and who ever evil side.
  2. Focusing on actions rather than just words. If you see, contradictions are present more in words than actions. Few people may be praised in superlative terms but there is no evidence to prove that. An example would be Krishna calling Shalya more powerful than Bhishma, Karna and Arjuna and saying that only Yudhishthira could defeat him. But there is no action supporting it. Second would be, even the non important warriors being named as Indra among men.

If we consider just these two factors, Mahabharata won’t be complex.

P.S. There is a third fan base — fan of Mahabharata the original epic. Interestingly, it tends to side with one of the fan bases.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-Arjuna-fans-always-say-that-Karna-broke-Abhimanyu-s-bow-from-behind-but-in-reality-Arjun-cut-Bhurishravas-hand-from-behind/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2021 11:11 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Great advise. In fact I did read Mahabharata. And both my facts and fables are aligned with the epic.

Just wondering why don’t you take your own advise?

If you read the original epic —

  • There you would find, Karna to be the trunk of a tree that is the evil Duryodhana.
  • Krishna and Shiva willed him be killed.

And you will miss those fables like —

  • Karna pushing Krishna’s chariot back
  • Karna donating his tooth at his death bed. There was no death bed. He died beheaded.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2021 11:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Well, yes, I know what is confidence. It is something that can’t be taken away by someone unless the confidence is baseless. Karna’s confidence decreased because he didn’t trust himself sufficiently.

You asked — Will you fight under someone’s command if he insults you.

Depends on what is more valuable for me — my friend or my ego. Duryodhana always insulted Pandavas. Yet they fought for his release.

You said — Everyone values his life dearly.

True. But for few, their Dharma preceeds their love for their own life. Did Abhimanyu flee out of fear? Did Duryodhana flee Gandharvaras? Why do you think, Bhima fought Bakasura? Was there no risk to his life?

You asked — Why did arjuna send ghatotkach to fight with karN? He planned to make karna use vasavi on ghatotkach?

You really haven’t read the epic. Have you? Can you show me a single reference where Arjuna sent Ghatotkatch to fight Karna? Can you show one reference that Arjuna planned to make Karna use Vasvi Shakti on Ghatotkatch?

Where do you people read such garbage?

You said — ULL never tell about the swaymvar of kalinga princess when karna helped his friend.

Is there something to be proud of here? Abducting a princess against her will? By the way which great adversary did Karna faced on this occasion.

You said — never tell about the incidents when Karna supported duryodhan.

You are WRONG! I always mention incidents where Karna supported Duryodhana —

  • He supported Duryodhana in many conspiracies against Pandavas.
  • He helped Duryodhana during Lakshagriha incident.
  • He helped Duryodhana in trying to burn Draupadi alive after her swyamvara.
  • He instigated Duhsasana in molesting Draupadi.

Most of these attempts failed. But we can’t deny that Karna helped Duryodhana in all his evil misdeeds. The same is confirmed by Krishna.

But tell me how does the fact that Karna helped Duryodhana negates that he deserted him on multiple occasions?

You said — Bhima couldnt even scratch duryodhN TILL karna was alive.

Define scratch.

All his brothers were killed in front of Karna and he couldn’t save any of them. Many of them died while trying to protect Karna from Bhima.

It is amusing to see you still carry such high image of Karna that you define his worst failure as his success!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 15, 2021 08:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I generally don’t value statements that are not backed by hard evidence. It may be personal opinion of Bhishma. But there is not a single evidence to prove Ashwatthama to be such a superior warrior. After the night massacre, Ashwatthama hid out of fear of Arjuna.

During entire Mahabharata there is not a single occasion where we see if better than everyone.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 15, 2021 09:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Where did you get this idea?

There is not a single reference that —

  • Pandavas wanted to kill anyone as a camouflage for their escape. They intended to leave six bodies not kill six person.
  • It is clearly mentioned that the Nishada’s reached their by destiny.
  • They were supposed to return back but got intoxicated and lsot their senses. Pandavas couldn’t have hatched a conspiracy based on destiny and fate.
  • There is not a single reference that they were aware of their presence during their escape.
  • Bhima made sure Purchana is dead. But he didn’t try to ensure that Nishada’s are dead. He should have made sure of that if it was such an important thing.
  • Later Yudhishtira is worried if Purchana died or not. He doesn’t wonder the same about Nishadas. Why?
  • No one in the entire epic ever blame Pandavas for killing Nishandas.

On one hand there is absolutely no evidence to prove that Pandavas intentionally killed Nishadas on the other hand there is plenty of evidence.

Yet few people spread fake propaganda. Don’t be fooled.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Though-Arjun-killed-Karna-unfairly-who-was-fighting-in-a-better-position-in-the-17th-day-of-the-war/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 15, 2021 10:03 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

True. Because Shalya wasn’t important enough. Anyone could have killed him. During Draupadi’s swyamvara Bhima had defeated him and thrown him on the ground.

Krishna’s remarks were to entice enterprise in Yudhishthira. I guess it was to give Yudhishthira a credit as warrior.

There is no reference other than Krishna’s word that suggests he was more powerful than Bhishma or Karna. Had he been so, why was he not chosen as the commander in chief in the first place?

All these suggests that he was powerful and renowned but not as powerful as Krishna said while encouraging Yudhishthira. Krishna wanted Yudhishthira to feel important.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-Arjuna-fans-always-say-that-Karna-broke-Abhimanyu-s-bow-from-behind-but-in-reality-Arjun-cut-Bhurishravas-hand-from-behind/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 18, 2021 09:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your problem is you think B.R.Chopra is the original author of Mahabharata and based you answer completely on B.R.Chopra+Fables.

If, however, you care to go through original Ved Vyas epic —

Arjuna

  • It doesn’t show Arjuna as arrogant anywhere.
  • Arjuna doesn’t have time to bother about Karna.
  • Arjuna is the only person to have pleased Lord Shiva and Krishna. This alone make him more underrated than whatever depiction you can imagine.
  • It was Arjuna who saved Duryodhana from Gandharvas when his best friend fled saving his own ass.

Draupadi

  • Draupadi never insulted Karna or his evil friend Duryodhana. Those myth were propagated later.
  • Draupadi didn’t call Karna Andhe Ka Putra Andha.
  • It was Karna and Duryodhana who tried to throw her to fire after her swaymvara.
  • It was Karna who instigated Duhssasana to disrobe her.
  • It was Karna who suggested Duryodhana to go to forest to see Arjuna and Draupadi in tree barks.
  • Draupadi had right to resent her humiliations in the hand of sinners and evil. And the epic doesn’t show her clinging to revenge through out the epic. In fact she agreed for peace and finally forgave Ashwatthama and Dhritrashtra.

The Evil Duo (Karna and Duryodhana)

In the first chapter of Mahabahrata Adi Parva, Duryodhana had been mentioned as an evil tree, with Karna as it’s trunk.

From the gurukul days the two friends conspired against Pandavas trying to kill them (especially Bhima) various ways including Lakshagriha.

Fakes

  • There is no Bhanumati beads incident in original Mahabahrata, on which basis you try to glorify Duryodhana
  • You said that Karna always opposed every treachery of Shakuni and always called them bad. WRONG! Karna was part of every single treachery. In fact most heinous of them were masterminded by him.

Now unless you want to call Ved Vyas, Shiva and Krishna stupid, Duryodhana was evil and Arjuna was the Hero not overrated.

If you hope people to see the truth, stop spreading the fakes based on TV Shows.

If you want to contradict my points do so with references.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Draupadi-an-overrated-character-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Teranath-Venkitan
Creation time: Jul 18, 2021 09:38 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If you want to quote Duhsasana as an authority, it would be your choice. For me the words of a monster doesn’t matter. He was not an authority of anything. It is clearly the statement of a frustrated loser.

And I would recommend read the chapter clearly. Vaisampanya had already explained the dialog between Krishna and Balarama earlier where Krishna explains Arjuna is the Brahamana. He had also used the name “Arjuna” as he explained his success in swyamver.

Please don’t misinterpret the epic — Karna fought his battle vigorously with all his might wishing to vanquish his opponent and then retreated scared.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-bori-ce-Did-Krishna-say-that-no-one-in-this-3-world-can-defeat-Karna-If-he-said-then-why/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 22, 2021 06:39 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I have heard this doppleganger theory before, however, it is flawed on many instances —

  • If Anga was part of Hastinapur then Duryodhana made Karna a regent and not a King. By that rule even after becoming a King Karna had no right to fight the battle against Hastinapur Prince Arjuna. In reality Anga belonged to Hastinapur till Duryodhana made it an independent kingdom under a new ruler. They had pact and alliance but it wasn’t under Hastinapur. It is no where mentioned in the epic. Pandavas didn’t attack Hastinapur but they did Anga.
  • It is absurd to think that there were two namesake kingdoms, Anga, that were ruled by namesake King called Karna. It is not mentioned anywhere in the epic. There had been different Kings of Anga at different point of time and one of them was Karna which is quite common — names your children after great people. BORI has given footnote at every point where such confusion exists. It doesn’t mention here.

To make your theory sustainable the only option is some other Karna defeated your Karna and started ruling Anga, and Bhima defeated the Karna who defeated your Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-bori-ce-Did-Krishna-say-that-no-one-in-this-3-world-can-defeat-Karna-If-he-said-then-why/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 22, 2021 06:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I infer from the epic and have also described in my book The Accursed God, the celestial weapons were not as important for their destructive nature as for their controlled destruction. More power the weapon, more precise the target. A malfunction and it can be more destructive. With proper use Ashwatthama managed to destroy the child in Subhadra’s womb without hurting her!

To answer your question, Shiva himself confirmed no one knew the exact power of Pashupata — not even Gods or humans — clearly excluding even Drona and Parshurama. Even Arjuna couldn’t have known it precisely. So Arjuna asked for Brahmasira and God Shiva’s version of the weapon — Pashupata — the most potent of all celestial weapons.

Answer: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/I-have-heard-that-karna-had-pashupatastra-He-also-possesed-brahmashira-and-vaishnavastra-as-said-in-the-internet-so-I-t-1
Creation time: Jul 22, 2021 07:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Very nice answer. bookmarked.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-there-a-mention-of-Lord-Rama-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Sailesh-Raut-1
Creation time: Jul 22, 2021 09:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna used every celestial weapon he had access to, during his lifetime using Brahmastra, Varunastra Bhargavastra and Vasvi shakti.

The term of use for Vasvishakti was it must be the last weapon at your disposal. The use of Bhargavastra against ordinary soldiers must be deemed a misuse.

You asked - why Karna didn't use celestial weapons against Gandharvas?

You need to focus to manifest celestial weapons, and Karna was too afraid to focus. Can you tell me what would be a good use for celestial weapon than to save the lives of your dear ones and your own life? So why do you think he didn't use it?

I agree not everything can be achieved by deceit. Alas, Karna didn't know that.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Aug 07, 2021 12:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Which part of your question should I answer first?

Karna’s last moment as per BORI and KMG

According to both the versions —

  • Karna’s chariot stuck on ground.
  • Neither says Karna was weaponless.
  • Abhimanyu, Bhima, Satyaki, Drona, Arjuna all fought war without their chariot. None of them complained. Why should there be an exception here?

Even in KMG, Karna and Arjuna were fighting when Karna thought Arjuna is unconscious and jumped to pull out his chariot’s wheel. That was a tactical error of judgement by Karna. He should have concentrated on the battle rather than working on his chariot wheel. He was in the middle of the battle and you can’t blame the other warrior for attacking back.

I am not going for BORI here which clearly states Karna died fighting, though I would like to believe that at least once Karna fought bravely.

The Three power Army general of Pandavas

Interestingly all the three powerful generals were present during Goharana episode. Along with them was a great army. And all of them were vanquished by a lone Arjuna.

Two of those great warriors fought for the first ten days without much success.

The second and the third powerful general fought against Abhimanyu and Arjuna on day 13 and 14 and clearly showed how powerful they were against their enemies.

Why couldn’t Arjuna kill them

You need to understand the epic and the commentary of the battle to understand the reason.

Bhishma

Why did Arjuna gave up his weapon on the very first day? Why did Krishna attacked Bhishma on day 3 and day 9? Because Arjuna wasn’t keen on fetal attack against his grandfather or guru. He could never muster that ruthlessness — neither for victory nor his duty.

All he agreed on is to make him fell down and that he did with great reluctance.

If you want to point out Arjuna attacked Bhishma unfairly, you must read the epic properly. It wasn’t Arjuna Shikhandi attacking a long Bhishma. It was a group to group attack something started by Bhishma himself on earlier occasions.

Drona

Arjuna doesn’t attack Drona vigorously even after the death of Abhimanyu. Not only this he tried to stop Dhristduymna from killing him. He knew is Drona’s gory act in killing Abhimanyu. He knew his plot against Yudhishthira. But he won’t kill his guru. Period.

Karna

Through out Mahabharata Arjuna attitude towards Karna was he couldn’t care less. Only once during the talent showcase he said he would defeat Karna for his impudence.

He spares Karna during swyamvara battle and lets him retreat. He let him escape thrice during Matasya battle. Even after Duryodhana’s killing he takes an oath to kill Jaidradh. He doesn’t care much about Karna. He tells Karna on day 14 that for the insult of Bhima and killing of Abhimanyu he would kill his son in front of his eyes. And he does that without much fuss.

I don’t see the power in those powerful warriors beyond few spoken words. At least none in the action.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Aug 07, 2021 12:41 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Why would we Indians be indebted to Karna for refusing Krishna’s offer?

Do you think we are descendants of Duryodhana? Or do you think Indians favour evils? Do you think Indian’s don’t know Ved Vyas called Karna the trunk of tree that was evil Duryodhana? Do you think we don’t know whom Ved Vyas named as hero of epic and who he named the villain? Do you think we don’t know whom Krishna and Shiva willed to die? Do you think we don’t know who conspired to imprision Krishna when he tried to bring peace?

I would recommend, better speak for yourself and don’t try to be spokesman for all Indians — we follow Krishna not some evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 08, 2021 01:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

First NO. I can’t speak for all Indians. No one can. No one should think they can.

And I agree with you when you say not all Indians harbor same belief as I do. But then you must agree, not all Indians have read the epic. Many follow the garbage like Suryaputra Karna and flawed edition of recitations that have distorted the epic.

But every Indian who cares to read even the first chapter of Adi Parva, would find him mentioned as trunk of tree that was evil Duryodhana. Then throughout the epic his misdeeds are described by people like Krishna, Bhishma, Drona, Ashwatthama and behold Karna himself.

Most important anyone who would care to understand the essence of Mahabharata would know it puts Krishna and Arjuna in high esteem.

Alas, many Indians don’t know about it and don’t carry the same believe as I do.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 08, 2021 08:53 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ok, you have proved that neither have you read the epic not Quora and while asking me to be gentle towards those trying to distort our epic, you are mounting personal attack on me without any reason. That is hypocrisy!

Mahabharata is NOT a narrative of fifty shades of gray. Lord Krishna didn’t stand for “No Good People”. Our epic has an essential message — Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah — He who protects Dharma is protected by it. So either you failed to understand the epic or disbelive Krishna.

Second, Every Quora question has a question log that includes the name of the person who asked the question. I am sure if you had know this simple fact you would have checked the log before mounting personal attack on me.

I AM NOT THE ORIGINAL QUESTIONER HERE AND I DON’T GIVE KARNA THAT MUCH VALUE TO FRAME THIS QUESTION IN HIS FAVOUR EVEN TO CONTRADICT IT LATER.

And I am extremely sorry, I can’t follow your advise. I don’t feel any duty towards those who belief is a distortion to our epic.

But the greatest fallacy of your argument is you think the two are mutually exclusive and one who hates in human evils like Hitler must be a fan of Churchill. YOU ARE WRONG! I have no respect or appreciation for either. Both were bad omen for our country.

Creation time: Aug 14, 2021 06:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Some would argue that British PM called Gandhi's role as minimal. Problem is people don't realize in twentieth century history was beyond control of any nation — world was watching. With evidence. There is no myth here.

Indian army didn't revolt. And Netaji wasn't around. Indian soldiers supported Britishers and one person taught them the mantra of non cooperation and civil disobedience. Army was getting restless. People were getting restless.

But I never said it was one man show. It was a nation of people taking control. It was unstoppable. Indians were ready.

And you are right only a powerful entity can talk peace. But you can't talk peace at gun point. Only cowards think they are powerful because of a loaded gun. Gandhiji too believed in this.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/The-quote-if-the-Muslims-want-to-kill-us-we-must-face-death-bravely-has-been-attributed-to-MK-Gandhi-Can-someone-confirm-its-authenticity/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 14, 2021 06:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Interestingly none of your points are related to either this question or this answer, so I am not sure the relevance of your comments. However, since you asked here it is.

Your answer is based on particular pulp fictions and not on the authentic epic.

  1. You said Karna was exploited. Care to explain how and when? He was trained by best of the bests. He was crowned King at an early age and remained a King.
  2. Can you quote any reference where Indra is referred as ‘cheap’. He has always been used in the superlative terms — Indra among men! etc. And Karna didn’t do it to save Indra from ridicule. He simply said so. He also said he would Kill Arjuna on multiple occasions. He was only too happy to get the one time arrow in exchange of a armor that didn’t protect him during Gurudakshina battle, Swaymvara battle and Gandharva battle.
  3. Kunti cared for Karna as much she cared for other Pandavas. If it were not for her, Karna would never have got the divine armour in the first place.
  4. Gandharva war wasn’t a cooked war. It was Indra’s decision to punish Kaurvas. Karna wasn’t alone. He had the support of entire army. He was not drunk either. He had his divine armor. And he didn’t retreat to rally up army. There is no mention of this. Karna never said so. He admitted that he ran away out of fear and he thought no one could defeat Gandharvas. He deserted Duryodhana and came back only after the war was over. This also proves the worth of Karna’s armour — It may have made him immortal but not invanquishable or brave.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/No-one-is-as-generous-as-Karna-Do-you-agree-with-the-statement-Support-your-answer-with-reasons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 16, 2021 07:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks

Creation time: Aug 21, 2021 09:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I disagree on just one point. Our Maharishis never called it mythology. It is Shruti-Smriti. And it is far more valuable that both history and mythology.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-the-concern-for-the-rights-of-women-being-displayed-in-the-case-of-Arjuna-abducting-Subhadra-indicate-that-the-present-generation-of-Quorans-do-not-mind-critisising-even-the-past-heroes-if-the-honour-of-a-woman/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 21, 2021 10:30 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am a fan of many things, but foremost I am follower of Dharma.

Unfortunately your response is based on glorified fables rather that authentic narratives.

You said — Karna didn’t have Vijay Dhanush during Virat war.

Does it matter? He had his celestial weapons which he himself explicitly bragged about. He said he would kill Arjuna and cut down his ape banner flag. Could he? He fled while Arjuna killed his brother in front of his eyes. What warrior would do that?

Do you know about Draupadi Swaymvara’s battle? Arjuna had no Gandiva when Karna fled comparing him to Vishnu, Indra and Parshurama.

You don’t need divine bows to win. And no celestial weapon can make you braver than you are.

You said — Karna moved the wheel of arjun and krishna…

Can you provide a reference? This is the kind of fable that make someone a Karna fan. It is not present in the authentic references.

You said — Arjun run away from Karna Bhargavastra

Can you again provide a reference that Arjuna ran away and not simply ignored a weapon that was anyway going to die down? So tell me why Bharagvastra didn’t kill fleeing Arjuna? Why didn’t it kill other Pandava generals who were still there? Why it didn’t the war for Karna? Are you sure you are not over estimating Bharagvastra? Krishna certainly didn’t

You said — On the 16 and 17 day of war karna was superior

Ok. So you confirm you haven’t read Mahabharata. But let me tell you even Karna would disagree with your statement. Karna himself admitted his defeat on day 16. Dhirtrashtra and Sanjay to agreed that Kaurvas were badly beaten on that day.

On the 17th day, Arjuna killed Karna’s son in front of his and he could do nothing. And if you read the detailed explanation of the day you would understand who was dominating that day.

You said — His wheel was stuck on the ground that time he tries to take out wheel then Krishna say to kill karna when he is taking his wheel because when he was taken the bow he was undefeated so Arjun killed Karna unfairly.

Were Karna’s weapons hidden inside his chariots wheel? Many warriors fought the battle from the ground.

Do you think Abhimanyu begged for recesses during the battle when he was without chariot. Did Karna gave him any opportunity.

Do you know Arjuna himself fought without chariot on Day 14 when his horses got tired? Could anyone kill him then?

Do you want to say Karna was so weak and inferior that he couldn’t fight without chariot like Drona, Bhishma, Satyaki or Abhimanyu?

But if you read the epic, Karna had his weapons with him and he died fighting.

You said — You are a fan of arjuna…

Oh yes. I am. But do you know who is one of the greatest fan of Arjuna? Karna! He dedicated all his life trying to model like Arjuna. He aspired to be compared to him. He failed. Nevertheless he lived and died to be better than Arjuna.

During Draupadi Swyamvara he compared Arjuna to Indra, Vishnu and Parshurama.

On the last day of his life he praised Arjuna and clearly admitted no one can be his equal. In between on many occasion he reluctantly praised Arjuna.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Aug 22, 2021 07:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It may appear difficult and rightly so when you consider Krishna as a mere human and not an enlightened being. Now I would like you to take to first four shloka of chapter 4 of Bhagwat Gita, where Krishna said that in the begining of the time I gave the knowledge of Yoga to Sun God, who passed it to …

Now it would be more difficult to imagine that! Right? But if you realize Krishna wasn’t talking about just the man who was the son of Vasudeva but his connection to divinity and destiny, the mystery would resolve itself. Krishna didn’t had to go in person to inspire Drona. He was the voice of destiny which prevailed on Drona.

And finally Eklavya may at best be a minor irritation to Krishna and not a threat even by a long shot. It appears even more difficult to visualize that a person who submitted to Rajsuy yagna would be threat to the most renowned warrior of the era.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Eklavya-participate-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 26, 2021 08:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Creation time: Aug 26, 2021 08:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I believe neither you nor I is powerful enough to throw Mahabharata several centuries back. And the very idea is as ludicrous as your attempt to denounce other’s views as ‘ignorant’. I keep getting this kind of personal attacks from time to time.

And what may be the parameter of being enlightened — criticizing Krishna’s action? Deciding what Krishna should have done and what he shouldn’t have? Trusting that everyone except your esteemed shelf is ignorant? Is that a progressive thinking?

Do you know the child in Uttara’s womb had died? And Krishna revived the dead one! Am I sending Mahabharata centuries back here? He who could revive a dead, why do you think can’t kill and Avadhya?

By the way could you provide the reference where Krishna suggested Arjuna to kill Ashwatthama? Do you know Ashwatthama’s longevity isn’t a boon but a curse?

Hiranyadhanu is a fact and his son is also a fact. Agree. And so is the fact that neither were a threat to Krishna — at best a minor irritation.

I would appreciate next time instead of using sarcasm and abusive word you bring some reference and substance to the discussion.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Eklavya-participate-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 26, 2021 09:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You answered for yourself. You are talking about dialogs from a TV Show and not from the authentic epic. In the authentic epic, Arjuna never said he is better than Lord Rama. He never said he is the best warrior. He never had any encounter with Lord Hanumana. And Krishna never said Arjuna arrogant. He called him sinless (Nishpaap).

Since Arjuna never boasted of being the best, there is no question of justifying that. But as I mentioned, Arjuna was certainly hailed as the greatest warrior of the era not nust by the likes of Karna, Bhishma, Drona, Kripacharya, and Ashwatthama but also by Parshuraam, Shri Krishna and Mahadeva himself. Could they all be arrogant?

Hope that helps.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Arjunas-arrogance-of-being-the-greatest-Warrior-of-all-time-not-justified-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 02:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think I would end the debate here. You have no words to offer other than being offensive and I want no part of it.

I asked you where is the reference that Krishna asked Arjuna to kill Ashwatthama. You have no reference to offer. So all you are left with is your lousy arguments. And no one could beat irrational brags. So I will let your judgement about my knowledge of Mahabharata slide away — It is not a reflection of mine, but yours.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Eklavya-participate-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 02:44 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Can you tell me where exactly Lord Krishna said Arjuna was arrogant?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Arjunas-arrogance-of-being-the-greatest-Warrior-of-all-time-not-justified-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 05:19 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Once again may I ask the source of this story in any authentic version of Mahabharata?

Ramananda Sagara wasn’t an ancient rishi. He didn’t quote from authentic version of Mahabharata.

Should we consider Arjuna arrogant because Ramananda Sagar said so? Won’t that be injustice to one of the finest hero of Mabharata who was appreciated by divinities like Parshurama, Krishna and even Shiva!

Once again, no one ever said Arjuna was greater than Shri Rama. Arjuna never said. If you see my answer you would clearly see, I mentioned him the greatest warrior of the era. And I based my answer of authentic texts of Mahabharata and quoted, Krishna and Shiva.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Arjunas-arrogance-of-being-the-greatest-Warrior-of-all-time-not-justified-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 05:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

That is the problem. It was a TV Show — a show that starts with a disclaimer that we have taken creative liberty. And people end up believing it.

Let me assure you, there is no such incident mentioned in actual Mahabharata’s authentic text. You may refer to Neelkantha Mahabahrata published by Gita press or the BORI CE.

Let us not condemn a hero based on a fictional tale

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Arjunas-arrogance-of-being-the-greatest-Warrior-of-all-time-not-justified-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 05:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Arrogance and Lies and not antonyms.

But by your parameter, even Lord Krishna didn’t reach heaven and was killed by a mere hunter. What does this prove? Does that mean Krishna was no Got? Does that mean he was such a weak person that he was killed by a mere hunter? Or does that mean you are missing some important point of the story? I don’t believe, all prevailing Krishna was weak. And the only other explanation is it was his will — a lesson to the eternity —

A hero is not someone who dies a glorious death, but someone who lives and dies for a good cause.

No wonder Krishna and Arjuna are considered bigger heroes than the seeker of truth, Yudhisthira.

Yudhishthira also explains the reason why Arjuna fell. According to Yudhishtira — Arjuna assured him that he would end the war in one day and he didn’t. But if you read the Udyoga Parva, Arjuna didn’t say he will. He said he can with the help of divine weapons but he doesn’t deem fit to kill normal soldiers with divine weapons. Does that mean Yudhishira wasn’t truthful? They how did he reach the heaven? Once again are we missing something here?

If I say something with an honest conviction to be fact, I am not a liar even though what I said may not be a fact. The fact is I am true to the best of my knowledge. Yudhishira thought Arjuna assured him to finish the battle in one day. That made his view truthful.

But most important. Swargarohana Parva ends with explaining that Dog was not a Dog but Dharma himself. And everything was a big illusion.

What you may also have completely missed that Arjuna was an incarnation of ancient sage Nara and achieved the divine destination of Narayana.

So none of your points prove Arjuna was arrogant. But I am certain you learnt something in the process.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Arjunas-arrogance-of-being-the-greatest-Warrior-of-all-time-not-justified-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 06:19 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t read my answer in the same way you didn’t read the epic. Just check what I said about Krishna above —

I don’t believe, all prevailing Krishna was weak. And the only other explanation is it was his will — a lesson to the eternity —

A hero is not someone who dies a glorious death, but someone who lives and dies for a good cause.

If you read it again, you would realize that I never doubted divinity of Krishna. I am trying to point the flaws in your argument — death doesn’t define either divinity or a hero — neither for Krishna, nor for Arjuna.

To be a hero you don’t need to be god or a divinity. You need to have courage and character. And Arjuna passes this test in flying color. That is why Shiva and Krishna blessed him. Do you doubt Shiva and Krishna? Do you think Krishna failed in his judgement to call Arjuna as inseparable part of his existence? What do you think?

To answer your final point — Shiva and Parshurama announced that Arjuna was incarnation of ancient sage Nara.

But please do reply with your views, why should Krishna and Shiva supported Arjuna.

Creation time: Aug 29, 2021 10:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

However current context is if Arjuna was arrogant and as per the authentic text, he was NOT.

Regards,

Content:

Actually yes. He did it twice — in front of Krishna and his mother. But his anguish was limited to the point that his mother abandoned him.

He never lamented for lack of opportunity or that he was oppressed.

This is also a proof of society we had then.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-so-generous-that-he-had-to-bear-the-curses-for-the-sake-of-others-During-Mahabharata’s-time-was-it-bad-to-be-good-and-generous-Why-was-fate-so-cruel-towards-Karna-but-not-others/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 30, 2021 08:28 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ah! when you have an epic of size of Mahabharata with over a 80K shloka, cherry pick is all you can do. Would you rather have me copy-paste entire epic? And references must be made to the point.

What I can proudly say, I cherry pick from authentic retelling and not from some TV Show or skewed perception biased against the epic. Let me show you a few proof —

You said — His mother only went to demoralise him. She was least interested to save him.

Can you provide your cherry pick reference? Kunti lamented profusely when she had to let go of Karna. It was her demand that Karna got the divine armors which you cherish so much, something she never asked for any of her other son. She had gone to him to ask to switch sides to Dharma and leave the evils. I don’t see any lack of interest. But you are free to come with your cherry pick reference that would be better than baseless arguments.

You said — celestial useless weapon which was not even the best weapon of the deity in exchange of immortal armour. Well great deal!

Once again, I am sure you can’t cherry pick any reference to prove your point. Because if you dive into the epic you would find the following points —

  • Karna was only too happy with the exchange. He himself admitted to Indra that he was terrified of the roars of Arjuna.
  • Krishna was worried about Karna having that celestial weapon.

Now unless you consider both of them fools, it was a great deal for sure.

I would discuss another reason for calling it a great deal in the next section.

Other warriors got their weapons by proving their worth. In such a scenario getting a weapon by seer exchange was really a good deal.

You said — A pack of hungry hounds are destroying a young man wearing impenetrable armour and celestial rudra bow.

Are you talking about that impenetrable armor that was penetrated and broken?

Now look at the Karna’s divine armor. Did it protect him from repeated failure, getting scared and fleeing terrified? What is the value of such an armor which can’t help you win a battle, and can’t boost your confidence to stay in the war?

Abhimanyu stayed put despite his armor was broken. He didn’t flee. Others never had such armor and yet they fought rather than fleeing.

If you see carefully you would see why exchanging the armor was a great deal and why Karna was too happy with the exchange.

Now go and cherry pick a reference to contradict me.

You said —the same man performed group attacks against others. And the hungry hound easily dominated 17+ group attacks on a single day which consisted 20+ maharatha warriors! No one speaks about this

Does it really matter? Do you know group attacks were often many to many and rarely one to many. In each group attack Karna had his supporters, who may have fled eventually.

Still it doesn’t change the fact that Abhimanyu was alone, weaponless, without no charitot, or armour against many heinous creatures. And important, he didn’t beg for any recess, didn’t teach others Dharma, he knew they had none.

You can’t compare this one with any other.

You said — A grand old man brutally pinned to the ground by his own beloved grandson!!! Great return of the love bestowed on grandson.

I don’t know why you even raised this issue, if not for your malice against Arjuna? This question is not about Arjuna or what he did. This question was about Karna’s generosity and ill fate. But since you asked —

Warriors die in war, often in the gruesome way. But I am sure being on quora for so long, even with little of what you may read, Bhishma was not attacked the way they depict on the TV Show. That was the example of group attack. Many warriors from both sides fought each other. It is a different story they most of Kauravas, with exception of Duhshashana, fled the fight deserting their general. And in such a war people tend to die a tragic death.

To be fair, Bhishma was a great warrior and the only way he could be removed from the battle is with such display of force. He wasn’t some ordinary warrior, who would have fled the battle field or who could be beheaded by a single arrow.

As far as beloved grandson and grand father is concerned, I would recommend read Gita. You would realize that the beloved relationship was considered by a single warrior in the battlefield. Others knowing that would kill their brothers were not only unbothered but eager. It took Krishna to teach Gita to a reluctant warrior and he devoted the time in explaining, why not hung on the word ‘beloved’. I am sure you missed it.

Interestingly Krishna didn’t have to teach a similar Gita to Karna when he met him and despite of Krishna’s attempt explain Dharma or beloved, Karna was unmoved.

Do you still not see the difference? A warrior who knows and cares for their ‘beloved’ fought under the guidance of Krishna for a greater cause. The other fought for self glory and doesn’t have any concern for those beloved. Arjuna killed Karna without knowing he was his brother and Krishna emphasizing how evil Karna was. Karna killed Abhimanyu knowing his relationship with the young warrior and still willing to kill. You still don’t know the difference. Do you?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-so-generous-that-he-had-to-bear-the-curses-for-the-sake-of-others-During-Mahabharata’s-time-was-it-bad-to-be-good-and-generous-Why-was-fate-so-cruel-towards-Karna-but-not-others/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 30, 2021 11:23 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:
  1. Yes, It was Karna who ordered disrobing of Draupadi.
  2. Present fictions extends to say last 100 years including Ramdhari Singh Dinkar and Ravindra Nath Tagore. Both are great writer, but neither were historians or authority of Mahabharata.
  3. No. It was Karna who flatly refused to give the kavach without an exchange. Indra offered the Vasvi Shakti only when Karna refused to give kavacha otherwise.
  4. No. Kunti never asked to spare his sons life. She asked him to switch sides. He unilaterally offered to spare the lives of his four sons which Kunti, no doubt, gladly accepted.

Even as a normal reader, we must agree that beyond perfect and imperfect, there exists the world of good and evil. A person who wants to molests a woman in public can’t be good. A person who abandons him friend and benefactor for his own life can’t be called loyal. A person who cheats his guru can’t be worth admiring.

Finally as a normal reader, we shouldn’t miss the fact that Ved Vyas included him in the four prime villains — He is mentioned trunk of the tree that was evil Duryodhana.

Karna’s bad quality overshadows his good ones by a large margin.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Karna-a-good-or-bad-person-in-Mahabharata/answer/Anshul-Gupta-458
Creation time: Sep 01, 2021 11:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I see a fundamental flaw in the argument. Kauravas can't represent satisfaction. They represented greed and lust. They represented people urge to go to any depth of satisfy their greed.

Pandava's certainly represented excellence and zeal to rise above selfish goals. They had to win. That is also a summary of Gita.

Creation time: Sep 05, 2021 12:51 AM PDT

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you think ‘satisfaction’, which you equated to Kauravas, is a negative attitude and that is why there were defeated?

Satisfaction has always been considered as a positive attitude. Once Atma becomes content, it becomes detached from expectations. And as per Krishna existence without attachment should be the prime goal of existence.

All I pointed out was the fact that Kauravas and their deceitful attitude can’t be equated to satisfaction. They didn’t fail because they had become complacent. They failed because their deceit failed them to have fair evaluation of the poisition.

Creation time: Sep 05, 2021 01:02 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think I answered that. Rape or molestation is just degree of abuse. And you can’t sentence any one more than one death. Can you. You can humiliate someone in public, but you don’t win unless you break their morale. You can abuse someone but can’t take away their dignity without their permission.

Draupadi was abused. She was humiliated. She was molested. But tell me this, who lost their dignity that day? Was it Draupadi?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Draupadis-humiliation-happen-actually-Was-it-a-result-of-enmity-between-kuru-and-panchala-or-enmity-between-Bhima-and-Duryodhana-or-enmity-between-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 05, 2021 09:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You missed the whole point. Krishna talks about working without expectation in Gita. And one can’t give up expectation unless one is satisfied. Krishna never said satisfaction is a liability. That is your perception.

But most important aspect — None of the Kaurva’s conducted pointed that were satisfied. They were constantly restless and greedy. No one who represents satisfaction would —

  • Try to usurp other’s property by deceitful means.
  • Try to humiliate other person.
  • Go to plunder other kingdom in their hour of distress.
  • Wage a war instead of paying five villages for peace.

Kauravas were polar opposite to what can be considered satisfaction. I don’t understand how do you equate them to satisfaction. Care to explain?

Creation time: Sep 09, 2021 01:45 AM PDT

Answer Comment

Content:

Now you are reaching somewhere.

BG 2.47 asks to do the duty and be satisfied. He said don’t urge to achieve fruits of your action. Nor be attached to action or inaction (be satisfied) with your action and action alone.

Do you see he talks explicitly opposite to what you said. He never said urge to achive would win.

But with so many quotes you missed to provide answer to one of the simplest question I have since the beginning of the this discussion — On what basis did you compare Kaurava’s with ‘satisfaction’? Is there any single point you can include?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Krishna-avert-Mahabharata/answers/305528957
Creation time: Sep 09, 2021 01:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. The problem is you are not trying to answer the only question I asked since the start of this discussion — by what parameter Kauravas represented satisfaction? Can you try explaining this one point without going into other details?

I would try to answer your question however.

You asked — What you mean is that a student who is totally satisfied by getting 10/100 marks and still can score 100/100?

No. Neither Krishna nor I said so. Perhaps you should re-read BG 2.47. Here Krishna says that a student shouldn’t strive to get either 10 or 100 marks. They should do their work — study. They should concentrate of subject matter rather than on the goal of achieving marks and that would bring excellence. A quest for marks makes you objective. You may get marks even by shortcut, say by analyzing question trend and focusing only on important portions rather than doing your duty of learning the subject matter completely. Krishna says when you focus on marks and strive to achieve future career you would defocus. Also a goal oriented achiever may not be averse to foul means like Karna or Duryodhana. But one who seeks to do his work and not burdened by those goals achieve perfection.

Let me give you examples. A brave army soldiers fights. If he focuses on the possibility of his death and his families insecurity, he won’t be able to fight. If he focuses on gaining a personal gallentary award, he would either deploy foolshardy or unethical practice. But when he fights for the motherland, not trying to outshine his fellow soldier, not trying to worry about his family, he would do the nation proud.

Let me remind you had Bill Gates or Steve Jobs strived for 100 marks, they may have achieved but perhaps we may not have even heard about them. You know them because they followed what Krishna said — Honor your higher calling without being attached (as in greed or fear) of it’s outcome.

Now can you, for once, answer my only question — By what parameter Kauravas, the greedy, the lusty, devil conspirators represented “SATISFACTION”?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Krishna-avert-Mahabharata/answers/305528957
Creation time: Sep 09, 2021 10:34 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Unfortunately your answer is based on modern fable and not authentic version of Mahabharata.

  1. Karna was taught by Drona, Kripa and Parshurama. So he had no dearth of opportunity as a kid. Yes, we can’t compare Karna and Arjuna because while both were contemporary is Drona’s gurukul one was striving for excellence, other conspiring with Drona. They, indeed, had different situation.
  2. He was given a chance to show case his talent. He participated in the talent showcase although it wasn’t meant for his and he had gate crashed. He had a chance at Draupadi’s swyamvara, during Gandharva’s attack, during Matasya attack. And he failed. Everytime.
  3. He was cursed for his deceit and not without a reason.
  4. You say he had to get indulged in Duryodhana’s bad activity but —
    1. It was his choice and not compulsion. At least not in gurukul days.
    2. Many bad activities were his mastermind rather than Duryodhanas —
      1. Draupadi’s humiliation
      2. Attempt to Mock Pandavas in exile
      3. Matasya battle
  5. You say — He wasn’t overestimated. In his days people knew his calibre. Bhishma, Ashwatthama, Drona, Kripa, Dhritrasthra everyone knew, he couldn’t win against Arjuna. But modern story teller made him an icon despite his repeated failures. Do you know how? By censuring them.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Abhimanyu-killed-so-mercilessly-Wasnt-Karna-or-Dronacharya-capable-of-defeating-Abhimanyu-single-handedly-Karna-was-as-skilled-as-Arjuna-which-puts-him-in-the-front-foot-against-Abhimanyu/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 12, 2021 07:55 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The story of Bali as incarnation doesn’t come from any authentic Santana literature but a more modern narratives. And I don’t see why would Bhishma get a punishment — for being a dutyful son?

Why do you think, martyrs of Phulwama attack got such terrible death? Is fighting for motherland a sin? Does the sin of past life out weighs any noble deed of the current life?

Even if you go by your own logic you would realize your life and afterlife can be considered heaven or hell. Right? That’s exactly what I explained. It exists. Just differently.

Creation time: Sep 14, 2021 10:06 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna was a student of Drona. There is no dispute or controversy on this issue. It has been explicitly mentioned in Adi Parva, Vana Parva and Shanti Parva.

You say some writing take help of Sanskrit to make it look real. Can you suggest what is the alternative? Take help of TV Shows? And you don’t have to read Sanskrit verses. You can check out —

  • Gita Press Sanskrit-Hindi version
  • Bori English translation by Bibek Debroy
  • Kisarai Mohan Ganguli’s English translation of Neelkantha Mahabharata
