Answer Comment

Content:

That’s Right. And I said the same thing. His flag had his mark.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/What-are-some-of-the-feats-shown-by-Arjuna-without-Shri-Krishna-and-Hanuman-Ji-Please-don-t-include-Virat-and-Gandharv-1
Creation time: Sep 14, 2021 10:13 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Laugh all you like, if it helps you hide your ignorance about the epic. There is absolutely no reference in epic that Bhima knew the true identity of Karna. But Karna knew it. Karna knew the identity of Abhimanyu. Karna knew he is evil as he confided himself to Krishna and yet he betrayed everyone — not just his family but his best friend.

Had you had any chance to read original epic, you would find Bhima’s acts were childish and innocent and not his fault if he alone out powered a hundred brother. Winner can’t be slammed for being powerful. But he never back stabbed anyone. He never poisoned anyone. He never tried to burn unsuspecting family.

Kauravas were killed for their heinous act. If you recall before that, with all the differences, Kauravas were still not harmed for their Lakshagriha conspiracy. They were, infact, invited to Rajsuya yagna. So, yes Duryodhana and Dussasana were bad. Karna was worst.

And yes, I hate all evils — Ravana, Hiranyakashyapa or Karna. But are you sure you admire Duryodhana and Dussasana with whom you showed your sympathy?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 16, 2021 08:44 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your comment.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-the-most-foolish-among-Kichak-Jaidrath-and-Karna-the-3-ones-who-tried-to-play-with-fire-but-themselves-got-burnt-All-tried-to-humiliate-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 16, 2021 09:21 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You asked — Bhima’s actions are childish.

Yes, and you would have know had you cared to read the epic.

You said — Abhimanyu was a killer, he was no innocent. he had to be put down.

Correction. Abhimanyu was a warrior who killed during the course of the war. You don’t refer to a brave warrior as a killer. A killer kills is one who may poison or try to burn a suspecting family or backstab. Abhimanyu did no such thing.

And yes, for Kaurav’s success he needed to be brought down. And he was brought down. Karna did it knowing who he was killing. And the worst part was the cowardly way he and his friends did. Good thing, they all were brought down.

You said — You are comparing Ravana, Hirynakashipu with Karna, those guys were literally monsters whereas Karna is a good guy.

So you don’t call a molester of woman as monster? So why was Raavan a monster? Why was Hiranyakashipu a monster? Just because he tried to burn down or kill his son? How was Karna different?

By the way these people, too, were good at somepoint, else they wouldn’t have got blessing from the Gods, nor would Karna incurred the ire of trinity.

Karna himself said world would be destroyed because of his misdoing. Ved Vyas mentioned him as root of the evil tree Duryodyana.

You said — Karna is a good guy. according to Krishna.

You clearly haven’t read the epic properly. Krishna called Karna evil on more occasion than he praised him. And most important he so desperately wanted him dead that he was greatly pleased when the Vasvishakti was expended and reminded Arjuna of all his crime so that he kills him swiftly.

But for all those things you need to read the epic.

You said —Krishna himself offered everything to Karna to switch sides.

Krishna also offered everything to Duryodhana to avert the war. And his first offer was to Duryodhana and not to Karna. Does that make him a better person? A good person?

No. It was a cost to avoid the full blown war. You see, what is happening in Taliban today? Do you think USA or anyone carries high opinion about them? You often compromise with terrorists for peace.

You said — you guys are so obsessed with Karna…

Really! Do you see me putting him on the top of my list? Do you see me praising that evil person? Do you see me defending his acts? Am I obsessed that I would jump to a question to save the honor of villains like Karna, Duryodhana and Dussasana.

I just followed the lines of the epic. But look around you would find real people who are obsessed with that evil person.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 16, 2021 09:36 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thank you very much for your kind words…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 17, 2021 08:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It is not necessary to keep proving you know nothing about the original epic.

You said — Karna a molester? he did that to provoke the pandavas to do something instead standing like ducks, he later felt guilty for it.

WRONG. Karna did this to please Duryodhana not to provoke the Pandavas to something. He himself accepted his fault in front of Krishna. There is not a single mention of this lofty goal of Karna as some of the fanatics like you mention. Had this been the goal, he would have stopped Dussasana when he saw his provocation had zero effect. He continued to abuse Draupadi long after. Not only this he was lusty and wanted to go to exile to see Draupadi and Arjuna in miserable condition wearing tree bark. You can’t hide his true character.

You said — if Abhimanyu’s death was unfair then Arjuna was a coward when put down Bhisma with Shikindi tricks.

Again, is there a point in asking you to read? Bhishma died in a battle where he wasn’t alone. It was a group fight — many warriors from both side fighting each other.

You said — Drona was tricked.

Yes, tricks or strategy is part of the battle. Drona tricked Arjuna away before forming Chakravyuh. He broke the rule of the war and extended it beyond sun set. It was his decision to sit down in the middle of the battle. And finally he was killed by a single person — who wasn’t a Pandava.

You said — Even Karna was killed in a bad way

Karna was killed on a one on one battle, where he had his weapons in his possession. He only had no chariot. Many warriors including Arjuna fought without chariot on occasions. It’s a different matter, only Karna pleaded his opponent to stop. When Bhima broke Drona’s chariot he took up another. Karna, he begged.

You said — Arjuna slaughtered an entire forest filled with lifeforms, does that make him a killer?

NO. That makes him a soldier obeying the order of the supreme commander Brahma. He just followed the orders. Again, if instead of reading garbage analysis if you read the epic, you would find Arjuna’s action is glorified by everyone — friends and foes.

You said — Arjuna needed a divine chariot with a god, divine bow with ultimate firepower, unlimited arrows, dozens of astras and anjalikas to just kill a guy that has been cursed thrice.

NO. without any special chariot or special bow, Arjuna had capacity to thrash Karna in such a manner that he compared Arjuna to Indra, Vishnu and Parshurama, before retreating like a scared kid. And yes, having great weapons is not bad, having bad character and coward temper is.

You said — Krishna himself admitted that if it wasn’t for his sake Karna would have killed Arjuna the first chance he gets.

You said. Not Krishna. He NEVER said, Karna could kill Arjuna. Not even once. You can check the article. He always said only you could kill him. In fact not only he, but Lord Shiva and Parshurama too said that Arjuna would win beyond any doubt. Krishna too said the same thing.

Finally and Funny, you said — The original epic is gone forever dust and all.

You have absolutely no understanding of ancient sanatan culture. It can’t be put to dust. NEVER. Because it is not something written on a piece of paper of tree that would be destroyed with time — it is propagated using Shruti-Smriti tradition. No one can put it to dust. Certainly not you.

This is always the final argument by Karna’s fanatics like you — create doubt about the authenticity of the epic also. But people like you don’t understand that if epic is gone, so is Karna. Do you think it is worth to condemn and epic just to sing the glory of a heinous conniving coward?

If you go back to your answer you would know who is a hater here — hater of epic, hater of those whom God helped because they were good. Unfortunate that you should lament for evils like Duryodhana and Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 17, 2021 08:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Interesting question. However the gap between arrogance and compassion may at times be blurred.

Karna had promised to Kunti, and may I emphasize, unilaterally that he would spare the lives of four Pandavas. No one asked him. He nevertheless made that promise. Now all he did was to keep his word. So would you credit Karna for being a man of his word or a man of compassion? Think, what act of compassion did Karna showed to Kaurava brothers who died protecting him and without knowing that Karna would not return the favor as he had taken a pledge not to kill Bhima? Didn’t Kauravas deserved his compassion, who always showered their generosity to him? You may call it call of duty or plain naked arrogance, but it can’t really be called compassion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Vrisha/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 18, 2021 08:07 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think, I already established who is biased.

Now, I happened to have read what Krishna said about using Vasvi Shakti. Now talking about the context, can you tell if that was the praise of Vasvi Shakti or Karna?

Also did you know Krishna said Karna is about to Kill Yudhishthira. So, I am sure you would agree Karna was not going to keep his promise of sparing their life. Right? Krishna also said Shalya was more powerful than Bhishma, Arjuna and Karna and only Yudhishthira could have killed him. Do you agree again? The point is Krishna, being a great motivator used words to maximize the impact. As for fact, just don’t count the words but also the action —

  • Krishna was too happy to realize Karna would be an easy kill. Why? Why did Krishna wanted him dead?
  • Why did Krishna promised that Karna would die and his wife will weap?
  • Why did Krishna said that Karna without Vasvi Shakti and Kavach is as good as dead?
  • Why is it that Karna couldn’t win a single battle against Arjuna?
  • Why is it that Duryodhana lost 31 brothers on day 14 trying to save Karna? Why did he need saving?

And since you talk about the context, can you also quote what is the term for using Vasvishakti that Indra had explained to Karna? Could Karna have used his vasvi shakti as long he had other weapons?

Finally let us talk about delusion

You said “I can quote…”

You say you can quote and also maintain the the original doesn’t exist? Do you see the paradox?

You say that the epic is lost forever, and yet jumping in a discussion trying to prove something.

  • How would you prove anything if there is no reference?
  • Where would you quote from if reference doesn’t exist?

Do you want to say that the ancient epic references are as good as the skewed modern narrative on which you are basing your answer or did you have a source unknown to the world?

Think, and unfortunately you would never know who is delusional, that being the key charactristics of one who is —

  • You reject the epic and lost and still jump at debate and calls other delusional!
  • You support people marked evil in the epic and call others haters!
  • You find Krishna doing tricks, Abhimanyu a killer and Karna innocent! And then you call other haters

That is the classic definition of being delusional, biased and hater.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 19, 2021 03:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,

The literally invincible warrior with his armor and earring was badly defeated during —

  • Draupadi Swyamver battle by Arjuna where he fled scared. Arjuna wasn’t using any special weapon.
  • Gandharva battle where he was badly injured and retreated for saving his life (his own words).
  • During the Matasya battle he fled thrice even after seeing his brother killed. Though he had lost his armor by this time, he had all his divine weapons (His own words). Krishna wasn’t with Arjuna in these battle. There is no mention of Hanuman in this episode.
  • He lost Rajsuy yagna battle with his armors.
  • If you read the details of the Gurudakshina battle you would find Karna lost that battle too.
  • Arjuna never fled Bhargavastra. He let it defuse. You can read the actual text. Bharagavastra was defused without killing even a single warrior of repute. Bharagavastra didn’t win the war.

The problem is you fail to understand between the theory and the practical. An armor or an earring can make you invincible, it can’t make you brave, or stop you from getting scared or running away. It is evident from his continuous fleeing from scene with or without armors.

It is interesting to see people respecting an conniving, deceitful, coward evil and undermine the hero of Arjuna who was admired by the likes or Bhishma, Drona, Parshurama, Krishna and above all Lord Shiva. Ignore all of these people and reference and be in your fictional world.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-will-win-in-these-battle-scenarios-Karna-vs-Bhisma-Bhisma-vs-Arujuna-and-Karna-vs-Arjuna/answer/Ambuj-Pathak
Creation time: Sep 21, 2021 09:11 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna did disarm Arjuna. And that proves, Karna wasn’t unarmed when he died. A brave warrior is not one who never loses the war or whose bows never slips off his hand. A brave warrior is how he behaves when he faces such a problem. Did you find Arjuna begging Karna to wait till he gets hold of his weapon? Hope that would give you the difference.

Karna was no innocent is an understatement. He was an evil man, one of the worst of his time. But Karna being Bhima doesn’t equate Bhima being evil. At least the original epic doesn’t say so. Killing evils is NOT an evil act.

And mud-slinging of Bhishma, Drona, and Ashwatthama doesn’t make Karna any less evil.

I am not sure why should you mock the ORIGINAL and favour dIsToRtIoN!

Orginal need not be written in distorted way.

Content:

Thanks for your comment.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-you-write-a-letter-to-Mahabharata-Admirers-on-behalf-of-a-particular-character-of-Mahabharata-confessing-the-feelings-of-that-particular-character-to-his-admirers-short-one-Request-please-dont-target-any/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 21, 2021 08:51 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

And yet, when you read the epic, during Mahabharata era, Indra was one of the highest revered God. His name is used in terms of superlative terms and not as some mean person.

So is it a possibility that we after all those years got the context wrong? Let me give you a hint — Karna had refused to donate his kavacha and was too pleased with the exchange he got. And that tells that Indra’s disguise had failed.

You said — it is like requesting somebody -“ kindly die please for my sake!”

Do you want to imply, that anyone without divine Kavacha was akin to “please die”? In that case that anyone included everyone. Right? Do you mean Karna was the weakest warrior who would be dead without the protection of a Kavacha? Maybe you are right. Krishna said the same.

You said — No courtesy ,etiquette , respect, consideration, stabbing from back nature.

Indra approached as a Brahmin seeking alms, right at the time when Karna used to donate. That is following etiquette. He didn’t use his divine power to snatch his armor. Rather he gave him an option. That is courtsey and respect. It is strange that you should call a seeker as robber. Do you expect Karna to be a great donor by donating Kingdom’s wealth and gold coins? Karna tried to offer substitutes. Indra refused. Karna demanded his weapon in exchange. Indra agreed — that was consideration. Can you imagine if Indra had refused the exchange and insisted in donation, what would have been the secnario? One Karna would have lost Kavacha without getting a weapon. But we can rule out that scenario as Karna flatly refused. The other scenario would be Indra won’t get the Kavacha, and Karna’s real face would have been unmasked as hypocrite.

Now my question — Indra, according to you was barbaric, selfish and destructive, and mean, and may well have escaped ancient wisdom. But why was Karna happy to be part of barbaric act? Why did he give the Kavacha when he had the chance to refuse and he had indeed refused? Why did Karna said with great happiness — take my Kavacha if you wish, but give me a divine weapon?

Let me give you two hints —

  1. Karna (in his own admission to Indra) feared a warrior whose roars scared him.
  2. He had lost many battles with Kavacha on.
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/I-Want-to-ask-something-if-indradev-did-right-by-taking-his-kavach-then-why-did-indradev-come-in-disguise-to-take-it-If-3
Creation time: Sep 22, 2021 04:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for commending on my explanation. Here are the answers to your questions —

You asked — Why Arjuna killed Bhishma? Drona in terms of unfair means?

Arjuna-Bhishma battle wasn’t one-to-many battle, but many-to-many battle which was the norms of the battle. There was nothing unfair here. To be more exact, if Arjuna did something wrong, it was to let Bhishma live for ten days incurring Krishna’s ire, fore he really cared about Bhishma. He still didn’t want to kill him and reluctantly agreed to disarm him. But he was not alone. It is a different matter that most of his protector fled.

You asked — Why Arjuna killed Drona in terms of unfair means?

Arjuna didn’t kill Drona. In fact he tried his best to stop Dhristduymna from slaying Drona. Interestingly despite of the death of Abhimanyu, Arjuna couldn’t bring himself to be ruthless to Drona and avoided a confrontation — not out of fear but out of respect.

You asked — Why did he choose to kill Karna when his curse were active? Why did he choose unfair means for killing Karna?

Karna was a cursed person — cursed for his misdeeds. Nothing in the world could have changed it. The only option was NOT to kill him. You must understand it was not a war of glory for Arjuna that he would have left that evil live for another day. Karna was without his chariot but not without his weapons. He died fighting an one to one battle. The only thing unfair was he fought an opponent that was unfairly more powerful than him. Can you suggest what option did he have?

You said — Arjun was saved by Krishna that is the fact.

Yes, Krishna saved Arjuna from Vaishnavastra fired by Bhagdatta. That is what friends do. That is what Ashwatthama did for Karna. In a similar effort, thirty-one Kauravas died trying to save him.

But if there is any consolation for you, Krishna didn’t help Arjuna in most of the wars of his life — Guru Dakshina Battle, Draupadi’s swyamver battle, Gandharva battle and Matasya battle. And there is another thing common in each case — Karna lost and Arjuna won.

You said — In terms of understanding I really don’t think you are up to the mark

Here the underlying point is — this is what you think — something I have no control over.

But, then I can understand sanatan Hindu epic may be a bit overwhelming for you. I would try to point out what I mean by answering your other point.

You said — Krishna on Arjuna’s chariot, Hanuman in his flag, Divine chariot, Divine Ghandiv bow etc.

Is there any objection here? Or is there a lesson that can highlight the underlying understanding?

First Hanuman wasn’t on his flag, it was his icon — in a way his blessing. But I will still take your point and assume Hanuman being on the flag. Now can you guess the significance of Krishna and Hanuman on Arjuna’s chariot? Do you think, they represented Arjuna’s weakness? Is this your understanding?

On the contrary, my friend, this is what you would call a litmus paper test of Arjuna’s adherence to Dharma. A divine endorsement is the greatest achievement one can hope to achieve. Not only Krishna but even Shiva bestowed his blessing to Arjuna.

Only those lacking the understanding of Dharma would consider the greatest power and blessing a human can acquire as a weakness. The weak and wicked never stand a chance to achieve such greatness.

With best regards

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 22, 2021 05:32 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh yes, He did you all his divine weapons —

His divine weapon had been destroyed by the maya. He began to think about what should be the appropriate course of action. All the Kurus saw Karna and the terrible maya. They said, ‘O Karna! Quickly kill the rakshasa now with your spear. 270 The Kurus and the sons of Dhritarashtra are being destroyed. What can Bhima and Arjuna possibly do to us? Kill the rakshasa who is destroying us in the night. We will be able to fight with the Parthas in the battle only if we escape from this terrible encounter. Therefore, kill the rakshasa, terrible in form, with the spear that Vasava has given you. O Karna! All the Kouravas are like Indra. Let these warriors not be destroyed in the night.’ O king! He saw that the army was being destroyed by the rakshasa in the night. He heard the loud lamentations of the Kouravas. ‘“Karna made up his mind to use the spear. Unable to bear the assaults made in the battle, he was like an angry and intolerant lion. That supreme spear was always victorious and he decided to use it to kill him.

Now unless we mistrust the basic premise of the epic and doubt Indra’s words, the reference is clear — Karna’s weapons had been used. Else he wouldn’t have managed to invoke Vasvi Shakti.

Before we talk about Brahmastra, let us understand that those weapons were nothing like our modern missiles. They were something modern science has still not managed to compete. It was not that each Brahmastra would have exactly the same effect. Those divine weapons considered the comparative strengths of both the wielder and the opponents. That is why, Naraynastra did nothing against Pandavas army, Vaishnavastra became a garland and Bhargavastra got diffused on it’s own finding no resistance.

Now coming to Karna had his Brahmastra. Karna was cursed by a Brahmana and disgraced by his own guru for his deceitful nature. Do you think such a weak and deceitful character would ever be able to command a devastating Brahmastra? He certainly could summon it, but it would meet almost the same fate as his Bharagavastra — could kill some weaker foes but not someone of great energies like Ghatotkatch.

Unlike other divine weapons, Indra’s spear was guided by Indra’s energy and was destined to kill one roaring enemy. It was not something Karna had earned or mastered. He was a barter gift. As such it was sure to work under the prescribed condition — When he would have failed with other divine weapons. Karna fulfilled that requirement.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 24, 2021 11:18 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Perhaps you missed salient points of my answer. Let me try again. Brahmastra was a very powerful weapon. It was one of the last of the weapons that Karna fired on Arjuna. Did that kill Arjuna?

He certainly had weapons of Parshurama, but as I mentioned invoking them required character strength that he sorely lacked.

Sanjay didn’t mention individual weapons here. Not a single weapon. But he did mention his divine weapons had been destroyed by maya. And Indra himself had said —

If you unleash this invincible weapon in a fit of fury, when you possess other weapons, there is no doubt that it will descend on you.”

Now the question is, did the weapon descend on Karna? Or you think Indra lie? Perhaps a simpler and more logical explanation would be —

  • Karna’s Brahmastra failed just like other divine weapons, and just the way it failed against Arjuna, fulfilling Indra’s condition.
  • Sanjay may not have explicitly mentioned Brahmastra but he did include it in the word divine weapon.

But if you still believe Karna had not used his Brahmastra against Ghatotkatch, here is a few question you can ponder about —

  1. Why didn’t Karna used Brahmastra or Brahmsira to kill Ghatotkatch and thus saving Vasvi Shakti for Arjuna as originally planned? Ghatotkatch was without doubt a enemy powerful enough to merit higher weapons.
  2. Why was Krishna so certain, that Karna would have to use Vasvi Shakti that he would expend Ghatotkatch?
  3. Why didn’t the Vasvi Shakti backfired on Karna for using it despite having other weapons? Won’t Indra would be too happy to destroy Karna for breaching the protocol?

Finally, Yudhishthira fear of Karna were not only unfounded but also a contradiction at many places. We can imagine them based on Yudhishthira’s lack expertise as warrior as eluded by Krishna.

I said the fear was unfounded because in every single direct or indirect battle between Karna, not only Arjuna was victorious but also Karna fled out of fear.

Now let us see contradictions —

  1. Yudhishthira leaves Arjuna and Bhima alone after swyamver, knowing Karna was also present there. It didn’t worry him in the least. And he was right. Arjuna easily bested Karna.
  2. Karna was badly defeated by Gandharvas. He fled terrified. Now Yudhishthira not only sends Arjuna to fight same Gandhavas but also advises for mild use of power. Is it not a contradiction.
  3. He was angry at Arjuna during the battle as to why hasn’t killed Karna yet.

So Yudhishthira’s worry is no prove of even Karna used his divine weapons or not.

But other proofs are more than clear.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 02:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

One Percent? I am sure you are no more quoting for the authentic verses.

Second, you don’t understand Brahmastra. It never needed the line of sight. Ashwatthama invoked Brahmastra against Uttara’s unborn son. He was miles away from there with know knowledge of the exact coordinate and it still hit the target. So the “illogic” is on your side.

Third, as I already pointed, Karna’s Brahmastra failed even against Arjuna. What you don’t want to agree that he was an evil and cursed man. He could never have invoked fatal Brahmastra. He could fire Vasvi Shakti only because it wasn’t guided or controlled by him, but Indra.

Finally you asked me so many questions, but didn’t answer my questions. Let me reiterate —

  1. Why didn’t Karna used Brahmastra or Brahmsira to kill Ghatotkatch and thus saving Vasvi Shakti for Arjuna as originally planned? Ghatotkatch was without doubt a enemy powerful enough to merit higher weapons.
  2. Why was Krishna so certain, that Karna would have to use Vasvi Shakti that he would expend Ghatotkatch?
  3. Why didn’t the Vasvi Shakti backfired on Karna for using it despite having other weapons? Won’t Indra would be too happy to destroy Karna for breaching the protocol?

May I expect a point by point answer to these three questions before you ask another question or debate on any other issue?

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 07:06 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

May I ask —

  • what is your source?
  • What reference do you trust to have such an high esteem about Karna?
  • Which version of the epic doesn’t call him the trunk of evil tree Duryodhana?
  • Which version doesn’t say he asked Duhshashana to strip Draupadi?
  • Why are you debating when you don’t trust the epic?

The problem is, you don’t understand the difference between epic narrative and written works. And when you say original doesn’t exist any more, I see you are lost. By the way do you have any knowledge of Jaymini Bharata? What does it say about Karna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 07:13 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for the to the point answer. It’s rare these days.

But now I must point out the flaw in your arguments.

You said — Karna didn’t used Brahmastra or Bharmashira on Ghatkoch as it was not etiquette it was night Battle Sir there were no light at that time. If I don’t know the exart target then how he fire astra that can kill his own seana.

Is there any written reference to what you mentioned? No. Because this is your perception and not remotely true.

I already gave you a reference in the previous comment — Ashwatthama fired a Brahmastra on Uttara’s unborn son. He didn’t had her exact location. Mind it the location needed to as exact as hitting the unborn child without actually hitting Uttara.

Second there was no etiquette about the night battle. Kauravas started it against the etiquette.

Third, you must understand Brahmastra didn’t follow the projectile motion. There was no danger of hitting your own army. They could trail their enemy to end them. Think, why didn’t they just move Uttara’s bed a little to protect her?

So all these arguments are just assumption that contradicts the original epic reference. None of the points you said, is mentioned in the epic.

You said — Shree Krishna was certain as he is supreme God and also he also knows its not a correct time to fire astra like Brahmastra or Brahmashira

Again, it’s just something you want to believe. Krishna never said so. In fact no one said that. Krishna was certain, Karna won’t be able to kill Ghatotkatch with his other weapons because of his weak character strength and he would be forced to use Vasvi Shakti.

Now you may ask, Do I have an exact reference to back my claim. In fact, I have. Once Vasvi Shakti was gone Krishna was too happy. He said — without Vasvi Shakti and divine armor consider Karna alreay slain. He is like a snake with poison removed, an extinguished flame. Can you reason, why he wasn’t worried about his other weapons? He knew Karna had no control over them.

What do you think about it? Why wasn’t Krishna worried about Karna’s Brahmastra?

You said —It didn’t backfired coz Karna didn’t had any astra left which is “etiquette” to be used on Ghatkoch at that time.

That was not the term of Vasvi Shakti. It was very clear, you can’t fire as long as you have any weapon left. It wasn’t about if you can/would fire or not. So beyond your perception you have no answer.

You said — his Brahmastra failed in front of arjuna as arjuna countred it But ghatkoch can’t counter Brahmastra.

No. Arjuna couldn’t have countered a properly invoked Brahmastra easily. Remember he recalled his own Brahmastra against that of Ashwatthama even at the risk of losing his only successor. Because countering it could be more devastating. But Arjuna easily countered Karna’s Brahmastra without invoking his own.

All of these boils down to two simple fact —

1. A deceiful, coward warrior couldn’t have invoked a lethal Brahmastra. He failed it against Arjuna and there is no reference that it couldn’t have failed against Ghatotkatch.

2. Against your perception, which have no reference, there are several reference to prove my point.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 07:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

I am very well realizing the characteristics of Brahmastra to realize that it’s power is vested in it’s wielder. Arjuna couldn’t neutralize Ashwatthama’s Brahmastra and he didn’t need to take any effort to neutralize Karna’s. Doesn’t the tale of two Brahmastra tell you something?

You say you are certain that Brahmastra was not used. But isn’t is a bit too ambitious for you to be certain? And that too without a valid reference? And against overwhelming evidence I provided you? Let me summarize it for you —

  1. Sanjay clearly said that Karna’s divine weapon had been destroyed. Last I checked Brahmastra was a divine weapon.
  2. Indra had clearly made a statement that he can’t use his weapon as long as he is in possession of any other weapon. There were no fine lines like in case he forgot or failed to use other you can use this. Because such small print could have allowed Karna to use it whenever he liked. He could have used it even during Matasya battle. But the same clause obstructed it’s use.
  3. Krishna never bothered about Karna’s Brahmastra. He protected Arjuna from Bhagdatta’s vaishnavastra and asked him to bow down against Narayanastra. But he seemed to be completely unworried about Karna’s weapon. He advises Arjuna to ignore Bhargavastra to meet Yudhishthira as he knows it will only tire down Karna. He made no effort to protect Arjuna from Karna’s Brahmastra.

Now do you want to say — Krishna knew Karna would not be able to invoke Brahmastra against Ghatotkatch although Brahmastra doesn’t require any line of sight between wielder and it’s target? How was Krishna so certain? Is it because he knew Karna wasn’t capable to invoke a lethal variant?

So, at the risk of being repetitive may I ask you few questions again —

  1. Now that, I have already given you evidence that divine weapons never needed line of sight or a visible target, why didn’t Karna use Brahmastra against Ghatotkatch?
  2. Why was Krishna not worried about Karna’s divine weapons and considered him already slain as soon has he lost his Vasvi Shakti? He was certainly careful against Ashwatthama’s invocations. Care to explain?
  3. How are you so certain, that Karna didn’t use Brahmastra although it was impossible to use vasvi shakti without exhausting your other weapons as per Indra’s terms.

Do you have any reference for any of your points?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 08:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t answer my questions. But I would answer yours.

You said — even Yudhisthira said not even indra can escape Karna’s brahmastra and this he said after his death.

Does that mean, you consider Arjuna more powerful than Indra? Because Arjuna certainly escaped without a scratch. But honestly, I would not give much weightage to Yudhishthira’s word who wasn’t a great warrior himself to be an expert on this subject. Even Krishna pointed that he doesn’t possess Kshatriya valor. Moreover, as you pointed it was a condolence statement where you tend to over glorify someone. The fact that Karna’s Brahmastra was ineffective against Arjuna shatters Yudhishthira’s perception in the same way as it does yours.

You said — Other weapons doesn't means brahmastra.

Oh it certainly does, specifically in the light of the fact that he couldn’t have invoked Vasvi shakti as long he had another weapon.

You said — A astra like brahmastra can't be used when a person is not certain about his enemy position.

You are WRONG. There is no such mention anywhere in the epic. Can you show any reference. And I have already mentioned it’s contradiction three times. Ashwatthama successfully invoked Brahmastra without knowing the exact position of Uttara. And it hit the target.

Although the reference is clear enough, let me remind you of an incident from Ramayana. Shri Rama manifested Brahmastra from a piece of straw against Jayanta, the son of Indra and expert in Maya. He fled and tried is best to doze it. But Brahmastra continued to follow him every where he went. Do you think he was stupid enough to run in a straight line?

Now my questions, some that you ignored earlier —

  1. Do you have any reference that says you can’t invoke Brahmastra against a target that is not in front of you? I have given two that contradicts this theory.
  2. My earlier question — Why wasn’t Krishna worried about Karna’s Brahmastra? Why he said Karna is as good as slain having lost his Vasvi Shakti?
  3. What proved right? Did Karna’s Brahmastra prove it worth, ever?

The problem is, you are unable to provide reference for you perception and considering them above the explicit references that exists. In this way we would keep moving in a circle — I would keep giving you valid references and you would keep repeating your perception devoid or either reference or logic. What’s the point in this debate?

If you can give valid answer, please do so. If you can provide valid references, do so. Else, Good Night to you.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 10:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. Arjuna didn’t use Pashupata weapon.

Pashupati and Rudra both are names of Shiva. Raudrastra is a generic name for any weapons that originates from Rudra. Pashupata is the name of a very specific weapon of Shiva — one he uses at the end the current cycle of universe.

Arjuna had his arsenal full of weapons. He didn’t need to use Pashupata.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Which-was-the-most-lethal-weapon-among-the-Brahmastra-the-Pashupatastra-and-the-Narayanastra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 10:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

I am not sure, your real problem is you don’t understand. I think, you don’t want to understand. Brahmastra was not like a gun, whoever fires would get the same result. There was no high Brahmastra for a warrior with low character strength. And are you sure you are not underestimating Ghatotkatch, who merited Karna’s use of Vasvi Shakti and the fact that Krishna knew Karna would have to?

Think! Did Arjuna use a Brahmastra to counter Karna’s Brahmastra? Is it not clearly mentioned two Brahmastra fired at each other would cause a huge devastation? Arjuna countered Karna’s Brahmastra without much fuss, or devastation. Remember Krishna interfered against Vaishnavastra, against Ashwatthama’s Brahmastra and Naraynastra. But he didn’t to bother against those of Karna’s weapon that he used with his own energy.

Now Soham, since you are so polite, may I dare to give you a word of advice?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 10:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

See you don’t need to get Brahmastra from Brahma or Naraynastra from Vishnu. The knowledge of those weapons were available to warriors from their gurus. Karna got his weapons from Parshurama and Arjuna from his gurus. Remember, he never stopped learning.

While it is not clearly mentioned, it may even have come from Drona.

But I strongly believe it may be from Mahadev himself. You see, Mahadeva not only handed over his divine weapon to Arjuna, but also taught him, there by becoming his guru. So a disciple of Rudra certainly would have possessed other Raudrastras.

Another place he may have received it is from multiple Devas who offered him divine weapons. Kuber, Varuna, Yama are explicitly mentioned. But Indra and other Gods too offered him weapons.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Which-was-the-most-lethal-weapon-among-the-Brahmastra-the-Pashupatastra-and-the-Narayanastra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 10:51 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t say, if you are open for some advice.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 10:52 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

Karna as a warrior had a extremely weak character. He lacked moral character and was a coward. The same is mentioned by prof Vishnu Suthankar. And it can be seen again and again. And yet again —

  1. He retreats from Swyamver battle. Scared. And before you say he spared a Brahmin, NO. He attacked a Brahmin thinking it is permissible to kill a Brahmin eager to fight. He retreated scared thinking opponent is invincible. He wasn’t afraid of a curse. He was afraid of the opponent.
  2. He fled Gandharvas leaving his best friend behind. And again, before you say, no he wasn’t drunk. And he didn’t retreat to bring reinforcements. He just retreated because he feared for his own life.
  3. During Matasya battle he had his divine weapons. And yet he fled thrice. He saw Arjuna killing his brother. Which brave person would see his own brother killed and instead of seeking vengeance would flee?
  4. Abhimanyu too killed his brother. And there again all he could do was to flee.
  5. He worshipped an snake all his life to use it against Arjuna. Which brave warrior would use a snake to kill his enemy?
  6. Which brave warrior would seek pleasure in mocking their enemy in misery?
  7. Which brave warrior would try to defeat their opponent while they are weak and before they could gather their own army
  8. Which brave person would try to disrobe a woman in public? Is this the sign of a strong moral character?

You claim to be a devotee of Shiva and Krishna. Both willed him to die. Krishna was too happy that evil Karna would meet his end.

Your post contradicts all points you mentioned so far and give evidence to what I have been telling — It was possible to neutralize a Brahmastra, if the Brahmastra either wasn’t invoked with devastation in mind or invoked by an inferior warrior. Arjuna didn’t invoke Brahmastra to be fatal. Karna couldn’t invoke because of his weak character.

You said — Don’t underestimate Karna’s power as a warrior.

You know, a Lion is very strong. But he isn’t called a warrior. As he doesn’t have warriors etiquette or a warrior’s code of conduct. Similarly Karna may have been strong, made stronger by possession of those divine weapons that he obtained by deceit. But he wasn’t brave. He wasn’t a warrior. You can say he was a fighter. He had no morality. I don’t consider him a warrior.

But since you game me a suggestion, I would like to offer you some, in turn.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 11:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

I would use this comment to offer you some advice. Since you are a big fan of Karna, let me tell you something about Karna.

Karna had a divine lineage. He was the son of Suryadeva and by that extension, a part of him. He was the only warrior in history to have been born with divine armors. He was born to be a great person, but conditioned himself to turn out to the trunk of an evil tree!

What was his main folly? Just one. His ego. He wanted to prove himself better than Arjuna and was ready to stoop to any depth to satisfy that ego. He conspired along side Duryodhana, he cheated on his guru, tried to molest a woman in public, mock a family in distress.

He let his ego consume him to such an extent, that he knew he was wrong, he knew he is one of the cause for upcoming devastating battle, but couldn’t pull himself out of it.

He was born with a divine gift, but his ego and fear made him trade that celestial gift for a weapon. He dug his own grave.

Now give yourself a moment. Are you sure, the current discussion and your constant emphasis that Karna couldn’t have invoked a Brahmastra, contradicting all evidences, is not an ego trip to prove Karna great?

Can you earnestly say that you haven’t realized that Karna was an evil and coward person and yet your ego is not letting you let go off him because you have announced that you are a Karna fan? Can you earnestly say why would someone by fan of a molester and a coward who sided with evils?

Now let me tell you another story. It was of a little boy who at a young age committed a mistake. He stole his brothers gold. But that mistake made him miserable. He thought it was a sin. He admitted his sin to his father. He didn’t go on an ego trip and justify what he did. Years later he was humiliated and thrown out of a railway compartment. He still didn’t let his ego get better of him. He didn’t used his skill as a barrister against railways. He fought a bigger battle — for the dignity of his countrymen. He is named revered as ‘Mahatma’ and ‘The Father of the Nation’. Our Nation.

Think, what is wise? Be a captive of our egos knowing it would mean siding with an evil. Or…

(You need not answer this comment. I would recommend if you want to respond, do at least after think about it. Do think about it.)

I wish you all the best. Your action decides your destiny.

With best regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 25, 2021 11:55 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Well I agree, discussion won't end unless we end it. But I disagree that I merely presented my perception. I presented references. I presented what Shiva said, what Krishna said, what Ved Vyas said. Its not my perception that he was called called trunk of evil tree. Its not my perception that Krishna was happy for his death. Its not my perception that he declared his wives would weep at her dead body.

Shiva mentioned even Ravana and Tarkasura powerful. Doesn't mean he called them good. He decreed even Karna to die. No private perception here.

The only difference between your perception and mine is — You value power, I value goodness of heart. For you it doesn't matter if a person is a molester or a criminal as long as he is powerful. For me it doesn't matter how powerful someone is if he is inhuman.

We may agree to disagree. However, my only regret is, I failed to make you realize that following Karna's path, we can't make a safe civil society, but a ruthless selfish society, full of conspiracy deceit and lust, where acid thrower would be next generation idols. I am sorry, I couldn't help you.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 04:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh!

Thanks for enlightening me. I got fooled by Shiva's words. May be you can enlighten Lord Shiva as well, because like me he too said, no one had knowledge of Pashupata. Do you think, he too is a fool?

And Karna must be a fool too, because he too said, in awe, that Arjuna has Pashupata that has no measure. And that coward failed despite of having all weapons. Fled again and again. So what's the lesson? Weapons don't win the war, bravery does. Someone can give you weapon but not courage.

Now, a simple warning to you — desist from personal attacks. Next time, I won't respond. Just delete such abusive comments.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 04:17 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. Here YOU are trying to distort Gita by this question. I explained the true meaning of Gita’s shloka along with the right context.

Krishna never said he favors farmers. In fact Krishna favors those who do their prescribed duties — they need not be farmers. Anyone who does their work gets Krishna’s grace. Ques

But in your question tried to present skewed perception with malice political intentions.

And since you try to make this debate political rather than philosophical, you must realize that my post isn’t against farmers but those corrupt brokers who are furthering their political agenda from behind innocent farmers. Farmer’s are being misled and ruining their own prospect and life. And a clear proof is the fact this protest is concentrated only is a very small part of the country and not all farmers are protesting. They know they are covered and cared by the government.

Tell me something, is this how people further their agenda? By misquoting Gita! And I become biased for pointing it out?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 07:02 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Good we agreed on something — Karna was evil. But then I don’t know when you admit Karna was an evil, why would you brand yourself a Karna fan?

You are a reasonable person and quite decent in your communication. You never used bad words. Why would you follow such an abuser? It is unworthy of you.

Karna or Ravana both were molesters. For me both were evil. Karna was powerful. But not brave. And the two words are different. Power is physical trait, bravery is a character trait. An no molester or conspirator can every be brave. Villains may be strong but not great enough that someone should admire them.

It was, indeed nice, to have conversation with you and get to know you.

Bye and Regards

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Does-Karna-have-Pashupatastra-and-Shambhav-Astra-in-his-vessel/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 07:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It still one and the same. Read back your question.

1.You said — As per Bhagavad Gita 3.14 Lord Krishna supports the farmers, and farmers support the whole planet.

You distorted the text. The shloka doesn’t talk about farmer but about nature. Here it says rain produces food. But you misinterpreted it as Krishna’s support for farmer.

The next shlokas 15, 16 clarifies that Krishna says do you duty and if you don’t take responsibility you are a sinner. Country is suffering huge losses due to blockade on the highway. Ambulances are getting stuck up. Delay is hampering daily activities. And government is not doing this. Farmers are being misled. Who is taking responsibility here?

2. You said — Isn't it obvious that farmers have God in support and no one can stop them?

Again it is obviously incorrect. God doesn’t support someone who backs out of their responsibilities.

So tell me who are agitating on the road? Who are blocking the roads? Are they real farmers? I don’t believe that. But if you say they are real farmers, is it their duty to block the road meant for general public and disrupt daily life? Do you think Krishna supported said he supported such irresponsible actions?

But as I maintain, the disruption isn’t caused by real farmers. A small population have been misled by power brokers who for their selfish interest misusing their innocence. And a clear proof is lack of such unrest elsewhere in the country. you know country is not just a few village around NCR but it expands from himalaya to Kanyakumari and seven sister states to kutch. And I don’t see much protest else where.

But that makes last part of your question relevant. Tell me —

How (long) can greedy capitalists and opportunistic power broker agents defeat the nation? How long can the fool innocent farmers? How long can they hold nation at ransom?

Read my answer again, where I talked about Krishna’s role as the messenger of peace and what happened when those greedy power hungry people didn’t budge and thought they can capture Krishna by deceit.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 09:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Have you read Gita 5.29? Can you point out in which word he claims to be the proprietor?

Can you also explain “सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां” in shloka 5.29? It means self-less friend of everyone. Doesn’t everyone also includes those capitalists whom you condemn. I don’t see Krishna doing that.

While you don’t have proper understanding of Gita, I am inclined to agree with you that the all prevailing God owns the entire universe.

But none of the shloka says that he has appointed any agent on his behalf to interpret his words. So why are you misinterpreting his words? If Krishna ever need to seek something from the private companies or government, don’t you think he is capable of imposing his will. Don’t you think he is capable to do so without needing your aid?

Please stop using Gita for your political agenda.

Answer: https://worldofsanatandharma.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-c-1
Creation time: Sep 26, 2021 10:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I daresay, you have not just deviated but also distorted the shloka. And you call yourself a devotee? Let us check shloka 18.44 —

कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् |
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम्

Here is mentions both Krishi (Agriculture) and Vanijya (Commerce) as part of Vaishya Varna. Now how can you condemn commerce or corporate going against Krishna’s words? He doesn’t condemn anyone.

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If you think Krishna needs you to implement his will, you are fooling yourself.

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you understand what is the meaning of becoming nimitta? It is to implement his will not your own. The first pre-requisite of becoming a nimitta is to give up your own selfish interest. Are you ready to do that?

Krishna never willed that his statements be distorted, the way you have done in this post continuously.

Krishna, who is a selfless friend of everyone, you made him a supporter to your political agenda! Is this how you respect him and his will?

You may think, you can fool few around. But do you really think, you can fool Krishna?

Good Luck trying.

Regards

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Sanjeev-Koushik
Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 07:27 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You are degrading Krishna by calling him a mere proprietor.

Do you even know the meaning of proprietor? Do you think he is a businessman who is collecting tax as offering?

But do you realize that Krishna actually condemns people like you here —

असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी || 14||

One who thinks he is a God and powerful enough. Read these arrogant lines — I'm supposed to write it down in the party manifesto, go to parliament one day or send a party member, declare the eternal proprietorship of Bhagavaan, legislate policies, track the offenders using government machinery and ensure justice on behalf of bhagavaan.

Can you see the ego of the author? He is supposed to legistlate policies. What would be the basis of these policies? Your whims? Your distortions of Gita? Your own selfish interest? All in the name of Krishna?

You are a bigger corporate than one whom you condemn. The trade in commodities, you are trying to sell Krishna.

Do you think Krishna is incapable of acting against corporate greed without an agent like you? Then you know nothing about Krishna. Do you realize why he don’t act against those corporates? Let me give you a hint. Krishna was rich and Sudama was poor. Why? Because Krishna wasn’t a communist. Krishna wanted Pandavas to become King not remain commoners. Why? Because once again he wasn’t a communist. He knew a powerful person with good heart can do more welfare than a beggar having nothing but bragging nature. He established a powerful ruler.

The corporate whom you are blindly calling profit hungry are ensuring millions of jobs. Do you know what does millions of jobs mean? Millions of less beggars on the street. Million fewer people protesting on the road side for the lack of opportunity. Krishna doesn’t punish blindly. Because Krishna knows a profession is not evil and evil can be lurking anywhere.

Don’t try to become God. Don’t try to become sole agent of Krishna. He doesn’t need an agent between him and his devotees.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Sanjeev-Koushik
Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 07:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t respond to my questions. Guess you don’t have the answer.

And please check out the meaning of proprietor. It is the head of a business.

Answer: https://worldofsanatandharma.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-c-1
Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 08:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Krishna supports farmer by sending rains. Farmers work in the field and pray for rains

You are WRONG. Either Confused or on usual mission to distort. Krishna support life by sending rains. Life that includes not only humans but also animals, plants, even the river and earth itself. Not just farmers. Do you even know rains helped sustain life much before human’s existence, long before the business of agriculture originated. I hope you realize agriculture is also business. Right? Krishna classified farmers in the Vaisya varna. Don’t dare to belittle Krishna.

You said — They don't sit in an AC room on a desk and spell out non sense like keyboard warriors as yourself. They work on the field and Lord Krishna supports them by sending rains and sunlight.

So you mean to say Krishna don’t send rain and sunlight to those who sit in AC. Is that what you mean?

And I am a keyboard warrior. Now I know you are not a warrior, but should I assume you are not using a keyboard but standing in sun and rain and these answers are getting automatically typed? Do you know what is the correct word for you — hypocrite.

I am sitting as a keyword warrior. But I am NOT distorting Krishna’s words. I am not trying to further my political agenda. And yes, I am a keyword warrior. Does Krishna condemn being a warrior who would stop someone from distorting his message?

You said — In chapter nine Lord says tapamy aham aham varsham, I send the sunlight and rains. Lord Krishna sends rains and sunlight for the farmer on the field not keyboard warriors like you.

You are again distorting. Aren’t you? Have you read the shloka? Here it is.

तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णम्युत्सृजामि च |
अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन || 19||

Now show me where Krishna said that he sent sunlight and rains only for farmers? Where did he say It is not for others? Where does he say it is not for keyboard warrior like me? You see you are again trying to further your agenda by misquoting.

Unfortunately I can’t see you even as a keyboard warrior. A mere conspirator trying to deceitfully further his agenda. I dare you show where Krishna said he sends everything for farmer and not to others.

You said — You depend on faremers not the other way around.

Krishna created a society of interdependence. In me, a teacher, he created means to propagate knowledge that is crucial to every walk of life, even agriculture.

In a soldiers he ensured defense of our borders so that farmer can work in safety. We needed traders so that a farmer who doesn’t have know how of business can focus on growing crop and get their due. There are artisans to build houses where farmer live and factories from where we buys our clothes.

Krishna’s society is of interdependence not a one way traffic. How else few corrupt agent managed to confidence a few farmer to sit on the road? Why sit on the road if they don’t depend on others?

Once again, you don’t really understand how the society works.

You said — Keyboard warriors need farmers. Farmers don't need keyboard warriors

Guess I partially agree with you here. Farmers need a keyword warrior like me to let world know about their plight. But they are condemned to have keyboard carriers like you, who can’t be honest even to God they say, they worship.

You said — Farmers need Lord Krishna. And they have Lord Krishna. The world needs farmers not keyboard warriors.

Farmers’ need Krishna… true

Farmers’ have Lord Krishna… true

The World needs farmers… true

Only famers’ need Krishna … false

Only farmers’ have Krishna … false

World doesn’t need keyboard warriors… false. Krishna said Brahmans originated from the head. I am one such person.

Krishna would support a person who distort his message… false

Krishna sent rains and sun only for farmers… false

Krishna doesn’t support non farmers… false

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 08:33 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

First things first, Parshurama didn’t put a curse perse. He stated a fact that was no less than a curse. Here is the exactly what he said.

However, he25 smiled and said, ‘You acted in this false way because of your greed for weapons. O stupid one! In a different place, when the time for your death has come, you will be engaged in a fight with someone who is your equal and the brahmastra will not manifest itself before you. The qualities of a brahmana will never remain with someone who is not a brahmana. Leave this place, since this is not meant for an untruthful one like you.

— Shanti Parva

Do you see him smiling here? Do you see him cursing? He sated a simple fact — Brahmastra won’t work for someone deceitful.

Karna did invoke Brahmastra, however it wasn’t feeble enough to be countered easily. That is what Arjuna did.

Had Parshurama’s words been without any effect, Arjuna couldn’t have done so. Remember Arjuna couldn’t counter Ashwatthama’s Brahmastra for the sake of humanity, even at the cost of losing his only grand child.

Hope that clarifies your doubt.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 08:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You used the right word now — “My Agenda”. It’s your agenda. Not Krishna’s. He never asked everyone to surrender before him. He never enforced himself. He in fact said, if some one worships other deities, I make them concentrate on whomever he worships.

A true powerful being, doesn’t seek endorsement by employing his powers. He doesn’t seek endorsements from inferior people. To seek such endorsement is the weakness of the character.

If Krishna the creator of universe really had an agenda that everyone surrenders to Krishna, we won’t be talking here.

You said — Surrender to Krishna, not to me.

Ha, Krishna won’t have use of cash donation. He won’t exert his powers in someone’s name. He won’t own properties in the name of temples. He won’t bother to live a life of luxury. This is what agents to, in the name of their deity.

If you really wanted someone to surrender in front of Krishna, be the example. First you should surrender. And the first step of that surrender would be respect his words rather than distort it for your own selfish motives.

Creation time: Sep 27, 2021 09:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Another meaningless response. Your problem is neither you understand Gita nor English.

Answer: https://worldofsanatandharma.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-c-1
Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 02:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I replied to your another comment. A servant doesn’t dictate their agenda. God didn’t appoint you to act in your behalf. He didn’t appoint you to distort his message.

Krishna doesn’t need funds. Krishna doesn’t need power to dictate his agenda. Their self appointed agents do. Self appointed agents like you think — I am the messenger of God. Today I have this much profit (influence and wealth)i have got now, now I will increase this much by my plans (agenda). I will go to parliament, cheat people in the name of Krishna and become wealthier.

Yes, Krishna did warn against people like you.

Do you how most of the demons got their powers? By proclaiming that they are God’s devotees. Do you also know what happened to them?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Sanjeev-Koushik
Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 02:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am not a farmer. I am not a politician. I am not a corporate. I have no selfish interest. I have no ambition. I am not against farmers. I am not against corporates. I don’t need to respond to your deceitful messages. Yet, if I respond, lest someone who doesn’t know Gita may get into trap and then, I would be responsible for not stopping this deceit. I am responding because this is what Krishna said in Gita.

Yes I am relaxed. I have my food. And I can enjoy my TV. Let me thank the real farmers. They are indeed working hard and doing there duty instead of sitting on the road and blocking my path of commute.

Let me warn those fake farmers, not to waste the rains and sunlight sent by Krishna. And I would warn those foolish people who think sunlight and rains are only for farmers, for they don’t fall only on non-farm lands or for other people — don’t let your deceit get better of you.

And I would advise farmers to thank everyone else, who made their life easy. They should thank factories and corporations for creating tractor and borewell to make their life easy. On occasions when Krishna tests them by not sending timely rains, the innovations of those corporate help them irrigate the land. Yes, they should thank, people who study and help them have higher yields from their limited land and artisans who make cloth for them and their family.

They should thank the teacher and doctors who look after their family and finally they should thank me, a pen warrior, who is trying to keep them safe from unscrupulous people with selfish agenda who is using farmer’s innocence and time for their own goals.

Vishnu remain in laid back position, with eyes closed and relaxed. He doesn’t go to the field and yet don’t dare to ignore his contribution to the world. Krishna doesn’t look upon any profession.

Do you how most of the demons got their powers? By proclaiming that they are God’s devotees. Do you also know what happened to them?

Now you are NO farmer. You are no pen warrior either. You too would eat. You would also distort Krishna’s lesson, You would try to further your agenda.

Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 02:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t get my point. You are not capable of endorsing Krishna. You are trying to seek and endorsement for yourself. You are trying to seek not voluntarily but forced endorsement for Krishna — My agenda is everyone follows the words of Krishna constitutionally, and accepts Krishna as the Lord and surrenders to Krishna.

That’s your agenda. Not as a servant of God but as a but to further your agenda. Krishna never forced anyone to worship him, accept him as his lord or force surrender before him.

Answer Comment

Content:

You are not capable of endorsing Krishna. Not just because you are too insignificant in front of him but because you are not worthy of it. You have no true love for Krishna. You are simply misusing his name to further your agenda.

You know all demons had same beginning. They started being the devotee of the God and then lost his true message midway.

Do you understand the meaning of above shloka? When you surrender to Krishna he would liberate you from sinful reactions!

He doesn’t say you must come to me. He doesn’t enforce his will. He doesn’t say accept me as the Lord of he universe.

If you really wish to surrender, but stop distorting the meaning of Gita and stop trying to enforce your own agenda in the name of Krishna.

Content:

Those 90% people accept Bhagwat Gita and Lord Krishna. They don’t accept you and your agenda. If they expect, they may not know how much distortion your are doing.

By the way what % of endorsement did you get when you raised this question? ZERO. Eight answers, several comments and 0 endorsement. Isn’t that right?

So take a pause. Close your eyes. Meditate. And try to find out where you went wrong. Now open your eyes and realize your mistake —

  • You presented distorted and misinterpreted Krishna’s word.
  • You misused Krishna and Git and acceptance in your favor. Don’t try to sell the Krishna. There are no buyers.

The problem is appointing yourself as an agent of Krishna, which he didn’t do.

The problem is misinterpreting his message to further your own goal.

And data does speak loud and clear — 8 responses to your question. None agreed to your skewed misrepresentation of the facts.

Should it not be a wakeup call for you?

If you really think everyone who didn’t respond agrees with you, now you have reached that final level of deceit where you are deceiving none but yourself. And that my friend, is the road of self destruction. Be aware!

This question is plain and simple distortion of facts. Every word of the question and follow up comment is attempt to misinterpret Gita. And such misinterpretation shows greed — the same greed you see in others — misusing something wonderful for your won selfish interest.

You may have noted, but understood nothing.

And if you think those who didn’t answer agree with you, then you deceive none more than yourself.

Any true devotee of Krishna or Gita would feel repulsive at such blatant and deceitful abuse of Krishna and Gita for their own selfish agenda.

And since you are taking a note, did you check your note. Have you noted — I shouldn’t misinterpret Gita for my own selfish interest? If not, your note is worthless.

Content:
