Now that you have noted, may I ask you to point one single deceit from all my answers and comments. Even one?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Sanjeev-Koushik
Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 04:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I understand your confusion about my knowledge of Mahabharata. The point is we don’t refer to same edition. I refer to the authentic version which you certainly failed to realize from my references. And you follow the fables. Let me highlight them for your —

You said — I think on 15 th day karna have chance to kill Arjuna but respect the war rules (after sunset)he spares his life.On that day indra ordered surya for early sunset because indra want to save his son's life.

Answer: That is something you think. It never really happened. I am sure you can’t quote any authentic reference. Naturally you would find my reference to authentic version alien.

You said — When Arjuna shot arrow karna chariot goes 10 foots back.When karna fires an arrow arjuna chariot goes 3 to 4 feets back.Always krishna praises karna.when Arjuna asked krishna about appreciation he tells In your chariot have me,you,hanuman and vishwakarma illusions but karna chariot have human being. So you can understand who is power ful.

Answer: Once again, this is not a narrative from authentic versions of the epic and once again you would fail to show a single reference from Mahabharata. So you can understand, who knows more Mahabharata.

You said — In final battle god's compare Karna looks like Shiva and Arjuna with Indra,so understand who is powerful.Lird Brahma and Lord Shiva confirms karna is formost of all heroes and krishna also said same.

Answer: Here is what Shiva and Brahma said —

Brahma and Ishana760 spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone. Karna, foremost in the worlds, is a bull among men. Vaikartana is brave and valiant. But let the two Krishnas be victorious.

If only you see what they exactly said you would realize —

  • He never said Karna is invincible. But he did say the same thing about Arjuna. No one is capable of standing before them.
  • He decreed that Karna would die.

You said — Krishna saved Arjuna many times, i.e Nagastra, Brahmastra.

Answer: I agree. Krishna himself said he manifested to protect the good and destroy the evil. He protected Arjuna. Called him his inseparable existence. He asked Arjuna to destroy Karna (evil).

You said — Arjuna don't know the knowledge how to counter the great Bhargavastra.

Answer: So what happened to Bharagvastra? Did it kill Arjuna? Did it kill any major general? Did it win the war for Karna? The fact is it could have been countered by any higher weapon, but Krishna realized it wasn’t worth their time and asked Arjuna to let us go and meet Yudhishthira. He thought Karna would be tired of holding it. As you yourself said Krishna protected Karna from Naryanastra and Vaishnavastra. Neither were fired by Karna. And he didn’t feel the need of protecting Arjuna from feeble attacks of Karna. It wasn’t worth their time.

You said — On 17th day Karna made Arjuna unconscious and he didn't not kill him because it against the war rules

Answer: BORI removed this reference as it is not present in most ancient narratives. (You see I know more Mahabharata than you). Moreover don’t speak of Karna’s war ethics. Wasn’t he the same person who attacked unarmed Abhimanyu and Bhimasena? Wasn’t he the same person who kept running away from the battle leaving his brothers to be slaughtered and friend to be imprisioned?

You said — Krishna confirms karna is superior to Arjuna many times.

You haven’t read the epic then. Krishna never called Karna superior. He certainly said Karna with vasvi shakti or armor is invincible. But if you read, it was not Karna’s praise but that of those divine artifacts. He also said Karna without vasvi shakti and armor is as good as slain. He compared him to a snake whose poison is gone.

On 17th day Krishna was frustrated by the fact as why Arjuna is letting Karna attack when he should be killing him. He asked why is Arjuna not concentrating? Can you see who is praised here?

And most important, Krishna called Karna evil more times than he praised.

You asked — So now you tell you is more powerful.

Answer: Let me consult some war experts and what they said.

Shiva — Arjuna (see the reference above)

Krishna — Arjuna (see Bhagwatyaan Parva)

Parshurama — Arjuna (see Bhagwatyaan Parva)

Bhishma — Arjuna (see Udyoga Parva)

Drona, Kripacharya, Ashwatthama — Arjuna

And behold now. Do you know what Karna realized before his own death? Here is what Karna said —

Karna (About Arjuna) — Where is the warrior on earth who is his equal?

Now let us see how they fared against each other in various battles —

  1. Gurudakshina Battle — Karna fled. Arjuna imprisoned Drupad
  2. Draupadi’s swyamver battle — Karna fled, scared from Arjuna comparing him with Indra, Vishnu and Parshurama.
  3. Gandharva Battle — Karna fled deserting his best friend who was imprisoned. Arjuna defeated a larger army of Gandharva including their chief.
  4. Matasya Battle — Karna fled thrice leaving his brother to be killed by Arjuna
  5. Kurukshetra Day 14 — Karna was badly beaten by Arjuna and Bhimasena and he fails to protect Jaidradh. Arjuna announces that He would kill Karna’s son in his presence.
  6. Kurukshetra Day 16 — Karna concedes defeat from Arjuna and promises to do better next day.
  7. Kuruksheta Day 17 — Arjuna fulfils his words. He kills Karna’s son in his presence and he could do nothing. He realizes there is no one who is Arjuna’s equal in the world. He finally meets his death after failing at his last plea for reccess.

So can you guess who is more powerful?

Now may I ask you a question?

What is more important — to be powerful or to be a good human?

Even demons like Ravaan and Hiranyakashyapa were powerful. Does that mean, people should cherish their power? What is the worth of such a power?

Why are you defending an evil, deceitful, conspiring, coward who was also a molester of woman? Even if you had read one chapter of Mahbharata, the first chapter, you would know he is referred and trunk of the the evil tree ‘Duryodhana’. Do you really want to defend an evil person?

He was part of every heinous act of Duryodhana which was confirmed not only by Krishna but he himself accepted he is one of the cause of upcoming war that would destroy the earth. Do you really want to side with such an evil?

How does it matter, if he found station with Adityas after death? Souls are immortal. Question is what did he achieve during his lifetime?

  • Did he protect a woman in distress or tried to burn down a bride for having failed to win swyamver?
  • Did he tried to protect a distressed woman or tried to strip her in public?
  • What were his great achievements in life that was dedicated to the society?

I don’t know which Mahabharata you really know that tells you that Karna wasn’t evil. I may not have read that Mahabharata, because I read only authentic one.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 06:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think you are talking about yourself. I didn’t attack you personally till you started the personal attack calling me “keyboard warrior” and having “capitalistic agenda”

You started Ad hominem.

Let us see from the beginning —

  1. I answered the question without knowing who asked this question. It was an answer to question and not to you.
  2. You responded to my answer with these words — Your answer shows bias more than anything else. Now you would know who started personal attack. You had no contradiction to my answer, so you blindly called me biased without assigning the reason. That was our first direct interaction where YOU started Ad hominem.
  3. I responded by pointing out the misinterpretations. Once again you couldn’t contradict my statements.
  4. Then you started the slogan of “keyboard warrior”

I guess you recall the chain of incidents and who started the personal attacks.

Now coming to your other points —

You said —When you couldn't find anything wrong in my presentation

WRONG! I pointed out all the misinterpretation of Gita your presented. Every single shloka. And I insist you misrepresented them starting with Gita 3.14. It doesn’t talk about farmer. It wasn’t difficult to point out flaws in your argument. In fact you couldn’t contradict one of my interpretations.

You said — Secondly, not admitting your vieled capitalistic agenda to oppose Gita as constitution and Krishna as the proprietor.

Ad hominem. Once again. There is nothing vieled about me. I have NO capitalistic agenda. I am not a corporate businessman, nor I am a politician nor having any agenda against real farmers, nor any reason to distort the Gita.

So tell me why did you call be deceitful? Did I distort any single shloka of Gita? Can you point that? Still you called me deceitful.

Your calling Krishna as a proprietor (a business term) is derogatory salutation for Krishna.

And to make my stand clear — I have nothing to do with your political agenda. But I reiterate Krishna doesn’t operate via agents and that is something mentioned in Gita —

यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्

Do you know the meaning of सृजाम्यहम्? Krishna says he manifests himself. He doesn’t get his will implemented via his agents or brokers.

I oppose any effort of brokers pronouncing their will in the name of Krishna. Krishna too won’t be pleased.

Would you call my last statement against you? Or is it pro Krishna? Can you tell this is not what exactly Krishna said?

If you want philosophical debate,

  1. Stop personal attacks that you started even before I knew it was your question.
  2. Contradict my interpretations if you can. I have countered all your interpretations with valid definitions.
  3. Proof with example what deceit did I adopt? Even if I attacked you personally in response to your first attack, it can’t be call deceit.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/As-per-Bhagavad-Gita-3-14-Lord-Krishna-supports-the-farmers-and-farmers-support-the-whole-planet-Then-how-can-greedy-capitalists-and-opportunistic-politicians-defeat-them-Isnt-it-obvious-that-farmers-have-God-in/answer/Sanjeev-Koushik
Creation time: Sep 28, 2021 07:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — I said your answer is biased in favour of the greedy capitalists, to whom the question was directed at.

That’s a personal attack. The question was not directed at them but on one of the Gita Sholka which you were distorting.

When I pointed out the attack, you called names like keyboard warrior.

You said — Proprietor is derogatory? Now that is a distortion.

Calling Krishna as owner of business or a holder of property is indeed derogatory. The term is almost always used in relation to a business and ‘proprietorship’ is granted by an authority. Do you mean there is a greater authority than Krishna to have granted him the proprietorship?

You may choose to continue to argu with me or may improve your vocabulary. Your choice.

You said — How can Bhagavad Gita as constitution be political agenda?

Read you own earlier answer. You mentioned it multiple time — My Agenda is…

And as I reminded multiple times, quoting Krishna himself — he doesn’t act via agents. He manifests himself. And it is not his agenda we are talking here.

You said — You have distorted things, and started pointing to my intentions as political.

Can you verify you don’t have a political agenda here? Is your idea of going to Parliament not political? Is ambition to write constitution not political? Whom are you trying to bluff?

You your self mentioned you have a political agenda. In fact you mentioned something “KCP” and I it has a written set of agenda. Am I distorting?

You said — I want to end power politics forever.

Read the above sentence again. It is what you want. Not Krishna. Did he try to end political power, by empowering Pandavas?

Do you know what is the only way to “end power”? It is by amassing a greater power.

If you really cared to be the servant of Krishna you won’t be distorting his messages.

Meanwhile, in comment you still failed to highlight which portion of Gita I distorted? You blamed me for distorting and misusing Gita. Can you prove that?

You proved you don’t want a healthy debate and you still couldn’t point out which shloka I distorted. You know the truth.

You end your comment by asking a question and then blocking option to answer that. Great!

Now don’t expect another response from me.

Now we both know, you can debate no more. You proved by blocking my option to answer you.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Gandhi-responsible-for-Indias-partition/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 30, 2021 05:17 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If you don’t want to tell, then you don’t need to tell. But since you already said saying you don’t want to say here is your answer.

You said — I know you won't tell that Arjuna loosed to Karna in swaymavar and other warriors were clapping for Karna…

You are right. I won’t tell a fake tale. As per authentic reference, Karna retreated scared. Which winning warrior would compare a loser with Indra, Vishnu and Parshurama?

As far as Kings clapping, people clap even for a team destined to lose a match, if their favorite player strikes a good shot. Do you remember, why they clapped? Had he hurt Arjuna? No. Just repulsed his arrows.

By the way I will ask you — Do you know what was the battle about? Karna wanted to burn Draupadi alive. Arjuna wanted to protect her. Who succeeded?

You said — Karna and he retreated against Brahmin because he didn't wanna kill him.

You are mistaken. Karna attacked Arjuna knowing he was a Brahmin. The Kings had decided it is permissible to kill a brahmin willing to fight.

What I don’t believe is why would you praise a person whose goal was to burn a bride?

Do you know Duhsasana and every other King saw Karna failed and that is when they decided not to fight any further. There was no change of heart. Just the raw fear —

When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened

You said — Karna was not having his Kavacha and Kundal in Virat war and on the other side Arjuna was having Number of Advantages.

Neither did Arjuna had any divine Kavacha or Kundala. Both had divyastras. Karna himself confirmed during the Virata battle. Karna had a large army and support of warriors like Bhishma and Drona. Arjuna was alone. Guess who had number of advantages?

But I agree, Arjuna had two great advantage —

  • He was a better warrior than Karna, right from gurukul days.
  • He was fighting to protect a kingdom from looters. Karna was one of the looters.

I will ask you this — Why do you want to support a looter?

You said — you won't tell that Gandharvas were friends of Arjuna and were fighting mildly against him. I know you won't tell that Karna was outnumbered by Gandharvas. You say that Karna was not able to defeat Chitrasen's illusion

Yes, you are right again. I would say NO such things. Because I understand the difference between the word ‘mild’ and ‘feeble’. Arjuna fought ‘mildly’, Chitrasen fought ‘feebly’. And there is a big difference.

Arjuna faced a larger army than Karna and the attack was more fierce. You too would have known, had you read the epic.

Arjuna victory against Gandharvas has been mentioned in superlative terms by great warriors of that era who weren’t fools! Only fools would call Arjuna’s victory over Gandharvas as anything but heroic.

I don’t know why would you support a coward who fled leaving his best friend and women of the family behind?

You said — Karna defeated Ghatotkach and shattered his Illusion which were 5x stronger than of Chitrasen.

You know what I would say here? This is how you people distort the epic. Show me a reference that says Gandharvas illusion was 5x stronger. You are imaginining.

But it doesn’t matter. Karna failed and fled from Gandharvas. You can’t change that face.

Neither can you change the fact that Karna failed against Ghatotkatch and won only by using Indra’s spear.

You said — Can't believe you all hate Karna so much that can't even depend on single Source you started changing your book of source to Prove Arjuna better.

YES. Believe It. I hate evils. I hate treacherous, heinous cowards. I had abuser of woman. I hate someone who would try to burn down a woman because he failed to win her. I hate someone who try to strip a woman in public.

And I have reluctantly started to believe that there are people who can support even those evils. So I am not surprised to see your rebuttal.

By the way where did I change my source? Which authentic version of Mahabharata doesn’t state what I have stated?

What I really can’t believe is people, in defense of a cowardly treacherous villain, create excuses that doesn’t figure in any authentic book of Mahabharata such as —

  • Ghatotkatch’s illusion was 5x of Gandharvas.
  • Gandharvas fought mildly.
  • Karna was drunk during Gandharva battle.
  • Karna donated his Kavacha without forcing an exchange.
  • Karna didn’t fail Draupadi Swaymvara, some other Karna failed.
  • Karna won Draupadi’s swyamver battle because he didn’t want to kill a Brahmin.
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 07:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Then please have a good laugh.

I don’t need to share screenshots, to prove something, you already know. And can’t make you see things you don’t want to see.

Do you want a prove of how you manipulate things?

I never said Karna lost to Ghatotkach. I said he failed against him, failed to win against him without Indra’s weapon. Can you tell me why else, he used that weapon against Ghatotkatch, that was his only hope against Arjuna? Why didn’t he kill him outright?

You said — Karna was on wrong side just because Duryodhan was his friend.

I agree. Karna was on the wrong side. But not just because Duryodhana was his friend. Duryodhana didn’t ask to strip Draupadi, Karna did. He didn’t deceive Parshurama for Duryodhana, but for himself. It wasn’t Duryodhana’s idea to go and mock Pandava’s in exile, It was Karna’s.

And when it comes to his friendship, yes I have read about it —

  • He fled leaving his friend behind. What a great friend?
  • He promised to spare the life of his friend’s nemesis because they were his brother. Great friendship!
  • He remained absent from the Kurukshetra war for 10 days for his arrogance. His arrogance was greater than his friendship.

But can you say, you are a truthful person?

The truth is you never had a liking for my answer. I shattered nothing for you.

Do you want to hear another truth?

Weather you would like to admit or not, you still know I am a learned person. So as I said, I shattered nothing for you. And you know it.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 08:36 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Nice to see you loved it. I knew you would.

You said — if some one addresses you it you all change the meaning of it. What does one mean by fail haa. I think loosing and defeating can be some sort of synonym to fail.

Are you sure, I changed the meaning. Are you sure fail only means defeat and not inability to win? You know here the defining would is “You think”. And my answer was, what “I think”.

You may say, I needed to clarify my stand. Let me do so once again so that you don’t get confused any further — Karna with all his powers couldn’t control Ghatotkatch, couldn’t subdue him, couldn’t stop him from creating the havoc, couldn’t control him with any of his divine weapons. This was a failure. It doesn’t really matter if he countered some illusion or not. End result was Kauravas were terrified. Karna was at his wit end. And he saw only one solution — Invoke Indra’s spear. He must have been thankful for the exchange.

Do I need to be more clear on this?

You asked — Can you please provide me texts where Karna tried to strip Draupadi?

Since you asked so nicely, I will certainly give you a proof. And no, it won’t be from my book on Mahabharata. Let us stick to the authentic version —

Droupadi was mentioned in the speech and the Pandava approved. According to what reason do you then think that she has not been won? If you think that bringing her into the sabha when she is clad in only a single garment is against dharma, listen to the words I have to say in response. O descendant of the Kuru lineage!213 It has been ordained by the gods that a woman should only have one husband. However, she submits to many and it is therefore certain that she is a courtesan. It is my view that there is nothing surprising in her being brought into the sabha in a single garment, or even if she is naked. In accordance with dharma, Soubala has won all the riches the Pandavas possessed, including her and themselves. O Duhshasana! This Vikarna is only a child, though he speaks words of wisdom. Strip away the garments from the Pandavas and Droupadi.”

— Dyut Parva, BORI

Before you jump and said — Ho Karna didn’t do it himself — think again! He commanded it. Duhshasana just obeyed his order. Do you understand the meaning of command? Do you know commander is responsible for his subordinate’s actions?

While we are here, let us also clarify the matter of misrepresentation. Karna said —It has been ordained by Gods. Do you know what God had ordained? Shiva himself has ordained — Draupadi would have five husbands. Now you know who is a deceitful liar here.

But Karna’s abuse of Draupadi didn’t end even here. Do you want another proof —

O tiger among kings! The birth of a son or the obtaining of a kingdom does not bring as much bliss as seeing one’s enemies in misery. What happiness will there not be, on being successful oneself, and on seeing Dhananjaya attired in bark and skins? Let your wives, attired in excellent garments, see the miserable Krishna, attired in bark and skins, and thereby increase her grief.

—Ghosha Yatra, BORI

Can you imagine any decent human being to find bliss is seeing someone else in misery? Can you imagine a decent human being lust over someone else’s grief?

Just think! whom are you really siding with?

You said about Karna’s absence from the war for first 10 days - Don't you know it wasn't his arrogance it was Bhism's arrogance of categorizing an Maharathi even less than Rathi and you also know why Bhishm told that to Karna.

Let us agree, Bhishma had some hidden agenda. May be he didn’t wanted an arrogant warrior to fight under his banner and zeopardize his strategy the way he did in Matasya battle. And Bhishma did provide factual reason to prove his points. But what Bhishma did or said isn’t important.

Question is what Karna did and why he did that? Bhishma called him less than a Rathi and he refused to fight. How was this not his arrogance? He refused to fight because his ego was hurt.

But tell me, why was Karna supposed to fight in the first place? Was it for Bhishma? No. It was for his so called best friend Duryodhana, who he had promised his support. Bhishma insulted him. No he had to pick between his ego and his loyalty to Duryodhana. Tell me what did he pick?

Let me give you more example. Dronacharya too was insulted on occasion by Duryodhana and Karna. Did he refuse to fight? No because his loyalty to Bhishma was greater than that of his personal ego.

That said you may be interested to see what was the exact reason Karna assigned for not fighting under Bhishma’s command. Here is what he said in Bhsihma Abhisechan Parva —

The sacred texts certainly say that we must listen to the aged. But it is also the view that one should not listen to the extremely old. They are like children. O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the commander is credited with qualities, never the warriors. O king! I will never fight as long as Gangeya is alive. But once Bhishma has been slain, I will fight with all the maharathas.’”’

Karna tries to provoke Duryodhana that he shouldn’t listen to Bhishma. He insults Bhishma. But I don’t see Bhishma refusing to fight. Karna could have done that. But then, there was another reason which Karna himself explains — He feared, he won’t get credit for his achievements. And that was the true reason why he refused to fight. Rest is mere conjuncture.

Can you imagine how thankless and ungrateful one can get? Duryodhana gave him all the prosperity of the world and in his real hours of needs —

  • He runs away to save his own life (Gandharva Battle)
  • He refuses to fight because he won’t get credit of his acts!

And yet, I know, there would always be people who would always admire evil. Why else does the evil flourish?

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 03, 2021 12:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Karna wants to see Pandavas(they are male) and Droupadi (she is female) without clothes. So it's totally meaningless that Karna wants to see Pandavas naked.

Now this is called misrepresentation. Question isn’t what you think is meaningless. The question is what Karna said. And I think reference is clear here.

Now coming to what you think is meaningless is your limitation to find out the meaning. It is clear He wanted to see them naked. He wanted to humiliate them. Even modern psychology confirms, many rapes are not for sexual pleasure but to exert your power. Of course only psychotics and depraved people derive satisfaction in such saddish actions. And as I mentioned earlier it is consistent with Karna’s character when he planned to Ghosha yatra.

You said — So we can understand by this that he was asking Dusashan to strip there Royal clothes.

Now that make me wonder if you have not read the epic or just trying to distort it. Draupadi is mentioned to have been wearing a single garment — royal or not. And he clearly mentions that she could even be brought naked. Is there really a room for confusion or distortion?

You said — We all know Karna is one of the Characters of Mahabharata who give his respect and his social status and recognition the first place then there are otherthings.

Where did you get that? When did he respect the social order?

  • He got his education by deceit.
  • He gate-crashed talent showcase that was exclusively meant to show the talent of Hastinapur Princess. He wasn’t one of them. Then he tried to misrepresent it as an open ceremony.
  • He had no reason to fight Arjuna yet he sought a fight.
  • He on multiple occasion insulted his own guru Drona and Kripacharya. Remember Matasya incident.
  • He reduced Hastinapur army to a band of looters by suggesting to attack Matasya.
  • He openly asked Duryodhana not to listen to Bhishma who was the general. Is it respecting social status?

You said — So taking advantage of this thing Bhishm, Dron and Shalya always insult him by taunting him always for his failure. So how long a man would not react those things haa. So when he finally reacted to it you all started taking it as negative thing but you won't see the mistake of Drona, Bhishm and Shalya.

Karna never failed to return any abuse to Drona, Kripacharya or Bhishma. The difference is Drona and Kripa were his guru and had right to remind him of his repeated failures. And reminder of failure while hurting can’t be called insult. But Karna shouting back at his guru surely is a different thing all together.

Now the question as to how long a man won’t react depends on the man and his priorities. As I reminded, Duryodhana and his arrogant friend (Karna) insulted Drona and Bhishma on multiple occasions. But they never deserted Duryodhana. Drona and Bhishma were reprimanded even during battle. They didn’t desert him.

And say, even if I agree with you (which I am proved otherwise) Bhishma, Drona and Krispacharya insulted Karna. Did Duryodhana insult him? Why did he ditch Duryodhana who was always fair to him? Can you call it a true friend?

Why did he ditch Duryodhana during Gandharva battle? He had invincible kavacha and Kunda. He was not going to die. Duryodhana had no such armor. Bhishma and Drona hadn’t insulted him. So why did he ditched Duryodhana?

Did he ever tell Duryodhana that I wouldn’t kill Pandavas? That fool prince, trusting his friend got his 31 brothers slaughtered to protect him. Did he ever tell him the truth?

Karna may have been humiliated. But that doesn’t change the fact that his own life, his own ego always had precedence over his friendship. For him friendship was secondary, his ego was primary.

Now if you want to continue discussion come up with something original. And stop distorting facts in favor of that evil.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 03, 2021 03:30 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — He died for that same friend.

Many warriors died in the battle include Shalya, Bhagdatta, Bhurisrava and Jaidradha. Does that mean they all were equally great friend of Karna? Thousands of the soldiers too died.

Death in a battle is what we call as “professional hazard”

But specially in case of Karna, let us see. He didn’t lift his finger to protect any of Duryodhana’s brother who were slaughtered by Bhimasena. Thirtyone died tried to save him. Is this friendship?

You may be tempted to say Karna had taken a pledge not to kill Bhima. But remember, Karna gave the pledge to Kunti unilaterally. She never asked to spare the life of her sons. And Karna knew sparing Bhima would be fatal to Duryodhana. Is this friendship?

Talking about his pledge. He made an exception for Arjuna. Why? Was Arjuna a thread to Duryodhana’s life? No. It was to satisfy his own ambition for proving himself superior to Arjuna, which he was not and he realized before he finally died.

If he were a true friend, he wouldn’t have taken a pledge which went against is friend. And remember he didn’t fight the first ten days for his ego.

You said — He was the confidence booster for Duryodhana he later explained why he retreated from battle of Gandharvas.

Yes. He boosted his confidence and also saved his face. And he did explained why he retreated — he feared for his own life.

You said — And secondly how can a woman wear single cloth explain me. It's totally problem in translation or it is interplotation.

If you can’t imagine how can a woman wear a single cloth, it is the limitations of your imagination and not an interpolation. Thousands of women do it even today. You may find an explanation of how in movie Ram Teri Ganga Maili by Raj Kapoor.

But don’t make a fool of yourself by calling anything you don’t understand or can’t explain as interpolation.

Your last question also proved, there is a lot you don’t understand.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 04, 2021 07:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Karna helped Duryodhana in marrying Banumathi by fighting with other kings which included Jarasandha too and Karna defeated everyone - for Duryodhana. Again, for Duryodhana, Karna conquered the entire India, conducted Vaishnava sacrifice and made Duryodhana the emperor!

Karna certainly helped Duryodhana on many occasion. Karna was also a reputed warrior and won many battles. But this is something any aide of a King would do for him.

But there is a difference between a true friend and a causal friend. And the difference is not apparent in the days of prosperity but in adversity. There is no real greatness when you won a battle and had no threat to your life.

In all the battles you mentioned, Karna didn’t face a warrior to match him. He had no threat to his life. And his friendship wasn’t really tested.

But on every occasion his friendship was tested, Karna backed out. He fled leaving Duryodhana behind. He let his brothers get slaughtered and still fled. He saw his own brothers slaughtered and he fled. His ego was hurt, and he again fled.

You can call Duryodhana a true friend. He never reprimand Karna for his failure or for deserting him. He let his brother’s die to save his worthless life of that friend who didn’t even tell him why he is letting his brothers die and not killing Bhima. Duryodhana must have thought Karna was just too weak to face Bhima. He may not have imagined him to be treacherous liar that he was. Did he ever tell Duryodhana that he won’t kill Bhima? Or Yudhishthira?

Action, and not words, define true friend. Adversary, and not prosperity, defines true friends. But I doubt you would understand this. You are busy defending a coward.

You said — It was totally the fault of Draupadi why she called him Suta Putra and insulted him in whole sabha.

Ok. Thanks for confirming by suspicion. You don’t know Mahabharata. Draupadi never insulted Karna by calling him a Suta. Suta wasn’t an insult. Karna was an invited guest and the same has been categorically mentioned by Prof Vishnu Suthankar (You may not have heard his name).

Almost all the ancient recitation have mentioned Karna failed at the Swyamver.

But just to humor you (only because you don’t know correct Mahabharata), say Draupadi didn’t wanted to marry him. She said I won’t marry a Suta that Karna was. Now tell me how was calling a suta, as a suta is insult? Was it not her right to refuse marriage? Does that mean any girl who rejects a boy deserves to be raped? Does that give the boy right to strip her in public?

Do you know in modern days there are many incident when a girl rejects a boy, he either rapes her or throw acid at her face. Karna first tried to burn her alive but his effort wat thwart by Arjuna. Then he tried to srip her in public and his attempt was again thwart.

Do you realize what does your argument make Karna — an acid thrower.

First you thought, Karna didn’t try to strip him. Now that you are proved wrong, you started supporting his action? How low can you stoop to defend a molester?

Now your final question — And what about your great Arjuna trying to Kill his elder brother between the war. I never thought he would do that but sadly it is what it is.

That makes him great. People get angry at times and may do something stupid. But the greatness is realizing his mistakes and not carrying out his actions.

And before you say, Karna too regretting, let me burst that myth of yours pre-emptively. Regret shouldn’t just be in words but also action. Arjuna didn’t really attack Yudhishthira.

  • Karna tried to burn Druapadi alive soon after the swaymver.
  • Many years later, he tried to strip her in public. Having failed to strip, he continued to hurl abuses at her.
  • Still many years later he still hoped to torment her in jungle and had lust to see her in tree bark.

Do you see the difference between great Arjuna and evil Karna? I bet you see but would not really see.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 04, 2021 07:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I already answered this question. Not because they really have admiration for Nathuram Godsey a heinous murderer. Nor any real respect for Netaji Shubhash Chandra Bose, else they won’t be questioning why Gandhiji is known as the father of the nation.

They only feel condemning Gandhi is condemning current congress and I repeat the decayed and dead congress has no real relationship with the Mahatma. They don’t follow his ideals and they are not worth wasting time.

Unfortunately condemning Gandhi is condemning one of the greatest legacy that boosts India’s respect on international arena like no other.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Modi-Shah-BJP-trying-to-defame-Gandhiji/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 04, 2021 08:02 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — You are really talking about Ram Teri Ganga maili film just feeling disgust.

Glad you know the feeling of disgust. But why do you feel disgusted on seeing a woman clad in garment and not the idea of stripping it off in public.

You said — you claim to be an neutral fan of Mahabharata but reality is you are fan of Arjuna who was always favoured by society, gods and devtas.

I never ever claimed myself to be neutral. A fan true. Neutral NO. I am biased towards Dharama. And Dharma alone. I am biased against Adharma.

And Make no mistake. Even Gods are biased, the same way as I am. And that is why he always favored one who had Dharma in him.

You talk as if being biased is bad and being neutral is good. Let me tell you he who says he is neutral is a liar. And if he is not a liar, he is a fool. For being neutral means equidistant from good and bad. And that means having a liking for bad as much as for good.

You talk as if Gods favor to Arjuna was a undeserving freebie and it makes Arjuna weak. You are mistaken. Gods favor is the single litmus paper testimony of his greatness and that is why the epic start with a saluation to Nara (Arjuna) and Narayana (Krishna). Guess you don’t agree with the epic.

You said — If Karna was backed up by greats gods and devtas then there would have been no competition between Arjuna and Karna.

I completely agree with you. 100%. But most importantly with your starting word — ‘If’. And that should be the key takeaway for you or anyone else admiring an evil man.

God didn’t backup Karna. They don’t backup evil. But do you know what is the precondition for getting God’s grace? It is not to wait for them to reach out, but to reach out to them. Arjuna did reach out to Krishna and Shiva.

But the most unfortunate are people, whom God himself reaches out to and they push them back — because they fail to see what a golden opportunity they got. It happened when Rama reached out to Ravan via Angada. And it happened again when Krishna reached out to Karna. But evils never accept the grace and condemned themselves to be destroyed. That is the moral of the epic.

If Karna was backedup… But he wasn’t. Not because Gods were blind, but because he was blinded by his lust, ambition, arrogance and life long tryst with evil deeds.

You seem to think, Karna was not supported by God is some kind of plus point in his favor. Don’t That is a testimony of his character well sketched by Krishna in the Karna Vadha Parva.

You said — no one can change your settled mindset.

Agree. I don’t think, I would be inclined towards evils anytime soon.

You said — You will always ignore bad things of Arjuna

I think you already said, Gods always favored Arjuna. I am just a humble human. If Shiva couldn’t see bad in Arjuna, Krishna couldn’t see bad in him, how do you expect me to have such a terrible evil eye to see bad? I have unquestionable devotion to Shiva, Krishna and Dharma.

You said — you will always point out bad things of Karna.

What can I say. I can see no good in a treacherous conniving, molester, ungrateful evil being who would like to molest a woman, boast in comfort and run away at the first sign of danger living even dear ones behind. Duryodhana was his greatest fan of that era. See what he got in return?

You said — Just become optimistic your perspective will change.

Do you mean, by becoming optimistic, I would start to side with evil? Is this your definition of optimistic?

You said — I never hated Arjuna but the reason I started hating him is because of his fans.

So you started hating because you heard so many good things about Arjuna from Arjuna fans, after knowing Gods supported him, and the epic starts by his salutation?

And you like for Karna continued to grow despite of hearing all the evil, unjustifiable things about Karna and knowing that Krishna and Shiva wanted him to be dead?

Are you sure that is what you mean?

My Last Question —

I Know you don’t want to debate further and would understand if you decide not to respond, and responding may even be difficult. I take it you are optimistic. I take it that more Gods favoured Arjuna more you started to hate him. Just tell me one thing —

What optimism, what good deeds of Karna made you his follower?

Why are you not feeling disgusted with a person who —

  • Tried to molest a woman under any circumstance?
  • Fled away from battle scared for his life abandoning his best friend and greatest benefactor behind?

Can you tell me one great thing of Karna for which you claim yourself to be Karna’s fan?

  • What was his contribution to the society?
  • Did he ever protect a person, a family, a village from any catastrophe?
  • Did he help any distressed family by putting himself on the line of fire?
  • Did he try to protect any kingdom from a bunch of looters?

What are the honorable acts of Karna that made you his fan?

I would wait for your answer, but understand if you decide to ignore it.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 04, 2021 11:11 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

He was worse than whatever you can feel about him.

Here is a few sanskrit line for you —

एको भर्ता स्त्रिया देवैर्विहितः कुरुनन्दन |

इयं त्वनेकवशगा बन्धकीति विनिश्चिता ||३५||

Now a here is the definition of बन्धकीति

Hope that clarifies the distortion. Neither meaning is used in an honorable sense. And if someone has distorted it, look no further than Karna. He said God ordained the rule. But the fact is Shiva ordained Draupadi to have five husbands. So which God was Karna talking about?

Now that you want sanskrit, let talk more about it —

अस्याः सभामानयनं न चित्रमिति मे मतिः |

एकाम्बरधरत्वं वाप्यथ वापि विवस्त्रता ||३६||

Read the highlighted text. Karna won’t be surprised if she is brought without a dress. But alas, she was brought in a single garment. So what did he do?

दुःशासन सुबालोऽयं विकर्णः प्राज्ञवादिकः |

पाण्डवानां च वासांसि द्रौपद्याश्चाप्युपाहर ||३८||

तच्छ्रुत्वा पाण्डवाः सर्वे स्वानि वासांसि भारत |

अवकीर्योत्तरीयाणि सभायां समुपाविशन् ||३९

He asks Dussasana to strip Druapadi and Pandavas of their garments.

Did you find any sanskrit destortion when he asks Dussasana to strip Draoupadi? Or when he advances towards Drupad and his family with the intention to burn him alive? Or again when he lusts to see Draupadi miserable in forest in tree barks?

He was a deceitful, treacherous, coward and a molester who can be compared with modern day acid throwers (whom some may find romantic hero)

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-the-Mahabharata-why-didnt-Karna-protect-Draupadi-instead-taking-part-in-harassing-her/answer/Chandan-Yadav-28
Creation time: Oct 04, 2021 11:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Long Post!

But no answer. You didn’t answer even one question I asked.

All you quoted is what people said about him, not any proof of his greatness. I must bust your myths. People have often spoken in superlative terms even about insignificant people. They have been called as Indra among men. You may have noticed those things had you read the epic. Alas!

Let me point out a few. Before approaching Karna, Krishna went to Duryodhana and offered him support of Pandava only if he agreed to a peaceful proposal. Does that make Duryodhana less evil? No he was trying to achieve peace! Period.

Krishna has said a few things at places —

  • Karna can’t be defeated with his kavach even by Krishna himself. Do you agree? Gandharvas certainly defeated him with Kavacha. So were Gandharavas more powerful than Krishna?
  • Krishna says that Karna is about to kill Yudhishthira was wasn’t a great Kshatriya warrior. Should we agree Karna lied to Kunti and he intended to kill Yudhisthira? That also means he could have killed Bhima but failed because he was a weak warrior? I think you would agree.
  • Krishna says that Shalya is more powerful than Karna, Bhishma and Drona. Do you agree? He also says that only Yudhisthira has capacity to kill him. Do you agree? If that is true then, why Krishna earlier said, Yudhisthira wasn’t a great Kshatriya warrior?

You said — If karna promise kunti not to kill his four brothers then he becomes someone who betrayed duryodhana.

Is there another way to express it? He did betray Duryodhana. Didn’t even tell him why he didn’t kill Bhima. Didn’t tell him he won’t kill them.

You said — if he kill was involved in abhimanyu's killing then he becomes heartless?

NO. That simply makes him a coward. He didn’t have the guts to fight directly. He even fled Abhimanyu as the later killed his brother.

The main problem

First things first, Karna wasn’t actually supposed to promise anything to Kunti. She didn’t ask him to make such a promise. Once he made the promise, she certainly ask him to hold on to it.

Second, if he had made a promise and betrayed Duryodhana, at least he should have told him not to count on him to kill Pandavas. The foolish Duryodhana continued to trust him till the very end.

You asked — Now tell me how an Super evil person obtained place among Adityas.

Karna the evil person was dead. One who obtained a place among Adityas was the soul, which was part of Surya, an Aditya. And don’t forget he languished in hell for 34 years, till Yudhishthira freed him from there. That was the purification process for expelling the evil Karna action from the soul.

You said — Although I am surprised and glad that you have 1.2 million views on quora I appreciate it. You are totally an newbie who haven't read Mahabharat. And has gained knowledge through some serials and quora answers.

Do you see your problem now? You have a habit of thinking other fools when you can’t answer a question! You think, like Karna it is already to personally abuse other. I have been courteous through out. Didn’t personally attack you. I attacked your logic.

You couldn’t answer one question so far. And you decided, like Karna, it’s best to abusing others? Wow!

But did you realize you aren’t mocking just my knowledge but also all those people who follow me.

And worst, you are again lying to yourself. For even you know the fact if I know Mahabharata or not. So very Karna like!

Now I can see, your only refuse is some quotes which were either condolence or persuasion to do the right thing. You have no real answer.

So, ignoring your abusive tone, I would ask once again — Can you point out one great deed of Karna that makes him a hero?

  • Did he protect some woman in distress as people tried to burn her down?
  • Did he protect a women when someone tried to strip her in public?
  • Did he try to put his life on fire to protect strangers?
  • Did he do anything great for the society?

Just wondering what was that greatness that inspires you to be a Karna fan. I know you have no answer. So yes, let us end this discussion. And do ponder on these question and ask yourself why do your really like him?

Many people have undergone transformation in the past. Maybe someday you too may see the light.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 06, 2021 11:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t answer even a single question in favor of Karna. Isn’t it a sufficient proof of my statement? Even a fan couldn’t point out one good thing?

And now you need me to provide the proof?

Sure. Which one do you disagree with? Just point out and I will provide you the proof. I didn’t say anything without proof. Here is what I said —

  • Karna conspired with Duryodhana on his various schemes against Pandavas. Till this point Pandavas didn’t do anything against him.
  • Karna cheated Parshurama.
  • He gate crashed a ceremony that was explicitly meant to showcase the talent of Hastinpur Princess.
  • He along with other Kings and princes tried to burn down Draupadi after Swaymver.
  • He asked Dussasana to strip Draupadi. He also abused Draupadi later.
  • It was his idea to go and mock Pandavas and Draupadi in exile. He was lusty and wanted to see Draupadi in tree bark.
  • He betrayed his friend and fled Gandharvas for the sake of his own safety.
  • He encouraged Duryohana to attack and plunder Matasya. There he boasted of his divyastra and fled leaving his brother to die.
  • He pledged to Kunti to spare the life of his four sons. She hadn’t asked for for it. He gave on his own knowing it would be against his friend. He never told Duryodhana about it. That was a treachery.
  • He refused to fight under Bhishma’s command because he feared he won’t get the credit. Even if his ego was hurt, it was still a treachery.
  • He left his brother to die on the hands of Abhimanyu while and fled.
  • He let Kauravas die in the hands of Bhima without trying to protect them.
  • He abused Bhima after defeating him once, something Bhima didn’t do even after defeating him multiple times.

Please feel free to ask for the proof of any point I mentioned.

Are you going to respond to my questions? Ever?

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 07, 2021 08:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Before I give you proof of your questions —

SUMMARY OF WHAT YOU SAID

  1. Karna never put his life on fire for others because you think, he never got an opportunity.
    1. You agree he never did anything that great.
    2. I will prove you wrong about the lack of opportunity
  2. He never protected a women in distress for one reason or other.
  3. His greatest contribution to society in your opinion is he set an example by becoming great. You couldn’t find anything better. Right. You are again mistaken.
    1. But did he help anyone like him to become great?

Even if I consider your point 3, is it so great or important to ignore his inumerable follies and become his fan?

I think the reason is —

SUMMARY OF YOUR WRONG BELIEFS

  • BORI says, Karna didn’t participate in swayvar.
    • WRONG. BORI says he participated and failed.
  • BORI says Dhritrashtra’s son Karna failed.
    • WRONG. BORI didn’t say so.
  • Draupadi insulted Karna.
    • NEVER Happened. BORI doesn’t have this episode
  • Karna was not among those who tried to burn down Draupadi after the swyamvar.

Now let us get in detail and I am giving you the authentic proofs that you find useful.

You asked — Can you please provide me proofs that he tried to burn Draupadi.

Let us see the pretext of the battle — The kings said, “We are assembled here and he passes us over like straw. He wishes to give Droupadi, supreme among women, to a Brahmana. This evil-hearted one does not respect us. Let us kill him. He does not deserve our respect or veneration because of qualities of age. On account of this, let us kill this wretch who insults kings and their sons. After inviting and entertaining in the proper fashion all the kings with food, he then shows them disrespect. In this assembly of kings, which is like a conclave of the gods, can he not find a single king who is his equal? The sacred texts clearly say that a svayamvara is for Kshatriyas; Brahmanas have no right in the choice of a husband. O kings! If this lady does not wish one of us as her husband, let us throw her into the fire and return to our kingdoms.

—BORI

After this the Kings attacked. And here is the attack — Thereupon, all the kings, their fingers clad in armour and with their weapons raised, violently rushed towards the Kuru princes, Arjuna and Bhimasena.

And now let us see who fought whom —

Eager to fight, those kings then said, “It is permissible to kill a Brahmana who is willing to fight.” Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna,

Oh, yes it is explicitly mentioned Vaikartana Karna rushed towards Brahmana Arjuna to kill him as he stood between them and Drupada’s family.

Can you suggest an alternative reason why Karna fought here? Mind it do it with proper reference.

You said — According to BORI Karna never participated in Draupadi Swayamvar.

WRONG! Karna did participate in swyamver

यत्कर्णशल्यप्रमुखैः पार्थिवैर्लोकविश्रुतैः |
नानतं बलवद्भिर्हि धनुर्वेदपरायणैः ||४||

Now the Karna who failed was a very famous for his skill. But Dhritrashtra’s son Karna wasn’t famous even among his brothers.

Here is what Prof. Shutankar says about this. And you may not know but Prof Suthankar was the editor in chief of BORI.

If one thinks about it at all, one fails to understand how Draupadī, who was, after all, then only an unexperienced maiden in her teens, had recognized the Kin g of Aṅgas (whom she had probably never seen before) and known him for the son of a coachman, unfit to wed a princess. He had been invited by her father. A t least he was given a seat of honour among the princes. He is specifically named by Dhṛṣṭadyumna among the suitors (1. 177. 4). Moreover, it does not appear as if the bride elect had much choice or voice in the matter, at the time of these elaborate and formal state functions notwithstanding that they were called svayarhvaras

—Prolegomena, by Suthankar

(for more detailed reasons follow my article: Had Draupadi really stopped Karna from Participating in her Swyamvara?)

You said — it was son of Dhritrashtra's son named as Karna

WRONG AGAIN!

Dhritrashtra had as son named Karna. But he was not referred as a great warrior anywhere in Mahbharata, so great as to be named explicitly. But more important, he wasn’t present there. Here is a quick reference —

‘Dhrishtadyumna said, “Duryodhana, Durvisaha, Durmukha, Dushpradharshana, Vivimshati, Vikarna, Saha, Duhshasana, Sama, Yuyutsu, Vatavega, Bhimavegadhara, Ugrayudha, Balaki, Kanakayu, Virochana, Sukundula, Chitrasena, Suvarcha, Kanakadhvaja, Nandaka, Bahushali, Kunduja and Vikata—these and others are the immensely powerful sons of Dhritarashtra. These warriors have come with Karna for your hand.

Karna, the son of Dhritrashtra is not mentioned here.

Now don’t say, the last Karna was Dhritrasthra son who was so great that he was named as a special appearance. And if you say so then you stands to contradict the editor-in-chief of BORI himself.

You said — Why would he protect that women when that same women didn't cared about the Respect of Karna And insulted him in whole Sabha.

Because that would be an honorable thing to do. Arjuna certainly protected the dignity of Kaurava women from Gandharva setting aside his personal grievances. Don’t you think settling the personal score in the most brutal way signifies a petty character?

By the way when did Draupadi insult Karna? Can you please point out? You certainly don’t still mean she rejected her in her swaymvar? Did you? Because you already know BORI doesn’t include that insult. In fact only 5 our of 1259 edition BORI considered included it. So please quote me one source which says —

  1. Karna was rejected by Draupadi
  2. Karna didn’t fail at Swyamvar.

Let me see your reference. But here is what prof. Suthankar (BORI) says about rejection —

Unfortunately, this melo-dramatic interlude, to judge by the documentary evidence, appears to be the handiwork of a very late Vyāsaīd, as it is found only in K * Ñ2 Dn D2.4. c, that is, one manuscript of the K group, one Nepali manuscript, and three composite Devanāgarī manuscripts, besides the Nīlakaṇṭha version! A l l of these are late and inferior or conflated manuscripts. It is missing, on the other hand, not only in the Śāradā version and the Southern recension (as in the case of many of the interpolations of the Vulgate ), but for once, also in the entire Bengali version

So please remember to share your reference or else we agree you don’t have a valid answer to this point.

You said — He never got the chance to put his life on fire for others. If he got any can you please tell me when.

You mean you lived for so many years on the earth and never got chance? Wow! You know you need to seek opportunity to do good. But Karna was busy in plotting evil plans so he didn’t get the opportunity.

But since you asked me nicely, let me give you a few instance —

  • When Kings attacked Drupada and his family after swaymvara, Karna could have done what Arjuna did. He could have stood with them to protect a distressed family. Instead he chose to be the attacker.
  • When Gandharvas attacked Kuru women were rounded to be captured. Karna could have protected them. But he fled. Arjuna who had every reason not to protect them, rose to the occasion and protected them.
  • Karna, again, could have saved Draupadi from humilation, but he was the one humiliating. You may again say Draupadi had insulted him, but then you know the hollowness of the claim. Now even if that were the case, him families humiliation didn’t stop Arjuna from from helping Kauravas. But it did stop Karna. Right?
  • When the mighty general of Matasya was killed a large army marched to pluder the kingdom. Arjuan stood alone to protect them. Karna too had the opportunity. But then he himself was the one plundering.

If you realize, people get opportunity to be on the good side and bad side of the humanity. People make choice based on their own. But I think I showed you ample situation to prove he had opportunity and he failed.

But do you know what was the greatest opportunity he had? Accept Krishna’s proposal and put and end to the war that was to gulp humanity. And what did he do?

You said about he do anything great for the society?— Yes of course he settled an example in front of everyone that caste doesn't matters infront of skills and talent.

Really? I mean really? Karna was a lower caste? Listen to what Karna said —

Karna was frightened of being cursed. He sought his favours and said, ‘O Bhargava! Know me to be between a brahmana and a kshatriya, born as a suta.23 People on earth speak of me as Radheya

Is a race between Brahman and Kshatriya a lower caste?

But let us move. How did he prove that? What skills and talent? Deceit. Treachery. Flattery. Conspiracy.

He earned the knowledge through deceit. Got Kingdom by flattery. Lived life in luxury and lust. Is this you call setting an example?

No wonder people prefer shortcut these days.

So now tell me what made you a Karna fan? I still am to see one reasonable point in his favor from you, who is a fan.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 07, 2021 10:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — The start of Karna's main storyline started with Bhima insulting him infront of everyone.

WRONG. The story started long time back in Drona Gurukul —

From childhood, he formed a friendship with King Duryodhana. This is because of the enmity he always bore towards you and natural destiny.

—Shanti Parva

The suta’s son was envious of Partha and always competed with him. With Duryodhana’s support, he showed his contempt for the Pandavas.’

—Adi Parva

In fact Bhima’s out burst was because Karna was involved in various conspiracies against Pandavas and specially Bhima —

‘“Thereupon, the evil-minded Duryodhana, with Karna and the son of Subala,13 tried to banish them and oppressed them in various ways.

So you can see, contrary to what you said Rangabhoomi was not the start.

———————-

You think — This is the reason why I said you are a fact twister. He never cheated Parshuram ji he just didn't revealed his real identity to Parshuram ji.

Let us see —

  • He then quickly went to Rama11 on Mount Mahendra. Having approached Rama, he lowered his head in obeisance before him and said, ‘O Bhargava! I am a brahmana.’
    • This is a lie.
  • O Bhargava! Pardon me. I was greedy for the weapons. A father isn’t just the biological one. The lord, who is a preceptor and gives one the Vedas and learning, is also one such. That is the reason why, in your presence, I described myself as a Bhargava.’
    • This is a deceit
  • “In earlier times, when Rama found that I had obtained the brahmastra weapon from him under false pretences, he told me, ‘When your end has come, it will not appear before you. ’143 The maharshi possessed fierce energy and was capable of burning up the entire earth, along with her oceans. I had committed a grave crime.
    • false pretenses
    • grave crime

Karna cheated. He gave false pretense and knew it was a grave crime.

Now who is twisting the facts?

You said — He was afraid that he wouldn't teach him if he is not Brahman

That is disrespecting someone whom you want to have your guru. Parshurama had taught Devbrata a Kshatirya. You assertion is WRONG! He just wanted a shortcut. Therefore he called himself not just any Brahmana but a Bhargava.

Once again yo twisted the fact.

You said — Yes he gate crashed that ceremony because Your so called Drona the great teacher declared Your so called Mahan Arjuna the greatest archer in world.

Can you please show the reference where he declared Arjuna the greatest archer of the world? This is what he said —

“Now behold Partha, who is dearer to me than my own son. He is skilled in the use of all weapons. He is Indra’s son himself and is like Indra’s younger brother.”

I don’t see him declaring Arjuna greatest warrior publicly? Can you provide a direct reference, lest people may think you don’t know the epic.

Now even if Karna felt compelled and entered the ceremony and Drona gracefully allowed him to show his talent. Period. Why did he needed to seek fight during a talent show case? What was the need?

You said — According to BORI he tried it but it is totally an interplotation because that translation not even clear which Karna was present in swaymavar

So now BORI is an interpolation! So what’s authentic? A comic book? What is your definition of interpolation? Whatever you can’t understand becomes interpolation? It is very clear that Vaikartana Karna was present in the swyamvar. Prof Suthankar himself clarifies that. There is no doubt and no interpolation.

You said — If Pandavas mock him them it doesn't count if He mocks Pandavas then he is Super Evil. And tell these Lusty things to them who don't know Mahabharat.

I just told to one who doesn’t know Mahabharata. Tell me when did Pandavas exactly mock him. Please provide a reference. And please don’t say Bhima did. Because here he wanted to mock specifically Arjuna and Draupadi.

Please share a direct reference like this —

Let your wives, attired in excellent garments, see the miserable Krishna, attired in bark and skins, and thereby increase her grief. Bereft of riches, let her criticize her own self and her life. She felt grief in the midst of the assembly hall. But on seeing your wives adorned in ornaments, the grief will be greater.”

Will wait for a reference where Pandavas specially Arjuna and Draupadi mocked him.

You said — If leaving war is betraying according to you then you are Mahaan Addmi.

Not leaving. Fleeing. Deserting your friends and army. If this is not betrayal, what is? Let me show you —

Holding a sword and a shield in his hand, the son of the suta jumped down from the chariot. He leapt onto Vikarna’s chariot and whipped the horses, so that he might escape.’

He escaped. Not left honorably. Duryodhana refused to escape and here is what happened when he fled.

With Chitrasena, others rushed at Vivimshati and still others at Vindu and Anuvindu and all the wives of the kings. The soldiers of Dhritrarashtra’s sons were routed by the gandharvas.

Here is what Karna admitted to Duryodhana later —

While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away.

Only Karna fan’s can distort the fact and blindly believe he left as if the party was over and he went back home.

You said — I hope you remember him saying He will end Kurukshetra war in one day lol

Let me know when your lol is over. For I don’t really remember this. What I remember is something like this —

When I have Vasudeva as an aide, on a single chariot, I can slay the three worlds in an instant, with the immortals, with their mobile and immobile objects, and with everything that is the past, the present and the future. That is my view. I possess the terrible and great weapon that Pashupati gave me when there was a duel with the hunter. 114 At the end of a yuga, that is employed by Pashupati to destroy all the beings. O tiger among men! I know how to use that. Gangeya does not know this, nor do Drona and Goutama. O king! Neither do Drona’s son and the son of the suta. One should not use such divine weapons to kill ordinary people in battle. Without resorting to deceit, we will vanquish our enemies in battle.

Please see the three highlights —

  1. Arjuna doesn’t boast on his power but Vasudeva’s
  2. Arjuna gives credit to Shiva
    AND MOST IMPORTANT
  3. He doesn’t say he would finish it in one day. He said he won’t use this weapon on ordinary people and finish them with ordinary means.

Is this boasting? Is this why Krishna called him sinless and an inseparable existence of himself?

You said — He probably forget that His pashuputa can be countered by naraynsatra.

Oh! is it true. Please… please… share a direct reference.

You said — That same goes for Arjuna he left his son dying alone in war and he didn't even teached him how to encounter Chakrvyuh properly.

Do you trust yourself? Do you really believe these two incidents are same?

  1. Someone kills your brothers (two brothers on different occasion) in front of your own eyes and you flee.
  2. Some one kills your son when you are far far away. You didn’t expect those cowards to stoop such low. And then you show the wrath like never before and destroy all hope of enemy to win. You challenge an arrogant fools with a warning that you will kill his son in front of his eyes. Not behind his back. Not in cowardly group attack. And you fulfill the promise.

Tell me are the two same? Please do answer.

You said — Arjuna fled from war on leaving his soldiers at the mercy of Bhargavastra and Karna.

Really. Please quote Arjuna fled Bhargavastra and didn’t leave it. So did Bhargavastra win the war? Did he kill Arjuna? Did it kill any reputable warrior? Was it directed at Arjuna? What finally happened to it. Please quote a direct reference.

You said — And won't killing his other brothers will fulfill his Dharma of being elder brother.

Last I know he was Arjuna’s elder brother too. And for Duryodhana Bhima and Yudhisthira were more important. Bhima and not Arjuna promised to kill him. Yudhishtira was the contender to his throne.

And if Karna did the right thing, why didn’t he inform Duryodhana? Why did he keep him in the dark? Please reply, how it wasn’t a treachery against Duryodhana?

You said — Arjuna and other Pandava brother they left each other on the hands of Karna for being humiliated.

Read the war commentary with great detail. When Karna was insulting Bhima, Arjuna did come to his rescue and a terrified Karna fled as usual. And in retaliation to Bhima’s insult Arjuna pledged to kill Karna’s son in front of his eyes. And he did fulfil his oath.

Please quote me a reference where Arjuna’s brother were humiliated when he was around. Karna saw his brothers killed and fled. Do you see any difference?

You said — Bhima never even came in situation of defeating Karna and abusing him is different thing.

Please read the day 14 battle in details as it is too big to quote entirely. You will find Bhima defeated him several times and he fled each time. A warrior fighting mildly need not flee. And Duryodhana lost 31 brothers trying to protect that ungrateful being who didn’t even bother to tell him that he wouldn’t kill Pandavas.

You said — Please feel free to ask for the proof of any point I mentioned. And now you have got all your answers so I think you will not have any queries further

Thanks for allowing me to ask my queries and sorry for disappointing you that I still have few. I need so many references that I have asked in this answer. Can you please reply them —

  1. Please show when Drona announced that Arjuna is the gratest warrior during Rangabhumi which forced Karna to break the decorum.
  2. Prof Suthankar says, Karna was invited and he failed at Swyamvar. Please show me a reference how it is interpolation.
  3. Please share reference as to where Draupadi and Arjuna insulted Karna? If you say he didn’t participate, he wasn’t insulted. Right?
  4. Please share a reference that Karna didn’t lie to Parshurama, he simply didn’t revealed his secret.
  5. Please share a reference that Parshurama taught only Brahmana.
  6. Please share a reference that Narayanastra could have countered Pashupatastra
  7. Please share a reference that Arjuna fled from Bharagavastra and not left the scene because Krishna thought, it would tire Karna out.

I am sure you would provide some reference to all the points you so generously promised.

By the way so far you have agreed —

  1. As per BORI, Karna did try to burn down Draupadi
  2. He did plundered Matasya kingdom.
  3. He did betray Durydohana by not telling his pact with Kunti.
  4. He did try to humiliate Arjuna and Draupadi for some imaginary insult they had done to Karna.
  5. He did flee his brother.

In the start of your post you mentioned that almost for a minute I made you think why you admire Karna, but then you convinced yourself that I am twisting the facts. Now that I have given you valid reference and you know I am not twisting the facts and you may also realize that it is you in fact tangled in your web of misconception (like Karna didn’t cheat Parshurama), I am sure next time you will have more time to consider your poins.

May not be today, may not be tomorrow. But someday, it will happen. And till then the question would keep vexing you — Why do I really admire Karna? I couldn’t find one great thing about him.

Think again, why do you admire Karna? In your last answer you tried to so Arjuna or Bhima equally guilty. Of course you failed. Because you or I are not as wise as Krishna or Shiva who supported him.

So the question is again — What is it that make you admire Karna?

Why don’t you write a post titled — Why do I admire Karna. I would surely like to read that post.

But for now the process of transformation has begun…

Think… Why do you really admire Karna? What did he do to earn your admiration despite of being a conspirator, molester, deceitful, coward?

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 07, 2021 12:00 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ok. I will wait.

Writing long answers require knowledge, reference and proper conviction.

But if it may help you, I generally write on my laptop and do lose a few drafts. You may choose to copy your draft now and then to notepad, just in case…

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 08, 2021 10:05 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Are you sure about your assertions?

Because Nobel Prize committee maintains it was for Gitanjali. They didn’t mention is for Jana-Gana-Mana, although I won’t be surprised if be awarded Nobel prize for such a wonderful creation.

Next did you read the poem critically? So let me ask your opinion on this stanga —

युग-युग ढाबित यात्री

हे चिर सारथि तब रथा चक्रे

मुखरित पथ दिन रात्री

दारुन विप्लब माझे

तब शंख ध्वनि बाजे

संकट दुःख त्राता

जन गण पथ परिचायक जय हे

भारत भाग्य विधाता

What do you make out of these lines ?

Oh! Eternal charioteer, the wheels of your chariot echo day and night in the path In the midst of fierce revolution, your conch shell sounds.

So your critical analysis suggest that King of Britain may have been referred as “eternal”? And that his chariot movement caused day and night? And he had some sort of conch shell? Even the most absurd stupid person would not be flattered by such baseless metaphors if it were indeed redirected to the King of Britain. Surely Ravindra Nath Thakur was sufficiently learned person to have used those metaphors which would make more appeal to a British monarch who may not be able to corelate to conch and chariot and certainly not find himself eternal and cause of day and night! If I were him I would find it ludicrous, even mockery.

I am also at my wit end. What do you think — Did Netaji Shubhash Chandra Bose lack the critical thinking or he did he form the Indian National Army for the service of the British crown? Because he had selected in as the national anthem on September 11, 1942, in Germany while founding German-Indian Society. And it was years before India made it official national anthem in 1950. So did Shubhash Chandra Bose, too sang it in the glory of a Brithish King?

Interestingly Gandhiji, didn’t want this song to be the national anthem because he found it more a prayer to God than country. Prayer to God, not some King.

Looks like the song managed to fool most of the great minds of the time and only in recent years we developed critical thinker who realized that Ravindra Nath Thakur may have called King of Britain as eternal chariot and the cause of day and night.

I would rather not be that critical thinker and just feel the vibration I feel every time I hear the song like a proud Indian and can never stop myself from repeating the verses myself.

Regards,

Answer: https://historyofindiasss.quora.com/In-which-year-did-Rabindranath-Tagore-surrender-the-knighthood-as-a-protest-against-Jallianwallabagh-massacre-3
Creation time: Oct 08, 2021 10:38 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Unfortunately those misconceptions are deliberate. The entire incident has been whitewashed to show Karna as a victim of caste prejudice in this case. Kripacharya’s gesture has been distorted in a dishonorable way.

It was a simple show of ambition and unwarranted conflict which was tactfully avoided by a learned guru understanding the underlying intention.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-said-this-line-to-whom-at-Mahabharat-Just-as-a-dog-has-no-right-to-eat-the-cake-that-is-offered-at-a-sacriﬁcial-ﬁre/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 09, 2021 05:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You can read the above reference. It was Karna who wanted the duel.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-said-this-line-to-whom-at-Mahabharat-Just-as-a-dog-has-no-right-to-eat-the-cake-that-is-offered-at-a-sacriﬁcial-ﬁre/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 09, 2021 05:36 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Who is “we” in we not support it? I can see you are the original questioner here. But do you know, you can neither control the fact nor the truth in the answers and answer may not always suite your understanding.

Why was Bhima totally wrong? Bhima said — “You have no right to enjoy the kingdom…”

Now can you tell me how was Bhima wrong about it? What right did Karna have? On the basis of which quality was he crowned the king?

Was it because he was a kind hearted generous person desirous of establishing the just rule or the fact that “he was always eager to do battle”? Can you tell me what was that great attribute of Karna that earned him the right — cheating Parshurama or gatecrashing rangabhoomi or life long conspiracy against Pandavas?

Now to answer your question — Why worst of men?

Well such superlative terms have been used again and again in the epic. It was an expression to indicate his nature. But you may also see the fact here when you compare with Duryodhana till that point of time —

  • Duryodhana never cheated his guru. In fact no demons in the epic ever cheated his guru. Karna did.
  • Duryodhana had some terrible selfish reason to try killing Bhima. It was that of an evil man. But Karna had absolutely NO reason to go against Pandavas. They did him no harm. And yet he went against them. Does that make him evil?

By the way scholars not only called Duryodhana evil, but also Karna is named as the trunk of that evil Duryodhana. Krishna explicitly named Karna as wicked, evil and equally guilty in Duryodhana’s design.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-said-this-line-to-whom-at-Mahabharat-Just-as-a-dog-has-no-right-to-eat-the-cake-that-is-offered-at-a-sacriﬁcial-ﬁre/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 09, 2021 05:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me tell you an answer based on authentic reference and not on suppositions — Arjuna was treated worse than any treatment Karna received. Unlike Arjuna Karna never saw any struggle. He didn’t have to go through fire, life constantly under unknown threads of conspiracy, and forced to live the life of anonymity. Karna’s wife was never humiliated by Arjuna, Karna was never exiled by deceit.

And yet, there came an occasion when their tormentor felt in his mercy. They came out to mock the sufferings of Pandavas and were routed by a powerful enemy. Karna fled leaving his benefactor and his family behind.

Now what would any normal person do? Well Arjuna needed to do nothing and he would still not be a bad guy. But he ignoring the well reasoned personal grudge, still went on the help and rescue those wicked people. This is called going beyond the call of duty.

All Vivek Omar asked here was why Karna didn’t do what he should have done as a warrior of honor. He is not seeking favor, just the duty.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-greater-warrior-Karna-or-Arjuna-according-to-original-phrases-from-the-Mahabharata/answer/Arijit-Bardhan-1
Creation time: Oct 09, 2021 06:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for late response, but I can’t corelate the two description with Mahabharata. So let me try out the scenario.

Two boys (or girl) born on same family A and K. Unfortuantely, the poor mother had to abandon the elder K. But the mother ensured that K gets a great foster family, a rich one and prays he/she gets a good upbringing.
