Unfortunately A’s early life was not as comfortable. His father was a King in exile and he grew up in jungle. His father died and his family moved in with his hostile uncle’s family where there were regular attempt to banish or kill them.

Who is more lucky till this point? A child who lost is father or lived in constant thread of a child well cared by a caring prosperous family?

Few years later, both A and K goes to the same school under the guidance of one of the greatest guru of the era. There while A concentrates of sincere hard work, K makes questionable friends, and wastes his life trying to conspire against A’s family.

Finally he realizes A excels in everything and K is no competition to him. He decides for a shortcut and asks guru to give him same grade as A. Guru refuses.

K leaves school, lies his way to another more reputed school and learns there. But his deceit was discovered and he expelled from the school.

Now the final scores stood

A tops with 98%

K scores just around 50% (or less). But he is still the second topper as there is no one between the two at this point.

Now the evil cousins of A who wants to torment them need someone to compete with A. They are friends of K and give him wealth to make sure he stays with them — after all he is the second topper and if any one can hope to give a competition to A it is K. Isn’t it? And K enjoys great incentives despite of being mediocre just because of his unique position.

Now K, although second, wasn’t really any match for A in the first place. He loses on every direct indirect confrontation as was natural because of such steep gap between the two. But his benefactor continues to hold on to him as they had no real alternative. K was still their best bait. And above all K never cared about the ethics.

But what made the gap wider was the fact that A never stopped learning. He learnt from the greatest guru that ever existed even dwarfing his original guru. He learnt from the guru of K! On the other hand K, stopped learning after being banished from his second school. Clearly his education was never complete.

Now I think, you can get a better perspective of who are A and K and who was greater.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-greater-warrior-Karna-or-Arjuna-according-to-original-phrases-from-the-Mahabharata/answer/Arijit-Bardhan-1
Creation time: Oct 09, 2021 06:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You may consider whatever you like but your assumptions don’t match the authentic narratives. And the question isn’t about what you feel. It’s about what Indra, Parshurama and Yudhishthira felt. And that was my answer. But now to answer your point.

You said — Lord Krishna too feared him sometimes.

NO. Krishna never feared Karna. He never said, not even once that Karna could kill Arjuna. He feared the define artifacts that Karna had possessed like armor or Indra’s shakti. He maintained that without them Karna is as good as he is dead.

You said — Krishna killed Karna through deceit.

Can you point out what was the deceit? Was Karna attacked by several warriors? Was Karna without his weapons? What was the exact deceit?

You said — Karna can kill Arjun very easily if Lord Krishna wouldn't be there.

Lord Krishna wasn’t there when Karna lost and retreated scared from Arjuna after Draupadi’s swyamver. Krishna was again not there when Karna fled those Gandharvas who were later defeated by Arjuna. Krishna was again not there when Karna fled thrice from Arjuna during Matasya battle even after seeing his brother killed in front of him. Krishna was again unfortunately absent when Abhimanyu made Karna flee several times and once again by seeing his own brother killed by Abhimanyu.

On the other hand Arjuna faced none other than Mahadeva and the greatest archer and warrior of all times, Shiva, himself praised Arjuna. In every single battle, with our without Krishna, Arjuna defated Karna. Every single time.

It is strange to see that you have such high regard for an evil man that you won’t hesitate in calling Krishna deceitful.

Do you know power doesn’t matter. Even Ravaan, Hiranyakshyap and Tarkasura were powerful. They too were killed.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 11, 2021 07:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Most of the points you gave is merely your perception and void of valid references. Moreover, you tend to ignore explicit reference with your whim. You are mistranslating Sanskrit shloka without knowing them. Why? All just to defend an evil person?

Let me divide the issue in several part. First of all I hope you realize that you are jumping between BORI and KMG, so I think I can answer you accordingly.

But what we have already agreed —

  1. Drona never announced Arjuna as the greatest warrior during talent showcase. So we agree Karna had no reason to gate crash the ceremony.
  2. There is not a single reference that Parshurama didn’t train non Brahmanas. So Karna’s action was guided by prejudice and not fact. Dorna’s decision can’t define Parshurama. If I agree to your words, Karna still though he would deceive Parshurama.
  3. Arjuna didn’t insult Karna. And Karna still wanted to see Arjuna in misery. He didn’t care about Bhimasena. Draupadi insulting Karna is not only not present in BORI but also missing in most ancient scripts including entire Bengali, Maithili, and southern recitation. Even KMG has a clear contradiction to this fact. But even if I agree, I don’t understand how calling a Suta a suta was actually an insult? Wasn’t Suta a combination of Brahmana and Kshatriya? Also you mentioned of cross-reference — There is not a single cross-reference to this insult. Karna pleads guilty of insulting Draupadi but never asks anyone why she insulted him. I think you know the truth and you simply ignore it.

Now let us consider important incidents we are debating.

Draupadi’s swyamver

Karna participated and failed swyamver and there is no doubt about it. I would recommend you to read my answer on this question in detail and put your queries there — https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Had-Draupadi-really-stopped-Karna-from-Participating-in-her-Swyamber-Source-Reference-The-Mahabharata-Project-Main-Ar?ch=10&share=66bc3219

A short summary —

1.I agree Bibek Debroy has translation problem. The original BORI shloka doesn’t contain the word Kshatriya —

यत्कर्णशल्यप्रमुखैः पार्थिवैर्लोकविश्रुतैः |
नानतं बलवद्भिर्हि धनुर्वेदपरायणैः ||४||

  • The word पार्थिवै Also means King and Karna was the King of Anga
  • र्लोकविश्रुतैः means famous in the world. Now Karna the son of Dhritrashtra wasn’t famous even among his brothers.

This shatters the favorite argument of you Karna fans, that the Karna who failed is not the great Karna of your dreams. You must understand whereever there was a room of confusion in names, BORI has provided a footnote. None exists. here

2.While you are trying to translate BORI Sanskrit, I am sure you don’t know much of Sanskrit — यत्कर्ण doesn’t translate to perform action. It’s Karna not Karma. Same Shloka is also present in Gita Press and It also suggests Karna failed. You may find it interesting to note that KMG is an English translation of the same text.

3.You are referring to BORI shloka and forgetting the editor in chief of BORI, prof. Suthankar clearly mentions, a Sanskrit scholar or repute clearly mentions Karna was invited and he failed. There is no translation error. No interpolation. BORI clearly mentions failure.

4.Now since you refer to KMG as an authentic reference let me put forward the exact reference from KMG — Then Arjuna approached the bow and stood there like a mountain. And walking round that bow, and bending his head unto that giver of boons--the lord Isana--and remembering Krishna also, he took it up. And that bow which Rukma, Sunitha, Vakra, Radha's son, Duryodhana, Salya, and many other kings accomplished in the science and practice of arms, could not even with great exertion, string, Arjuna, the son of Indra, that foremost of all persons endued with energy and like unto the younger brother of Indra (Vishnu) in might, strung in the twinkling of an eye.

Now go and search for another Radha whose son failed.

Some common misconceptions —

  • Dhrishtumynma said — only noble lineage can get my sister. So how Karna fits in?
    • It was his wish and not a term of the swyamver. He also said handsome.
    • If only nobel lineage was permitted how was Arjuna permitted? Did he appear of nobel lineage?
    • Drupad wonders after swaymver, not sure who won his daughter a Brahmana or Shudra. He still couldn’t stop him.
  • If a Brahmana was not allowed in Swyamver how can a suta be allowed?
    • Who said Brahmans were not allowed? It was those Kings, who words were not binding on Swaymver.
    • If a unidentified beggar who wasn’t sure to be a Brahman was allowed without asking about his lineage, how can a reputed and invited King be debarred? Prof Suthankar agrees on this point.
  • Even Krishna is named as suiters. Does that mean he too failed?
    • No. It means Dhristduymna thought he will particpate in the swyamver. Doesn’t mean he necessarily did.
    • He is not mentioned to have either participated or failed. Karna is mentioned to have participated and failed.
  • Shalya doesn’t mention Karna failure during or after swaymvara while he mentions his other failure like Matasya and Gandharva.
    • At least we agree Karna failed at Matasya and Gandharva. Those were personal failure of Karna and Shalya could mock him for it.
    • Swyamvara failure was a shared failure for Karna and Shalya. Both were badly beaten. So Shalya may not have deemed fit to raise the issue where he too failed.
    • Shalya not saying something doesn’t make it an interpolation. It is one of the important incident that is mentioned in all earlier text.

You said — More importantly, VS Suthnakar, the guy who termed ‘rejection of Karna’ as an interpolation, himself thinks that Karna was superior to Arjuna.

First things first. VS Suthankar isn’t just another guy. He is the editor-in-chief of BORI. So a little respect is in order. He is an authority greater than you or I.

Second, he never considered Karna superior to Arjuna. Rather it’s just the opposite.

Q. Can you provide a reference that Suthankar said Karna was superior to Arjuna?

I will wait for your reference.

You said — But Karna did apologized to Krishna…

The rapist of Nirbhaya to apologized. That is not enough to commute someone’s punishment. He repeatedly insulted Draupadi. He never apologized to Draupadi.

And apology should be sincere. Karna didn’t show any sign of redressal. He continued to be conniving deceitful person he always was.

Parshurama Incident

  • Parshurama never said he won’t teach non-Brahamana. He taught Bhishma.
  • Karna approached Parshurama. He didn’t introduced himself as a Bhargava Brhamana. That was not, not disclosing truth but a outright deceit.
  • Parshurama expelled him from his ashrama saying that such deceitful person can’t continue with his ashrama.
  • Karna again confessed he had committed a grave crime.

It is you who is twisting this episode. Now provide reference that —

  • Karna didn’t lie to Parshurama deliberately.
  • Parshurama didn’t expel him.

About Naraynastra

You shared your perception. And your perception isn’t a valid reference. Share a reference that Narayanastra couldn’t have countered Pashupatastra. There is no such reference.

Arjuna reverted back his Brahmastra against Ashwatthama because this collision could be devastating for the earth. It was the same reason Krishna asked everyone to surrender against Narayanastra — to avoid a disastrous collision if there is an easier way out. Only fool would apply power when there is easier way.

That said there is only one weapon in the the universe which is known to destroy the whole universe at the end of time (Kalpa) and it is used by Shiva.

Karna and Vijaya Bow

I would recommend you to watch my podcast on this issue and then we can debate —

महाभारत के महान धनुष by Storian Hindi

Arjuna and Bhargavastra

I read the reference. I don’t see Arjuna fleeing. I see Arjuna saying there is no way to flee. Krishna doesn’t want a confrontation. He sees no need to encounter this weapon as it would cause a fatigue to Karna. He leaves the scene. And true to his assumption —

  • Bhargavastra loses it’s power without even confronting it.
  • Not even one prominent warrior was killed.
  • It didn’t win the war for Kauravas.
  • It didn’t kill Arjuna or anyone.
  • It wasn’t fired at Arjuna directly.

As you can see, Krishna avoided un-needed confrontation. But Krishna never ignored any serious thrad. He planned to handle Naryanastra and Vaishnavastra. He even ditched Nagastra. But he simply ignored Bhargavastra.

SUMMARY

  • As per both BORI and KMG Karna was defeated. There is no other Karna mentioned in this section. NO footnote. BORI Editor in Chief clearly mentions Karna was invited.
  • Draupadi humliating Karna is marked and removed as interpolation. Karna’s failure isn’t.
  • Karna cheated Parshurama and you couldn’t provide a single refutation to this point.
  • Karna gate crashed the Kridasthal without any valid justifiable reason.
  • You failed to show when Arjuna insulted Karna and Draupadi insluting Karna is a wel accepted interpolation. Karna humilating Draupadi can’t be justified.
  • You failed to find a single greatness of Karna and your only objection was he didn’t get any opportunity. I showed you that he had many opportunity.

In your previous answer you didn’t provide reference to most of my points. Just many conjuncture and supposition. But no valid reference. Can you provide a reference. I know, you can’t. Because there doesn’t exist any.

So let me ask you again —

  • Where did Suthankar say Karna is superior to Arjuna?
    • If you can’t find it, don’t hesitate to ask the reference for the opposite statement.
  • What made you a Karna fan? You couldn’t enumerate any of his great quality so far.
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 11, 2021 09:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me divide the issue in several part. First of all I hope you realize that you are jumping between BORI and KMG, so I think I can answer you accordingly.

But what we have already agreed —

  1. Drona never announced Arjuna as the greatest warrior during talent showcase. So we agree Karna had no reason to gate crash the ceremony.
  2. There is not a single reference that Parshurama didn’t train non Brahmanas. So Karna’s action was guided by prejudice and not fact. Dorna’s decision can’t define Parshurama. If I agree to your words, Karna still though he would deceive Parshurama.
  3. Arjuna didn’t insult Karna. And Karna still wanted to see Arjuna in misery. He didn’t care about Bhimasena. Draupadi insulting Karna is not only not present in BORI but also missing in most ancient scripts including entire Bengali, Maithili, and southern recitation. Even KMG has a clear contradiction to this fact. But even if I agree, I don’t understand how calling a Suta a suta was actually an insult? Wasn’t Suta a combination of Brahmana and Kshatriya? Also you mentioned of cross-reference — There is not a single cross-reference to this insult. Karna pleads guilty of insulting Draupadi but never asks anyone why she insulted him. I think you know the truth and you simply ignore it.

Now let us consider important incidents we are debating.

Draupadi’s swyamver

Karna participated and failed swyamver and there is no doubt about it. I would recommend you to read my answer on this question in detail and put your queries there — https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Had-Draupadi-really-stopped-Karna-from-Participating-in-her-Swyamber-Source-Reference-The-Mahabharata-Project-Main-Ar?ch=10&share=66bc3219

A short summary —

1.I agree Bibek Debroy has translation problem. The original BORI shloka doesn’t contain the word Kshatriya —

यत्कर्णशल्यप्रमुखैः पार्थिवैर्लोकविश्रुतैः |
नानतं बलवद्भिर्हि धनुर्वेदपरायणैः ||४||

  • The word पार्थिवै Also means King and Karna was the King of Anga
  • र्लोकविश्रुतैः means famous in the world. Now Karna the son of Dhritrashtra wasn’t famous even among his brothers.

This shatters the favorite argument of you Karna fans, that the Karna who failed is not the great Karna of your dreams. You must understand whereever there was a room of confusion in names, BORI has provided a footnote. None exists. here

2.While you are trying to translate BORI Sanskrit, I am sure you don’t know much of Sanskrit — यत्कर्ण doesn’t translate to perform action. It’s Karna not Karma. Same Shloka is also present in Gita Press and It also suggests Karna failed. You may find it interesting to note that KMG is an English translation of the same text.

3.You are referring to BORI shloka and forgetting the editor in chief of BORI, prof. Suthankar clearly mentions, a Sanskrit scholar or repute clearly mentions Karna was invited and he failed. There is no translation error. No interpolation. BORI clearly mentions failure.

4.Now since you refer to KMG as an authentic reference let me put forward the exact reference from KMG — Then Arjuna approached the bow and stood there like a mountain. And walking round that bow, and bending his head unto that giver of boons--the lord Isana--and remembering Krishna also, he took it up. And that bow which Rukma, Sunitha, Vakra, Radha's son, Duryodhana, Salya, and many other kings accomplished in the science and practice of arms, could not even with great exertion, string, Arjuna, the son of Indra, that foremost of all persons endued with energy and like unto the younger brother of Indra (Vishnu) in might, strung in the twinkling of an eye.

Now go and search for another Radha whose son failed.

Some common misconceptions —

  • Dhrishtumynma said — only noble lineage can get my sister. So how Karna fits in?
    • It was his wish and not a term of the swyamver. He also said handsome.
    • If only nobel lineage was permitted how was Arjuna permitted? Did he appear of nobel lineage?
    • Drupad wonders after swaymver, not sure who won his daughter a Brahmana or Shudra. He still couldn’t stop him.
  • If a Brahmana was not allowed in Swyamver how can a suta be allowed?
    • Who said Brahmans were not allowed? It was those Kings, who words were not binding on Swaymver.
    • If a unidentified beggar who wasn’t sure to be a Brahman was allowed without asking about his lineage, how can a reputed and invited King be debarred? Prof Suthankar agrees on this point.
  • Even Krishna is named as suiters. Does that mean he too failed?
    • No. It means Dhristduymna thought he will particpate in the swyamver. Doesn’t mean he necessarily did.
    • He is not mentioned to have either participated or failed. Karna is mentioned to have participated and failed.
  • Shalya doesn’t mention Karna failure during or after swaymvara while he mentions his other failure like Matasya and Gandharva.
    • At least we agree Karna failed at Matasya and Gandharva. Those were personal failure of Karna and Shalya could mock him for it.
    • Swyamvara failure was a shared failure for Karna and Shalya. Both were badly beaten. So Shalya may not have deemed fit to raise the issue where he too failed.
    • Shalya not saying something doesn’t make it an interpolation. It is one of the important incident that is mentioned in all earlier text.

You said — More importantly, VS Suthnakar, the guy who termed ‘rejection of Karna’ as an interpolation, himself thinks that Karna was superior to Arjuna.

First things first. VS Suthankar isn’t just another guy. He is the editor-in-chief of BORI. So a little respect is in order. He is an authority greater than you or I.

Second, he never considered Karna superior to Arjuna. Rather it’s just the opposite.

Q. Can you provide a reference that Suthankar said Karna was superior to Arjuna?

I will wait for your reference.

You said — But Karna did apologized to Krishna…

The rapist of Nirbhaya to apologized. That is not enough to commute someone’s punishment. He repeatedly insulted Draupadi. He never apologized to Draupadi.

And apology should be sincere. Karna didn’t show any sign of redressal. He continued to be conniving deceitful person he always was.

Parshurama Incident

  • Parshurama never said he won’t teach non-Brahamana. He taught Bhishma.
  • Karna approached Parshurama. He didn’t introduced himself as a Bhargava Brhamana. That was not, not disclosing truth but a outright deceit.
  • Parshurama expelled him from his ashrama saying that such deceitful person can’t continue with his ashrama.
  • Karna again confessed he had committed a grave crime.

It is you who is twisting this episode. Now provide reference that —

  • Karna didn’t lie to Parshurama deliberately.
  • Parshurama didn’t expel him.

About Naraynastra

You shared your perception. And your perception isn’t a valid reference. Share a reference that Narayanastra couldn’t have countered Pashupatastra. There is no such reference.

Arjuna reverted back his Brahmastra against Ashwatthama because this collision could be devastating for the earth. It was the same reason Krishna asked everyone to surrender against Narayanastra — to avoid a disastrous collision if there is an easier way out. Only fool would apply power when there is easier way.

That said there is only one weapon in the the universe which is known to destroy the whole universe at the end of time (Kalpa) and it is used by Shiva.

Karna and Vijaya Bow

I would recommend you to watch my podcast on this issue and then we can debate —

महाभारत के महान धनुष by Storian Hindi

Arjuna and Bhargavastra

I read the reference. I don’t see Arjuna fleeing. I see Arjuna saying there is no way to flee. Krishna doesn’t want a confrontation. He sees no need to encounter this weapon as it would cause a fatigue to Karna. He leaves the scene. And true to his assumption —

  • Bhargavastra loses it’s power without even confronting it.
  • Not even one prominent warrior was killed.
  • It didn’t win the war for Kauravas.
  • It didn’t kill Arjuna or anyone.
  • It wasn’t fired at Arjuna directly.

As you can see, Krishna avoided un-needed confrontation. But Krishna never ignored any serious thrad. He planned to handle Naryanastra and Vaishnavastra. He even ditched Nagastra. But he simply ignored Bhargavastra.

SUMMARY

  • As per both BORI and KMG Karna was defeated. There is no other Karna mentioned in this section. NO footnote. BORI Editor in Chief clearly mentions Karna was invited.
  • Draupadi humliating Karna is marked and removed as interpolation. Karna’s failure isn’t.
  • Karna cheated Parshurama and you couldn’t provide a single refutation to this point.
  • Karna gate crashed the Kridasthal without any valid justifiable reason.
  • You failed to show when Arjuna insulted Karna and Draupadi insluting Karna is a wel accepted interpolation. Karna humilating Draupadi can’t be justified.
  • You failed to find a single greatness of Karna and your only objection was he didn’t get any opportunity. I showed you that he had many opportunity.

In your previous answer you didn’t provide reference to most of my points. Just many conjuncture and supposition. But no valid reference. Can you provide a reference. I know, you can’t. Because there doesn’t exist any.

So let me ask you again —

  • Where did Suthankar say Karna is superior to Arjuna?
    • If you can’t find it, don’t hesitate to ask the reference for the opposite statement.
  • What made you a Karna fan? You couldn’t enumerate any of his great quality so far.
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 11, 2021 09:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Can you please point out, why did Karna came to Rangabhoomi in the first place? It was not a “open for all to participate” ceremony, but a public exhibition of the skills of Princess of Hastinapur. Karna wasn’t one of them.

Can you also point out why did Karna wanted to convert a rangabhoomi into a battle field? Was there any justification?

Why did Kripacharya intervened? Was it to please Arjuna? Won’t he had a greater reason to please Duryodhana who had taken Karna as his friend?

What justified worthiness did he Karna possess to become the King of Anga?

Not being a Karna fan, why did you ignore these important question and reached a conclusion that Bhima was trying to make Arjuna happy?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-said-this-line-to-whom-at-Mahabharat-Just-as-a-dog-has-no-right-to-eat-the-cake-that-is-offered-at-a-sacriﬁcial-ﬁre/answer/Shine-1183
Creation time: Oct 12, 2021 08:39 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

First things first. No one ever captured Shakuni’s family. Vichitraveer was dead long before Dhritrashtra and Pandu were born. Naturally long before Gandhari was born. Now how do you think Gandhari was born when his family was dead? And when there family was dead how did his father fought along side Kauravas in Kurukshetra? Please read Mahabharata.

Now coming to your unbiased opinion about Karna as the greatest friend/daanveer/warrior/pandit. Let us take them one by one —

Greatest Daanveer

Mahabharata doesn’t have this word in it. And Karna donated like many other Kings of their time. The only donation that gave him claim to fame was that of his divine armor. But Karna didn’t gave it away outright. He exchanged it for a Indra’s weapon. And he refused to part with it without exchange. So what makes you think he was a daanveer?

Greatest Warrior

Karna repeatedly lost battles after battle and fled. A warrior may lose battle. But one who flees scared can’t be called as a warrior, certainly not the greatest.

  • He fled Arjuna after Draupadi’s swyamver battle where he was part of the force trying to burn down Draupadi
  • He fled Gandharvas leaving his best friend behind to be captured with his wives.
  • He fled Arjuna again in Matasya battle as he saw his own brother killed.
  • He fled Abhimanyu again seeing his own brother killed.
  • He was badly beaten by Bhima and Duryodhana had to lose 31 of his brothers to protect him.
  • He lost to Arjuna again on day 14,16 and finally met his death on day 17 accepting that there is no warrior equal to Arjuna.

Yet you call him the greatest warrior.

Greatest Pandit.

Now a great pandit wouldn’t misinterpret Shastras and call Draupadi a whore for marrying five husbands using vedic ritual. Shiva himself gave her boon. A no pandit would be so inhuman to find pleasure in tormenting others.

Greatest Friend

You talked about Gandharva’s battle and fiddled an excuse that Karna was out of practice. I would agree with you, and this can well be a testimony to his inferior warrior skills, for a good warriors never remain ill prepared. But here the question is friendship. Does friendship too require practice? Karna may not have won the battle, but he could have shared his dear friends misfortune. Duryodhana didn’t abandon his family. Duhsasana didn’t. Why only Karna needed to flee? Was he the most inferior of warriors? Or ungrateful human? But how does that prove him a great friend?

But is this the only proof of his friendship? Nay! Let me quote a few more —

  1. Karna pledged in front of Kunti that he would spare four Pandavas life. Why? Did she ask for it? No. Was Karna unaware of Bhima’s pledge to kill his best friend? No. Yet he unilaterally gave such a pledge. Duryodhana lost 31 of his brothers trying to save Karna. Was Karna’s pledge in favor of his friend? Did Karna ever tell Duryodhana that he won’t kill Bhima or other Pandavas? No. What kind of friendship was this?
  2. Karna could have avoided the war by agreeing to Krishna advise. That would have given Kauravas their life back. And Karna, if wanted, could have handed the kingdom to them. Why did he reject Krishna’s advise?
  3. Karna refused to fight the war for 10 long days. Why? You may say his ego was hurt. May be it was. But was his ego greater than his friendship and loyalty? As a mater of fact it was.

What makes Karna the greatest friend? Supporting him in his evil ways? Making him greater evil? Is that what you call friendship?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-more-loyal-to-Duryodhana-Karna-or-Shakuni/answer/Shine-1183
Creation time: Oct 12, 2021 09:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Your whole answer is a mess.

Kindly explain the mess. I gave you point wise answer for each of your point. Just highlight the mess.

You said — Whatever reference I gave you is false.

Is it my fault that you provide such a reference? You are mistranslate shloka, invent factious characters out of think air and misrepresent events. Your assumptions far outweighs direct references —

  • You say Karna didn’t deceive Parshurama. I gave you proves he did. And he himself admitted to his crime.
  • You say Karna wasn’t allowed to Participate in Swyamvara, but even BORI, KMG, Neelkantha, southern recitation all have his explicit failure mentioned.
  • You try to quote references from some Youtube videos and expect me to accept them as authentic reference?

You give a valid reference and I would certainly respect that.

You said — You said my Sanskrit is weak then why should I believe I your Sanskrit translation give me one point.

You asked me one point, I will give you five —

  1. You have no validated reference for your translation. I dare you to provide one. And if you believe in the translation you prove you know nothing of Sanskrit. ‘Karna’ doesn’t mean action or task. The nearest word is Karma. Had you known even a bit of Sanskrit…
  2. Why you should trust me is the fact that I know Sanskrit and his grammar. But since you don’t know it, I will refer you to more reputable sources.
  3. BORI translation was done by a group of sanskrit scholars with detailed study over forty years. And they essentially knew sufficient Sanskrit than you do. And they mentioned it as Karna and Shalya failed. But I take it that, in your expert opinion, you don’t trust BORI’s translation as well so here is a fourth one.
  4. The Neelkhantha commentary which is basis of KMG’s translation is also translated by Gita Press, which is one of the most reputable source of Sanskrit works in India. And here is how they have translated this shloka —

5. Even your KMG translation mentions Radheya Karna’s failure explicitly.

Hope these five references are good enough. Now Do you have one proof supporting your statement?

You said — I watched it in some video I will provide you link as soon as I am getting it.

I thought so. I would recommend don’t bother searching for YouTube video. It doesn’t belong to Prof. Sukthankar but someone as misguided as you are. Let me spare you of the trouble. Here is what Sukthankar said —

Read carefully —

  • He says, in technical skills Karna was equal to Arjuna if not superior. I never doubted that
  • But he lacked spiritual strength, that is the strength of character. That is the reason Karna despite of having talent lack perseverance and continued to flee every time he faced a life-threatening situations.

You said — And atleast Suthankar didn't stated Karna character as whole as interpolation unlike Arjuna where Mahabharata started with the phrase of doubt that is Arjuna an interpolation or not.

You are officially confused. Neither Mahabharata, nor BORI starts with any doubt about the existence of Arjuna. It is a preface by Bibek Debroy who is not a Mahabahrata scholar, just a translator with notable blunders in translations. He has decorated his words with several ‘maybe’ and ‘it appears’ and forward arguments out of imagination rather than based on references.

If you can’t understand the difference between official BORI research and a English translation, there is no point in debate.

There is not one ancient reference, I repeat not one, that projects such a stupid idea.

You said — I became Karna fan because of his anti heroic nature. I became Karna fan because of his human nature.

Thanks for admitting and concluding the debate. I always knew it. Just wanted you to confirm it. Even his best fans couldn’t point out anything good in him.

You are fan because he was a villain (anti-hero). You are a fan because of his anti-hero human nature involving lust, ambition, greed and deceit.

Thanks for confirming. I always knew Karna doesn’t boast of any great virtues that even his fans could enumerate.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-have-guts-to-kill-Karna-fairly-without-Lord-Krishna-5
Creation time: Oct 18, 2021 12:01 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Yes. KMG has the reference of Druapadi’s denial to Karna, because it is based on Neelkantha commentary which is one of the only five of over thousands scripts available that included this tory. But even Neelkantha (and KMG) includes Karna’s failure in Swaymvara which essentially negates the rejection story. Further there is not a single cross reference of this incident in KMG.

For a detail on this following my post — https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Had-Draupadi-really-stopped-Karna-from-Participating-in-her-Swyamber-Source-Reference-The-Mahabharata-Project-Main-Ar?ch=10&share=66bc3219

Content:

I don’t understand what you don’t agree with.

What do you mean by literal? Does it mean mere literature with no backing real world incident?

Here is how I summarized it —

  • They are based on real world incident dating five thousand years and more.
  • Apart from being real world, they have learning in them and some may take those learning as symbolism. That doesn’t make them unreal.

Answer Comment

Content:

Ashwatthama was a fool and blinded by anger. Had he listened to Mahadeva he would have known he won’t be able to kill Pandavas but only Panchalas. But as I said, he was overcome by anger and lost senses.

Destiny works in strange ways. Considering Shiva entered Ashwatthama would be only half truth. Mahadeva is all prevailing and doesn’t need to enter or leave. He just used Aswhatthama as a tool with twin goal —

  1. Panchalas and Uppandavas were destined to die, they died.
  2. Ashwatthama’s punishment for his life long deeds were still to be paid. He brought out the worst of Ashwatthama which was so far not fully exposed. He too met his justice.

Content:

I wont use the extreme words like fool or rascal. Those people are just confused and delusional mostly misled by some progressive literatures that made them believe in symbolism to such an extent that they loose sight of reality.

And Bhishma did give Pandavas such a tough time that Krishna had to break his own oath. But that was because Arjuna wasn’t giving his best. He couldn’t bring himself to go offensive against Bhishma. At the end of the day it was Arjuna who gave tough fight to Arjuna.

Answer Comment

Content:

Who decides which is the right place? And what make this not the right place? Wherever people seek information, becomes the right place. If people are insincere in there quest, all the more reason to show the right path.

By the way it would be wrong to say all have their own version of saying. All the authentic version mentioned above differs in fewer details but not in the important message — All calls evils and evils and good as good.

For your information — KMG and Gita press have similar narratives as both use Neel kantha as their primary material. Sri Rajgopalachari’s version, although condensed is true to southern recitation and that make it an interesting read and can give you a clear picture as to how all version converge to same common theme and broad incident narratives.

BORI does a great job in analyzing all the version and bringing out a critical edition. But if you go through them, barring some detailing there is a remarkable consistency.

They don’t tell their own version (unlike modern TVShows), they narrate in their own words.

Regards,

Content:

Just did it.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Oct 26, 2021 07:32 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for a delayed response. Your comment was somehow collapsed and didn’t appear in notification. Your response goes mostly in line of what I said and I agree. But then you disputed a point, where I imagine you expected a response.

You said — I did notice a very condescending tone in the last line , when you were talking about Karna ,but he did fight many good battles also.

You see being a coward or evil and fighting good battles are not mutually exclusive. In fact victories can only vouch for power not courage or braveness. And victory may not be a candid prove of greatness else Raavana and Tarkasura too were great.

Karna abducted a princess against her wishes. Would you call it a good battle?

He did win against several Kings. But you must agree none of them were Pandavas. Perhaps counting them is not properly justified.

The real courage comes when you are losing, when your life is at stake and how you behave in those trying moments. So ask yourself, how Karna fared in those battles that were actually tough — Draupadi’s swyamvar, Gandharva battle, Matasya battle, the battles against Arjuna and so on.

Now compare it with courage of Arjuna during Draupadi’s swyamvara. He knew what he up against. He knew the risk involved in getting discovered. He didn’t have his own weapons and yet he didn’t give a second thought as he jumped to protect her new relatives from mortal danger. Now contrast it with Karna’s action during Gandharva battle. He fled living his old friend and benefactor. Arjuna climbed down of his chariot to protect his horses to fatigue and taking the risk that he may not reach Jaidradh in time. Karna cared for his brothers lesser than Arjuna cared for his horses. He fled leaving them to be slaughtered.

And finally I agree, Arjuna had been hell of a guy. So great that people may wonder if he was real.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Oct 26, 2021 08:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You, like all of us are entitled to our opinion. The problem is we are not ready to offer the same entitlement to Mahatma Gandhi.

You said — A person’s actual motives and character will be known after his death.

Nothing can be farther from the truth. No one can know the true motive of a person other than the person himself. Others can at best speculate. Once a person is gone, the motive is gone.

And every decision a person takes is based on the context and challenges of the era, so the best judge of the character can be people who too experienced that era. That excludes you and I. We can speculate. We can’t be judge of the character.

But those who lived in that era includes the likes of Dr Rajendra Prasad who equated him to Ganga. Lord Mountbatten compared him to Buddha and Jesus Christ. These were the people who shared the world and challenges with Gandhiji. Do you think you understand him better?

But if you feel the best judge of the character would be generation next, what about Steve Jobs, Barak Obama, Dalali Lama, Narendra Modi? Why do you consider only selected few as the judge of the character and not millions of other who celebrate his life world wide? Do you think there were all fools or misled by some Indian political propaganda? Think Again!

You said — He is not at all Father of Nation.

Like it or not — He is actually our father of the nation.

Your statement can at best be considered as your personal view or the view of a marginal few, whose number is certainly and unfortunately growing.

As I mentioned earlier, you need to understand the context and the era. Do you know who first called him the father of the nation? Netaji Shubhash Chandra Bose.

Who do you think, an average Indian would respect more — Bose or those marginal few who consider himself greater than Gandhi and Bose?

You said — Why Gandhi wrote letters starting “My dear friend" to Hitler!

My Dear Friend, do you really think Gandhiji called Hitler a friend? Have you ever written a letter ending with “yours sincerely” or “yours faithfully”? Do you understand the meaning of salutations?

You said — Gandhiji was always against Hindus not supporting him.

RSS Chief Mohah Bhagwat would differ from you. He considers him a staunch Hindu. I too consider him Hindu.

You asked — Why didn’t he…

Don’t you think he too had a freedom to decide what he would… and what he wouldn’t…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Mahatma-Gandhi-really-supporting-non-violence-If-yes-then-why-did-he-allow-Indian-soldiers-to-take-part-in-the-World-War-Why-did-he-support-the-Khilafat-movement/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 28, 2021 08:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Check out these lines — Had that bull among men possessed those, you with your Gandiva and I with my sudarshana chakra would not have had the capacity to defeat him in a battle.

But we know Karna was badly defeated with his Kavacha and Kundala on several occasions —

  • By Arjuna (without his Gandiva) where Karna fled scared.
  • By Gandharvas where he was badly and severely injured despite having Kavacha and Kundala.

Is Mahabharata incorrect? Is Krishna lying? Neither. Krishna expressed a possibility. You know “would not have” is always a probability and not always verifiable. What is verifiable is Karna’s very real defeat with Kavacha and even without Gandiva.

Now, let me re-emphasize, what Krishna said as a probability had the potential to be a reality. When you have a armor so powerful that your death is a virtual impossibility, how can you be defeated?

But then there are two important element here —

  1. Kavacha can prevent your death, it can’t ensure your victory as Kavacha doesn’t attack your opponents.
  2. Kavacha can protect your life, it can’t make you brave and it can’t stop you from getting afraid or running away.

To conclude —

  1. Krishna didn’t lie. He suggested a possibility based on the powers of Kavacha and not on the powers of Karna.
  2. Do you know what Krishna said about “Karna without Kavacha and Vasvi Shakti…”? I think you know and I don’t need to repeat that. Do I?

So Let me explain you from a mathematical equation —

  • Armours Defensive Power = 100%
  • Armours Attack Power = 0%
  • Chances of Winning = Warriors Chance of Winning
  • Chances of Loosing = Kavacha’s Defensive Strength + Warriors Defensive Strength
    • Now Kavacha Defensive Strength = 100%
    • Warriors Defensive Strength = -50%
      • Gets scared very easily
      • Runs away even when he doesn’t need to
  • So now calculate Karna+Kavacha winning chance.
  • Next compare to the actual facts.

But before you go let me put a few question which you ignored during our earlier interaction —

  • Krishna also told Arjuna that Karna would Kill Yudhishtira who doesn’t possess great Kshatriya strength.
    • Do you agree with Krishna here? Was Karna going to kill Yudhisthira? That means he lied to Kunti. Right?
  • Krishna said Shalya was more powerful than Karna and Bhishma. And only Yudhishthira in the all the three worlds can defeat him? Do you agree with Krishna?
    • If you agree then naturally Yudhishthira was more powerful than Karna and Karna couldn’t have spared his life. Right?
    • So according to Krishna Yudhishtira lacked Kshatirya strength and yet was more powerful than Shalya who was more powerful than Karna and Bhishma!

So was Krishna lying? No. There is a world beyond truth and lies, facts and fictions. The world of motivators. The power of belief. First he needed to explain Arjuna to take Karna seriously and finish him as soon as possible. In the second case he wanted to motivate Yudhishthira to kill Shalya.

Can you understand the difference between truth, lie and motivational speech? Krishna unlike Yudhishthira wasn’t addicted to those truth that may obscure the path of justice or Dharma.

If you can’t understand this, and I hope you try you would understand it, try this approach —

  • Try to cross-reference statements against facts and other references. You would find a more holistic picture.
  • You won’t have to cherry pick context.

SUMMARY

  • Karna was defeated even with his Kavacha on multiple occasions.
  • Krishna had suggested a possibility, which could have been true had Karna been a brave person. But he lacked character strength and used to get terrified rather easily and there fore he fled even with his armors. Again and again.
  • Armors can protect someone’s life, not win battles for them. They can’t attack opponents. They can’t protect warrior from getting afraid. They can’t stop them from running away.

And there is sufficient reference in Mahabharata for each of these facts.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Could-Krishna-s-Sudarshan-Chakra-kill-Karna-with-his-kundal-and-kavach-on/answer/Madhava-33
Creation time: Oct 28, 2021 08:53 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your argument is flawed. You can kill someone even by defeating. Defeat happens when person concedes. A warrior may die without ever getting defeated.

Take example of Karna. It wasn’t difficult to defeat him. He was defeated again and again. It was more difficult to kill him. Because he had a habbit of running away before he could be killed. Arjuna asked him to wait in Draupadi’s swaymvar and fight. But he fled. He fled Gandharva’s too.

So killing and defeating is not one and same.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Could-Krishna-s-Sudarshan-Chakra-kill-Karna-with-his-kundal-and-kavach-on/answer/Madhava-33
Creation time: Oct 28, 2021 08:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Which answer?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Some-Karna-fans-are-telling-me-that-the-Gandharvas-fought-Arjuna-mildly-Is-that-true/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 29, 2021 09:32 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

So you agree, what Shiva said and I am sure you wont be offended if I don’t value your words over and above Shiva.

Do you know the meaning of interpolation — whatever doesn’t suit your or you fail to understand can’t be considered as an interpolation.

Shiva said … you don’t tend to believe. what you don’t want to believe becomes interpolation. wow!

Let me clarify your confusions —

The meaning of valour is very very different from power. valor is not about winning. It is the courage to face your opponent. It is about staying put in the face of certain defeat. And despite of facing mighty Shiva, Arjuna didn’t retreat. He didn’t flee. Only a warrior who possess such a real valor can be entrusted with a potent weapon like Pashupata. You see, Shiva not just praises his valour, he also offers Pashupata weapon. It is not a question of mere generosity but also responsibility.

Do you know what happens if a potent weapon is handed over to someone who lack valor? The find out a reason to use it. Shall I give you two hint — Narayanastra and Vasvi Shakti.

You said — because when he utters this others including karna were day dreaming .

Must to your disagreement, these dialogs are not interpolations. And no Karna and other weren’t day dreaming. It was something far beyond their dreams.

You said — this brihanala couldnot even defeat saindhava and samasakta foot soldiers.

I don’t know which Mahabharata you have read, but the one I read there Arjuna complete destroys Smsaptaka warriors. Completely. But since you read a different version, I am curious, what happened there? Did they defeat and Kill Arjuna? Did they make Duryodhana king again? They must be very brave to defeat Arjuna who always scared fighters like Karna and forcing his to flee. Please tell me how Samsaptaka warriors defeated and killed Arjuna.

You said — bori version is still under processing and probably after they finish work if u were alive then discuss it

Yes, now both of us know who is day dreaming. But let me tell you one thing for sure. In every version, Arjuna will remain the hero and Karna the evil villain.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karnas-death-the-fairest-death-of-the-Mahabharata-as-per-BORI-CE/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 29, 2021 10:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Mahabharata doesn’t answer it directly. But if you look at the whole incident you would understand.

  • What is the difference between an NDA cadet and a Naxalite? They both start using guns at the same age. Right? The difference is about ethics, protocol, and right user manual. That is one reason guns are not allowed to everyone and there is no distant learning in NDA.
  • If you aspire to join NDA and you are not selected, no reason is attributed for this and decisions can’t be challenged. And one important test is test of psychology.
  • And if you start using the weapons without getting admitted to defense institutes, you are not considered as a soldier, but a terrorist.
  • Drona had declined to be Ekalavya’s guru. He not only continued to train himself in the warfare, he also continued to impersonate himself as Drona’s guru. If Drona had allowed it to slide, he would be responsible for all his misadventure, which we know happened later.

Now coming to your question —

  • Since Ekalavaya misused Drona’s name, Drona had right to demand his fee.
  • Perhaps he thought, after losing a thumb Ekalavya won’t be a threat he was seeing him in future.
  • But it wasn’t the harshest punishment of the era. Had he got the lesson, he would have saved his head. Karna suffered worse punishments for his misadventures — multiple curses.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Arjun-was-an-undefeatable-warrior-then-why-did-he-require-the-divine-guidance-and-protection-of-Bhagwan-Srikrishna-during-all-18-days-of-the-Mahabharat-War/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 31, 2021 06:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think everyone has a blind spot. Ashwatthama was Drona’s. That said, Ashwatthama was much better as a warrior compared to many of his contemporaries and that maybe the reason of Drona’s trust.

We don’t see him participating in any conspiracy of Duryodhana and often see him arguing in favor of his father and uncle. He was certainly hungry for weapons but not made about them. He lost is cool when his father was brutally murdered. I guess we can see two different Aswhatthama — before his father’s death and after it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 31, 2021 06:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I know I am late but I feel it is justified to add a few contradictions for the readers —

You said — Scene from the MahaBharat War (Karna Pushing Krishna’s chariot)

This is not a scene for authentic and accepted editions of Mahabharata, neither the critical edition nor the ancient ones. It is more of a folklore. So all conclusion based on this incident is erroneous and can be ruled out.

You said — Draupadi loved Karna secretly. He was her hidden crush.

F A K E. A G A I N.

There is not a single reference to this anywhere in any ancient or accepted version. This is how an evil like Karna attains a heroic position.

You said—Karna along with Duryodhana asked Dushashana to strip Draupadi of her sari in the Assembly Hall.

Karna asks Dushashana to strip Draupadi. Not Duryodhana.

You said — Karna’s prowess was most feared by the Pandavas.

With an exception of Yudhishthira, there was no one in either camp who ever felt that Pandavas were afraid of Karna — not even Karna believed this for a moment.

Karna lost every single direct or indirect battle against Pandavas — Gurudakshina battle, Rajsuy battle, Draupadi’s swyamver battle, Gandharva battle, Matasya battle.

He lost so many battle that made Kripacharya wonder — When did you ever defeat any Pandavas!

Now in the words of Karna —

  • He compare Arjuna with Vishnu, Indra and Parshurama, during Drauapadi’s swyamver before he retreated scared.
  • He confides in Indra that he is afraid of Arjuna who roars terrifies him.
  • On the 17th day of the battle, he confides in Shalya — there is no warrior compared to Arjuna.

Against this we never saw Arjuna worry or fear any warrior let alone Karna.

You said — Whereas, all of Arjuna’s obstacles were removed one by one – Karna’s odds were stacked against him one by one.

Let us see. Arjuna lives first part of his life in exile with his father. Then under the constant threats of his cousins constant conspiracy. They after lakshagrih he suffers exile. Then after suffers another exile for supporting a Brahmin retrieve his cows. Sees his family and wife molested. Then thirteen years of exile. And you see his obstacles were removed one by one! Who removed them? Did he get a free gift of Pashupata from Mahadeva? Arjuna earned his achievements. No greatness was bestowed on him.

Now Let us compare with the thorny life of Karna. He was trained by the greatest gurus of the era. He became the King at a very young age and life a life of propriety and luxury. Never faced any exile. Never saw his wife humiliated. Participated in attempt to burn Draupadi alive after her swaymver. Tried to disrobe Draupadi in public. And yet gets fan who make a hero out of him. They feel odds were stacked against him? Which odds? Parshurama’s curse to him, was repayment of his own deceit. Brhamins curse. You got it. It’s ancient philosophy — you reap what you seed. Don’t bend natures rule for a brute.

You said — Karna exhibited 100% loyalty to Duryodhana

Really! Let us check.

  • Karna flees Gandharva leaving Duryodhana and his family that included royal women at the mercy of enemies. Is this the definition of loyalty?
  • Karna without any reason pledged, not to kill four Pandavas including Bhima who had sworn to kill Duryodhana. He saw Kauravas killed one after another, but did NOTHING. Duryodhana on the other hand sacrificed 31 of his brothers to save his dear friend. Is this loyalty?
  • Forget protecting them, Karna never even revealed to Duryodhana that he won’t kill Pandavas. Was that loyalty? I am inclined to call it a treachery.
  • Karna abstained from Kurukshetra because his ego was hurt by the harsh words of Bhishma. He said he won’t fight as long as Bhishma is at the helm. Is this loyalty?

I think we have very different standards for loyalty/

Your Krishna Karna comparison

That is fanciful and long. I agree with —

  • Both name started with K and ended with A.
  • Both had two mothers — a birth mother and one who nourished them.

BUT COMPARISION ENDS HERE.

  • Krishna understood his birth mother’s plight and never rejected her.
  • Krishna never sided with Adharma.
  • Krishna never deserted his friend and brothers in life-threatening situation. Karna had a long list where he saw he left his brothers killed by Arjuna and Abhimanyu and fled.
  • Krishna never wavered from Dharma, Karna rejected the proposal for Dharma. What you call as offer of Kingdom, wasn’t just an offer of Kingdom, but a plea to avert a war that would push humanity to misery. Karna rejected that offer. For the friend you say? But then he didn’t enter the war for ten days for the very same friend. How do you explain that?

In the comparison you didn’t mention —

  • Krishna loathed Karna so much that his limbs burn thinking of his heinous acts.
  • Krishna called Karna as the trunk of an evil tree that was Duryodhana.
  • He was too happy to realize Karna death would now be easy that he lost Indra’s weapon.
  • He persuaded Arjuna to kill him.

Please don’t insult Krishna by comparing him to Karna — One who manifested to establish Dharma, other side all his life with Adharma.

Finally You said — Krishna/Karna – Everything they did they did for others, not for themselves. Two selfless souls

Neither were selfless. Krishna did everything in line with his mission to establish Dharma. That was his duty and the sole purpose of existence. His every actions were motivated. But you can call it selfless because he did it for general good.

There are not many incidents in Karna’s existence that can be called as selfless. Even if we call him a true friend, he had to pay his debt to his friend and is not selfless.

True Karna, didn’t had ambition of being King. But everyone has different aspirations. Karna had the lust of recognition. He wanted to be deemed as the greatest archer, greater than Arjuna and he devoted all his life to that one purpose —

  1. He asks Drona for Brahmsira because he wanted to be Arjuna’s equal who was better than everyone in gurukul.
  2. He deceives Parshurama for the same reason. And he was so focused on Arjuna that he killed a Brahmins cow without realizing. Brahamin saw his through his lust and cursed him.
  3. He gate crashes a private ceremony meant only to exhibit talents of Hastinapur princes to prove his greatness.
  4. He promises Kunti to spare four of his sons, but made an exception for Arjuna. Why? Why he couldn’t have spared Arjuna’s life? Because of Duryodhana’s loyalty? or for his life-time ambition?
  5. He didn’t accept the throne. As a loyal friend he could have taken it and handed over to Duryodhana. But that would have ended the prospect of the war and any future chance of Karna-Arjuna face-off robbing him of any chance to fulfil his ambition.

The list is long, but none of my observations are based on folklore. They all are based on epic references.

Hope that would help you revisit your theories.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Karna-the-greatest-character-in-the-Mahabharata-Why-or-why-not/answer/Vivek-Gupta-21
Creation time: Oct 31, 2021 11:14 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thank God you woke up and realized it was an absurd dream. Now before you dream again, read a few lines from Mahabharata —

  • Karna didn’t say she wasn’t a Patni, He said she was an unchaste woman.
  • He didn’t remain quite, He asked Duhasasana to strip Draupadi. Now that must be some way to protect a woman’s dignity.

And please don’t insult Krishna even in dreams. He consoled Draupadi that those who humilated her, their wives would weep. He named Karna explicitly and was too happy to ensure his death.

Answer: https://bdpxrcmrmnzozbhz.quora.com/Can-you-write-an-imaginary-conversation-between-you-and-any-character-from-Mahabharata-Suppose-that-you-are-an-intervi-6
Creation time: Nov 01, 2021 10:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There is a difference between being a warrior and being a commander. A commander need to be a great leader and manager and being a great warrior would be an added bonus.

Think 1971 war. Whom do we hail as the hero of the war — Field Marshal Sam Manekshaw. But he didn’t fire a single bullet. But he was a great visionary.

Krishna was a great leader himself even when he didn’t fight in Kurukshetra battle. Right?

Similarly a great warrior is not the most ruthless person, or someone who fought and won maximum wars. A great warrior is one who avoided all circumstances of the war and fought only when war was the last option. And there was no warrior greater than Sri Rama to quote.

Kartikeya took the command of the army of Gods, when the morale was all-time low. They had be defeated, and driven out of their homes. And he turned that army into a victorious army. That made him a great general as Krishna pointed. He too needed to be like Kartikeya to transform a smaller army a victorious one.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-best-among-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 02, 2021 06:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hey Cute Doge,

Do you know comments like these help almost no one and gets collapsed.

Have you ever tried to be a bit constructive and less abusive in your comments? Do try. You will find it a pleasant experience.

You could have debated on the content rather than mocking the answer and putting senseless remark like — not even a single proof.

Had you read Mahabahrata, you would have known that everything I mentioned has explicit proofs and won’t be making such worthless comments.

Come on, tell me what part of my answer is unknown to you? Tell me what part you haven’t read and find that there is no proof of it and I will provide you the proof of the same?

Next time, please try to be constructive.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-best-among-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 02, 2021 07:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No worries. It is better to ask valid question than to put stupid questions. See another comment on this post. I should be thanking you for asking logical questions.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-best-among-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 02, 2021 07:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Of course. A great warrior has the capacity of being a great commander. Examples will include likes of —

  • Krishna
  • Maharana Pratap — A great warrior and equally great general. He inherited a destroyed Mewar from his father. Lost almost everything during Haldighati Battle. But then he regrouped an army that can hardly be called as an army and before his death had managed to recapture 32 out of 33 forts of Mewar with the sole exception of Chittor Garh. That makes him great general and warrior both.

Example of a great warrior but not a great commander

  • Prithviraj Chauhan: Sometimes a commander needs to have a greater view than a warrior. He can’t afford to focus only of the eye of a bird but the whole jungle. Prithviraj Chauhan, as a general, shouldn’t have spared the life of Mohammand Ghori. That was a blunder. There is no doubt about his honorable or brave conduct as a warrior.
  • Arjuna: Arjuna was never a great commander, at least not till the Kurukshetra battle and specially before Gita. He was a great warrior who had ability for follow the command well. However he out did any great commanders of the word when he fought Dwarka robbers and forced them to retreat.

Hope these examples help you understand.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-the-best-among-Arjuna-and-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 02, 2021 07:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Shiva is beyond all classification. He is one beyond every comparison, without any competition and without enemies.

How can some one be a warrior or general when there is no one to war with? He is pashupati — the god of all existence. Ever wondered why even demons and asuras worship him? Because there is no higher point of appeal.
