Content:

No Anjalika was the name of a family of arrow that had head of the shape of crescent moon. As I mentioned it has been used by a number of warriors in Kurukshetra. So I doubt it was originally like an arrow of Arjuna. It may have got the fame because Arjuna used it to kill Karna.

Sunil Gavaskar used to play with a bat with monogram SG. I always thought it stood for his names initials. It was not.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Arjun-using-Anjalikastra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 03, 2021 11:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Krishna did say. But then Krishna offered unjustified proposals to Duryodhana also — just five village. It was his attempt to peace. And he knew Yudhishira would agree to the proposal.

But then, we also need to see the precedence. Did Ved Vyas got a chance to throne of Hastinapur? He had the exact same claim as Karna had. Isn’t it?

So maybe Pandu as an individual could have take him as his son. But what we are discussing here is — real undisputed ruler for the throne of Hastinapur. Only one person had that claim — the real legal heir of Pandu — Yudhishthira.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-the-real-and-undisputed-heir-to-the-Hastinapura-crown-and-legacy-of-Panduraj-being-the-eldest-son-of-Panduraja-s-eldest-wife-Was-it-the-reason-why-people-like-Bhishma-Dhritarashtra-did-not-reveal-it-though/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 06, 2021 07:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for late reply. Your comment was collapsed.

You asked — What is your aim?

My aim is, that people like you see the truth. The whole truth and not what you see on the TV.

My aim is you realize that Karna is referred as the trunk of an evil tree Duryodhana in the very first chapter and then repeated again by Krishna.

My aim is that you realize that Abhimanyu didn’t enter the battle for the first time on the thirteenth day as you want to believe seeing those TVShows. I want you to realize that Karna couldn’t defeat Abhimanyu by any rightful means and when he killed Abhimanyu there was no Dharma in him.

I want you to realize that Krishna called him evil more number of times than he praised him and wanted him to die. Desparately.

My aim is you know the true version of Mahabharata.

You said — He wished a Crown to lift the righteousness be the goal of his entire life & to reject the castesism from Aryan region.

He wished no such things. Not once in the entire epic. And not just because he didn’t have any spare time from his own self, but also because no caste existed in the era we are talking about. What existed was varna. And varna allowed the son of a fisherwoman to become king and the greatest saint of that era, son of a maid servant was the prime minister of Hastinapur and a Yadava, the eighth Vishnu.

Karna himself belonged to Suta a varna based on a Kshatriya father and Brahmana mother and doesn’t belong to so called lower caste.

Open your eyes to the truth.

You said — If you helped someone hundred times incessantly & unfortunately you can't help him for a morsal today,then also some people underestimate you,Similarly,Karna had done so many good deeds in his life,but due to some of his evil deeds,he was disrespected so highly by some people like the writer. After all,you may say It is the rule of the World.

I will say no such thing as it is the rule of the world. Rather I would like to ask you —

  • Tell me what good and selfish deed Karna did all his life? Can you name five? Two?
  • Tell me did Karna ever saved a village or a Kingdom in distress? No he planed to loot Matasya when their general was killed.
  • Tell me did Karna ever protect a bride, whom people wanted to burn alive? No. He was one of the principal culprit in the crime.
  • Tell me, did Karna try to protect a woman from public shaming? No, he commandeered her disrobing.
  • Tell me, did Karna tried to protect a woman and his five sons from getting burnt alive? No, he was part of that conspiracy.
  • Tell me, did Karna try to protect even his friend and his wives from enemies? No he ran the other way and left them to be captured and humilated.
  • Tell me, did Karna try to protect even his brothers when they were in mortal danger? No. While Arjuna and Abhimanyu killed his brothers, he fled.
  • Tell me, did Karna ever tell Duryodyhana that he wouldn’t kill any Pandava? Duryodhana lost his 31 brothers trying to protect that ungrateful person. I would call that treason.
  • Tell me, why didn’t Karna fight for the first 10 days for Kurukshetra battle? Do you want to say Bhishma forbit him to participate? WRONG ANSWER, just like your belief that Abhimanyu fought at Kurukshetra on 13th day for the first time.

Can you answer any of these questions? Tell me what made Karna so great?

And once you answer these questions, we will talk about what is just and what is unjust.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-so-generous-that-he-had-to-bear-the-curses-for-the-sake-of-others-During-Mahabharata’s-time-was-it-bad-to-be-good-and-generous-Why-was-fate-so-cruel-towards-Karna-but-not-others/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 06, 2021 11:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your answer has but two correct reason you mentioned —

  1. He made Asura Vow to fight Arjuna to the death. This was the only reason.
  2. He owed to the sons of Gandhari. Did he repay is a different matter.

Now coming to you other suppositions —

You said — He owed nothing to Kunti, a woman who left you like garbage to save herself means

There you are wrong! He owed everything to Kunti — his existence, his protection, his blessings. Every child owes his life to his mother, but few owed more than Karna.

Kunti had to let her son go, and it was as much for her as it was for him (Karna) and her family. Her misadventure would have brought nothing to anyone but disgrace. As the son of an unwed mother, Karna couldn’t have expected a respectable life, neither Kunti any respected wedding. Remember Lady Satyavati, too, could never share her son to her husband.

The term “leaving as garbage” is ungracious. If a mother wanted her new born dead, even Gods like Vasus can protect themselves. She never intended her to die. She gave her a protection no one got before or after — Kavacha and Kundala. Yes, read it again. It was Kunti who exacted it from Surya, which clearly indicate she willed him to live. And she ensured him a decent life with no want of wealth for his upbringing. So much wealth that he got the name Vasusena.

In exchange, what she got is a life long pain, guilt and an ungrateful ruthless response from her son, who she hoped would understand him.

You said — Leaving your foster parents who raised you with pure love and care all your life for a woman who left you like garbage to save herself means

Who asked anything about leaving? Do you know the story another child from that era? Her mother too had abandoned him. He too got a foster parent who raised with pure love and care. And here are his four prominent name of —

  • Nanda Kishora/ Nanda Lala
  • Yashoda Nandan
  • Devaki Nandan
  • Vaasudev

And one name is not more important than the other.

Like him Karna could have rejoiced two mothers together.

That leaves to the last part —

You said — he owes so much to son of Gandhari.

True he did. But did he repay?

  • He saw all but one Kaurava brother killed in front of him. It was within his power to protect them. But he didn’t. You may allude to his promise to a lady but remember —
    • Kunti never asked for such a promise. Why did he promise something putting the life of his benefectors at risk?
    • How was his promise aliened in favor of sons of Gandhari, whom he owed?
    • 31 Kauravas died trying to protect him.
  • Did he ever inform Duryodhana about his secret pledge? Do you think he had no right to that information? Is this how you replay your debt?
  • Why did he made an exception for Arjuna in his vow? Did Kunti say it is ok if you kill one of my sons?
  • If he could have made an exception for one, why not for two? Why not for Bhima? Why was Arjuna more important than Bhima who had sworn to kill the sons of Gandhari, whom you say Karna owed?
  • Why did he not fight for first 10 days for the sons of Gandhari, whom you say, he owed. Was it for them or his own ego?

There was NO SELF RESPECT. No self respecting man would ever insult his mother, no matter what. It was plain, simple, unadulterated ego and ambition. There was only one true reason and you know it, although you mention it yet another reason.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Karna-refuse-the-proposal-of-Krishna/answer/Tirumalasetty-Sriramsiddhartha
Creation time: Nov 07, 2021 10:27 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The comment was collapsed. Never got notification. Not only BORI clearly indicates Karna came to be known as Vaikartana after he cut off his armor. But apart from the evident fact that Karna is known as Karna throughout the epic, Karna doesn’t really related to “cut off”.

That said, sometimes there are a few astray shlokas that don’t have any correlation or cross references elsewhere.

There are also instances when a word may become more sensible in retrospection and other times makes absolutely no sense.

Consider the name Vrisha, which lterally means Dharma. But he was named as Vrishna when he was a kid, unaware of Dharma of Adharma. And then he is referred as Vrishna even at the moment he was conspiring in lakshagriha. What can we say?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Karna-got-the-name-Karna-after-donating-his-Kavach-Kundal-to-Indra-When-did-Karna-exactly-donate-his-earrings-and-Kavach-to-Indra-What-was-he-referred-to-before-that-by-everyone/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 09, 2021 05:03 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Bhimasena was an extremely powerful warrior. Even Duryodhana mentions his powers again and again.

That said, Bhimasena never defeated Samsaptkas. You can check the reference. There is no mention of samsaptkas during goharana parava. Some claim Karna defeated them. They too are misled.

You see Samsaptkas isn’t another name for Trigarth’s army. Same way as a group of terrorist can baffle local city police of our country but not NSG (National Security Guard) or trained commandos. BSF, SSB, city police all are part of Indian defense system, but they aren’t automatically same.

Samsaptakas were specially formed armies with one mission — fight Arjuna. It contained mostly Trigarth but included many other armies including part of Narayani sena.

Bhima fought a bunch of looters, Arjuna fought a specialized force who had taken an honorable pledge to kill or die trying to kill Arjuna. And there lies the whole difference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Pandavas-able-to-win-the-war-without-Arjuna/answer/Dharshman-Selva-1
Creation time: Nov 09, 2021 10:26 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There were a few great men you should know about —

Ved Vyas — Called him the trunk of the evil tree

Krishna — Too called him the trunk of the evil tree. Wanted him dead. Persuded Arjuna to kill him.

Shiva — willed him to die.

Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Ashwatthma, Dhritrashtra all called if evil or inferior on multiple occasion.

Can you quote a direct reference where Vivekananda loved Karna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karna-abuse-elders-or-elders-pulled-him-down-Provide-citations/answer/Dharshman-Selva-1
Creation time: Nov 09, 2021 10:37 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna was no lower caste. There were no caste concept in that era. And if you know the meaning of similies and metaphor you would see Bhima didn’t compare him to Dog. He called him unworthy of throne.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karna-abuse-elders-or-elders-pulled-him-down-Provide-citations/answer/Dharshman-Selva-1
Creation time: Nov 09, 2021 10:39 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Please check again. He indeed defeated Trigarthas and even Susarma. But there were no Samsaptaks then. They were formed during Kurukshetra battle only and not before. Here is a direct reference —

That large army was protected by maharathas from Andhaka, Vrishni and Bhoja and also those from Sourashtra and the south-west, skilled in the use of weapons. There was also Kritavarma, who advanced behind your army. There were ten thousand samshaptaka rathas, 62 who had been created for death or for triumphing over Arjuna. O king! They were skilled in the use of weapons. They advanced with the brave Trigartas, resolved to follow Arjuna at every step.

If you read carefully

  • There were created for the death of Arjuna, more like a sucide squad.
  • The word samshaptaka isn’t referred anywhere before Yan Sandhi Parva where this new troop specially created for combatting with Arjuna is mentioned.
  • It is nether mentioned in Goharana Parva nor during Karna’s expedition.

You may cross verify. Even in your reference the word Samshaptakas is not mentioned and is just your interpretation.

As I mentioned earlier. There is a difference between the regular troop of Trigartha and a specially formed army with best of their men under the oath. You can’t compare NSG (National Security Guard) and civil police.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Pandavas-able-to-win-the-war-without-Arjuna/answer/Dharshman-Selva-1
Creation time: Nov 10, 2021 08:27 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Please tell me what part of this story I didn’t mention?

  • Didn’t I mention Arjuna was envious?
  • Can you provide a single reference where Arjuna asked Drona to chop off the thumb of Ekalavya?

It was Drona’s decision. Not Arjuna’s. Arjuna did not suggest any such thing. Do you see the difference. I repeat, Arjuna was present there and yet not involved. It was not his place to interfere in his guru’s decision.

Consider a realistic scenario — There is a star student “A” in college aspiring to be a topper. He meets a stranger “E” who informs he too studies in the same college. “A” finds “E” intelligent, but still a stranger. He informs his teacher. Teacher investigates and finds him to be a forger misusing the college name. Teacher takes severe action against the forger who was not a student. “A” was very happy, now there is no “E” to compete with him. Now tell me —

  • Who was wrong “A” or “E”?
  • Was “A” wrong in informing the teacher about “E”?
  • Was teacher wrong in punishing “E” for misusing his name?
  • Was “A” responsible for Teacher’s action against “E”?

You said — Now you same people justify these incidents on the name of human behavior but when it comes to Karna you all become ignorant and refer him as evil.

I remember, once you asked me why someone tries to convert every question about Arjuna into a Arjuna-Karna debate. Do you remember, I had pointed you back to your answer. Take this one as another example. You are again trying to covert it to Arjuna and Karna debate. But that doesn’t mean, I would not answer your question.

But first, let me assure you, we don’t become ignorant. We compare without losing our rationality. Let me show it to you.

Can you compare Arjuna’s one time jealousy with Karna’s life long jealousy?

    • Out of jealousy, Karna asked Drona for Brahmastra so that he could fight Arjuna. Did Arjuna asked anything of that sort from Drona to combat Eklavya? I expect and explicit answer with explicit reference.
    • Out of jealousy Karna went to Parshurama, deceived his guru in order to obtain a supreme weapon. Did Arjuna deceive anyone? Again an explicit answer and reference is expected.
    • Do you know the exact words of Brahmin who cursed Karna? Here it is — ‘O wicked one! O evil-minded one! You should be killed. Therefore, reap this fruit. You have always sought to rival someone14 and you have been striving against him every day. Because of this crime, when you are fighting with him, the earth will swallow up the wheel of your chariot.
    • Did Karna continued any life long crusade against Eklavya to prove himself better?

Can you compare Arjuna and Karna as evil?

Karna’s wickedness and evil design wasn’t limited to being jealous —

  • Karna was deceitful enough to deceive even his guru. Can you say that for Arjuna?
  • Karna was involved in various plots and conspiracies to murder someone. Was Arjuna to involved in conspiracies? Please quote exact reference
  • Karna tried to burn down a bride. Can you say the same about Arjuna?
  • Karna tried to molest a woman by disrobing her in public. Can you compare him with Arjuna?
  • Karna schemed to mock a distressed family. Did Arjuna too try that?
  • Karna tried to plunder a kingdom in distress. Was Arjuna too involved in such a terrible crime?
  • Karna deserted his own friend and family to save himself. Can you compare Arjuna and Karna?
  • Karna is referred as the trunk of an evil tree Duryodhana. He himself has been referred by names like evil, wicked, deceitful multiple times. Do you still want to compare the two?

Would you answer my questions?

Please…please… don’t bring any new point till you have answered all the questions I raised. That is a naïve distraction technique.

There are at least 3 or 4 different threads where you gave up answering my references. A polite thing would be to at least acknowledge the answer you sought from me and say that you want to end that debate. Don’t you think?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 10, 2021 09:46 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Just for your information —

  • Anjalika wasn’t powerful divyastra. It was an ordinary arrow with cresent moon shaped head.
  • Karna too had Anjalika arrows as he had used used it earlier.
  • Karna was chariotless and not weaponless.
  • Karna did participate in killing Abhimanyu as part of a gang.
    • Karna died fighting a single man
Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Why-did-Arjuna-use-Anjalika-Astra-to-kill-Karna-when-he-was-unarmed-Why-didn-t-he-use-normal-arrow-to-kill-karna-2
Creation time: Nov 10, 2021 09:43 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Ohhh really! I don't remember anything like that.

Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Why do Arjun fans include Karn in each and every question asked about Mahabharat or Kurukshetra War? Aren’t they tired of making Karn famous by themselves?

check the answer requested by attribute here

You said — Arjuna didn't asked for anything because he knew that he is Drona's dearest student he (Drona) will find a way to get rid of Eklavya.

So you agree Arjuna didn’t ask. And rest is your opinion, without any valid reference and doesn’t matter.

You said — Karna deceived Parshuram because of misinformation given by Drona if anyone has to be blame here for Karna's action it was Drona he misinformed Karna that only Brahmanas and Kshatriyas can learn Brahmastra.

So you agree he deceived Parshurama. Is there a similar reference for Arjuna as well? No. And that is the only thing relevant for this question.

As far as blaming Drona is concerned, even an evil like Karna didn’t blame him. You need to be a bigger evil to blame Drona without having any reference and simply because you fail to understand the epic. Drona gave no misinformation. He talked about Varna. Read again —

he replied, ‘The brahmastra can only be known by a brahmana who is observant of the vows, or by a kshatriya who has performed austerities, and by no one else.’

Karna didn’t qualify by the yardstick, so he used deceit. And you see, even Parshurama regretted give him Brahmastra and kicked him out — Leave this place, since this is not meant for an untruthful one like you.

Drona was kinder to Karna.

You said — First it wasn't Karna who disrobe her it was Dusashan. Although it was Karna's order

Wow. No comments, If you know the fact and still trying to defend him.

You said — Ohh so he died for that same friend, he rejected the offer of becoming king of whole Earth.

That doesn’t change the fact that he deserted him and left him to die.. Does it? Had Arjuna not saved the day, he won’t have to fight for him.

Rest we already debated. Many people died during the war for Duryodhana like Bhagdatta and Shamshaptaks. Duryodhana handn’t done them as much favour as Karna did. They had never deserted him.

Karna had no ambition to become King and death in a war is an occupational hazard which Karna tactfully avoided for ten days. He tried to save his miserable life by begging to Arjuna even at the end. Alas, Krishna didn’t permit it.

You asked — If Eklavya becomes evil just for aligning with a bigger empire then you need to think again.

You are right, one doesn’t become evil just by aligning with one. But he was evil. He continued to attack Krishna and Dwarka long after Jarasandha was dead — He was challenged by Ekalavya, the king of Nishadha, who was thought to be invincible. But Krishna killed him and robbed him of his life, forcefully smashing him against a rock, like Jambha.

You said — And look Krishna killed Eklavya not because he was evil but because of the sake of Arjuna he killed him.

Yes. But but then you need to understand the deeper meaning of Krishna words. Did he not kill him for attacking Dwarka and because he challenged Krishna? Remember, Krishna calls Arjuna his inseparable existence and Shiva called him the greatest warrior on earth.

In the light of the above facts ask your self — What does “for the sake of Arjuna” mean? Arjuna was on the side of Dharma. That means Ekalavya would have fought from the side of Adharma and Krishna killed him.

You see, unlike your answers, my answers always covers the ground with given references. In this case important references are —

  • Shiva’s statement about Arjuna’s power
  • Krishna’s statement about Arjuna’s power
  • Krishna’s battle with Ekalavya leading to laters death.
  • Krishna’s certaintly that Ekalavya would ahve fought against Arjuna and sided with Kauravas.

You said — Yes I leave your comments empty and did not reply them because I am not some 50 year old man who doesn't has any work and quora is only his work. I have my work also and your single comment is longer than many answers.

+

You said — Now stop fooling others you still have time

I know you are Karna fan. You don’t need to abusive to prove you are one. You could have accepted the truth that you have no valid reference for you wild theories and had no option but to give up.

Unlike you, I tried to fool no one. I always base my answers on references and side with Krishna, Shiva and Dharma. Krishna and Shiva were no fools to side with one and not the other.

Question for you —

  1. Do you have any single reference that Krishna killed anyone in battle who was not evil?
  2. You do you always side with people like Karna or Eklavya went behind the back of great gurus, and were killed to establish Dharma? Do you have no respect for teachers or Dharma?
  3. You do you always go against Arjuna? Why do you hate Arjuna?

Let me see if you would answer or, like always, leave again…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 11, 2021 03:40 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The problem is two fold.

1. Original shlokas are in sanskrit and not in English.

दुर्योधनो मन्युमयो महाद्रुमः; स्कन्धः कर्णः शकुनिस्तस्य शाखाः | दुःशासनः पुष्पफले समृद्धे; मूलं राजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽमनीषी ||६५||

“मन्युमयो” doesn’t literally translate only to passion. It also means anger. And when combined with “mindless” word used for the root, it becomes mindless anger. This is how the same shloka has been translated in Mahabharata by Gita Press. Please read the foot note.

2. People don’t understand the context. You can’t understand the meaning of a word when torn out of context.

Here we have two trees mentioned. The tree we are discussing is Duryodhana. And Duryodhana has been called an evil throughout the epic. So it doesn’t matter if in the current shloka it is passion. If the tree is Duryodhana and Duryodhana is evil, the tree is an evil tree.

Also note the attributes. The root is a “mindless” Dhirtrashtra. So the passion it talks about is not a positive one, but a mindless one.

Second the tree is Yudhisthira. Here is how the second tree is depicted —

युधिष्ठिरो धर्ममयो महाद्रुमः; स्कन्धोऽर्जुनो भीमसेनोऽस्य शाखाः | माद्रीसुतौ पुष्पफले समृद्धे; मूलं कृष्णो ब्रह्म च ब्राह्मणाश्च ||६६||

This tree is contrasted with the first tree to represent the short narrative of entire epic. Now what is contrasted with धर्ममयो or righteousness?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-Karna-fans-say-that-Pandavas-were-unqualified-and-Adharmi-but-they-were-made-to-win-by-Krishnna-Do-they-think-Krishna-the-Supreme-Persoanlity-of-Godhead-is-fool-to-make-unqualified-and-cheaters-and-Adharmis/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 11, 2021 05:25 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You didn’t answer - Do you have any single reference that Krishna killed anyone in battle who was not evil?

You didn’t answer — You do you always side with people like Karna or Eklavya went behind the back of great gurus,

You missed the issue that these two men wend behind the back of great gurus. And you blame guru for their conduct. Why?

You didn’t answer — Why you blamed celebrated guru Dronacharya, who valued his disciple no less than his son, of spreading mis-information, when it is just your misunderstanding. With what reference you said Karna lied because of Drona, when Karna himself never said that?

You said — I side with Eklavya and Karna because they we'rent evil It were the circumstances that made them evil and to side Adharma.

Every evil claims the same thing. circumstances made me evil. Common issues include excessive greed, lust and ambition that make you disrespectful, deceitful and conniving. They don’t consider no for an answer even from their respectable ones and can stoop to any depth to fulfil their greed and ambition. Sounds familiar.

But Karna was evil. It is confirmed by Parshurama, Dhirtrashtra, Drona, Bhishma, Vaisampanya, Krishna and everyone including himself. Most of the time with proof.

And it is not just the words of those people, it the glorious career as criminal from the inception. Few criminals would have such a resume — A conspirator, a molesters, a looter, arsonist, cheater. You name it. If Karna was not an evil, then there never had been an evil in the world.

And yes, you can say the trunk of an evil tree Duryodhana was not evil, merely based on your opinon, not based on epic reference where he has been referred as evil a number of times throughout the epic.

You said — Karna was never involved in killing of Pandavas, yes he tried to show off against Pandavas but he wasn't included in plan of killing any pandavas.

WRONG! Karna was involved in killing Pandavas. He was involved in various schemes including Lakshagriha. And before you go on you stereotype distortions Karna involvement in Lakshagriha incident is not omitted from BORI and is present with cross reference, along with detailed account in ancient texts. So there is no point in denying it.

Question for you — What were the circumstances that made Karna evil?

  • You fail to prove your worth and want marks out of turn. Teacher refuses, and you call teacher was evil.

You said — I don't go against any character

But you yourself said earlier, you hate Arjuna. Now I am sure you would say, I don’t remember. Here it is

Do you know, why you people need to take your words back, because you have no conviction. You side with evil and so naturally become defensive.

And beyond you explicit admission, all your answers are testimony to it. Common themes —

  • You would feel happy to defame Arjuna by your hypothesis (AND NO PROOF) that Arjuna wanted Eklavaya’s finger chopped. This is no love for Eklavya, but a hate campaign against Arjuna.
  • You try to remove so called misconception that Abhimanyu died unfairly. Now the entire epic says loud Abhimanyu was killed by multiple warriors. There is no reference where his death is fair. But you would extend you hate for Arjuna to his son. And the other reason you would do is to shield you favorite evil from being branded what he really was.
  • You find Ashwasena justfied in his vengeance (NOT A SINGLE PROOF IN EPIC) and try to exonerate Karna from the despicable deeds (WITH VALID REFERENCE IN EPIC) by calling interpolation.

Can you be honest — Why do you hate Arjuna?

You said — I go with texts what is written and what's my knowledge.

NO. The fact is you mostly ignore text. So many time you have tried to explain BORI references as interpolations. If you really go with texts, how did you miss, Karna was an evil guy and Arjuna was the sinless hero?

You don’t trust the epic reference, spawn theories just with two goals — glorify Karna and everyone who stood against Arjuna. And you still say you don’t hate Arjuna?

You said — If I am wrong I accept it but you never accept,

I don accept, when I am wrong. It so happens, that such case didn’t arise so far in our interactions. And I did say your are right on the rare occasions, you were. And I have little reason to be wrong, because I don’t do evil worship and trust the words of Krishna and Shiva.

You said — you connect one thing with another which isn't even relevant. Just like you connect killing of Eklavya with Shiv ji's statement and with Krishna's statement.

At least I connect one thing with another based on reference and not wild conjuncture.

As far as relevance is concern, is it possible, that the relevance skipped you understanding? Why did I remind you of Krishna’s and Shiva’s statement? Simple, because both agreed Arjuna had no personal threat from the like of Eklavya was was subdued during Rajsuy yagna by Bhimasena and had accepted Pandava’s superiority.

It was to motivate you understand Krishna’s deeper meaning of his statement that he killed Eklavya for Arjuna. Let us check it out —

the immensely strong Ekalavya from Nishadha—all of these have been killed through my yoga, for your sake. 3 Later, other Indras among rakshasas have been killed—Hidimba, Kirmira, Baka, the foremost among them, Alayudha, the destroyer of enemy soldiers, and the spirited Ghatotkacha, the performer of fierce deeds.’”’

Can you see, what are the other names mentioned along side Ekalvya? Rakshasas like Hidimba, Kirmira, Baka, Alayudha. Why do you think Ekalavya is mentioned along with these evils? Am I lacking reference?

You said — Again this statement is your opinion it doesn't even make sense. (Krishna’s certaintly that Ekalavya would ahve fought against Arjuna and sided with Kauravas)

Does it really not make sense? Or is it just that you don’t want to make a sense? It is not my opinion, but with a valid reference. Tell me, why do Krishna need to kill Eklavya for the sake of Arjuna, if he were not to fight against Arjuna?

You said — If I am wrong I accept

Yes. I agree and admire you on this. I have seen you so many times accepting your mistakes.

I just hope you realize few more of your mistakes here. But the biggest mistake you have made is choosing the side. You picked the evil side. And it would force you to accept your mistake or be arrogant about it times and again. It happened with Karna, too.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 11, 2021 10:44 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — There are many Fake versions of Mahabharata circulating online and offline. This is being done to falsify ancient Hindu scriptures.

You are right. And these fake version make an evil, abusive molester like Karna a hero. They tend to depict Krishna as an evil conniving person. And this is common in all ancient and critical version.

Do you want to know what Krishna said about Pandavas— He who hates them hates me. He who follows them, follows me. Know that I am immersed like a single soul in the Pandavas, who act in accordance with dharma. He who follows desire and anger and, because of his delusion, hates those who possess qualities is known to be the worst among men. — Bhagwat Yaan Parva

Do you really believe this — According to original versions of Mahabharata, Bhishma, Dronacharya and Karn were GREATEST WARRIORS of MAHABHARATA. Lord Krishna considered Karn NEXT only to HIMSELF in warfare. That's why you have so many TV serials and movies LIONISING Karn, not one on Arjun?

Then you haven’t really read any original version of Mahabharata. You have personally seen only those videos, that you say have been deleted.

If you had read even a little of Mahabhrata here is what has happened —

  1. Arjuna defeated Karna who retreated scared after Draupadi’s swyamver. Here Karna was part of a team trying to burn Draupadi alive.
  2. Karna was the one who prompted Duhsahasana to strip Draupadi naked in public.
  3. Karna fled scared of Gandharvas leaving Duryodhana and women of the family at the mercy of there captors. Thanks to Arjuna, they were released.
  4. Karna fled Arjuna thrice in Matasya battle, even when he saw his brother killed. He also let his other brother die at the hands of Abhimanyu while he fled.
  5. There had not been a single battle between Arjuna and Karna where Karna won.

And you can’t provide a single authentic reference to contradict whatever I mentioned above. And my authentic text, I mean authentic ancient or critical text not some video that you have seen somewhere and trusted them.

Now here is what people said about Karna and Arjuna at different point in time —

Lord Shiva (To Arjuna) — O bull among men! Accept a boon from me. What is it that you desire? You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.” — Kairata Parva

Yama — O descendant of the Kuru lineage! 308 You will pacify kshatriya s who are like fire to the touch and are protected by Bharadvaja,309 immensely valorous danavas who have been born as men and the nivatakavacha s.310 The greatly valorous Karna is a part of my father, the sun-god, who heats all the worlds.311 O Dhananjaya! He will be slain by you. You will kill in battle those parts of the gods, danavas and rakshasas

Parshurama — O king! Therefore, discard your vanity and go to Dhananjaya82 before a weapon has been affixed to Gandiva, the best of bows. He possesses Kakudika, Shuka, Naka, Akshisamtarjana, Santana, Nartana, Ghora and Ajyamodaka as the eighth.83 All men who are pierced by these confront their death, or move around insane, or lose their senses and become unconscious, or go to sleep, or jump around, or vomit, or urinate, or incessantly cry and laugh. Partha’s qualities are innumerable and Janardana is superior to him. You have known him as Kunti’s son, Dhananjaya. O great king! But know that Nara and Narayana are Arjuna and Keshava, brave warriors and bulls among men.

Krishna — This entire army of kings assembled by you, with Bhishma, Drona, Karna, Kripa, Somadatta’s son Bhurishrava, Ashvatthama, Jayadratha—all of them are incapable of withstanding Dhananjaya. An angry Arjuna is incapable of being vanquished by the gods, the asuras, men and the gandharvas. Do not get fixated on battle. In this entire army of kings, is there a single man who can face Arjuna in the field of battle and return safely to his home?

Krishna laughed and spoke these supreme words to Partha. ‘The sun may fall down from its place. The earth may shatter into many fragments. The fire may become cold. But Karna will not be able to kill Dhananjaya.

Shiva Again — ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them.

And behold now. This is what Karna himself said —

Karna said — Where is the warrior on earth who is his equal? The mighty-armed one, whose spirit does not wane, also obtained the chariot, with a fierce and loud roar. It is yoked to white horses. He obtained giant quivers that are divine in form and are inexhaustible. The bearer of oblations681 also gave him celestial weapons. He slew the daityas in Indra’s world and destroyed all the kalakeya s in a battle.682 He obtained the conch shell Devadatta there. Who possesses greater fame than him on earth? He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds. The various guardians of the world assembled and gave him weapons that have no measure. The lion among men swiftly slew in battle the assembled kalakhanja asuras. In Virata’s city, alone on a chariot, he defeated all of us who were assembled there. He retrieved the wealth of cattle from us and robbed the maharathas of their garments.

Question for you —

  • Can you contradict any of my references as not authentic?
  • Can you quote any incident where anyone, Shiva, Bhishma, Krishna, Parshurma, Drona, Ashwathama or any warrior of repute called Karna better than Arjuna?

We have both the proof in terms of what happened and what people said. But few people like you are trying to destroy our ancient script are trying to make villains and evil men as the hero of our culture.

People like youforget Krishna’s words —

Tomorrow, the gods, the gandharvas, the pishachas, the serpents, the rakshasas and all the worlds will know me as Savyasachi’s well-wisher. He who hates him, hates me too. He who follows him, follows me too. Use your intelligence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of my body.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 15, 2021 08:00 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Can you provide the reference for your claim — According to original versions of Mahabharata, Bhishma Dronacharya and Karn were GREATEST WARRIORS of MAHABHARATA. After that we have Ashwatthama, Ghatotkach and finally Bhim.

I mean authentic reference and not you tube videos.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-according-to-Lord-Indra-Karna-was-a-far-better-archer-than-Arjuna/answer/Visalakshi-Mahadevan-1
Creation time: Nov 15, 2021 08:05 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

According to original versions of Mahabharata, Bhishma Dronacharya and Karn were GREATEST WARRIORS of MAHABHARATA. After that we have Ashwatthama, Ghatotkach and finally Bhim.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Visalakshi-Mahadevan-1
Creation time: Nov 15, 2021 08:05 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Your first three allegations that I didn't answered you is wrong I answered all of them. Just check again.

NO. You didn’t. Read again. and let me ask those questions again.

  1. Did Krishna ever killed anyone who isn’t evil? [YES/NO]
  2. Didn’t Karna and Arjuna went behind the back of their Guru? [YES/NO]
  3. I already explained with proof Brahmana and Kshatriya were Varna (or qualities) not caste. Do you agree or not? [YES/NO].
  4. Did Parshurama said it would work for Karna? Did it work? [YES/NO]

You said — Teacher did refused him as well teacher misinformed him about that weapon so ultimately Teacher has to be blamed for incidents related to study of Karna. And his lie to Parshurama.

Do you study? Imagine a student of grade VIII, asks teacher to admit him to Graduation and teacher refuses. Who is wrong here — Student or Teacher?

Next that student forges a XII certificate and goes to another college and gets admission to graduation. Can I blame his first teacher for spreading mis-information? Remember he got admission on false pre-text.

Now let us re-read what Drona said — ‘The brahmastra can only be known by a brahmana who is observant of the vows, or by a kshatriya who has performed austerities, and by no one else.’

He doesn’t say Brahmana and Kshatirya, he spelled out the exact quality of who is Brahmana and who is Kshatriya. Was Karna observant of vows or had performed austerities?

You said — See again You just misused my words I said I never hated Arjuna it were his fans which make me to hate him. And that statement is about a month older so can't I change my perspective in around a month don't forget you only said that changing is good.

Yes, even for external reason you do say you hate him.

But I would welcome a change is perspective and I stick to my words, such changes are welcome.

You see, you don’t need to share a screenshot, because I NEVER disown my words, for I never use them lightly.

You said — Abhimanyu was attacked by multiple wariors but his end was fair and square at the hand of Dusashan's son.

Can you provide one single reference that called Abhimanyu’s death fair and square. Give me just one reference. In response to your post, I had given almost 20 references that said it was unfair and one to many. Now give me just one reference, which explicitly says Abhimanyu’s death was fair.

You said — I don't hate Arjuna I hate his fans that's it.

If you don’t hate Arjuna why hate Arjuna’s fans? This fan group includes Krishna too. Why hate?

I never hated you for being a Karna fan. Why do you hate a fellow human, who cherish a good man from the past?

I am sure, one that that perception too shall change.

You said — You just picked a statement and try to say that his name is along with Rakshsh to Eklavya was evil, great logic Vivek!!

No Logic here. It is a plain and direct reference. Three reference to Eklavaya —

  1. He continued to go behind his Drona’s back and called him his guru despite his explicit rejection as if Drona had no choice in the matter.
  2. He was killed by Krishna whose incarnation was to kill evils and protect good. He killed Eklavya. He didnt’ kill a single person who wasn’t evil.
  3. This reference is from same place where you say Krishna killed him for Arjuna. But Krishna mentions him along side demons and Rakshashas.

There is no logic for me to forward. just Krishna’s words.

You said — You may have notice that many wariors (literally everyone) in Mahabharat has mentioned each other as Indra amongst men and foremost of Kuru lineage or Bharat lineage multiple times so does that make them what they are being called ?

I COMPLETELY AGREE WITH YOU.

This is something, I have been telling all my life. I said it several times to you as well. Just don’t trust words, they often contain exaggerated metaphors. Always validate against actual facts. So time for you to go back to the previous reference and think again, why Krishna killed Eklavya? Could it be because of his powers? Remember he was defeated and subdued by Bhimasena during Rajsuy yagana and Shiva himself said no warrior is equal to Arjuna. So cross verify words against actions. What do you get?

You said — And one more thing I wanted to tell you I said you a 50 year old man with no work. So I am sorry for that because I disrespected you and you are always respectful towards me . I am sorry again.

Apology accepted.

While biologically I may not be 50 years of age, spiritually I am eternal, thousands of time more than your assumption.

You see, I don’t trust everything said on social media including your incorrect assumptions about my age (both biologicals and spiritual) or about the misconception that I am without any real work, which even you know is false. I am sure you could have googled me an looked into my profile to know me better.

As far as being disrespectable to you, I try to be not disrespectful to any one. Being abusive is the manifestation of frustrations. I hope you would learn. I have great hopes in you. I believe, I am not wasting my precious time on you, I am investing it. I have great hopes in you that one day you would see the things as they have eternally existed.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 15, 2021 09:31 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Yes he did try to Kill Bhishma twice even though he wasn't evil.

You miss one of the simplest point, if Krishna tried to kill Bhishma, how did he survive? Do you mean to say that Krishna failed? Think again. I am sure you would realize your mistake of mistrusting Krishna. Krishna never compared Bhishma to demons. Did he?

How can you people blame even Krishna, to justify the evil people?

You said — Explain this question again please. Didn’t Karna and Arjuna went behind the back of their Guru?

Drona, in no uncertain terms denied teaching Eklavya. As such he had no right to call Drona his guru. Yet, Eklavya continued to call him his guru, thus insulting his explicit command and desire. Do you think, Drona had no right to chose whom he would like to teach?

Similarly Karna after being clearly told as ineligible for higher weapons, lied to Parshurama to obtain the same. He misused his guru’s trust.

Do you still have doubt about my question?

You said — You probably forgot that Karna was one of the Drona's student so why are you giving admission example. That example doesn't suit here.

I advised you so many times earlier — before rejecting a proposal as baseless, try to understand it.

Karna was a student of Drona (Grade VIII in my example). He wanted a graduation certificate. Drona denied. He forged an eligibility certificate and got admission to Parshurama’s college hoping to get a degree. His forgery was exposed and he was expelled. Now do you understand why I gave admission example?

If you understood, now you can explain weather such conduct is acceptable as per law or not? Do you know today, if someone is found to have taken admission to any professional college on forge basis, his final degree, even if completed successfully, would be cancelled and he would be tried for his crime. Even if the person becomes a doctor or lawyer, he would be debarred. But if some one pulls stunt with Naional Defense Academy, he would be tried for treason.

You said — It's clearly written that only Brahmana who is observant of the vows and Kshatriya who has performed austerities can get Brahmastra. So why you are being confused.

I am not confused. You are. Here Brahmana is not some caste but a Varna. And those higher weapons weren’t available to everyone. Only those who possess moral value, which Karna clearly lacked. And Parshurama concurred with Drona’s decision. So there is no misinformation. Even worst of demons were more faithful to their guru. Even Karna could never blame Drona for misinformation. Only his fans stoop that low.

Question — Can you give a single reference that says Drona gave mis-information to Karna? Is there a single reference that justified Karna’s deceit? [YES/NO]

Don’t forget to reply this question.

(There is exact reference that Karna too knew he committed a crime by lying to Parshurama.)

You said — But the main question here is he wasn't Brahmana nor Kshatriyas so it doesn't even matter here that he possessed those quality or not.

So he thought he isn’t qualified to own higher weapons. And then he decided to cheat. How is it different from forgery? How is different to enter a prestigious college on the basis of forged degree? How is it not a crime?

You said — Shri Krishna was Arjuna's friend not fan.

Who says that being friend and fan is mutually exclusive? What is a fan? Here is definition from a collins dictionary —

If you are a fan of someone or something, especially a famous person or a sport, you like them very much and are very interested in them.

Synonyms: supporter, lover, follower, enthusiast More Synonyms of fan

—Fan definition and meaning | Collins English Dictionary

Now Krishna did say there is no one more dear to him than Arjuna. Didn’t he? So on what basis do you say he wasn’t a fan?

You said — These Arjun fans compare him with Ram bhagwan and laxman bhagwan without even thinking once that Arjuna survived because of Shri Krishna

Yes Krishna did save Arjuna. But if some one saves you doesn’t make you weak or smaller. Does it? Didn’t Hanuman saved the life of Rama and Lakshmana? Does that mean Rama and Lakshamana were weaker? Are you also an Indrajeet’s fan, apart from being a Karna and Ekalavya’s fan?

Being saved, proves the dedication of your savior towards you. It says you have friends who would do anything for you. That is a great strength. I don’t know why you see benefactors as proof of weakness.

And who would not like to be compared with great Sri Rama? What more proof you need for someone’s greatness that Krishna calling him his inseparable existence? Do you know Krishna was Rama himself. And he says Arjuna is a part of him. Do you think Krishna was a fool?

I never said, Arjuna was as powerful as Shri Rama. Probably Sri Rama could have destroyed Arjuna with a blink. But he would never have done so. You know why? Because Arjuna was no evil.

And I would tell you this, without doubt, even Shri Rama would have blessed Arjuna will all his heart just like Krishna, Shiva and Hanumana did. Shouldn’t that count for something?

By the way do you know Arjuna was incarnation of sage Nara? Do you know what Shiva and Parshurama said about Arjuna?

You said — This is what makes me hate them and they don't even respect other Characters they think Bhishma and Drona were no match to Arjuna (this includes you as well)

Yes, you are right and wrong. to include me in this list.

I don’t deny Bhishma and Drona were no match to Arjuna. Even they knew this simple fact.

But that doesn’t mean I don’t respect them. I am not a fan of brute power but goodness of heart. And I can prove that. The main protagonist of the first novel of my epic series — The Lost Epic Book — is Bhishma.

And I have always argued, Drona was a great teacher. But you don’t have to be greatest to earn my respect. You just need to have great heart. AND YOU MUST NOT BE AN EVIL.

Question for you — Is this why you hate Krishna and Shiva? Because both Krishna and Shiva explicitly said Bhishma and Drona are no match for Arjuna —

Shiva — You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

Krishna — This entire army of kings assembled by you, with Bhishma, Drona, Karna, Kripa, Somadatta’s son Bhurishrava, Ashvatthama, Jayadratha—all of them are incapable of withstanding Dhananjaya. An angry Arjuna is incapable of being vanquished by the gods, the asuras, men and the gandharvas. Do not get fixated on battle.

And once again, I would say, cross reference these divine words with actions —

  • Do you know what happened at Matasya battle?
  • Do you know why Drona didn’t form Chakravyuh in presence of Arjuna?

Question for you — Do you hate me because I trust Shiva and Krishna? Do you hate Shiva and Krishna because they considered Arjuna more powerful than Bhishma and Drona?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 16, 2021 11:04 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — If Arjuna wasn't ready to Kill Bhishma so Krishna would have killed this shows that Shri Krishna can kill anyone who is sided with Adharma then it doesn't matter tha he or she is evil ore not.

You underestimate Krishna. Everytime. Remember, I gave you reference — Krishna knows past, present and future. He knew he won’t kill Bhishma. He did kill Eklavya. Period.

You said — And yes I want to read that text where it is said that Shri Krishna is born to Kill evils only.

Not just to kill evil, but also to protect the good. Those who were good were to be protected by him, the evil to be destroyed by him. Here is a text —

यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत ।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥४-७॥
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् ।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥४-८॥

Do hesitate to ask for my help if you don’t understand the shloka. And feel free to counter me by giving exact reference that Krishna didn’t care if he killed good or evil.

You said — But for your sake let's consider that example, so according to it Karna has already got punishment so why blame him again and again?

Why praise him? Why justify his misdeeds? Why sympathize with an evil? Why use if as an idol for his misdeeds? What message does it carry? That it is ok to cheat if you can’t get it fairly?

Do you know about intellectual rights? Why do you think Drona and Parshurama had no right on their knowledge and no decision whom they would like to share with it? What right Karna had to steal the knowledge?

You asked, why blame him again and again? It is important that young generation learns the virtue of hardwork and desist from corrupt pratices.

You said —hence remains called the greatest and best shishya of Parshuram ji. That's it.

Can you provide a single reference that Karna is the greatest and best shishya of Parshurama? Give me a direct reference and not your views, which you have a habbit of presenting just like the next point below.

You said — Yes ( The whole statement is infront of you) (in response to Can you give a single reference that says Drona gave mis-information to Karna? Is there a single reference that justified Karna’s deceit? )

Which statement is in front of me? Which statement of Mahabharata says that Karna was right in deceiving Parshurama? Who said? When they said? Which Parva? I am not asking about your opinion. I am asking about Mahabhara reference that says that Karna did the right thing in deceiving Parshurama. Don’t give your views.

In respnse to Even Karna could never blame Drona for misinformation. Only his fans stoop that low.

You said — Yes because Karna wasn't a bad person he never seened Drona as a true teacher unlike Parshuram ji. Karna wasn't a bad person

But you are WRONG! Karna had an abusive habbit and he used abusive words against Krispacharya and Dronacharya on different occasion. And despite of being such an abusive person, he could never blame Dronacharya.

And yes, Karna wasn’t a bad person. He was worse. A molester, deceitful, conspirator can’t be just bad.

And even if I agree with whatever you say (I don’t agree), It still confirms, you have no proof that anyone ever blamed Drona for misinformation or as a cause of Karna’s deceit. There is NOT A SINGLE REFERENCE.

So, why are you trying to spread unfounded misinformation?

You said — I myself as Brhamin can confirm that Brahmana is a caste. But how can you say that Brahman isn't caste do you have any proof or reference?

It’s simple. There is no reference of caste anywhere in the grand epic of Mahabahrata. And here is a very clear definition of Varna defined by Krishna himself —

चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः ।
तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥

According to the three modes of material nature and the work associated with them, the four divisions of human society are created by Me. And although I am the creator of this system, you should know that I am yet the nondoer, being unchangeable.

Can you now give me any proof of caste?

You asked — Stop making assumptions on your own. Take this as a example - My friend is a footballer, I daily go and watch his matches, I cheer for him so does that make him his fan?

I am not making assumptions. I gave you reference to the official definition of fans. Check the link is from a reputed English dictionary — collins.

As per the official definition of fan, if you admire someone, yes, you are a fan. By the way, are you not a fan of your friend? I am a fan of all my best friends.

In response to — Now Krishna did say there is no one more dear to him than Arjuna. Didn’t he? So on what basis do you say he wasn’t a fan?

You said — So what if he said that.

Seriously? Tell me what can be greater than Krishna calling someone his inseparable existence and his dearest being? Tell me could you imagine something of greater importance!

You said — Shri Ram also told that Laxman ji is most dearest to him so does that make Ram bhagwan Laxman ji's fan.

YES. You may not be a friend of you footballer friend, but Rama was certainly a fan of his younger brother. And being a fan is a matter of pride.

You said — Who said that if someone saves you then that make you smaller? I said - NO, You assumed I said - Yes

NO. You explicitly said — Arjuna survived because of Shri Krishna an Avatar of Lord Vishnu and Ram ji is Lord Vishnu himself.

And not for the first time. You often mocked Arjuna saying Krishna had to save him. Read your statement. And ask yourself, you will get the answer. I know you are good in recognizing your mistakes.

You said — Ohh my dear I never said I hate Shiva ji and Shri Krishna can you tell me when I said this. Don't make assumptions of your own.

This is what you said — This is what makes me hate them and they don't even respect other Characters they think Bhishma and Drona were no match to Arjuna (this includes you as well)

Aparat from me, both Krishna and Shiva are guilty of announcing that Bhishma and Drona are no match to Arjuna. And you said you hate those people who think that way.

You may want to take your words back.

You said — I don't hate you I hate those who compare.

Read the previous quotes. You explicitly mentioned me.

You asked — Why he said that it was there fortune that they were saved by Karna.

Because Pandavas were fortunate. You see, only fools assume that they wont die. Only evils aspire for immortality. Karna was a cruel person and tried his best to kill Arjuna. He may have succeeded, not by his virtue but by Vasvi shakti or Ashwsena. So there is no harm in calling Pandavas fortunate. I agree. But did you notice your quote. Let me highlight the important part —

The cruel and immensely strong Vaikartana, son of a suta, has been slain. O Indra among kings! It is through good fortune that you have obtained victory.

You said — Looks Shiv ji and Krishna ji praised Arjuna they said that in affection of Arjuna.

Correct! That proves their affection for Arjuna. And their affection proves Arjuna was worthy. I am sure you don’t want to call Shivaji biased.

But surely you don’t mean to say Shivaji lied. Did you?

You said — Why would I hate them they are my idol they are my gods

See some of your statement about Krishna —

  • He many not know the truth about Karna’s identify. This proves, you don’t think him to be all knowing God.
  • You asked what is the proof that he incarnated to kill evil.
  • You are not willing to trust that Krishna killed Eklavya because he was evil. You think, may be he killed an innocent person just to please Arjuna.

So with this much mistrust on Krishna, you want me to belive that you don’t hate him. Do you know what Krishna said — He who hates Arjuna, hates me.

And you would be lying to yourself if you say you don’t hate Arjuna.

You said — Krishna ji is different from Ram Bhagwan it's that they both are Avatar of Lord Vishnu and nothing else is common in between them.

Why are you confused? There is nothing uncommon between them, for they are one and the same.

You said — Krishna ji said that in affection, you only said before that don't go on words but you yourself doesn't implys it.

Read my statement again. And then again. I have again and again said, don’t go only for words. Cross reference them against actual events. So did you cross reference Krishna words with his actions?

Do you know when he said these words? And do you know what he did on that day. And the day before? And the day after? And the day after that? Do you find a contradiction? Didn’t you Karna fan always blame him for always being eager to protect Arjuna?

This is a brilliant example where Krishna’s word and actions are in perfect sync. Do you have a contradiction that disproves Krishna’s words?

You said — Is this Lost epic book written by you ?

YES. Have you read it? Do you like it? If you are a Bhishma fan, you should read it. I am sure, you would enjoy it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 17, 2021 10:33 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Which one?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Would-it-not-be-intelligent-move-by-Duryodhana-to-accept-proposal-of-Shrikrishna-to-give-five-villages-to-Pandava-and-avoid-war-and-lead-peaceful-royal-life-Or-did-he-see-some-catch-in-this-proposal/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 18, 2021 12:42 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Abhimanyu died in a fair mace fight.

Can you give me one single reference from the Mahabaharata epic that says Abhimanyu’s death was “fair”? One reference that said it is fair. I challenge you.

There are over dozen of explicit reference that says Abhimanyu died fighting many warriors and that his death was unfair.

But I can understand if you don’t know it. To know it you need to read the epic and your statement clearly indicate you haven’t.

You said — He was also a rape victim ulupi used him only for sex.

You confirm my theory that you have no understanding of Mahabharata. And now you tell me you have no notion of rape. A consensual sex is not considered rape.

You said — As per epic all suta woman were semi nude the kouravas asked her to remain as a servant lady but she came in one cloth so they had to remove it.

Wow! You are literally first person after evil Karna to justify Draupadi’s disrobing. But your argument is as flawed as evil Karna’s.

Just like Karna had no reference Draupadi was not a legally wed wife, you have no reference for your statement. Many years ago, Karna lied that God said a woman can have only one husband, knowing Shiva himself blessed Draupadi to have multiple. Many years later you are lying. Now let me show the flaw in your argument —

  1. Even Duryodhana accepted Druapadi’s contention that she had not been won. He asked Draupadi to come and ask her question in the court.
  2. In Hastinapur court, neither Duryodhana nor Karna was the King. And the King hadn’t ruled either for or against Draupadi being a slave. Till the time judgement was delivered, she wasn’t a slave.
  3. Karna didn’t ask Duhsasana, remove Draupadi’s cloth because she was a servant, but because he felt she wasn’t whore. That was his opinion, his judgement.

You asked — Where it is said in any yuva adopted child has property rights.

This question is ambiguous and confusing. You may want to explain it again.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-so-generous-that-he-had-to-bear-the-curses-for-the-sake-of-others-During-Mahabharata’s-time-was-it-bad-to-be-good-and-generous-Why-was-fate-so-cruel-towards-Karna-but-not-others/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 18, 2021 03:29 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for bringing such a great question to me.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Would-there-be-any-difference-in-Mahabharata-if-Bhishma-Pitamah-spoke-against-Duryodhana-and-Karna-in-the-Dyut-Sabha/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 18, 2021 03:30 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

answered

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Would-it-not-be-intelligent-move-by-Duryodhana-to-accept-proposal-of-Shrikrishna-to-give-five-villages-to-Pandava-and-avoid-war-and-lead-peaceful-royal-life-Or-did-he-see-some-catch-in-this-proposal/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 21, 2021 02:34 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

In general, I can’t degrade myself by talk to Nathuram Godse (the original) , because neither then could he understand the virtues of Santana Dharma nor would he understand now — Gandhi was no Jinnah, neither is Santanan Hindu civilization any other religion to compare with.

If only you could have understood the core philosphy of sanatana philosophy you won’t be debating on this issue —

  • Ekam Sat Vipra Bahudha Vadanti
  • Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah, Sarve Shantu Niramaya

Securlarism is a recent word and can be misconstrued. But what it could mean in the most perfect essence is known as sanatana hindu sanskriti.

But you won’t understand that as you were busy in creating a new format called Hindutva which was quite distant from Santana Dharma.

You asked — Given the grip Gandhi had on general Indian Public's mind regardless of religion,

Why do you think, Gandhiji had such a grip on general Indian mindset which was unparallel? Why do you think, your namesake or their god fathers couldn’t build such a trust or grip? Think about it.

And most important, why do you think Gandhiji would use his infinite goodwill to preach something against his will? Do you think, he was your subordinate to preach what you expect him to?

You said — I believe Gandhi was a bigger criminal than Jinnah or Nehru.

The biggest highlight in this line is — It is what you believe.

And it would surprise you to know it doesn’t really matter.

People who mattered, never cared about what Godsey thought. He may have become a hero of marginalized people. But that’s it.

Look around —

  • Modiji seeks inspiration from Gandhiji not Godsey
  • Subhash Chandra Bose called Gandhiji, the Father of the nation
  • Dr Rajendra Prasad equated him to holy Ganga
  • Barak Obama, Lord Mount Batten, Steve Jobs seeks inspiration from him.
  • Martin Luthar King Junior, Dalai Lama, Nelson Mandela, Albert Einsitien, Geroge Bernard Shaw praise Gandhiji.

Now who stands with a delusional criminal like Godsey? You. Few others who never bothered to read history. Few of those who didn’t get a change to talk to their father or grand father who were Gandhiji’s contemporary. Few more misguided by whatsapp, facebook and social media. That is all.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Nathuram-Godse-assassinate-Jinnah-who-was-the-actual-person-responsible-for-creation-of-Pak-and-instead-killed-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 22, 2021 09:25 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The answer to your first question lies in your last question. We the commoners need to be polite and firm to voice our opinion. We should try to defend our legacy, our history for such toxic hatred.

Will be able to contain it? Not sure. Should we try? I fear, we have no choice. We have to counter it with all our might.

But we can do a better job, if we understand the true reasons behind this toxic atmosphere. It has plagued many countries like Bangaladesh. For political rivalry, one party in order to throw mud on other don’t realize two things —

  1. The mud is staining not a person but our glorious history.
  2. No one dominates the world for ever. Imagine how would history portray Modiji once BJP cease to be power? Do you think, it would be better than how Gandhiji is portrayed today?

But the most important reason of this toxic hatred is the fact that we have given up to reading books and trust the knowledge gained on social media. We blindly believe whatever circulated on a pale looking faded paper must be true. It is not. Unless people devote more time reading books, just garbage will continue to occupy their mind.

You asked — Why do you think good messages on Gandhi is not as famous now like the fake ones?

NOT REALLY TRUE. Gandhiji’s goodness has spread in the world. His name has become an adjective for peace, truth and non-violence. You will find him mentioned constantly even in regular hollywood movies and TV shows like.

Bhurj Khalifa was lighted to portray Gandhiji tall personna on his birth anniversary. Steve Jobs and Barak Obama draw inspiration from him.

Unfortunately we Indians condemn him.

Content:

I am not sure even if you make the consolidated count, of retreat, what would that prove? Ghatotkatch wasn’t out to kill Karna in particular. His agenda was to create a chaos and he did it perfectly well. Karna may have survived, which even I agree, but what would have happened to the army?

Do you have any reference to contradict my references? Is there any reference that suggests Karna would have overpowered Ghatotkatcha any other way? Neither Karna nor Kaurava thought so.

Please, first share valid references and then we can start the debate.

What say?

Creation time: Nov 26, 2021 10:31 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Should we really convert every debate into a Arjuna - Karna debate?

You said — Hey my friend you are trying to mock Karna, but it's no one other than you who gets offended (somewhat) when anyone (Me) mocks Arjuna.

No. I didn’t mock Karna. He made a mock of himself by fleeing again and again. And yet again. I repeated what is already mentioned in the epic explicitly. And if you still think, I mocked Karna by saying what he was, well I did. And you know I never denied the fact that I hate evils like Karna. Have you ever seen me being diplomatic about it?

But the good thing is, you finally agreed you mock Arjuna. Now please answer my long pending question — Why do you hate Arjuna?

Remember, when you mock Arjuna, you mock Krishna and Shiva who generously supported him, you mock the grand epic that starts by a salutation to Nara and Narayana. And yes, I feel offended at such blatant insult to our epics and Gods.

You said — When Karna retreats you mention it as coward act but when Arjuna retreat and saving his life from Karna you (generally Arjuna fans) justify it.

You are confused between ‘retreat’ and ‘flee’. Everyone retreats. Even the victorious. They don’t stay back in the battle field even after winning. Do they?

Fleeing is retreat with fear. Fleeing is leaving behind your loved ones to perish for the sake of his life. Fleeing is valuing your life above others. Retreating scared is fleeing.

I hope someday you would see the difference.

But when in doubt, check out the original epic. It would tell you who scared for his life and who fought for others life. In each incident it has mentioned the cause with explicit mention of circumstances. Checkout if it says flee or retreat or scared.

If still in doubt, do come back to me and I may guide you.

Content:

We should be voicing for our national pride. Mahatma, he can take care of himself, without us pitching for him.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Nathuram-Godse-assassinate-Jinnah-who-was-the-actual-person-responsible-for-creation-of-Pak-and-instead-killed-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 26, 2021 10:44 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — When you don't have answers just say anything which doesn't even matters.

Please clarify. What did I say that didn’t matter?

You said — It's your fault that you are mocking an ancient character.

No. There is no fault in mocking an ancient villain who didn’t leave any opportunity to mock others with worst foul language.

You said — if you are calling him coward then you are indirectly mocking Bhagwan Parshurama, Shri Krishna and many others (by your logic).

No again. I most certainly didn’t mock lord Parshurama and Lord Krishna by following their words. Neither of them ever said Karna was NOT evil. To refresh you memory Krishna himself called Karna as trunk of “evil tree Duryodhana.” And here are two more of his many statements —

Krishna — Karna is evil-minded and extremely injurious towards the Pandavas. O best of men! Kill him today with your sharp arrows. This is your supreme task and there is nothing wrong in it. We applaud this and there is no sin attached to it. O unblemished one! O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

Krishna again — The evil-minded one has encouraged Dhritarashtra’s evil-souled son and roared in the assembly hall. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! Kill Karna today. In all the evil acts that Dhritarashtra’s son has done towards you, in all of these, the evil-souled and extremely wicked Karna has been present.

And here is what Parshurama said to Karna. His parting words —

Parshurama — The qualities of a brahmana will never remain with someone who is not a brahmana. Leave this place, since this is not meant for an untruthful one like you. There will be no kshatriya on earth who will be your equal in battle.’

Parshurama not only called him untruthful but also expelled him from his ashrama. And if you allude to last part of his statement it was about physical strength and not character. But that too is contradicted not only by Krishna and Shiva but also by Parshurama himself and not ignoring various actual clashes where Karna indeed was equal to no Kshatriya in battle where he kept fleeing again and again. And yet again.

Here is what Parshurama said about Arjuna in a court room that included even Karna and I don’t see him praising Karna here —

Parshurama — discard your vanity and go to Dhananjaya82 before a weapon has been affixed to Gandiva, the best of bows. He possesses Kakudika, Shuka, Naka, Akshisamtarjana, Santana, Nartana, Ghora and Ajyamodaka as the eighth.83 All men who are pierced by these confront their death, or move around insane, or lose their senses and become unconscious, or go to sleep, or jump around, or vomit, or urinate, or incessantly cry and laugh. Partha’s qualities are innumerable and Janardana is superior to him. You have known him as Kunti’s son, Dhananjaya. O great king! But know that Nara and Narayana are Arjuna and Keshava, brave warriors and bulls among men.

Questions for you —

  • How mocking an evil deceitful person, whom even Krishna and Parshurama found as such is an insult to these people?
  • Didn’t Krishna urged Arjuna to kill him calling him evil souled?
  • Didn’t Parshurama expelled him for being deceitful.

Why don’t you shield that evil whom Krishna wanted dead? Why don’t you expel that deceitful person who was expelled by Parshurama? How can you defend an evil by using the name of Krishna and Parshurama?

You said — And yes about your question, didn’t I answered it previously when we met, I clearly said, I hate Arjuna fans who mock

If you hate “Arjuna’s present fans”, that is no excuse for hating Arjuna who was loved and graced by all Gods including Krishna and Shiva.

If you hate “Arjuna” because of his fan, even you know your hate is misplaced.

If you don’t hate “Arjuna”, then your mocking Arjuna is unjustified.

The problem is, you are confused. Sometimes you say you hate “Arjuna” because of his fans. At other times you say, you hate “Arjuna fan”. Can you make your mind and clarify?

PLEASE DO RESPOND TO THIS QUESTION CLEARLY, EVEN IF YOU IGNORE EVERYTHING ELSE OR DECIDE NOT TO CARRY ON THIS DISCUSSION LIKE MANY YOU LEFT OUT.

You said — do you know that there is a special Space dedicated for trolling Karna which has about 300 followers and now you say should I respect them?

I am aware, though I never visited or shared my post on such spaces. You know, I don’t degrade my writing.

But are you aware there are numerous space mocking Arjuna and the predate the space you just mentioned.

I never said you should respect fans. But that can be no excuse for not respecting Arjuna. Can there be?

But yes, anyone who mocks and evil should be respected. It is important that we should weed out evil from our society.

You asked — Yes I will because I want to know how do you use large number of words with such efficiency.

You should write regularly. But more important, you should have conviction, passion and truth on your side. Getting the last part and supporting evil and mutually exclusive.

Content:

Sorry, I misunderstood. In that case, I hope you and I are on agreement.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-Karna-had-no-Shakti-weapon-during-his-fight-with-Ghatotkacha/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 27, 2021 01:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Are you sure, you being sure, is a sure proof of logic and illogic? Could it be that you didn’t understand the virtue of non-violence?

Let me show the flaws in your arguments — Who said anything about winning the armed combat with non-violence? Do you know Gandhiji himself advocated Indians joining armed forces? He explicitly said, Indian should earn expertise of weapons else independent India won’t be able to defend itself. It was part of his vision that Independent India was born with capable army leadership. Had he not encouraged Indians to join army, Netaji would have no resource to build INA. Two great minds supplemented each other.

But Indian freedom struggle was no war. It was a movement. It required people’s participation and people’s preparation. What kind of nation it would be even people are used to armed rebelling against their government? Do you know the fate of Seria, North Korea and your own twin sibling Pakistan?

Do you know Krishna became ranchod and moved to Dwarka from Mathura? Was he a coward? Or a Disney character? Do you know which Ashoka’s emblem adorns or flag? One who showed is wrath on Kalinga or one who preached non-violence?

Do you really subscribe to Netaji Bose? Suits me. Now tell me why didn’t Netaji formed Indian National Army within the borders of India? Why did he do it outside? Think! Do you need a hint? Krishna didn’t ask Mathurans to fight battle against Jarasandha. Did you get any hint. If you still don’t understand the virtue of non-violence, at least understand this — neither Bose nor Krishna advocated senseless violence. Neither did they ask their ordinary civilian to fight an armed battle.

And since you subscribe to Netaji Bose, let me remind you, this great leader at bestowed the title of The Father of the Nation on Mahatma Gandhi as he sought his blessings. Are you going to subscribe to his views?

Do you know the fate of valiant heroes like Chandrashekhar Azad and Bhagat Singh? No, it’s not their death I am talking. I am talking about their life long struggle to let people understand their mission. Do you know many robbers robbed people taking the disguise of revolutionaries?

We needed a movement to bring people together to create a civilized nation and that needed wisdom and sensibility rather than crude violence that no one ever advocated.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 27, 2021 01:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — in response to — Please clarify. What did I say that didn’t matter? — You yourself know what doesn't matter.
