NO. I don’t. And from your response, it is clear, even you don’t.

You said — That statement is for Pandavas as he (Karna) was on the enemy side and its true that no one praises his her enemy everyone

It shows how little you think of Krishna. Did he condemned Karna only to please Pandavas? Was Krishna a flattering stooge like Karna?

But you are, as usual, wrong. Krishna was a God incarnated to destroy evil. And he explicitly wanted Karna be destroyed. He said that on multiple occasions.

And you yourself quoted Krishna praising Karna in front of Arjuna. Once again proving Krishna was not a flattering stooge you think Krishna was.

You said — That statement is for Pandavas as he (Karna) was on the enemy side and its true that no one praises his her enemy everyone is evil minded towards each other if they are enemy

Do you mean to say, Krishna lied on all the occasion when he called Karna evil? Do you think Karna was not evil? Why Dhritrashtra, Sanjay, Bhishma, Ashwatthama everyone called him evil? Why Vyas and Narad called him trunk of the evil tree Duryodhana? Why do you find a person who tried to burn down a woman alive or tried to disrobe her in public not evil? What is your definition of evil?

You said — Everyone sees one part of the coin same applies here they didn’t see that Draupadi insulted Karna in a whole Sabha so this was the revenge.

They don’t see that Draupadi insulted Karna because that never happened. You know well it is an interpolation already removed by BORI. Can you quote a single reference where Karna or anyone says his action was a revenge of the insult? Karna explicitly mentioned he did that to please his friend. Why do you debate without any basis? Please quote a direct reference or take back your words.

And even if I humor you in this non-sense. A lady refuses to marry you. It doesn’t give you right to molest her. I repeat it doesn’t give your the right.

You said — Karna was present in mocking Pandavas he wasn't included in killing Pandavas.

YOU ARE WRONG. AGAIN. Karna was involved in all of Duryodhana’s action. He is not called trunk of an evil tree without a reason. Read this again —

Krishna — Karna is evil-minded and extremely injurious towards the Pandavas. O best of men! Kill him today with your sharp arrows. This is your supreme task and there is nothing wrong in it. We applaud this and there is no sin attached to it. O unblemished one! O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

And Karna’s involvement is clearly mentioned in Adi Parva also. In fact can you quote one single reference that Karna wasn’t involved in earlier incidents. Here is a direct reference that he was —

‘“Thereupon, the evil-minded Duryodhana, with Karna and the son of Subala,13 tried to banish them and oppressed them in various ways. That evil son of Dhritarashtra gave poison to Bhima with his food, but the warrior Vrikodara14 digested it. One day, the evil one tied the sleeping Vrikodara on the banks of the Ganga and throwing Bhima into the water went away to the city.

When the Pandavas were not killed through all these means, open and hidden, since they were protected by fate and destiny, he16 consulted his advisers: Vrisha, 17 Duhshasana and the others. With Dhritarashtra’s consent, he had a house of lac built.

This is what we call cross-reference. Krishna’s statement is a cross-reference to Karna’s initial involvement.

Now provide me a direct reference that contradicts my reference and says that Karna was not involved in these conspiracies. Let me tell you there exists none.

You said — That statement was the result of Drona's misinformation and see after saying those words.

You mentioned this so many times without giving a single reference. Everytime I asked you for reference you ran away from debate. During our last interaction I gave you clear reference that during Mahabahrata period there was no caste system but Varna system. I gave you reference from Gita. I asked you to give one reference that there was a caste system. You ran away. You couldn’t provide any direct reference that Karna cheated on Parshurama because Drona gave mis-information. There is not a single reference that Drona gave mis-information. Karna admitted to his crime but the he didn’t attribute his crime to Drona. And mind it he insulted Drona and Kripa on ocassions so don’t repeat that he was polite to his guru.

NOW since you raised this incident multiple times it is your moral obligation to prove your point —

Prove —

  • Drona gave mis-information.
  • Prove he talked about caste and not varna.
  • Prove Karna did this because of Drona’s misinformation.

But guru agreed on one thing. Karna was ineligible for it.

You said — Parshuram still managed to praise Karna. And what more do you need.

And expelled him the very next moment. His praise didn’t really worked for Karna. He was constantly defeated.

Parshurama praised Karna before knowing his true deceitful nature and expelled him on knowing his true color.

For more sky is the limit. But the least I expect from a student is to be truthful to his guru.

You said — See Now Shri Krishna praised Karna infront of Arjuna.

I have read this. But did you realize —

  • Krishna not once called he was not evil. And we have seen politicians who donate freebees on public fund and perform austerities.

And as I have told you so many times. Search for action that collaborates words. Just don’t go for words.

Krishna called him evil explicitly. He never denied it. He wanted him dead. And he was dead.

You said — If you (Arjuna fan) will mock Karna then why can't I mock your favorite Character

That is, my friend, you main problem. I don’t mock Karna to get even with Karna’s fan. I condemn him because he was an evil person. And I can say with conviction I hate evils like Karna. Out of my natural hate for evils, I would condemn him and it has got nothing to do with Karna fans like you. I never condemned you. Did I?

Now what is your excuse? You still didn’t answer the question concisely?

Answer clearly — Do you hate Arjuna?

If you are honest your answer would be yes. Because you won’t condment someone you like just to piss other. But problem is you dare not express your true feelings. It is difficult to call a good person as evil with conviction.

You said — Last time when we had a debate, it was you who left my reply emptier and discontinued the debate.

NEVER HAPPENED. Please share the reference and I will surely respond. It may not have appeared in my notification. But do share where I left the debate.

You said — Yes you are right mocking evils is good but not an hero(Karna).

So you agree mocking a hero is not right. So why do you mock Arjuna? He was the hero. You know it. Right? He is known as the trunk of righteousness. He was praised by Krishna, Shiva and everyone. He stood to protect people in need. So if it not good to mock and hero, why do you mock Arjuna. Please respond clearly.

By mistake your wrote Karna in bracket. I am sure you meant to write Arjuna —Karna was no hero

Karna was the trunk of evil tree. He was an evil person. He was part of conspiracy of murder, arson, molestation, and loot. He had been repeatedly mentioned as wicked deceitful, and evil. And these were no hollow words. Each was vindicated by his actions. And last time even you failed to give one good deed of Karna that made you think he was a hero. All you could say that his gray character is more relatable —

  • Who considers a molester as a hero?
  • What ideal a hero presents if he is deceitful even to his guru?
  • What ideal does a coward present when he ditches his own best friend and runs away for his life?
  • You may consider a person who tries to throw a bride into fire as a hero, for me he was a condemned being.

You said — Yeah I will work on this thanks a lot for your Gentle words. My writing is an inspired version of your writing and style.

I am glad, I proved to be of some service to you. I would be, however, truly happy should you get inspired by me in following the path of Dharma.

P.S. I saw your appeal against Karna troll space, but didn’t see a similar appeal against Arjuna’s troll space. Any specific reason?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-Karna-had-no-Shakti-weapon-during-his-fight-with-Ghatotkacha/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 27, 2021 03:04 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — History gives me proof of destruction so in order to survive if I had to do violence ‘so it be’ because its inherent nature of humans to survive first then ill think of non-violence stuff.

That is not true nature of humans. That is the primitive beastly nature summarized so well by Charles Darwin in his theory called — Survival of the fittest. But Darwin also proposed an equally great theory — Theory of adaptation and evolution. All living organism have capability to adapt and evolve, humans have more than anyone else.

So please don’t neglect the evolution in favor of history. Our past need not define our present and future. Don’t you see Rama and Krishna strived to show the right path to humans? Shri Rama crossed over to Lanka, and yet he sent his emissary to strive for peace. Krishna himself was the peace emissary. Why did these great minds strived for peace and non-violent approach? Because as a human, we were evolving to get rid of animal instincts. Guess what we worship Rama and Krishna not Ravana and Kansa.

You said — You are saying it was better to make a ‘deal’ with British to create a civilized nation (I doubt this happened) and get partitioned based on religion and hoped that there will be no violence in coming years.

NO. I NEVER SAID THAT. That’s your view of what you thought happened.

You know the hardest decision a surgeon can take is to surgically remove a limb so as to save the precious life. It wasn’t some sort of deal with Britishers. It was realization of reality. It wasn’t an attempt to hope there would be no violence in future, it was to control the violence of that day that would have gulped a much larger population then had measures not be taken.

And yes, there is not much scope of an independent uncivilized society in this world. As you see so many countries living the life of horror because they succumbed to primitive animal instinct of violence's than the non-violent nature of more evolved species.

Those who are not focused on destruction of other’s get better chance to self preservation and improvement. If you think, with violence you can create a just rule, you are WRONG. It never happened. With violence you can simply redefine justice in the same way people are violently trying to re-write our history on social media.

You said — I just pray because that's all I can do that India’s neighbors don't start nuking India resulting in elimination of India from face of this planet — “this could have been avoided”

I think answer likes in your question. “This couldn’t have been avoided”. You may have lost half your population and avoided Partition. But you couldn’t have stopped China and Afganistan from being a threat. With unified India, we would still have hostile neighbours like China and Afganistan.

The solution was and is to have a strong army, that Gandhiji actually advocated. But a strong army is expected to defend the nation from external threats and not fuel civil war. Armies are not supposed to fight within your border.

Your problem, as I see, lies in not able to distinguish between external threat (or war) and internal struggle for independent country with its value deeply rooted in its ancient history.

You said — Ill stand to my point, that non-violence act gave more path to violence.

I gave you real examples — from Rama to Krishna and from Ashoka to Shubhash Chandra Bose. And non of them advocated senseless violence.

I can’t change your views. Evolution is a slow process and being non-violent is something that requires much greater courage. But if you can’t change your views, at least don’t say you subscribe to Subhash Chandra Bose, because like many of us, he too subscribed to Gandhiji.

Now my question — What is the alternative you sought should have happened?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 27, 2021 10:01 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for you comments.

I agree with you when you say India was like a milking cow for Britishers but the same observation makes my conclusion stark opposite to that of yours. India was a source of money for Britishers, something they needed more than ever with their treasury badly depleted due to world war II expenses. It was the worst time for Britishers to leave India. So why did they do it? Did they turn humanitarian overnight? Or was there some other more significant reason?

A little deliberation and you would realize, A colony ruler can’t really rule the colony without the cooperation of those whom they dominated. They can’t even mine those resources without their support. Do you see the imprint of non-cooperation and civil disobedience here?

Let me detail it for you. In 1919, Indian soldiers, with unquestioning devotion, obeyed general Dyer in murdering their own countrymen. Flash forward to 1946. Indian navy rebels against the throne to express their solidarity with their fallen countrymen serving under INA. Do you see a change in the mindset of Indians. We were no more cooperating. We were ready for the civil disobedience.

I understand that leads to a lot of follow up questions —

1. If it was non-cooperation and civil disobedience, why didn’t India become independent in 1920’s?

The seed of an idea takes time to root itself. It is a gradual process and not a 20-20 match. It took it’s time, but certainly took a strong root. Indians were ready to revolt. INA and Navy mutiny is a testimonial to the fact. Britishers couldn’t have hoped for another Jalianwala Bag or 1857 in India. They knew they had to leave. And it was more from the realization that ruling enlightened masses is no more possible. And fear of losing often makes people more humanitarian. But this humanitarian approach didn’t made them grant independence to South Africa before 1961.

2. How other nations too got their independence between 1945-1955? They had no Gandhi or Bose.

If you think of Gandhi and Bose as mere individual human beings, maybe they weren’t blessed like we were. But if you realize that they were ideas and not just individuals, you can’t fail to see their contribution in the freedom of other colonies as well.

You see, in twentieth century, countries were no more isolated islands unaware of what was going around the world. News and media had become global, long before the internet. The ideas that took birth at one place at far reaching effect at the other. To prove my point, let me quote something from your shared reference ---

Indonesian struggle for independence from the Netherlands (1945–50), the Vietnamese war against France (1945–54), and the nationalist and professed socialist takeovers of Egypt (1952) and Iran (1951) served to reinforce such fears, even if new governments did not directly link themselves to the Soviet Union.

Don’t you see the imprint of Gandhi’s and Bose’s ideas here? Colonies were no more ready to be ruled. And without their cooperation and with their explicit civil disobedience…

You see, just like dominoes fall, there is dominion fall. Success of one nation, on one hand becomes a beacon of hope for all oppressed nations, and a warning bell for the oppressors on the other hand. Just like colonies started on a similar pretext it ended on a similar note.

3. Were there no humanitarian concerns?

Of Course there must have been. Why else does the British Prime Minister sound apologetic even after a century of Jalianwala Bagh? But what do you think, could be the reason for that sudden shift in humanitarian thoughts in mere two decades?

During his visit to Britain, Gandhji had also visited colonies of those mill workers who had lost their jobs because of Gandhi’s policy. And yet instead of being resentful they welcomed him with open arms. Even today Britisher tourist guides recount that moment with pride.

Was it not an explicit Gandhian thought that through our sufferings we would show them their injustice? So, why should we be surprised when we have a clear proof of his doctrine in action? Gandhiji was an ambassador of his ideas, something that was influencing the whole world.

An Appeal

Have a look at the west-world and their attitude. These were some of those people who took pride in the fact that in their empire, the sun never sets. And these are the same people who called themselves first and the second world and reigate others to the third world. And thus far, the third world took the solace in the fact that Kalias was called Indian Shakespeare.

From that third world, rose a man who changed the nomenclature forever. Now we call them African Gandhi and Russian Gandhi. And Gandhi is not used as an adjective for peace, truth and non-violence. He was denied the Nobel prize, was unanimously accepted as the greatest man of the twentieth century. Lord Mount Batten equated him to Jeasus Christ and Gautam Buddha and Albert Einstein rightly predicted that generations to come shall scarcely belief that a man of flesh and blood such as Gandhi ever walked on this earth. And see, how correct he was --- While world leaders like Steve Jobs and Barak Obama seeks inspiration from him, we wonder if Gandhi had any contribution in India’s freedom movement! Did we win the freedom or we begged for it!

Please don’t be misguided by those western opinions which are more to balm their bruised ego and hide their loss in the facade of being humanitarian --- You know the grapes are a sour story.

But Let us not destroy our great history and legacy. Let them not fool us into thinking that we got our freedom by begging. Our freedom is NOT the result of someone's goodwill, rather our leaders were liberators not just for our nation but for many more who got independence during the period.

Be proud of our nation, and our heritage.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Britishers-left-India-due-to-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-and-not-due-to-Mahatma-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 28, 2021 02:02 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

This is a very popular wishful thinking that Karna fan generally linger to. It was not Chitrasena but Arjuna who fought mildly, at least in the start. Chitrasena fought feebly. And these two words are in complete contrast to each other. I would refer you to a good dictionary —

Definition of MILD

1: gentle in nature or behaviorhas a mild disposition

2a(1): moderate in action or effecta mild sedative(2): not sharp, spicy, or bittermild cheesemild aleb: not being or involving what is extreme an analysis under mild conditions

3: not severe

Definition of FEEBLE

1a: markedly lacking in strengtha feeble old manb: indicating weakness taking only feeble steps

2a: deficient in qualities or resources that indicate vigor, authority, force, or efficiencya feeble argumentcities are growing uncreative and feeble— Jane Jacobsb: INADEQUATE, INFERIOR

Once you grasp the difference two words, I would encourage you to follow the story with a clear mind. Here is the start of the battle —

Following describe Arjuna —

O descendant of the Bharata lineage! Remembering the words of the king who was Dharma’s son, the first battle that was engaged was a mild one. But the soldiers of the king of the gandharvas were deluded in intelligence. They were incapable of realizing that something that was mild might benefit them.

It clearly indicates the foolish Gandharvas didn’t understand Arjuna’s mild attack was beneficial to them. So what did they do?

There were four brave Pandavas and thousands of gandharvas. O king! They attacked each other in that battle and it was extraordinary. Just as the gandharvas had shattered the chariots of Karna and the sons of Dhritarashtra into a hundred pieces, they attempted to do it again.

They attacked Pandavas in the same way, they attacked Kauravas. It clearly indicates they showed no extra care or mildness for them. Rather —

They angrily hurled showers of maces, spears and swords at Arjuna.

Now enter the dear friend of Arjuna, but not really as a dear friend. Here is what he does —

On seeing the gandharvas terrified by Kunti’s intelligent son, Chitrasena grasped a mace and rushed towards Savyasachi. In that battle, he held the iron mace in his hand. But with his arrows, Partha sliced the mace into seven pieces. On seeing that the mace had been cut into many pieces by the arrows of the nimble one, he57 made himself invisible and began to fight with Pandava. Established in the sky, he repulsed those celestial weapons. The powerful king of the gandharvas hid himself through his powers of maya. Arjuna saw that he was invisible and was striking.

You can see there is no mild attack from Chitrasena. He uses maya. He becomes invisible. He continues to fight using mace, arrows and maya. And then something happened.

Dhananjaya used the weapon known as shabdabheda 58 and restrained his disappearance into many different forms. Pierced by the weapons of the great-souled Arjuna, the gandharva revealed himself to his beloved friend. 59 On seeing his friend Chitrasena, fighting feebly in battle, 60 the bull among the Pandavas withdrew the weapon he had released.

Arjuan breaks Chitrasena’s maya and he was wounded by Arjuna. Then as an act of surrender, he revealed himself. And watch out Chitrasena was fighting feebly, not mildly. Can you see the difference?

And this is line with what Shiva and Indra said about Arjuna — He was invincible.

You asked — If Karna can counter illusions of Ghatotkacha then why can't he defeat Chitrasena. Or It may be the thing that Karna realized his weakness and worked on it afterwards.

The important problem that you must realize is to break enemy formation of any kind you need to stay put. No war can be won by running away. Karna had divine Kavacha at this point in time. He couldn’t have been killed. He could have fought through his maya.

I won’t compare Arjuna and Karna here. Consider a spectrum between them. Neither Madri twins nor Duryodhana and his brothers had special divine weapons. Yet none of them fled the battle. They may have been killed but not defeated.

And yes, Karna may have worked on his weakness, but neither there is any explicit testimonial to it nor an exact reference to prove it. He did break through Ghatokatch’s maya. But it may been weaker than that of celestial beings like Gandharvas. To add on to that, Ghatotkatch was just one warrior not an army. Then again, despite of breaking his maya, Karna couldn’t neither win him, nor restrain Ghatotkatch without the use of maya.

But then, this question is not about Karna. It is about Arjuna and his battles with Gandharvas. But would love to clear your doubt.

Hope now you understand the difference between mild and feeble and which one is which.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-there-any-relation-between-the-Gandharva-who-encountered-Pandavas-and-got-defeated-by-ARJUNA-in-Adiparva-and-the-one-who-tried-to-abduct-Duryodhana-during-Ghoshayatra-Is-hethe-same-Gandharva-as-per-some-versions/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 30, 2021 10:39 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry missed your question by a long margin.

You asked — What lie karna told to his guru can you elaborate on the same

You question confirms you don’t know anything of Mahabharata, the original epic. Karna introduced himself as a Brahmana, not just any but a Bhargava Brahmana. Now Parshurama too was a Bhargava Brahamana.

In fact Karna himself recalls his action as “crime”

You said — Draupadi was own by arjun but by the time she reached pandava home she became wife of five please let me know who was behind this trick.

WRONG. Draupadi wasn’t owned by Arjuna. Arjuna won the swaymvar as a pre-requisite to marriage. He didn’t won her. He didn’t own her.

What was the trick here? Have you heard about polygamy? And polyandry? But if you think, it was a trick, it was Lord Shiva who was behind the trick. Remember, it was his blessing (not curse).

You said — If you recall one day when arjun asked to step down of the chariot by krishna he feels offended and gets down then krishna gets down instantly chariot burns into ashes krishna says that is why he asked arjun to get down first and he also says it is power of karnas arrows or his warfare skill

NO. I don’t recall Krishna talked exclusively about Karna or his warfare skills. He included the names of Drona and Bhishma also. And he didn’t give the credit to their skills but to the divine weapons used by them. Do you understand the difference?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identity-of-Karna-in-Swayamvara-and-let-him-marry-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 02, 2021 11:48 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Actually NO. No one made fun of him because of his birth.

  1. He was trained by Drona and Kripa knowing his origins.
  2. Parshurama never resented he was a Suta ( born of Kshatriya-Brahmana reunion). He cursed him because of his deceitful nature.
  3. Vidura was born to a maid servant. He was the prime minister of Hastinapur and never disrespected.

Now coming to the talent showcase event. It was a private event (for participation) and a public show case for the talents of Hastinapur princess only. Karna had no right to interfere in the ceremony. Yet, Drona allowed him to participate and matter should have ended there.

But Karna wanted to fight. He had gatecrashed the ceremony unprovoked and now was looking forward for a duel which was uncalled for. Kripacharya asks his identity as per the protocol of duel. There was no humiliation in asking once identity.

His identity was revealed by entrance of his father, Adhiratha, a well-known friend of the care-taker King Dhritrashtra. This proves he can’t be mocked for his low birth. There is no explicit reference.

So why did Bhima compared him to a Dog?

Bhima didn’t call him a Dog. He called him unworthy. He used Dog as a metaphor. He said Karna had no right to throne in the same way a Dog had no right to yagna offerings.

But why would Bhima say that? Because Bhima had known him since childhood as Karna constantly sided with Duryodhana in numerous attempt to kill and torment Pandavas and specifically Bhima.

SUMMARY

  • No one mocked Karna. Kripacharya asked his identity and authority with which he demanded an unprovoked duel.
  • Bhima called the man Karna unworthy. He didn’t call him low born.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-You-prove-Karna-was-Low-Born/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 04, 2021 04:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks. Now updated.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karna-used-Rukmis-Vijay-bow-or-he-obtained-from-Parashuram-Can-we-believe-in-Karnas-statement-who-himself-lied-many-times/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 05, 2021 07:23 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,

Again you are quick to make assumptions and inference without valid reference.

You said — Bhishma also had a mistake in his list which later resulted as a loss for Duryodhana, and that mistake was placing Karna as a Arthrathi (lower than ratha/rathi).

You say as if had Bhishma not placed Karna as Ardharathi, Kauravas would have won. That is a wishful thinking explicitly contradicted by Krishna, Shiva Parshurama. And as I have said again and again, don’t just go on words. Check the facts —

  1. In every direct and indirect collision between Kauravas and Pandavas, Pandavas won. This time they were sided by Krishna. Even Karna knew before the start of the war, Kauravas would lose. So youare assumption is baseless.
  2. On the other hand Bhishma gave tangible reason as to Karna is a ardharathi. He was boastful and often fled the battle with no track record of winning against Pandavas.

You said — As we all know Karna was a man of respect, he loves his social image more than anything, he can even donate his life for fame.

Loving your social image makes you arrogant and boastful and NOT A Man of respect.

No man of respect would try to molest a woman in public. No man of respect would try to burn down a bride. And no man of respect would conspire to kill enemies by deceit. No man of respect would deceive his own guru. A man of arrogance would do all those things.

But I agree with you on this — Karna donated for his fame. But that, once again is mark of boastful arrogant nature and not man of respect.

You said — But this classification resulted in Karna withdrawing from war which was ultimate cause of slow starting of Kaurav sena.

Once again you confirm he was a man or full or arrogance for whose own respect was a higher priority than his friends well being, which was higher than dharma and saving of humanity.

Below inequations sum Karna’s priority —

Self Respect > Friend > Humanity

You said — he was more distructful than both Drona and Bhishma combined.

I think, you mean destructive. Yes, Karna was destructive by nature. And look he destroyed the whole Kaurava’s army and their hope of victory as Dhritrashtra lamented on the end of day 16.

But if you mean he did more damage to Pandavas, you must check your facts again —

Bhishma’s Tenure

Kaurva’s held their forte. Bhishma’s presence made winning a remote possibility for Pandavas. Krishna himself rose to attack Bhishma twice. And this is a testimony to his valor. Not to forget, he was the longest serving general.

Drona’s Tenure

Drona served as general for five days. Pandavas lost some of their greatest warriors during his tenure — Abhimanyu, King Virata, King Drupad.

He honestly tried to win the war for Duryodhana. His strategy of day 13 would have won the war had Abhimanyu not intervened. He also tried to push the battle through the night. Unethical it was, but was for the welfare of Duryodhana.

Karna’s Tenure

Karna was out in just two days. During these two days no major warrior from the Pandavas side were killed. Sixteenth day, Kauravas were badly by Arjuna and were made to retreat. Karna himself conceded the defeat. Seventeenth day, Arjuna killed Karna’s son in front of his own eyes and he could do nothing to save him. He soon met his maker after conceding that Arjuna was the best warrior of the time.

But more important, Karna never fought honestly for Duryodhana. He had chance to capture Yudhisthira and put an end to the war. He didn’t, something that Drona tried with all sincerity. Unlike Bhishma he never even bothered to tell Duryodhana that he won’t kill Yudhishthira.

In retrospection, Bhisma was right. Duryodhana was wrong to make a Ardharathi, the general of his army.

So what is your point here?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Are-the-classifications-of-warriors-done-by-Bhishma-in-the-Ratha-Atiratha-Parva-appropriate/answer/Kushal-Tiwari-86
Creation time: Dec 05, 2021 07:47 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — without your comment my answers are incomplete.

NOT just incomplete but factually incorrect.

You said — Duryodhana has to incur for 10 days because of not having a Maharathi for 10 days. It's same like Hardik Pandya is not playing for Indian team.

Consider Hardik Pandya insists on removing the captain of the team as a precondition for the team because he things captain insulted him. Now no one dreams of removing the captain so disgrunted Hardik Pandya decides to sit out the match for his personal ego knowing Indian team may suffer. Do you really think Indian team needs such an arrogant player who keeps his ego above the team?

Thank God Hardik Pandya is no Karna and team India is not Duryodhana! This is too absurd as an analogy

You said — Just take a example of famous footballer Cristiano Ronaldo, he loves his social image but that doesn't make him boastful but yes sometimes he may over speak.

Has Ronaldo also ditched his team for so called ‘self respect’?

Boastful is when you claim more than what you can deliver. Is it the case with Ronaldo? I don’t follow football. But if anyone claims more performs less, he would be called boastful.

By the way he has been called boastful a number of times within the epic itself.

You said — At least Karna never said that he will finish war in one day.

No. Karna said he would finish it in five days. Could he? That is called boastful. Saying that he would finish in one day would be even more stupid. Right?

By the way, did someone say he would finish it one day? Can you share a reference?

You said — But this society doesn't wants to see other part of the coin which was committed by Draupadi in her Swayamwara. in response to — No man of respect would try to molest a woman in public.

TWO QUESTIONS FOR YOU —

  1. What do you mean by — But this society doesn't wants to see other part of the coin which was committed by Draupadi in her Swayamwara? BE EXPLICIT.
  2. Are you trying to justify Karna’s attempt to disrobe Draupadi. BE EXPLICIT.

You said — And to burst your assumptions let me give you some references. Here it is clearly said that it was Pandavas who attacked Kuru princes.

Don’t bust my assumptions. Read the reference again. Reading will not only improve your English but also help your realize that here the term “Kuru Princes” refers to Pandava who too were Kuru.

Even if you don’t understand English, at least understand the context. Why would Pandavas attack Kauravas? Is there any reason? But I am sure if you read again, you will realize your mistake.

You said — Again don't make assumptions. Karna never tried burn anyone.

Can you please confirm the exact reason as to why Karna entered the Swyamvara battle? Please explain where exactly the reason is mentioned that is different from the stated one below —

O kings! If this lady does not wish one of us as her husband, let us throw her into the fire and return to our kingdoms. Though that Brahmana has done injury to kings out of his impertinence and greed, he should not still be killed. After all, our kingdoms, lives, riches, sons, grandsons and all our other wealth exist for the sake of Brahmanas. But something must be done to prevent insult and to protect our own dharma, so that other svayamvaras do not end like this one.” Having said this, those tigers among kings, with arms like clubs, rushed at Drupada with diverse weapons, so as to kill him. ‘Seeing those angry kings rush at him with bows and arrows, Drupada was frightened and sought the protection of the Brahmanas. However, as those kings rushed at Drupada like mad elephants, Pandu’s two sons,25 the great archers who conquered their enemies, advanced to repulse them. Thereupon, all the kings, their fingers clad in armour and with their weapons raised, violently rushed towards the Kuru princes, Arjuna and Bhimasena. Bhima, the performer of amazing deeds, with immense strength and like a thunderbolt, tore up a large tree with his hands and stripped it of all its leaves, like an elephant. With that tree, the mighty-armed Bhima, Pritha’s son and the destroyer of enemies, stood next to Partha, bull among men and with long and thick arms, like Yama with his terrible staff.

Do you know which names are prominently mentioned among those who violently rushed towards Kuru Princes, Arjuna and Bhimasena? Here it is —

Eager to fight, those kings then said, “It is permissible to kill a Brahmana who is willing to fight.” Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant. Shalya, king of the Madras, rushed at Bhimasena. Duryodhana and the others rushed at the Brahmanas, but they fought with them lightly and carelessly.

Here is clearly mentions three prominent name. Can you read them for me?

You said — And yes it were the other Kings who tried to burn Draupadi not Kauravas or Karna there isn't any mention about Kauravas or Karna burning Draupadi.Here it is explicitly mentioned Kauravas Princes and it's true they we'rent king they were princes and Dhritarashtra was King.

Actually, only Karna, Duryodhana and Shalya are explicitly mentioned here. And Kuru Princes refers to Arjuna and Bhimasena, not the sons of Dhritrashtra.

So you myth lay shattered against hard reference.

You said — Pandavas who really burnt 6 Nishad citizens to save their life.

You read too much on Quora. If you read original epic you would learn —

  1. Nishadas were supposed to return after dinner.
  2. Pandavas were NOT aware of their presence in the house.
  3. Bhimasena ensures that Purochana is dead. He never bothered to ensure the same about Nishadas
  4. Yudhishthira wonders if Purochana actually died. He never wondered about Nishadas.
  5. There is no reference or cross reference that Pandavas willfully killed them.

Why do you hate Arjuna and Pandavas so much as to propagate such baseless accusions?

You said — He wasn't there in the Lakshgriha conspiracies. If you want to prove then give proper references about that event.

I have given you explicit references a dozen times. Let me do it again. And it not just about Lakshagriha but many more —

‘“Thereupon, the evil-minded Duryodhana, with Karna and the son of Subala,13 tried to banish them and oppressed them in various ways. That evil son of Dhritarashtra gave poison to Bhima with his food, but the warrior Vrikodara14 digested it. One day, the evil one tied the sleeping Vrikodara on the banks of the Ganga and throwing Bhima into the water and went away to the city. But when the son of Kunti woke up, he tore the ropes with which he was tied with his strong arms and Bhima’s pains disappeared. While he was asleep, he was bitten everywhere in his body by black snakes with virulent poison, but that destroyer of enemies did not die. However, in all this oppression, the great-souled Vidura was always on guard, to neutralize the evil plans and save them from oppression. As Shakra15 ensures happiness in heaven and the world of living beings, thus did Vidura always ensures happiness for the Pandavas. When the Pandavas were not killed through all these means, open and hidden, since they were protected by fate and destiny, he16 consulted his advisers: Vrisha, Duhshasana and the others. With Dhritarashtra’s consent, he had a house of lac built.

Note:

  • Duryodhana’s plans were carried out with the help of Karna and Shakuni.
  • Duryodhana explicitly consulted Vrisha before building the house of lac.

Now, I know what you are going to say next — Karna was consulted, but where is it mentioned Karna actually said yes. Yes, this is a common theme of followers of evil. Let me explain carefully —

  • Karna had already tried to kill Pandavas earlier (see the above para). There is no reason he won’t do it again.
  • Karna had a clear knowledge of the conspiracy. This knowledge itself make him complicit. Anyway neither of them had to actually put on the fire. They were mastermind. If you know a criminal conspiracy and do nothing to thwart it, you are equally guilty.
  • There is NOT ONE REFERENCE ANYWHERE THAT KARNA DID SOMETHING AGAINST THIS CONSPIRACY.

This reference is from BORI. But story doesn’t end here. There is another explicit reference I gave you so many times. Here it is —

O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

Do you know who is the speaker here? Let me give you a hint, he is one person whom followers of Dharma admire and you always distrust.

Question for you — Can you give any single direct reference that Karna wasn’t involved in Lakshagriha conspiracy? Be explicit and DON’T RUN AWAY FROM THIS QUESTION.

You said — Well again blame Drona for this he was the real man behind this scene it was his misinformation which caused this.

No point in debate. I explained it to you on a dozen of occasions but you don’t seem to understand. There was no misinformation. And you have always failed to provide any direct reference that Drona’s statement was a mis-information. Either prove it by reference or stop giving such reference.

Question for you — Is there any explicit reference in Mahabharata that says Drona’s statement to Karna was a mis-information?

But just to carry on with your lack of knowledge, even if Drona gave mis-information (which he didn’t), deceiving Parshurama wasn’t his idea. It was Karna’s. Karna was alone responsible for his crime, which he himself admitted. And he alone paid a price of his crime.

You said — And one more thing if Brahaman was a Varna then didn't Karna technically became Brahaman by performing austerities and learning Vedas?

NO. Reading Vedas doesn't make anyone a Brahmin. Karna never really learnt it. Had he learnt he wouldn’t have misquoted Vedas to suggest Draupadi is a whore rather than a legally wed wife. He says Gods said… He was lying. God of God had blessed Draupadi.

And deceit is not a part of any austerities. He was no Brahmana.

You said — Great now you are distorting the facts. In response to — he was a man or full or arrogance for whose own respect was a higher priority than his friends well being, which was higher than dharma and saving of humanity.

Question for you — Please explain how I am distorting the facts? Explain which part is not explicitly mentioned in Mahabharata?

You said — I would like to ask you if someone is insulting you again and again then still will you go to that place ? The simple answer is No. But Karna stayed at Hastinpur for Duryodhana even after being insulted by Dron, Kripa and Bhishma.

The answer may be “no” for you. Don’t generalize the answer.

There are true friends who would bear every humiliation but will not desert their friends or benefactors. Karna and Duryodhana on multiple occasions insulted Dronacharya. But did he leave Hastinapur? Do you know why? Because he was loyal to Bhishma. He didn’t leave Bhishma even if Duryodhana and Karna insulted him. For him Bhishma was more important than two stupid creatures.

And you are wrong. Neither Bhishma nor Drona nor Kripa insulted Karna. They only mentioned facts. Drona and Kripa were his guru and had the right to reprimand Karna. Karna being abusive shows the low character of Karna

You said — And yes Karna did followed his Dharma by not killing 4 Pandavas as their big brother and by excluding Arjuna he assured that Kauravas would win the war.

No he didn’t follow Dharma. He fulfilled his pledge. He had no respect for relation, else he wouldn’t have killed Abhimanyu being his elder uncle.

And excluding Arjuna he could have ensured Kauravas victory but not the life of Duryodhana. But most importantly — Did he ever tell Duryodhana about his treachery?

You said — Self Respect > Friend > Humanity. Again it your personal opinion.

NO. It’s an epic reference

  1. Karna refused to fight for Duryodhana because he thought he was insulted

Self Respect > Friend

  1. Karna refused to avoid the war that was to destroy humanity because he said he is doing for his friend.

Friend > Humanity

There is no personal opinion. It is clearly mentioned. Provide reference to prove me wrong.

You said — 16th day no one had clear victory although Pandavas gained upperhand in evening time.

You haven’t read the epic. If you had, you won’t be telling there was no clear victory. All hopes of victory were gone from Kaurva camp. That was first achievement of the new commander. Let me quote it for you —

‘Sanjaya replied, “They were killed, wounded and shattered. They were deprived of their armour and the vehicles that bore them in war. Their voices were distressed. Those insolent ones grieved and were defeated by the enemy. The Kouravas went to their camps and sought counsel and advice from each other. They were like serpents that had been defanged and had lost their poison, having then been trod on by the foot. Karna angrily spoke to them, sighing like a snake. He rubbed one hand against another hand and glanced towards your son.286 ‘Arjuna is always firm, skilled and persevering. He understands and when the time is right, Adhokshaja287 instructs him. We were deceived by him today, because of that sudden shower of weapons. O lord of the earth! But tomorrow, I will destroy all his intentions.’288

You said — Indeed, neither Bhishma, nor Drona, nor any other warrior of thy army had ever achieved such feats as were then achieved by Karna in that battle.

Who said this? When. Please quote a reference. And how many times should I tell you, don’t give blank statements. Give a proper cross reference. Tell me what were the exact achievements of Karna as a commander and how was it greater than Bhishma or Drona?

  • Could he protect his army for a longer time?
  • Could he ensure the victory?
  • Could he kill one important army general?

You said — And one more thing the biggest Carnage of Mahabharata happened under your so called great Guru and Senapati Drona's Tenure he conceded the death of 7 akshouhinis (I think you forgot to mention that)

No, I didn't forget the most wonderful event of the entire war. It wasn’t relevant.

But now that you want to bring it, I won’t shy away from it either. Please remind me where was Karna on that fateful day when 7 akshauhinis were destroyed? Let me jogg your memory. He was there in the center of it getting badly thrashed. It was there shared failure.

You said — in response to — Arjuna was the best warrior of the time — He was good warrior but Best word is too much for him.

In your opinion, he may not have been the best warrior. But I am not talking about your opinion. I am talking about the opinions of Shiva, Krishna, Indra, Parshurama, and Karna. They all said he was the best warrior of the era. And I am not just going on the words, but also on the deeds. Now tell me who was the best warrior of the era if not Arjuna? And don’t just name a person. Give proper reference to contradict Shiva’s statement.

You said — And remember the reason why he fall from Mountain because of his arrogance. Now you say wasn't Arjuna arrogant according to Dharmaraj.

Yes. According to Dhramaraj, Arjuna was arrogant. But according to Dharmaraj Karna was brave. It is just an opinion. NO backing proof. In fact Yudhisthira remembered the incident wrong and believed his memory.

And I told you earlier, will tell you again — I trust Shiva and Krishna more than Yudhishthira. And both called him humble and sinless. I don’t know why would you distrust Shiva and Krishna.

You said — Read it in his own words poor Arjuna afraid Ardharathi. Lol

LOL! You laugh because you think Arjuna is afraid of Karna and still say you don’t hate Arjuna. Why such duplicity. Please answer, why do you hate Arjuna?

And once you finished laughing out loud. Read the quotes again. Arjuna’s worry isn’t Karna. It is Bhargava weapon and it’s misuse. The term “running away” refers to the fact that he thinks he had to fight the divine weapon and that would mean he too will have to misuse Divyastra, something he never wanted. That is what he said — I don’t see any means of running away.

You see a warrior who is not willing to destroy the earth by firing higher weapon even if one of the irresponsible wielder misused it. But do you know what happened to the Bhargava weapon? Did it win the war? Did it kill Pandva’s generals? What really happened?

You said - in response to my observation — In retrospection, Bhisma was right. Duryodhana was wrong to make a Ardharathi, the general of his army. — Again another baseless opinion.

How is it baseless? Did Karna succeed in winning the war? Could he hold on to the army as long as Bhishma or Drona did? NO his failure vindicated Bhishma.

IMPORTANT!

You have a habit of running away without answering the question and then keep repeating the same question which I have answered in many post. This should end now. There are few questions I have asked you in the post. Please reply them with reference or say you have no reference. Don’t repeat the same question which I have answered with proof and you failed to provide any reference in your own defence. Here is all question for you —

  1. What do you mean by — But this society doesn't wants to see other part of the coin which was committed by Draupadi in her Swayamwara? BE EXPLICIT.
  2. Are you trying to justify Karna’s attempt to disrobe Draupadi. BE EXPLICIT.
  3. Provide an explicit reference that Drona’s suggestion to Karna was mis-information.
  4. Provide reference that Pandavas knowing and willfully killed Nishadas.
  5. Provide reference that Karna didn’t attack Druapad and his family with the intention of throwing Draupadi in fire.
  6. Provide reference that Karna was not involved in Lakshagriha conspiracy. I have given two explicit reference that he was involved.
  7. WHY DO YOU HATE ARJUNA? You Laughed on him. You called his a killer of Nishada. And you have no reason for it.

I am bookmarking this discussion so that I can remind you if you didn’t or didn’t answer these questions. Unless you provide the references to prove your point, let us agree you won’t quote them again.

By repeating baseless facts without reference won’t make them correct.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Are-the-classifications-of-warriors-done-by-Bhishma-in-the-Ratha-Atiratha-Parva-appropriate/answer/Kushal-Tiwari-86
Creation time: Dec 06, 2021 07:15 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — I think this habbit belongs to you as well, you also haven't replied my comment twice or thrice. So first see yourself before telling me anything.

Never happened unless I didn’t receive notification. Last time I requested to share the link so that I can answer. Can you share any one ocassion where I didn’t answer?

You said — I wanted to say that everyone wants to see that part where Karna insulted Draupadi but they want to ignore the part where Draupadi insulted Karna in whole Sabha. First her husband insulted Karna now she has insulted.

Are you sure you have ready BORI as you mention in your signature?

Draupadi NEVER Insulted Karna. It has been deemed as an interpolation because only 5 out of 1259 manuscript included this tale. Prof Suthankar clearly reasons why it was an interpolation.

On the other hand Draupadi humilation is not only included in BORI it has dozen of cross reference.

You said — And contrary to djthat Karna apologized for his actions but did Draupadi apologized ? The simple answer is No.

That is the point. You can apologize for a mistake you do and not for a fiction someone creates later. Draupadi couldn’t have apologized for what future writer like you would make her do. But Karna’s crimes are documented with dozens of cross references.

You asked - I want to justify his actions that were caused by Draupadi herself. She insulted Karna infront of everyone and when Karna did the same with her she played Victim Card.

You don’t know Mahabharata as well as you say you do.

You said — And I want to clear one thing that disrobing part is little bit overrated he was for sure talking about royal clothes because he didn't mentioned Draupadi alone he mentioned that, take the clothes of Pandavas and Draupadi.

Thats your opinion. And I am petrified of such an opinion. To be fascinated by an evil is one part, but to endorse such a brutal deed is an entirely different ball game.

And that is one of the reasons I hate Karna. In order to justify his mis-deeds, his fans may actually first justify his deeds and maybe imitate it. Society is better off without such evil being an idol.

Draupadi’s disrobing is NOT overrated. Kauravas lost their life for it. Krishna pronounced the death for the doers. Everyone then condemned it. Everybody except Karna’s fans like you still condemns it.

Really sorry to hear such a statement.

You didn’t answer — Provide an explicit reference that Drona’s suggestion to Karna was mis-information

No. You didn’t answer this. The reference is a statement made by Dronacharya and I already explained it refers to Varna. The statement doesn’t include the word mis-information.

I asked you to give a reference where this statement has been contradicted or quoted as a mis-information by anyone in Mahabharata.

I will stop asking this question the moment you provide the correct answer to this question — Any reference where it is said that Drona gave mis-information. What Drona said was completely valid. Karna failed to wield Brahmastra because he lacked Brahmana or Kshatriya strength.

You said — Lakshagriha conspiracy

What you find illogical doesn’t matter. What matters is a reference —

  • Now look here it was already planned to kill Purochana
    • Exactly. They planned to kill Purochana. They ensured Purochana is killed.
    • But there is no mention they planned to kill Nishadas.
    • They didn’t ensure that Nishada’s are killed.

You said — but if you are trying to say me that they don't know that someone is sleeping in their palace and they don't know about it then it's totally illogical.

Read you own quote —

Then he set fire to the mansion in several parts all around. Then when the sons of Pandu were satisfied that the house had caught fire in several parts

  • Tell me what did Pandava’s were satisfied of? Is it that Nishada’s are dead? Or House caught the fire?
  • He set fire on several parts. Not every where. They didn’t live in a 2BHK apartment.
  • So me one reference that explicitly proves Pandava’s were aware of the presense of Nishada family in the house.

You are just making baseless assumptions. You have NO proof. No reference.

For detailed answer and followup question check out my answer here — Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Pandavas end up killing a Nishada woman and her five sons while escaping the House of Lac. How were you able to reconcile this fact?

You said — First of all Karna attacked Arjuna who was disguised as Brahman. And Karna has never ever attacked Draupad so how can you say he intended to kill her.

You missed the whole point. Karna and the Kings attacked Drupad and Draupadi. They got into the protective custody of Arjuna. Arjuna and Bhimasena interfered to protect Drupad. Tell me, you really don’t understand English or trying to make a fool of yourself to defend Karna? Please seek the help of some elder who can read the para to you and explain what it really means. Or read the Gita Press hindi edition and you will understand.

I know you are not as naive as you want to prove yourself. My suggestion don’t worship and evil so bad that you have to prove yourself naive to support him. It is not worth it.

Story is simple — Kings try to attack Drupad. Drupad seeks protection from Pandavas. They protect him. period.

You said — Can you provide me the crosss reference of this scene. And one more question why would Shalya attack Arjuna and Bhimsena even after they were assumed to be recognized.

Yes, Duhasasana says that had they known the true identity of Pandavas they may not have lost.

Arjuan and Bhimasena were not assumed to be recognized. Not till then. And they did attack him. There is no controversy there. It is not an interpolation.

But what is your question here? Do you mean to say Drauadi swaymvar is an interpolation? Only Karna fans think so. It is present in all authentic reference.

You said — You haven't even given a proper reference that Karna was involved in Lakshgriha and on opposite to it you are asking me for proof. Hypocrisy at its peak.

I agree hypocrisy at its peak. But whose?

I gave you two explicit reference from BORI. And you say it is not a proper reference. What is the definition of a proper reference? Krishna’s statement is a direct cross-reference of Vaisampanya’s statement from Adi Parva.

And you find my references improper. Do you have a even an improper reference to prove —

  • Draupadi insulted Karna?
  • Karna was not involved in Lakshagrih conspiracy?

I have two reference. Do you have a single reference that he was not involved. BE EXPLICIT. GIVE A REFERENCE. I HAVE GIVEN YOU TWO.

YOU SAID — And one more thing all those references provided by you are generalizing about Karna it is there opinion what they think. And they don't know about the real situation.

Who don’t know the real situation? Vaisampanyadas or Krishna? And if these two guys don’t know the real situation then who does? You? I can’t call this hypocrisy. It is absurdity at it’s peak!

Might I remind you Krishna knew eveything— past, present and future. I gave you the reference.

You say they did’t know the real situation. Can you prove that by a reference. Do I need to take your words against exact reference?

You said — Lol, I don't have to prove the already proved statement it's you, who have to give proof that Karna was there in that conspiracy. And you failed to do so.

How did you unilateral reach this conclusion? Why you don’t have to prove that Karna was part of conspiracy when it is explicitly mentioned in the epic? And how did I fail after giving two references and you won this without giving any?

Do you want to say BORI is fake? KMG is fake? Because you don’t have to give an answer.

Tell me how come you don’t have to prove your absurd statement? You made this statement in contradiction to BORI references. IT IS YOUR MORAL OBLIGATION TO PROVE AND MORE SO BECAUSE I PROVED BY GIVING EXPLICIT REFERENCE

YES YOU CAN SAY WHAT I AREADY KNOW — THERE IS NO SUCH REFERNCE IN MAHABHARATA THAT CAN EXONARATE KARNA FROM HIS CRIME.

CONCLUSION — BORI CLEARLY INCLUDES KARNA’S INVOLVEMENT IN LAKSHAGRIH AND OTHER CONSPIRACY WITH AT LEAS TWO EXPLICIT REFERENCE. YOU FAILED TO PROVIDE A SINGLE CONTRADICTION.

You said - By repeating baseless facts without reference won’t make them correct.— Same applies to you.

NO IT DOESN’T. I ALWAYS PROVIDE REFERENCES.

YOU SAID — then what kind of Caiptain he is who can't satisfy his team.

He was the kind of captain, that no one dared to think a match without. He was a captain for which the opposing player was let go without even a second though by anyone including Duryodhana. It is like a out of form batsman after being critisized by captain refuses to play under him and no one bothers to ask him to stay put or reconsider his descision. Because everyone knows the captain in more capable that an boasting player. Even his friend lets him go without a second though.

YOU SAID— Karna deserved all those weapons that he achieved, it was his devotion that he achieved them. As said by Parshurama ji.

It was before Parshuramji knew about his deceit. You know a person may win olympic gold medal under influence of performance enhancing drug. But once the truth is out his stripped off the medal and remains a disgraced sportsman. You might find the story similar.

You said — Yudhishthira replied, “Arjuna always said that he would burn down the enemy in a single day.

This is what Yudhishira said. This is called heresay. Show me where Arjuna said this. If you recall, I have aready showed you what Arjuna said. And it was I could finish it by the grace of Krishna and Shiva but I will not do it.

When you know the truth, shy debate of heresay?

You said in response to - Please explain how I am distorting the facts? Explain which part is not explicitly mentioned in Mahabharata? — You are calling Karna as arrogant but do you know what arrogance means it means when a man think that he is never wrong and he has घमंड on it.

Is Karna called arrogant in the epic or not? Question is how am I distorting it? And for your information arrogance doesn’t mean a man things he is never wrong. Arrogance means someone who has greater sense of achievement than reality.

If you don’t know the meaning of a word, you may find me distorting.

So please explain how am I distorting.

You said -in response to - There are true friends who would bear every humiliation but will not desert their friends or benefactors. — Same that's what I am saying Karna was that type of friend.

That is the problem. Karna did desert Duryodhana. And not once. Karna was not that kind of friend.

You said —If Karna spares life of four Pandavas then he became traitor but if that same man helps in killing Abhimanyu he became cruel (Hypocrisy at its peak) Just think before what you say Vivek.

I NEVER called cruel for killing Abhimanyu. NOT ONCE. Abhimanyu was an enemy combatant and it was Karna’s duty to kill him. YES I DID CALL HIM COWARD THAT HE WAS. He did lack the strength to fight Abhimanyu. And Krishna said the same thing.

Read my last response again. I raised the issue of Abhimanyu to remind that Karna had no respect for relationship. If he could kill Abhimanyu he could kill others. He spared them for his arrogant and uncalled for peldge.

Do I make myself clear?

Did you just learn the phrase — hypocrisy at its peak?

Let me sum up — Killing Abhimanyu wasn’t cruel. It was cowardly. Not killing Pandavas and above all not telling Duryodhana his true intentin was an act of treason.

You said — Why would he tell ? Did Arjuna told Yudhishthira about his secret Vow of Gandiv ? NO, in the same way it was his secret Vow.

The two are different. Arjuna vow was his personal and doesn’t effect his friend or relative anyway adversely.

Karna’s vow directly impacted Duryodhana’s life and that of his brothers. Bhishma clearly told he won’t kill Pandavas. Did Karna say so. Do you think Duryodhana had no right to know the truth. On contrary he made Duryodhana belive that he would help him kill Pandavas.

You asked — Why would Karna try to Kill Pandavas, it was always Duryodhana.

Why are you defending an evil? People don’t always do something for selfish interest. Sometimes they love to be part of evil design. I am talking about Karna. You yourself said he was eager for such action.

And now you are out to defend Duryodhana? Please read the last word of your quote. Bhimasena had not malice. And read the next para which tells the true reason for Duryodhana “evil” action which was his “greed”

You said — And yes I didn't laughed at Arjuna I laughed at you

No you laughed at Arjuna not at me. Read the line again —

And the biggest irony is that Arjuna (classified as Mahrathi) was afraid Karna (Ardharathi) LOL

Yes you laughed at Arjuna. And if you laughed at me, then you laughed at Shiva, Krishna and Parshurama. All of them said the same thing. He was the greatest warrior of the era.

You said — I know it's Shri Krishna who knew everything as claimed by you. So I have question Parshuram ji is also Avatar of Vishnu ji then why can't he figure out that Karna was not Brahman.

Shri Krishna knew everything, as claimed by Krishna himself. Remember I quoted it to your from Gita.

You may not know but Parshuramaji had given up his divinity during the Ramayana era itself. He was no more a divinity.

And it may be (assumption) he wanted to test if Karna ever reveals the truth. But either way it doesn’t change the fact.

What do you think, Krishna didn’t know everything? Did he lie in Gita?

You said — See what toxic language this rotten old man used for Karna

That rotten old man was more revered than a wicked devil. Everyone respected him. And what you find toxic was ultimate truth. Each word of it. Bhishma said when pitted against Arjuna, Karna wouldn’t survive? Did he? He said he was cursed by Parshurama and Brahmana. Was he not?

You may find it toxic. But that was the truth.

You said — Ohh really thanks to Shiva ji and Krishna that Arjuna was able to kill Jaydrath otherwise he would have to burn himself alive. It was Shiva who was killing army of Pandavas. Lol

Again, your hate speech and malicious LOL is directed towards Arjuna. Not me.

And yes, I always thank Shiva and Krishna fore ensuring evils and crushed. They are my guiding force. Like them, I never side with evil creatures. They kept Arjuna alive. They kept Dharma alive. They destroyed evils like Karna. Hail to Mahadeva, Hail to Krishna. And don’t laugh on the judgement of Shiva.

You said— Wonderful excuse by the Arjuna fan. What more to expect from you who can give any logic to defend defeat of Arjuna. LOL !! By this logic Karna didn’t killed Chitrasena because he didn't wanted a Carnage unlike Arjuna.

You understand NOTHING. Karna tried to fight Gandharvas. Couldn’t fight. Fled. Result? Kauravas were captured.

Now Arjuna doesn’t actually fight Bhargava. He ignores it. Result? Bhargava weapon fails. No one is captured. Karna dies at the end.

DO YOU SEE A DIFFERENCE? Gandharva threat was real and lead to defeat of Kaurava. Bhargava did nothing.

You said — And Arjuna also failed before to counter big astras such as Vaishnavastra, Sarp mukhi astra and Bhargavastra as well and now don't give excuse that he didn't want to destroy Earth. It's his track record of running away or getting helped by Krishna whenever he faces an unknown astra or powerful astra

Do you know the meaning of countering a weapon? You don’t destroy a bomb by firing gun at it. You render it useless by defusing it. Arjuna didn’t fail those big astras, they were diffused. Had Arjuna failed, Pandavas would have lost the war. Never happened.

There is no reference of Arjuna running away. That unparallel record belong to Karna. And karna alone. Can you quote a reference where epic says Arjuna fled?

And wait for my next comment to show you what Shiva said. And it deserves it’s own space.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Are-the-classifications-of-warriors-done-by-Bhishma-in-the-Ratha-Atiratha-Parva-appropriate/answer/Kushal-Tiwari-86
Creation time: Dec 07, 2021 04:22 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

PART 2

You asked — You are mentioning Shiva ji everytime. I would like to read that reference I am eagerly waiting to read what Shiv ji said to Arjuna. Can you please quote it for me.

Glad you asked. Here is what Shiva said —

O bull among men! Accept a boon from me. What is it that you desire? You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

  • You are the one who shows respect → contrast of arrogance
  • There is no one on earth who is your equal → definition of best
  • The Kshatriya find their chief in you.

But then Shiva again expressed his opinion about Arjuna on day 17th —

Ishana760 spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone.

Bow to Shiva! Har… Har… Mahadeva!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Are-the-classifications-of-warriors-done-by-Bhishma-in-the-Ratha-Atiratha-Parva-appropriate/answer/Kushal-Tiwari-86
Creation time: Dec 07, 2021 04:35 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Nothing to be sorry of. We are not debating on different sides. Alexandre lost in India. That is what I said. His army did try to march towards Magadha, but never reached there.

I refer to “historically incorrect” to the assertion that India never won against invaders.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-no-Indian-king-was-able-to-defeat-the-invaders/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 07, 2021 04:39 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Duryodhana’s armor was powerful. Karna’s armor too was powerful. Yet both lost their wars. Morale of the story — armors don’t win war. Their powers don’t matter. period.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Wearing-the-armour-presented-by-Drona-Duryodhana-could-not-stand-before-Arjuna-From-that-point-is-the-natural-kavach-and-kundal-of-Karna-over-hyped/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 10, 2021 08:32 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. None of the Pandavas ever insulted Karna accept Bhimasena whose statement was ruthlessly true.

By the way Krishna himself called Karna evil-souled, wicked and the trunk of the evil tree Duryodhana. But I guess telling the truth wasn’t an insult.

And yes, no matter what you or I say, it won’t change the fact — Karna was the trunk of an evil tree. Well documented. He was arrogant and abusive. Again well documented. Krishna wanted him dead. Well documented. He was an evil, wicked, deceitful who conspired in murder conspiracy, molestation and robbery. Well documented. He was sentenced to death at Krishna’s will.

Nothing you are I say would make a heinous villain great.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Karn-great-among-all-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 12, 2021 12:28 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I can assure you I have been reading the authentic epic for over three decades now, although I must admit I may not have read all the fictions written around it.

And going by the original epic, it is Karna who had been abusive to everyone.

You said — Karna was made one mistake that is participated in dute kreeda. He never supported the crime act of Duryodhana . always raised objection.

Your perception is based on TV Shows. Karna was involved in every evil conspiracy of Duryodhana from poisoning of Bhima to Lakshagriha conspiracy. And then there are several conspiracies spawned explicitly be Karna —

  • Disrobing of Draupadi
  • Goharana Parva
  • Ghosha yatra parva

You said — Vyasa never mentioned that he is evil minded.

You are again WRONG! Vyasa mentioned Karna as the trunk of the evil tree Duryodhana right in the first chapter of of Mahabharata. Krishna repeated the idea in Udyoga Parva.

Please refer to this answer to see how many times Krishna alone called him evil and wicked multiple times — Why did Krishna like Karna in the Mahabharata?

Here you will get detailed extracts from the original epic of Mahabharata.

You said — He could push 2 inch of rath where god godess and hanuman were residing.

Another folktake with no mention in authentic versions of Mahabharata.

You said — You may compare his birth hood life with Krishna.

Everyone is born the same way. What they do after the birth makes the whole difference. Krishna sided with Dharma, Karna with Adharma. Both were abandoned by their mothers. But Krishna never abandoned his mother.

You said — Krishna and Indra were worried about him. But not helped to save him.

Krishna himself reached out to Karna. Karna refused to help him. Karna rejected Krishna. And anyone who sides with evil shall be destroyed. That was the goal of Krishna’s birth.

You said — No worry if you call him evil minded.

Yes. doesn’t matter what you are I say. What matters is what Krishna, Vyas, Parshurama, Bhishma, Drona called him. They all called him evil minded. I shared references with you from the original epic.

NOW QUESTIONS FOR YOU

  • Prove that Krishna didn’t call Karna evil. (Reference in the link)
  • Provide authentic reference of where Karna pushed Krishna’s chariot back.
  • Provide authentic reference of where Arjuna, Nakula, Sahdeva, and Draupadi insulted Karna.

Your response will prove you knowledge about what is the authentic epic of Mahabharata.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Karn-great-among-all-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 13, 2021 02:56 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry, I missed your post. Let us break your question in two parts. First let me remove the lingering doubt —

If Arjun was as such powerful warrior?

Arjuna was a powerful warrior which was not only confirmed by Mahadeva, Krishna, Parshurama and Indra by explicit words, but also by his unparallelly track record, where he won battles after battles. So let us be clear on one point whatever your view point about 14th day war may be, it doesn’t undermine Arjuna’s credentials as the warrior.

Now let me turn to answer your second part.

Did Mahadeva fight on Arjuna’s behalf on day 14?

NO.

Mahadeva blessed Arjuna. Arjuna saw the aura of the supreme being and in that moment of enlightenment he realized what Krishna had shown him earlier — destiny is supreme, humans are merely hands of providence.

Had Mahadeva really fought in the battle, do you think it would have lasted the period it lasted? Do you think Mahadeva needed to escape Drona without fighting? Do you think he would have taken time to defeat Duryodhana? Do you think there would have been a fifteenth day? Do you think it would have taken that much time that would tire the horses? Do you think, Mahadeva needed to move at the speed of Arjuna?

It was Arjuna who struggled. It was Arjuna who risking his life and losing vital time, paused to give relief to his horses. Do you think such act of compassion goes unnoticed? Mahadeva always support those who are established in Dharma.

Remember God helps those who help themselves. Arjuna vowed to kill Jaidradha. But instead of resorting to fair fight Kauravas tried to use is oath against him. They had no honor. They hid Jaidradh behind his entire army. Such cowardly act never pleases God.

WHAT EXACTLY HAPPENED ON DAY 14?

Here is what Arjuna said —

I saw a man advancing in front of me. His complexion was like that of the fire. In whatever direction he advanced, with a blazing and upraised spear, in that direction, the enemy was seen to be shattered. His feet did not touch the earth and he did not hurl his spear. But because of his energy, thousands of spears were released from that spear. He is the one who routed the entire enemy, though people think I routed them. From the rear, I only pursued the soldiers whom he had scorched.

See the contradiction — He didn’t hurl his spear. But thousands of spears were realized. Now others could see neither Shiva nor his spears! So did those spear turned into arrows? And since when Shiva started using spears?

The moment someone realizes the presence of supreme being he also realizes the insignificance of his own existence.

—Vivek Dutta Mishra

The experience was humbling and enlightening for Arjuna. And despite of the fact that he himself destroyed his enemy he had given the attachment to his action just like Krishna guided him in Gita. He realized that he did nothing.

But Shiva himself didn’t take part in human fight. Here Ved Vyas clarifies —

‘“‘O Partha! This was the god Mahadeva who was stationed in front of you. You saw the wielder of Pinaka in the battle, destroying the enemy. In the battle, this was the illustrious god who advanced in front of you. He is the one who gave you the weapons with which you destroyed the danavas.

This section of Mahabharata is essentially a praise to the God of Gods and emphasis that by reaching the day 14 Arjuna had learnt — “act with attachment” and “dedicating the fruit of his action to the divine being.”

Krishna had given a lesson to Arjuna, Arjuna had now passed the test.

Hope you are satisified with the answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Karn-great-among-all-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 17, 2021 05:28 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

An interesting reimagination for a context that has more than sufficient explicit reference. Krishna himself explained why Ghatotkatch must die. But obviously you don't take Krishna's words and feel the need to reimagine politics.

But I must say on a few flawed premise -

1.Yudhishthir's claim to throne was not being eldest in the lineage

Dhritarashtra wasn't the true king of Hastinapur but a caretaker and being the son of Pandu Yudhisthir was the true King. This fact was explicitly mentioned by both Gandhari and Dhritarashtra to Duryodhana.

Yudhishthir was the most logical choice and people were eager to see him as the king. This too is explicitly a topic of discussion between Duryodhana and Dhritarashtra leading to Lakshagrih conspiracy.

2.Being eldest wasn't really a regid tradition of that era

King Shantanu wasn't eldest of his siblings. In fact he was the youngest. Yet he became the King.

Further if we consider this argument, the Bhisma may never had been the crown prince in the first place. He was the eldest son of the King, but not eldest among the cousins - that would be Somedutta the son of Bhalika, the elder brother of Shantanu.

Now Ghatotkatch never had the claim like Yudhisthir. He wasn't the son of King. He has a similar status as that of Somedutta, eldest son among the brother of the kings. And guess what, neither were explicitly considered for succession. Not once. Unlike Yudhisthir.

So why did Krishna wanted Ghatotkatch to die? Now we don't see Yudhisthir or Pandava desiring it. I believe you have partial answer hidden in the post.

But then, if we ignore epic reference, this is an interesting reimagination that reduces Krishna and Pandavas to modern day conniving power grabbing politicians.

Creation time: Dec 17, 2021 12:58 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dhritarashtra and Gandhari themselves clarified the fact. If Pandavas didn't it was simply their graceful conduct.
