Hi, I had clarified your doubts here — Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to Why was Karn great among all in the Mahabharata?

And here is a detailed article I wrote — Did Mahadeva fight instead of Arjuna on day#14

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Duryodhanas-upper-body-strong-as-Siva-and-his-lower-body-delicate-like-Parvathi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 19, 2021 03:29 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me tell you this — Don’t play the game of those stupid. The problem in arguing with stupid is they will bring you down to their level and then win by there experience (in stupidity).

Could Arjuna have won without Shiva’s blessing?

NO. Nothing can work without Shiva’s blessing. Not air, not animal, NOTHING. Arjuna won because Shiva willed him to win.

If Arjuna won with Shiva’s blessing where is Arjuna’s greatness?

Arjuna’s greatness lies in the fact that Shiva blessed him. There is nothing more powerful and worthy than God’s grace and the fact that God may come down on earth to protect you or complete your mission. It is much greater than being a great archer.

To Answer — That army was protected by great archers and strikers like Drona, KARNA and Kripa. O Partha! Who other than that great archer and God could think of countering it, even in the mind. “

This great army was completely vanquished by Arjuna during the Virata battle. A larger and more powerful army was vanquished by him during Niavatakavacha battle, Khandava Battle and Ghosha yatra battle.

Krishna explicitly said that this army is incompetent to fight against Arjuna. And the same thing was said by Shiva.

So why did Vyas said this? The fact is no one is anything without Shiva’s grace and Arjuna had it.

To Answer — THOSE FOES WHOM THOU HAST SLAIN WERE ALL, IN THE FIRST INSTANCE, SLAIN BY HIM.

Krishna said the same thing in Gita Chapter 11, Shloka 34. That they have already been killed. And yet he asks Arjuna to kill him.

Question is —

  • How can Arjuna kill someone already killed?
  • Did Krishna kill them (Gita 11.34) or Shiva killed them (Shanti Parva, KMG)?

This is a prove that these statements are symbolic and not real.

  • They willed them to die long before the battle started.
  • Arjuna acted as the hand of providence and therefore Krishna asked him to kill them.
  • Only Arjuna saw that figure and no one else. That was his detachment from the fruits of his actions.

To Answer — “ O Partha! This was the God Mahadeva who was stationed in front of you. “

c) “ O Kounteya! Advance towards the battle. You will not confront defeat. Janardana, your advisor and your PROTECTOR is by your side. “

These are answer in themselves. It clearly indicate that Shiva was the protected who was stationed with Arjuna. They didn’t fight the human battle.

Hope that clarifies your point. For further doubts, you may DM me.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Duryodhanas-upper-body-strong-as-Siva-and-his-lower-body-delicate-like-Parvathi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 19, 2021 03:40 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your question.

Drona didn’t impart just the ‘primary education’ to Karna.

He gave him all the knowledge except that of divine weapons. And Karna wasn’t an exception there. Most of warriors including Bhimasena and Pandava twins too were not given the knowledge of those celestial weapons.

Here is a direct reference to what Karna learnt with Drona —

There, he went to Drona to learn about weapons. The brave one became Duryodhana’s friend. Having obtained the four kinds of weapons from Drona, Kripa and Rama, he became famous in the world as a great archer.

—Kundala Harana Parva

Here Drona and Kripa is explicitly credited along with Rama for Karna becoming a great archer. This wouldn’t be the case had Drona given just the basic knowledge.

This proves too things —

  • Not imparting the knowledge of celestial weapon is not equivalent to imparting just primary knowledge.
  • Drona denial to Karna wasn’t because he was a Suta. Even Kshatriaya’s were denied.

WHO DESERVED THE DIVINE WEAPONS

We must understand divine weapons were quite potent and their misuse could lead to mass devastation. It was the responsibility of the guru to ensure that the disciple is deserving before imparting him such a weapon. Even today not everyone is allowed to carry a gun, and no one a nuclear weapon. And we are talking about something much greater.

Remember, Shiva thoroughly tested Arjuna before imparting him the knowledge of Pashupata. He ensured Arjuna would never misuse the weapon. And he never did. In fact Arjuna didn’t use most of the divine weapon he got.

Now, consider the reason, why Karna actually wanted the celestial weapon —

He saw that Dhananjaya was superior to everyone in learning about dhanurveda. Karna secretly went to Drona and spoke these words. ‘I wish to know about brahmastra and the secrets of releasing and withdrawing it. It is my view that I should become Arjuna’s equal in battle.

—Shanti Parva,

Karna wanted to use the weapon against Arjuna in a battle. There was no reason for such a battle, and yet he was dreaming of having a battle where he could vanquish Arjuna! Who would offer such a potent weapon to a war-monger?

Now Drona was partial towards his favorite student who never asked him for a favor and even saved his life. What would a guru do? Here is what Drona said —

Drona was partial towards Phalguna. He also knew about Karna’s wickedness. Having been thus addressed by Karna, he replied, ‘The brahmastra can only be known by a brahmana who is observant of the vows, or by a kshatriya who has performed austerities, and by no one else.’

Note:

  1. Drona knew about Karna’s wickeness.
  2. He laid down the qualification of who can possess the Brahmastra.

Before you consider that point #2 excludes Karna let me remind you two things —

  1. During Mahabharata era, there was no “caste” but “varna”. Drona laid down the qualities for being Brahmna and Kshatriya. Remember, not every Kshatriya was awarded the weapon.
  2. Who was a Suta?

Here is the definition of Suta mentioned by none other than Karna to Rama —

‘O Bhargava! Know me to be between a brahmana and a kshatriya, born as a suta.23 People on earth speak of me as Radheya

—Shanti Parva

Now consider the lineage of Pandu and Dhritrashtra. They too were not pure Kshatriya. There were born of Kshatirya mother and Brahmana father (Vyas). Arjuna’s grandfather was born of Kshatirya father and a fisherwoman for a mother. So technically Arjuna too wasn’t a pure Kshatriya. So tell me, where is the question of rejecting on the basis of caste or birth?

Karna was denied the weapon by Drona because he was wicked. Parshurama cursed him because he was deceitful. Do we need more proof?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 21, 2021 05:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There could never we a fair fight between a valiant and a coward. They are not in the same level in the first place. How would you kill someone who would flee the battle rather than fighting? Karna fled Draupadi’s swyamver battle. He fled Matasya battle. He fled Gandharvas. He fled Abhimanyu. He fled leaving Duryodhana behind. He fled leaving his brothers to be killed. He couldn’t save his son from getting killed in front of him.

He wanted to flee again. But his chariot was stuck in mud.

Now tell me how was he killed unfairly?

His chariot was stuck in mud. But NOT his weapons. They were still free. So why didn’t Karna fight? Don’t warriors fight without chariot? Didn’t Arjuna fought without chariot on day #14? Did Abhimanyu asked for a break despite of the fact that he had no weapon and no chariot? Did many warriors attacked Karna together? How was it an unfair fight?

Don’t watch TV Shows. Read authentic version. Karna died fighting. He attacked Arjuna with Brahmastra and Varunastra.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Without-divine-weapons-who-will-win-between-Arjun-and-Karn/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 21, 2021 05:48 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — The Karna who went for guru Dakshina was another Karna (Gandhari's son). There were two of the Kaurava brother whose name was Karna.

I am aware of the fact that Dhritrashtra’s sons too were known as Karna. But then none of those sons are referred as a great archer anywhere in the epic. Mahabharata clearly mentioned only four chief sons of Dhritrashtra and Karna was not among them. Yet you say out of no where he is mentioned in such and important battle.

You and I both know the truth and coverup. So I would let it pass. Then we both agree on the third option here —

3.Karna didn’t fight the battle and like a coward, selfish person ditched him right after he made him the King.

Now don’t tell only princess had gone to battle or Drona had denied Karna. An army had gone and Karna after being crowed, like a ungrateful coward refused to fight.

If I were a fan of Karna, this would be the worst choice for me. But if you say so…

You said — text clearly states that they were fighting mildly thus proves that arjuna was already under the mercy of kind hearted karna.

Text certainly clearly says, only few people don’t clear read it. Let me help you—

Myth about mild fight

Then the mighty Vaikartana Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant. Shalya, king of the Madras, rushed at Bhimasena. Duryodhana and the others rushed at the Brahmanas, but they fought with them lightly and carelessly.

NOTE: Karna didn’t fight mildly, others did. because they fought unarmed, non-warrior Brahma.

How did Karna fight?

Then those two invincible warriors fought violently, each eager to vanquish the other. Such was the speed that they became invisible.

So let us clarify there is no doubt, there is no mild battle here. They fought to vanquish each other.

What feat of Karna did warrior applaud?

Seeing the valour in Arjuna’s arms, unparalleled on earth, Vaikartana Karna fought even more vigorously. Repulsing Arjuna’s swift arrows, Karna shouted out aloud and the warriors applauded his feat

Karna merely repulsed Arjuna’s arrow. He couldn’t hit him. He couldn’t make him circumvent. He repulsed the arrow. Looks like King had no more hope from this warrior.

But Karna’s statement proves what he thought about his opponent —

O supreme among Brahmanas! Are you Rama,33 the personification of the knowledge of weapons? 34 Or are you Harihaya35 himself? Or are you Achyuta Vishnu himself? Have you assumed the form of a Brahmana to disguise yourself and are now fighting strongly with me for self-preservation, mustering the strength of your arms? When I am angry in the field of battle, no one except Shachi’s husband36 and Pandava Kiriti37 can withstand me.”

I again don’t think Karna to be so devilish that he would insult his own guru by comparing him to a losing warrior. And you know what Karna said about Arjuna here. He knows Arjuna could withstand even his anger.

Who won the war?

Arjuna asks Karna to keep fighting —

Wait for a bit. I stand here today, to vanquish you in battle.” At these words, Radheya Karna withdrew from the battle, because the maharatha thought that Brahmana strength was invincible.

It is Karna who withdrew from the battle because he thought his opponent is invincible.

Did Karna retreat because he was kind hearted? A kind hearted person wouldn’t try to burn down a bride. Would he? But we should see more explicit reference —

When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened and surrounded Vrikodara.

WHAT WAS THE WAR ABOUT

Evil people were trying to throw a bride in fire and kill their family. The war was between evil killers and their protecting heroes. What was the conclusion? Were evil successful? Or were protectors successful?

You said — Prince Dussasan too confessed…

I would stop you right there. Dusssasan made a statement. I don’t consider the statement of a evil person as proof of Dharma. Had Dussasana known the true identity of Arjuna, he would have fled much earlier. Haven’t we seen them running away again and again. and yet again? When did any of them ever won against Arjuna?

Now avoid making baseless remark and making a ridicule of yourself. If you can’t read text properly, that is no my fault. So read properly and respond —

  • Why Dhirtrashtra son Karna has never been mentioned as a great archer or warrior?
  • What was the role of Dhirtrashtra’s son Karna during —
    • Gandharva Battle
    • Matasya Battle
    • Kurukshetra Battle
  • Where is written that Karna fought Arjuna mildly? Did you miss out —
    • other kings fought mildly
    • Karna fought violently to kill each other.
  • Is there any explicit reference that
    • Arjuna was hit by Karna
    • Arjuna fought with circumspection?
    • Arjuna was scared
    • Arjuna retreated

Kings applauding means nothing. If a Pakistani batsman will score a boundary on a losing match, Pakistani fans would still applaud for his attempt. That applaud is no proof of victory. But running away ‘scared’ is a sure proof of defeat. If you fail to score your goal, is the defeat.

Unless you can provide a direction reference we can consider this debate over. I wouldn’t have time for baseless argument that is not backed by a proper reference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Without-divine-weapons-who-will-win-between-Arjun-and-Karn/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 21, 2021 06:21 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ok Since you talk about BORI, Let us focus on the final battle rather than debating on what someone said in the past or the present —

When Vasudeva addressed Radheya in this way, Pandava Dhananjaya remembered all this and was overcome by great rage. Energetic flames of anger seemed to issue out from all the pores on his body and it was extraordinary. ‘ “On seeing this, Karna again invoked brahmastra against Dhananjaya. He showered down arrows and tried to extricate his chariot. Pandava countered those weapons with his own weapons. Kounteya then released another weapon, beloved of the fire god, towards Karna. It blazed fiercely. Karna pacified the fire through a varuna weapon. He covered all the directions with clouds and it was as dark as a rainy day. Pandaveya was not frightened. While Radheya looked on, the valiant one used the vayavya weapon and dispelled all the clouds. The supreme one’s standard had the marks of an elephant’s housing. It was decorated with gold, pearls, jewels and diamonds. It had been crafted by excellent artisans over a long period of time. It was expensive and beautiful in form. It always inspired your soldiers and terrified and frightened the enemy. It was renowned in the world and blazed like the sun and the moon. Kiriti used a razor-sharp arrow that was gold-tufted and pointed. With that, he brought down the handsome and blazing standard of the great-souled maharatha, Adhiratha’s son. O venerable one! When that standard was uprooted, fame, dharma, victory and everything that was dear to the hearts of the Kurus also fell down. Great sounds of lamentation arose. ‘ “To ensure Karna’s death, Pandava took out an anjalika arrow. It was like the great Indra’s vajra, or like a rod that was made out of fire. It blazed in its rays, like the one with one thousand rays.800 It was capable of penetrating the inner organs and smearing itself with blood and flesh. It was extremely expensive and was like the fire and the sun. It could destroy men, horses and elephants and was three cubits long, with six tufts.801 It travelled straight and possessed a great force. In its energy, it was like the vajra of the one with one thousand eyes. It was as difficult to withstand as predatory beasts. It was like the pinaka and Narayana’s chakra. It was fearful and destructive of living beings. He invoked mantras and affixed that supreme and great weapon to Gandiva. He loudly said, ‘I am grasping this great weapon, which is in the form of an arrow. It is extremely difficult to withstand and is capable of destroying the body. If I have tormented myself through austerities and have satisfied my seniors, if I have listened to what my well-wishers have told me, through that truth, let this arrow slay my armoured enemy, Karna, and bring me victory.’ Having said this, for the sake of Karna’s death, Dhananjaya released that terrible arrow. It was as fierce as rites performed by Atharvan and Angiras. It blazed and was impossible to be endured in a battle, even by Death itself. Kiriti cheerfully said, ‘Let this arrow bring me victory.’ Wishing to slay Karna, bring about his end and convey him to Yama, he released the arrow, which was as radiant as the sun and the moon. Cheerfully, so that he could be conveyed towards victory, the one with the diadem and the garland shot the arrow. It was as radiant as the sun and the moon He harboured feelings of enmity and wished to slay his enemy. That weapon, blazing like the sun, was shot. Like the sun, it lit up the earth with its radiance. The head of the commander of the army was severed.

Debroy, Bibek. Mahabharata: Volume 7 (Kindle Locations 4981-4983). Penguin Books Ltd. Kindle Edition.

NOTE:

  • Karna fought back with Brahmastra and Varunastra.
  • Arjuna invoked Dharma and put a condition that he would succeed only if he had performed austerities and followed truth.

This is the exact description from BORI where Karna died a fair death, or as fair as an evil creature like him deserved.

But I would respect your long research and assume

You said — no narration about position of karna by sanjaya

Does that mean we have to agree that Karna was not fighting? Whatever was his position, he did fire Varunastra and Brahmastra. Can you negate that? A little common sense would tell you a person not fighting can’t fire weapons. And since Karna was firing the weapon, no explicit mention of position was required.

You said — It proves that karna was extracting wheel and fighting at a time. His aim was releasing weapons and lifting wheel.

No the only thing it proves is —

  • Karna wasn’t weaponless.
  • Karna was fighting.

If he wanted to extract his wheel or smoke or sleep in addition to fighting it may be his strategy or foolishness, not inability to fight.

You said — KARNA WAS EXTRACTING WHEEL OF CHARIOT WHEN LORD SHIVA KILLED KARNA AIMED BY ARJUNA

Wow! That’s a new one. Lord Shiva killed Arjuna! No one called Arjuna, as Shiva before. You must be a great fan.

But let me humor you. Please tell me —

  • Why did Lord Shiva kill Karna? Does that prove Shiva evil or Karna?
  • Do you want to say that Shiva killed Karna by Adharma?

You said — After hearing golden words of Duryodhana, all gods adopted the side of Duryodhana. All of them approved the words of Duryodhana and accepted that Karna, Bhishma, Drona and Bhurishrava were killed through adharma.

Duryodhana’s was an evil. And there are numerous reference to that fact. Interestingly you find his words golden. Krishna incarnated with the moto — Paritranaya Sadhunama, Vinaschayata Dushkritam — To protect the goods and destroy the evils. Shiva himself willed that Pandavas would win. And yet you bestow all your trust on an obituary — a praise for a dying man?

Can you not apply your common sense? Wasn’t Duryodhana an evil? Did he ever followed Dharma?

You said — Lord Krishna revealed the truth and admitted that those four warriors (Karna, Bhishma, Drona and Bhurishrava) were incapable of being defeated in fair battle

How do you know Lord Krishna revealed the truth here and not before? Let me remind you, what Krishna said before —

This entire army of kings assembled by you, with Bhishma, Drona, Karna, Kripa, Somadatta’s son Bhurishrava, Ashvatthama, Jayadratha—all of them are incapable of withstanding Dhananjaya. An angry Arjuna is incapable of being vanquished by the gods, the asuras, men and the gandharvas. Do not get fixated on battle.

In fact this is exactly the same think Shiva and Parshurama too pronounced —

‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them.

So, the words are conflicting here. What about some action —

Arjuna defeated all those warriors alone in Matasya battle. Every single of them. He defeated Gandharvas and demons which was something no one else dreamt of and Karna positively ran away. They lost each and every duel against Arjuna.

Shiva, Krishna, Parshurma and even the evil Karna accepted Karna to be the greatest warrior.

So why did Krishna said so? Just an obituary — paying respect to the dead. In our culture we pray even for departed evils.

You said — DON'T FORGOT CURSE OF BRAHMANA

Why should I forget the curse. And evil man was cursed for his evil nature. You don’t expect a deceitful person to be blessed. Do you?

You said — KARNA'S WIFE SAID 'SHAME' TO ARJUNA FOR HIS COWARD ACT

Did you expect anything different? Even the family of the rapists of Nirbhaya blamed judiciary for hanging him. Which criminal’s family don’t blame heroes for killing them? I don’t see his wife feeling shame of Karna’s misdeeds.

It is natural, but proves NOTHING. She wasn’t an eyewitness to battle. She was given an information and she reacted.

By the way Gandhari to said such word shame of Karna, Drona and everyone for their wicked acts.

You said — Apart from them aswathama himself mentioned the unfair death of karna.

Are you talking about the same Ashwathama who burtally murdered sleeping people in the midst of the night? And you expect any honorable person to consider his words worthy?

By the way same Ashwathama before he had gone insane and pure evil, had reprimand Karna again and again reminding him he could never compete Arjuna.

You said — Gandhari herself mentioned the unfair death of karna while giving curse to lord.

Gandhari also mentioned Karna misdeeds again and again. She was a fair lady, but not an eye witness to the battle. What she said was heresay.

You said — Arjuna in the fear of Gandhari's wrath hide behind lord krishna, however Nothing expected from that coward 😅😅😅😅..so be it

You claim to know BORI. Can you quote a single explicit reference where Arjuna has been called a coward?

Gandhari and Dhritrashtra couldn’t control their son and blamed Pandavas for their death. They were unfair. Dhritrashtra tried to kill Bhimasena and Gandhari cursed Krishna. Bhimasena could have crushed Dhritrasthra and Arjuna could have beheaded Gandhari before she could have uttered her curse. But Pandavas were compassinate. They respected even the wicked King who always conspired against them with his son.

Sometimes you take a shield because you can’t counter attack. It is not an act of coward but the greatest bravery. You know Indian army officials during election who had to take cover from stone pelters despite having guns in their hand. It was a similar situation.

YOU WON’T UNDERSTAND. YOU FOLLOW THE EVILS LIKE DURYODHANA AND KARNA. YOU IGNORE SHIVA’S AND KRISHNA’S WORDS. THERE IS NOTHING TO DEBATE THERE.

  • Krishna, Ashwatthama, Gandhari, Bhishma, Drona, Parshurama, Ashwathama everyone called Karna wicked, evil, deceitful on multiple occasion. Karna himself knew he is the one of the cause of destruction. He has been explicitly referred as coward, both by words and action. And he is your hero.
  • Duryodhana was an evil, wicked person and his words are golden words for you.
  • Shiva blessed Arjuna. He called him the greatest warrior of the era. Krishna called Arjuna his inseparable existence. He proclaimed he who hates Arjuna hates me. And you hate Arjuna. You hate Krishna and Shiva.

We have nothing in common to discuss.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Without-divine-weapons-who-will-win-between-Arjun-and-Karn/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 06:28 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Long response. But you didn’t respond to a single question I asked. So let me answer them for you.

  • Why Dhirtrashtra son Karna has never been mentioned as a great archer or warrior? NO WHERE
  • What was the role of Dhirtrashtra’s son Karna during —
    • Gandharva Battle NO
    • Matasya Battle NO
    • Kurukshetra Battle — Only as badly beaten by Bhimsena. Does this show him as a great archer?
  • Is there any explicit reference that
    • Arjuna was hit by Karna NO
    • Arjuna fought with circumspection? NO
    • Arjuna was scared NO
    • Arjuna retreated NO

You said — Moreover there is not even a single mention of karna defeat or failure in Drona, bhisma and shalya parva be it kripa or others statements

Oh there is a mention in Matasya Parva that proves who lost this battle. Here is Ashwathama asking Karna a question he had no answer to —

In which duel in battle have you vanquished Dhananjaya, Nakula or Sahadeva? Whose riches have you robbed? Has Yudhishthira, or Bhima, supreme among strong ones, ever been defeated by you? And in which battle did you win over Krishna?

And then again Bhishma and Drona talks —

The mighty-armed Drona, supreme among the wielders of weapons, then spoke. ‘It is exactly as you have said and there is no falsehood in this at all. In battle after battle, I have seen him retreat. Karna is generous. But he is also distracted. It is my view that he is half a ratha.’

So one thing is clear, Karna had no upper hand in the war as you want to believe. Now let us talk about your quotes —

You reference — Have you assumed the form of a Brahmana to disguise yourself and are now fighting strongly with me for self-preservation, mustering the strength of your arms?

Did you miss the full statement here — O supreme among Brahmanas! Are you Rama,33 the personification of the knowledge of weapons? 34 Or are you Harihaya35 himself? Or are you Achyuta Vishnu himself? Have you assumed the form of a Brahmana to disguise yourself and are now fighting strongly with me for self-preservation, mustering the strength of your arms? When I am angry in the field of battle, no one except Shachi’s husband36 and Pandava Kiriti37 can withstand me.”

Read carefully. Karna calls him —

  • Supreme among Brahmanas
  • Personification of knowledge of weapons
  • Rama
  • Vishnu
  • Indra

Who compares a weak opponent to the most superlative term. Also note, he knows Arjuna can counter him even when he is angry.

You said — According to biase vaisampayana, karna withdrew from fight because he thought that strength of brahmana is invincible.

Why am I not surprised at ‘biase Vaisampanya’? Simply because I have heard this word verbatim before. Can you guess from whom? Well from other Karna fan. Who else?

Now can please tell me, if Vaisampanya was biased, what is you unbiased source of information? Can you dare to answer this question?

This is how people like you distort our great epic.

You said — Few facts about that battle All kshtriya warriors already decided not to kill brahmanas (arjuna and bhima) thus both of them were already spared by kshtriyas refer.

The problem with you is, you don’t read authentic epic, just quora. Only if you had read a few lines more you won’t be raising this point. It was their past resolve which changed very quickly —

Eager to fight, those kings then said, “It is permissible to kill a Brahmana who is willing to fight.” Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant.

Note only after Kings decided that it is permissible to kill a Brhamana only “Then” might Vaikartana Karna rushed to Arjuna eager to fight. And Arjuna never said he is not willing to fight. Did he?

You said — Liar arjuna caught !!!

Arjuna didn’t lie. You missed out. Brahmana wasn’t a caste but a Varna. And practicing Brahmana’s austraties, Arjuna was indeed a Brahmana. Please Read Gita.

He couldn’t have divulged his his identity without his brother’s permission especially in front of the conspirators who had tried to kill them by burning. But his identity provided him NO PROTECTION. Kings had decided it is permissible to Kill Brahmana. And once again you quoted only half. Let me give you the full picture —

“O Karna! I am not the science of weapons personified. Nor am I the powerful Rama. I am only a Brahmana, chief among warriors and supreme among those who have the knowledge of weapons. Through my preceptor’s grace, I have become skilled in the use of brahma and paurandara39 weapons. O warrior! Wait for a bit. I stand here today, to vanquish you in battle.” At these words, Radheya Karna withdrew from the battle, because the maharatha thought that Brahmana strength was invincible.

Karna withdrew from the battle because he thought he couldn’t defeat the Brahmana strength. The thought that the opponent is invincible is the very definition of surrender.

You said — Karna just withdraw from fight as the word ‘withdraw’ denotes act of pause where warrior is not defeated. Karna was invincible after destroying arjuna in battle

I agree with you. ‘withdraw’ in itself doesn’t indicate defeat. We must understand the reason of withdraw. Did you check it out? Let me help you. There are two explicit reference of this why —

Karna withdrew from the battle, because the maharatha thought that Brahmana strength was invincible.

So you are mistaken. He didn’t withdraw because Arjuna was destroyed. He withdrew while Arjuna was challenging him to keep fighting and he though his opponent is invincible.

And there is another proof that I will provide in answer to your next question.

You said — Few facts about that battle All kshtriya warriors already decided not to kill brahmanas (arjuna and bhima) thus both of them were already spared by kshtriyas refer to

Once again you are misguided by half reference into believing Kshatriya decided not to kill Brahmana. They had already decided to kill them. Here is the true reason for Karna’s withdrawal and Kings retreat —

When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened and surrounded Vrikodara. They said, “These bulls among Brahmanas are supreme. Let us find out in what lineage they were born and where they live. Who can fight with Radheya Karna in battle except Rama, Drona, Sharadvata Kripa,41 Devaki’s son Krishna and Phalguni, the scorcher of enemies? Who can fight against Duryodhana and who can vanquish in battle the powerful Shalya, king of Madra and chief among warriors, except the brave Baladeva and Pandava Vrikodara? Therefore, let us desist from this fight against the Brahmanas. Let us find out who they are and then we will happily fight with them again.”

You said — Even In Rangbhoomi karna when Karna surpassed arjuna, Yudhishthira thought no one is equal to him in archery and compared him with Rama even though Karna was not disguised as a ordinary brahmin,

Yes, Yudhisthir had a undue respect for Karna’s valour and it continued till the very end despite of seeing his repeated failures. But then you must excuse Yudhshira because he lacked Kshatirya strength of a warrior. He wasn’t the right judge.

So why not talk about the right judges and recognized warriors, what they thought about their comparative merits? Do you know what these warriors said about their comparative strengths —

  • Shiva
  • Krishna
  • Parshurama
  • Bhishma
  • Drona
  • Kripa
  • Ashwathama
  • Karna (suprised!)

You said — we can say he defeated Arjuna…

Sorry we can’t. Because Karna retreated scared and having failed to achieve his target. You remember your favorite Dussasana’s golden word you mentioned earlier — O king! If Dhananjaya had not been disguised as a Brahmana, he would never have succeeded in obtaining Droupadi. No one recognized him because of that

Read who succeed. Who failed.

You said — Karna fought mildly and arjuna used his full strength still karna easily gained upper hand.

WRONG.

I know all of this may be a suprise to you because you are a victim of ignorance who is brainwashed into believing that Vaisampanya was a biased person and there is a more authentic source of Mahabharata. Let me enlighten you —

‘Seeing Vaikartana Karna rush towards him, the wise Arjuna stretched his mighty bow and pierced him with three arrows. The impact of these sharp arrows stunned Radheya31 and he approached with greater circumspection. Then those two invincible warriors fought violently, each eager to vanquish the other. Such was the speed that they became invisible.32 “Look at the strength in my arms”, “See how I countered that”, they taunted each other in words that only warriors understand. Seeing the valour in Arjuna’s arms, unparalleled on earth, Vaikartana Karna fought even more vigorously. Repulsing Arjuna’s swift arrows, Karna shouted out aloud and the warriors applauded his feat.

  1. Karna was pierced by the arrow and was stunned
  2. Karna moved with circumspection.
  3. Both fought violently. Karna wasn’t fighting mildly.
  4. He retreated while Arjuna asked him to stay and fight.
  5. He was scared.
  6. He compared Arjuna to Rama, Vishnu and Indra.
  7. And the best he did to deserve a praise was repulse Arjuna’s arrow.
  8. Arjuna wasn’t hit.
  9. He didn’t retreat.
  10. He is not mentioned scared but of unparalleled valour
  11. Karna couldn’t answer Ashwatthama’s question if he ever defeated Arjuna, because it never happened.

Now there are explicit proof of Karna getting scared and retreating. You don’t want to believe thinking Vaisampanya was a biased narrator. You have zero direct reference that Arjuna lost and want to make everyone believe that you are not biased? Wow.

Next time if you expect and answer first answer my question with proper references

  • Is there any explicit reference that contradicts Karna wasn’t scared.
  • Is there any explicit reference that Arjuna retreated?
  • Who failed in his mission — Karna’s was to throw Draupadi in fire, Arjuna’s to foil Karna’s attempt. Who won?

If you fail to reply my answer with explicit reference and not useless illogic, I would be forced to delete those comments as SPAMS.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Without-divine-weapons-who-will-win-between-Arjun-and-Karn/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 10:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Bhagwat Puran isn’t a valid reference for Mahabharata.

Draupadi was NEVER disrobed. Attempt was made. It failed. A failed attempt is still as heinous as successful and deserves same punishment's. The attempt is part of BORI CE and ever ancient text with tons of cross-references.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 10:57 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don’t like to tone of your question — give me all relevant evidences and not just your opinion.

Do you really want to know something? How is it relevant if any suta got education of celestial weapon or not? The knowledge of such weapon wasn’t for everyone.

Important thing is Sutas were not descriminated or looked down. Keechak was a Suta and he was the commander of Matasya. Sanjay was a suta and was the ambassador of Hastinapur. Vidura was the prime minister and he wasn’t even a Suta. And these are not my views but facts.

Krishna knew about celestial weapon. He was neither Kshatriya nor Brahmana. Arjuna wasn’t a pure Kshatriya as his grand father was a brahman and great grand mother was a fisher woman. They all got the best education. Is this my opinion?

Is this good enough for you?

Please improve your tone before seeking next response.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 11:03 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sometimes a outword answer may not be sufficiently accurate to understand the whole picture. Here is the exact verse —

Ekalavya was the son of Hiranyadhanu, the king of the nishadas. He came to Drona. However, since he was the son of a nishada, 346 Drona, who was learned in dharma, thought about it and refused to accept him as a student of archery, out of consideration for the others.

Since you asked me to limit to critical edition I would stop here, leaving you to ponder, why did he really refused a nishada when he didn’t refuse a suta.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 11:07 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

THIS IS A COMPLETELY IRRELEVANT DISCUSSION. THE DISCUSSION IS TO COMPARE ARJUNA AND KARNA NOT DRONA.

Yet, I will reply —

You said — Guru Drona being preceptor of arjuna called his favourite disciple coward for running away from battle like cowardSo unable to withstand Drona in battle, turning his back mighty arjuna fled away from battlefield.

You don’t really understand the epic or pretend not to? First let me remind you Arjuna defeated same Drona again and again. And Drona knew the valor of Arjuna and he dreaded it so much that he didn’t form Chakaravyuh in his presence. He knew he could never defeat Arjuna. Only thing he could do was to hope Arjuna fights him wastes his day and kills himself. He knew Arjuna will never kill him and he could hold on to him.

Krishna knew the deciet of Kaurava’s commander and he left. Drona yelled from the back trying to insult Arjuna hoping he would return back to waste his day with him. It was similar attempt as the one made earlier using Samsaptakas.

You said — Yudhistira himself told arjuna that it would have been better if he would have been aborted in fifth month of pregnancy of kunti.

Yes Yudhisthira isn’t the best judge of characters. So I would recommend see what Krishna, Shiva, Parshurama, Drona and Bhishma says.

By the way do you know what Krishna said — Arjuna is an inseparable existence of Krishna. He who hates Arjuna hates Krishna.

I hope you understand your position.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 22, 2021 11:15 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You should ask question in the right thread. I have answered a few questions related to Rangabhoomi. You should search them and you will find your answer. But here is a brief answer to each of your question.

You asked — Why karna was not allowed to compete with arjuna in rangbhoomi

Because it was a “rangabhoomi” and not a battle field. Karna wanted a duel which was uncalled for. It was rude to gate-crash a ceremony and demand a battle without any reason.

You asked — Kripa says arjuna is from lunar dynasty and asked karna about his lineage. Why question of lineage came to avoid duel. Duel should be allowed. Otherwise kripa should say u r not invited, u should not interfere.

There is no debate. Karna wasn’t invited. It was a private ceremony. Yet Dronacharya graciously allowed him. But then Karna didn’t care for the protocol and demanded for duel. Kripacharya asked him this question to remind him there are some protocol related to duel. A commoner can’t challenge a prince without any reason. There is a protocol violation in such cases. Kripacharya was learned in Dharma and did what was right —

Sharadvata Kripa, well versed in all aspects of dharma and skilled in the rules of duels, said, “This son of Pandu is Kunti’s youngest son. He is a Kuru and will fight a duel with you. O mighty-armed hero! You should also tell us your mother, father and lineage and the royal dynasty of which you are the ornament. On knowing this, Partha will fight with you. Or he may not fight. ”22 At these words, Karna’s face was flushed with shame.

Check out —

  • Krispacharya was well versed in aspect of Dharma and rules of duels.
  • He asked for royalty which was required for challenging a King or Kings son.
  • He doesn’t ask for caste.
  • Karna face flushed with shame? Why? Suta weren’t lower caste. But he had violated the protocol and had no answer.

You asked — If karna got education previously from dron. Then why its written that on seeing adhiratha bhim deduced karna is son of charioteer.

There is no but. Karna was taught by Drona and it has multiple cross references.

Now why Bhima couldn’t recognize him earlier is not specifically mentioned in the epic, but there can be few simple reasons for it —

  1. Karna had gone for many years. In young age, a person’s face changes more rapidly.
  2. Bhima was sitting in the gallery and may not see Karna clearly. Have you ever gone to watch a cricket match in stadium? Could you recognize the faces of the player playing in the field?

I hope you got your answer.

You asked — What's the matter of calling Karna dog.

Bhimasena didn’t call Karna a dog. He called him worst of man!

He used a metaphor. Here is what he said exactly —

O worst of men! You have no right to enjoy the kingdom of Anga, just as a dog has no right to eat the cake that is offered at a sacrificial fire.”

Note:

  • Here Dog is a metaphor for “unworthy”. He said just like Dog is unworthy of cake offered to sacrificial fire, Karna has no right to enjoy Kingdom of Anga.

Now the question is why did Bhima said so? Because Bhimasena recognized Karna as soon as he saw his father. He recalled the numerous conspiracy where Karna along with Duryodhana tried to kill him. Now he could see that Karna’s soul goal is to fight his younger brother. Why do you think, this shouldn’t anger Bhima?

Now, if you have any further question, check out my answers and ask in the right thread.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 23, 2021 04:52 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There is no other student of Parshurama mentioned in the Mahabharata. However as per Vishnu Purana Rukmi too was his disciple. But since we are talking about Mahabharata, there wasn’t any.

Since Parshurama taught Kshatirya Bhishma, it goes without saying that caste was no bar. There IS NO MENTION that Parshurama taught only Brahmana.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 23, 2021 07:02 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What is the source of so many doubts? Do you want to drive this discussion to some specific point?

Why do you think Parshuraam should have many students. He wasn’t running a commercial institute. He was leading a retired life making just a few exceptions. He had already given away all his weapons and worldly possession. So tell me why he should have many students?

You asked — And why Karna was required to lie. He could say I m suta give me education. What was actual need for karna to lie.

What is your view? The poor Karna was forced to lie by arrogant Parshurama?

Karna was looking for a shortcut. He approached Drona with same design in mind. He needed Brahmastra without working hard. He thought calling himself a Brahmana would make his life easy and people don’t expect Brahamana to be deceitful.

Now let me ask you two questions

  1. He could have called himself any Brahmana. Why did he explicitly mentioned himself a Bhargava Brahamna?
  2. If he taught only Brahmana’s then he must have several Brahamana students. Right? Can you name any Brahmana disciple of Parshurama? Any one? Drona wasn’t his disciple. He donated him his weapons and in the process taught him.

Now I have been patiently answering your questions for a long time. I think, I deserve to have a few answers.

Can you tell me your opinion about the whole episode? Where do you want to drive this discussion?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 24, 2021 03:23 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I can’t really force convince you. At the end of the day it is your choice.

Kripa didn’t stop Karna because he would have defeated Arjuna. That never happened. Not once. And Arjuna was better than Karna even in Drona’s gurukul before he was cursed.

That brings back to the original question — What would have been other reason?

Why is Kripa mentioned explicitly as knowledgeable of Dharma and skilled in the rules of duel?

Don’t you think it was imprudent of Karna to first gatecrash a private ceremony and then demand for a duel without any reason or enmity?

What would have been a better way to avoid an uncalled for conflict than to remind you that duel has certain protocol.

Can you provide convincing answer for such a question.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 24, 2021 03:36 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am a software technology enabler, a corporate trainer and the author of the best-seller The Accursed God.

I am not active on FB.

You can DM me on Quora if you like.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 24, 2021 03:44 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your appreciation.

You said — Basically caste system. Rejection by Drona of ekalavya and karna. Sound reasons.

There was NO Caste system. There was a varna system. There is no reference of caste anywhere in the epic.

What contradicts the sounds reasons is —

  • Suta was someone born of Kshatriya father and Brahman mother. This definition doesn’t make a ‘lower caste’.
  • Drona trained Karna. He was not the only one who was declined for higher weapons. Most students were. No caste biased again.
  • Karna was allowed to become the King of Anga was a proof enough that no reservation in terms of caste.
  • Eklavaya was a Nishada. A similar Nishada was a gurukul friend of Shri Rama. So can we call it a cast biased?
  • Vidur was the son of a maid servant. He was also the Prime Minister of Hastinapur.
  • Shalya, a Kshatirya, consented to become charioteer of Karna a suta. Does that reveal something of caste biase?
  • Arjuna too played the role of charioteer once. Why was it humiliating only for Karna?

You said — Where karna was offered anga desh. Which solidified their friendship.

Karna and Duryodhana were already friends for a long time before this incident. They were ‘thick and thieves’, nay, ‘think as co-conspirators’. What you see in Rangabhoomi was a formalization of their relation for public display.

While it is not mentioned explicitly in the epic, it is not difficult to see it was a well orchestrated event —

  1. How did Karna gain an entry to the private ceremony in the first place? The epic explicitly mentions he had visited Duryodhana soon after he was expelled by Parshurama.
  2. How Duryodhana got the right to make Karna or anyone a King, when he himself wasn’t one? He wasn’t even a crown prince. We don’t see any other prince having access to a whole kingdom.
  3. Why no one asked Duryodhana about his right to make Karna a King? Why was no one surprised? Why no one objected?

By the way, you didn’t answer my query. What is your convincing point of view of why Kripacharya asked Karna’s lineage and why he was wrong to stop Karna from duel?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 25, 2021 05:31 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You are right. People don’t want to see the truth.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-credible-is-the-book-Why-I-Assassinated-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 27, 2021 08:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Unfortunately your thoughts are incoherent and misdirected. Read carefully. We are not talking about congress. Are we? Was Mahatma Gandhi involved in the death of Chitpawan Brahmins? Was there killing the last will of Mahatma Gandhi? Do you see me defending killing? My friend, I condemn killing. And I condemn Godsey.

And why do you think Godsey was not most directly responsible for the outrage that was directed towards them? Had someone hired him to kill Gandhiji? You are talking as if victims of 26/11 were responsible for Kasab’s death.

And who said death of displacement of over two million Indians didn’t matter? Is there a single statement in my answer that even hints at that? But then was it Mahatma Gandhi who had first proposed the two nation theory? Don’t you know who were really responsible for our partition? Don’t you know who were really responsible for the death of those millions of Indians? If you don’t know the answer to these question, you should be seeking it — search who first proposed the two nation theory. If you know the answer and still debating then…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-was-more-patriot-Mahatma-Gandhi-or-Nathuram-Godse/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 28, 2021 09:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — the way you call this feeling for someone with a life such as Karna’s?

I can’t really understand what you mean by this sentence. What feeling? What do you mean my ‘with a life such as Karna’s’? Would you care to clarify in a simple and understandable English?

You asked — I may assume safely that you do believe that Parshuram is God.

Parshurama had given up his divinity and divine power in Treta Yuga after his encounter with Shri Rama. During Mahabharata times he was a great rishi, warrior, and human, but one who had renounced his divinity. But that doesn’t mean he lacked experience or may not have guess Karna’s dirty secret.

You asked — Do you really believe that Parshuram had bad intentions for Karna at all or at least in the scope of Eternity?

NO. Parshurama had no bad intentions for Karna. Karna had bad intention and disrespect for his guru. That is why the wicked disciple tried to cheat on his merciful guru. He accepted his crime later in Udyoga Parva.

You said — Do you really think that a human, no matter how exalted s/he may be, can fool God, even from the beginning? Don’t you realize that the most fundamental trait of God is that He is merciful beyond our understanding?

NO. I don’t think you can fool a God. And God is limitless mercy. But then let me ask you this — Why did merciful God killed Ravana? And why did the merciful God killed Kansa? Did they forget being merciful? Why did Parshurama destroyed Kshatiryas twenty-one times over?

Think. And you would realize Ravana, Kanasa and Karna were not the only people who deserved their mercy. The society as a whole deserve mercy. And the greatest act of mercy God can bestow is to ensure that justice prevails. They also must set a morale precedence for the society to follow.

As I see, Parshurama must have seen through the deceit of Karna. And yet in his mercy he gave him opportunity to come clean. Confess his crime. But Karna didn’t do it. He continued to live in his own deceit thinking — Ha! I succeeded in cheating Bhagwan!

Now tell me what kind of precedence would it set if a deceitful wicked is forgiven. What kind of society would it create? Who would devote their life to hard word, dishonest people are to punished? Do you see God’s mercy?

Think an examiner sees a person cheating. If he exposes him he would be expelled and punished. If he doesn’t expose and punish him, he may top the class and continue same dishonesty in his life and may destroy many lives. How do you think a dishonest wicked student behave once he become a professional? Do you expect him to be honest?

And won’t that be injustice to hard working, honest and dedicated students? What should the examiner do? punish or not? What would be a merciful act?

Yes, when I visit a temple or even think about God, I reach upon the same conclusion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parshuram-readily-taught-Bhishma-a-kshatriya-but-cursed-Karna-for-being-a-Kshatriya-even-though-he-did-not-know-he-was-one/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 28, 2021 10:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The most important part — There is no official record of this so called statement of Clement Attlee. This is a classical example of heresy. Some one said, that some one else said this…

There is no official endorsement from any authority in Britain including the PMs office or official archives. There is no written or audio proof of such a statement.

Content:

You are right. India is a nation. And few loafer can’t decide who should be called Mahatma and who shouldn’t be.

Neither can any loafer decide what Gandhiji’s plan of action should have been.

He never said India was his Jagir. I never said that. And yes, if my grand children trust me to act on their behalf, I would do what is best for them. I won’t wait for a loafer to tell me something.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Nathuram-Godse-assassinate-Jinnah-who-was-the-actual-person-responsible-for-creation-of-Pak-and-instead-killed-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 05, 2022 08:41 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Should you even bother to read it? Do we have no better source to learn Indian history of the era that I would go and read what a killer had to say to glorify his crime? Do you know Duryodhana called Krishna an adharmi? Do you know few people are out to justify Ravana kidnapping Sita?

You didn’t answer my question — would you read a book titled “Why I raped…”

But yes, I have read several summary of that killer’s blabbering to convince me that no sane person should read him rather than reading what great minds like Rajendra Prasad and Bose thought.

Answer Comment

Content:

I wish you read the history. And by history, I mean history and not social media posts.

You will find Ambedkar had no reverence for Gandhiji.

Gandhiji was ready to allow Jinnah be the prime minister to avoid partition. But Nehru and Patel were not ready.

In fact Patel was one of the first person ready to move on with the idea of partition and Gandhi devoid of any political post was completely ignored during the partition. Patel and Jinnah were debating on the modals of partition and not Gandhi.

Do you know who first proposed the two nation theory? Let me give you a hint. He was not Gandhi.

You said — This india belongs to me as much as of Gandhi , Patel or Nehru. Beware.

So why didn’t you prevent the partition? Do you expect that they should have waited for your to born so that you can avert the partition? India belongs to you today. 1947’s India belonged to them. Not to you or I. Beaware!

Now Question for you —

  • How was Gandhiji responsible for partition?
    • Did he propose the partition of India?
    • Did he accepted the proposal on the day #1?
    • Did he propose two nation theory?
    • Was he involved in Direct Action Day of 1946? Do you know anything about it?
    • So tell me how was he responsible?
  • How Khilaphat movement showed seeds of hate?
    • Was there a Hindu Muslim Harmony before Khilaphat movement?
    • Was there any hindu-muslim riot before that era?
    • Have you read Anandmath by Bamkimchand Chattopadhyaya?
  • You said Jinnah could have prevented Partition. Can you explain how? Who was actually asking for partition?

Can you provide to the point answer to these question?

This incident also proves that Khandava dahan was part of destiny for a time that predates Arjuna’s birth. He just fulfilled divine destiny. This confirms Arjuna’s act as heroic as mentioned again and again in the epic but skip few people so blinded against our epic and the epic heroes that they name this incident as crime.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-could-only-a-few-like-Arjuna-Karna-Sita-Rama-and-Parshuram-lift-Lord-Shiva-s-bow-dhanush/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 06, 2022 06:15 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There are two parts of your quesiton. Let me take them one by one.

  1. Why Surya provided Karna with such an armor

Because Kunti demanded for it as a precondition to enter into relation with him. She was concerned about her child’s safety and she knew she can’t hold on to him without incurring humilation or curse on her parents. She tried her best to request Surya to pardon her mistake but Surya threatened her with dire consequences. Surya too didn’t want to return back without coupling as he feared he would be mocked. Kunti’s demand seemed a reasonable bargain.

2. Even Krishna couldn’t defeat him in that armor.

WRONG. Krishna said so as a figure of speech. He offered an hypothesis and not some fact. And the glaring proof of this is Karna repeated defeat while he still had divine armor on him —

  • He was defeated and he fled scared after Draupadi swaymvar battle. He had his Kavacha. Arjuna still pierced him and stunned him.
  • He was defeated by Gandharvas and he fled leaving behind his best friend.
  • He was defeated by Bhimasena during Rajsuy yagna.

On all these occasion Karna’s armor was with him.

So why did Krishna said so?

We must understand Karna’s earrings were made up of amrita and he was virtually indestructible. He couldn’t die. So he should theoretically be undefeatable.

But then there is other side of the story. armors or earrings were no weapon. They couldn’t win a battle. They can’t attack enemy. They can’t make a coward brave. They can’t stop them from getting afraid. They can’t prevent them from fleeing.

Karna may have been undefeated had he possessed courage to match is armor. But he had a tendency to flee. Again and again.

In short, the armor was undefeatable, not Karna. Krishna also said Karna without armor is as good as dead, an extinguished flame and a defanged serpent.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Wearing-the-armour-presented-by-Drona-Duryodhana-could-not-stand-before-Arjuna-From-that-point-is-the-natural-kavach-and-kundal-of-Karna-over-hyped/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 06, 2022 11:17 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Before you make conclusions you must consider few things into account —

  1. All divine weapons were not same. The vajra weapon is not Indra’s vajra. And is not necessarily a divine weapon of high order.
  2. Weapons have code of use. You must not fire on weak opponents and must fire only when you are out of options. The case you quoted, Arjuna’s life was in peril as clearly mentioned. He didn’t fire against a single opponent. He didn’t fire it carelessly. In case of Karna he wasn’t facing a similar situation. He didn’t fire Bhargavastra to to protect someone rather to kill. And that is a BIG Difference.
  3. Misuse of divine weapon attracted penalty. It was mentioned by Drona and Shiva to Arjuna and by Indra to Karna. If you misuse it will have adverse affect. When Karna killed Ghatotkatcha, he met the condition. He had exhausted all his weapons and was left with no option. The weapon worked for him. Similarly Arjuna’s initiation was successful with no adverse consequence for Arjuna. But for Karna, Krishna mentioned he would get exhausted and the weapon failed to meet it’s goal. This is a the difference.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Were-Pandav-warriors-helpless-in-front-of-Karna-in-the-Mahabharata-War-at-any-time/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 12:22 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

I have read enough to know that you are not quoting from chapter 1108(131) as you claim, but from chapter 1003(26), and there were 10000 samsaptakas there and not 3000.

Now once you checked the reference back and get assured that my understanding is not lacking, please go through the 3 points I mentioned above.

In case of Arjuna-Samsaptakas war, the opponent were aggressive and sworn oath to kill Arjuna. And note 10000 sworn warriors can’t be called “weaker” specially when they managed to baffle even Janaradana.

And we still don’t see Arjuna using his highest weapon. Again check the point 3 above, misuse of weapons always attracted penalty. Do we see such a thing in this case? Do we see any sword band of warrior attacking Karna, that he was forced to use a weapon as powerful as Bhargavastra? Arjuna’s initiations annihilated the entire opponent because he was righteous. Can you say the same thing for Karna’s initiation? No. It exhausted Karna and Bhargastra initiation couldn’t kill even one warrior of repute.

If Arjuna had to misuse the weapons, the war would have ended even before starting. But he had decided not to misuse weapon. And when warrior with such a code of honor initiates a weapon, even God sides with him.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Were-Pandav-warriors-helpless-in-front-of-Karna-in-the-Mahabharata-War-at-any-time/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 02:26 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Soham,

You said — all arjuna weapons can be countred by kuru expert pashupat and if he fires it the whole earth will be destroyed.

You are mistaken here. Pashupata wouldn’t necessarily destroy the whole earth. And I can understand the reason of your confusion. You missed the most vital part of Shiva’s statement. Let me highlight it for you —

But it must not be suddenly released at any man. If it is released at someone who lacks in energy, it will destroy the entire universe. There is no one in the three worlds, mobile or immobile, who cannot be killed by it.

What does suddenly mean? Without giving due thought on the validity of the purpose. Pashupata wouldn’t necessarily destroy the whole world. It would happen if he released him on someone who lacked energy (for example Karna). But if you initiate it against a powerful weapon such as Brahmasira or Bhargavastra that didn’t lack energy, it wouldn’t destroy the whole world. And it wouldn’t be against a man but against another weapon. Hope you can understand the context.

You said — Bhargav astra didn’t killed any powerful warrior because it was not fired on them.

Exactly my point. It wasn’t fired against Arjuna. So there was not question of Arjuna losing this battle. But it did made Arjuna helpless on seeing unnecessary misuse of divine weapon against ordinary soldiers who were not overwhelming Karna and he was in no real danger. And that is why Krishna clarified, Karna would be exhausted.

ABOUT TRIGARTH AND SAMSAPTAKAS

Before we start we must understand the difference between — Trigartha and Samshaptakas. It is similar to Indian Police Force and NSG Commandos — both are part of Indian security forces, but both are not same. One is a specialized branch of combatant other with normal training. And government deployed NSG to take on 26/11 for a reason.

Now Karna fought normal Trigarth soldiers. Karna never faced Samsaptakas. Samsaptaka was an special elite troop which was like a sucidal squad formed with sworn oath to kill Arjuna. And I would also point out while Samsaptakas were primarily Trigarth they included elite troops from other kingdoms also, most notably Krishna’s narayani Sena.

Karna must thank his stars, he was never so great a warrior or so serious a threat that anyone would care to form a Samsaptakas army against him. Such an honor is reserved only for the greatest warrior. So please don’t compare Karna and Arjuna’s war.

As far as the number of soldiers attacking Arjuna is concerned, I accept I was wrong. They were not just 10000 but 14000. Here is the extract from chapter 1003(26) you wanted me to read —

As he was going there, fourteen thousand maharatha samshaptakas summoned him from the back. O lord of men! Ten thousand of those were Trigartas and another four thousand were followers of Vasudeva.90 O venerable one! On seeing that Bhagadatta was shattering the army and on also being challenged, he91 was caught in two minds and thought, ‘What is the best course of action for me? Should I return here, or should I go to Yudhishthira?’ O extender of the Kuru lineage! Thus did Arjuna reflect in his mind and decided that he should kill the samshaptakas. The one with the foremost of monkeys on his banner suddenly returned. Vasava’s son wished to single-handedly kill thousands of rathas in battle. This is what Duryodhana and Karna had also plotted, when the two of them had thought about means of killing Arjuna. That is the reason they had arranged for this divided feeling in Pandava’s mind. But he foiled them by deciding to take on those foremost of rathas on his chariot. ‘“O king! The maharatha samshaptakas shot hundreds and thousands of arrows with drooping tufts towards Arjuna. O king! Enveloped by those arrows, Partha, Kunti’s son, Krishna Janardana, the horses and the chariot could not be seen. Janardana was deprived on his senses and began to sweat. At this, Partha used the vajra weapon and killed most of them.

Can you count them Now?

As I explained earlier, and repeatedly, that initiation of divyastra against a mighty opponent is justified. And the opponents had proved their might, just justifying Arjuna’s use of force. It didn’t backfire. It didn’t exhaust Arjuna. It killed the opponent as intended.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Were-Pandav-warriors-helpless-in-front-of-Karna-in-the-Mahabharata-War-at-any-time/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 03:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

How many samshaptakas?

I can see your confusion. It is related to dates. Let me try to simplify for you.

1. Formation of samshaptakas

Samshaptakas were part of Duryodhana and Karna strategy to kill Arjuna. It was a sucide task force consisting of combatants not only from Trigarth and Narayani army but from several other nations. The took their formal oath in Drona Abhishek Parva (Chapter 933). At this point there were over 30000 (thirty-thousand) soldiers

We will do everything that your heart desires and brings you pleasure and also brings us fame. We will draw him away from the field of battle and kill him. Let the earth be without Arjuna today, or without the Trigartas. We swear this before you and this pledge will not be falsified.’ O descendant of the Bharata lineage! O great king! Satyaratha, Satyadharma, Satyavarma, Satyeshu and Satyakarma—these five brothers arrived,3 with ten thousand chariots, and spoke in this way. They took the pledge in that battle. The Malavas and the Tundirekas came with thirty thousand chariots. Susharma of Trigarta, tiger among men and the lord of Prasthala,4 came with the Machellakas, Lalitthas and Madrakas, with ten thousand chariots and his brothers and took the oath. There were another ten thousand from many different countries. They arrived specially, for purposes of taking the oath.

They attacked Arjuna several times each time leading to their destrcution

2. Shamshaptaka Vadha Parva (Day 12 chapter 1003)

This was one of the most massive attack on Arjuna. It comprised of fourteen thousand soldiers (10000 from Trigarth + 4000 from Narayni forces). I have already given reference in the previous answer. so please refer it from there where the number is clearly mentioned. Most of them were killed at this point

3. Abhimanyu Vadha Parva (Day 13 Chapter 1009)

Despite of the fact that thousands of Samshaptakas lost their life on day 12, Drona couldn’t capture Yudhishthira. So the remaining Samshaptakas renewed their mission and attacked Arjuna again on day 13. This is mentioned in Chapter 1009. While Kaurvas had a great victory of killing Abhimanyu, Arjuna had slaughter even more Samshaptakas that had followed him that day. Now there number had drastically reduced.

3. Jaidradh Vadha Parva (Day 14 — Chapter 1076)

It is after Abhimanyu’s death and the deaths of those Samshaptakas who tried to engage Arjuna on the previous days. Now their number had reduced. Among them three thousand (3000) again tried to attack Arjuna. Their number diminished over days and this is mentioned clearly.

Hope now thinks are clear to you.

TRIGARTHA VS SAMSHAPTAKAS

You asked — Sir what do you think when Karna was defeating Tigartas at that time samshaptakas were resting no they were initially trigartas warrior but later after taking oath became samshaptakas?

Do you think any country or kingdom sends their entire army to the war? Trigarth was no big kingdom. They dared not attack Matasya alone despite of the death of Keechak and that is why they sought Duryodhana’s help.

After humiliating defeat in Matasya, Trigarth along with other friendly kingdom created as new elite suicide task force. They were Samshaptakas.

Now your question were Samshaptakas resting when Karna attack? Well there were no samshaptakas. There were soldiers sure. But do you know the biggest difference between ordinary army and a suicide squad — it is the motivation. And don’t discount this motivation. When you are afraid of your life or you know the war is not worth risking your life you accept defeat more easily. But when you have sworn an oath you are a different person.

Let me give you an example. Few school going kids like Kasab brought our mighty nation almost to his knee making our entire police force ineffective and holding nation to ransom for over a day. Does that mean all terrorists are more powerful than us? Same terrorist nation was forced to surrender into a humiliating defeat in 1971 with their army chief himself surrendering. What is the lesson here? What made people like Kasab more potent? They were not afraid of die.

And they have had a better training between the time they had battle with Karna and when finally they collided with Arjuna on Kurukshetra.

You said — Karna fired Bhargavastra there was no side effect on him

Oh But Krishna said he would be exhausted. And he was. Why else Bharagavastra didn’t win the battle? Naraynastra too failed because that was fired irrisponsibly.

You said — They were trashed by arjuna many times but at that time in order to kill them fast Arjuna used Vajra astra!

Arjuna knew the worth of divyastra. He never fired them unless it was absolutly needed. Now that we have established the timeline and the fact that there were overwhelming number of combatant directly attacking Arjuna with intent to destroy him, his use of divyastra in his self defense is completely justified and was a success.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Were-Pandav-warriors-helpless-in-front-of-Karna-in-the-Mahabharata-War-at-any-time/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 07:25 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You answer carries such an irrational blind hatred that knows no bound. Just see what you are writing —

You said — …so Godse was killed

Godse was killed? Who killed him? Do you know the concept of justice? He was hanged as a criminal that he was. Our justice system doesn’t allow murders to roam free. Do you want to call India killed an innocent man?

You said — Moreover, the title ‘Mahatma' was actually given to Jyotiba Phule.

Is “Mahatma” a peace of land whose ownership rests with a single person? Can there be only a single Mahatma? Who gave Jyotiba Phule the title of Mahatma? Was it government of India? Was it British government? Some temple priest?

People with great souls are revered as Mahatma and few hate campaigns are not going to change that. Jyotiba Phule was Mahatma, and so was Mahatma Gandhi. And you would be surprised to know Gandhi’s contribution is acknowledged world wide and doesn’t depend on your endorsement.

You said — If Godse would remain alive, then Gandhi's fake mahatmahood would be dead

In your opinion Godsey was some one much bigger than Dr Rajendra Prasad, Subhash Chandra Bose and Ravindra Nath Tagore?

Do you know who Godsey was? A failed person who had zero contribution towards our great nation. And whatever you think you know about him is wrong. He didn’t kill Gandhji because of partition. He had earlier attempted to kill him in 1944 long before the issue of partition was raised.

You said — Godse had said about his book where he gave 150 reasons for killing Gandhi

Well guess what no one is interested in reading a book by a murderer. Even the worst murders and criminals find a way to justify themselves.

Would you read a book — 150 reasons I raped a girl? Should we lament why the rapists were hanged because if they had lived the mask of modesty from rape victims would be dead?

Thank God, majority people in the world are sane enough, not to read such craps. But fringe group exists everywhere.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Monami-Banerjee-15
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 07:50 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

When we talk about an epic of the size of Mahabharata, cherry picking is all one can hope in the best case. But some instead of choosing cherry (heros), end up picking muds (evils).

First things first. Parshurama never cursed Karna. He reprimand him and predicted his future. He did wish well for Karna. But just like he never cursed him, he never blessed him. What he actually did was to banish him.

Let me prove it to you. Parshurama said — “No Kshatriya will be thy equal in battle”. Was that a blessing? Was that a prediction? Think. True no Kshatriya was his equal in battle. Arjuna and Abhimanyu were too superior for him. So superior that he saw them kill his brothers and fled. He saw his son killed in front of his eyes and could do nothing. He saw Arjuna too fight without chariot, but couldn’t reciprocate. He saw Abhimanyu die without chariot but never begging for recesses and couldn’t emulate. Yes, there was no Kshatriya equal to Karna.

Parshurama indeed wished Karna despite of his criminal tendency (a word Karna himself used to describe his action). But did you forget what same Parshurama said in Bhagwat Yaan Parva? No one could stop Arjuna! Did you forget what Shiva said in Kairata Parva? And while you highlighted the text in above screenshot did you miss out the text that you didn’t highlight? Let me help you with it —

And above all did you forget every single battle fought between Karna and Arjuna? Why did Karna flee on day 14 on Ashwatthama’s chariot? Why did he accepted defeat on day 16?

Since Parshurama never cursed Karna (I don’t see where he did), there was no greatness in Karna initiating Brahmastra. But what Parshurama said can’t be taken lightly. Brahmastra initiated was so mild that it was easily countered.

But at least, we both can agree here Karna didn’t die an unfair death as he died fighting and initiating Brahmastra and dying by an ordinary arrow. The only thing unfair was he didn’t get to flee like always.

You said — Indra due to the fear of karna demanded for the boon from Lord Brahma, then see how karna was praised,this is the combined statement of lord shiva and Brahma.

I won’t engage on a baseless perception that has no reference in the epic. Anyone who can read the above text know who Shiva declared winner and who got the consolation prize.

You said — coming to Vijaya bow karna got it from lord prashuram and he got it from lord shiva himself..that bow of Indra was different vijaya bow which was given to rukmi by druma…it is not the same now in mahabharata there were 2 bows.

The only problem is, your assumption has no validation. Zero Reference. And you contradict even Karna.

Karna says that he has the Vijaya bow that Vishwakarma created for Indra. He says Indra (Sakara) gave it to Parshurama. But you say Shiva gave it to Parshurama? So who is saying the truth? You or Karna? I would say neither. Vaisampanya clearly mentions only three divine bows — Sharanga of Vishnu, Gandiva of Varuna and Vijaya of Indra. He also mentioned the owners. So there is no further discussion.

On the other hand if Parshurama had Vijay bow (for which there is no reference), he would have given it to Drona while donating all his weapons.

Let us move on, During Devyaan Parva, Parshurama refers to Gandiva as best of bows. Why didn’t he mention that Karna too had a equally powerful bow? Did Karna steal that bow?

It was Indra’s bow. Right? Didn’t Indra know, his bow belongs to Karna? When he came for his kavacha, why didn’t he ask for his bow also?

We see Krishna worried about armour and vasvi shakti. Once the two are gone he was delighted. He said consider Karna already dead. Did he not know about Vijaya bow?

The problem is you trust the word of a liar, who invented the lie right on that moment and foolish Duryodhana, like always, trusted him. There is no one in entire epic who seem to had any idea about Arjuna’s Vijaya bow.

You said — And coming Arjuna lifting shiva dhanush it's a joke after the departure of lord krishna when Arjuna faced Dwarka robbers Arjuna was unable to string gandiva properly and all weapons escaped from his poor memory.

You comment proves that you haven’t read the epic. Do you really think all weapon escaped from his ‘poor memory’? Is there really any point to discuss? I think you should read. Vyasa had given a very specific reason for it. And let me give you a hint — It is not Krishna’s departure. So go. Read. Learn. And then come back for a debate.

And before you mock, him whom Lord Krishna called his inseparable existence, give a second thought on the Dwarka robbers incident. You may finally realize why Krishna loved Arjuna so much. His Gandiva didn’t work. Neither did his Divastra. But without Gandiva and Divyastra Arjuna was still a warrior and not some coward. He continued to fight for those women who were NOT his responsibility.

Now compare this incident with Ghosha Yatra. There was another so called warrior. His weapons didn’t fail him. His divine armors didn’t fail him. He failed them when he ran away abandoning his friend and benefector and women of the family. Can you guess the name? Yes, I am talking about the same evil despicable person whom you are trying to defend. Now you may come up with some excuse for his cowardly act (drunkard etc.), but when you try doing it, do it with a valid reference not hypothesis.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-could-only-a-few-like-Arjuna-Karna-Sita-Rama-and-Parshuram-lift-Lord-Shiva-s-bow-dhanush/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 08, 2022 12:02 PM PST
Content language: English

Content:

You are mistaken, if you think people didn’t knew Netaji then or today. Netaji’s face exist even in the book of constitution. And were Netaji alive he would have advocated Gandhiji’s photo on the currency not his own. After was he, the one to call him the father of the nation?

But for you ‘Indians’ is a derogatory term and you are doing a good job trying to defame the country on the public forum.
