Answer Comment

Content:

Yes I am immence respect for Mahatma Gandhi. And we neither need to prove Godsey is a murderer nor the fact that Gandhi is Mahatma. Godsey paid the price for his crime. Justice prevailed.

Name of Mahatma Gandhi is respected world wide with world leaders of present and past including the likes of Dr Rajendra Prasad (The first president of India), Gurudev Ravindra Nath Thakur (you may have heard the name) and Netaji Shubhash Chandra Bose. And I have read the works of these great men I mentioned.

And I don’t need to defend Gandhi. You are too small and insignificant to hurt him. If you want to see the proof, check out this thread — since you couldn’t respond back to any of the question I raised, you brought more unrelated accusations.

So let me ask you this —

  • You claim Bose is your ideal, then how can you disrespect him?Was Shubhash Chandra Bose a fool or a flatterer that he called Mahatma Gandhi the father of the nation? Do you think you understand Mahatma Gandhi more than him?
  • Was Gurudev Ravindra Nath Thakur, too a fool to call Gandhi Mahatma and that too with so high regard that he celebrated his Mahatma’s birthday every year in Shantiniketan?
  • Dr Rajendra Prasad compared Gandhji with Bhagirathi Ganga.

Whom do you think, we Indian consider our role model — these heros? Or those worthless people who claim Gandhi to be a British agent and follow the ideals of a delusional killer?

Answer Comment

Content:

Is evident from your statement that you think so low about Shubhash Chandra Bose. You think he called a “Duratma” as the father of the nation? One may be magnanimous towards a person, but them bestowing him the greatest honor of the nation on someone, you need to rise above your person feeling. I firmly trust Netaji as a nationalist who kept nation first. I don’t see how he can do act which is almost like a treason to call a bad man as the father of the nation. But for you, his words don’t matter. Your own blind prejudice does. Naturally because he follow the books and saying of Godsey. Naturally you can’t understand Bose and simply calling him “Netaji” doesn’t show you respect him. Following his ideas and his words does.

No you started to slander. Why am I not surprised? Because I expected nothing better from you.

Creation time: Jan 09, 2022 05:48 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — How can I believe your words. Give the source.

What source are you seeking? Ask and it shall be given. Are you asking about the source of if Netaji called Gandhji the father of the nation? Please be specific.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Monami-Banerjee-15
Creation time: Jan 09, 2022 05:50 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,

Thanks for your encouraging words.

Yes the word “daanveer” doesn’t exist in the original epic. And it is tough to find the origin of a word one that can be established via critical edition.

But I would say some of the earlier works that included such term was Rashmirathi by Shri Ramdhari Singh Dinkar. To proof that Karna was a ‘daanveer’ it refers to two key stories —

  • Donation of his Kavacha: Betraying the essence of the epic the poetry glorifies Karna act as so ultimate a charity that puts him at part with Sivi and Dadhichi. It also mentions that Indra out of guilt of his deceit offers his spear to atone for his misdeeds. This is in line with a shift from Vedic to Puranic literature where Vedic Gods specially ‘Indra’ was degraded. In Mahbharata, however, ‘Indra’ is a almost a superlative adjective. The poetry doesn’t mention the fact that Karna asked for an exchange and refused to part away with his Kavacha unless his wish was granted. He himself uses the term ‘exchange’ twice and was content and pleased when Indra agreed to do the exchange. But if you read the work of Dinkar ji which also inspired the works of BR Chopra, Karna certainly appears as “danveer”. And I must not forget Mrityunjay by Shivaji Savant, which I didn’t have the opportunity to read yet.
  • Sparing the lives of Four Pandavas: This is second instance where Karna’s act appears heroic, although, in my understanding this was the ultimate act of ‘treason’ with no trace of loyalty or charity. Charity must be given from your own account and not from someone else’s. Karna’s act of sparing Bhimasena’s life was not from his own account but from that of his unsuspecting friend Duryodhana. He never told him that he wouldn’t kill Bhimasena. 31 Kauravas lost their life trying to protect him. And yet all people show is Karna remembered his oath even during the battle and degrade Kunti by portraying it was her wish that his son’s be spared.

Then there are more stories created to make Karna daanveer, the ones that don’t find mention in even Rasmirathi —

  • Karna donated his palatial doors and window to Krishna for yagna on a rainy day.
  • Karna donated his golden tooth on his death bed. Now it beats the reasoning as to how a headless Karna donated his tooth.

But these stories are created to overshadow the devilish nature of Karna. They don’t mention Krishna himself called him the trunk of the tree ‘evil’ Duryodhana.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-many-warriors-defeated-Radheye-and-How-many-warriors-Radheye-defeated-not-in-the-Kurukshetra-only-but-in-his-whole-life-span/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 09, 2022 06:45 AM PST

Content:

What kind of good man will call a Duratma — The father of the nation?

You are not just a Gandhi hater, but also keep Netaji is such a low esteem. You mistrust him. You call him a flatter and stupid to call a bad man with highest title of respect.

And yes, for me words of Netaji Shubhash Chandra Bose, Dr Rajendra Prasad, Gurudev Ravindra Nath Thakur are evidence. Because these people lived with Gandhiji, shared the challenges of the era, saw him in action.

Do read impartial history from the era to find out the evidence — Read book by Dr Rajendra Prasad. Read the book Freedom at midnight by impartial pair of author.

Now tell me what is your evidence? Your evidence too is based on works of some author. Right? Give me names whom you consider more credible than Shubhash Chandra Bose and Ravindra Nath Thakur.

You continue to slander me in person. I didn’t personally attacked you. But then, I don’t expect anything better than supporter of a heinous murderer.

Content:

Actually the burden of proof is NOT on me. The entire world knows it. Our great freedom fighters like Bose and Ravindra Nath Thakur and Dr Rajendra Prasad gives the testimonial to it. People like Albert Einstein, Barak Obama and Dalali Lama seeks inspiration from him.

Now what are your credible source on the basis of which “you have enough evidence”? 150 reasons by a criminal? Is that your evidence. Bring your evidence from a credible source, someone you think is more credible that the people I mentioned. My inspiration is Netaji, Bose and Dr Rajendra Prasad.

Remember when you refer a source, to override the words of these people, you automatically claim that you trust them more than these people.

So let me see your evidence.

Content:

You said — You don't believe my words but believe Gandhi, right?

ABSOLUTELY. WITHOUT DOUBT.

My experiments with the truth

Few questions —

  • Do you have absolutely any idea at what period the book ended?
  • Do you know what kind of moral courage it requires to tell uncomfortable truth. Only a Mahatma can.
  • Do you know the story of Balmiki?

You said — if you say that Gandhi was mahatma or father of nation

YES. He is the father of the nation. You can’t change that. And remember, Bose himself said that. And he knew Gandhi.

You said — then I must say you are also like Gandhi.

This is flattering. I wish I could reach his level. Sadly I am not Gandhi. But even the thought is flattering.

You may consider Netaji you father, you certainly don’t respect him or his views.

I don’t care of historians, who would make stories from the comfort of their study, over those who actually worked for the nation.

Using the correct spelling is not same as respecting someone. Respecting his views does.

Content:

That is your problem. You don’t understand even the meaning of historian. It is not a post that you should ask — who made him historian?

A first hand witness is always more reliable than second hand story teller. A contemporary is always more reliable than a later narrater.

And in your skewd attitude, you missed that I included Shubhash Chandra Bose among those reliable source. But for you it looks, RC Mazumdar and N.N Bhattachrya knew more than Netaji and Gurudev.

Had either of them ever walked with Gandhiji? Had either sat on the same table and shared views? Had they ever debated with him? No they just copied paragraph from his own writing and that of others and wrote their own story.

And if you think them more reliable than Bose and Gurudev, it is your choice.

And look around. You are part of that isolated infringe group who based on a criminal statement think your lie will prevail.

Keep day dreaming…

Throughout the discussion I maintained the decency of conversation never personally accusing you. But since you don’t have even basic ethic of polite conversation, and absolutely no respect for great leaders like Bose, I would no more waste my time.

If you don’t respect Bose’s view, where do anyone else stand.

End of conversastion.

You said — and yes, that father of nation tag will be removed from Gandhi very soon…….Indians will do it.

Content:

The important point is — BORI didn’t remove vastraharana. It didn’t remove Karna’s role in Vastraharana. It didn’t remove the attempt. And the whole incident is not isolated. It is well connected to every incident that happened later from that point of view.

The only thing BORI changed (NOT REMOVED) is the role of Krishna. I t changed Krishna to Dharma and if you retrospect the words are synonym — Krishna incarnated to establish Dharma. He was the Dharma of that era.

THERE IS ABSOLUTELY NO DOUBT THAT BORI RETAINED THE ENTIRE VASTRAHARANA. HAD THEY REMOVED IT, IT WOULD HAVE BEEN A QUESTION ON BORI’S CREDIBILITY RATHER THAN VASTRAHARANA AS IT FINDS A CONSISTENT MENTION IN ALL ANCIENT SCRIPTS.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Bori-remove-the-prayer-of-Draupadi-to-Krishna-during-Vastraharan/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 12, 2022 08:11 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

AGREE. It is not removed.

Creation time: Jan 12, 2022 08:11 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The incident appears in Kirata Parva. She was desperate and meeting Krishna for the first time after the incident. It is at this point Krishna promised that the wives of Duryodhana, Duhashashana, Karna and Shakuni shall weep for their sins. And this incident is a clear cross reference to vastraharana.

Content:

You said — First of all, there is no such proof that Netaji called Gandhi father of nation.

and

You said — He didn't know about Gandhi's bad deeds, that's why he respected him.

Thanks for confirming both my suppositions. You don’t know anything about Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose. You don’t know what he said. And you think he is a fool to call someone father of the nation without knowing his deeds.

You are just a blind fan of Godsey who was trying to portray herself as Netaji’s fan. Had you known anything about him you won’t have made either of the above statements. Subhash Chandra Bose called Mahatma Gandhi as father of the nation is a historic documentation as he called him this name on historic international address from Singapore radio on 6th July 1944. Wow! a really great fan of Netaji. Or actually a fan of Godsey in the mask of Netaji’s fan.

There is a complete documentary evidence of Netaji’s speech. At every fan of Netaji knows that. I mean real fans. Fakes like you may not.

You said — And most importantly let me tell you Rabindranath Tagore did never like Gandhi. So, just stop giving me knowledge and open your eyes if you can.

You know nothing about Gurudev Rabindranath Thakur (not Tagore). Not only did he like Gandhiji, he even celebrated his birthday at Shanti Niketan. Godsey Academy where syllabus teaches 150 reason why he killed Gandhi, didn’t they teach about these things?

You said — And I find you absolutely illogical, insignificant, uncultured to talk.

Wow! you talked without knowing. You abused me calling names and Duratma and I became uncultured. Sure that’s logical.

Answer Comment

Content:

Just for your information, neither did Gandhiji ever suggested that get raped for non-violence.

I think you are unaware of the views of Gandhiji about both rape and non-violence and is misled by social media propaganda.

Gandhiji clearly advocated violence over cowardice and suggested that women to do everything in her power to attack their molester.

Non violence doesn’t mean being coward. It means being strong. Stronger than violence. That is what Rama said. That is why Krishna moved from Mathura to Dwarka.

You will not found ‘Tiger’ or ‘Lion’ advocating non-violence. You will not find a rabbit following ‘non-violence’. Tiger can only be violent. Rabbits can only run away — Only humans can follow the idea of non-violence.

Creation time: Jan 14, 2022 09:35 AM PST

Content:

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Karn-great-among-all-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 14, 2022 09:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Actually most divine weapons including Brahmasira, were more like a knowledge. And once you have the knowledge you can impart that knowledge to others. That is how Drona was able to impart this knowledge to Arjuna and Bhagwan Parshurama to deceitful Karna.

Also Brahmasira, it appears, was generic name for higher level weapons. Even Pashupata has been referred as Brahmsira. As I see, Pashupata was generically classified as Brahmisra.

So Indra could have technically passed on this knowledge. But that doesn’t mean it would work for him.

On the other hand Indra’s Vasvi Shakti had no relationship with Karna’s character strength. It was based on Indra’s character divinity. It was sure to hit it’s target. That worried Krishna. Even this weapon too would have missed Arjuna as predicted by Indra himself, but that would have needed Arjuna initiating higher weapons.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 15, 2022 11:35 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna had the knowledge Brahmastra from Parshurama. But I don’t think that fool would have understood the difference anyway. He was not destined to will those weapons.

Knowing something doesn’t automatically mean that it would work for you. The weapons required the strength of the character to work for you. Ashwatthama was better than Karna. He managed to invoke Naryanastra. But he didn’t is in a irresponsible way and it didn’t work for him.

Parshurama had projected (not cursed) Karna that a deceitful person like him would never be able to manifest Brahmastra properly.

Initiating such weapons required a lot of concentration. But Karna lacked focus. It was easy for him to be distracted. He was lustful. He fled battles after battle.

Even in the last battle his focus was on the chariot wheel rather than fighting. With all the focus he could muster he managed to initiate a feeble Brahmastra which was easily countered.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 15, 2022 11:38 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

User-11712739973154721873 if you block me from responding, I would be forced to delete you comments.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 15, 2022 11:40 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Yes Karna did bash Yudhishthira who lacked valor of Kshatriya warrior. But is this relevant? All it proves Karna was a better fighter than Yudhisthira.

But does that mean other warriors weren’t ready to give up their lives? Wasn’t Abhimanyu willing to give up his life? Didn’t Shamshaptak warrior took oath to give up their life? So why would you give credit for this only to Karna? Any answer?

When Abhimanyu penetrated the Chakravyuha, everyone knew it was a deadly task. And yet it he did it. Did he ever fled his task? Did he ever asked his enemies to wait because he has lost his chariot and weapon? He was willing to give up his life.

When Arjuna swore that he would die if he failed to kill Jarasandha, he was baiting his life. And he too fought without chariot for a while. Did he ask his opponent to wait till he gets back on the chariot? That is what you call willing to sacrifice.

Karna fought a battle more for personal glory than anything else. He confessed the same thing to Duryodhana while explaining why he wouldn’t fight under Bhishma’s command. Tell me what kind of loyalty prohibited Karna from fighting for the first ten days?

You may say, he was insulted. But was his self respect more important than his friend for which he sacrificed the welfare of entire humanity?

Some ‘fake’ Krishna Devotee may say Karna refused the entire Kingdom. He did. But we must understand not every human being as same ambition. Not everyone needs Kingdom. Some needs wealth, some strive for recognition and fame and some work for Dharma.

Karna rejected the Kingdom because accepting the kingdom would mean he losing a chance to prove he is better than Arjuna. And that was his bigger ambition than being the King.

But same Karna who rejected the Kingdom also rejected fighting for his friend for which he rejected the Kingdom. How many other warriors took ten days off when they could have helped Yudhishthira? I don’t know anyone. Was this willing to die?

Forget die, did he ever tell Duryodhana that he wouldn’t kill his enemies? He spared the lives of enemy during a war because of a secret pack with enemy. Do you know what is the modern day word for it — treason.

By the way can you tell me why Karna didn’t capture Yudhisthira after vanquishing him?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 15, 2022 11:57 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — He gave up life for his friend

As I said most warrior give their life for a lot less. Giving life is part of the charter of a warrior. Shamshaptakas died. Bhurisrava died. Even Aswhasena died. That doesn’t make Karna unique.

What makes Karna unique is —

  • He promised to spare the life of the enemy when no one asked for it.
    • Why did he do that?
    • Was that not a treason to his friend?
    • Did he ever tell Duryodhana he wouldn’t kill Pandavas?
  • He abstained from the war for 10 days.
    • How was that being loyal to his friend?

You said — He didn't captured Yudhistra because it could have risked his life!

I know you have no proof or reference for your statement. So I will continue with your reference-less argument.

Can you please clarify, how? Actually he risked his life by attacking him. Any arrow could have killed him. He attacked Bhimasena also brutally.

Question is, why did he attack someone, he was never going to kill? To prove that he spared him? To fool Duryodhana that he is trying to kill them?

Yudhishthira was disarmed. There was no risk to his life at that moment. Had he captured Yudhishira the battle would have been over. Karna would have proved himself a good friend while still keeping his oath to his mother.

So let me ask you this again —

  1. Why did Karna risk Yudhishira’s life by attacking him, when he didn’t want to kill him?
  2. When Yudhishthira was actually captured, why didn’t he end the war? How was his act a proof of his friendship for Duryodhana?

(If you care to restrosepct, you will find the answer to few more questions —

  • Why was Arjuna an exception in his oath?
  • Why did he reject Krishna peace offer (If you think it’s for Duryodhana as Karna said, you are being cheated the same way as Duryodhana was).
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 16, 2022 07:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Arjuna failed to counter Karna’s brahmastra that is why it had its effect on his divya rath and Karna countered Arjuna’s brahmastra twice in the last Battle sir.

Krishna gives credit to Brahmastra initiated by not only Karna but also Bhishma and Drona. Why do you people miss that part? It is not Karna but Brahmastra that is praised. Divyastra would have that residual side effect. It is not a matter of failed to counter. Had he failed to counter, he would have died.

You must understand the symbolism here. Krishna and Arjuna must have got down of the chariot every day. Even after the 17th day. Why didn’t chariot burn then? Why did it wait till 18th day?

You said — Parshuram clearly cursed him that he will forgot brahmastra

Please read again. I don’t clearly see the word ‘curse’. What I see is Parshurama smiled and said. People don’t smile and curse. But such projection can be considered as curse. And once a word becomes popular people use it as such. Same goes with Narada.

I wanted to emphasize that point. But I don’t have problem in going ahead with curse as it doesn’t change the narrative.

You said — Karna concentration was very good that is why when cursed affected him then also he was able to use brahmastra twice this was his level of concentration!

Karna’s concentration was extremely weak. He lacked perseverance. And a clear proof is his inability to stay put and fight. He lost many battles where he could have won had he not ran away. He did manage to manifest Brahmastra but not powerful enough to be damaging. Once again, had he of stronger will power, he could have manifested.

And the fact that he manifested Brahmastra is an evidence of the fact that Parshurama didn’t curse him. Because had he cursed his concentration couldn’t have overpowered Bhagwan’s curse. People thought he was cursed whereas he just stated a fact which proved to true.

See there is a direct reference when Parshurama made his statement. He was smiling and he didn’t use the word curse. What we see later is a heresy. People add things over a period of time. Karna may have felt it to be a curse. Narada repeated his words. And when you feel you are cursed it weakens your resolve.

By the way you didn’t reply to my other questions which are mentioned below in this thread.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 17, 2022 09:24 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said - Karna concentration was very strong that is why he was able to invoke brahmastra in middle of fight with 17+ pandava warriors + seana. Parshuram smiling can have many meanings and it is irrelevant to proof it was not a curse and not even Narad but also sanjaya and Karna Confirmed it was a curse and it is said from genaration that Karna was cursed and in bori there are couple of times said that it was curse and sir we can not speculate what is real or what is fake we should go with what Mahabharata says.

First things first. I never challenges Mahabharara. Not in this question, nor anywhere else. What, I highlighted is based on these mistakes direct reference -

  • Parshuram didn't use the words curse. He may have been smiling first many reason, but can your imagine why a person would smile at that moment? Am I misquoting?
  • The important aspects of curse is even God’s aren't immune from it? Do you think Vasus had less power that they were bond by Vashishth's curse?
  • If a curse can be overcome with concentration then why value it? Why do we say Karna was cursed? Let's agree his curse can't be an excuse for his failure because he was powerfully enough to overpower Parshuram's curse, though not powerful enough to thwart an ordinary Brahman's curse. Is that right?
  • Can you tell why he couldn't overcome Brahman's curse with same concentration?
  • Sanjay relayed what he heard from Karna. And as I said, Parshuram's prophesy was no less than curse.

But if you want to insist invoking Brahmastra was a proof of his concentration, rethink -

  • Ashwathamas Brahmstra killed Parikshit. Krishna himself intervened to protect Pandavas against Naryanstra and Vaisnavastra. बट ही doesn't care about Karna's initiations, He doesn't bother to counter Bhargavastra. He says Karna without Vasvi shakti is good as dead, an extinguished flame and defanged serpant. Why was he noticed worried?
  • You quoted Karna initiated Brahmastra against 17+ Pandava warriors. How many died? Why was his Brahmastra so lack of energy?

You said- after that all pandava warrior lost and Yudhistra and karna one to one battle started and here Karna was able to invoke brahmastra easily because he was not fighting with someone equal!

Was that not putting Yudhisthir’s life at stake or did he know his Brahmastra are powerless? You didn't answer my earlier question- Why did Karna attack Yudhisthir if he didn't want to kill him? Was it to show off? And how capturing him would have risked his life when Brahmastra didn't?

You said - Karna was demi god he was a partial incarnation of parabrabhma Lord Surya

The Surya of Mahabharara is not referred as Parambrahma. You can check. And if you read Gita every atma is part of Parmatma. Buy when atma is born it comes in contact with flesh, desires and three gunas. It is those lesser characteristic that pollutes Atma and people are condemned to do evil act. Karna was no different. Why else that part of Surya languished in hell for so many years before Yudhishthira came to his rescue?

You said — you also can’t say that being evil makes you weak if then how Ravana and Indrajit astra were so damn Strong?

You assumed I can’t say. But actually evil makes you weak. And you need to compensate your deeds. Indrajeet and Ravana performed a lost of tapa in their life. They worshipped and renewed their power. Do you have any reference of Karna ever praying of doing any tapa other than bowing before Surya every evening? Arjuna got Pashupata at the end of along tapa.

You still didn’t reply my other questions which I raised in the thread below. I assume you don’t have answer for them and that is natural.

May I take this opportunity for a personal appeal?

You have been quite polite in your conversation. You are a nice man. Why do you support an evil man? You trust the words of Mahabharata. Right? Why then you support —

  • The trunk of the tree that was evil Duryodhana?
  • Someone who tries to throw an innocent bride to fire?
  • Someone who wants to disrobe a women in public?
  • Someone who relished at the torment of their enemy? Someone who felt more pleasure in seeing Druapadi's in tree bark than his own sons and prosperity?
  • Someone who was a wicked deceitful and evil?
  • Someone who cared for no one above himself — not his best friend not his brothers?

Let as say, I agree Karan had a great concentration, does that change the fact that he was an evil man.

I appealed to you because I see you have potential to see the light. Follow Krishna. Follow Dharma. Condemns evils like Ravana and Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 18, 2022 09:11 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

First things first. Karna had knowledge of Brahmastra not of Brahmsira. There is a difference.

Second you contradict yourself in a single sentence -

  • Karna had bharmashira and he can use it because he was a powerful warrior with powerful character
  • Sir try to understand use of brahmashira dosen’t depends on character its power

Can you please tell what you believe? You ignored all my questions in that regards

  • Can curse be overcome by strong concentration? If yes, why worry?
  • Why couldn't Karna overcome ordinary Brahman's curse? Why Vasus couldn't overcome Vashishth's curse?

Third you said Sir no where it is written that your character strength determine that you can use an astra or not.

Actually it is written. Multiple times. Parshurama said Brahmastra will note work for who doesn't possessions Brahmana characters. Drona said it is for Brahmna and Kshatriya who performer austrities. They all refer to character strength. Shiva and Drone said don't use the weapon against those who lack energy. So it depended not only on character of wielder but also that of the opponent.

The only theory that covers everything is - Karna wasn't under curse. Parshuram knew the inevitable. Karna lacked character strength and Brahmastra won't help him. People may think or call it curse, but if it were a curse there was no way karna could have overcomes it. As per Parshuram's prophesy Brahmastra didn't work for Karna.

Finally you said - he was a powerful warrior with powerful character

Tell me which person of strong character would try to molest a woman? Try to throw into fire? And flee a battle leaving his best friend and even brothers to die? How did you concluded an evil wicked characterless man was a person of strong character?

It seems, you ignored my appeal. I wish you luck. May Shiva shows you light some day.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 18, 2022 10:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — sir I am trying to say his character strength was powerful as a warrior

And I am trying trying to explain you it wasn’t. First things first, Karna was a fighter and a war-monger. He was not a warrior. Warriors have ethics, Karna had none. Warriors always try to avoid wars. Look at Shri Rama, Krishna, Arjuna. Karna favored war. All his life.

But if you are trying to tell me he was strong fighter, you are still mistaken. Karna ran away from battles after battle. Which strong warrior does that? He ran away deserting his best friend to get captured or die. Along with the women of the family. Which respectable warrior would do that? Even Duryodhana didn’t flee. The twin Pandava brothers didn’t have celestial weapons, or divine armors, yet they didn’t flee Gandharvas. But Karna did. He fled and left his brothers to be killed — one killed by Arjuna and other by Abhimanyu.

There is a difference physical strength and character strength. Anyone can appear strong when they are winning. True character strength shines how they behave in the face of defeat.

You said — Ashawathama was a brahman but was unable to call back its astra which clearly tells that his character power was not that strong.

NO. It is clearly mentioned, he couldn’t recall his Astra because he lacked the knowledge, not the strength of character. Ashwatthama had never been evil (like star plus show). until the death of his father. And his death changed him completely. He was seeking vengeance and his decline started. Steep decline. He did things which were outright demonic. But he wasn’t evil all his life. Unlike Karna.

You said — see he uses the word curse and after that this happened

Yes. I never denied Sanjay used this word. But Sanjay only relayed what he heard from Karna. But you see he also says — Because of Rama’s curse, the weapons no longer manifested themselves.

Tell me is this statement true? Were the weapons no longer manifested? Karna did manifest, Brahmastra, though not powerful enough. So don’t you see Sanjay’s is mistaken here? He says weapons didn’t manifest. But they did. How do you explain this? Even Sanjay was wrong about manifestation of weapon, he could be about the curse.

You said — the curse started affecting him it didn’t affected him completely!

Can you please explain why?

In order to prove you point, you are deliberately ignoring my simple question — Can curse be averted by simple concentration?

Or isn’t there a very simply explanation, Sanjay was mistaken. They took a simple prediction as curse. At least reading the main text clearly says so.

Can you please try answering this question?

You said — It up to you sir you, I believe he had Brahmashira!

No dear, it is not up to me. The epic no where mentions Karna obtained Brahmashira from any one. Karna never had Brahmsira.

You said — I don’t follow Karna that much i know he was a wicked character and i don’t appreciate or justify his misdeeds but have a childhood attraction Karna’s Character because of star plus Mahabharata

I completely agree about childhood attraction and and star plus Mahabharata. I too was attracted to Karna because Rashmirathi was my favorite book and National Poet Ramdhari Singh Dinkar my all time favorite poet. But nothing should change the truth.

You can take it from Krishna and I, and everyone who knew him, he was an extremely wicked and evil souled.

Karna donated. No one doubted it. But Mahabharata doesn’t say anywhere he was the biggest or “daanveer”. And his biggest trade is often passed of as his donation. Irony.

You said — my favorite character is Arjuna from childhood!

I Must say you have chosen wisely. Now you just need to discard the evil.

Best of luck.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 18, 2022 08:21 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think you are missing the main point —

It is not about winning it is about the purpose. None of the Karna’s victory had any social or lofty goal that he can be called a hero or a man of character.

The strength of the character doesn’t lie in winning every battle or not ever retreating. It is what is the cost you pay when you retreat. It is how you handle victory and defeat.

In victory, Karna was boastful and foul mouth. He always humiliated his opponents in most abusive language. He humiliated even his brothers and used abusive words against his gurus like Drona and Kripa. And such a person can’t be considered as a man of character.

In defeat Karna cared nothing more than his own life. He ran leaving behind his friends, family women, brothers. They paid the price for his cowardice. He let Bhima slaughter 31 Kauravs who died trying to save his worthless life.

You said — Karna won more fight than loss he has defeated almost more than 60 percent of Warriors who were precent at that time.

Are you sure? Is 60% a flat average or a weighted average? I don’t see him defeating many warriors of repute. He certainly never defeated Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Parshurama, Ashwatthama, Krishna or Arjuna. And I would say they together make more than 90% of warrior worth considering.

We can’t say he defeated Bhimasena or Satyaki squrely. Sometime he won, sometimes he lost. Bhima defeated Karna more number of times than Karna defeated Bhimasena. On 14th day Bhima defeated Karna many times while Karna could do it only once. Bhima also defeated Karna during Rajsuy battle.

But do you know what is the main trait of character? Even when Karna could manage to defeat Bhima only once, he abused him badly. Bhimasena didn’t abuse Karna. That is the main difference.

You said — Sir there are many indirect statement which proves that Karna had brahmashira and In bori

Can you share a single reference? Brahmasira belongs to generic category of Brahmastra. But whenever Brahmasira is mentioned it is mentioned explicitly. Not all Brahmastra were Brahmasira. There is NOT A SINGLE REFERENCE.

You said — Lord Shri Krishna called him evil but he also said this about him:

Lord Krishna called him evil many more times than he called him good. When he called him evil he gave proofs for it. And above all he wanted Karna dead. He called him the trunk of the evil tree Duryodhana. He called him evil souled. Perhaps you haven’t read Krishna’s statement about him on multiple ocassion and just follow one.

What do you think, Karna was evil or not? No matter what you say, you are a Karna fan.

You said — Can curse be averted by simple concentration? Sir Karna was forgetting his His astra not Forgotten his astra completely!

What was Parshurama’s curse — was it to forget completely or incompletely? Let me ask you this question once again — Can curse be averted by simple concentration?

Can you answer this question straight? Since last several post you are evading this question. Is it because you have realized, my assertions are correct?

Please give a direct answer — Can a curse be averted by concentration? Is there any other reference?

About Chanakya and Kabir Das

There is no authentic proof of their praising Karna. It doesn’t appear in any of their main compilation. And these posters are certainly not created by them. Don’t trust every poster you find on internet.

What confuses me is despite of having so many clear incidents and statements how can you admire a confirmed molester, wicked, deceitful, evil man?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 19, 2022 05:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I understand your need to defend Karna. The only question - Is he worthy?

You said - You said he retreated from the battlefeild when he was badly overpowered ok but who didn’t expert on some instances?

NO. I didn't say he retreated. He fled. He deserted his people. Do you understand the difference? Retreat is strategic. Fleeing is cowardly. In modern world it is a war crime.

You asked, who doesn't when overpowered. Honorable warriors don't flee leaving their people behind. Do you know the names of Major Shaitan Singh, Captain Vikram Batra, Manoj Pandey, Hawaldar Yogender Singh, Major Sandeep Unni Krisnan? They all fought in hopeless situations. They all had option to flee like Karna had. They chose to die. Do you know Captain Batra had already shown bravely and was granted leave. That was an honorable exit for him. But he chose to fight. Do you know why Major Sandeep died? He left the protection to save the life of his team.

Do you know, who else didn't flee when overpowered? Duryodhana. He was captured but didn't desert his family.

You said - Here, brahmastra seems to be equated with brahmashira. However, brahmastras were used in the Kurukshetra war. This is the only instance of brahmashira being used. The two weapons were presumably different.

The keyword is seems. And you yourself see the later part. Brahmsira is explicitly mentioned when it is used. No references in Mahabharata. Footnotes too concludes they were different

You said - no off course not, but the curse started its effect on Karna he was forgetting the mantras but he recalled it with extreme concentration and used it before it escaped away from his mind fully.

So you stand firmly that with great concentration Karna manage to overpower Parshuram's curse. Then we must agree Karna died a fare death. He was in possession of his concentration and weapons. All he missed was his chariot. And great warriors can fight without chariot.

You said - and in whole Mahabharata it is written that it was a curse so by disagreeing with it you are contradict the whole mahabharata!

No. I didn't contradict Mahabharata. Contradiction would be if I say Sanjay didn't say so or if I say it is an interpolation. I didn't say so. Mahabharata records what someone said, it doesn't say what they said was correct. Do you trust this statement completely - Sanjay said due to curse Karna couldn't manifest the weapon?

Karna said God said A woman can have only one husband's. It's written in Mahabharata. Does that makes it true? No. It only makes Karna a misogynistic liar. Shiva himself decreed Draupadi to have five husband.

Karna claimed he would kill Arjuna and throw down his ape banner flag. It's written in Mahabharata. Does that make it a fact? No. It just proves Karna a boosting fool.

I never debated what Sanjay said. My point is curse can't be undone by concentration. People assume Parshuram's word as curse. After all he never said so.

But let's agree, He was cursed for his misdeeds and Karna manage to do the impossible. He made Parshuram's curse a joke. He overcome it. Are you happy with this conclusion? What's next? Despise course's failure and Karna's full possession of weapons, he was killed by an ordinary arrow in a fair fight. Let us move on because you are not ready to see facts and my repetitions of obvious won't change your mind.

You said - by Shri krishna statement i ment to say he was also raised it was not that he was only called evil ye he was called evil because of his middeeds by Shri Krishna but he was not pure evil Like Ravana!

Do you know many believe even Ravana to be a good man. A devotee and wise. Many worship him. They may disagree with your statement. But that doesn't change the fact that he was an evil man. Neither that makes Karna any less evil. Karna was an extremely wicked and evil soul. That is Krishna's words. And beyond the realms of words, his actions are testimony to it.

You said - Sir before calling the poster Fake you should have read Chanakya neeti!

Three things -

First, Chanakya lived over two thousand years ago. There was no printing press. His words of wisdom too propagated like our epics. There are many books floating in the name of Chanakya Niti but there is no critical edition. His critically acclaimed work Arthashashtra doesn't include these references.

Second and very important is relevance. You know popular tale that Karna was a demon incarnate off some demon? The source is some Puranic text. I Never used this in any of my answers. Not once. Will never endorse such an answer. Simply because it has no reference in Mahabharata. Chanakya and Kabir Das too don't find a mention in Mahabharata. I respect both. But with all due respect neither is a greater authority on Mahabharata than Ved Vyas who enumerated all his misdeeds calling him trunk of evil tree Duryodhana and reminding Krishna's was delighted at the prospect of his death.

Third. I never disagreed that Karna was a donor. But Bali and Harishchandra weren't evils. Lord Vaman didn't kill Bali. No one called them trunk of evil tree. They didn't try to molest any women.

Even today we have many corrupt politicians who donate lots of public wealth and even there wealth. They are also evil. I don't respect them.

You said - No one was as dear as Arjuna, but he also was treated like this.

Doesn't this statement, if truly belongs to Kabirdas tells he didn't know Mahabharata well? Krishna proclaimed Arjuna his inseparable existence. He desisted Karna. Proclaimed his wives shall cry on his deadbody. Urged Arjun to kill him and announced he himself will kill him. There is not a single reference that Krishna liked Karna forget at par with Arjuna. In fact he said he who hates Arjuna, hates me. And Karna hated Arjuna.

That is why I insist, debate if you must, base it on authentic references. What's next? What Siddharth Tiwari said and what Devdutt Pattanayak said?

Question for you -

Why do you like Karna so much?

What was heroic about him?

Did he ever save a woman in distress? Did he protect anyone from being thrown in fire or from shaitans who tried to strip her in public?

Did he ever protect a distressed village from demons or any kingdom from plunderers?

Did he try to protect humanity from all devastating war?

Did he ever helped anyone outside his own selfish circle?

What part of Karna's character that make you his fan?

If you say donation-

  • Is donations a license to crimes?
  • Can you name some of his great donations that is worth mentioning?

I can see you know more about Karna than you say. You must be knowing something I don't know. From what I know it is difficult to even comprehend that someone won't desist that evil forget liking.

So tell me, what makes you a Karna fan?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasavi-Shakti/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 20, 2022 02:56 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Bookmarked. Brilliant Post.

A very detailed and well researched and referenced article. Delighted to read it. Thanks अभिjeet for sharing the article with me.

My only suggestion, don't remain ‘unknown’.

Answer: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/Why-was-Gandhi-silent-on-the-Malabar-or-Moplah-rebellion-1
Creation time: Jan 20, 2022 05:57 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No. That's my view. There is no direct quotes. But that is certainly the essence of Mahabharata. Krishna said I am neutral and yet he supported Dharma. Guess that's the definition of being neutral. Maintain balance. Protect goods. Destroy evil. If Rama, Krishna and Shiva were biased, I am proudly biased.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 22, 2022 06:50 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks.

Completely agree. These people don't understand the difference between warriors and warmongers, betweenstrongandbrave. Karna was an evil war monger with no ethics. Even if he was strong, he still doesn't qualify as a warrior. He was a coward who fled everytime he saw a threat and never cared whom he deserted in the process - his friend, his brothers…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 22, 2022 06:57 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I can’t make you agree. And I have immense respect for Bose to talk against him.

But Gandhiji was the last person who can be blamed for partition. He fought till the last for “no-partition”. But it was your capable man Jinnah who forced his way. He had a simple theory — If I can’t have it, no one should have it. Do you know anything about the Direct Action Day 1946. If you knew you won’t be talking about it. Partition was the last resort to avoid destruction. And the seed sowed by your capable man Jinnah, versus the wisdom shown by our leaders should be apparent by seeing the state of the two country today. Tell me, where would you like to live? Do you really want the seeds of hatred laid by Jinnah should have covered all of us? That Direct Action should be the justice system of the country? Sometimes even you try you best you need to ampute a vital limb to save the body. Not that you love amputation. Not that you didn’t love that limb. But because life is far more precious.

Tell me how Bose would have been Premier? Could INA even reach Indian soil? Could it win India’s independence? I honor the sacrifice and cry on the futility of it all. Precious lives wasted.

You said Bose who have been a better Premier. Have you read his ideals? He wanted India to be under controlled dictatorship for at least two decades. Now tell me one nation in the world that has actually prospered under dictatorship?

Shubhash Chandra Bose was a great patriot. He was a brilliant organizer and a warrior of the highest order. He deserves highest honor from every Indian. And only someone with no sense of patriotism would show disrespect to such a great man.

Netaji was a great warrior and a patriot, but not an administrator. His ideas of dictatorship was not in India’s favor. No civic society can exist under dectatorship. The beauty of democracy is you can condemn even the father of the nation with the choicest slangs. Under dictatorial regime, you don’t dare to do that. India had a short dictatorial regime when Indira Gandhi enforced emergency rule. Was that good? Do you know the suffering of that era? Who doesn’t condemn that period, although some may say many good things happen. Railway started to run in time. Government officials were punctual in their office. But that was still a reign of terror. For me above punctuality and discipline is the essence of freedom — A freedom you get in a democratic process and not dictatorial regime. Consider yourself blessed that you have the freedom to condemn even your own leaders.

Now coming to your main question — You said Gandhiji sidelined Bose. Actually Gandhiji exerted back when Bose was sidelining him and advocating decision that may have led to civil war. We may have been independent earlier. But it would have been no different than most dictatorial regimes. You can’t teach a army to rebel and hope it would not eventually rebel against you.

The difference between Netaji and Gandhiji was ideological and NOT a political enmity. After leaving congress, Netaji went on to become commander in chief of INA. And still he called Gandhiji, the father of the nation. Nehru, Patel and congress headed creation of constitution and the original book of constitution has photograph of Subhash Chandra Bose, a rare honor not granted to even Nehru, Patel, Tilak, Gokhle or any other leader. Why congress who presumably hated Bose gave him such an honor.

The problem is few people and spreading fake propaganda by shouting at the hightest pitch of their voice. That is all they have — high pitch voice. No fact. No respect for the legendry heroes that build this great nation. They feel to praise one you need to condemn the other. I disagree. I respect all my national legendry heroes.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 23, 2022 09:40 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I would say please go and read the meaning and origin of “Nepotism”. Inheritance is the “Law”. Even today. You inherit your father’s property and we don’t call it Nepotism.

Drona didn’t say I will teach only the sons of the King.

And there is a protocol of every war. A prince can’t go and fight a commoner. If he is killed people will say — look a powerful prince crushed a poor commoner.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero/answer/Lakshmi-Telidevara
Creation time: Jan 24, 2022 10:08 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — you decide.

What should be the basis of decision? Fables woven with creative liberty? Let us check it out point wise.

You said — two very honorable figures.

No. One very honorable figure and another extremely evil soul (Krishna’s word and his actions). One trunk of Dharma tree, other the trunk of the evil tree.

How can a molester be called honorable to begin with? He was part of the group who tried to throw Draupadi alive in fire and it was he who commanded Duhsasana to disrobe her.

You said — As a cast away one tried to fulfil his dreams by what-ever means available to him, the other got it on a silver platter.

Krishna was a cast away. He didn’t leave Dharama. And how does being a caste away could have shaped someone when he wasn’t even aware of the fact. The problem was Karna focused on his egos and ambition an as you said — by any means possible. How is that honorable when you deceive your own guru for you own ego?

And who got it on silver platter? Arjuna? Think again. Unlike Karna who lived with prosperous Adhiratha in city luxary, Arjuna started his life with exiled father. When his father died, he had to join his hostile relative where he and his brothers lived under constant threats. Arjuna got nothing on silver platter. He struggled and proved his worth by hard work, and sincerity.

You said — One became indebted to a person who gave him recognition

He became indebted to a child hood friend and partner in many crimes. Can we say, master repaid his stooge?

And how did ‘honorable’ Karna repaid his debt? By deserting his friend and his family in front of Gandharvas while he saved his miserable life? By abstaining from Kurukshetra for first ten days for his own ego? By never telling him that he won’t kill his prime opponents for lying?

Since when treason became an honorable act?

You said — One was cursed to the hilt to ensure he never got ahead

NO. He was cursed for his wicked acts. Karna himself recollected them as ‘crimes’. By glorifying his curses you are making Bhagwan Parshurama a conspirator.

You said — He resisted temptations thrown at him at him at a very late stage to a better life and the long awaited recognition but chose honor than wealth & recognition.

No he didn’t reject wealth and recognition. He had both. He rejected one of the greatest opportunity to do something good for the humanity. And yes he rejected that.

Karna never had ambition to be King or being recognized as Kunti’s son. He wanted to be recognized as the greatest archer, something he was not. Now accepting Karna’s proposal would mean he would lose opportunity to fight Arjuna. Why else do you think, he made an exception for Arjuna in his promise to Kunti? Why for Arjuna? Why not for Bhimasena? Wasn’t Bhimasena a personal threat to his friend?

Every action of Karna was triggered by his own ego and ambition. He cared for his friend as long as it is not uncomfortable to his own designs. And whenever he needed to chose between himself and his friend, he would chose himself.

Yes. Now we can decide.

Creation time: Jan 25, 2022 01:25 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Karna wasn’t just laughing and jeering. He commanded Duhsasana to disrobe Draupadi.

Answer Comment

Content:

Actually that is the main point. The world does not suck. These great people do not suck. What really sucks is the mind set the whole world is a fool, and you, who wasn't around, alone know best.

And not only Obama of Einstein, according to You even Bose and Dr Rajendra Prasad and Gurudev Rabindranath Thakur too sucks. Right?

You asked —do you really believe the British would have given India freedom, when they had not done so in the 30 odd years between Gandhi’s arrival and independence?

You must understand 30 years is not a long time when the destiny of a nation is being written. India had been fighting in for independence for a time much longer than 30 years. More than half a decade had passed since the revolt of 1857. What had we achieved during that period? But yes, I strongly feel sooner or later Britishers had to leave India. They had no choice. They didn't leave India by choice. India was a milking cow. After World war II, more than ever they needed to exploit Indian resources, and yet they chose to leave. Why? because the tradeoff between exploitation of Indian resources the effort to keep India in control was getting way higher. You must thank Indian revolution for it.

You talk about the unrest in the defence forces, but do you really not understand what was the cause of such kind of unrest in the defence forces? Why didn't see such an unrest after the Jallianwala Bagh incident? Are you really so naive as to not see the hand of the civil disobedience movement in the entire unrest and the prime reason why Britishers left India?

Remember the world does not suck and the world is not a fool to admire the greatest man of the century without a valid reason.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jan 28, 2022 07:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Every logical person understands the meaning of hearsy. And no sensible person blindly believes them contrary to the common logic and against the ideals of their own great leaders like Subhash Chandra Bose and Dr Rajendra Prasad.

The point is, I simply do not trust some hearsay which is illogical at its foundation, following astray remarks that the speaker can't even dare to own. And especially when the alternative is well documented and highly logical. Take examples of statements by Lord Mount Batten, the man on the ground and uncle of Queen. He owned his statements. Churchill owned it?

Now question to you. Why should I trust an unverifiable statement? Why should we not consider it a cheap political stunt, that it is, to undermine India's freedom struggle where millions of Indians participated? Why would I highlight a baseless statement that insults the struggles our forefathers?

Creation time: Jan 28, 2022 07:58 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I have no wish to continue. You have already proved how much you know about Netaji. You close your ears. But It still doesn't change the fact that you neither know him,nor respect his views. Be in your cocoon with your hatred against them, following your real idol — Godsay.

I am a proud Indian, proud Gandhian and a Netaji fan. You are nothing. You talked ill about India, put Bose and his views in low esteem. If you say you are a Bose fan, You cheat none but yourself.

Good luck.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Monami-Banerjee-15
Creation time: Jan 28, 2022 08:05 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why don't you share the experts that made your sensibility trust him over our own leaders like Bose, and made you realise, he understood India better than them.

But since you consider his words blindly.

Ok, let me ask you a few questions —

  • Why would any government fear any army that is thoroughly destroyed and disbanned? Was there a remotest possibility of its revival?
  • Do you know who rallied support for INA during trials? INA? No. It was the that common Indian understand then contemporary leadership whom Attlee and you are so eager to condemn, and whom charismatic personalities like Bose kept in high regard till the end. You may trust Attlee whose knowledge of India was at best limited. I trust my own leaders like Bose and my ancestors who struggled for it.
  • You rightly talked about Neval uprising in support for INA. Their sympathy certainly was with INA. But you must agree it wasn't controlled or coordinated by INA. Do tell me honestly, you don't see the spirit of non cooperation and civil disobedience in this uprising?
  • Tell me why army didn't uprise for Jallianwala Bag massacre? Or to draw support for Bhagat Singh? Because ideas take time to germinate.
  • What made British rule infesible? Had India's resources exhausted? Had Britisher’s endless greed subsided? No. They knew it's imposible to rule a nation without their cooperation. Armed rebellion had been crushed. Naval uprising too eventually subsided. But what really worried them was spirit of non cooperation and civil disobedience that had awaken in Indian masses, an Idea seeded by Gandhiji.
  • Finally, whom do you respect more, our leaders like Bose or a foreign politician with very limited knowledge of India and whose intention was to malign and discredit freedom movements, and divert attention from the fact that he was most directly responsible for India's partition.

Attlee's so called and unverifiable statement doesn't only belittle Mahatma Gandhi, but millions of Indians who were part of his movement and made British rule infesible in India.

I appalled to think, what kind of brainwash or hatred for one's root would entice educated people to trust a conspiracy theory of a foreign invader so much, and distrust your own people and ancestors in equal degree.

I pity on you. I don’t pray that you should go to hell. I pray to God, that one day wisdom downs on you that you may see things in correct perspective. I never lose hope from humanity. And you are a human being, misled, but still human. I am sure one day you will see the light.

Answer Comment

Content:

First things first, what is the main question? Is this incident an interpolation? Short answer NO. It is NOT an interpolation.

Calling a critically endorsed incident as interpolation just shows malicious intention. There is no point in defending such questions by telling actually such questions are asked because…

Now coming to Nivatakavacha being overrated, that is your opinion that you are well within your rights to form if you haven’t read the epic. I don’t compare him to Ravana. Such comparisons are irrelevant.

And now without comparing to Ravana, Nivatakavacha was a group of extremely powerful demons and Arjuna’s act was extremely heroic as he destroyed them. Is it my opinion? NO. It is very clearly mentioned in Mahababharata with a lot of cross reference and in each case the incident has been narrated with high regards for Arjuna’s heroics.

Hope that answers your question.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Arjuna-Nivatakavacas-Fight-is-an-interpolation-Because-Arjuna-who-defeated-by-foot-soldiers-how-could-he-defeat-powerful-Nivatakavacas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 02, 2022 02:01 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

A wonderful and nice recitation by Gurudev. And a really nice translation in English. With poetries like these and that of Shri Ramdhari Singh Dinkar almost forget the true character of Karna. They are awesome glorification. But we cannot call them authentic. Neither app call them best on Udyog parva. They are just the examples of brilliant rethinking of what an ideal Karna should have been.

Karna of the Epic Mahabharata did never talk so softly to Kunti and had a completely different set of interaction with her which can be known only by going through some of the authentic works on Mahabharata like the critical edition or ancient Neelkanth recitation. I do not recall that Gurudev made any such assertion that his work depict if their account of the authentic works of Mahabharata.

I too am an author off an epic fiction based on the epic of Mahabharata but I always make a point to differentiate between the creative freedom antic work and the scope of creativity which in my understanding should be creative and not destructive to the extent of destroying the spirit of the original text.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-arguments-does-karna-present-before-Kunti-for-not-accepting-her-offer-in-the-temptation-of-karna/answer/Red-Hood-290
Creation time: Feb 02, 2022 04:53 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Because our lawmakers dare not. Fear. Appeasement. Votes. Whatever be the reason, no law maker dares. Not in the past. Not in the present.

But that is no excuse for denying the fact that secularism in its trues, purest and most perfect form would be Sanatana culture. Unfortunately we got lost in the gimmick of a new word from the west world (European) not realizing that the word originated in India long before even Europe existed.

Now because I can’t let see the greatness of our civilization shouldn’t be an excuse for giving up our civilization ourselves. Does it? It is our duty to adhere to our culture to prove its worthy. Because if we give it ourselves, it would be accepting its not worth following.

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

And I have already agreed with you, it’s a beautiful poem. I only wish it were at least half true. It has no place in authentic verses because it contradicts the authentic verses and it’s spirits.

My main point is when you say

“For this particular poem, Tagore takes details from two contiguous sections of the ‘Udyogaparva’ of the Mahabharata”

We must clarify that while Gurudeva re-imagines the incident from Udyogaparva of Mahabharata, he doesn’t take the details from these section. His reimagination is not a rich filling of details but a contradiction.

I would like to believe that the poem is Gurudev’s way of wishing what should have been the ideal character of a Karna and that takes him to a reimagined world where Karna was not an arrogant ruthless person rather a man of character. And that is the power of a beautiful writing it even makes an ‘extremely evil soul’ likeable.

I didn’t call you arrogant. For arrogance, you need to have knowledge. And you, in your own words, proved your ignorance. But honestly ignorance is not bad, if you realize it. You can after all learn something.

You condemned me saying that I will go to hell. That is your hatred speaking against someone who did you no harm, just differed from your views. Is this rationale?

You hate Gandhiji so much that you hatred makes you blind even to the ideals of Netaji, whom you say you love as father. But unfortunately your hate of Gandhi makes you indirectly hate him also. Because Netaji, beyond doubt respected Gandhiji for ever. And he was no fool. They had over two decade association.

Point is hatred make someone blind and irrational. It doesn’t destroy others. It destroys the hater. Your hate can’t harm Gandhiji or Netaji. It will harm you. So I would suggest get over it. By the way do you know the names of five Brigades of INA as named by Netaji? Go check that out. You may learn something about Gandhiji’s and Bose.

I don’t have to hate Netaji to show my respect to Gandhiji. I respect them all. They all were too great men for us to even judge. And remember, Gandhiji let even go Godsey free when you attacked Gandhiji earlier. If I have to follow a man of such a compassion, how can I condemn you to hell?

No, I wish you all the best in the life. Live long and happy. And live with wisdom.

If you want to stop and shouldn’t respond to my message.

Perhaps you didn’t read Mahabharata or Gita. Just some blind fan fiction that filled you with hate.

In Gita Krishana says why Pandavas should fight their Guru and why it is the right thing to do. Karna himself admitted his crime (Karna’s own word). It is unfortunate that you people don’t know the difference. Unfortunate but not surprsing — you are a Karna fan, afterall.

Pandavas didn’t kill their guru by deceit. They killed the commander of an opposing force, who was himself fighting with deceit. Arjuna had saved the life of Drona many years ago from a crocodile. He again tried to save his life from Dhrishtduymna despite the fact that Drona killed his son by unfair means.

Content:

I agree. Karna would never be called Vibhishan. He never saved any kingdom of the world. He never rose against injustices. Shri Ram never embraced him. Never blessed him. Like Ravana and Kumbhakarna, he was destroyed. Krishna guided his execution.

But you are wrong when you say, he never betrayed his friend —

  • He deserted him and fled Gandharvas.
  • He spared the lives of his enemies. He never told his friend that he has secret pact with enemy. He let the fool believe he will kill Pandavas. That is treason.
  • He refused to fight for first 10 days of Kurukshetra for his ego.

And you are wrong to say he donated his kavach. In his own words, he refused to donate and demanded exchange.

I can't see from your eyes, but from Krishna eyes the extremely evil souled was the trunk of an evil tree Duryodhana.

Answer Comment

Content:

It is a folklore. There is no such reference in the authentic versions of Mahabahrata. It is a tale created to glorify Karna.

In original Mahabharata the evil Karna, could never overpower Arjuna. Not even once. And Arjuna defeated Karna on many occasions even when Arjuna fought without chariot or without Krishna around —

  • Everyone tried to gain victory over Drupada after Gurudakshina, only Arjuna succeeded.
  • Arjuna defeated Karna after Draupadi’s swaymvara, where Karna retreated scared.
  • Karna again fled scared from Gandharvas, Arjuna defeated them to rescue Duryodhana and his family.
  • Arjuna defeated Karna along with entire Kaurava army during Matasya battle. Karna fled. Thrice. He didn’t wait even to protect his own brother who was killed by Arjuna.
  • Karna fled Arjuna on day 14 and couldn’t protect Jaidradh.
  • Karna and his army fled on day 16 from Arjuna
  • Karna couldn’t protect his own son whom Arjuna killed in front of his eyes.
  • Finally Karna was killed by Arjuna.

There was not a single incident anywhere where Karna actually could match Arjuna forget overpower.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-Karna-defeated-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 16, 2022 12:10 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks again for your appreciation. That is really motivating.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Nathuram-Godse-assassinate-Jinnah-who-was-the-actual-person-responsible-for-creation-of-Pak-and-instead-killed-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 16, 2022 06:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Interesting. And your frustrations are legitimate, especially when people misuse those quotes without understanding the hidden meanings. Let me try to take on a few topic, though, I can’t say I disagree.

Duryodhana goes to heaven despite all his wrongdoings.

Consider it in two ways.

First, it was more of yet another illusion shown to Yudhisthira. Duryodhana was finally sent to hell once the balance of his good deeds were over.

And that leads to the second point — There may exists a few good quality even in bad person. And good should also be rewarded, without which we can’t motivate a bad person to become good. Duryodhana had a few good qualities —

  • He never did anything wrong against his parent or brothers or friends.
  • He was brave. He fought in the face of adversaries.

He was still evil. But did something good.

Why didn't Indra show him the illusions as he did to Yudhishtira?

The heavenly pleasure he was relishing was the illusion for him. Now imagine how miserable he would be suffering in hell after knowing what he could have achieved had he not been an evil. Imagine, his misery in realizing what Pandavas would relish infinitely. How would he realized that without understanding what he would miss?

Still, he conceded to Duryodhana's statement when the so-called Gods showered flowers on him and applauded him.

There is no satisfactory explanation to this point and I mostly agree. But as I see, this is more of

  • obituary. One deathbed, you praise even the worst. sometimes. Not sure why, we do it anyway.
  • In dying, Duryodhana followed his Kshatriya Dharma which Krishna explained in so details in Gita.
  • People didn’t applaud for him as a person, but as as a warrior.
  • Krishna’s silence is not conceding. It’s just a silence and Krishna said in Gita — Among all the mysteries, I am silence.

Agree — Duryodhana was nothing without his supporters.

Disagree — He was anything even with his supporters. Those who side with Adharma will lose no matter how mighty they think they are. And that is the final lesson.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-are-the-worst-things-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Kavitha-Ramnarayan
Creation time: Feb 16, 2022 11:36 PM PST
Content language: English

And no physical strength is great enough to kill entire humanity. You need to go beyond. For example, those invoking nuclear weapon needn’t be physically powerful.

And yet, nuclear weapons haven’t reached that level of sophistication yet.

Your examples from Ramayana is relevant and re-emphasizes the same thing.

Content:

Agree. But Abhimanyu isn’t an isolated incident to show the true color of Karna. He had a long history. He fled Gandharvas deserting his own family, friend and King. And few people think such a traitor can be called even a warrior, forget the best.

Then we have Matasya battle, born out the greed to plunder a distressed Kingdom and when it back fired, the coward fled leaving his brother to die at the hands of Arjuna. The entire army failed against one man, and yet people want to crown the coward as the best warrior.

How wonderful!

Content:

In fact, it is good for you to consider that I lack knowledge. This way you won’t have to question your judgement.

I don’t feel angry on people like you. Just pity.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Gandhis-cowardly-role-in-achieving-freedom-over-glorified-and-exaggerated-when-the-real-reason-why-we-got-independence-was-due-to-the-fear-British-had-of-Subash-Boses-INA/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 12, 2022 08:19 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Jaidradha was a minor minion. stupid. He wasn’t a decision maker. He couldn’t have decided, whom to allow a pass and whom to stop. Most likely Chakravyuha wasn’t supposed to be penetrated. It was supposed to surround Pandavas. But that was under the ideal condition. They had not hoped that someone would enter it and destroy from within. He was expected to stop everyone till Chakravyuh can gulp them. And Jaidradh didn’t let Abhimanyu pass. Abhimanyu smashed his way through.

Karna’s ambition was his greatest enemey.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-the-most-foolish-among-Kichak-Jaidrath-and-Karna-the-3-ones-who-tried-to-play-with-fire-but-themselves-got-burnt-All-tried-to-humiliate-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 15, 2022 12:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your inputs.

2. King Shantanu

He suffered for a promise made to Ganga. Not by him. But by his father.

Ganga drowned his seven sons and he could do nothing.

Later he loved Satyavati. As a man. But didn’t ditch his duty as a King. He declined Satyavati’s fathers demand to promise succession by Satyavati’s son.

Devavrata nevertheless accepted the proposal.

You may read all about this in my epic fiction https://thelostepic.com/buy

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-are-Ashwatthama-and-Ekalavya-so-unlucky-Why-do-both-have-to-suffer-so-much/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 20, 2022 09:31 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry never got a chance to see this **collapsed** comment. Thanks for enlightening me that I haven’t read Vyas Bharat in your esteem opinion.

Now do me and other readers of this thread a favor. Please tell what part of my answer is incorrect as per the Vyas Bharata that you have read?

Also please suggest what is it you have read that you call Vyas Bharata? Was it that script that was hand written by Ved Vyas or Shri Ganesha? Was that work un-researched? Did you research and discovered the missing original hand-written text of Ved Vyas?

While you are free to suggest someone, it is rude to attack the person rather than idea.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-are-Ashwatthama-and-Ekalavya-so-unlucky-Why-do-both-have-to-suffer-so-much/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 20, 2022 09:39 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks your suggestions are always informative, just like suggestion of using few words. And relevant words. It is a different matter, you don’t follow what you say.

You missed out the whole point of previous question in not so short narrative. I asked you what do you mean by Vyas Bharat? I didn’t see one line on that. Should we call this pointless argument?

Did I say Arjuna called Aswhatthama ungrateful? Can you check? It’s my view. Arjuna doesn’t blame Arjuna and Ashwatthama for being thankless. But that doesn’t make Ashwatthama any less thankless. His fortune was due to Arjuna.

I agree with you Drona didn’t align himself with Duryodhana because of Ashwatthama. He aligned himself because of Bhishma. But again read my point. I said it is not way TV wants us to believe. His father died because of his attachment to his friend. And Ashwatthama proved his loyalty to Duryodhana by stooping the that depth that earned him the curse. Now do you think, it was a day of affection.

But these weren’t the core question. You raised this issues just to create an argument. You took the word Hitler literally and may think Jarasandha a saint just because he didn’t ran a concentration camp and just made hundreds of King to languish in captivity to be slaughtered at the right time. But to me more important than exact degree, is the intention. Krishna had the same view.

I didn’t go to your thread. I didn’t advise you read the actual Vyas Bharat. You made your own opinion that you tried to thrust on me. And again your response shows more aggression and need to prove yourself right that to be right and answer the right question. Let me see, if I can make you understand —

  • What is Vyas Bharath. Where do you get it?
  • How do you do your own research rather than relying on other’s research?

Can you answer this time — short and to the point? You may chose to move on in case you don’t have answer or realized your mistake.
