Content:

You should read my answers before giving an expert opinion. Giving opinion without understanding may show ignorance. You may have gone through my profile. But no. You needed to personally attack. Few people suffer from this ailment.

Subhash Chandra Bose wasn’t a foreigner and you will often find me quoting Bose, Rajendra Prasad and Narendra Modi.

But then you missed the whole point of question. Question was why Gandhiji’s photo exists on our currency. And Had you read my answer you would recognize the question praise Britain or French or Portuguese. They talk about our father of the nation.

Even those looters bow their head to one man. Even the most arrogant or races and even the popular TV culture recognizes.

I don’t have a scale to rate biasedness. If had one, I don’t have habit of personal attacks. Neither do I appreciate the same.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Do-you-support-the-image-of-Mahatma-Gandhi-on-Indian-currency-notes-Whose-image-do-you-want-to-see-on-the-Indian-currency/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 28, 2022 10:52 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I understand why you would call me answer utter nonsense. Because its not based on modern propaganda but on facts from the authentic epic.

I think you should read. And you should avoid quoting by reading half and understanding nothing.

So go and read what Bhishma said — He didn’t say Karna was better than Arjuna.

But don’t stop there. Read What Shiva said — Arjuna was the greatest warrior in three world. Then read what Krishna said — All the forces assembled by Duryodhana including both Bhishma and Karna couldn’t face the wrath of Arjuna. Then go and read was Karna himself said — He said there is no warrior in three worlds who is Arjuna’s equals.

Then stop reading who said what and start reading what happened each time the two warrior collided. What happened at the swaymvar? Who ran scared? Who again fled from Gandharvas and who defeated them. Who was defeated in Mastasya battle? Who was defeated in Kurukshetra again and again. Who was defeated on day 14, 16 and 17.

So let me turn to few of your questions —

You asked — Is it wrong to have big ambitions? We all have. If he thought he is the best archer, what's wrong in it?

Well there is NOTHING wrong in ambition. There is nothing wrong in day dreaming. But there is something devilish wrong in cheating your guru. It is a crime to try and disrobe a woman in public. It is criminal to conspire to burn a family alive.

You asked — Embarrassing him and calling him suta putra all his life was right for you?

You do you think calling a Suta Putra a Suta Putra should be embarrassing? Arjuna didn’t feel embarrassed when he was called Pandu Putra? What do you think a Suta Putra should be called? And by the way should he be angry with Duryodhana and Dhristrashtra for they too called him Suta Putra a number of times? Do you even know the meaning of Suta?

You said — Arjun would have been dead in the first 10 days itself without sri krishna. Even if not, uthishthir would have been captured.

That is your perception. Period. Drona, Krishna, Bhishma, Kripa, Aswhatthama, Parshurama and Shiva didn’t share your opinion. Did they?

Do you remember Matasya battle? They were all there. Didn’t they? Do you remember why they didn’t die that day? Ask Bhishma.

You said — U said u read original MB. Tell me who was standing in front of arjunas rath throughout the war?

No One. Have you heard the phrase Dharma Rakshathi Dharmaah?

You said — Virat yudh why did arjun use Sammohan astra? It's against the rule of war?

Have you read Virata Yudh? Do you know when and why Arjuna used Sammohanasatra? After he had defeated each warriors alone and had defeated your favorite Karna thrice and the cowards decided to attack alone. That was an act against the war.

Now where did you read using Sammohanastra was against the rule of the war? Can you provide a reference? Don’t bother. There doesn’t exist one. Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Ashwatthama all talked high about Arjuna’s victory there, never saying anything against the use Sammonhastra.

You said — Why did he need divine rath, divine bow, divine akshay tunir, help from hanumanji and on top of that vasudev himself??

Dharmo Rakshati Dharamaah!

You certainly don’t expect Krishna and Hanuman to come to the aid of heinous evil like Karna or Duryodhana. Do you? Hanumanji also helped Shri Rama. Does that make Rama weak? Hanumanji didn’t fight for Arjuna. Nor did Krishna. Krishna wasn’t with Arjuna when he smashed Karna in swaymvar or when he rescued Duryodhana from Gandharva in the same war where Karna had fled or again when he single handedly defeated everyone in Virata?

Why do you think Gods grace as something evil and why do you defend the trunk of the evil tree whom even Krishna called extremely evil souled.

You asked — Who ran away from bharghav astra?

NO ONE. At least it is not mentioned in Mahabharata. The Bhargastra wasn’t fired on Arjuna. Krishna left the scene to assure Yudhishthira that Arjuna would kill Karna today. Does that sound to you fleeing? Tell me which great warrior was killed by Bhargavastra?

You asked — Who cut 11 strings of gandiv one after another?

Karna did. His arrow had 100 strings. Karna could cut 11. That is 11% damage to a weapon and the not warrior. Is there a point you want to make here?

You asked — Who saved Arjun from Nagastra?

Krishna did. Do you know what was Nagastra? An evil snake whom Karna had worshipped for many years because he was an equally evil man who knew he didn't’ stand a chance to win against Arjuna on his own and he knew he was too evil to be supported by Gods like Shiva, Krishna or Hanumana. So while Arjuna worshipped Shiva, Karna worshipped an evil snake. Karna thought it work. If you thought it. Right?

You said — Karn was equal to arjun in archery.

How did you deduce that? Drona, Bhishma, Kripa, Ashwatthama, Krishna, Parshurama, Indra and Shiva no one ever said that. There was not a single war where there was slightest hing.

You said — Karna was Way ahead of him in wrestling, gada yudh, spear, sword fighting.

Can you give any reference to prove your point? Even one direct reference that Karna was better?

I will wait for your answer.

You said — Sorry to disappoint you.

YES. I am disappointed. I am disappointed to see that someone appreciates an evil man like Karna who who try to disrobe a woman in public and whom Krishna called extremely evil soul. I am disappointed to see that someone seeks pleasure in condemning Arjuna whom Krishna called his inseparable existence and Shiva pronounced the greatest warrior of the three worlds. It indeed pains to see people hating Krishna.

But that is the world. People hated Krishna then. Some hate even today.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 01, 2022 12:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree. It wasn't Swayamvar if you consider Draupadi as an individual. But it was a swayamvar as far as bride side is concerned. They set forth the condition.

Agreed again, No one asked Draupadi's choice. But did she object even for a moment? Did she regret even once? When she never complained, should we?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-ever-regret-sharing-his-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 03, 2022 09:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Perhaps you didn't read my reply. For family it was swayamvar, they decided whom they would marry their daughter. They called it swayamvar. Now, only Draupadi had right to reject the ceremony as swayamvar. Did she?

Let me ask you this — Why don't you consider Draupadi as an individual? Why must she submit herself to your views or that of evil Karna?

She was extremely happy when Arjuna won Swayamvar. Does that tell you it was against her wish? Now tell me on what references you suggest it wasn't swyamvar? Can't a daughter trust her father for her swyamvar? Please share a valid references or logic to make your point. And if her father can't decide for her, how can you or I? Let me reiterate she was extremely happy when Arjuna won her hand. That should put your objection to rest.

What happened next was not related to swyamvar. And once again we don't see Draupadi resending polyandry. Not then. Not everafter.

Now questions for you — Why does the words of an extremely evil and wicked man like Karna becomes gospel of truth for you?

Why are you so much focused on Karna and Kunti that you forget, it was Shiva's decree and Kunti was just a means to an end? Why don't you tell what Ved Vyas and Shri Krishna said about her marriage? Did they call her whore?

Do you know Draupadi is considered amongst Panch Sati?

अहल्या द्रौपदी तारा कुंती मंदोदरी तथा।

पंचकन्या: स्मरेतन्नित्यं महापातकनाशम्॥-

ब्रह्म पुराण 3.7.219

Why should you side with Karna and quote him? How does that matter?

I hope to hear back from you.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-ever-regret-sharing-his-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 05, 2022 01:50 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

First things first, You views can’t be a representative of views of ‘Malabar Hindus’ just in the same way, mine can’t be a reflection of all Hindus or all Indians. They best you are I can offer is our views.

That said, I am sure no sensible human would compare Mahatma Gandhi and Tipu unless he knows nothing about either and his simply quoting some social media post without realizing the content. And only in those state of ignorance can one pass a judgement like that.

So let me ask you two simple questions —

  1. Do you know who was Tipu Sultan and what were his crimes? You may like to google once if you didn’t have the opportunity to read the history.
  2. Can you tell exactly what Mahatma Gandhi did that you would equate him with Tipu? Do you know the details of that rebelling? Do you know Gandhji’s reaction?

I would like you to come and justify your statement.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Mahatma-Gandhi-really-supporting-non-violence-If-yes-then-why-did-he-allow-Indian-soldiers-to-take-part-in-the-World-War-Why-did-he-support-the-Khilafat-movement/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 08, 2022 04:36 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. A BIG NO.

Kunti’s words alone doesn’t matter. Shiva’s boon is supreme. And it included five and not six.

Even more so, she was married to five Pandavas by Vedic rituals. That didn’t include Karna.

Kunti’s words didn’t amount to marriage. It just means a suggestion which Pandavas agreed and following it carried out to obey it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-trying-to-win-Karna-over-to-the-Pandava-side-did-Krishna-suggest-that-Draupadi-would-accept-him-as-her-sixth-husband/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 13, 2022 07:26 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You should be sorry. For misquoting Dharma.

Tell me, who decides “just the appropriate force"? How do you decide appropriate force without considering the fruit? What does appropriate means. And who are you to own even those actions?

As I see, you failed to grasp the Karmayoga completely. Tell me, why Krishna said that despite the fact I need to do nothing, I still do my job, for if I don't do… Can you complete the sentence? Was Krishna not thinking about fruit here? What is the lesson? Think again before you quote. Hint: He didn't say be irrisponsible.

He didn't teach Gita to Duryodhana or Karna, but to Arjuna. Not everyone can understand Dharma. Some just quote flowery words without understanding it. Some Villains misquote Gita to make themselves Hero.

Who said Mahatma Gandhi wanted to own the fruit? Who said he wanted annihilation of Hindus. I call them fools. Fools to fall in propaganda. Fools not to respect their own ancestors, fools to insult their own history. Fools to fall on social propaganda without studying, without applying common senses.

Yes, you should be sorry.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/The-quote-if-the-Muslims-want-to-kill-us-we-must-face-death-bravely-has-been-attributed-to-MK-Gandhi-Can-someone-confirm-its-authenticity/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 16, 2022 09:52 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Krishna did ask Arjuna to weild weapon. But he did attempt at peace before going to the war. Did you miss that point? And did you miss out that Krishna himself didn’t wield the weapon in the same war? He guided Arjuna to do what is right.

Did you also realize he didn’t gave a similar discourse to Duryodhana or Karna? He asked them to desist fighting. Not everyone should be allowed to resort to senseless violence. It can be endorsed only when some highter authority sanctions it — like constitution, law and not at individual whims or to satisfy personal agenda or thoughts.

And that is the difference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharat-where-is-written-slok-number-also-ahinsa-parmo-Dharma-Dharmao-hinsa-tthaiva-ch/answer/Tarun-Bansal-76
Creation time: Apr 17, 2022 12:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

A teeka is just an analysis or interpretation. It is not a reference. With due respect to Dhinmayanananda (whom I actually respect), it doesn’t change the fact that धर्म हिंसा तथीव च doesn’t find a mention in Mahabharata.

It becomes fake the moment you say —

  1. It is from Mahabahrata
  2. Gandhi misinterpreted it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-evidence-of-Mahabharat-happening-If-yes-what-are-the-pictures/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 29, 2022 01:36 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Who is biased can be easily be proved —

You said — In the first, according to Ved Vyas Mahabharat it clearly stated that Karna arrows were powerful but couldn’t move Arjun’s chariot becuse Krishna was sitting as a Charioteer.

Can you quote a direct reference where Ved Vyas mentioned it in Mahabharata? Which Mahabharata? Can you explain why Karna failed and fled against Arjuna during Matasya battle and Draupadi Swaymver battle when Krishna wasn’t around.

For your information Bhagwat Gita doesn’t mention Karna for any reason. And Krishna never said Karna was better than Arjuna. Not once. But these people certainly said Arjuna is the best — Shiva Krishna, Parshurma, Drona, Kripacharay, Ashwattham, and for you what may come as surprise — Karna. Read the last name again.

You can try providing the reference from Ved Vyas works. I would like to see, what I missed in my 30 years of reading.

You said — third, False Karna didn’t order Dushasan, he never listens to anyone except his brother Duryodhan. It was Duryodhan who ordered to disrobe Draupadi.

Your answer shows you do not know anything about Mahabharata. Never mind here is the direct quote from Mahabahrata —

Karna said — “However, she submits to many and it is therefore certain that she is a courtesan. It is my view that there is nothing surprising in her being brought into the sabha in a single garment, or even if she is naked. In accordance with dharma, Soubala has won all the riches the Pandavas possessed, including her and themselves. O Duhshasana! This Vikarna is only a child, though he speaks words of wisdom. Strip away the garments from the Pandavas and Droupadi.”

And this was not the only harsh words against Draupadi.

You said — Fourth, Karna had the capability to kill all the Pandavas, which is why he had to promise Kunti to not kill any Pandavas except Arjun. Also you have no source for what you had said, don’t bring anything out of air.

I have the source and you don’t have. Karna was defeated by Arjuna, Bhima and Gandharvas before the battle of Kurukshetra. He was defeated again and again by Abhimanyu, Satyaki and Bhimsena. And since you haven’t read Mahabharata you don’t know anything about the reference. Can you give me any reference where Karna defeated any Pandava before Kurukshetra. He was just a boasting evil man whom Krishna called extremely evil soul.

You said — Fifth, Of course Krishna removed all the Obstacles from Arjun’s path. How do you think Arjun gained Pashpastra?? Without being Krishna’s friend Shiva cannot give that weapon to anyone. It was Krishna who told him to gain Maa Durga’s blessings which was not possible Krishna.

Reference Please. As per Vyas Mahabharata, Shiva didn’t give Arjuna Pashupata because he was Krishna’s friend. He gave him the weapon and knowledge because of extreme penance Arjuna performed.

You said — If you see the entire Mahabharat Arjun always followed what Krishna taught him, show me where he did things his own way?

Don’t see Mahabharata (TV Show). Read it. Arjuna defeated Karna during swyamver battle. Krishna wasn’t around. Arjuna defeated Gandharvas who had defeated Karna and Karna had fled out of terror. Krishna wasn’t around. Arjuna defeated Karna again and again in Matasya battle. Krishna wasn’t around.

But even if you consider Krishna to be on Arjuna side. Ask why Krishna favored Arjuna and wanted Karna to die. Because Krishna was born to protect good and destroy evil. period.

You said — In the War, Krishna saved arjun from Karna’s arrows numerous times. In fact Arjun once forgot the mantra for Pashupastra, so he had to call for Lord Shiva to give him that Mantra once again.

Krishna saved Arjuna only once from Karna’s arrow. That’s common for charioteers to do. After all wicked Karna had thrown a snake at Arjuna.

You said — You say these are all folkfore. what proof of these are folkfore to you?? These are from original Ved Vyas Mahabharat. Some of them are also taken from Bhagvat Gita.

The proof is they are not present in Ved Vyas Mahabharata and not even in Bhagvat Gita. How would anyone give proof of something that doesn’t exist. You need to read the book to find they don’t exist which you have clearly not read.

So why don’t you prove me wrong by giving exact reference from Mahabharata for any incident I have called fake? If you can’t provide you know the fact.

Next time you respond, please do so with reference and not just saying something you haven’t read and you don’t know about.

Answer Comment

Content:

I think that was the idea of this question and this question of mine answers the parent question. Just like war cannot spread to inhebitant planets, same way Mahabharata/Ramayana could have only included that extent of of geographic boundary that were civilized. There was no Islam and no Christianity then.

I hope this clarifies your doubt.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-the-Mahabharata-and-Ramayana-were-taking-place-in-India-what-was-happening-in-the-rest-of-the-world-Why-did-the-Gods-mentioned-in-the-Mahabharata-and-Ramayana-confine-their-actions-to-Indias-boundaries-Are-they-just-made-up-stories/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 07, 2022 09:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Did it ever occur to you to stop and ponder over a simple question — Why did Arjuna get those books? Were they free gifts or rewards for the virtues?

In a time when people fought for divine weapons, divinty fought for Arjuna, doesn't that tell you the true power of Arjuna?

Meanwhile please quote where Arjuna said he forgot the use of Pashupata. I couldn't find that reference. Consider this why Arjuna needed it when he wasn't going to use it? Why did Arjuna needed to fight so long that his horses got tired if Mahadeva fought for him. Was Mahadeva so weak?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-were-of-Boons-of-Arjuna/answer/Prasad-Bhat-94
Creation time: May 07, 2022 09:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Chitrasena didn't fight Arjuna mildly. He fought feebly. It was Arjuna who fought mildly. And there is a huge gap between the two words. Huge enough to change the answer. And once you realize that, you may look into what Shiva, Krishna and Parshuram said about Arjuna. Then may be realising Arjuna as Nara, you may like to revise your answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 17, 2022 11:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Chitrasena didn't fight mildly. He fought feebly. And there's a huge difference between those two words. Huge enough to change your answer.

Then you may like to checkout what Krishna, Parshuram and Shiva himself said about Arjuna. Unless you find Shiva to have lesser expertise on warfare than you, Arjuna was incapable to be vanquished. With Shiva's Pashupata in his hand Indrajeet didn't stand a chance. Not even with all his illusions. And it's not a hollow argument. Chitrasena too tried Illusions that was destroyed and Shiva himself declares nothing in three world can face the weapons that he bestowed on Arjuna.

And most importantly evils shall be destroyed and Indrajeet was an evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 17, 2022 11:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your last line is a perfect answer. Rama could have destroyed the illusion. And Brahma's boon couldn't have prevented that.

Similarly even Brahma isn't protected against Shiva's wrath. Pashupata at the end of Kalpa absolves even him. And every illusion in front of him is mere children hide and seek. Yes a kid’s play. Period.

There has been a simple moral in entire epic verse. Evils shall be destroyed. By Dharma. Weapons and Illusions doesn't matter. On one hand God's boon and armour of Amrita fails to protect evil, on the other hand hell fire can't scratch a kid (Prahalad). Nagpash, Amogha astra, Narayanastra fails against those protected by Dharma.

When it comes to Arjuna he had the weapon of all the protectors of the world. More important he had blessings of all of them. When Shiva says ‘no one in three world can be his equal’, it's not his expert opinion; his words becomes a decree. When Shiva says he is ancient sage Nara and no one can defeat him, I tend to trust the words of Shiva and Krishna, the great heroics (actions) of Arjuna and ultimate epic wisdom of Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah, over hypothetical scenario and evil worshipping ideology where people imagine, without foundation, that evil could vanquish Dharma or the protectors and protectee of Dharma. Indrajeet was destined to be doomed.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 17, 2022 07:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You asked — Where is it written Arjuna fight mildly?

In fact in the same chapter, where it is said that Chitrasena fought feebly. But then you don't give importance to words. Since you asked here it is —

Arjuna’s mild action was something that was in benefit of Gandharvas and they were incapable of realizing that. But I am sure you can realize seeing how battle turned out.

But do you give importance of actions?

So what happened in Gandharva battle? Did millions attack Arjuna? Did they win? Didn't Chitrangada tried all his illusion? Do you know how everyone related Arjuna's heroics later?

Now tell me, why did you interpret feeble as mild? Is it because you don't understand the difference or is it because you don't care about the words?

Let me clarity—

  • Feeble: Lack of strength or weaknesses.
  • Mild: gentle, not sharp, not involving extremes (shows strength)

Hope this helps you understand the difference. So tell me, why ignoring the actions you tried to degrade Arjuna by using improper word? And by that extension, why ignored Shiva and his power? And if you don't value statements, on what basis are you glorifying the evil?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 17, 2022 08:26 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Don’t you Understand the meaning of The words “Lack of Strength". He wasn't Going all out on Arjuna.

One main problem with many reader is their total lack of understanding of Sanskrit and complete reliance on English and then misinterpreting it at their will. But if you honestly want to understand, I would recommend learning a little Sanskrit. Here is the exact shloka which tells that following advise of his king Dharma the first battle in which Arjuna was involved was a mild one —

राज्ञस्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा धर्मराजस्य धीमतः |
क्रमेण मृदुना युद्धमुपक्रामन्त भारत ||९||

—Chapter 233, Aryanak Parva

Can you locate the sanskrit word for Mild in the shloka? (Hint: I have made it bold)

Good Now let us see what is written in Sanskrit for Chitrasena and this time I will not give you any hint —

चित्रसेनमथालक्ष्य सखायं युधि दुर्बलम् |
सञ्जहारास्त्रमथ तत्प्रसृष्टं पाण्डवर्षभः ||२६||

Chapter 234, Aryanak Parva

Even if you know a little Hindi, you would be able to see the difference and won’t make the same mistake again.

And not just words. Look at context. Chitrasena attacked with weapons and Maya. And he revealed only when his Maya was destroyed.

And remember except for few quorans like you the epic doesn’t say anywhere that Chitrasena fought mildly. Even his worst enemies admired his feat here. And I though you value feat.

You said — I'VE SAID CHITRASENA Fought Mildly.

Ya. Many people say many thins without any reference. They tend to ignore the heroics of true hero to show evils powerful. I have heard this before.

But either you consider deeds or consider words, either way Chitrasena didn’t fight ‘mildly’.

You said — And We can see The MILDNess of Arjuna When He Used Multiple Celestial weapons to Kill Millions of Gandharvas.

Unfortunately you see nothing. Because you don’t want to see. Look again. The first battle that Arjuna was engaged was a mild one. Not the later ones. Not after Gandharvas ignored his mild maneuvers. Because they were incapable of understanding what was beneficial to them.

Such kind of satires don’t change the facts.

You said — is it said Arjuna Went Mildly on Chitrasena?

No. Because Arjuna didn’t attack him mildly. He was already in full fledged battle. And Chitrasena too attacked him with his might.

The reason I told Arjuna attack mildly so that you understand that the learned author knows the difference between the world mild and feeble. But then I am sure you might have got the point till now.

You said — Speaking of Statement vs Feat

That is good, now I don’t have to explain you the difference. So let me ask you are these feat of statement?

  • Chitrasena grasped a mace and rushed towards Savyasachi. In that battle
    • Feat
  • he made himself invisible and began to fight with Pandava. Established in the sky, he repulsed those celestial weapons. The powerful king of the gandharvas hid himself through his powers of maya. Arjuna saw that he was invisible and was striking.
    • Feat
    • Using illusion and continuously fighting. Here using illusion and fighting relentlessly is same.
  • Dhananjaya used the weapon known as shabdabheda 58 and restrained his disappearance into many different forms.
    • Feat
    • Chitrasena’s weapon and Maya is destroyed. It is NOT his will. It was Arjuna’s power.
  • Pierced by the weapons of the great-souled Arjuna, the gandharva revealed himself to his beloved friend.
    • Feat. But whose?
    • Why did Chitrasena revealed himself. Was it because Arjuna was getting weak? NO. it was because he was getting pierced.
  • On seeing his friend Chitrasena, fighting feebly in battle, 60 the bull among the Pandavas withdrew the weapon
    • Feat. Not statement.
    • Chitrasena was feeble (दुर्बल in original text)
    • Arjuna showed his compassion and withdrew the weapon.

And while we are debating between feat and statement, even statements are also of two types — Projections of what may happen and a factual record of what actually happened.

Example:

  • Lakshmana may have died without treatment
    • It is a projection. Never happened.
  • Lakshman killed Indrajeet.
    • This is also a statement. May be by someone not present there. But that is called fact. Not feat. And this fact is equally valuable.

Now let me give you some factual statements that are equally great as feat. Tell me feat or statement?

  • That destroyer of enemies swiftly vanquished Chitrasena, king of the gandharvas, in a battle, together with his invincible army.101 In a similar way, he alone defeated the Nivatakavacha and Kalakhanja danavas, whom even the gods could not slay. 102
  • O Karna! Like that, when you were attacked by the gandharvas in Dvaitavana, 403 you abandoned all the Kurus and were the first to run away. Partha killed and defeated the gandharvas, with Chitrasena at the forefront, in battle. O Karna! He freed Duryodhana and his wife.

I can go on. But I think, I gave you sufficient material to understand Ghosha Yatra and Arjuna’s battle.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 18, 2022 06:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — I don't Want to get Into a Lot of Sectarian Crap. All ready Fed up with That.

Your reference contradicts the fact. If you were fed of those craps you won’t have shared them with pride as You know I can contradict them with more references. But that would be as irrelevant as your expression “utter nonsense” because you know well what I said is based on reference and is not “utter nonsense”. So I would let it pass so that we don’t deviate from the main topic.

Back to the main topic

You said — If Shiva Himself Is Against Indrajit He would Easily ONESHOT Indrajit irrespective of Boon.

Yaah! This is a great example of utter non-sense evil worshipping. Shiva doesn’t need even one shot to destroy evils like Indrajit. And this is the example of what you can call baseless statement not backed by any feat.

You said — Shiva's Mere weapon doesn't Equate to Shiva Himself.

Another utter non-sense. No one in the right mind would equate Shiva and his weapons. But then, they wouldn’t even challenge Shiva’s power against Indrajeet.

Now I will, once again, point you to your own example —

Vishwamitra couldn’t scratch Vashishtha. Neither could Narayanastra destroy Pandavas, nor Vaishanavastra could kill Arjuna nor Brahmastra could destroy Parikshit. Nor the hell fire could destroy Prahlad And they have a common morale that goes beyond this discussion — the nature of Divyastra. If you don’t understand the true nature of Divyastra you would make a similar mistake.

Divyastra has a sacred use. It shouldn’t be fired on people who lack strength or without due consideration of the responsibility.

Now consider an evil monster who is powerful and knows the use of maya and weapon. There is no reason that a weapon like Pashupata won’t work against him. And you know what, Indrajeet, with all his maya and power was killed.

You said — Pashupatastra is Not the Only ALL POWERFUL WEAPON. It's said By Arjuna Fanatics or Shiva Bhaktas.

Yes and those Shiva Bhakta and Arjuna fanatics include likes of Krishna and Karna. Here is what Krishna said —

‘O Partha! There is a supreme and eternal weapon named pashupata. With this, the great god Maheshvara killed all the daityas in battle.

And here is what Karna said

He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds.

Here is what Arjuna said —

I wish for the terrible and divine weapon known as pashupata. 288 It is known as brahmashira. It is fearful and is terrible in prowess. When the terrible end of a yuga approaches, it destroys the entire world. Through this weapon,

I believe it is better to be a Shiva Bhakta and an Arjuna fanatic whom Krishna called his inseparable existence than an evil worshipper like Indra fanatic or a Karna fanatic.

Your answer also reveals the low esteem in which you hold Shiva. And yet you are fed up with sectarian crap.

If you see my answer where you will always find equal respect for Rama, Krishna, Shiva and Dharma you can imagine, how much I must have been fed up with those sectarian craps.

You said — Yeah. If youre Including Dharma Factor Then It's a Completely different story.

What you fail to realize is Dharma isn’t a factor here. It is the core. It is the summary. And if you ignore Dharma, which is a important for evil worshipping, that that would be a different story, a story in which Rama and Lakshama would be a villain and Ravana, Indrajeet heros.

And I know such craps are being promoted with a great vigor.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 18, 2022 06:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Are you seriously Saying “Durbalam “ means Chitrasena Fought with all His Strength? It literally means With Little Strength.

First things first. I am not saying anything from my side. I am telling what the epic says. Chitrasena is not called feeble when he enters the battle. Chitrasena is called feeble after his powers were sapped. And I am surprised why you can’t grasp this little fact.

After fighting a terrible battle against a stronger warrior, your strength saps. And you lack energy.

But then I understand your problem. You try to see the word and not the context. The whole context is when Arjuna saw Chitrasena was lacking strength not when Arjuna saw Chitrasena was using little strength.

You said — This Statement simply means Chitrasena Strikes Arjuna Mildly. Because it's Clearly written In the following statements.

You are imagining things that are not written. It says powerful King hid and continued to attack. Which part of this statements means mild.

You said — And For me Fighting With Little Strength and Mildly are The Exactly same Things. I don't Know how you Comprehend That Stuff.

I don’t blame you. It happens with people who don’t know Hindi or Sanskrit.

Sure it can be for you or anyone who don’t understand the difference between two word.

It is understandable you won’t know how I comprehend that stuff. But for people like us who understand the difference between two words and evaluate context wisely and not blindly there is a difference.

दुर्बल is a common word to represent weak not for someone powerful who doesn’t use his full potential. But maybe you don’t know Hindi or Sanskrit. This word is never used as a substitute for mild. You can ask anyone with the knowledge of Hindi or Sanskrit.

You said — Because Chitrasena was Mostly on DEFENSIVELL STANCE.

Really. Tell me which of the Chitrasena instance of defensive other than when he surrendedred. Let me enumerate those isntances so that you can point out the defenseive ones —

  • Chitrasena grasped a mace and rushed towards Savyasachi. In that battle, he held the iron mace in his hand.
    • Offensive or Defensive?
  • He57 made himself invisible and began to fight with Pandava.
    • Offensive or Defensive?
  • The powerful king of the gandharvas hid himself through his powers of maya. Arjuna saw that he was invisible and was striking.
    • Offensive or Defensive

I must say you have strange definition of what is offensive and what is defensive

You said — These along with the description that he fought Weakly is Enough for me to Understand the fact that he wasn't Fighting with His FULL STRENGTH

Then may I ask how would you distinguish between a powerful person not using his full power and a weak person whose full power is just insufficient? Perhaps you would never know.

But those who know use different words to explain the quality. Mild for people having strength who doesn’t exert completely. Feeble those lacking strength. But perhaps you may never understand.

You asked — Are you seriously Saying Full Strength and Little Strength are the same Thing?

No. Where did you get that?

I am saying what the epic is saying. Chitrasena started with a full strength. But his weapons were destroyed and he was left weak.

You would have understood by now even without any knowledge of Hindi or Sanskrit only if you wanted to see. Anyone reading this post would surely understand.

You said — Arjuna's weapon didn't Break Maya. Chitrasena appeared In his Form After being Pierced By Arjuna With That arrows. There's a Huge difference between These 2 Things. So Arjuna's Power goes to The Dustbin here.

Why do you hate Arjuna so much that it shuts your eyes to the ‘feat’ and you start to make ‘wrong statements’?

Dhananjaya used the weapon known as shabdabheda 58 and restrained his disappearance into many different forms. Pierced by the weapons of the great-souled Arjuna, the gandharva revealed himself to his beloved friend.

You see Arjuna broke his maya. He restrained his disappearance and could pierce him. Restraining his capability to disappear is the destruction of maya because maya was his ability to become invisible. Is this sentence too hard to understand?

You said —Next Thing. Arjuna Defeated Chitrasena while The Latter was Fighting mildly.

No. Chitrasena did not fight mildly. He fought feebly. Only on Quora Arjuna haters called it mild. I have given you two cross-references that you ignored. There is not a single cross reference that Arjuna didn’t vanquished Chitrasena. There is not a single cross-reference that Chaitrasena didn’t gave his best. Epic uses the word दुर्बल. And it doesn’t mean using less strength. It means weak.

And if you don’t find the difference between mild and feeble, may I request you to use the word ‘feeble’ in your future post. This is the word used in epic and translation.

For you the two words is same, so you should have no problem. But others who understand the difference will understand it.

I see this discussion can’t move forward as you don’t know the difference between the key words, so I would leave in the hope that anyone who reads this thread would understand the whole context.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 18, 2022 07:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — When I said I didn't wanted to Get into More sectarian crap. I meant I don't want to Use more and more Statements which Shows What Shiva Is To Brahma.Doens't mean what I've said Is Crap.

This is just double standard talking which by definition is crap. Because quoting a statement knowing it has ample contradiction is being dishonest to your own statement.

Do you value feat over statement? Tell me —

  • What happened to the fifth head of Brahma?
  • How many temples of Brahma exists? How many places is he worshipped? Now ask the same questions about Shiva.

But I know you won’t understand even the feat.

You Said — Shivas Power is Not against Indrajit here. It's Against Brahma. You fail to Understand This Simple Fact.

No you fail to understand simple fact that it was between Dharma and Adharma. And no boon of Brahma has ever been able to save a single evil. That is the fact.

You said — Indrajit was Killed When He wasn't Protected By Brahma's Boon. Because Lakshaman Asked him to Fight Like a Warrior and Not a Thief.

Do you mean to say, Brahma was protecting a thief and his boon was a waste moment Indrajeet follows warrior conduct? Is that how you understand epic?

You said — That was His Raw Power. Same as when He Faced Devas. But Against Lakshaman it wasn't Enough.

Same thing would have happened even if he had faced Arjuna. An evil won’t ever be enough for a warrior who follows Dharma.

You said — So the Pashupatastra killing Him Factor Only Applies When He's Not protected By Boon.

NO. Pashupata would kill when enemy is powerful enough and Pashupata has been used in accordance with Dharma.

You said — This Thing Arises when People say Even Brahma Cannot Escaoe Shiva's Wrath and Such NONSENSE like that

Not really a nonsense, the moment you find the answer to my first quesiton.

You said — The first argument goes to Bin By simply Assessing who Fired and On whom It was Fired.(In case of Vasista and Vishwamitra)

You threw a great argument in the bin. That’s your problem. But what I mentioned is based on epic reference. In case of Vasistha and Vishwamitra, the weapon was not used with due responsibility and in accordance with Dharma.

You said — Ashwatthama's Brahmashira Worked Against Parikshit. Later Krishna revived him.

True. It worked till it didn’t. A weapon had a goal to destroy. And if it is revived, it is not really destroyed.

You said — Some hardcore Shiva Bhaktas and Arjuna Fanatics. Other weapons Are Nothing compared to This So called Mahapasupata.

Did you see me using the word Mahapasupata? If not what’s the point of raise unnecessary debate?

But then, I would take the pride in being a Shiva Bhakta and even an Arjuna fanatic than a Arjuna hater and an evil worshipper.

Do you know what Krishna said about Arjuna? He who hates him _______________.

And I see no glory in being an evil worshipper and follower of likes of Indrajeet.

Continue to live in your bubble of “My Indrajeet is great” when the feat said he was killed. Evils are destroyed.

No I would take your leave. As I have understood the futility of this discussion. You have no knowledge of either Sanskrit or Hindi. Moreover you have double standards when it comes to response.

No constructive debate can happen in this scenario. I will stop here and hope you would let these comments be part of the thread so that it may enlighten those who follow Dharma and follow debate with open mindset.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 18, 2022 09:55 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — You may be good at twisting sentences But It won't work here.

Completely agree.

First, because I didn’t twist anything and quoted epic without changing any word to suit my convenience. Twisting is when you try to replace word or misinterpret its meaning knowing or unknowing.

Second, my arguments would work for someone who either knows the meaning of word or the context or sincerely tries to understand it. You DON’T fit anywhere.

You said — And Durbalam literally means Little Strength. So There No Space for any Confusion There.

No. You have confusion. Because you still didn’t understand the actual meaning because of your lack of knowledge of Hindi or Sanskrit and following non-standard translations. See the translation from Oxford Langauage

Just to reiterate, irrespective of what you may think — दुर्बल means weak. It doesn’t mean using less power.

And I quoted these references not because I needed to look up the meaning but for you. By the way any person with limited knowledge in either Sanskrit or Hindi will give you the same meaning without any doubt.

Any attempt to derive another meaning is twisting the fact. And that won’t work anywhere or with anyone who knows the meaning. Don’t believe ask in your own circle of people you trust.

You said — About scenarios

Scenario #1

All right Then A and B were Fighting Battle. B striked A very hard. A fighting with Little Strength appeared Before B.

Scenario #2

And A and B were Fighting Battle. B strikes A very hard. A Lost His Strength and Continued fighting and Appeared Before B.

Actually both are inexact. You know what the true scenario was.

Actual Scenario#3

A large army commanded by Chitrasena (no A or B needed) fought against four warriors. And they were routed. Then Chitrasena enters the battle employing all his maya and all his strength attacks Arjuna. Arjuna restrains his capability to become invisible and strikes him hard. He reveals himself and surrenders. Arjuna graceful accepts the surrender of a friend who lacked strength (दुर्बल).

This is exactly what happened. Chitrasena was tasked to capture Duryodhana and he and his army failed.

You said — You Know What's Tough to Understand “Revealed Himself To Arjuna As His Friend “

REVEALED HIMSELF. if That Was Arjuna's Power alone It wouldn't have Said Revealed HImself.

As long as you sincerely desire to clear your doubts I can help. But you should be sincerely.

Arjuna restrained his capacity. It is a clear mention. No room for doubt. But it didn’t mention it was completely destroyed. Is it? It was sufficiently destroyed to pierce him. Again clearly mentioned. His strength was diminishing and it was only a matter of time that it would have been completely destroyed. And that is when Chitrasena revealed himself.

So simple explanation. But I am sure you would understand it. Go and read it again.

You said — When Arjuna didn't even Fully Unleashed the weapon chitrasena's Illusion were Baffled. Smart.

Now the question isn’t smart. Really not smart. Let me simplify this for you. It doesn’t say Arjuna didn’t unleash any weapon. Let me give you the flow.

  1. Chitrasena enter the battle with a mace
  2. Arjuna destroys the mace. You see he unleashed his weapon. Any confusion here? No? Very well let us move on.
  3. Chitrasena employs maya. He disappears and continues to strike Arjuna.
  4. Arjuna gets angry. He invokes Shabdabheda to restrain his disappearance capacity that is not fully destroyed it. But sufficiently restrained that Chitrasena could still be attacked. You see weapon is again unleashed.
  5. Chitrasena is pierced. It is a matter of time that his maya which is currently restrained is completely destroyed. He is feeble. He reveals himself as friend not willing to fight. Arjuna withdraws his next weapon. Next one that he was about to fire.

See if you look from biased eyes with the goal of twisting facts you would miss even such a simple point and try to use ‘unsmart satires’

You asked — Running towards an Archer with a Mace. How OFFENSIVE is that?

Ask Duryodhana or Bhimasena or Hanumana. Mace had been reputable weapon. And twist whatever way you like, this is called OFFENSIVE.

And Hiding using Illusion and STRIKING. but How OFFENSIVE is That?

Extremely offensive. Using illusion, hiding yourself and striking. What would be greater offensive?

You said — Countering Arjuna's Divine Weapons Again and Again. how OFFENSIVE is that. Chitrasena shouldve Showered Multitude of Arrows Towards Arjuna. Did he Do That Now?

YES. He did that. That is what striking means.

You said — The statement which Says Arjuna Vanquished Chitrasena is Same As The One Which Says He Vanquished deva.s th X factor There, Krishna is Not Mentioned. Understood?

You need to understand. We are talking about Gandharva battle here not Khandava Dahana. And Krishna wasn’t there. And Chitrasena was vanquished. This is a fact. Feat. NOT statement.

And in Gandharva battle Arjuna did vanquish Gods. Though not alone. Read details. Do you think every arrow that vanquished Gods were fired by Krishna. No. They both did. Arjuna gets credit. Though that shouldn’t be the sole credit.

But in both case Arjuna rightly gets credit for valour, in case of Gandharva sole credit and no twisting of facts can ever change that.

And once again, I would leave you here, as I see you don’t want to acknowledge the fact, which even you have understood deep within you.

There is a proverb in Hindi that I would translated for you since you don’t know Hindi. It says — You can wake someone sleeping, not some one pretending to sleep.

And as I said, if you don’t find a difference between mild and feeble (दुर्बल) may I ask you to change the word mild to feeble and दुर्बल in your post and see the effect? Can you do that?

You may ask, why should I do that? Simply because that is the exact word mentioned in the epic?

If you change your answer with word feeble (दुर्बल) world will understand its true meaning that you want to hide.

If you won’t change, you and I both will know you have understood the meaning and the true reason, why you are not changing it.

With this I will leave you here with your feeble arguments. (you may think I mean mild).

May I expect you would leave these threads undeleted so that others can at least see and learn.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Eklavya-put-up-a-good-fight-with-Indrajit-or-in-how-many-seconds-will-he-be-defeated/answer/ഋതുപർണ്ണൻ
Creation time: May 18, 2022 10:42 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As a warrior, it was beyond doubt Ashwatthama as is clear from numerous instances including Matasya battle.

As a command, I would say neither. Neither could boast of any great achievement. Kaurava’s family suffered huge casualty during Karna’s tenure as commander and he had least time among the first three commander, which was in proportion to his capability. A escapist who values his life more than anyone can’t become a great commander.

On the other hand, Ashwatthama becoming commander was little more than a joke. The war was already over. And so was Ashwatthama, the one we saw before the death of Drona. What we had was hunter, who can neither be called warrior nor a commander.

So if I have to chose between the two as better for commander, it would be a tough call between an ineffective warrior and commander and an effective hunter who hunted down is prey at the night inflecting maximum damage to opposing camp than anyone else. But still I would pick an ineffective warrior over a hunter. I would go with Karna as better of two commanders.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-think-Karna-was-lucky-to-be-commander-before-Ashwatthama-when-everyone-from-Duryodhan-to-Shakuni-and-everyone-and-even-Bhishma-Pitama-wanted-Karna-to-be-commander-even-Drona-was-ok-with-it-but-it-was/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 19, 2022 11:39 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

We have debated before and you after putting few estray remark never bothered to return. I don’t have time to debate with you.

And yes I have read enough Indian history (not from whatsapp university) to understand the truth, a truth which was acknowledged by great Indian leaders like Subhash Chandra Bose, Dr Rajendra Prasad, thinkers like Paramhansh, Rabindra Nath Tagore, and world leaders, writers, thinkers like Nelson Mandela, Dalali Lama, Barak Obama, Martin Luthar King.

And only and extremely stupid would venture say “Hell with his name and fame in foreign country”. It is a great Indian legacy. It’s India’s identity.

I would rather say “Hell with your rotten ideology.” It has no takers other than few like mindless people.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-over-cheap-and-wrong-use-of-Mahatma-Gandhi-by-Congress-for-75-years-to-just-win-elections-the-root-cause-of-Gandhi-bashing-by-new-generation-people/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 19, 2022 11:46 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don’t think much would have changed. With Bhishma gone, Kaurava were just waiting for the inevitable. But what really sealed the fate of Kaurava was the death of Abhimanyu as it unleashed Arjuna, who was still a reluctant participant in the war.

Moreover after the death of Dronacharaya Ashwatthama was irrevocably lost. The warrior was gone. A mad avenger had replaced him. With just blind rage, Ashwatthama wasn’t likely to be of much service to Kaurava.

The only change I perceive is that after having participated in the battle as the commander, he may have channeled his anger better and would not have stooped low enough to be inspired by an owl.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-think-Karna-was-lucky-to-be-commander-before-Ashwatthama-when-everyone-from-Duryodhan-to-Shakuni-and-everyone-and-even-Bhishma-Pitama-wanted-Karna-to-be-commander-even-Drona-was-ok-with-it-but-it-was/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 20, 2022 02:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Arjuna never felt jealous in authentic text. NOT ONCE. And Krishna NEVER praised Karna. Never said Karna was better than Arjuna. Karna never pushed Krishna chariot back. All these are fables to glorify a brutal evil, whom Krishna desperately wanted killed and called him “extremely evil soul.”

That is the whole truth.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-think-Karna-was-lucky-to-be-commander-before-Ashwatthama-when-everyone-from-Duryodhan-to-Shakuni-and-everyone-and-even-Bhishma-Pitama-wanted-Karna-to-be-commander-even-Drona-was-ok-with-it-but-it-was/answer/Param-Vishnu
Creation time: May 20, 2022 05:36 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There were a number of factors that controlled the behaviour of divyastra most important being comparative powers of the warrior and urgency of use. It was stated by Drona, Indra and above all Shiva himself. Indra forbid Karna from using Vasvi dart as long as he had any other weapon left. And when Karna exhausted everything against Ghatotkach, the initiation worked, while most invocation done in haste failed.

So if a weapon is used even with malicious intent but against a worthy opponent, it should work.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-old-Indian-epics-why-was-the-knowledge-to-use-the-legendary-Brahmastra-given-to-multiple-people-Doesnt-giving-it-to-a-lot-of-people-lessen-its-power-just-like-if-everybody-had-nukes/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 21, 2022 01:23 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I already explained this debate is futile with someone having no understanding of either Sanskrit or Hindi. And I already said, let us conclude because you won’t be able to understand the meaning of दुर्बल which is crucial for this discussion. Must we continue to debate?

But if you insist on the debate, here I would try for one more time. But that is the last one.

You said — Wielding an Iron mace and Running towards An archer Is Offensive.

Actually It can be very very offensive. You evidently don’t know Bhimasena with a mace broke down Dronacharya’s (an archer) defenses on day 14. Neither you know anything about Mahabharata where with a lesser weapons like trees and stone destroyed power demons and their archers. But since you don’t know you can’t comprehend that a mace fight is offensive.

And if you still can’t understand, no one can help you and you can go and laugh. But you would be laughing on yourself. I don’t laugh on others mistake. I try to correct them.

And thanks for saying — How Brilliant of You… 😁

You said — Using Illusions to Hide. Is Offensive Nice.

Hiding using illusion is not offensive. But may I advice you to read the full text. Chitrasena was NOT JUST HIDING. He was also attacking. At THAT IS OFFENSIVE, even if you don’t want to believe this, you may go on laughing.

You said — So now we Agree That Arjuna didn't Broke His Illusions. right. Whether He could Or Not.

Answers aren’t always binary. They are on a scale. But if you don’t want to understand no one can make you. Arjuna did restrain Chitrasena’s form of appearance, sufficiently to attack him and pierce him. Completely or not I won’t vouch. But sufficiently to make him feeble. And this is clearly mentioned without reasonable doubt. Chitrasena became feeble, a word that means inferior and not mild.

You said — Why Not. I will write Chitrasena Fought Weakly. Is that Good ENOUGH for you Know.?

NO. That would be distorting the fact. Let me reexplain for the last time —

  1. Chitrasena attacked with all his might, using all his illusions and divine power.
  2. He eventually became feeble or दुर्बल or weak by Arjuna’s continuous attack. And then he surrendered.
  3. Arjuna stopped his attack when chitrasena became weak.

So what you should say — Chitrasena attacked with all his might but lost his strength and became feeble and surrendered. Anything else would be a misrepresentation.

And I can see throughout this answer you have tried to misrepresent. You changed feeble to mild. you tried to mis-represent the meaning of दुर्बल। You tried to bring your own interpretations that you can’t back from epic narratives. And all these can’t be attributed only to you ignorance of Sanskrit and Hindi which is evident. It is a willful distortion and that tells that you have realized the truth just don’t want to accept it.

And as such, I would put a hard stop on this debate from my side. You have to decide if you want to be honest or continue your evil worshipping ideals.

Regards,

Content:

I would first read myself and then try to correct anyone. Draupadi wasn’t lawfully wedded wife of Arjuna. Arjuna won the swyamver. Formal ‘lawful’ marriage was not solemnized. And if you think, it couldn’t be without the presence of the family of both bride and bridegroom’s family. If Rama and Sita’s marriage awaited Dashratha’s arrival. But most important, their marriage is clearly mentioned at a later point of time in Vaivahik Parva which is a later incident.

Hope that helps.

Content:

Thanks for letting me know about what channel I have to avoid.

I would recommend ignore them. Because more we talk about them more it would boost them. say some one may be tempted to go and watch their few episode to see what garbage they are selling and they would gain a viewer.

Best way would be to counter them with more proper and quality answer and not to use vulgar language. It never defines the subject. It defines the speaker.

Answer Comment

Content:

Nice answer. Just one point — Chandradeva didn't wish Abhimanyu should die. He projected he would die. And projections were based on current reality he had foreseen.

In a hypothetical alternative, Chandradeva opinion or projections too would been different. Maybe he would be destined to rule as greatest King of Kuru dynasty after the great war and Pandavas Nirvana as the desciple of Krishna and Arjuna and the custodian of their joint legacy.

Just a thought.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-would-be-the-output-of-war-if-Pandavas-reach-to-help-Abhimanyu-in-Chakravyahu/answer/Kavana-N-13
Creation time: May 28, 2022 09:07 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you have a reason to believe, he didn’t?

As I see, he was an extremely capable army general. During Matasya battle he saw the capabilities of Karna, and how his boastful nature can cause a rift in the ranks. At that point, he defended Karna. But if you look closely, he did so to avoid the rift.

But then perhaps he learnt his lesson and used Karna’s ego against him into backing out of the war and avoid a similar trouble in Kaurava army. His plan succeeded and worked for them. Bhishma had little trouble in his command.

Once Bhishma retried hurt from the war, he knew Kaurava needed Karna to add to their power. He again used the right words to encourage him and bring to the fold. That is what a wise general should do. Avoid miscreants and use them only when needed.

But I would be keen to understand an alternative perspective if you have one.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-being-the-son-of-Surya-the-God-and-the-virtuous-woman-Kunthi-as-per-Mahabharatam/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 29, 2022 10:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

In my understanding, Bhishma had no animosity towards Karna. He had a dislike for him for the same reason, you offered. Duryodhana was indeed misguided by Karna on multiple occasions and he wanted to reduce Karna's influence on Duryodhana.

But it would be wrong to say that he always talked poor about him. During Matasya battle he actually came out in his support and shielded him from the ire of Drona and Ashwatthamaa. This shows his intelligence and balance in views. When he belittled him, that too wasn't without a reason. He needed to protect Duryodhana from getting further wayled because of his bragging. Remember Gandharva fiasco was the result of his scheme. Once again Matasya plan was first endorsed by him. And it was trusting his words Duryodhana ignored Drona and they suffered humiliating defeat.

Finally I must disagree with you that Bhishma knew or thought that Karna had the ability to take on Arjuna. For having such opinion three things were required —

  1. He needed to be as blind fan of Karna as Duryodhana to trust his words over his achievements.
  2. He needed to ignore every single confrontation between Karna and Arjuna, in each of which case Arjuna badly thrashed Karna, directly and indirectly.
  3. You needed to consider Krishna and Parshuram fools.

And I don't see Bhishma fitting this criteria. He saw Karna fighting. He heard Krishna's and Parshuram's view and never gave any indication that Karna had capabilities to stand against Arjuna. He felt his army better without him, and clearly said Arjuna would destroy him.

And we all know it for a fact.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-being-the-son-of-Surya-the-God-and-the-virtuous-woman-Kunthi-as-per-Mahabharatam/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 30, 2022 02:05 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don’t call the last conversation as an interpolation. Calling everything I don’t agree with as interpolation is being dishonest and escapism.

But at the same time, we must understand, last statement of a person may not be his absolute truth. It could be simply psychological. Let us agree Bhishma belittled Karna on a number of occasion and made a contradictory statement later. But how do we know which one he said with conviction and which one he didn’t? That is where cross-reference comes into the picture. Which statement fits better with Karna’s character? Was there any single occasion where Karna rose to Arjuna’s level to give Bhishma any reason to consider them equal? The simple answer is NO.

But is there another way to see when Bhishma was stating the fact?

In Yaan Sandhi Parva, Bhishma says Karna is not even sixteenths of Pandava. He recounts how Karna fled battles after battles. He specifically mentioned Matasya and Gandharva battle and Karna’s defeat and coward nature.

But in Bhishma Vadha Parva, he calls Karna invincible. Does that sound as Bhishma’s true assessment of Karna when he saw Karna fleeing in front of his own eyes letting his brother killed?

No. It must be clear that Bhishma’s last statement was not his true assessment of Karna.

Also note, Bhishma wanted Karna to be out of the battle field as a commander-in-chief. In peace time, when he had chance, he opposed Karna. But during the battle when he realized that he could no more help Duryodhana but Karna can, he changed his instance and praises Karna. As I see, it was psychological support a patting to his ego, which was known to be effective. Bhishma as employed the same technique in Matasya battle. It was more psycological excercise that a statement of fact.

Finally, even here Bhishma doesn’t say Karna was better than Arjuna. As per KMG, he does call him equal but that statement doesn’t find a mention in BORI. And it appears more logical and consistent that Bhishma may not have called them equal.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-being-the-son-of-Surya-the-God-and-the-virtuous-woman-Kunthi-as-per-Mahabharatam/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 31, 2022 02:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I agree and disagree.

I agree when you say, Bhishma was aware of his previous statements. I agree when you say that neither Karna nor Bhishma believed Karna could win against Arjuna

I disagree when you say

Second must be truer because unlike first, the second statement doesn’t have a backing proof. Just like us, even Bhishma had the stats of Karna failure and he did use them to remind Karna of his failure during Matasya and Gandharva battles. Yes, he had seen Karna fight and more often than not flee. He had a corroboration of his experience in the statement of experts like Drona, Parshurama and Krishna.

As I see, your observation stems from the fact that you don’t find that it was necessary for Bhishma to encourage Karna to fight better and you call it out of character. In fact, as I see, this is a conclusive proof of the character of both Karna and Bhishma.

Karna always lacked focus and perseverance and looked for shortcuts that suited him and not to people around him. That was the reason behind his rejection of Drona, deceit with Parshurama. His tendency of self preservation is at the cost of honor, friendship and blood relation is evident in every war he fled.

Another important trait for Karna was boasting, which is a form of self assurance to convince himself and world that he was more worthy than he really was. He boasted and liked to be flattered and became abusive every time someone showed him the mirror, so much so he deserted the first ten days of battle for having felt humiliated. Such people need constant hand holding, patting and reassurances.

Coming to Bhishma, we see he was brilliant in his maneuver. He supported Karna over Drona during Matasya battle. Why? Why did he say Karna is right and trying to encourage us? Were Drona and Ashwatthama wrong in their statements? NO. But for Bhishma, more important than truth, at that point in time, was ensuring effectiveness in the ongoing battle. This is exactly what he did in Kurukshetra.

Now we seriously can’t expect Bhishma to sincerely call Karna invincible when his failures were glaring and a common place knowledge. Can we?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-being-the-son-of-Surya-the-God-and-the-virtuous-woman-Kunthi-as-per-Mahabharatam/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 31, 2022 10:25 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I think the word “nepotism” doesn’t fit here nor was one of the concerns of Gandhiji. It was simply Nehru was better suited. He connected better with people, which even Patel recognized, he had better foreign relationship and he was much more younger. Moreover the selection of president in 1946 was a mere formality, Gandhiji had declared Pandit Nehru as his successor many years ago and everyone knew that.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Gandhiji-choose-pro-Congress-Nehru-over-pro-BJP-Patel-for-the-PM-post/answer/Kavana-N-13
Creation time: Jun 03, 2022 10:28 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am not sure why do you think Ved Vya’s “fled” didn’t mean ran away fearfully? And more important, why do you expect me to understand your view over Ved Vyas’s.

For your information most of the time, his fled mean meant fled cowardly —

  1. During Draupadi’s swaymvar, he fled because he thought the opponent is invincible. And Ved Vyas explicitly adds “scared away”.
  2. During Matasya battle, when he fled the entire Kaurava brothers were captured and were lost forever. Karna himself explained he ran away because he can’t face them in the battle. He also thought no one could defeat them. That is what you should call being afraid.
  3. In Virata war, Karna retreated even after seeing his brother killed by Arjuna. Is that bravery? And finally he was doomed.
  4. On Day 13, Karna again retreated even after seeing his brother killed by Abhimanyu. And he himself admitted he is unable to face Arjuna.

And the word ‘fled’ is not an isolated term and its idea is echoed in the words of other experts of the era.

  1. Drona says he constantly sees Karna ‘fleeing’ battle after battle. Do you think he had no idea what does the word ‘flee’ means?
  2. Bhishma reminds Karna of Gandharva battle and Matasya battle and ask why did he flee? Karna couldn’t answer. He didn’t say Ved Vyas misinterpreted it.

I can tell you Karna fled as a coward does and no amount of whitewash can change this fact. You may choose not to believe it, but please don’t try to mis-represent the words of Ved Vyas.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 06, 2022 06:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah.

But you mention only two points as if there were no other points in the time line. Let me show you expanded time line.

k1/————-/k2—————/k(3–4)——k(5)——k(6)——k(7)——/A——/B

  • All the k points, Karna would have been killed.
    • k1, he would have been killed by Arjuna who let him walk
    • k2, killed by Gandharvas but he fled.
    • k3–4, killed by Arjuna but he fled
    • k5, he was unable to flee. Rendered Unconscious. Arjuna let him live anyway.
    • k6, He fled climbing on the back of Ashwatthama chariot on hearing Arjuna has arrived (Day 14)
    • k7, He fled in the dark unable to fight Arjuna
  • A → Karna throws a deadly serpent that he had worshipped. It wasn’t a divine weapon, it was an extreme treachery. Krishna as a charioteer changed the trajectory and saved Arjuna. That is what an expert charioteer is supposed to do.
  • B → Karna’s luck ran out. He still had his weapons with him. Like Arjuna or Abhimanyu he could have fought from the ground. But he was no Arjuna or Abhimanyu. He was Karna, and he was more focused on his greatest protection the chariot wheel. How would he have fled otherwise? When he failed to take it out he asked for a recess. Many fought without chariot, but no other warrior in the battle asked for a recess. When that wasn’t granted. Karna started fighting again and was killed.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 06, 2022 11:00 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I see two reasons

  1. Bhishma had almost no influence on Duryodhana. He had already tried all he could to show reasons to him in vein.
  2. Bhishma wasn't someone whom you can turn against Hastinapur.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Krishna-only-assist-Pandavas-and-what-is-the-relationship-between-them/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 07, 2022 01:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I have the same advice for you with better conviction. Your answer is a contradiction not only of the history, and insult to great leaders like Patel and Bose but also yourself. Allow me to point out —

You say Modi -Advani share a great relationship. WRONG. They once shared. If you don't have contemporary facts right, how would you understand what happened 75 years ago?

You say had Modi stepped aside for Advani, he would be insulting people's mandate. Do you want to say Patel insulted people's mandate? Do you want to say Rama insulted people's mandate? Perhaps you don't know what Patel himself realized in late 1948. He said descision of our leader has proved correct. I consider him a man of integrity. Do you?

You say Patel was chosen constitutionally. Which constitution? There was no constitution. It wasn't people's mandate. It wasn't for India's PM. It was an internal party voting. And people who voted were not mandated to choose the PM of India. And when people got a chance they voted did vote for Nehru.

You want to make people believe there was a bad blood between Gandhiji and Bose. Then there must be a bad blood between Rama and Dashrath who ousted him. But then why did Bose called Gandhiji, the father of the nation? Do you think he was stupid or a flatterer? Let me give you another hint: Do you know the names of the five brigades of INA, crested by Bose?

Patel, Bose, Nehru have immense contribution for the country. Please don't tarnished their name for your petty political propaganda. They are not your puppets.

Please do read history before offering such ignorant opinion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Gandhi-responsible-for-Indias-partition/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 10, 2022 06:10 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree. There would have been no POK. The entire Kashmir would have been part of Pakistan. Perhaps you don't know Patel was willing to trade-off Kashmir. With Kashmir not part of India perhaps Askai China would have been a Pak’s problem.

And you are WRONG about UNSC permanent membership. Patel was a great nation builders and one of the greatest Indian leader, but didn't have such an international reputation to mobilize the world to enter UNSC. Moreover he died way too early because of his illness.

And every thing said, a few working committee's voting is a working committee's voting and cannot be considered as people's mandate. Period.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Sardar-Patel-won-the-majority-then-who-made-Nehru-as-PM/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2022 10:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Gandhiji didn't play the game. He passed a descision which Patel accepted. And Gandhiji had taken this descision many years ago. So if someone tried to play a game, it was CWC. They played and they lost. Period.

And if it was a game played by Gandhiji's, Patel played in his team against CWC. And not only then but also after when he publicly accepted Gandhiji's descision to be correct. You may want to blame Patel to ditch CWC in favour of Gandhiji, I respect him for the same.

For the rest of your wishful thinking refer answer to your next comments

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Sardar-Patel-won-the-majority-then-who-made-Nehru-as-PM/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2022 10:14 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Krishna did urge Karna to switch sides to Dharma. That is what God do. They bless even evils like Karna, Ravana, Hiranyakashyaap and Duryodhana and gave them repeated chance to atonement. Remember, Rama sent Angada to Lanka for peace. Didn't he want to kill Ravana. Wasn't Ravana an evil.

Had Karna accepted Krishna proposal he would have prevented an apoloclyptic war and that would have atoned for his past crimes, at least partially. He would have redeemed himself just like Ratnakar became Valmiki and Vishwaratha, Vishwamitra.

And for your information, Krisna first offered Pandavas alligiance to Duryodhana. Does that make him great? He didn't offer Kingship to Arjuna. Can we say Krishna liked Duryodhana more than Duryodhana?

There are two important points. First, God grants even evils, a chance to redemption. Second by rejecting Krishna's offering Karna chose to remain evil and Krishna urged Arjuna to slay extremely evil soul Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 12, 2022 10:52 PM PDT

Content:

If there is something fake, it is your comment or wisdom to think Abhimanyu was killed by Duhsashana's son in a fair duel. His end came while fighting his final duel.

You know when a covid patient die, it is not due to lungs failure where covid virus attack but most of the time due to cardiac arrest or heart failure which is not infected by COVID-19 virus. Does that mean there is no COVID-19 death? Your supposition that Duhsashana's son killed Abhimanyu is equally flawed. He was so worthless that even his name doesn't find a mention in the epic.

And do read what Krishna, Dhritrashtra, Ved Vyas, Sanjay and Gandhari said about Abhimanyu's death.

You are free to learn the truth or live in your bubble with your evil idol.

I dare you to show one quote that says Abhimanyu was killed fairly.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
