Answer Comment

Content:

Your comment was collapsed so responding late .

Here your response is your views, mine is based on reference. That is the key difference.

You said my answer about Surya is wrong. But I gave you the direct reference. Surya did feard for Karna's life. If you say my answer is wrong, prove by a reference.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 14, 2022 12:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your comment was collapsed so responding late .

Here your response is your views, mine is based on reference. That is the key difference.

You said my answer about Surya is wrong. But I gave you the direct reference. Surya did feard for Karna's life. If you say my answer is wrong, prove by a reference.

Surya had reason to fear. Arjuna defeated Karna in every battle. Every Single.

That brings to your next point. You said Arjuna won every battle with his divine bow. That is WRONG. Arjuna thrashed Karna with an ordinary bow after swayamvar. Bhima and Abhimanyu defeated him with ordinary bow. Karna wasn't powerful enough that his opponent need protection.

Then you ask why Krishna was protecting Arjuna. Simple answer he was doing his jobs as a charioteers and was driving him even during first 10 days when Karna was away. Can you tell me how Krishna protected Arjuna? He wasn't fighting for him. Did you mean, when he manuvered the chariot to protect Arjuna from Karna's decietful naga weapon? Surprise!!! That's what charioteers are expected to do.

You also said since Karna was taught by a Parshuram and Arjuna by Drona (desciple of Parshuram) Karna must be great. Alas, you don't understand being student of a great guru doesn't make you great. A guru is not a factory that all it's product would be great. Didn't Bhishma himself defeated his Guru? Another desciple of Drona, Karna, was a complete coward.

But if you insist on Gurus, Arjuna was also taught by Shiva, the Guru of Parshuram, who himself announced Arjuna as the greatest warrior in three worlds.

You finally said that Karna didn't use Vijay bow ever except on the last day. Can you tell me why? Can you quote a single reference that says what you say. And why did he fail to protect his son or himself despite having a Vijay bow?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 14, 2022 12:21 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don't think divine weapons or armours matter in winning or losing a battle, courage matters, and courage was something completely negligent in Karna, who was a coward. Killing him may have been difficult, beating him not so much. With little force applied against him, he would run away for cover and any warrior of worth and perceverence would have defeated him. Bhima and Abhimanyu subdued him by normal weapons.

That said, I won't compare our epic characters with comic character and if I do I would pray for good side to win. Since Batman fights against crime and for justice (Dharma), Krishna would ensure that he would win.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Batman-defeat-karna-from-Mahabharat-using-prep-time/answer/Sūrya-S-A-सूर्य-एस्-ए
Creation time: Jun 14, 2022 08:29 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am not here to refute beliefs as long as it doesn't contradict the epic in such a way that it distorts the epic. This is not one of those. So, with respect I would put forward my views —

Firstly, this question is not about whom Draupadi loved more, but if she was actually shared as a commodity.

Second, Bhima indeed was more vocal than others towards Draupadi's needs. But does that mean others didn't care? Does silence mean apathy? Why can't it mean strategy? Why didn't Drauoadi took a vow to destroiy Kauravas or disvow her other husbands? Why did she ask the freedom of Yudhishthir before others? Love? Or strategy?

And yet, If the beliefs are correct, it only proves no one should have regretted Draupadi's marriage to Pandavas. Isn't it.

Honestly believe only Draupadi could have answered the question whom she loved the most and I believe if she was asked this question her answer might have been — I am not sure.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-ever-regret-sharing-his-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 16, 2022 07:10 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No, Karna was someone willing to kill his younger brother. He was someone capable of running away on seeing his brothers killed by the enemy. He was someone who was willing to run away to save his worthless life while his friend, benefactor and their family women were captured.

And in your own words Karna was so egoist that his his ego was hurt on listening his own truth and his sense of self glory was more important than loyalty to his friend. He didn't fight for the first 10 days because his ego was hurt by listening to facts.

And the difference between Arjuna and Karna — Arjuna didn't really kill his elder brother. He got angry. But he heeded to Krishna's advise and proved that, for him, his ego wasn't greater than his brother or friend. When Krishna gave similar counseling to Karna, did he listen? No.

Do you know, Krishna called one of them his inseparable existence and said he who hates him hates me. And he called the other extremely evil soul. Quiz for you — Guess who was the first and who was the second.

Apoint for you to self reflect —Why do you hate Krishna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Bhishma-had-finally-explained-why-he-was-belittling-Karna-though-he-knew-Karna-to-be-a-great-warrior-However-why-did-Drona-always-sided-with-Bhishma/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 16, 2022 07:21 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If we look that way, no one deserve to die. Uttar died in a extremely normal battled combatting Shalya directly and there was nothing special or out of ordinary there (Don’t trust everything you see on TV Shows). If we analyse we see a great and heroic transformation in Uttar from the Matasya battle where he was a timid boy. He died a warriors death.

Drona deserve no sympathy. It didn’t showed one to anyone. Neither to Abhimanyu nor to his favorite Arjuna. It was he who extended the battle well into the night of fourteenth day. He who respected neither the battle ethics nor relation was supposed to die the way he died.

Tell me, why did Drona sat down in the midst of the battle field? Had he really and completely given up his weapon on hearing the news of his son’s death? NO. He continued to fight for a long time and finally gave up his weapons when Bhima questioned his Dharma. But even if I were to believe that he gave up his weapon, does he deserve any mercy? Did he ever stop any attack on unarmed warriors? And why did he stayed back in the battled field after giving his weapon? Why didn’t he retire? That would be called stupid but doesn’t deserve either sympathy or can be called tradgedy.

But then, that is my opinion. Feel free to pen your opinion and share your views. I would love to read them.

Answer: https://devidraupadi.quora.com/While-many-characters-died-in-Mahabharata-war-a-few-of-them-would-touch-our-heart-as-to-the-manner-how-they-died-finally-2
Creation time: Jun 23, 2022 08:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I respect your opinion and this question is all about opinion. It is now rare on quora people discuss in a civil manner and I thank you for that. Most of the time it is a baseless argument.

Here is my points, at the risk of being repeatitive.

Bhurisrava

I already mentioned, his conduct towards Satyaki was unjustified. But then he was punished by Arjuna. And he was repenting for his conduct. Why I called it as a tragedy was the fact that Satyaki was not punished with same measure for same conduct as Bhurisrava and in that way his death was not properly vindicated. Now you can’t punish someone twice for a crime he was about to commit, but didn’t realy succeeded.

Drona

Drona chose is own death. He could have gone down like a valiant warrior or retired like a enlightened old man to repent his misdeeds. He chose to be a escapist. He alone is to be blamed.

Jayadrath

Agree. He didn’t deserve any boon. But then, when he had the boon, he used it for his relative. And he trusted them to protect him in return. He was betrayed by the same people. And that makes his death a tragedy.

In my discussion, I didn’t include Jaidradh or Bhurisrava because they were good or bad, but because they were in some way betrayed. Same goes true for the death of Karna’s brothers and sons.

And I have no sympathy for the evils like Karna, Duryodhana or Duhsasana.

And I don’t lament on the death of heroes like Abhimanyu or Bhishma.

Answer: https://devidraupadi.quora.com/While-many-characters-died-in-Mahabharata-war-a-few-of-them-would-touch-our-heart-as-to-the-manner-how-they-died-finally-2
Creation time: Jun 24, 2022 09:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

While talking about discrimination did you realize Karna enjoyed a life full of respect and luxury as the King of Anga and friend of Duryodhana? Do you know he was taught by Drona and Parshurama? If there ever is a question of abuse, majority of the time Karna was a perpetrators and not victim? And only times he was denied something was because of his own evil design?

Similarly Eklavya was a King, a part of elite Jarasandha’s team and an invited guest in Yudhishthir's Rajsuy yagna. He died because of his evil campaign against Dwarka. He didn't die because powerful people wanted him dead or Drona rejected him. He died of his actions.

Krishna a cowherd was called cowherd because he was one. He was insulted by likes of evil Shishupal but respected by Bhishma and Pandavas and everyone. vidur born to a maid servant was the prime minister of Hastinapur. And these facts destroy your theory.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Why-did-Karna-and-Eklavya-face-social-discrimination-if-there-was-no-casteism-in-ancient-India-1
Creation time: Jun 24, 2022 08:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Actually they are ok with polygamy but feel repulsive over polyandry.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-ever-regret-sharing-his-Draupadi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 26, 2022 03:41 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Ah! After a long time and almost forgotten. The Vijay bow question was directed to Mr Shashidhar Murali because he had inserted that idea here.

My understanding is Indra didn’t ask for Vijay bow because Karna never had that. Here is my detailed view on the topic — महाभारत के महान धनुष by कथाकार (Storian Hindi)

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Arjuna-was-having-armour-like-Karna-from-birth-would-Suryadev-had-come-to-beg-it-like-Indra-did/answer/Sesha-21
Creation time: Jul 01, 2022 08:06 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for sharing your view. Let me reply you point wise.

You said — Karna asked for weapon as insisted by surya.

You sound as if Karna was discharging some obedience towards Surya.

But didn’t Surya first tell him not to part with the Kavacha? Did Karna agree? NO.

Didn’t Surya also and explicitly told him to obey the calls of Kunti? Did he agree to either his mother or Surya?

So why did he agree to this suggestion of Surya? Simply because it suited him. Face it, it was Karna’s decision to accept Surya’s advise.

You said — But karna accepted all conditions.

NO. Karna didn’t accept all conditions. He refused to part with Kavacha and Kundala without condition. Conditions imposed by Indra were pre-conditions. Had Karna not liked it, he still had the option to back out just like he did earlier. He accepted the exchange happily.

And what you call as conditions were standard operating procedures for all Divyastra. Even Shiva and Drona said similar things to Arjuna except the one time use deal. Arjuna anyway didn’t use it.

Indra had also forewarned Karna that the weapon may not be able to kill Arjuna. But Karna hoped Indra may be wrong. It was his arrogance.

You said — Maangne par uska koi benefit nhi hua.

You are wrong to say he didn’t get any benefit. He did succeed in killing Ghatotkatch that would have destroyed the entire Kuru Army. Remember that was only the great feat of Karna in the entire Kurukshetra battle. All because of that weapon.

And if you compare this weapon with what he exchanged, you would find it was infinitely more valuable. Tell me this, what great benefit did Karna get while he had the Kavacha? Didn’t he constantly loose battles after battles even after dawning the Kavacha? Did he win against Bhima in Rajsuy Yagna or Arjuna during Swaymvara or against Gandharvas? NO. NO. NO.

He might have thought, the divine weapon may change his fate. He didn’t know only adherence ot Dharma had that capability

You said — Krishna kept Ghatotkatch alive only as a option for wasting that weapon.

NO. Krishna kept Ghatotkatch alive to wreck havoc on Kuru army and he successfuly destroyed a large chunk of that army.

Krishna, indeed said, he would have killed Ghatotkatch. But did he mention when? He could have killed him after Kurukshetra battle was over.

But had Karna not killed him, there would have been no battle to fight.

And do you forget, Indra himself said his weapon may not succeed in killing Arjuna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 01, 2022 08:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Drona did give the knowledge of supreme Brahmasira to Arjuna. What made Arjuna different from others is well summed by Drona. He never stopped learning. And his learning was not one way. He also passed the knowledge to other warriors which we don’t see anyone other than Drona doing.

Then Arjuna got Shiva, Indra and Chitragupta as his guru and his being a desciple of Shiva enabled his claim on Divyastra by all the Gods.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Before-the-imminent-Kurukshetra-war-Shri-Krishna-asked-Arjuna-to-do-tapascharya-and-acquire-Divyastras-Why-didnt-any-5
Creation time: Jul 02, 2022 06:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

NO. Arjuna didn’t use Pashupata.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Before-the-imminent-Kurukshetra-war-Shri-Krishna-asked-Arjuna-to-do-tapascharya-and-acquire-Divyastras-Why-didnt-any-5
Creation time: Jul 02, 2022 10:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Even I gave my answer based on BORI CE.

Answer: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Before-the-imminent-Kurukshetra-war-Shri-Krishna-asked-Arjuna-to-do-tapascharya-and-acquire-Divyastras-Why-didnt-any-5
Creation time: Jul 02, 2022 11:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Chill he never said that he hates lord Krishna.. just because he tried to condemn Arjun doesn't mean that he hates Krishna

Actually it means exactly that. Do you want to know how? Here is what Krishna himself said —

Tomorrow, the gods, the gandharvas, the pishachas, the serpents, the rakshasas and all the worlds will know me as Savyasachi’s well-wisher. He who hates him, hates me too. He who follows him, follows me too. Use your intelligence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of my body.

So now, I ask the same question to you — Do you hate Arjuna (and by extension, Karna)?

Now coming to the rest of you answer. It suggests, you know nothing about Mahabharata the original epic.

  1. Karna’s list of crime is a long one. He not only used abusive words against Draupadi, in fact, he was the one who persuaded Duhashashana to disrobe Draupadi. And read this statement multiple times. He never apologized to Draupadi. And Apology after a heinous crime doesn’t become absolve you of your sins. Otherwise every molester and rapist would ask for forgiveness after his misdeeds.
  2. Jarasandha Fight: Can’t be a parameter to judge comparative strength of Bhimasena and Karna, and certainly doesn’t say anything about the comparative strength of Arjuna and Karna. What speaks about their comparative strength was their battles where in every single battle, Arjuna thrashed Karna and made him flee. Every single one. Read it again. In fact Bhimasena too defeated Karna multiple times. And since Bhimasena and Arjuna never fought you don’t really have a parameter to check here…
    But I would like to clarify about this confusion of Bhima, Karna and Jarasandha. Karna vs Jarasandha wasn’t a serios duel. Jarasandha was testing him and he gave up the battle quickly enough when he realized that Karna may tear him apart. He didn’t do the same with Bhimasena, rather continued to fight till his death. There is a difference when you give up easily and save your life and when you fight for your life knowing giving up is not an option. Do you understand this difference.

Now coming to few of your misconceptions —

You said — I don't think Shri Krishna ever called him an extremely evil soul.

That’s what you think. But Krishna did call him extremely evil soul in Karna Vadha Parva. Here is a direct reference to what he said. Read it yourself —

Karna is evil-minded and extremely injurious towards the Pandavas. O best of men! Kill him today with your sharp arrows. This is your supreme task and there is nothing wrong in it. We applaud this and there is no sin attached to it. O unblemished one! O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that. Suyodhana always thought that he would be saved by Karna and angrily tried to seize me too.647

Karna has always said, “I will defeat the assembled Parthas, Vasudeva and the kings in the great battle.” The evil-minded one has encouraged Dhritarashtra’s evil-souled son and roared in the assembly hall. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! Kill Karna today. In all the evil acts that Dhritarashtra’s son has done towards you, in all of these, the evil-souled and extremely wicked Karna has been present.

And these are not the only instances. There are many more.

You said — infact Krishna himself had praised Karna several times.

No. He didn’t praise him several times. Just twice. First when he wanted him to switch sides and second after the death of Ghatotkatch. He never praised him as a warrior. But he condemned him on multiple occasions. Healso condmened him in Kairata Parva and Udyoga Parva.

You said — He praised Karna's skills and him being the greatest daanveer ever.

WRONG! He never called him daanveer forget greatest. In fact no one in entire Mahabharata ever used the word Daanveer. He did mention Karna used to donate to Brahmins. But that’s it.

Can you show any reference where he called him ‘greatest daanveer’?

You said — And how can such a Daanveer and selfless person be EXTREMELY EVIL SOUL…being Daani is literally one of the most pure thing to be.

There are many criminals today who are calling themselves daanveer, by giving their black money in charity. Charity doesn’t absolve anyone from their henious deeds. If a rapist donates his wealth would you call him pure?

Daani doesn’t mean pure. It simply means a giver. By the way can you tell me what great daan did Karna ever give other than wealth from the treasury?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 08, 2022 06:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If anything is really irresponsible, it is your statement and shows the lack of your understanding of the epic. Gurus were responsible enough to create the greatest warriors of the time on one hand and and deny the right knowledge in the wrong hand. They neither hesitated in making a student better than their own son nor they hesitated in demanding the thumb from impersonator. Gurus were also immune to flattery and could see through deceit of student to curse them and debar them from the knowledge even if they show reverence as a façade to their deceit.

Soldiers die in a battle. That is unfortunate. I wish a world without wars and woes. But we all know that is a wishful thinking. And if there would be war soldiers would die. That is what they all signup for just the way everyone who walks on the road signs-up for the eventuality of meeting an accident (They take insurance) and everyone who is born eventually dies.

But you failed to grasp Mahabharata battle. And remember, not only soldiers died. Even generals died.

A shallow understanding may make you believe it was a family feud. But that would be a superficial understanding. Let me give you a few points to think —

  • Why did other rulers of the land joined in Hastinapur family fued.
  • Do you think if Kauravas and Pandavas were not there —
    • Drona and Drupada strife would have not taken place?
    • Bhishma and Shikandi story would have remained incomplete?
    • Bhurisrava and Satyaki fued would have remained unfinished?
    • Matasya and Trigarth enmity would have ended?

I would recommend you to read the epic properly before commenting.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Lord-Sun-and-Parshuram-had-supported-Karna-in-the-Kurukshetra-battle-what-would-have-happened/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 08, 2022 11:50 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Arjuna defeated Drona, and Bhishma in Matasya battle. No Krishna around. Even they knew they can't fight Arjuna. Shiva himself proclaimed him greatest warrior.

Krishna didn't help Arjuna fight Bhishma. He motivated him to kill him. It wasn't that Arjuna was unable to defeat him. He wasn't willing to kill him. Hope you see the difference.

You might be wondering why I didn't mention Karna. Because he don't deserve to be named alongside them. He was defeated on on multiple occasions not only by Arjuna but by so many people. No Krishna was needed.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-Arjuna-Ashwatthama-Karna-or-Eklavya-Can-you-rank-them/answer/Swetavahana
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 05:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You must understand an evil and arrogant would never have agreed to Krishna's proposal. And he didn't. Becoming the King would have meant letting go of his ambition to prove himself the best archer. Accepting Krishna's proposal meant leaving Duryodhana and his arrogance behind. If Karna would have done that he would have been cleansed. But we knew what happened. Same applies to Ravana. Those with evil mindset think about their ego and not other's welfare.

And yes, you are right both were evils. And Krishna offered similar proposal to both. Do you know, before Karna he offered Kingship to Duryodhana? But Krishana knew with evil dictating them, they wouldn't agree.

Krishna tried for peace. But not at the cost of Dharma.

Do you know with sincere repentance some Robber Ratnakar may become Valmiki and a ruthless Ashoka can become Ashoka Priyadarshi?

But Karna and Ravana weren't supposed to have that fate. They were evils. Just evils.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parsurama-Indra-and-Yudhisthir-believe-Karna-as-a-better-warrior-than-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 07:41 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Now you have started spamming the answers. I have already challenged you enough time to prove your stupid claims and every single time in the past, you ran away.

You don't know Vyas Mahabharata isn't based on YouTube and Vyas never said Karna was greatest warrior. He was a wicked soul.

Next time I will delete your stupid comment

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-was-Karn-the-biggest-warrior-of-all-Kauravas-and-Pandavas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 07:44 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said That weapon was not of much use?

How did you deduce that? And as I said that's the only real contribution of Karna in Kurukshetra. Latest face set karna was incapable of killing Arjun anyway. Why else he needed special weapon to encounter Arjun. Do you see Arjun keeping a special weapon to kill karna? He killed him within ordinary anjalika kabo arrow? Tell me why did karna really needed special weapon to kill Arjuna unless he knew he was incapable?

And if that weapon was of not much use covers was still much less valuable. It would never have killed Arjun, and it never manage to protect Karna from any of his past defeat. It was certainly a better trade of which is evident from the fact that Karna was both happy and content.

You said — Even vaishampayana said indra smiled at his deception. Even in opinion of ved vyas karna was cheated by indra.

Both these are third persons opinion. Tell me what was the opinion of karna? Wasn't he content and pleased? How can an exchange really be a sacrifice? Tell me didn't karna clearly said that I will not give the kavach without the exchange?

In a condense message to the departed people often make remarks to please that may be pleasing to the disease and very often they don't really tally the facts and that is what we see in this remarks which cause that exchanges sacrifices.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 07:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you know Kunti never ask for making any promises like that. Then tell me why karna made a promise like that which was neither Kunti’s wish in the welfare of his own best friend Duryodhan for which karna supposedly refuse to change sides.

Do you know not only karna spared Bhim's life even been spared Karna's life because Arjun had taken an oath to kill him. Had been killed him on the 14th day, would not have got opportunity to uphold his promise. Is promise was way beyond is caliber we can promise moons and stars.

There was a second said of the coin corner did not promise to spare four Pandavas life but told a mother that he will not spare the life of his fifth son. Do you really call this a great work worth an applause?

Now tell me this why did Karna refuse to give Kunti what's she desired? Was he not a danvir?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 08:03 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for your appreciation. Here are answers to your new queries —

You said — so we don't even know that how many times it has been translated and edited by different writers and editors over 5000 years so I don't think it's right to believe everything that's written in a book of incidents that took place over 5000 years old. We don't know the difference between the actual story and the story that we read today.

You obviously don't understand how sruti Smriti tradition works else you wouldn't have said this. Yes their are many variations. Too many. But interestingly it is actually is a blessing in disguise. When you compare thousands of versions and see what is common to them. And that must be the truth. Few authors may have tried to add stories to glorify villains like Karna. But that won't be present on other versions. And that would help you separate truth from fakes. And that is how we know the truth. Some things extremely close to real.

You asked —Could you please tell which source do you use(the book from where you are giving examples)

Glad you asked. This answer will also expand my previous comment. In 1999 a scholar committee was constituted to research on Mahabharata and bring out a critical editions. The Bhandarkar Research Oriental Institute, Pune researched for more than forty years and compared more than 1200 manuscripts in Sanskrit, Bengali, Maithili, Kashmiri, Nepali, Tamil and more and created the Mahabharata CE, based on common text from all of them making it as real as it can get. 40 years, 1200+ variations! And that is my primary source of reference apart from Neelkantha commentary the reputed translation from 16th century. Now you know my credentials.

You said — And DON'T ever compare Karna with present generation criminals and rapists. I understand you are a Karna hater but please don't be such a blind hater…. There are a lot of positive things about Karna.

I am a Karna hater. I am a Ravana hater. I hate all evils. But then, I dont hate Krishna. And I already gave you a reference Krishna said, he was extremely evil and wicked soul. He commandeered Draupadi's disrobing. Krishna called him the trunk of the evil tree. Why do you think, I should follow your advice instead of Krishna. He hated Karna. I hate him. And he was no better than any petty criminal.

You said —you asked about a great donation of karna- Karna literally donated his kavach kundal despite knowing that he is donating it to a god who is his enemy's father.

NO. Karna didn't donate the Kavacha. He exchanged it. His own words. He refused to give the Kavacha unless Indra agreed to give Vasvi Shakti. He was pleased by the exchange. If that was donation, then every shopkeeper is daanveer and keeps donating goods after getting payments.

You said — And his “daanveerta" has been tested many times by Krishna and other gods themselves.

Only in fictions. Never in Vyas Mahabharata. Never.

It is these fake tales that make people like you Karna fan. Can you tell me when Krishna tested his “daanveerta”? I would wait to hear from you.

You said — I am a Karna fan but I don't dislike Arjun and I love Shri Krishna.

That's unfortunate. A Krishna lover is a Karna fan. How are you a Krishna fan if you like him, whom Krishna disliked and wanted killed?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 08:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

More than anything your views are immaterial because they baseless as you don't trust texts and still want to debate.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Lord-Sun-and-Parshuram-had-supported-Karna-in-the-Kurukshetra-battle-what-would-have-happened/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 08:39 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Don't give stupid excuse. Kunti did not ask for boon. Kunti was happy at what karna said.

There is no excuse here. Kunti didn’t ask Karna to spare his sons lives. She came to tell the truth hoping he was side with Dharma. But Naturally she was happy if there is a chance for any of his son to live. It is natural and NOT selfish.

You asked — So what. Was she aspecting for her pandovs to die?

No She wasn’t thinking Pandavas to die. Even Karna knew with Krishna on his side it was time for Kauravas to die. NOT Pandavas. No one thought that for a moment. Not Kunti. And Not Karna.

Then ask yourself again. Why did Kunti reached out to Karna? What did she demanded? Did she ever asked that you spare my four sons? Can you quote a reference?

You said — She thinked before coming to karna that karna can kill his sons except arjuna.

NO She didn’t think this way. Not at least in Vyas Mahabharata. Can you quote with a reference where Kunti thought he can kill is sons except Arjuna? Was there any previous instance where Karna managed to defeat any sons of Kunti in the past? Even one instance? Bhima had certainly defeated Karna before this incident. Arjuna on multiple occasions. Why would Kunti think he can kill his other sons when Krishna sided with him? Please quote a reference where you read this. Can you?

But even if I were to take your words for a moment, which I clearly don’t, did you realize that Kunti wanted to save four Pandvas, out of which two were not even her own son! How can a mother who cared so much for her step sons would not care for Karna? Now think again, why did she came to Karna? What did she ask?

You said — It's true bhima spared life of karna. It does not mean Karna did not spare life rest three. If karna had killed nakul definitely number of his sons would be less than five.

Had Bhima not spared his like, Karna won’t have got a chance at the rest of them. And Karna always maintained in front of everyone that he would kill Pandavas. All of them. He even tried to kill Bhima. I think even a wretch would have spared the life of the brothers of him, who spared his life.

You asked — U want to say agar nakul ya yudhistor mar jate toh kunti bahut khus hoti.

No she would have been tormented. As tormented as she was when Karna was killed. She was a mother, not a trader. And that is why she didn’t come to trade or barter or bargain. She asked Karna to switch sides. Come with his brothers. That way no one would have been killed. Kunti what have been the happiest mother. So tell me — Who robbed Kunti of this happiness?

But for Karna, everything was a bargain and exchange. You need my Kavacha, I will take Vasvi shakti in exchange. You want me to come to your side, I won’t but I will spare the life of four sons in exchange. I will not kill those people whom I have never been able to defeat in the past. I will spare the lives of those who are in Krishna’s protection and even I know I can’t harm them. I would spare the life of Bhima, who had defeated me even in the past and I have never been able to win against him.

You said — Ur comment that Kunti herself did not ask for boon was illogical for me.

You are free to your opinion. But that doesn’t change the fact. She didn’t ask for a boon to spare the lives of his four sons. She didn’t.

So instead of the logic, why don’t you check out what did Kunti really say to Karna? If you don’t have the source, I can provide you.

If you find this illogical, then you must find Karna illogical to assume that no harm would reach Pandavas while Krishna is on his side. And you find Krishna’s power to protect Pandavas illogical. Well it is your choice if you want to ignore Krishna and the Ved Vyas and value your own opinion.

By the way, can you tell me what is foundation of you ‘logical’ opinion?

You asked — Or u can say nakul was far better than Karna.

As a person, definitely. Far Far better. Loyal.

As a warrior, I can’t say. What I can say, is he didn’t fled while battling Drupad in Gurudakshina battle. And he didn’t fled while fighting Gandharvas. And He didn’t fled while fighting Trigartha. He stood and fought. He was certainly more courageous and respectable warrior. And Karna couldn’t reply to Kripa’s question during Matasya battle — In which battle you have defeated any Pandava? Can you answer this question?

Can you tell me why do you think, Karna was a better warrior than Nakula? Did he ever fight for Dharma? Did he ever fight to protect someone else? Nakula certainly did.

You said — I hate karna. But it doesn't mean I cannot give credit to his few good works.

No. You don’t hate Karna, else you won’t come in defense of his so called ‘few good works’

QUESTIONS FOR YOU?

  1. Why didn’t Karna gave Kunti what she asked for when even Surya suggested Karna to do the same thing — Follow Kunti’s advise and switch sides?
  2. Why did Karna spare the life of only four sons and not five. Did Kunti ask for it? Was Kunti ok with Karna killing Arjuna? Had Karna killed Arjuna, would she have been happy? Or less sad than if he had killed other three sons?

Can you answer these question?

You said — I cannot change my opinion.

That is the main problem. People can’t change their opinion even against overwhelming evidence that lie in front of them. How else do you think people consider Duryodhana great? Because they can’t change their opinion. They think Karna and Duryodhana were great. Why else do you think they call Draupadi whore? Because great, daanveer, archer, Karna called her a whore. So she must be a whore.

Do you know what these people say when they are contradicted by giving proper evidence? They say “Jisko jo bolna hai wo bolega — I don’t care about Ved Vyas. I don’t care about evidence. I don’t care about reference. I care about what I think.”. Yes, this is what these people say. No I.

Let us summarise —

  1. Karna clearly told Indra, I will NOT give my Kavacha with an exchange. During the exchange he was happy and content. This is Vyas reference. He succeeded in killing Ghatotkatch, while his Kavacha didn’t achieve anything for him in his entire life. But you say “Jisko Jo bolna hai wo bolega. He didn’t get a good deal”
  2. You say the weapon was not good enough to kill Arjuna and forget, Karna wasn’t a good enough warrior to kill Arjuna. Krishna said so. Shiva said so. Parshurama said so. But you would say “Jisko Jo bolna hai wo bolega…”
  3. Arjuna didn’t use any special weapon to kill Karna. Why did Karna needed a weapon to kill Arjuna? And why was he pleased to gain the weapon despite knowing that it may not kill? But you can still say “Jisko jo bolna hai wo bolega…”
  4. Kunti asked Karna to come on the sides of Dharma. She wanted to tell her the truth. She didn’t ask him to spare the lives of his sons. She never said Karna may kill his other sons. But then yes, Jisko Jo bolna hai…
  5. You find Kunti was interested saving his (step) son Nakula’s life. Of course you are correct. That is the kind of mother she was. But you miss the point, Karna too was his son, whom she loved so dearly.
  6. You think, Karna made a great “daanveer” by asking “exchange” from Indra and giving something to Kunti that she didn’t ask for and denying to give what she asked for? You don’t need to change your opinion, after all Jisko Jo Sonchna hai…

You said — Gandhi ji has haters far more than his lovers.

WRONG.

Gandhiji has far more lovers than haters. Yes, haters are loud, but that doesn’t make them more. Do animals hate Gandhiji? I don’t know. What I know is it doesn’t matter. They are not intelligent enough to understand Gandhiji to either love or hate him.

Those who hate Gandhiji are ignorant. They lack proper knowledge and study. There opinions don’t count. They are themselves victims of a propaganda.

Don’t believe me, let me give you an example. Here is a partial list of Gandhiji’s fan —

  • Shubash Chandra Bose → called him father of the nation.
  • Ho Chi Minh, father of Vietnam → called himself as the desciple of Gandhji.
  • Dr Rajendra Prasad → compared him to Bhagirathi Ganga
  • Albert Einstien
  • Barak Obama
  • Dalali Lama
  • Martin Luther King Jr
  • Steve Jobs
  • Shri Acharya Sharma Shukla

Now can you start counting the haters of Gandhiji? Any great leader from any walks of life?

No, that is just a false perception. Gandhji’s follower even among common man far outnumbers few haters who are active on social media and it reminds me of a interesting line

मजलिस में बैठे जुगनू सारे,

और फैसला हुआ है, कि सूरज को बदला जाएगा।

All the fireflies have sat together and decided that Sun would be replaced. Ha! Those millions of fireflies DO NOT MATTER.

But I truly hope you would answer all me questions point by point that I have raised in this disucssion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 10:31 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I sincerely believe you won’t be disappointed.

Do share your review once you have read it.

If you want a author signed copy, you may send a DM.

Answer: https://theaccursedgod.quora.com/Is-Accursed-God-accurately-based-on-Mahabharata-1
Creation time: Jul 13, 2022 10:31 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No. These great people didn't watch TV show. But none of them are Ved Vyas. None of them claimed that there work is authentic. Dinkar wrote a poetry and didn't call it history.

You don't need to follow what people say on Quora, but to understand the real truth, you have to read the the actual epic and not some poetry or essay. Can you do that?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-like-Karna-I-dont-think-he-deserves-love-and-liking/answer/Avichal-Sharma-12
Creation time: Jul 14, 2022 04:21 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Wow.

Let people read your reply and find out who is the angry one here. I didn't start the debate. You raised questions. You challenged my assertions. You tried to push your views in my answer and now you claim I am imposing myself? You are polite! Not really. Plain abusive.

And I don't abuse people. I just shatter their propaganda, politely. I didn't call you stupid. You did. Go back and read. Then comeback and tell me where did I abuse you?

To answer your questions, no, it's not a crime not to hate Karna. It's not crime to praise Ravana or Karna or Kasab. It's a free country. But what makes you think calling an evil an evil is crime? Why do you think reminding the world what Krishna said is a crime? What makes you think defending ancient civilization is a crime? Why do you think, saying wrong as WRONG is wrong?

I know there are many Gandhi haters and it may not be a crime. But then how hating those who abuse my father of the nation becomes a crime? Why do you think, I won't rise up to shatter their propaganda? Why do you think, I owe to those fools any respect who don't know how to respect our great heroes, our culture?

I never claimed I am the only knowledgeable person, but then I refer to Vyas epic and not what some Mr X or some Mr Y. And I know more of epic to know Bhima didn't call Karna a dog, he called him unworthy. Those who say otherwise don't understand metaphors.

You said Karna died like a Dog. Chariotless and weaponless. You said that was Arjuna's first and last encounter with Karna. I have to say, YOU ARE WRONG. Arjuna had defeated Karna again and again and yet again. Everytime. Karna had just lost the chariot not the weapons. He died fighting. And some Mr X or great artist may not have told you this, but that is the truth.

You said Indra smiled and Pandavas smiled. True. I never deny. They were happy. Now the extremely wicked and evil man had lost the divine protection. Even I would be happy. I can't and won't stop you for feelings sorry for that evil molester who tried to burn down an entire family with their mother in fire.

You said Gods showered flowers. Demi gods. I don't have time to debate on the actions of those unnamed crowds. What I care about is the views of Krishna and Mahadeva and everyone else is insignificant dwarves. But I won't stop you from clinging to their action and forgetting what Krishna, Shiva and Parshurama said.

You talk about kmowledge, but what did you do in the last remark? Just repeated many times that you have right to your opinion and I have no right to contradict yours. Wow!

You could have used the opportunity to respond to my questions. You didn't because your assertions were baseless. Can you answer them?

  1. When did Kunti originally asked Karna? Did he gave her what she wished?
  2. Did Kunti actually asked him to spare the life of her four sons?
  3. Did Kunti ever said Karna could kill her sons?
  4. Who was really responsible for Kunti's torment?
  5. Why didn't Karna followed Kunti's advise.

If you can't or won't answer that's your choice. You may leave and live with your views.

You are welcome to debate, welcome to seek answer or cross.

But please avoid being abusive like your last post.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2022 11:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Again long message, but no content. No reply to any single question I raised. I know why. They are difficult to answer.

Yes you call my statement stupid but then you are not abusive. Can you tell me then how I became abusive without using any UN civic language with you? Can you tell me where I became abusive? Is that not agreeing to your statements makes me abusive? If I ask for valid references and ask you cross questions does that become imposing? According to you the polite thing for me would be to accept your opinions which may be here say and may not match with their epic? Sorry can't do that.

I don't know any Pawan or Mrinal, but as per Ved Vyas and BORI CE, Karna didn't die weaponless. He died fighting.

And finally I agree with you. Thinking of everyone is different. There are Arjuna haters. There are Karna's fan. There are those who support and stand for terrorists like Kasab and those who seek good side of worst criminals and defend the rapists like Dhananjoy Chakraborty and those rapists of Nirbhaya, or the molester of… Yes, there will always be people like them.

There would always be people who would bend, misinterpret and distort the ancient epic to prove themselves right. They would tell you Karna died weaponless, or Bheem called him a dog or Kunti was afraid of karna that he might kill his sons, aur call Draupadi a whore because karna said so. For them Dronacharya was wrong Parshuram was wrong, Bhishma, Kunti, Bheema everyone wrong, even Krishna wrong to prove Karna good. They would even call Epic wrong and interpolated just for one person, one evil person. They would post of sacrifices of karna but will not be able to count them. They will tell you that Karna spared the life of four Pandavas but won't be able to answer why he did not switch and why he did not spare the life of all five Pandavas? These people won't answer these uncomfortable questions as it would break their narrative and lead them to self doubt.

They would tell you I am right because so and so person had told me these things. They will tell you I believe in myself because others told me the stories. They will tell you I am right because many others feel that way. Yes there would always be people like them who would go to any extent to justify villains.

It's good you believe in yourself. I wish you best of luck.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 14, 2022 07:24 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Don't forget what Krishna said — it would be a mistake to consider them as separate entities. Consider Arjuna a part of Krishna's being. That's what Krishna wanted. Who am I to judge?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-has-killed-4-Pandavas-excluding-Arjuna-how-would-that-affect-the-Kurukshetra-war/answer/Swetavahana
Creation time: Jul 15, 2022 08:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You still didn't tell me, why didn't Karna gave Kunti what she desired?

Ok, tell me this, How sparing Bhima was a good deed? Didn't Bhima killed all brother's of Duryodhana? Didn't Karna promise Duryodhan to protect him and his family? Didn't many Kauravas die protecting him? Was it a good thing or bad?

Did you ask that girl where is it mentioned that Karna was a daanveer? What was his great daan?

Think!

Opinions should be formed based on facts and not just on what Mr X or Miss Y said.

Try thinking about the questions I asked ( even if you don't want to answer them). In those questions, you may find answer. And you don't have to agree with me. Just agree with Truth, Dharma and Krishna.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 15, 2022 01:14 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You still didn’t reply my two simple questions —

  1. Why didn’t Karna gave Kunti what she demanded?
  2. Why did he made an exception for Arjuna? Why did he want to kill Arjuna?

Now you accepted Kunti never asked Karna to spare the life of his four sons. He wanted all six of them alive. That is what I wanted to tell you. But then, that begs the question that I asked you earlier —

3. Who was responsible for Kunti’s suffering? Why didn’t Karna gave Kunti what she asked?

4. How was sparing Bhima’s life a good work and not a treachery towards his life long benefactor?

Karna gave his reasons — He thought, people would think he is afraid because he knew no one could win against Arjuna and Krishna.

And I have no comment for those who know the facts and still like to create sympathetic opinion about evils. You have right to your opinion.

But if you really trust your opinion and that of Mr X or Miss Y, why don’t you answer my simple questions. (I have made them bold this time).

Can you actually answer these questions? For once? Or at least tell me that you won’t answer them because that would hurt your opinion?

You may check out my detailed opinion about Karna and his promise to spare four sons here —

Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to If Karna has killed 4 Pandavas (excluding Arjuna), how would that affect the Kurukshetra war?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 16, 2022 12:55 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am telling what Krishna said. If you separate the two, Krishna was superior. But when you separate the two, as per Krishna, it would be a wrong judgement.

Not refuting what you said, just iterating what Krishna said.

Content:

Yes. Here is what he said —

Tomorrow, the gods, the gandharvas, the pishachas, the serpents, the rakshasas and all the worlds will know me as Savyasachi’s well-wisher. He who hates him, hates me too. He who follows him, follows me too. Use your intelligence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of my body.

Creation time: Jul 16, 2022 01:26 AM PDT
