Answer Comment

Content:

No Karna couldn’t have hunted them even one by one. Even if one Pandava was killed, Arjuna wouldn’t have given him time enough to reach his other brother. Plus, he had a tought fight with each of them. Even Nakula wasn’t an easy catch.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-has-killed-4-Pandavas-excluding-Arjuna-how-would-that-affect-the-Kurukshetra-war/answer/Swetavahana
Creation time: Jul 16, 2022 01:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

At last you tried to answer a few question and I can see you opinion slightly changing. That’s a good start.

Here are few points that have no ifs or buts.

  • Karna hated Pandavas for no reason for a time much longer than he had any knowledge about his relation to anyone. This hate was unwarranted.
  • He got to know his relation with Kunti when Surya told him during Kundala Harana Parva. But no one ever told Pandavas about the relationship, so then had no idea.

Now coming to your points —

You said — Number of Kunti’s son would have reduced to 4, 3 or 2.

No. Worst case he could have killed only one son. Most likely Nakul. He was the first to get his way. He would never have got the chance to reach Yudhishira.

And yes, he may have died like Abhimanyu or Drupad. And that would still have been Dharma for Nakula. That would be the natural price for upholding Dharma. But that would have ended the war that moment itself. Duryodhana knew it. I hope you got the point.

You said — Duryodhan asked krishna that who is more blessed. Kauravs or pandovs. Pandovs suffered before and after war.

Duryodhana never asked this question. This a folklore. Pandavas indeed suffered. So did Rama. But they became immortal legends for their great deeds. And suffering was a small price.

But yes, Karna did betray him. Again and Again.

You said — Sparing life of pandovs is in favour of dharma. Just like bhishma and dron did. Yes he did his best to kill arjuna. Arjuna was most powerful. That was also duryodhan main concentration. Karna and bhishma and dron were never right in favouring duryodhan.

No all the three cases are different. Bhishma and Drona always favoured Duryodhana. They always told him what is good for him. Both died for him.

Bhishma honestly explained even before the war he wouldn’t kill Pandavas. It is not a treachery if you make a pre-condition.

Drona did his best for Duryodhana even tried to kill his best and most beloved disciple.

What Karna did was a treachery. He always made Duryodhana believe that he would kill all Pandavas. He lied to him.

You said — Karna cud not have changed sides. Actually woh vibhishan wala case ho jata. Because he eated salt of duryodhan just like dron. How he can betrayed that salt.

I know Karna couldn’t have become Vibhishana. Vibhishana traded his brother for humanity. He got in the good grace of Shri Rama. Not many get a chance to be with Shri Rama.

Karna was worse. He ignored Dharma. He ignored humanity. He ignored relationship. For what? Do you think because he was loyal to Duryodhana?

Think again!

  • Why didn’t he participate in the war for the first ten days? Was that in the benefit for Duryodhana? Where was his loyalty?
  • Why did he promised Kunti that he wouldn’t kill four Pandavas? What that loyalty?
  • Why did he never tell Duryodhana that he won’t kill 4 Pandavas? Was that loyalty or trechary?
  • Why did he made an exception for Arjuna? For Duryodhana? Think Again!

You said — Arjuna was most powerful. That was also Duryodhan main concentration.

WRONG! Duryodhana’s main enemies were Yudhishthira and Bhima. Yudhishira was the contender to the throne. Bhima was his nemesis. Bhima, and not Arjuna, had taken an oath to kill all Kauravas. Had he killed either of the two it would have favored Duryodhana.

So question for you, Why did Karna made an exception for Arjuna?

You said — But I don't agree with ur opinion and analysis u provide me in the link you posted at bottom.

What part do you disagree? They are all reference based. Even Duryodhana didn’t disagree. Better respond back on the right thread.

You said — Vaisampanya said Karna was established in Dharama

In the entire epic there have been many exaggerations when it comes to describe a person. Even Duryodhana is praised by Gods at points. Many Kings and princess were called Indra among men minutes before they were killed. And they did nothing great. Don’t go just on hollow words. Validate those words against the actions —

  • Karna refused to follow Kunti’s advise for the sake of Duryodhana but refuses to fight for him for the first ten days.
  • He gives a boon to Kunti, which was not in favor of Duryodhana and never tells Duryodhana about his oath.
  • He actually tries to kill Bhima on multiple occasions.
  • He deserts Duryodhana to save his own wretched life from Gandharva.

Question is where is the loyalty? And if it wasn’t loyalty why did he not spare the life of the fifth Pandava?

Think! And your opinion changes.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Truthlogy-call-Karna-dealveer/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 16, 2022 04:48 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You are right Karna had no moral obligation to protect Draupadi. And why should he when others too were silent.

But then no one else asked her to be stripped naked. No one asked her a whore. No one asked her to chose other husband and no one distorted God's definition of Dharma.

The point is,it is wishful to expect Dharma from someone who had no moral compass.

You said Pandavas were his archarivals. Why should he protect their wife? Maybe you have a point. Maybe you missed the definition of morality and humanity. But tell me did Karna protect the wife of his best friend from Gandharvas?

You gave a hierarchy as to who should have first protected Draupadi. A precedence order. Now tell me did Pandavas protect the wives of their archrival? Where did they stand in that precedence order?

But I am wrong. I am seeking Dharma from a complete evil man.

Karna didn't become evil, because he didn't protect Draupadi. He was evil, that's why he tried to molest her. He helped neither friends nor foes.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-the-Mahabharata-why-didnt-Karna-protect-Draupadi-instead-taking-part-in-harassing-her/answer/Dhinesh-Dass
Creation time: Jul 17, 2022 12:05 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I pity you. You didn't get chance to read or underetand the epic. Can you show me where Shiva or Krishna called Arjuna an evil? Then maybe I can so you what you missed.

Quiz for you. See the below line

He who loves him, loves me. Know him as my half body.

  • Who said this?
  • about whom the speaker was talking
  • Do you hate him or love him?

And next time you reply, try to be a bit civil in your communication.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: Jul 19, 2022 01:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

This answer was given a year ago but somehow people got it removed. Posting again

Great! You still couldn’t reply the direct question.

So let me answer those questions and contradict them if you can —

  1. Among the below referred names tell me who the only person was who devas like Vishnu (a.k.a Krishna) and Shiva didn’t wanted killed — like Ravan,Tarkasur,Arjuna,Mahihshasura and Hiranyakashyapu, Karna? (HINT: There is only one name)
    ANSWER: ARJUNA. His name can’t be used in the list of people divinity wanted dead to establish Dharma! So you can’t use his name along with Raavana, Tarkasura etc. Karna fits in, because he was evil as said by Krishna, Parshurama, Drona.
  2. Between Karna and Arjuna one has been referred as evil and wicked on multiple occasions; other has been referred Nishpaap (sinless). Can you guess whose who? (This will tell how much of epic do you know)
    Answer: Karna has been referred evil on multiple occasions. Arjuna has never been referred by evil.
  3. You status says Karna was the greatest warrior and greatest character of the epic. You failed to mention, which epic? Can you quote any reference from Mahabharata to substantiate your claim that he was the greatest warrior and greatest epic? Or you refer to another epic.
    ANSWER: NEVER. The grand epic Mahabharata including both ancient and BORI CE not once referred Karna as the greatest warrior of the greatest character of the epic. He is often referred as a coward who flee the battles and conspire.

Now time for your answers, again.

You again asked — I have already said that Krishna wanted trio of Bhishma Drona and Karna to die in Kurukshetra War. So this doesn't make them evil at all

But you didn’t bother to see, I already replied. All the three needed to die to establish Dharma. But Krishna and epic times and again referred only one of them evil. You know, even if you are unwilling to accept — Karna!

And that is the difference.

You said — Karna to die in Kurukshetra War. So this doesn't make them evil at all. And Karna was never in any conspiracies of Duryodhana like lac house.

Sorry to bust you myth. But Karna was involved in every conspiracy including lac house. It is present both in KMG and BORI and has cross-references. And you would have known it had you bothered to read the epic. You know it but you put a blind eye — that’s the only way someone could like Karna.

You said —dyut sabha incident everyone was culprit there.

Agree. Yudhishthira was guilty of putting his kingdom and family as a bait in what he thought was a family game. He lost himself before losing his wife and perhaps never thought he would be molested. And perhaps no one would have stooped to such depth till a man with dirty mind suggested to strip a woman in public. And that is the difference. Yudhishthira was foolish to trust his family. Karna was evil.

And that is what epic said. Krishna didn’t say Yudhishthira would be killed. But he did say Karna would be killed. Do you think you are more intelligent than Krishna?

You said — Lord Shiva and Lord Bramha called Karna a Hero just like Bhishma and Drona and said Karna deserves to be worshipped in heaven…

Have you read the epic? What was the conclusion of Shiva’s and Brahma’s statement? That Karna need to be killed. Shiva decreed it. Can you guess why? The message is given in epic. Krishna, Parshurama, Ved Vyas, Drona, Ahwatthama all of them said that — He was evil. Haven’t you read that? And where did you read that Shiva or Brahma said that Karna is a hero and should be worshipped? Here is what they said (Read carefully) —

Brahma and Ishana760 spoke these words to the lord of the thirty gods. ‘The victory of the great-souled Vijaya is certain. He is spirited, powerful and brave. He is skilled in the use of weapons and is rich in austerities. Great energy is manifested in him, especially in dhanurveda. Through his greatness, he is capable of overcoming destiny. He is capable of controlling and overcoming the worlds. When the two Krishnas are angered, no one is capable of standing before them. Those two bulls among men are truly the creators of the universe. They are the ancient and supreme rishis Nara and Narayana. 761 Those two scorchers of enemies control everything and cannot be controlled by anyone. Karna, foremost in the worlds, is a bull among men. Vaikartana is brave and valiant. But let the two Krishnas be victorious. With Drona and Bhishma, let him762 obtain the great world of heaven and the worlds of the Vasus and the Maruts.’

  • So now tell me where did Shiva said he should be worshipped?
  • Where did Shiva said he isn’t evil?

Please don’t distort the facts! Shiva never called Karna sinless. He just called him brave and valiant and every warrior of Mahabharata ascended to heaven. Even Duryodhana and Duhsasana. So Karna was as sinless as Duryodhana and Duhsasasna.

You asked — by your logic you disrespected statement of Bramha and Shiva by calling Karna evil.

Not at all. Krishna said Karna was evil. Shiva and Brahma willed that he should be slain. Karna himself said he is the responsible for the destruction of the earth (due to war). And they didn’t say he was not evil.

Did Shiva ever blessed Karna? No. He willed him to die. Did Krishna call Karna his inseparable part? NO

Did Shiva himself blessed Arjuna? Did Shiva willed his victory? Did Krishna called Arjuna his inseparable part? YES

MAIN DIFFERENCE — KARNA AND ARJUNA

Karna was called evil on multiple occasion by multiple prominent people. Trinity wanted him to lose and die. No one ever called Arjuna an evil. And that is the difference.

You said — And indirectly comparing Arjun with Supreme Lord Rama comparing mortal with God 😅😅.

Do you understand comparisons? I also compared parent being proud and fan of his child and a person being fan of his own creation. I wanted to drive the idea by giving examples that it is possible that God would be a fan of his devotee and someone great. Remember Indra’s Vajra is made up of a mortals bones! So I didn’t compare Arjuna with Lord Rama. I gave examples. And I think if you revisit my answer (which I didn’t edit) you should understand it better. By the way read the above text to see what Shiva called Arjuna. Now I am sure you must have to plot something to condemn Shiva.

You asked — Yes, Karna is Greatest warrior and Greatest Character of epic. Karna was called hero by Supreme trinity(Bramha, Shiva and Krishna) so what's wrong if I call Karna a hero.

Do you understand the significance of ‘est’ suffix at the end of the word like great or brave? It is absolute superlative term. Did trinity called Karna greatest? No Shiva and Brahma praised him before condemning him to death sentence. And Krishna positively called him evil.

So let me ask you again — Where did you read he was the greatest warrior and the greatest character? Ok. We both know you can’t provide reference. So tell me how a warrior who fled battle after battle was the greatest warrior? How a person who tried to strip a woman in public became the greatest character? How a person who fled leaving his best friend and family women behind at the mercy of his enemies can either be termed as greatest warrior or greatest character — greater than even Krishna!

So you have no reference to prove your point. And you would like to contradict all the epic reference where he was called evil. Sure you have read the epic well!

I think this concludes our question answer, because you couldn’t answer a single question I asked and only repeated your points despite of the fact that I answered them all on the basis of actual epic reference.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-still-support-Karna-when-he-was-exposed-badly-by-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra/answer/DP-IND-SA
Creation time: Jul 19, 2022 01:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Thanks for you suggestions and liking my answer. These days it is getting rare to find a polite commenters who would discuss without losing their civic ways. You are polite and I thank your for being so.

As you might have noticed in the preface of my answer, I differentiate between a warrior and a war monger or a fighter. A warrior fights with ethics, a war-monger fights for lust and without ethics.

You said — There were some invincible warriors like Karna who deserves a place in your beautiful answer.

First things first, the word invincible CAN’T be applied to Karna. Invincible means someone too powerful to be defeated. Karna was anything but that.

He was defeated by Drupad during Gurudakshina battle, by Arjuna during Draupadi’s swaymvar, by Bhima during Rajsuy yagna, by Gandharva’s during Gosha Yatra, again by Arjuna during Matasya battle, multiple times by Satyaki, Bhima and Arjuna during Kurukshetra. We can’t call him an invincible warrior.

And I believe Karna has already received more honor than he ever deserved. He can surely live without I giving him a place. For me evils are war monger and not warriors.

You Quoted what Krishna said

Yes, I have read this. But let us understand their is always a deeper purpose in what Krishna says. Krishna told Arjuna that Yudhishira doesn’t have a Kshatriya quality, which was true, but then he told Yudhishthira that Shalya is more powerful than Bhishma and Karna and no one in three world can defeat him other than Yudhishthira? Does that mean Yudhishthira was more powerful than Karna or Arjuna? NO. It was meant to excite enterprise in Yudhishthira, encouraging him to take the up the job.

Similarly Krishna says Radheya cannot be killed by any enemy, even the gods, including Vasava. But then even he knew Radheya was defeated again and again and yet again my multiple warriors I named above. Just like he needed Yudhishthira to realize no one other than him can Kill Shalhya, he said this Arjuna that he shouldn’t hesitate in killing Karna. You may verify the fact by looking at the last line of your own quoted text. He doesn’t doubt Arjuna can kill him.

No list of mine that includes honorable people like Rama can also include evils molester and murderers like Karna.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 19, 2022 11:08 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Agree. And unlike Karna, none of them begged for recess.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Karna-has-killed-4-Pandavas-excluding-Arjuna-how-would-that-affect-the-Kurukshetra-war/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 19, 2022 11:14 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Dear Pranav,

Thanks for your response. I am aware that one of the Dhritrashtra's son was Karna, but he wasn't prominent enough to be named separately. Here is how it is mentioned in Maharashtra —

Four of Dhritarashtra’s sons were chief—Duryodhana, Duhshasana, Vikarna and Chitrasena.

Can you point any the great deeds of this Karna?

You said —Sir and also karna's real name was vasusena, when he lost his kavas and kundal, then he was called karna. During fight with drupada he had kavas and kundal..( KARNA= PEELER OF ONE'S OWN SHIELD)

NO.

He got the name Vaikartana for cutting his shield. Here is the reference —

He grasped his sharp sword and began to cut up his entire body. On seeing Karna cut up his own body, the gods, humans, danavas and masses of siddhas began to roar, because despite the pain, there were no distortions on his face. Celestial drums were sounded and divine flowers were showered down from above, at the sight of Karna, the brave man, smiling repeatedly as he cut up his own body with the sword. Having sliced off the divine armour from his body, while it was still wet, he gave it to Vasava. Then he sliced off and gave the earrings. Because of this deed, Karna came to be known as Vaikartana.

Also see, he was already Karna at this point. Again recall Karna is called Karna in Adi Parva, Draupadi's Swyamvar and other places before Kundal Harana. Here is an example from Draupadi swyamvar where Arjuna says —

O Karna! I am not the science of weapons personified. Nor am I the powerful Rama. I am only a Brahmana, chief among warriors and supreme among those who have the knowledge of weapons. Through my preceptor’s grace, I have become skilled in the use of brahma and paurandara weapons.”

It is possible that Karna defeated Drupada at a later date. But that doesn't mean he couldn't be defeated earlier.

And even if you want to believe Karna didn't participate, it just proves he was thankless for not helping his friend in the very first battle after being crowned. It doesn't prove him a great warrior. And he had so many defeats in life to be called invincible.

You may not read or reply this message immediately, but may read it someday and maybe this helps some other readers.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-best-warrior-from-Hindu-epics/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2022 12:11 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What a fake answer? Can you highlight one fake point here?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Kunti-have-real-love-for-Karna-or-was-it-just-a-fake-show-off-to-save-her-all-5-sons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 21, 2022 01:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry just saw the comment but feel an answer is still justified and valid.

Ekalavya would never have been great warrior, with or without Drona. He could have been an archer but not a warrior. He could be a millitant but not an army officer, for which you need descipline, restraint and proper training.

Arjuna was savvyasachi, ambidextrous. Eklavya lost one thumb. He still had chance to be as great as Arjuna. Was he? Could he? He was defeated by Madri's son.

And Krishna's friendship didn't depend on Drona's endorsement. It depended on your adherence to Dharma. Remember Krishna's motto Paritranaya Sadhunama, Vinaschyas Dushkritaam. He destroyed Eklavya. Period.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-will-you-want-to-be-Eklavya-or-Arjuna-of-Mahabharat-Why/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 25, 2022 09:00 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I would recommend you not to read text literally but also cross verify with all the incidents.

You asked — Ekalvya gave his right thumb as a gurudakhina to drona who never taught him anything. Who said ekalvya didn't had discipline?

In the question lies the answer. You are right, Drona didn’t teach him anything. Not only this, he declined to teach him. And yet Eklavaya introduced himself as the disciple of Drona. Do you call this honest? Dedicated? or Discipline?

You said — Krishna said — For thy good, he was slain by me on the field of battle. Ekalvya always lived his life following his dharma. Krishna killed him during a fight. That's not made him adharmi.

What do you think “thy good” mean here? Do you want to say that he killed him without his fault, because he may have been a challenger to Arjuna? Then, perhaps you haven’t read it full. Let me share it —

Partha! Had Ekalavya possessed his thumb, the gods and the danavas, with the rakshasas and the serpents, would never have been able to defeat him in a battle. His fist was firm and he could continuously shoot, throughout the day and night. How could a mere human have looked at him? It is for your sake that I killed him in the field of battle. I killed the powerful king of Chedi in your presence. He was capable of conquering all the gods and the asuras in a battle. O tiger among men! I have been born to slay him and also all the others who hate the gods, with your help, and desiring the welfare of the worlds. Hidimba, Baka and Kirmira have been brought down by Bhimasena. They were the equals of Ravana and destroyed the sacrifices of brahmanas. Similarly, Alayudha, who used maya, was slain by Hidimba’s son.

Here Krishna informs —

  1. Ekalavya was as powerful as Shishupal, whom Krishna too called powerful enough of defeating all Gods and Asura.
  2. Krishna said he is born to slay him and others who hate Gods (Vinashchayasa Dushkritam) and for the welfare of the world (Paritranaya Sadhunama). Yes, This statement of Krishna does explicitly condemn him Adharmi.
  3. Now check out whom else does Krishna mention along side Ekalavya? Shishupala, Hidimba, Baka, Kimira and Ravana? Which of these are not among evils.
  4. Now let us come to “thy good”. It is also explained in the above reference. She Krishna says — “I have been born to slay him and also all the others who hate the gods, with your help, and desiring the welfare of the worlds.”
    1. Notice, Krishna talks about Arjuna’s help. Can you see for what purpose?
    2. Krishna helps Arjuna to help himself to help establish Dharma.

You said — Krishna praised Ekalavya’s valour.

Yes. But Krishna also said, with his armor no one in the three worlds could have defeated Karna, not even Arjuna and Krishna. But Karna was defeated by Arjuna (without Gandiva) in swaymvar battle, by Bhima in Rajsuy yagna and Gandharvas in Ghosha yatra. Were these people out of the three world? Was Shiva and Parshurama wrong to say no one could defeat Arjuna?

Let us see what more Krishna said. He at one point mentions about Yudhishthira —

He is established in the strength of the brahman, but he does not possess a great deal of strength of the kshatriya variety.

But then he tells Yudhishthira —

Barring you, there is no other man in all of heaven or earth. O descendant of the Kuru lineage! In a battle, there is no one else who can kill the angry king of Madra.

He also says Shalya was more powerful than Karna, Duryodhana and Bhishma.

Do you agree? If you look carefully Krishna statements are to motivate people and doesn’t always reflect facts.

The fact is —

  • Ekalayva, despite of explicit refusal by Drona, continued to claim to be his disciple which was an insult to Drona’s explicit directives.
  • Ekalavya was killed because he was an evil.
  • He couldn’t have become a great archer without Drona. To be a great warrior you need to also have the know how of Divyastra which he couldn’t have achieved.
  • Krishna’s endorsement never depended on Drona’s. Both went against him and Krishna explained why.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-will-you-want-to-be-Eklavya-or-Arjuna-of-Mahabharat-Why/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 26, 2022 08:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for a very late answer. Guess didn’t get notification for it. Putting the answer for completion of discussion.

You said — 99.989% to Karna.

I am not sure under what marking system you would mark Karna so high?

Did you consider Valour? Karna fled battles after battles. He fled leaving his best friend behind, his brothers behind. Doesn’t that qualify for marks deduction? His brothers, sons, friends died and he fled. Does he still get almost same marks as heroes who fought risking their lives for strangers and heroes who never lost? In Arjuna-Karna battle, Arjuna won every time. Does Karna still complete with him on marginal scale of 0.01%? In Karna vs X, Karna would hardly score 50%. In Arjuna vs X , Arjuna score 100%. So please tell your marking criteria.

Did you consider strength of character? Arjuna was considered a dedicated student well appreciated by his gurus Drona and Shiva. Karna was considered wicked and deceitful by both his gurus Drona and Parshurama. Karna rushed to burn a bride alive, Arjuna rushed to save the lives of his worst enemies. Does that count?

You said — Bheeshma some where mentions that matsya and Virat war is not equal to Mahabhart Yudh.

Does it matter? What matters in Karna said he would kill Arjuna today. Arjuna killed his brother in front of him. He fled. Twice. Then couldn’t flee for a third time. He was rendered unconscious.

And Bhishma did mention Karna is not 1/16 of Arjuna. So what is 99.99/16? I think, I gave more marks to Karna than he deserved.

You said — Krishna mentioned that no one could kill karna in fare battle even Tridev.

NO. A BIG NO. KRISHNA NEVER SAID THAT.

He didn’t talk about fair battle. He said Karna can’t be defeated with his Kavacha. And is having such a divine Kavacha fair? Without Kavacha, Krishna called Karna as a poisonless snake and extinguished fire. He also said sun and moon may change their direction but Karna could never defeat Arjuna.

Do you like to know Krishna’s ranking of Karna? He said Shalya is superior to Karna and Bhishma. And only Yudhishthira can defeat him.

So let us not go on words. Reason. And tell when Karna proved better or even remotely equivalent to Arjuna?

You said — When he defited Arjuna on final day.

Karna never defeated Arjuna. He died on the last day.

You said — Why Krishna and Indra are worried to save arjuna.

No one ever said he was worried about saving Arjuna from Karna. Karna had a Kavacha by the mother of Indra (Aditi) and he was misusing it. Indra took it back. Krishna was worried about Vasvishakti that was a proof of fairness of Indra. Even Indra knew that the vasvishakti couldn’t kill Arjuna who was protected by Krishna. Yet Krishna decided to avoid that confrontation.

You said — Do you think Pandeva's fairly could have killed him ?

YES. Karna was defeated by Arjuna in every single battle. The only reason he lived was Arjuna decided NOT to kill him. He could have killed him during Matasya battle, but he left he rendered unconcious.

And He killed him fairly. He didn’t use any divine weapon to kill the wretched devil. Just killed him with a simple arrow. Karna was killed because his chariot was stuck not because his weapon was stuck. He died because he couldn’t flee like every other day and this time Arjuna decided — enough!

You said — he got many curse for no reason.

In making such a statement you are insulting Parshurama, and incarnation of Vishnu. He didn’t get any curse without a reason.

Parshurama expelled him for being wicked and deceitful. Isn’t that a good reason?

Brahmin cursed him for irresponsibly killing his cow because he was distracted harvesting hate against Arjuna and tried to find his way out by bribing. Is hate and bribe no reason?

You said — he died only because of destiny. Not because of his inability.

Everyone lives and dies because of destiny. Karna destiny was is evil nature and inability to fight against Arjuna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Bhishma-consider-Karna-equal-to-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 02, 2022 09:02 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for a late reply. Just saw the comment.

You said — we all talk about karna,s mistake .we never talk about pandavs mistake.

Two reasons. First they have fewer mistake. Only one I can count. Second theirs were mistake while Karna’s was crime. Karna was a molestor, murder conspirator and arsonist. You can’t say that about Pandavas.

You said — We know karna insulted drupady at the time of vastraapharna. we dont talk about when draupay in swaymwar saying karna was sutputra.

That is because Karna not just insulted Draupadi at the time of Vastraharna, he masterminded it.

And second, because Draupadi never insulted Karna. It is an well acknowledged interpolation. And that is the difference.

But even if I were to accept the interpolation that Druapdai rejected Karna, it was still her prerogative as a bride. Remember Swyamvar (I choose the bride). And calling a Sutaputra and Sutaputra can’t be an insult, when Suta were respected part of society (Brahmin Mother, Kshatriya Father).

You said — We value Abhimanyus life. we don’t value karna’s son life.

NO. We do value Karna’s sons life as well. I feel more sorry for Karna’s son than Abhimanyu. In fact Abhimanyu died a hero and I feel sorry for the co wards that killed him unfairly.

Karna’s son didn’t die unfairly. He didn’t die in group attack. But I still feel sorry for him. While Abimanyu died fighting for Dharma his son paid the price for the sins of his father. His father’s abusive nature made Arjuna kill him. And he was unfortunate that while his father was present, he was not capable to save him from Arjuna.

You see, I value his tragic death and feel sorry for him to have such a worthless father.

You said — Bhishma never disclosed karna about his identity before when karna started hating pandavas.

First, Karna started hating Pandavas while he was still young in Drona’s gurukul long before even Bhishma knew about his true identity.

Second, not knowing true identity is no excuse for hating someone else. Had Karna known he was an abandoned son of Kunti, he may still would have a valid reason to hate Pandavas. But since he didn’t know, why should he hate Pandvas? It is pure jealosy and evil nature.

You don’t get right to hate someone because he isn’t related to you. Why blame Bhishma or Krishna for Karna’s failure as a human being?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-does-Bhishma-consider-Karna-equal-to-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 02, 2022 10:41 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why sorry?

I agree. Karna was a war monger and he was always at a disadvantage because he was no match for Arjuna. He always thought, maybe next day…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-killed-when-he-was-weaponless/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 05, 2022 07:50 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Perhaps you didn't read my answer and have most certainly not read the Epic Mahabharata.

Let me explain.

  1. There is no Barbarik in Mahabharata. Yes. You read it right. It's a later add on. Can you imagine the weapon given by an unknown godess to be more powerful than Brahmasta, Narayanastra or Pashupata?
  2. Arjuna not only killed Jaydradh but also destroyed a large Kaurava army. And battle extended well after Jarasandha's death. CE doesn't include sunset incident. But even if you want to believe it, that's what friends do. That's what comrades do. Kauravas tried to hide him. They failed.
  3. I think you have greater trust on Indras weapon than Indra himself, for he had informed Karna it may not kill him who is under Krishna's protection.

Finally and most importantly, every single Kuru warrior had been defeated by Arjuna before Kurukshetra and without Krishna's support. Every single of them. Parshuram, Bhishma, Drona, Krishna, Shiva, and even evil Karna knew Arjuna could have defeated them squarely.

Hope that helps.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-would-happen-if-Krishna-didnt-take-Pandavas-side-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 06, 2022 01:43 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You are right. He is not going to die. But the secret is many people are made great not because people like them but because they act as puppets to make others dwarf.

Barbarik is used in modern context to show him more powerful than the most powerful warrior of the era, his weapon from an unknown diety to be more powerful than most powerful weapons like Brahmastra and Narayanastra, his self boasting more powerful than relevant facts and Krishna as a conniving politician.

Guess he was powerful.

Nice Answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-secret-about-barbaric-in-the-Mahabharata/answer/Sesha-21
Creation time: Aug 06, 2022 08:20 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Neither should be forget that Karna never won a single battle against Arjuna. Neither can you count any of his achievements in the battle other than killing Ghatotkach with the help of Vasvishakti or Abhimanyu along with other cowards. Even Karna knew and said no one competes with Arjuna. Krishna called him defanged serpant and extinguished flame after losing Vasvi Shakti. Shiva and Parshurama said he could never defeat Arjuna. Krisha said sun, and moon may change their course but Karna could never defeat Arjuna. And he never did. That begs the question, on what basis did you conclude Karna was an obtacke in Pandavas path? Did he prove an onstacle when Arjuna killed Jarasandha or Karna's son? Or did he give any resistance to Bhinasena as he continued to kill those Kuru brother's who were trying to save his worthless life?

You said – There is every possibility that he would have intentionally took the chariot at such a place that the wheel of the chariot got stuck and it became easy for Arjuna to kill Karna.

Is this possibility anything more than your imagination? Did you miss that Shalya had developed a grudging respect for Karna and even promised him to complete his mission should Karna die? Did you miss that he protected unconscious Karna from Bhinasena?

As for an easy kill, Arjuna could have killed an unconcious Karna during Matasya battle easily. He lived because Arjuna chose not to.

You said –It’s not mentioned anywhere that Shalya even tried to help Karna🙏

Neither is it mentioned that he intentionally stuck the chariot. What is clearly mentioned is

  • He fought for Kuru forces.
  • He saved Karna's life
  • He promised to complete his mission to kill Arjuna and Krishna.
  • He commanded Kuru army

You said —Shalya was a real maternal uncle of Nakula and Sahdeva so though he was fighting for Kauravas his heart was with the Pandavas.

Can you please explain, why Shalya chose to fight against his real nephews if his heart indeed lied with them? Why did he become commander of Kuru forces? Why did he die fighting against them? Why did he protect Karna from Bhinasena?

Think!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-killed-when-he-was-weaponless/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 07, 2022 08:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

So, a little care made him obligated towards Duryodhana enough to fight against his own nephews. He must be a great loving uncle!

But then, when he died fighting for Kuru, why do you insult him by calling he betrayed? Didn't he protect Karna?

Shouldn't you call Karna a greater traitor who let Duryodhana's brothers die because he couldn't reveal the truth that he, whom Duryodhana trusted the most, wouldn't kill his enemy Pandavas. He always said, he would. Isn't that backstabbing?

May I recommend read authentic version rather than fanfiction like Mrityunjay.

Content:

Agree.

There are often imagination added to facts. Sometimes distortive, sometimes constructive and they become inseparable over a period of time. Thats where logical explanation and cross reference helps. More reasearch and more clarity. That said, todays logic may not be logical for the years gone by and we must be careful.

And yes Indra was a position not an individual. It is mentioned in Mahabharata

Content:

Because that is what a charioteer is supposed to do. He is supposed to do manuver to protect the warrior from harm.

He protected his warrior from two treacherous snake s — Ashwasena and Karna.

Nagastra was a Naaga sittings on an arrow.

Answer Comment

Content:

It's not I but you are misguiding.

Did Indian army rebel? No. Did they try to prevent India's partition if they were powerful or eager enough to throw Britishers out of India? No.

Mutiny of Navy may be unknown to you but certainly not a suppressed part of Indian history. And let me tell you, while there is no denying that not only the army but entire India were pro to INA. But neither sympathy nor respect translates to action. The facts remains unchanged —

  • Indian army didn't join INA.
  • Indian army didn't win us independence. Can you name a single top brass army general who led their troop for India's freedom?
  • Indian army continued to seve under British general to oversee partition.
  • INA didn't reach Indian soil, forget getting total independence.

And if Indian army had woken up in sympathy for INA, it would still be a civil disobedience.

You have just as much data to support your tall claims as the questioner of this question has — ZERO Data, hollow arguments.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 10, 2022 06:19 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

That is just your views. No Backing. There is no basis for telling that army would never accept a suta to became a commander when the Army actually accepted him as commander and before that his King.

Do you know Keechak, another suta, was also the commander of Matasya army? Do you know Sanjay, also a suta, was the ambassador of Hastianpur who was bestowed the divine vision and Karna, the suta was trained by Drona?

And I may agree, calling a coward and a deserter even Ardharathi is exaggeration.

And if you think loss of army led to Karna’s appointment, think again. The remaining army needed their moral more than ever. Then, if suta’s were not respected, it was a greater reason to side track Karna. But he was given the post bypassing not only Ashwatthama but also Shalya.

The fact is even Karna knew Drona was a better choice and he gave way.

Can you tell me what were the great achievements of Karna as a warrior or commander in the entire Kurukshetra battle? Killing Ghatotkatch with the help of Indra’s weapon? Anything else?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-think-Karna-was-lucky-to-be-commander-before-Ashwatthama-when-everyone-from-Duryodhan-to-Shakuni-and-everyone-and-even-Bhishma-Pitama-wanted-Karna-to-be-commander-even-Drona-was-ok-with-it-but-it-was/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 13, 2022 12:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

While I consider Drona as one of the greatest gurus, in my understanding, he wasn't really a great army general or a warrior. He had no real war experience. He was rather easily beaten in Matasya battle. He couldn't dare to formulate chakravyuh in presence of Arjuna, couldn't defeat Abhimanyu, couldn't save Jaydradh and his misadventure to continue fighting after sunset costed dearly to Kuru forces.

True, Ghatotkach was killed in his tenure as the commander, he had no role in it. He, indeed killed Drupad and Virata and while they were good warriors, they weren't that great as clear from Gurusakshina battle, Swyamvar and Matasya episode or even Kurukshetra.

I would go with Bhishma, who alone could hold on to 10 days. He was the only one who wasn't stunned in Matasya and not to forget his legendary battle with Bhagwan Parshuram.

Content:

Agreed Karna had many vices. He was lusty, deceitful, and coward. But Vyas never mentioned he was drunk. Even Karna didn't mention he was drunk. He simply said he was afraid and ran away. Only BR Chopra and Karna fans call him drunk. Without a single valid reference.

You said Ghatotkach's illusion was more powerful. Once again there is not a single comparative analysis in the authentic epic. Just wishful thinking. And again it doesn't matter. What matters is,he failed against Gandharvas and ran away afraid as he did on so many more occasions.

Did he defeat Chitrasena during Digvijaya or some other Gandharvas? Not sure as it is removed in BORI.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-karna-received-weapons-from-God-Gandharvas-and-rakshashas/answer/Swetavahana
Creation time: Aug 18, 2022 06:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I am not sure if you are looking forward for a court certificate for the marriage. Actually those days the marriages were conducted by priests following Vedas. That is how all marriages were conducted. And by those rituals, Draupadi was legally married to all Pandavas. Perhaps you didn’t know there exists Vaivahika Parva after swaymvara Parva which discusses her marriage to five princess in great details. Had you read that you might have known following facts —

  • Ved Vyas endorsed the marriage.
  • It was due to a blessing of Shiva that he gave long before the birth of Pandavas. Naturally his blessing didn’t depend on Yudhishira’s lust.
  • Epic doesn’t refer to Yudhishira’s lust
  • Arjuna won swaymavara. Actually marriage was conducted much later.

You may not have read the facts properly —

Crowded with those who had come for alms, the extensive festive grounds thick with lotuses and lilies, lined with heaps of precious stones, his palace looked like the sky, studded with brilliant stars. Then the young Kuru princes bathed. Adorned with earrings and ornaments, dressed in expensive garments and perfumed with sandalwood paste, they performed the preparatory ceremony and other auspicious rites. O lord! Accompanied by their priest Dhoumya, as radiant as the fire, they then joyfully entered, in due order and one after another, like mighty bulls entering a pen. ‘Then the priest58 who knew the Vedas built and lit the sacrificial fire and poured offerings, with mantras. He then called Yudhishthira and united him with Krishna. Then the Veda-knowing one married them, making them walk around the fire, hand in hand. Then the priest took leave of that ornament of battle59 and went out of the palace. Then those maharathas, bringers of fame to the Kuru lineage, sons of a king among men, took the hand of that supreme among women, in succession, one day after another.

‘When the marriage was over, Drupada gave those maharathas a lot of gifts—100 supreme chariots adorned with gold, each yoked to four horses with golden bridles; 100 elephants with lotus marks on them, like a hundred mountains with golden peaks; and 100 young maidservants dressed in expensive garments and adorned with ornaments and garlands.

‘After the Pandavas had taken a wife, Hari75 sent them many golden ornaments, adorned with pearls and lapis lazuli. Madhava76

  • Notice each brother was married at a gap of one day.
  • Drupada gave each Maharatas (plural) when the marriage was over.
  • Even Shir Krishna gifted them after the Pandavas (plural) took a wife.

And this is Draupadi’s own word —

I was born in a great lineage through divine destiny. I am the beloved wife of the Pandavas. I am the daughter-in-law of the great-souled Pandu.

And This is what the epic says. Without any doubt. And Draupadi has been referred as wives of five Pandavas throughout the epic.

This also proves that my points are not my misunderstanding but based on valid references.

Now, it’s your turn. Can you provide any valid references that —

  1. Draupadi was not legally married to five Pandavas, just one?
  2. The marriage was due to the lust of Yudhishira and Shiva’s blessing had no role?

I would suggest, do so, else everyone would know who is twisting the facts based on his mis-understanding.

The onus of proving such absurd tall claims lie on people making those claims. Had Gandhi made a statement favoring such a corridor, there has to be a public statement, records and archives. None such records exist. Can you give a valid reference?

Here there is no fantasy world. Just a great Indian history and some fools trying to destroy by pushing baseless claims and asking proof to disproof those baseless claims that didn’t exist.

Creation time: Aug 23, 2022 09:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Who proved that? And can you explain how did he divide India?

Answer: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Gandhiji-wanted-to-join-East-Pakistan-and-West-Pakistan-through-a-highway-parting-India-Why-would-some-1
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 05:58 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Winners are the king and losers are the thieves. Pandavas won so they are kings Karna lost so you blame him.

This opinion is biased. Indian epics don’t favor winners. They favor good. If it was a winners tale, it won’t include Yudhishira gambling his wife or Bhima tearing Duhshashana.

By the way if all the references are against Karna, what is your source? Imagination? TV Shows?

You said — First, A child was abandoned,

Yes it is unfortunate. But he was abandoned at too young a age to have any psycological tauma. And it was in his favour. Can you tell what was the alternative? Grow up as fatherless? And since he didn’t knew he was abandoned, it couldn’t have affected him.

You asked — And you expect the child to be a great guy?

I know two other abandoned children from that era — Krishna and Ved Vyas. They both turned out to be great. Didn’t they?

You said — He was humiliated, laughed at, wasn’t given rights, forced to accept the discrimination and such.

When was he humiliated? He was taught by greatest gurus of all the time. What was his ‘right’ was not given to him? He got more than his right. He had no right to become King of Anga. He nevertheless was given the Kingship. He lived a life of luxary, and lust he himself admitted to Krishna. The only discriminations were in his favour. Out of turn Kingship, un-deserving friendship.

You said — Karna had nothing. His father was sun. God of light but he didn’t have light in his life.

He had light. He chose to turn his back on them. He had Drona. He left him. He got Parshurama. He cheated him. Krishna and Kunti came to him. He rejected them. He had no reason to have enmity with Pandavas. They had done him no harm.

You said — I am not saying Kunti is wrong or anythnig, but If you want to answer, look at the story from both prespective. Karna wasn’t a monster but the world made him one.

Who made Karna the monster? Drona? Parshurama? Arjuna? Who asked him to abuse Draupadi? Who asked him to attempt burning her? Who asked him to advise Duryodhana to make fun of Pandavas in exile? Don’t blame entire world to support one monster.

He chose to side with Duryodhana. He chose to cheat his guru. He chose to bribe Brahmana. He chose to burn Draupadi. He chose to strip Draupadi.

World allowed him to be a King. World allowed him to sit among honorable people. World allowed him to lead armies. World allowed him to become Army general. World did him no harm

You said — Pandavas weren’t monsters because they were guided by the best.

Same “best” offered his help to Karna. Guess what Karna did.

You said —Karna wanted to help the world and he lost his protection.

Tell me what help did Karna offer to the world? Did he save a woman whom others were tryin to burn down? Did he try to protect any village from demon? Did he try to protect people captured by enemies? Did he try to protect a kingdom in distress? NO. He helped no one. Not even his best friend. He cheated even his best benefactor.

You said — The world isn’t fair. It’s true but if you think Kunti would suffer, why ddin’t you see that Karna suffered more? Who suffers most? A child or a mother?

Because Karna didn’t really suffer. Karna had no knowledge, that he lost a mother, so he had no reason to suffer. He was rich enough to be called Vasusena, raised by a loving parent, taught by great teachers and became King and live entire life in luxary.

Kunti bore Karna for 9 months. That itself is the greatest pain. She knew, she lost a son. That was agonizing. She saw him trying to kill her and her sons. She could do nothing. Guess who suffered more?

You said — Kunti had so many chances. She could have asked Krishna to bring Karna to her and with a snap Karna would be with her. She could have gone with him or helped him. She could have told the truth but didn’t.

Yes. She could have. And She did. She did reach out to him. She did tell him the truth. Krishna did reach out to him. But did Karna change Karna? NO. He had become an ally of Duryodhana long before Kunti saw him for the first time. There was no going back. Evils don’t change. And Karna was evil.

You said — I won’t say she was a bad mother as she was a great one for Pandavas. But, If you ask me, I think No Karna should have a mother like Kunti.

Interesting you find Kunti was a great mother for Pandavas. Do you realize two of five Pandavas were not even her son. Still. She loved them. Now imagine his love for Karna.

If you think, no Karna should have mother like Kunti, then by the same extension no Krishna should have mother like Devaki. Both abandoned their son. But then if they Kunti and Devaki were not there, neither would be Karna or Krishna. Radha won’t exist without Kunti.

They only thing great about Karna was the fact that he was Kunti’s son. It was for that one reason, he had Surya for his father. It was for her that Karna got his divine armour. None of these things would have been possible without Kunti.

I wish everyone should have a caring mother like Kunti.

You said — I would prefer Radha (Karna’s foster mother over Kunti anytime.) No comparison

Radha was a compassionate woman — loving and caring. A wonderful mother. But I agree there is no comparison. She was an ordinary woman with ordinary challenges of life. She wasn’t chosen to be mother of divinities. She didn’t have the life of constant struggle. She didn’t live her life from one exile to another. She wasn’t forced to bear a child against her wish. She didn’t have to chose between the life of his father and son. She didn’t have to chose between his sons. In fact she didn’t have any great discission to make. She had a blessed life and she was loving. But she can’t be compared to Kunti.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Kunti-have-real-love-for-Karna-or-was-it-just-a-fake-show-off-to-save-her-all-5-sons/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 06:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — I’m Arjuna fan but karna also defeated Arjuna in the swyamwar.

No. Karna got scared and ran away. Arjuna won. It is clearly mentioned.

You said — Karna defeated Arjuna on 16th day

WRONG! Arjuna defeated Karna badly. Karna himself admitted. Sanjay and Dhritrashtra lamented.

You said — swooned him on the 17th and could’ve killed him if he didn’t jump down the chariot and tried to lift it

Making someone unbalanced is not same as defeating. It was a momentarily distraction, small enough that Karna didn’t get time to even take out his chariot wheel. Krishna himself said no matter what Karna can’t kill Arjuna. Shiva said so. Even Karna said so. So, you see your opinion is just colored. I doubt you are really an Arjuna fan.

Karna had no reason to waste time with chariot wheel. Warriors fought battle without chariot. Do you know any other warrior to waste his precious time over a chariot wheel?

You said — Pandavas sometime with Arjun sometimes without him and defeated them all many times

NO. There was not a single battle where Karna defeated Arjuna. You should be proud of this fact as Arjuna fan, that you say you are.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Arjuna-s-character-degraded-by-Indian-TV-serials/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 06:59 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your answer reflect your great knowledge of TV Show and zero knowledge of authentic texts—

  • Draupadi didn’t insult Karna. First she never called him Suta Putra. It is an interpolation. Second even if someone called a Suta putra as suta putra couldn’t be called a disgrace. Sutas were born of Brahiman and Kshatria marriage and were not inferior.
  • During Gandharva battle, Karna knew Duryodhana has not gone away. He himself said I ran away in front of your eyes.
  • You said Karna’s words were according to Gods. Which God? which reference? Karna’s words were according to his own. Do you know what was God’s word? That she will have five husband. Which God? Shiva.

Now next time you come out abusive and using your stupid phrasings I will delete your response.

If you want to discuss, be civic.

Answer: https://historyplanet.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-2
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 07:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If you say our historians didn’t mention anything about heroes like Netaji and Shashtri ji, can you tell me how do you know they were great heroes? Based on whatsapp? What is your source?

Do you know one of these two people was our Prime Minister and the other is one of the only two people to have his photo in constitution. Please read history before commenting based solely on social media forwards.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Britishers-left-India-due-to-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-and-not-due-to-Mahatma-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The problem with people like you is, you fail to differentiate between Mahtma Gandhi and contemporary Gandhis (which is now irrelevant and useless), you failed to differentiate between Congress of 1947, which represented the people of India and the useless and almost extict congrss which is NOT the Congress of Mahatama Gandhi but renamed Congress (I), one with which I have ZERO affiliation.

And when it comes to be blind, you should have checked, when I wrote this answer, before calling it a congress propaganda. Neither I degrade myself by running propaganda wings for congress nor I degrade Mahatma Gandhi by associating him with today's Gandhi.

I too passed my academic in India. And I did learn about Bose and Shastri ji there. And I didn't stop there. Read several more books from across the world cross referenced with people like Rabindranath Tagore and Dr Rajendra Prasad, and Larry Collins.

But looks like todays mantra is not history but theories. All Mr Anuj Dhar has, is the same theories that existed for last seventy years or more but couldn't be incorporated in any book of history due to lack of credible evidence. Theory makes good stories but cant be used as relevant sources of history.

You said, I didn't answer your two questions. Read again. You had two points, and just one question and that can be answered only if people have relevant perspective. Anyway I will anwer that. I have been a Modi fan since he was Gujarat CM and trusted him. Like Gandhiji he focused on cleanliness, he referred to Gandhiji in his missons, and like him tried to convert his plans, like Jana dhana, into a mass movements. In his first speech in USA, he credited NRI for the growth and reputation of India. He galvanized the masses. Now either he truly meant to follow Gandhiji, as he says, or it was all a glimic of a hypocrite, you may take a call.

Now, coming to your source, the extensively researched book by Mr Dhar, can you answer these questions to prove your knowledge—

  • When did Shubhash Chandra Bose died? In the plain crash or later?
  • What is the exact evidence Mr Dhar presents in his book that is suppressed?
  • What does DNA analysis says about believed ashes of Shubhash Chandra Bose in Japan?
  • Who called Mahatma Gandhi, the Father of the nation for the First time?
  • What were the names of 5 brigades of INA named by Bose?
  • What is Indias national salutation? Where does it comes from? Who added it to India's constitution?
  • Who are the only two freedom fighters to have their picture in original copy of India's constitution?
  • Who were the ideals of Lal Bahadur Shashtri according to Lal Bahadur Shashtri?

Perhaps in findings the answer you may reach truth.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-it-true-that-Britishers-left-India-due-to-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-and-not-due-to-Mahatma-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 08:29 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why wait for Dhritrashtra's son to born, grow up and become King? Why do you think Dhritrashtra had more right? Just because he was elder? Why do you think Pandu didn't have right to be real king rather than care taker? Pandu was a young and worthy man to be King. That is funny.

And what made you conclude Yudhisthira was greedy for becoming King? Is it mentioned somewhere? Did he try to kill his cousins to become a King? Did he cheat? Was his father not the King? Do you know the story of how Shantanu became King? Do you know what Dhritrashtra said about Kingship? Or Duryodhana? Do you know anything at all to conclude that criminals like Duryodhana and Karna who are mentioned culprits were innocent and Kunti was culprit? Can you quote any reference where she is blamed? Can you at least give a common sense logic to this?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 08, 2022 11:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

As I see, Divyastra is not a ordinary weapon that can be triggered easily and limitlessly. It takes a toll on its wielder and thus the cautious. Even On his first initiation, Krishna was relaxed. It would tire Karna. And never really was worried about it. It was as it is difficult to wield it again. And of course he knew Arjuna would counter it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Arjun-flee-from-Karnas-Bharghavastra-Was-not-Arjun-enough-capable-to-counter-Karnas-Bharghavastra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 10, 2022 01:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

At least you agree, there is no official statement. Thats a start.

Do you know what the other PM's of UK said officially? Do you know Churchill's remark? Didn't a UK PM officially appologised for Jalianwala Bagh?

And why do you think one should give any credibility to a heresy from a comparively insignificant political personality against the overwhelming evidences that we have?

You say a private conversation may be important. But is there a proof of that private conversation? Any records? There are hours and hours of records of Lord Mountbatten's conversation. Private but recorded. And they admit their failures too. They are unofficial but public. What do you have? Did he confide this with his aide? A fellow British politician? Naah.

This is a classical definition of heresy from a politician with almost no experience with Indian politics.

Bottom line heresy by someone who could dare to speak his mind in public.

Irony is you defend a heresy so zealously and discredit history. You consider the views of politician so seriously completely ignore your own legacy who are well renowned across the globe. At least tell me this, do you know what Subhash Chandra Bose thought about the contribution of Gandhi in India? And that is on official records not heresy.

I don't take such statement of a nation whom we forced out of our soil to tarnish the sacrifices of millions of Indians.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 10, 2022 06:57 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Well, Mountbatten gave public recorded interviews for getting them part of his biography. He was a courageous man acknowldging the facts officially, on record. Not backing out but publishing. That makes it official and not heresy. And Lord Mountbatten is a far credible man both in British and Indian politics.

And pointing finger at Mountbatten won't change the fact that there is not a single verifiable evidence of what Atlee said. Second, Atlee has no great credibility anywhere expect for haters of India, Gandhi and Bose.

I would love to hear you talk about what Bose said. So instead of focusing on Mountbatten or Atlee why dont you answer the two questions I asked? Do you know the answers?

  • Who called Gandhiji, the father of the nation?
  • What were the names of five brigades of INA, named by Netaji?
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 13, 2022 07:11 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What is obvious is you haven't read any authentic version of Mahabharata and formed your opinion on pulp fictions.

You said — Karna never failed Swyamvar

Karna's failure in swyamvara is explicitly mentioned in almost all ancient narratives, including Neelkantha commentary which is the foundation of Kisari Mohan Ganguli's version and Gita press Mahabharata, in the southern recitations, in the abridged version by Sri Rajgopalchari and highly revered critical edition by BORI.

My negaive emotions are based on authentic reading and reflection of Krishna's view that he was extremely wicked soul.

You said —In his ( Karna) entire life, the only man who gave him respect was Duryodana.

WRONG. He became the King of Anga at a young age. He was a respected member of Hastinapur court and had say in political matters. He was an invited guest in Draupadi's swyamvar explicitly named by Dhristiduymna. Yes, it's a different matter, he was unworthy of honour.

You said — He was killed when he had no weapon in his hands.

WRONG. He died fighting. Divyastra don't need hand. And if he had no weapon it was his stupidity that he chose to drop them and waste time on chariot wheel than fight. Warriors have fought an won without chariots.

You said — Krishna knew that was the only way to beat Karna.

Karna was defeated by Arjuna in Draupadi's swyamvar, by Bhima in Rajsuy battle, by gandharvas during ghosha battle, thrice by Arjuna during goharana where he fled as his brother was killed, by Abhimanyu on day 13, several times by Bhima on day 14, again by Arjuna on day 16, and on day 17. What Krishna certainly knew was Karna can't defeat Arjuna. Same was said by Shiva, Parshurama, Bhishma, Drona, Ashwatthama, Kripacharya and behold, Karna himself.

You said — the only man who gave him respect was Duryodana

And how did Karna repay? By treachary. He fled Gandharvas to save his life and left Duryodhana, the only man who had respected him.

He refused to fight the first 10 days in the Kurukshetra battle for his ego. Yes, Bhishma didn't stop him. He declined. Is that how you repay friends?

He not only spared the life of his friend's worst enemies, he let them die for himself and never told Duryodhana that he would not kill four Pandavas. That was treason.

Only people who trust pulp fictions would ever respect that henious monster.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 14, 2022 07:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No. The two are not same.

Lord Mountbatten said something with the intent of making it public. He allowed the talks to be recorded. Here there are two important point

  1. There is no breach of trust.
  2. There exists an evidence that even Lord Mountbatten cannot deny even if he wanted. It his his recording, his voice.

Did Atlee said something officially? No. Did he leave a voice record? Did he intend to make his personal discussion public? We will never know. Was Mr Chakravarthy in any official capacity to make remarks on behalf of Attlee? No. It was heresy. Periods. No historian, no nation can take it seriously. Such tales are good only on social media propaganda. You say justice Chakravarthy referred in his letter. Mr Chakravarthy's letter can be a proof of his own statement, not someone else's Had Atlee written it, I would not have called it heresy.

Tell me what if I write a letter saying Mr Modi said so and so to me in private? Would you take that seriously?

And thanks for your salam, even if it is a comment. But I do understand PM's value. Haven't we seen PM's like Chowdhary Charan Singh, Devegowda, Gujaral and Manmohan Singh in our country? And haven't we heard about General Manik Shaw? Attlee was one time PM, who assumed the office in 1945, two years before India's independence. But if you research the most influential British PM in last century he was Churchill, who understood India better. What did he say?

Not every PM becomes credible. It's a political post. And the opinion remains heresy. Period.

But any specific reasons, you are avoiding the questions I raised earlier?

  1. Who called Gandhiji the father of the nation?
  2. What was the name of the five brigades of INA named by Netaji?
  3. Who were the advocates defending INA trial at Red Fort? Name all of them.

Are these questions too uncomfortable for you to answer? Is it because they are Indian whose statements you don't value or is it because they are hard facts that break the myth created by such baseless heresy?

If you can't answer the question, it is a nice time to call off the debate.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Clement-Attlee-actually-say-that-Subhash-Chandra-Bose-was-responsible-for-Indias-independence/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 15, 2022 09:22 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Wow that is some pervert statement. someone is out their distributing the certificate of chaste.

But even if, in your pervert world, Draupadi wasn't chaste, that still doesn't give right to an evil to lay hands on her. Chaste or not, her body, her will. Period.

And yes, revenge was sweet. The King himself pleaded for her forgiveness, the court remained mum. The evils saw their empire devasted — brothers, sons killed in front of their eyes, warriors deserted friends and family to save their own miserable existence for few days and died in agony, realising their crime had no escape. Yes, the revenge was indeed sweet.

Content:

I hope you realize, that Drona had right to his knowledge and it was his previledge to choose or reject a Student. Eklavya had no right to either force himself on Drona or use his name, when Drona explicitly rejected him. Drona didn't need to justify himself.

In Kurukshetra, Drona fought for his Dharma. His loyalty towards Bhishma, who supported him, all his life.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Some-people-are-saying-Dronacharya-did-right-by-taking-Ekalavyas-finger-and-Eklavya-is-adharmi-because-he-had-stolen-knowledge-How-to-refute-this-misconception/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I agree mastering knowledge of weapon is a very time talking process. But that's just one aspect of it. It also is a dangerous skill, not to be shared lightly with everyone. Further, it must be under proper guidelines. And that is what distinguishes millitory and millitant.

And you are WRONG, if you think, knowledge can't be stolen for long. Karna stole almost all knowledge from Parshusm. Knowledge piracy is rampant even today.

Just remember, How Eklavya met his maker. And there unravels all the mystery.

Content:

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Panchali-died-why-did-Yudhistira-replied-to-the-Pandavas-that-she-loved-Arjuna-more-than-the-other-Pandavas-I-heard-that-Draupadi-loved-all-the-pandavas-equally/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 20, 2022 11:17 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There are many flawed assumption in your theory.

You said — Kavacha was stolen by Indra?

Are you sure you know the epic? Did Indra steal it while Karna was sleeping? Did he forcibly snatched from him? Didn't Karna asked for exchange? Wasn't he happy with exchange?

You said — By keeping his kavach, he may have been a formidable force?

Why Karna needed a kavacha to be a formidable force? No one else who defeated him and made him to run for his life had Kavacha.

Why wasn't Karna a formidable force when he had the Kavacha? He fled scared of Arjuna during swyamvar, was bested by Bheem during Rajsuy yagna and was badly thrashed by Gandharvas.

You said —He lost to Arjuna-Krishna, not because he lacked a kavach, but because he was cursed by Parshuram to forget his battle-skills when he most needed them.

No he lost to Arjuna because he was an extremely wicked and soul and a coward. He didn't lost to Arjuna once, but again and again and yet again. Parshurama's curse was results of his crime.

You said — Neither Arjuna nor Krishna reached heaven. Only Yudhisthira and Duryodhan did

Please read again. You missed the illusion part of this story.

You said — did you also think of Eklavya. He trained on the same principles that Arjuna and his siblings did. If Dronacharya had not asked for his thumb, he (Dr'ya) would have been saved.

No. Eklavya wasn't thaught on the same principle. He had no knowledge of divine weapon. Arjuna was savvyasachi (ambidextrous), equal expert from both hand. Eklavya still had a thumb. He could have…

You said—The war was won, despite the reasons for it by kaminapan,

Completely agree. Kaminapan of Karna and Kauravas. That evil tried to disrobe a woman in public, tried to burn her alive, tried to laugh at her torment. Yes, that Kaminapan got him killed. No Kavacha can save such evils.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-karna-also-fought-from-the-first-day-with-his-kavach/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 20, 2022 11:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh, someone surely need to read Gita, specially where Gunas are discussed. And a study of Karmayoga would also do good.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-karna-also-fought-from-the-first-day-with-his-kavach/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 20, 2022 11:40 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It would be naive to call Krishna lied. A projection is neither true nor lie. It's projection. Period. Krishna made similar contradictory statements at many places which few naive may call lie.

  1. He told Arjuna that Yudhisthira lacks Kshatirya valour and Karna will surely kill him today… truth or lie?
  2. Then he told Yudhisthira that Shalya is more powerful than Bhishma, Karna and Drona and only Yudhisthira, in all the three worlds, has the capacity to kill him. Truth or lie

Think, and now let me to take you to facts beyond lies. Karna was defeated by Arjuna during Draupadi's swyamvar without Gandiva. He was defeated by Bhima in Rajsuy yagna and by Gandharvas. None of them had Sudarshana chakra or Gandiva while Karna had his Kavacha.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Could-Krishna-s-Sudarshan-Chakra-kill-Karna-with-his-kundal-and-kavach-on/answer/Madhava-33
Creation time: Nov 20, 2022 07:00 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The greatest flaw of the argument is to assume that Shiva and Shakti are inseparable existence. They aren't. Thats what Ardhanarishwar form explains.

  • Shiva without Shakti is Shava
  • But Shiva is not shava. Never Shava.
  • So Shiva isn't without Shakti.

If Shiva can't move an inch without Shakti, what happend after Sati entered Yagna fire? Didn't Shiva moved even an inch? What is the legacy of Shaktipith?

The problem is you are trying to make one superior to others where there is no two. Just one Shiva with his Shakti. Once Sati ended her physical form she returns back to Shiva.

What is a sun without heat and light? But then sun is source of that heat and light that defines it — inseparable without duelity. Thats Shiva and Shakti.

Also I feel just looking for feminism would be a very narrow point of search. Look for the over all social structure to understand that society that worshipped multiple feminine forms, one that dares to proclaim the Ardhanarishwara form, and that Shiva is Shava without Shakti.

All the best. You can reach out to me by sending DM or follow me on my podcast on Mahabharata http://vivek.vnc.in/m

Content:

Pandavas were as much son of Pandu as Dhritrashtra was son of Vichitraveer. And by your logic too Dhritrashtra had no claim to throne.

Now ignoring your opinion, which are mere views, as per mahabharata, Dhritrashtra had no claim on throne. He never was King. And Duryodhana too had no claim. This was explicitly and with precedence explained by Dhritrashtra and Gandhari in the Udyoga Parva. For more details check out my podcast.

https://open.spotify.com/episode...

Creation time: Feb 05, 2023 05:00 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Contemporary lenses to see a different world would always be narrow. Polygamy and Polyandry are WRONG. No question there. No justification either. But here there is a difference of tense and time.

It is bad, is not same as it was bad and it will be bad. Yes, we can neither define nor control the standard for past or future. We can do it only for today.

A married person visiting prostitutes is not polygamy. It will still be bad.

What about Live in relationship? What about marrying and divorcing and remarrying? Would you call it polygamy/polyandry? What about extra marital relationship? What about same sex marriage? Right or wrong? What did the society think about it till recent times?

There are questions we can’t dictate rule about.

But during Mahabharata era, polygamy and polyandry were acceptable customs. And people following them can’t be judged by todays law. Law can’t be imposed retrospective.

As I see only person who could have called out wrong or foul was Draupadi. And I don’t see her complaining.

Content:

No. Neither a defeat. Charioteers are supposed to protect their warrior the same way as shields and armors. That's their job. It's no different than Karna protected by armour. Bhagdatt broke etichs of divyastra, and Karna basic trait of a warrior by using snakes in human battle. Krishna foiled their evil plan3

She abandoned him. But then, Devaki also abandoned Krishna. And so did Satyavati to Ved Vyas. But they never held their resentment for their mother. They bowed in front of their mothers. And Kunti never left her without protection. Can you imagine an alternative and better descision that Kunti could have taken?

And I am a believer in Mahatma Gandhi, the man who defined Gandhian Philosophy.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 25, 2023 05:15 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It's not Gandhism. It's common sense. Sinners need more blessings. And no *ism stops me from calling an evil as an evil. How else would you teach your kids whom not to follow. Ramayana, Mahabharata all talks about good and evil. Everyone censure evils.

And you are wrong. A Brahmin didn't kill Gandhi. An misguided fool who had no idea about what it means to be a Brahmin killed him. I am a Brahmin and I didn't kill him. And I don't call them dharmatma by their birth.

Now I may not hate someone from other religions and I denounce my own, these two are essentially two different things. So don't be confuse. If you desert your relegion how can you follow any?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 26, 2023 03:13 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No. Please do not ignore animal's comment. What Bhima used was a dead animal to protect himself from an evil warrior who had history of attacking unarmed warrior. He used it impromptu. Karna used snake that he had worshipped for ages to kill Arjuna.

Now coming to charioteers, they are supposed to protect their warriors to the best of their ability. Bhishma's charioteer protected him when he was rendered unconscious in his fight against Parshurama and Arjuna (Matasya battle). Prince uttara's charioteer protected him by fighting on his behalf.

Bottom line charioteer's are supposed to protect warriors to the best of their ability. It so happens Krishna had greater ability. Period. And what you call as hypocrisy is very well summed up in shloka – Dharmo Rakshati Rakshitaah.

Arjuna was protected because he chose a great charioteer who foiled the evil plans of evil warriors. And when you talk about Godly interventions, all divyastra including Vasavi Shakti and Vaishnavastra were Godly interventions, weapons that shouldn't have been fired in the first place.

And even if they had succeeded in killing Arjuna, they wouldn't have defeated him. Only cowards get defeated. Arjuna would have died fighting not begging for mercy.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-Karna-defeated-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 27, 2023 02:47 AM PST
Content language: English

Content:

Long message, but complete zibrish. Godhara? Shikh riot? How are they related to Gandhiji? You are delusional. Gandhiji never fasted to give money to Pakistan. NEVER. You may again call your friend's to help you here. But that won't change history. A criminal (not a Brahmin) killed Gandhiji. Period.
