Did godsey followed this rule? And sinner and dharmatma are not for layman. It's the essence of Dharma. And no Gandhian philosophy is against this.

Fight or run is what animal's do. As a human we should be better.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-killer-Godse-hanged-and-not-forgiven/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 27, 2023 03:02 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

If you quote epic, Arjuna is Vijaya, the one who never lost. Arjuna is neither Srinjaya nor Kekai nor too insignificant to be named in common herd.

Neither living nor death is proof of victory or defeat. When you loose your hope of victory, when you fear death and start to run away, you are defeated. On the other hand you may be killed and yet not defeated. Like Abhimanyu died defeating the plan of Drona to capture Yudhishthir. Karna lived in Matasya battle, gandharva battle, swayamvar battles. But there was no victory for him. None.

But do you have a point to make or just want to contradict?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-if-Karna-defeated-Arjuna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Feb 27, 2023 05:46 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said: karna was drunk

WRONG. There is Zero reference that Karna was drunk. Zero. No cross reference. Even Karna NEVER said he was drunk. He couldn’t fight Gandharvas. Period.

You said: Also he came back later to find out Pandavas rescued Duryodhana

WRONG. He didn’t come back. He waited for Duryodhana to return. He never tried to rescue Duryodhana. He had no plan to rescue anyone. He himself said —

  1. He ran because he was afraid (not drunk)
  2. He thought no one could win Gandharvas (thats why he ran)
  3. It is fortunate that Duryodhana is still alive (Never said he was going to try to protect Duryodhana)

You said — he didn’t lead the kings to burn Draupadi they all attacked at once and karna only started to attack when Arjuna attacked karna

Let me debunk your claims with exact reference

Having said this, the maharatha took up the bow he had obtained as dowry and accompanied by his brother Bhima stood there like an immovable mountain. Seeing the Kshatriyas, with Karna leading them, furious to do battle, they fell upon them, like two elephants against hostile elephants. Eager to fight, those kings then said, “It is permissible to kill a Brahmana who is willing to fight.” Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant. Shalya, king of the Madras, rushed at Bhimasena.

What is clear is

  1. Arjuna didn’t attack. He stood in defence of Drupad family like an immovable mountain.
  2. Karna was leading the Kings.
  3. Karna attacked Arjuna.

You said — Abhimanyu died fairly

Only Karna’s fans say so. Sanjay clearly mentioned unfair. Dhritrashtra agreed. Gandhari said it unfair. Everyone else said so. Please read the entire reference and you will see what kind of cowards you are talking about. Karna fled seeing his own brother dead. He himself said he is unable to resist Abhimanyu. You know people who suffer multiple wounds don’t die because of just the last one but all of them. And even you would agree Karna couldn’t fight Abhimanyu alone. He was no match. Dont believe? See what Karna did and said during Abhimanyu battle.

You said — Arjuna summoned the anjalika Astra and killed him karna

There was no need to summon Anjalika. It was an ordinary weapon with the head like that of a crescent moon.

You said — virat war is an interpolation

Again. Only Karna’s fan believe it. It is present in all the important ancient reference. It is present in BORI. It has innumerable cross references in Udyoga Parva, Karna Vadha Parva and so on. It is an important foundation of the story.

You said — if karna was evil he wouldn’t get a place between the aditiyas

Karna was part of Surya. Once Karna died his soul merged back in the partent body. That doesn’t change the fact that he was evil. Think, a person who tries to burn a lady, disrobe a lady, hatch conspiracy to burn down entire family, who is coward to leave is best friends and brothers to die to save his sorry existence, if he is not evil then I don’t think anyone in the world can be truely called evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Karna-s-death-make-me-cry/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 15, 2023 11:36 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

That is your view. Think, why would Drona want Arjuna to be best? Wont a natural choice would be Duryodhana or Yudhishthira? If you read the epic you would find he actually wanted someone else to be better. Not Arjuna.

Also Eklavaya lost his thumb for a different reason. For better understanding of the story, I would suggest listen to these three podcast episodes.

How Arjuna became Drona’s favorite: https://vivek.vnc.in/m/s01e18

Why Drona asked Eklavya for his thumb?

https://vivek.vnc.in/m/s01e19

Why Drona refused to give Brahmastra knowledge to Karna?https://vivek.vnc.in/m/s01e20

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-trained-by-Drona-as-Pandavas-Kauravas/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 15, 2023 11:40 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Why Arjuna couldn’t kill Bhishma?

If you read the epic, you would find, the only reason Arjuna didn’t fatal attack these warrior was because of his deep rooted love and respect for these people. He couldn’t brin himself to kill Bhishma. He defeated him on several occasions. But didn’t kill him. Even Krishna was agitated because of Arjuna’s action so much that he himself stood up to kill Bhishma. Twice!

Was Bhishma killed via cheating?

Tell me what was the cheating? It was a many to many battle. Many warriors from Pandavas side were fighting many warriors of Kauravas side. Kaurava’s warriors fled. Bhishma was killed. Now where is the cheating?

Why Arjuna couldn’t kill Drona?

Arjuna defeated Drona multiple times. Drona was so afraid of Arjuna that he didn’t dare to form chakravyuh in his presence. He couldn’t even defeat is son. But yes, Arjuna not only didn’t kill Drona, he tried his best to protect him from Dhritduymna. Why? Again same reason as Bhishma. He had so hight respect and love for him that he didn’t attack him despite Drona’s deceitful ways of forming chakravyuh, killing Abhimanyu, fighting at night and his desparate attempt to let Arjuna die by protecting Jaidradh.

Why was Drona killed by cheating.

Cosmic justice. A person who cheats a lone warrior with a band of thugs, a warror who breaks the rule and prolongs fight into the night, some one who schemes to kill his own desciple and his sons, will meet a similar fate. But, then Arjuna had no role in his fate.

Was Karna killed by cheating?

Tell me what was the cheating?

Karna had his chariot struck to the ground. Well in the same battle he had continously attacked chariotless Bhima and Abhimanyu. In the same battle Drona had lost several of his chariot. In the same battle Arjuna fought many warriors togehter without chariot.

Karna was not weaponless Karna had his weapons. He attacked Arjuna with Brahmastra and Varnuastra.

Karna and Arjuna had one to one battle: No third warrior was involved in the battle

So tell me how was Karna’s death by deceit?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 15, 2023 11:53 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — So there is not a case of being ally with enemy in the name of drona as both drona and eklavya made this clear that drona didn't teach him.

Yes Drona didn’t teach Eklavya. But Eklavya still maintained that Drona is his perceptor. That is misuing Drona’s name. And there was a clear case of being ally with enemy. Krishna killed him and compared him with Hidimb and Bakasura. So question is who is whitewashing?

You said — Karna blamed his mother because this is why he was deprived of many opportunities by being considered a varnshankar (a sut) and a charioter's son. You just read the book but never tried to think from a character's perspective.

Ok. Let us try to understand Karna perspective. His mother abandoned him. He didn’t know her. He thought he was son of Suta (not shudra). Then how is it a misfortune? Was being son of Suta a misfortune?

You said, He was deprived of many opportunity?

Can you tell me which opportunity Karna was deprived with? Right to Education? Well he was trained by Drona, Kripa and Parshurama. Right to be a ruler? Does everyone have right to be a ruler? NO. But Karna became a ruler. Was he ever exiled? Did he ever suffer in his life for reasons other than his own deceit, and coward nature? NEVER.

Now you want to think for a character’s perspective?

Think of a young girl, who hasn’t started her life yet. Who should be most important to her? Herself or a son? Karna always was self centered. But Kunti willed Karna to have a better life. She procured divine protection for her.

Do you think, Karna would have a better life had Kunti not let her go? Do you think any King would have married Kunti knowing he had a son? Do you know any precedence? What would have been Kunti’s life in such a scenario? What would have been Karna’s life? That is thinking for character’s perspective. For more details, I would recommend follow this podcast episode where I have discussed Karna’s brith and education: 20 जिसके पिता सूर्य थे (The Son of the Sun God) https://vivek.vnc.in/m/s01e20

You said — At that stage of life it was very difficult for karn to digest the fact that pandavs whom he had enemity with are his brothers and duryodhan who never disrespected or discriminated him and uplifted him by giving Anga should be betrayed.

You are assuming that Karna didn’t betray his benefector Duryodhana. But the problem is he did it again and again.

  1. He fled Gandharvas to save his life leaving behind Duryodhana to die or imprisioned. This is Betrayal. No jusfitication.
  2. He promised Kunti to spare the life of her four sons. These son’s included Yudhishir the main contentder for the Hastinapur throne. He also spared Bhima who had promied (and later fulfilled) to kill Duryodhana and all his brother. This is treacherous. Did Karna ever tell Duryodhana that he had given a boon to Kunti? NEVER. He let Kauravas die silently. 33 Kauravas died trying to protect his life.
  3. Karna refused to fight for first 10 days of battle. Bhishma may have insulted him. His ego might have been hurt. These are meaningless. What matters is his own ego, his own respect was bigger than his loyalty to Duryodhana. Period.

Why did Karna give Kunti a boon? Had see asked for it? NO. It was all the way his ego. Why did Karna made an exception for Arjuna and not Bhima?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 12:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

This is funny and only Karna fan think that way. Karna was defeated by Arjuna in every single battle they had. Every single battle. Karna retreated scared from Arjuna during swyamvar. Scared away. He again retreated scared from Gandharvas leaving Duryodhana behind. Arjuna defated same Gandharvas. He was defeated by Bhima in Rajsuy battle. He ran away thrice from Arjuna during Matasya battle even after seeing his own brother killed. He again ran from Abhimanyu seeing his other brother killed. Arjuna killed his son in front of him and he could do nothing. Nada.

He indeed broke 11 out of 100 bow string of Arjuna and may have attacked Arjuna’s vital part. But then Arjuna attacked just one vital part and he lost his head.

Some Karna fans may feel he would have defeated devas single handedly. But he never did so. Couldn’t even defeat Gandharvas that were lesser powerful than devas.

But other than Karna’s fan many knew Karna could never defeat Arjuna. Do you know the list — Bhishma, Ashwatthama, Drona, Kripa, Parshurma, Surya (yes, you read it right), Krishna, Shiva. And I would like to add one more name to this list — Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 01:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There is no agenda other than keep the records staright. Do you know what is called agenda?

You said — INA men were hanged to death after Bose lost the war against British.

Not a single INA men were hanged after INA trial. Three were found guilty. They were sentenced to life. That sentence was commuted and treated as war heros. So tell me, what is your agenda is telling INA men were hanged? Do you want to brainwash?

By the way did you know Nehru was among the panel of defence for INA? Brainwash or fact?

You said — If you rely on Atlee statement to Bengal governor /lawyer

I would stop you right here. I don’t rely on hersy. There is no official record. Just hersy. British government never endorsed those statements. There is no official recording or paper. And those things don’t define history. I would rather trust the valour of Indian people who fought along with Gandhiji and Bose, I would trust public statement of likes of Shubash Chandra Bose, Rajendra Prasad, Ravindra Nath Tagore, George Bernard Shaw, Albert Einstine and the list goes on.

Would you consider “what Rahul Gandhi said” or “What Kapil Sibbal Said…” or “What Mamta Banerjee said…”? No, these are personal opinion. Not History. Mr Atlee had a similar strature as these leaders. He was a one time PM and not a great one at that. It was his government that engineered partition of India. There is no reason why any Indian shold be proud of any statement by such a person. But then again, there is no proof that he said anything. Nothing official. Jusy heresy.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-the-story-about-Netaji-Subhas-Chandra-Bose-and-Hitler-true/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 01:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Yes, I agree. Karna had no greateness like Krishna or Ved Vyas.

And Karna was WRONG for neither understanding his mother predicament nor her greatness. It was his mother who gave her divine protection. She could have killed her like Ganga killed her sons. But she chose her to live. And just like you, even Karna didn’t understand that he would have worst future had Kunti not let her go. Think! Who would have married the mother of a child? Do you know any single reference? And what would have been Karna’s future with an unwed mother? Better or worse?

Yes, Karna had every right to not forgive his mother. But then that defines who Karna was and not who Kunti was. Karna not forgiving Kunti doesn’t make her any less great but certainly proves Karna to be vengeful and cruel. Tell me how was it Arjuna’s fault Kunti abandoned Karna? Why did he decide to kill Arjuna?

And Finally, Karna had right to not forgive his mother. But at whose expense? Humanity suffered. He remained the part of evil force. And he was killed for his inumerable evil acts. Karna had to forgive himself and not his mother.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 02:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — he doesn’t have to understand his mother

I think neither you have to understand anything.

You said — Kunti to him, is nothing beyond a medium that brought him to this world, that’s it.

Well that could be the definition of every mother. Sadly everytime that would be extremely WRONG definition. Even if someone is “just a medium to bring you to this world” it is not “just”, it is “everything”. If Karna wasn’t brought to this world…

But Kunti didn’t do just that. She ensured her an unparalleled divine protection. Unparalleled. No one else got that. Thanks to Kunti. She ensured Karna has a safe life away from blemished existence of being the son of an unwed mother. But for you and Karna that doesn’t matter.

In that case do you have a better solution? What did Karna do for his mother? NOTHING. Tried to burn her down in Lakshagriha. Kunti, at least she brought him to this world. Karna? He was just a bad omen.

You said — she treats her Daughter in laws like trash,

Can you explain, how?

Was she aware about Draupadi when she suggested that they should share it?

Do you think, she alone was responsibile for Draupadi’s marriage to five husband?

Did Draupadi ever complained?

Do you mean to say that Shiva, too, treated Draupadi like a trash? Afterall wasn’t that the prime reason for Draupadi’s five marriage?

Or you think Shiva too was a cruel?

You said — pandavas themselves were quite evil, they went to hell for a reason.

Are you sure Pandavas were evil? Krishna called them sinless. Krishna also said those who hate them hate me. Do you hate Krishna? Why? Just for your love for evil Karna?

Are you sure Pandavas went to hell? Or could it be that it be that you are delusional? It is a common symptom with Karna’s fans. Let me explain —

Partha! O lord of the earth! Your brothers are not in hell. This was arranged by me and by the great Indra, the king of the gods. O son! There is no doubt that all the kings must see hell. Therefore, for a short while, you faced supreme unhappiness. O king! However, Savyasachi, Bhima, the twins, bulls among men, and truthful in speech, do not deserve to be in hell for a long period of time. O Yudhishthira! Nor does the princess Krishna deserve to be in hell.

Note

  • Dharma arranged the illusion of hell
  • All Kings must see the hell.
  • Pandavas didn’t deserve to be in hell.

Krishna, Shiva and even Dhritrashtra called them sinless. But then you can keep calling them evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 04:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said Every character is grey.They are sinless because they have already paid for their sins

No. That's not what Ved Vyas says or Krishna says. Both talked about two trees — tree of righteousness Yudhishthir and evil Duryodhana. Krishna calls Arjuna sinless (Gita). Dhritrashtra says Pandavas never did anything wrong. Krishna called Karna extremely evil soul. He was born to protect good and destroy evil (Paritranaya sadhunam vinaschayas dushkritam). Now whom did he protect and whom did he destroy?

Tell me, do you think Krishna was evil? Because he said Pandavas were his inseparable existence. He who hates them hates me. Now you call them evil. Can you give any reference where Vyas or Krishna talks about gray character?

For details a check out my episode https://vivek.vnc.in/m/s01e12

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 09:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Mate, thats right they were not hanged to death but were given life imprisonment for waging the war against British

Yes, British government did try to imprison them. Afterall most freedom fighters went to jail to protest against them. But they were forced to commute their sentence. And thats how we Indian's treated their heroes.

You said —These kahkhi jokers try to demean Mahatma Gandhiji and his deeds which is their agenda bcoz Congress have used Gandhiji surname (one family)

Fully agree. Gandhiji and Bose don't belong to contemporary political parties. Neither Congress nor BJP. They belong to our history, our legacy and they should be respected. And that's my agenda. I have no love for Congress. But Gandhiji is beyond those politics.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-the-story-about-Netaji-Subhas-Chandra-Bose-and-Hitler-true/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 09:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Please read. Arjuna was someone whose emotion often overpowered his descision making. Wasn't he someone who gave up his arms at the beginning of the battle ? Tell me why Krishna took up the arms twice?

Bhishma wasn't killing one Akshauhini per day. He was killling 10000. Had he killed one Akshauhini per day Pandavas would have been finished in 7 days.

Shikhandi had boon from Shiva. He was a purush on the 10th day. So no rules were violated.

Your point 2 is incomplete.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 16, 2023 09:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:
  1. Bhishma

You said — Arjuna was not someone who would run away from his duty. He was kshatriya he was responsible for the death of his army.

Actually Arjuna was someone who often let his emotions guide him over his duty. Wasn’t he Arjuna who laid down his weapon at the begining of the battle and Krishna had to recite Gita to him? Why do you think Krishna rose against Bhishma on two occasions? Because Arjuna wasn’t willing to kill Bhishma. Not that he couldn’t. He was not willing. You read the epic and you would understand. Let me quote the reference why Krishna rose against Bhishma —

Day #3

“Krishna witnessed Bhishma’s valour in battle and saw the mildness with which the mighty-armed Partha countered him. In that encounter, Bhishma created an incessant shower of arrows. In the midst of the two armies, he was like the tormenting sun. He was killing the best of the best among the soldiers of Pandu’s son. Bhishma was like the fire of destruction amidst Yudhishthira’s army. The lord Keshava, the destroyer of enemy heroes, could no longer tolerate this. The one with the immeasurable soul thought that Yudhishthira’s army would not be able to survive. In a battle, Bhishma was capable of destroying the gods and the danavas in a single day, not to speak of taking on the sons of Pandu, with their soldiers and their followers, in a fight. The large army of the great-souled Pandava began to flee. Having seen the Somakas shattered and fleeing in that battle, the Kouravas were delighted and advanced to the fight, gladdening the grandfather. He142 thought, ‘For the welfare of the Pandavas, I will armour myself and kill Bhishma today.

Day #10

‘“Vasudeva saw that Partha was fighting mildly. Bhishma was continuously showering down arrows in the battle. Stationed between the two armies, he was as scorching as the sun. He was killing the best of the best in the army of Pandu’s son. Against Yudhishthira’s forces, Bhishma was like the destruction at the end of a yuga. The mighty-armed Madhava, the destroyer of enemy heroes, could not tolerate this. O venerable one! He abandoned Partha’s horses, which had the complexion of silver. Full of anger, the great yogi descended from the great chariot. The powerful one advanced towards Bhishma, with his arms as weapons.

To this Arjuna said

O Madhava! The world will say that you have uttered a falsehood. Let the entire burden be on me. I will kill the one who is rigid in his vows. O Madhava! If it is otherwise in this battle, let there be a curse on my truth and my good deeds. O destroyer of enemies! I will do everything so that the end of the foes is ensured. Behold. As I wish, I will bring down the invincible one who is great in his vows today, like the full moon at the end of an era.’

As you can see, Arjuna wasn’t fighting with full force and only after Krishna’s rage he reluctantly agreed to kill Bhishma to save Krishna from falsehood.

You said — Bhishma's kill was cheating because pandavas brought someone who is not Purush or masculine which was considered cheating at that time especially when bhishma vowed not to fight with someone who is not purush.

Shikhandi was a purush on Day 10. He had boon from Shiva. So if it was a cheating, Shiva was the cheater. Do you really mean that? Bhishma himself offered that path to Pandavas. And the battle was between two armies not two individuals.

You said — But, ask yourself one thing, if they didn’t kill abhimanyu like this, would you even rememer his name? Would you know how great warrior he was?

Yes, He would still be considered a great warrior who single handedly foiled Kauravas plan. You don’t need to die to prove your worth. Arjuna didn’t die. Ashwatthama didn’t die. Shri Rama and Lakshman didn’t die. Don’t you remeber their name? Don’t you consider them great warriors?

You said — It was actually abhimanyu’s win if you see it that way.

It was Abhimanyu’s win not matter whichever way you see. Those cowards couldn’t kill him individually, couldn’t fight in group so killed him unethically. Abhimany won. He foiled Drona’s best laid plan.

You said — But the fact was that his kill was cheating done by pandavas. And the cheating was planned because arjuna couldn’t kill Drona.

Yes. Arjuna couldn’t kill Drona. But it was not because he wasn’t capable as a soldier. He couldn’t kill because he couldn’t bring himself to kill his guru. And that is why he didn’t fight Drona on day #14. Even on day 15, as Dhrishtduymn approached to kill Drona, he was the one who protested that the perceptor must not be killed. Someone who was stopping others from killing Drona, how could he have killed him. Not because of power, but because of passion.

You said — Thirdly, Karna was definetely weaponless as he was cursed by parshurama that he will forget all his vidya when he will need it the most. Karna tried to invoke brahmastra but he couldn’t because of this curse. So the statement you have given is false that karna help brahmastra and varunastra when he was off the chariot.

Let me quote the exact reference to you — (This is after his chariot was struck in earth)

“Where did dharma go then?’ When Vasudeva addressed Radheya in this way, Pandava Dhananjaya remembered all this and was overcome by great rage. Energetic flames of anger seemed to issue out from all the pores on his body and it was extraordinary. ‘ “On seeing this, Karna again invoked brahmastra against Dhananjaya. He showered down arrows and tried to extricate his chariot. Pandava countered those weapons with his own weapons. Kounteya then released another weapon, beloved of the fire god, towards Karna. It blazed fiercely. Karna pacified the fire through a varuna weapon. He covered all the directions with clouds and it was as dark as a rainy day. Pandaveya was not frightened. While Radheya looked on, the valiant one used the vayavya weapon and dispelled all the clouds.

— Chapter 1217(67)

  • Here you can see, very clear mention of Karna using Brahmastra and Varunastra.
  • I have not given any false statement as is clear from the statement.

Hope these references helps you clear your doubt. Difference between unable to kill and unable to bring himself to kill your loved one.

Next time you want to contradict my answer, I would recommend do so with a valid reference. So one reference where Bhishma, Drona or anyone says Arjuna was unable to defeat him. Here Krishna finds Arjuna mild (not feeble). Hope you know the difference between these two words.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Draupadi-regret-not-accepting-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 10:48 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — Should have multiple cross reference.
You guessed it. There are dozens of cross-references to the incidents. Dozens. Karna reretted his behavior. Ashwathama reminds him in Matasya Parva. Drona in Drona Parva. Krishna reminds Arjuna in Karna Vadha Parva. Bhima reminds it befor Duryodhana.

You said — One more thing Bori is Pro-pandas (Proof? There are several but the most prominent being they cut off the digvijaya of Karna, Which like you said most ancient texts mentioned)

Actually most ancient texts do not mention Karna’s Digvijaya. Drona himself reminds he has seen him mostly running away from battle. Forget BORI, the entire Mahabharata is based on “Pro Pandavas”. Vyas recites the narrative by bowing to Nara (Arjuna) and Narayana. Krishna calls Pandvas inseparable from their existence. Shiva praises Arjuna and bless him. They are together called tree of righteousness.

To think of who would be pro evil being whom Krishna calls — The most wicked soul? Krishna wanted him dead. And Krishna was born to destroy evils

BORI is not pro-karna. It is based on authentic references.

You said — By the way the thing Karna said was not not his words, Kunti also said it which is Draupdis mother in law and Drupad also said it and he is her father.

What Karna said was his own design. That’s why he regretted. That’s why he was killed. What he said was against Shiva. Remeber he had blessed Draupadi.

And you didn’t understand Kunti’s statement. It was for herself and NOT for Draupadi. Do you know the difference? Kunti wasn’t wed to Dharma, or Vayu or Indra. She just had relation with them for child.

Draupadi on the other hand was wed to Pandavas. It was polyandry.
Do you know she is counted among Pancha Sati?

So stop trying to justify and evil person. He had no right to comment on Draupadi. None. He paid for his sins.

You said — Even Shri Krishna talk ill about woman amd say they are low-born, Which you will not mention right, Will you?

So a valid reference. your statement doesn’t count.

You said — every one of MB is a criminal even bheesma and Shri Krishna was our 21st judgment.

Thats your view. Karna was the trunk of evil tree Duryodhana even the ancient standard. Even in that standard Krishna who had incarnated to protect good and destroy evil, chose to destroy him. Even then few people brutalized others others protected. Even then few people fled deserting their best friend and other protected their worst enemy.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Karna-support-Draupadi-Vastrapaharana/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 11:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me first correct your sequence

Chapter 53 — Karna announces that I will not fight the battle

The grandfather will see me in the assembly hall, but not in the war. But until he has obtained peace, I will lay down my weapons and all the kings of the earth will not witness my power

So let me point out Karna refused to fight before Bhishma said so.

Chapter 57 — Bhishma refuses to accept the command if Karna fights under him.

There is one condition under which I will willingly become your general, not otherwise. You should hear about this. O lord of the earth! Let Karna or me fight first. This son of a suta always seeks to rival me in battle

You asked, why did Bhishma say so. It is clearly mentioned. Bhishma was a wise general. He didn’t want a subbordinate he couldn’t trust and who seeks to rival him. This would cause a disarray in the army. Also note, Bhishma didn’t refused to fight alongside Karna. He refused to take the command. And that’s a big difference. Karna on the other hand outright rejected to fight.

‘Karna said, “O king! As long as Gangeya7 is alive, I will never fight. I will fight with the one who wields the Gandiva only after Bhishma has been killed.”’

Not only this, he was actually hoping that Bhishma be killed in the battle.

Now the prime question is —

Why Karna didn’t want to fight under Bhishma?

This is clearly mentioned in Chapter 59, the truth of Karna —

What does the dim-witted Bhishma know about the science of rathas? I will repluse the armies of the Pandavas. When they encounter my invincible arrows, the Pandavas and the Panchalas will be dispersed in the ten directions, like bulls before a tiger. Where is the turmoil of battle and the counsel of good advice and where is the aged and evil-souled Bhishma, driven by destiny? He always boasts that he can alone fight the entire universe. With his deluded vision, he does not think that any other man exists. The sacred texts certainly say that we must listen to the aged. But it is also the view that one should not listen to the extremely old. They are like children. O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the commander is credited with qualities, never the warriors. O king! I will never fight as long as Gangeya is alive. But once Bhishma has been slain, I will fight with all the maharathas.’”’

Note:

  • He wanted Bhisma removed from the position of general
  • He lied to Duryodhana that he would kill Pandavas. (Or did he lie to Kunti that he wont? Or to both?)
  • For him, getting the credit was more important than his friend.
  • Once again he wanted Bhishma to be dead.

S U M M A R Y

  1. First Karna refused to fight along side Bhishma. (Chapter 53)
  2. Bhishma refused to keep Karna under his command. He didn’t say he won’t fight along side Karna. But he didn’t wish for a person under his command who may insubbordinate hime. (Chapter 57)
  3. Karna again refuses to fight under Bhishma’s command.
  4. Karna wanted to get the credit for his valour which he felt he wont get under Bhishma. He tries to oust Bhishma from general’s poisiton. Having failed he refused to fight. Again. He also lies to Duryodhana that he would kill Pandavas.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-was-the-actual-reason-behind-Bhishma-not-allowing-Karna-to-participate-in-war/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 11:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Do you have any reason to wish that an evil like Duryodhana should have won?

He tries to kill his cousin by various means — poison, arson,drawning.

He tries to burn down a woman (Draupadi).

He cheats them in gamble.

He tries to mock people in exile. He was protected by the same people. He still had no shame.

He tries to loot a kingdom in distress.

There was no trap. If it were a trap, it was un-necessary. Pandavas could have killed him during Matasya battle or let them die when Gandharvas captured.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karnas-depiction-in-TV-shows-entirely-different-from-that-of-BORI-and-other-authentic-versions/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 11:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Not really. Not all literature talks about Karna. Take example Jaidradh by Maithili Sharana Gupta or Mahabharata by Suryakant Tripathi Nirala or Mahabharata by Rajgopalachari.

Yes, there existed few like Mrityunjay or Rashmirathi. But they never claimed authenticity and were more written by people with leftist mindset and wanted to show exploitation of a poor man, which was not true by a large margin.

I agree it was never about Karna. And it was never about Krishna against Karna. It was Karna against Dharma

You said — Plus Karna was considered as a hero way before TVs, while Arjun came in spot light after TV serials.

I don’t really know which world are you leaving in. Let me give you a few hint —

  • Mahabharata begins with saluation of Nara (Arjuna) and Narayana (Krishna). Not Karna.
  • Mahabharata, the ancient references are full of glory to Arjuna, who Krishna calls his inseparable existence, Shiva calls the greatest warrior in three world, Bhishma, Drona, Parshurma and even Karna considers unparallel. And Ved Vyas didn’t watch TV Shows.
  • Krishna became the charioteer for Arjuna not for Karna as some TV shows would like to show. Now you may say he was the charioteer of Karna and some TV shows wrongly showed him to be the charioteer of Arjuna
  • We have Arjuna award. And it was constituted before TV shows were aired.

In fact most TV shows snatched away the glory of Arjuna. Star Plust TV show deleted Gandharvas battle, and showed Matasya battle a draw where Karna was thrashed trice and ran for cover while Arjuna killed his brother. This is not glorifying Arjuna but Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karnas-depiction-in-TV-shows-entirely-different-from-that-of-BORI-and-other-authentic-versions/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 12:02 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Just one more time. Just show me a reference where Karna said he was coming back to fight for Duryodhana.

See the sequence —
1. Karna abandones Duryodhana and flees. Why? Here is what Karna said — While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away.

  • Note
    • He didn’t say he was coming back to rescue him. He ran away

2. After Karna ran away (his words) Duryodhana fought a long battle. Karna didn’t return till now.

3. Duryodhana was captured and Gandharvas imprisoned them. They wanted to take them away. Karna didn’t return yet.

4. Poeple rushed for help to Pandavas. They debated. Pandavas prepared for the battle and rushed. Karna’s preparation is not over yet.

5. Arjuna tries for a truce with Gandharvas. Then he fights a terrible battle with enough cross-references. Must have taken a long time. Karna still doesn’t return.

6. Arjuna defeats Chitrasent. They had a long discussion. Karna still doesn’t show up.

7. Arjuna brings Duryodhana and Chitrasena to Pandava camp. Again there was a long discussion. Karna hasn’t shown up.

8. Duryodhana is freed. He is miserable. He goes slowly. He camps in the say for the night. Karna doesn’t show up.

Now even if Karna wanted to help Duryodhana, it would be too late. Gandharvas would have captured and taken them prisioner a long time ago.

But was Karna really coming for help.

That late! Is there any reference where Karna said so. NO. A VERY BIG NO.

But here is what Karna said —

  • It is fortunate that you are alive and fortunate that we have met again.
    • Karna wasn’t even hoping to meet again. It was fortunate
  • You have freed yourself from that superhuman battle. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! O great king! There is no other man in this world who is like you, or can achieve what you have done in battle with your brothers.”
    • Karna clearly says he didn’t seem capable to achive this what he thinks Duryodhana achieved, which was actually Arjuna’s achievement.

All the references shows Karna fled —

  • He said so himself
  • He thinks it was luck (fortune) that they met again. He wasn’t expecting
  • He felt no one could defeat them. Thats why he fled. Why would he return then?

THERE IS NOT A SINGLE REFERENCE THAT THE COWARD HAD ANY PLAN TO RETURN TO PROTECT DURYODHANA. AND IT WAS ALREADY WAY TO LATE.

Now if you want to say something, say with proof.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-is-Karnas-depiction-in-TV-shows-entirely-different-from-that-of-BORI-and-other-authentic-versions/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 12:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

What you think is your prerogative. But if Krishna feels and desires, nothing is impossible. Remember, Arjuna was capable enough to wield great Pashupata, and all other weapons of the world.

Now Krishna loved Arjuna as his inseparable existence. Do you think, he intended to make public mockery of Arjuna? I don't think so. Do you think there was anyone else capable enough to have Krishna as his charioteer?

By the way, do you have any reason for your 'thinking'’?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Karna-Arjuna-fight-was-on-for-a-long-time-Krishna-offered-his-Sudharshan-chakra-to-Arjuna-which-tge-latter-refused-What-would-have-been-the-impact-if-Arjuna-had-killed-Karna-with-Sudharshan-Chakra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 16, 2023 12:56 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh! But Karna was a molester. Firstly he led the pack that wanted to burn Draupadi alive, for their own failure. Second he ordered Draupadi be disrobed. But his lust had no end. He wanted to see Draupadi in pain, and that's why he planned Ghosha yatra. He was a lusty man who found more satisfaction in other's pain than in his own son! (Karna's own word)

You said Whole world insulted him . including Draupady,Arjuna and Bheema

Draupadi never insulted him. Never. And no one was a bigger abuser than Karna in whole of Mahabharata. He insulted everyone.

You said Karna is the only competitor to Arjuna.

Funny! Karna could never stand Arjuna. Not once. He fled everytime. Last time, when he couldn't flee, he whined. In Gandharva battle, he deemed Arjuna's valour unimaginable, in Matasya he fled seeing his brother killed. In fact, Ashwatthama and Drona proved better challenger to Arjuna.

Please don't say illogical things. For authentic epic follow

https://vivek.vnc.in/m

Answer: https://www.quora.com/If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 17, 2023 07:53 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

It's what you feel…

Evidence? None.

Karna wasn't overpowering Arjuna. Arjuna wasnt fighting with all his heart and this irritated Krishna and Bhimasema, both of them knew Karna can't survive this long against Arjuna.

But it doesn't change the fact that

  • Arjuna was trained by Shiva who considered him worthy to hold Pashupata
  • Arjuna was given all the divine weapons of the world proving his calibre
  • Krishna referred him his own inseparable existence. He is himself in Arjuna. Now if you think, Krishna can't hold Sudarshana, I would conclude by saying that's what you think… But question is what Krishna thought?

Now tell me, what do you think would have happened, had Arjuna accepted the offer? Do you think Krishna would have said, I was joking? Or he would have let Arjuna fail? The problem is you overestimate the power of Sudarshana chakra and underestimate the power of Dharma, devotion and friendship. Arjuna was a Krishna devotee and chakra wasn't more powerful than his devotion.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Karna-Arjuna-fight-was-on-for-a-long-time-Krishna-offered-his-Sudharshan-chakra-to-Arjuna-which-tge-latter-refused-What-would-have-been-the-impact-if-Arjuna-had-killed-Karna-with-Sudharshan-Chakra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 17, 2023 08:42 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The answer is based on the authentic epic and not on some fantasy tale. So, please avoid 'cheap' words. Ignoring an answer could be an option.

And 'an extremely wicked soul ’ whose even thought burns the limb (Krishna's words) deserved hate from a civil society.

The answer you quoted gives only indirect references that are known as heresy. For example, Ashwatthama, who affected by his father's death utterd a few words. He wasn't even there when Karna died .

The most important of all references are the one which are in the main context. And in BORI main context it is clearly mentioned Karna invoked Brahmastra and Varunastra after his chariot was stuck. He was NOT unarmed. People may feel it unjust because he was not on his chariot. But when had that trunk of evil tree cared about anyone to be on the chariot? NEVER. He was killed for his misdeeds. Period.

With best regards

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-killed-when-he-was-weaponless/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 22, 2023 09:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

A tika is, as you said, just an interpretation. And interpretation can't be called reference. When you call it reference that's when it becomes ' fake reference’. Period.

Now interpretation is just your understanding. And your and mine understanding could be different. Now calling someone else's interpretation as misinterpretation is hypocrisy. but calling your own interpretation as reference from Mahbahrata is pure deceit, worse than misinterpretation.

Tell me, which edition of Mahabharata contains this shloka? Give the chapter and shloka number.

And please read Gandhi's view on violence and Krishna's on non violence yourself and not just in tika. Then we can debate.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharat-where-is-written-slok-number-also-ahinsa-parmo-Dharma-Dharmao-hinsa-tthaiva-ch/answer/Tarun-Bansal-76
Creation time: Apr 22, 2023 09:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let's try to understand the full context, and the claims.

  • Karna got his curses for his wrong doings and NEVER without a reason. The Brahman said, he remained distracted because he carries malice intention for someone else without a reason.
  • His wheel was stuck and he was immobilized. He indeed tried to extract his wheel. He requested Arjuna to wait till he could extract is wheel. He wasn't granted the time, and was killed before he could extricate it. Duryodhana is right there.

BUT

  • Does that mean, he didn't fight after his wheel got stuck?
    • No. There is clear reference, that he fought. Remember, his weapons were free. He attacked Arjuna with Brahmastra and Varunastra. And he died in the process.
  • Is it fair, to attack a chariotless warrior?
    • It had become a norm at Kurukshetra.
    • Abhimanyu was chariotless and also weaponless. Karna did attack him.
    • On the 14th day, Karna destroyed Bhimasena's chariot and continued to attack him.
    • On the same day Bhimasema broke several chariot of Drona. He never asked for recess.
  • Can a warrior without chariot fight one on chariot?
    • Parshurama fought Bhishma without chariot for 21 days.
    • Arjuna defeated entire Kuru forces on 14th day, again without chariot
  • What were Karna's options
    • Based on precedence, he could have changed his chariot like Drona or could have fought from ground like examples given above.
    • There was no precedence of recess in the war. No one asked for it.
    • He may have surrendered.
  • Why people say, he was killed unfairly
    • Perhaps, they expected Arjuna to follow rules created on the day #1 and not fight chariotless warrior. But, there were a few points —
      • The rule was broken long ago and repeatedly. Firstly by Kuru forces including Karna.
      • Karna wasn't a chariotless warrior. He lost it in the process of battle. That's an important difference.
    • Have you seen any accident? You always blame the other party for their fault.
    • Have you heard a death in hospital? Mostly doctors gets blamed for medical negligence. It is a human tendency to find a culprit for the death of someone.

Duryodhana didn't say, I fled away and Karna died. Did he? Did he take his responsibility? Does that mean, he didn't flee? People are not always fair. They just expect other's to be. They think, it's ok if I killed Abhimanyu despite being chariotless and weaponless, I should get a fair war. They think what I do is strategy and what other's do is unethical.

Now, a question for you – Why are you supporting an extremely wicked and evil soul? How can someone support an evil who tried to disrobe a woman in public, tried to burn her alive, humiliate people in distress. How can you admire a coward who runs away as his own brother is killed or his friend, his King is captured by the enemies?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Karna-killed-when-he-was-weaponless/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 22, 2023 09:20 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said – Karna couldn’t defeat ghandarva because he was drunk

Karna couldn't defeat Gandharvas because he felt no one can defeat them (Karna's own words) and he fled for his life (Again Karna's own words)

If Karna was drunk, he wasn't aware of that fact. Even Duryodhana was not aware. Ved Vyas was not aware. There is not a single explicit reference that Karna failed because he was drunk. I challenge you to quote an explicit reference that says Karna failed because he was drunk. There are several that says he fled because he was afraid.

You said –ghatochkacha who was way more powerful than ghandravas

This is from Karna's fan wiki. Nowhere in the epic the power of Gandharvas and Ghatotkach is compared. And Karna couldn't really defeat Ghatotkach. He killed him using Vasvi shakti.

And nothing changes the fact that Karna fled scared. Not drunk

You said – Krishna sweared

Krishna also said –

  • Karna without divine armour and Vasavi Shakti is like a extinguished fire and like a defanged serpent.
  • Earth may change the direction, planet's may fall from their position, Karna could never kill Arjuna
  • Shalya is more powerful than Karna and Bhishma and only Yudhishthira, in all the three worlds can kill him.

So, you need more wisdom to understand what Krishna said and why.

I would recommend, focus on what happened –

  • Karna fled Arjuna after swayamvar comparing him with Vishnu, Indra, Ram and personification of knowledge of weapon's
  • Karna fled Gandharvas thinking no one can defeat them.
  • Karna fled twice in Matasya battle and third time rendered unconscious. He fled as his brother was killed.
  • Karna fled Abhimanyu multiple times. Again even after seeing his brother killed.
  • Bhimasena defeated Karna so many times that Duryodhana lost 33 brothers trying to save him.
  • Karna fled seeing Arjuna approach on day 14
  • Karna was vanquished by Arjuna on day 16
  • Karna could do nothing as his son was killed by Arjuna in front of him on day 17.
  • Karna acknowledged superiority of Arjuna in front of Shalya
  • He died begging for recess.

Unless you can give a direct quote that Karna failed against Gandharva because he was drunk (intoxicated) we can end this discussion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 24, 2023 04:30 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No BORI Scholar said anything anything like that. Prof Suthankar never said that. Critical edition doesn't say that. Ancient edition doesn't say that. Karna never said. Duryodhana never said.

Only Karna fan say so. Without any evidence. They had a party night before. But that too doesn't say any one was intoxicated. Intoxicated people don't go to battle. Karna was drunk enough not to fight but not drunk enough to run away! Wow.

Was he drunk during –

  • Swayamvar battle
  • Rajsuy yagna
  • Matasya battle
  • Battle against Abhimanyu
  • Day 14
  • Day 16

Keep your imagination up. You have no facts.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1

Content language: English

Content:

Keep your imagination high.

The problem is you have zero reference, just conspiracy theories.

  • Karna lost swayamvar battle has clear reference. But for you there is some clapping Kings, the same people who saw him scared later. Do you have a proof, Karna won? A direct proof? Karna had no answer when Kripacharya asked him during Matasya battle, if he ever defeated any Pandava.
  • Arjuna killed Karna's son in front of him.

So, if you don't come with reference next time, I will have to ignore your comments as spams.

And yes, I am a Arjuna fan and hate evils like Karna.

But why do you hate Arjuna?

Content:

Give a reference not just opinion. So far all you have is your opinion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 25, 2023 06:05 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

No, you don’t love Arjuna. You lie even to yourself. This is not the kind of word some one uses for some one he loves —

“Arjuna like a coward killed karna and also Arjuna was defeated by footsoldiers samsaphaktas and susharma he had to use vajra to kill them 😂 he was defeated by nikumbha achyuta, and Dwarka robbers who defeated Arjuna using bamboo sticks Arjuna was defeated by Drona on 14th day and ran away Arjuna was defeated by karna on 16th day and ran away”

I can contradict all these points with valid references. But there is no need. because you have no references. But see your emoji. You relish Arjuna as “coward”. Before calling someone coward, at least show courage to admit, you hate Arjuna.

Now coming to evil and follower of Dharama

What is the definition of evil and follower of Dharma? Someone tries to burn down a family by deceit, evil or follower of Dharma? Someone tries to burn down a bride, evil or follower of dharma? Someone tries to strip a woman naked in public, evil or follower of Dharma? Someone betrays his own friend and runs away, evil or follower of Dharma?

Krishna incarnated to destroy evils and protect good. He wanted to destroy Karna and called him extremely evil soul. He also called him trunk of evil Duryodhana tree. Who is evil here?

If you call Karna Dharmatma then all molester and criminal of this world too becomes Dharmatma.

But tell me, and with reference, what are the acts of Dharma Karna did? Did he ever save a woman whom others evils tried to burn down? Did he ever save a village in distress? Did he try to save a kingdom whom looters tried to plunder? Did he ever save his worse enemy?

Who is Dharmatma?

  • One who tries to burn down a family or one who tries to protect a family from burning down?
  • One who tries to molest a woman or one who tries to protect women's dignity even if they are the family of your worse enemy?
  • One who tries to plunder a kingdom or comes forward to protect it?

Reference, my dear, reference? Your opinions doesn't matter. So me the ‘acts’ and not just empty words spoken in condolence.

Finally, you said — karna on 17th day wasn’t present when vrishasena was killed he was fighting with other warriors and vrishasena defeated Arjuna and nakula and Bhima and the trio attacked vrishasena in a group like cowards and killed the 17th yr old and only later did karna appear.

Again, you have stories and NO facts. Let me give you clear reference.

The son of a suta saw that his son had been struck by arrows and had fallen down from his chariot. He was tormented because his son had been slain. Powerfully and violently, he advanced on his chariot towards the ratha Kiriti.”

“On seeing that Vrishasena had been killed, Karna was overcome by anger and rage. Sudden tears of sorrow flowed down from Vrisha’s eyes.

Once again, next time you talk, stop ‘lol’. Give some rerference. You do ‘lol’ and cheap emoji’s because you don’t have references.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 25, 2023 06:19 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Yudhishthira's actions can be called as judgemental error and not an evil mindset. While, he did bait his wife, he didn't bait her, till he lost himself. Can you not see, the respect he had for his wife? And can you tell me, what would have happened if he had not baited her? Would she be considered slave or not? What does Mahabharata says? She was still a slave! NOW think, why did Yudhishthira baited her.

You said – he will forever be remembered as a man who gambled his wife.

By whom? His wife herself exonarated her. He is deemed as tree of righteousness by Krishna and Vyas. So, how does it matter, what other's thinks?

You said –Krishna is lord Vishnu himself, Lord Shiva and lord Vishnu are brahman, they are above concept of good and bad.

Again, this is not what Krishna preached. He said he incarnated to destroy evils and protect good. They are the propagator of Dharma and not beyond it. At least thats the gist of Gita and Upanishads when they say yato dharmasya tato jayah. We can't differentiate good from God

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 30, 2023 01:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said –By the whole world, if I say talk about pandava, everyone will remember they gambled draupadi.

No. Not by the whole world. By that world who hasn't read Mahabharata but formed their views based on contemporary writing. Not by that world who respects the words of Krishna and Ved Vyas, who called him the tree of righteousness and his inseparable existence.

And you didn't answer, if Yudhishthira had not baited her, would she be a slave or not? What does Mahabharata say?

You said – Krishna protects the dharma that is way beyond than just good but that is for the world, but that doesn't mean he is bound by good or bad.

If you trust Krishna's words 'sanbhawami yuge yuge’, then yes. Dharma is embodiment of good, destruction of evil, and cause of Krishna's avatar.

Answer Comment

Content:

You said – Hindus don't believe everything that is written in books blindly.

Hindus never followed blindly. And epic is not a written book. Those who read the epic with open mind will see the rationale behind, why Krishna and Ved Vyas called Yudhishthira righteous.

The problem is with those people who do not read authentic epic and follow modern interpretations blindly.

Take your own example. You weren't able to answer one simple question I raised in my previous two comments – What if Yudhishthira had not baited Draupadi, was she considered lost or not?

Is it so difficult to answer this question⁉️ This shows you understood the problem but still not ready to trust Krishna. That's the definition of being blind.

You said – dharma transcends just “good”.

Agree. But it goes in the direction of good and not evil. Everytime to establish Dharma, you protect good and destroy evils.

  • Paritranaya sadhunam vinaschayas dushkritam, Dharma sansthapnarthaya, sambhami yuge yuge
  • Dharmo rakshatih Rakshitaah
  • Yato dharmah tato jayah

Krishna protected good, destroyed evils and ensured the victory of righteousness.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 01, 2023 08:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Looks like it is difficult, for you, to answer this question, because you still couldn't answer this question. And unless you answer the question you won't understand. Everyone who would run away from that question would grasp Krishna's message. And you won't be able to understand it.

Anyone who simplifies the matter as he gambled away his wife will consider him a shit till the time –

  1. They realise Yudhishthira first gambled himself and his brothers and didn't gambled her till the end.
  2. Answer to my one question you have been trying to avoid.

Draupadi understood the answer, Krishna understood it, Ved Vyas understood it. But yes, few won't. They NEVER will.

You said –Kauravas were following the dharma of the soldiers, but they were not on the good side.

You are right. But you miss the point. The soldiers were following their true paths. They weren't evils. They weren't rapists, they weren't conspirators. They were good.

This doesn't change Krishna's pronouncement to establish righteousness, to protect good and destroy evil.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-do-people-hate-Karna/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 01, 2023 09:10 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I already answered about Kaurava soldiers. They weren't evils. They did no wrong. Just duty. That makes them good. But that doesn't change the fact that path of Dharma goes through good. Evils doing bad deeds doesn't become part of Dharma.

You said – And what question are you taking about?

While you may find my question repeated several times in the chain, for your convenience I would repeat it one more time.

If Yudhishthira had not gambled Draupadi, was she already lost along with her husband or not?

Now do you think you can answer this question?

You said –It doesn't matter who yudhishthira gambled first,

Actually it makes all the difference. It tells if Yudhishthira valued her more or less than himself. Don't ever miss the sequence. And this becomes more important if you can afford to answer my question.

You said – So no way he is righteous

That's your view. Not Krishna's, Not Vyas. Not Indra. Not Dhritrashtra. Not even Shakuni.

You said –but he doesn't even have the guts to protect his wife…

Guts doesn't define righteousness. Even Shri Ram couldn't protect his wife. That doesn't make him adharmi. Even Harishchandra sold his wife. Krishna migrated from Mathura to Dwarka. Didn't he have the guts to fight Jarasandha.What you need to understand is the context. The Point is, sometimes peace requires more courage than use of the brute force.

Gandhiji spent years in British jail. He didn't apply for clemency. He didn't gave in writing he will not go against Britishers. He didn't take any pension. No, he wasn't an agent of British. It is a wishful thinking of those whose idol did write multiple mercy pleas, accepted pension, supported Britishers openly, coined two nation theory and didn't participate in any freedom movements after getting the mercy plea approved.

So neither of your baseless arguments are related to partition.

Answer Comment

Content:

This is called misinterpretation. Gandhiji NEVER said, don't lift weapon because it goes against Dharma. NEVER.

What he did say is non violence is superior than violence. And guess what, Shri Rama and Krishna did the same. Rama sent Angada as his emissory for peace after crossing over to Lanka and Krishna himself acted as an emissory of peace. And they weren't fools and they weren't talking about animal cruelty.

But fools often mistake non violence for cowardice. And then, they miss out what Gandhiji said “If I have to choose between violence and cowardice, I will choose violence”

Look he didnt call violence is adharma, he said, non violence is superior. And today, the world agrees to this notion.

And either way, what is beyond doubt is the shloka mentioned in parent question is fake. It doesn't have a mention in Mahabharata.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharat-where-is-written-slok-number-also-ahinsa-parmo-Dharma-Dharmao-hinsa-tthaiva-ch/answer/Tarun-Bansal-76
Creation time: Jun 05, 2023 04:39 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

He who hates Arjuna, hates me.

—Shri Krishna

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-the-Mahabharata-why-didnt-Karna-protect-Draupadi-instead-taking-part-in-harassing-her/answer/Chandan-Yadav-28
Creation time: Jun 09, 2023 12:41 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You may not be aware, but during Mahabharata age, there was no whatsapp chat.

But there were conversastions. Face to Face. Direct.

One such conversastion happened in Kairata Parva, when Krishna meets Pandavas after Draupadi’s humiliation and their exile. And here is the firs reference —

‘Janardana then spoke to Partha, “You are mine and I am yours. All that is mine is yours too. He who hates you also hates me. He who follows you also follows me. O invincible one! You are Nara and I am Hari Narayana. We are the rishis Nara and Narayana, born from that world in this world. O Partha! O descendant of the Bharata lineage! You are no other than I. I am no other than you. O bull among the Bharata lineage! No one can know any difference that exists between us.”

But this is not the only reference. When after the death of Abhimanyu, Arjuna vows to kill Jaidradha next day, Krishna “chats” with Daruka. And here is the chat history —

My wives, my friends, my relatives and my kin are not dearer to me than Arjuna, Kunti’s son, is.

He further says —

Tomorrow, the gods, the gandharvas, the pishachas, the serpents, the rakshasas and all the worlds will know me as Savyasachi’s well-wisher. He who hates him, hates me too. He who follows him, follows me too. Use your intelligence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of my body.

Now that you have the direct chat history of Krishna, I would recommend what Krishna recommended — Use your intellegence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of Krishna and He who hates him, hates Krishna.

Regards,

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-the-Mahabharata-why-didnt-Karna-protect-Draupadi-instead-taking-part-in-harassing-her/answer/Chandan-Yadav-28
Creation time: Jun 11, 2023 09:58 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

My Dear,

It's not just BORI. It's everywhere. And by everywhere, I mean literally everywhere except those who don't know anything about authentic epic. Who don't know Krishna Shakha and Partha Sarathi connection. Everyone who sees the light and everyone who uses the intelligence.

But then, I know there are people who live in the other part, where light hasn't reached and who don't think beyond modern social media and WhatsApp chat. Yes, I can see that world. Even pity that world.

I have shown you the proof. Can you show a contradiction? Nah!

The fact remains — He who hated Arjuna hates Krishna. And those who hate Krishna are destined to live in delusion.

In Bhagwat Gita Krishna calls him sinless. In Udyoga Parva, Dhritrashtra says the same. In Kairata and Karna Vadha Parva Shiva says the same.

We all Shiva, Krishna, and Parshuram will continue to live in delusion.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-the-Mahabharata-why-didnt-Karna-protect-Draupadi-instead-taking-part-in-harassing-her/answer/Chandan-Yadav-28
Creation time: Jun 13, 2023 11:01 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Three points of correction

You said Mahatma Gandhi did not foresee the tragedy of Partition.He should have known as he was a leader of the masses.

Mahatma Gandhi was the only one who saw the exact magnitude of tragedy and disaster and that's why he opposed it with all his efforts. And to quote lord Mountbatten “No one was listening to him in the mid summer of 1947”

He was not the official signatory on partition plan. He conceded on realization there is no other and less violent alternative left by frenetic like Jinnahas and Sawarkars. Not only Gandhiji but also Bose and Bhagat Singh condemned these fanatics.

To understand what Gandhiji did during partition search for Calcutta history between Aug 15 – Aug 27, 1947 also known as Miracle of Calcutta.

You said —He should have insisted on total transfer of population on religious lines,that would have been most practical.

India was NEVER going to be partioned on lines of religion for Hindu isn't a relegion. It is the original definition of all inclusiveness. ekam satyam vipra bahudha vadanti.

The only way to divide nation on the lines was to create a new religion similar and opposite to Islam. Only fanatics could have killed the spirit of being Hindu with a mask of being Hindu. Tell me how did snakes and other wildlife survive after great flood during Matasya Avatar?

And what you call practical is not even a good theory.

  • Do you know the magnitude of disaster during transfer of partition just on Punjab border? Now imagine a similar transfer in every citi and Mohalla. It would have been a 1000x disaster.
  • Now imagine when you ask Shikh and Hindus to return to India leaving their properties behind! Would they live like refusees like Kashmiri Pandits or fight other Hindus and Shikhs for their share in land and property? This conflict would have continued for generations. Wiped out more population.
  • What about Shikhs demanding a country on religion lines and Assam on ethnicity? How many partition would have been practical?

You said — in order to keep his image of Mahatma intact

No. Not inorder to keep his image intact. It was to keep the spirit of Hindu alive. Do you know the spirit of being Hindu? ॐ सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः ।सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः । सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु ।मा कश्चित् दुःख भाग्भवेत् ॥ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

Have you heard about Shivi, Dhadhichi, and Neelkantha?

You said — which is creating all sorts of today's problems India is facing.

Two nations were created in 1947. One on relegious lines, other on Hindu (secular) principle. Both faced their own challenges. Which of these two performed better? Which of the two is more peaceful and more prosperous? Would you like India's fate similar to Pakistan? Would you like to live in such a country?

That said, as I see, Mahabharata is beyond Itihaas. Itihaas has it's own limitations. It is dated and becomes out dated. Mahabharata is epic, far Better and useful than Itihaas.
