Answer Comment

Content:

You said – Other so- called great people’s opinions, etc but only must concentrate on bare truths.

You know what, for psychos, history is biased, real great people of history are dubbed 'so called great people’, their contemporary observations becomes mere opinions and their own WhatsApp university knowledge becomes '’bare truths"

But then, those “baseless truths" are just baseless opinions. Period.

History can be learnt from events and references from contemporary world not from fantasies they create based on their biased miss—learnings.

People should follow credible sources And not just biased views.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Should-I-firmly-believe-that-Mahatma-Gandhi-was-a-good-person-despite-knowing-his-secret-roles-in-the-division-of-India/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 05, 2023 09:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Nice answer. But that too is judgemental.

Let's talk about Shantanu, first. He did wish to marry Satyavati. And lust did have a part in it. But he had other reason too. He said, he needed more son's to ensure throne is protected. Can you fault with this? Think, how many people died young – Chitrangada, Vichitraveer, Pandu… What if Bhishma had died and Shantanu had no other son?

And contrary to popular belief, Shantanu didn't marry someone of his daughter's age. Satyavati had a son who was of a similar age as Shabtanu’s son.

Now let's talk about Bhishma, next. Here you endorse the popular theory that Bhishma committed the kingdom to an unborn. But don't we all do it? Do we know the future of a child before their education? Who is more important for a Kingdom? The reigning King or a Prince? Bhishma chose his father and the King over himself, same as Shri Ram did eons ago. As you say, pawns are not at fault. Wasn't Bhishma too a mere pawn in the hand of destiny?

But let me offer an alternative perspective —

What happens when a pawn reaches Queens spot (in chess)

No one is merely a pawn. They too get a chance to checkmate a king. And when the pawn Satyavati reached the Queen's spot, she was the descision maker. And she did make decisions. It was her descision that Dhritrashtra was barred from being the King. And some may say, that made Dhritrashtra bitter enough and his unfulfilled ambition was reflected in Duryodhana. True or false, it doesn't change the fact that Satyavati was not merely pawn.

But we are shifting blames on the innocents

The real blame belongs to them, whom Krishna and Vyas called the evil tree. Dhritrashtra, Duryodhana, Karna, Shakuni and Duhshasan.

We can't blame a man for loving a woman or a son for loving his father or a queen of sidestepping an unworthy prince.

The blame should be with selfish,greedy conniving villain's alone.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Satyavati-the-cause-of-all-Mahabharat/answer/Preeti-A-1
Creation time: Jul 08, 2023 03:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Your opinion could become a fine point for a fictional tale of Mahabharata where Satyavti’s parent may blackmail Shantanu when Devbrata returns, but then there would be a couple of flaws in that narrative apart from the fact that it would digress from ancient Vyas Mahabharata.

  1. If Shantanu married Satyavati before Bhisma's return from Ganga's place
    1. Satyavati may already have had her son (it had been many years) who might already been the crown prince. In such a case Bhishma won't have a say. The only way he could have been a contender was to fight a battle and not make a promise.
    2. There was no scope of Satyavti's father to blackmail Shantanu. They were already married and there was no trump card left with them. Can you think of any, that was possible without Satyavti's direct involvement?
    3. Why won't Bhishma accept the throne after the death of his brothers, if he wasn't the one to make a promise? Why did he remain silent? There could have been a partition of the kingdom even then. Or he could have been made Kingdom of another smaller place.

IN AN ALTERNATIVE NARRATIVE…

If you choose to right a fictional narrative, I would recommend

  1. Making Satyavati a kingspin, and not a pawn. Without she pulling the threads story won't move forward. No one could have more sway on Shantanu. Something like Kekai, while her family playing Manthara.
  2. After death of her son's she still wanted to hold on so…

So in both narratives, Vyasa or yours, I don't see Satyavti as a pawn. If you ever write this fiction, you would get a reader in me

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Satyavati-the-cause-of-all-Mahabharat/answer/Preeti-A-1
Creation time: Jul 09, 2023 04:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Glad to know you read and liked my book “The Accursed God”. Would really appreciate if you can review the book on Amazon and social media. Reviews help.

It is nice to have such a healthy discussion. So let us take it a bit farther.

You said — But Vyasa portrays her as pawn.

I don’t see her as a pawn. She had major share in descision making. She was the one who rejected Dhritrashtra twice — once before his birth knowing his fate and second during the succession.

And from another perspective, who wasn’t really a pawn in the hand of destiny. If she was a pawn, she was a pawn who reached the queen’s spot.

You said — If heir was reason for marriage, why wait for 24 years for devvrat to return and then marry someone. When Ganga leaves and king is left with no queen and no heir. There was no guarantee either would return. Second marriage makes more sense here to have more sons as backup.

Actually this is the point that proves it wasn’t just politics. Because Shantanu did wait all those years, which he wouldn’t have, had it been just politics.

He was left not just without an heir, but with a bigger tragedy of loosing so many sons and a wife. He may not have had the urge to go for another wife. And as years passed, he may even have thought to go the way of King Bharat, and declare someone else his heir. It was the mindset of a sanyasi — detached from the world!

But then, Devvrata returned. He became happy again. Life gave him a second chance. Second chance to love. And he fell in love. The detachement turned to attachment. And he needed another dire reason to love. And that reminded him that he should have more sons to secure Hastinapur. That may just have been excuse.

And as I see both are natural human emotions — being detached in the face of great misery and becoming attached again, when life showers its blessings. And I believe this is what Ved Vyas represented in his epic saga.

In that alternative world…

What is agreeable

Satyavati, like Kekai and so many other queens like the wife of Rana Uday Singh emotionally blackmailed her husband to nominate her son as the crown Prince. And Shantanu, like Uday Singh, may have succumed to the blackmail and decided to remove Devvrata like Uday Singh did to Maharana Pratap.

But what doesn’t fit in

Shantanu may have declined throne to Devvrata or Devvrata may have backed off out of the respect for his father’s wishes (like Maharana Pratap did). But in neither case he needed to take an oath. It served no purpose. Rama didn’t need to take the oath. Devvrata would have remained Devvrata.

Second, after the death of Shantanu, Chitrangada and Vichitraveer, there was no reason for Bhishma not to become the King.

Just a thought…

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Satyavati-the-cause-of-all-Mahabharat/answer/Preeti-A-1
Creation time: Jul 09, 2023 09:53 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Nice story. But that's it. It's just an added story to make Karna great. There is no authentic reference.

Neither is there a single authentic reference in Sanskrit that uses the term danveer for Karna. None. Just another fable.

He did perform charity. No doubt. But then so did many other people of the time. There is no comparitive analysis done between Karna and others. None. Just another fable.

What you refer as donation of Kavacha, Karna himself called it exchange! He refused to give Kavacha without exchange. Is it the definition of daan? And he knew Indra as Indra and was prepared to offer a bargain.

Answer: https://philosophyofreligion1.quora.com/Why-is-Karna-called-Daanveer-42
Creation time: Jul 10, 2023 06:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hey, welcome back after a long time !

You said – Karna badly over ran the entire Pandavas army before that.

While we may disagree on “badly” and “entire” and Sanjay, Dhritrashtra, and even your evil hero may side with me on this, I will let this point slide away for the time being.

I have rather two questions –

  1. Is your point relevant either for the question or my answer?

Question is if Karna spared Arjuna's life? Answer is NO. On the contrary he fled with his army. Frightened. And in a way Arjuna spared his life by letting them withdraw. Statements of Dhritrashtra, Sanjay and Karna points to the same conclusion.

2. What is the point you want to make in defence of that evil man and why?

Karna and Kaurava did fight till they couldn't. Karna did shower arrows but not on entire army. His Valor was gone against just a single person like it had happened every time in the past.

But say, Karna did show great valour, does that change the fact that he was an extremely wicked 🖤 soul (Krishna’s words and intent)?

Why do you cheer for such an evil man? Why do you relish that he may have won against those, whom Krishna called his own? Why support evil?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-would-Mahabharata-have-gone-if-Karna-had-killed-Arjuna-instead-of-stopping-his-arrow-because-of-the-impending-sunset/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 11, 2023 07:10 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You asked — Where it was written that karna fled away? Where it was written that Arjuna spared the life of karna?

Glad you asked. Here is the direct quote —

We could not see anything, favourable or unfavourable. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! The great archers were frightened of fighting in the night. With all their horses, they therefore withdrew.

Dhritarashtra said, “Arjuna killed all of us easily, as he wished. I don’t think Yama would have escaped, had he attacked him in a battle.

‘Sanjaya replied, “They were killed, wounded and shattered. They were deprived of their armour and the vehicles that bore them in war. Their voices were distressed. Those insolent ones grieved and were defeated by the enemy. The Kouravas went to their camps and sought counsel and advice from each other. They were like serpents that had been defanged and had lost their poison, having then been trod on by the foot.

Here is what your hero, Karna, himself said —

‘Arjuna is always firm, skilled and persevering. He understands and when the time is right, Adhokshaja287 instructs him. We were deceived by him today, because of that sudden shower of weapons.

And funny, you think, Arjuna didn’t spare his life. He left battle field alive, isn’t that a proof. Then let me remind you Matasya battle. He was rendered unconcious. How do you think he lived?

You said — Arjuna was also fighting with karna at that time. He couldn't save anyone from defeat.

Karna disagrees with you. Read what he said — “sudden shower of weapons”. And it is not Arjuna’s duty to save others from defeat. His duty wat to defeat.

You said — Where is Arjun's valour when he can't save defeat of his whole army from a single person?

Yes Karna was overpowering Pandavas. But where did you get the point that he can’t save his army? How do you think, his army was saved? Do you think, Karna felt pity on them? No read on.

You said — At last he countered karna and kaurava forces fled but not karna.

Really? Can you quote a reference tha Karna didn’t flee? I couldn’t find any reference. But What I could find is —

The great archers were frightened of fighting in the night. With all their horses, they therefore withdrew.279

After this line, I don’t see Karna standing or fighting. And why do you think Dhritrashtra, Sanjay and even Karna forgot to mention that Karna stood back in the battle?

You said — Pandavas are much more wicked soul than karna.

Can you quote a reference where it is said that Pandavas were wicked (forgot more wicked thatn Karna). This is your opinion. Can you give a reference? Nah! You can’t.

Because Krishna, Shiva, Dhritrashtra everyone called them sinless. So please provide a reference that Pandavas were more wicked than Karna. Please provide a direct quote and not your opinion.

You said — Can you have the courage to accepts Pandavas faults!

Do you mean based on your opinions? Do you mean, without any valid reference? Do you mean going against Krishna’s words? Do you mean knowing what Krishna said — He who hates Arjuna, hates me!

No. I don’t have the courage to go against Krishna, I don’t have the courage to go against the authentic epic reference. I don’t have the courage to call a tree of righteousness as wicked, just because you want me to believe.

But if you can prove Krishna and Shiva wrong, with valid reference, I would be keen.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-would-Mahabharata-have-gone-if-Karna-had-killed-Arjuna-instead-of-stopping-his-arrow-because-of-the-impending-sunset/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 12, 2023 09:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Milord, I refuse to answer the question as the answer may incriminate me.”

This is the fifth amandment right in USA. Good point. But I feel there is a valid reason for fiction to exist

Answer: https://www.quora.com/What-books-related-to-Mahabharat-should-readers-avoid-reading-whether-it-is-a-fictional-novel-based-on-Epic-s-characters-or-full-story-What-editions-should-one-avoid/answer/Sesha-21
Creation time: Jul 17, 2023 08:55 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said –He didn't choose bheem possibly because he knew duryodhan can take on bheem in mace fight.

Actually even Duryodhana didn't think, he can take on Bheemsena. This would be clear if you read Duryodhana's lamenting after he returned from Rajsuy yagna. Then again, in the first chapter of Gita where he explicitly mentions Bheema alone among Pandavas army. Then again, you should read Baka vadh to see on whose strength, Yudhishthira had pinned his hope.

But I think, you miss the point. There was no need to spare anyone’s life. Kunti NEVER asked for it.

You said –Arjun was the only one whose defeat will assure Kauravas victory.

Maybe. But then even that victory wouldn't have nullified Bheemsena’s oath of destroying Kauravas. Kayravas army may have won and Bheemsena still would have killed Duryodhana and his brothers. As he did. And Karna let them die!

You said — Additionally world would've thought that he left his friends side by not even fighting the war and joining other side.

Completely agree. The evil had more value for what the world would think about him than for humanity or loyalty.

If he had really cared about his friend, he wouldn't have –

  • Deserted him in Gandharva battle
  • Abstained from war for 10 days
  • Given an uncalled for promise to Kunti
  • Kept his oath a secret from his friend.

You said — If he wanted to fight Arjun he could do that even by joining hands by having a tournament with him rather than a war.

  • No. Then it would have been a friendly tournament where Arjuna would have let his brother win. No one would have trusted that a person who lost against Arjuna again and again, and kept running would won. Not, in a friendly tournament. And Karna was a person who sought to turn a tournament to a battle.
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 04:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Sorry for a delayed response. I didn’t see it earlier. But does that mean it is too late to accept your offer?

You said — Show me one Kunti who can give birth to divine kids by chanting some mantra, Gandiva or someone with the might of 1000 elephants and I will be your follower.

Is it compulsary to prove that Kunti gave birth to divine kids by chanting some mantra, to make you my follower? I never said that. And interestingly, even Vyasa never said that. But then you accepted, you haven’t read. Then, allow me to tell you Kunti got his sons through normal physical relationship which modern retelling tried to censor. The mantra, most likely was a contract which Surya or Dharma were duty bond to honour. Does this sound plausible? Because that is the orginal reference.

Now do you really think that a weapon with power of a thousand elephant was a mere figment of imagination? Do you know any weapon with the might of a 1000 elephant or more?

I by no means say or endorse that 100% of narratives are factual. Few are metaphors and few may even be down right fables. Thankfully those narratives are not related to either the question or the discussion we are having.

You said — I just wonder why there are no Surya putra's, Indra putra's, Vayu Putra's, etc. anywhere now a days.

Are you sure there are no more Suryaputra and Indraputra? Sanatana philosophy suggests that we all are integral part of supreme existence — parambrahma! But few can realize and harness that connection. That is known as enlightenment. And that is how after many years one Siddhart became Buddha. You can be a Suryaputra or a Vayuputra. Just look within.

You said — Grownup kds coming straight fire is a mystery to me.

Oh yes! that is a mystry. Isn’t it. But do you know something, till 1904 the thought that iron that is heavier than air could fly in air, too was a mystry. And to those enlightened mind, Pushpak Vimana too was a mystry. Test tube babies were mystry. The idea that Ashwatthama could have fired a weapon from a distance, with such a precision as to attack a child in the womb without destroying the womb was a figment of imagination till the time Drones and Micro drones were created. And many mystry would remain till modern science reaches the maturity of that era.

Wait, who knows the cloning of humans may unravel this mystry too. But then as I said, an alternative way to look into this incident is to see it as a metaphor.

You said — To prove someone great we don't have to demonize others. Everyone had shades of grey.

Agree. But then, we should also not de-demonize demons. Vyas referred Duryodhana as an evil tree. No. He wasn’t a tree actually. It was a metaphor. But he was an evil. Who else would try to kill people for his selfish interest?

And yes, I have heard a lot about shades of gray. Let me show you something 🤍🖤. What colors are they? They both are gray! But would you blame someone for thinking that one is black and one is white? Those who have more white than black are good people, those who have more black than white are good. Simple!

You said — Yudhistar playing dice by putting his brothers and wife as stake. If this is his dharma then sorry.

While talking about gray, you presented your views pretty black and white! And missed one very IMPORTANT point here. He staked even himself! And he did all those after losing his Kingdom. He had to do something to free his Kingdom. But then, the question was, why did he gambled in the first place? To prevent a bigger disaster! Being enslaved was preferable to a war that he was trying to prevent. Poisons are bad. But sometimes the only treatment.

If you think, my answers are clear enough to uphold you to your promise of being my follower, you can follow my podcast on Mahabharata —

महाभारत एक खोज - Vivek Dutta Mishra

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 12:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — I didn't read any line that shows while retreating karn knew duryodhan was captured.

You are in luck. You reached the right place. Here it is —

While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! I think it is extraordinary that I see you here, without harm and without injury, and with all your wives, riches and vehicles. You have freed yourself from that superhuman battle. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! O great king! There is no other man in this world who is like you, or can achieve what you have done in battle with your brothers.”

  • Karna fled knowing Duryodhana was fighting
  • Karna fled knowing no man in the worold could have fought Gandharvas.
  • Karna thinks Duryodhana “freed” himself.

Do you still think he didn’t know Duryodhana was captured? Maybe, you are right. Karna ran far off to return back in time to be of any help. In fact he didn’t bother to think if Duryodhana was captured. He must have realized that Duryodhana didn’t flee. But took no attempt to aid him.

You said — Also Arjun didn't protect his wife when she was disrobed.

Yes. Arjuna couldn’t protect his wife from the same fate he too was suffering at the hand of worst evil creatures. But he didn’t desert her. They both shared the same fate. He didn’t run away. Don’t you think that counts? But the same Arjuna protected the wives of same evil creatures. Wonderful, isn’t it?

You said — When karn asked Arjun to stop, he stopped seeing which shri krishna got anxious. Then he said that karn is extreme evil just to enrage Arjun to kill him.

No. Krishna urged Arjuna to kill Karna long before that. And several times. He just didn’t say Karna is extemely evil, he quoted all his crimes. And he expressed his pleasure after Ghatotkatch’s death that paved the way for Karna’s killing. And before that he promised to Draupadi that Karna would die and his wives would weep.

And then, more important than what others said, was what Karna did all his life. He was an evil man.

You said — In bori ce itself ved vyas had praised karna.

Yes everyman has few good qualities. But to call Ved Vyas praised Karna in BORI CE more times than he called him evil would be a mis-representation. In the very begining Ved Vyas called him as the trunk of an evil tree. Again in Udyoga Parva same thing. The Brahmana cursed him finding him evil. Parshurama cursed him for the same reason. Drona refused to give him weapon, again for the same reason. And we have a long long list…

By the way what praise are you talking about?

Now a question for you — Why are you tryng to support a treacherous coward for whom pains of his enemy was more worth than acts of dharma and having a son?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 12:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I didn’t mean you believe in Suryaputra. In fact I know it for sure that you don’t.

But then even Brahma or Agni may not have belived.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Brahma-recommend-Agnidev-to-ask-for-Karnas-help-to-get-back-his-strength-by-burning-Khandavprastha-I-know-you-wont-find-it-in-any-MB-books-or-in-any-other-puranas-But-I-want-to-know-your-views/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 12:36 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — You are literally a karn hater,

You guessed it right. I am a Karna hater. I am a Ravana hater. In fact I hate all evil beings.

You said — see how beautiful you whitewash drona who gave the idea of cutting down abhimanyu’s bow and ordered karn to do it.

Where did I white wash Drona? I couldn’t find it in this answer. Did you refer to any other answer? Please quote. Yes it was Drona’s idea. And Drona can’t be white washed for such a cowardly act. And that’s what I said — Guru got his son killed mercilessly. The blame is on Drona. Not Karna. At least here. He was just a insignificant minion.

You said — Abhimanyu was never killed by kaurav army but injured, he was killed by dushasana’s son.

Wow just wow! Really? But then you know Abhimanyu wasn’t even killed by Dushashana’s son! He died because his breath stopped.

Do you know the cause of almost every covid 19 deaths — cardiac arrest! But then covid 19 virus doesn’t effect heart. It affects lungs. That means There is literally zero death due to covid 19. WRONG! When body gets seriously ill the blood pressure goes low and the body sends all blood towards heart, overwhelming it and causing cardiac arrest. Most death’s, no matter what reason, the immediate cause would be cardiac arrest.

But then you would be wrong to think Covid 19 doesn’t kill anyone. Cardiac arrest, the immediate cause is as insignificant as Duhshashana’s son. The body was already pushed to the brink of death by the real disease and inability to sustain any longer leads to cardiac arrest.

Take example 2. To win a match you need 200 runs. Kohli gets out when the team was at 199. Then a new comer comes and scores the final run. I am sure you would call this man as the man of the match. Right. After all he scored the runs.

So doesn’t matter what you think. Please read the book, what Dhritrashtra said, what Sanjay said, What Gandhari said. What everyone in Mahabharata said. And see what really happened.

But then, I understand and to an extent agree with you — Kaurava never killed Abhimanyu.

They were cowards who couldn’t even fathom to kill Abhimanyu. Abhimanyu killed Karna’s brother. And what did that coward did? What he always did. Ran away! Seeing his brother die, he ran away. How can such cowards kill Abhimanyu. I think, you are right. They were too afraid to kill Abhimanyu. Abhimanyu died because of cardiac arrest.

And yes, just to reconfirm — I hate evils like Karna.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 12:55 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

And I must say the question is interesting. The reference to Suryaputra Karna was intended for those blind fans who see an evil man as the greatest hero.

And as a fellow community member who has known you and liked your answers, I know you don't belong to that category.

My answer targets the question’s belief and NOT you. I don't intend to offend you or anyone personally.

Content:

Yes, Karna did give his kavacha to Indra when the later came asking for it. But can we call it a donation? Karna flatly refused to give away unless he gets Vasvi Shakti in exchange. He knew Indra was coming, he was prepared to ask for it. And he insisted for it. I would call it an exchange rather than donation. Interestingly Karna used the same word

Answer Comment

Content:

1. Drona had all the right to refuse anyone from imparting his knowledge to anyone. It was his knowledge. He rejected him but didn't ask for his thumb at that point.

2. Eklavya, on the other hand, had no right to call him his Guru after being refused. He misused Drona’s name. Drona had to punish him. He demanded his thumb.

3. Krishna killed him vindicating Drona's descision. You expect to see events in isolation at your convenience. That doesn't work.

If it were Drona's selfishness either Ashwatthama, or Duryodhana or Yudhishthira would be his favourite, not Arjuna.

If it were his cruelty, He would have asked his head. But you can't lets evils go scot free. Parshurama too didn't let Karna leave after his trechary.

By the way, How did you decide, their attempts aren't working? Do you think, no one is willing to see the truth? I would recommend see the upvotes and other comments and realise, truth triumphs.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-are-Ashwatthama-and-Ekalavya-so-unlucky-Why-do-both-have-to-suffer-so-much/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 15, 2023 01:16 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Hi,

Two points -

  1. Kavach Kundal may protect fatal damages by slowing down the impact and not prevent 100% impact. And they don't cover the entire body. Karna was pierced by Arjuna even in Draupadi Swyamvara. And even in Gandharva battle he was badly injured.
  2. More importantly, a Kavach can prevent death, it can't overcome fear. It can make you strong, but can't make someone brave. Karna lacked courage and perseverance and that was the reason we see him fleeing battles after battle.

By the way Shri Krishna met an equally brutal end. May be worse. That doesn’t make him evil. Phulwama martyrs met the worse death. That doesn’t make them evil.

On the other hand many evils die peacefully.

Content:

Actually we had. Full chapters on Bose and INA.

And I agree, people don't read books, just WhatsApp University.

Tell me something, if you had no chapter on Subhash chsndra Bose, how do you know so much about him? WhatsApp?

Do you know –

  • Indian constitution original copy has Bose's picture, not Nehru's?
  • Jai Hind, the war cry of INA is our national salutation?
  • INA heroes carried our national flag to Red Fort for the first time, to fulfill the dreams of Netaji?

Let's learn to respect our great heroes. And yes, do read books.

Creation time: Sep 24, 2023 10:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Can you please tell, when did that happen? As per the epic, NEVER

You said –when karn first came to nullify the claim that arjun is the best bow & arrow warrior on the planet.

No one made that claim. There was a talent showcase organized for the princess of Hastinapur, a private event with public spectacle. Karna gate crashed it. He wanted a battle. Kripa averted it. Since there was no battle, there were no winners or losers.

But then there were winners and losers in real battles, Panchala battle, Swyamvara battle, Ghosa battle, Matasya battle, and finally Kurukshetra. Every one had same winner and same loser. Any guesses?

BR Chopra showed Karna winning 16th day. That's fake. Actually Arjuna defeated them all, badly.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Arjuna-undefeated/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Sep 24, 2023 11:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You said — even GOD were not able to give what asuras demand

When God grants boon, they are not donations. They are in exchange for virtues and merits. We don't call God's as donors. Dadhichi gave his bones without exchange. So did Sivi. No conditions attached. That's donation.

You asked — Do you believe pandavas able to defeat karna or kauravas without krishna, In actual sense Kauravas fought against the Krishna not Pandavas.

It's not a question of what I beleive. It's what exactly happened. Pandavas defeated Karna and Kauravas several times, directly and indirectly.

  1. Kauravas were defea by Drupad and Pandavas defeated them.
  2. Arjuna and Bhima thrashed Karna and Duryodhana and shalya during Swyamvara.
  3. Bhima defeated Karna during Rajsuy battle.
  4. Gandharvas thrashed Karna and Kauravas army. It was Pandavas who defeated Gandharvas.
  5. Arjuna defeated millions of Nivaykavach demons.
  6. Arjuna alone defeated entire Kaurava Army during Matasya battle.
  7. Abhimanyu alone defeated all Pandavas general several times.

In all these occasions Krishna wasn't around. I didn't talk about Khandava and Kurukshetra. Arjuna had the greatest weapons and dare. Karna was defeated by several people and so many times that even Drona said “I have seen him running away from battle after battles”

Krishna didn't fight the battle. He guided Arjuna. If he thought they were unable to do the job, he would have fought.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Is-Karna-a-hero-or-a-villain/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 24, 2023 08:38 PM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Answer lies in your question. He didn't kill Mahabali.

Second we are talking about Krishna who was born to “Paritranaya Sadhumam vinaschays Dushkritam” Protect good, destroy evils.

Hope that clears the doubt

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Asked and answered. Devoted followers don't deceive their Guru.

What were the circumstances Karna lied to Parshurama?

Karna was studying under an able guru Drona. There he wasted his time in several conspiracies and started lagging in class. He saw Arjuna best. He asked Drona for Brahmastra so that he can fight Arjuna. Why fight? Read last statement. Drona seeing him wicked refused.

Then he went to Parshurama. And lied to him. He lied for the greed to earn power. It wasn't necessary. It wasn't only way. He could have worshipped God. But that was the hard way. Why was Brahmastra so important that he cheated his guru?

Was there any circumstance, I missed?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Who-is-better-in-Karna-and-Arjuna-if-Kawach-Kundal-and-Brahmastra-are-kept-aside/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 24, 2023 08:53 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Good redemption? Where? He is one person who didn't spare even his best friend from deceit. On the last day of his existence, he used a deadly snake as a weapon. There was no redemption anywhere.

Creation time: Oct 24, 2023 09:01 PM PDT

Answer Comment

Content:

There are many such temples with their own lores. Some stories are attached to multiple places, some stories are confused. Take jyotirakinga. People assume Vaidyanathan to be in Parli, while others at Jharkhand.

Think, why would Barbarik called Khatu shyamji? Why isn't his temple present across country? Because his lore was attached in regional Mahabharata retelling, not in core part.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-did-barbarik-become-undefeatable-in-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 24, 2023 09:06 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Satyavati didn't reveal the same as long as Shantanu was alive. She had to reveal in emergency. Ved Vyas was a rishi and was looked differently. And yet, he was never considered a successor to the throne.

Karna was never acknowledged by Pandu. There was no emergency here. Karna was much safer and comfortable with Duryodhana for most part of his life. While other Pandavas faced hardship, Karna enjoyed a life of luxury tormenting Pandavas, plundering kingdoms. Kunti had no reason to reveal his identity.

But then, in the end, Karna had a real risk — to pay for his life long sin. Kunti wasnted to protect him. Alas!

Answer: https://www.quora.com/In-Mahabharata-instead-of-abandoning-Karna-couldnt-Kunti-just-have-said-that-she-found-him-in-the-forest-or-floating-in-the-river-and-couldnt-abandon-him-They-could-have-had-him-grow-in-their-place-to-become-a/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 01, 2023 03:15 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The only thing “idiotic” here is you language.

Now the question is who is this “everyone like you” and where do they know from what the do?

FYI, Gandhiji continued to oppose the partition to the very end. Two nation theory wasn’t Gandhiji’s plan. Neither was Direct Action day his call.

Do you know who was the author of “Two Nation Theory”? Do you know who formed alliance governement with people who called for the direct action day?

Do you know what was the voting for Pakistan? Do you know what was the agreement? Was there a mention of transfer of power? Was India made exclusively for Hindus? Do you know anything about it?

And in your response do you know you missed two very IMPORTANT names — Jinnah and Sardar Patel.

Jinnah (not Gandhi) was the father of Paksitan. And do you know who accepted the proposal on behalf of congress and convienced the rest of the members? It was NOT Gandhi. Not Nehru. It was Sardar Ballav Bhai Patel (Thats the history). And The iron man of India, too wasn’t against Muslim staying in India. In fact he was the one who had banned RSS in India. Hope you are aware of it.

And please tell those “everyone” that thing that happen under you nose may not have your sanctions.

There were two fanatics who wanted nation on the lines of religion. Interstingly they didnt’ fight for the country. They fought for their vision of nation. And They didn’t fight against each other as both were interested in breaking the country apart. They supported Britishers hoping they would support back. They opposed INA. One caused direct action day, the other was part of the alliance government. Does “everyone” know who these people were?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Gandhi-Ji-allow-Muslims-to-live-in-India-despite-creating-a-new-country-for-them/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 14, 2023 06:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I will ignore your uncivil tone for one time and try to educate you. There is no fan war here. There is a war between those who take saddish pleasure in supporting a heinous devil and those who defend the true spirit of the ageless epic.

People in this conflict forget a few simple things. Let us begin with few important points, if you understand the difference.

  1. There is a difference be warriors and warmongers. Warriors are greater than warmongers. Everyday.
  2. There is a difference between battle and war. Battle is losing a round, war is losing the match.
  3. There is a difference between being brave and being powerful. The two word doesn’t have the same meaning. A brave even in defeat is more honourable than a strong coward in victory.
  4. The most important thing is being on the side of the Dharma. There is no victory for evils, no defeat for follower of Dharma.

Now let me answer your trivial points.

  1. Got captured by samsapkthas(foot soldiers) and Krishna saved him.
  • Let me correct your understanding. Samsaptaka weren’t just foot soldiers. They were an elite team created with one sole motive. Kill Arjuna or stop him. Do you know any other warrior against whom such a dedicated task force was created?
  • No just got overwhelmed with the multitude of soldiers. If he was captured war would have ended. Krishna Aruna was a team in the battle like a double match. They both would win or lose.
  • Who won the war? The entire Samsaptaka were annihilated.

2. Susharma(ordinary king) attacked Arjuna and made him unconscious.

  • That is what weapons do. An arrow can make someone unconscious, can maim someone and kill someone. That is not the parameter of a warrior.
  • Just for your knowledge, even Shri Rama got unconscious in the war.

3. Bishma was about to kill Arjuna but Krishna saved Arjuna by trying to attack Bishma.

  • Read again, why Krishna attacked Bhishma. If you fail to understand please provide a reference and I will explain you.
  • Did you ever wonder, why Krishna saved Arjuna? Perhaps you missed the most important lesson of Mahabharata.

4. Arjuna was fighting with full intention with Bishma, he was not fighting mildly at all that's the reason Krishna asked Bishma way to defeat him, and then he asked to hide and attack behind Shikandini.

Read again, Don’t go by TV Shows. I am sure you will understand the truth.

5. Krishna himself said after Ghatochkada Vadha there were many warriors stronger than Arjuna Lolll.

Read Again. Provide one reference. Maybe you will understand better. Don’t just rely on fan posts. He did talk about evils like Hidimba, Eklavaya and Karna. But didn’t call them warriors greater than Arjuna.

6. Defeat against Ashwatama, he made him subconscious.

Read the answer to point 2, related to Susharma. Focus on what happened in the war. Don’t just read selectively. Do you know their war at Matasya? Do you know how he fled with Karna on day 14? Do you know how he failed to save Jaidradha?

7. Defeat against Karna on 16th and 17th day to Bhargava astra.

Same suggestion. Read the Bhargavastra story. Arjuna didn’t run away a rats. He left the place to inform Yudhishthira that he would kill Karna today. And He did kill Karna that day. Bhargava Astra didn’t kill him. Try to understand the nature of Divyastra. You can’t evade it by running away. It is not a gun’s bullet that can be dodged.

Ok let me humour you. Arjuna ran away. What happened next? Was Bhargavastra incapable of following Arjuna? Did it win the war for Kauravas? Which great warrior did it kill? Name one.

8. Arjuna’s sweet words: I can't counter Bhargava astra, karna was looking at me like Rudra I can't escape from him.

Just don’t stick to words. Follow the essence. Was Bhargava astra more powerful than Brahmastra, Bhramasira, Pashupata, Naryanastra or Vaishnavastra? Any reference?

So did Arjuna said what he said? Because to counter this weapon he would have to use a higher weapon, for which Arjuna wasn’t ready. Thus he was out of options. Shiva says there is nothing more powerful than Pashupata. Krishna says so. Even Karna acknowledges. Why didn’t Krishna defuse this weapon like he did to Ashwatthama’s Narayanastra or Bhagdatt’s Vaishnavastra? Because he wasn’t worried. He says this weapon would tire Karna. And thats exactly what happened. Without trying to counter this weapon, the weapon diffused without causing much damage.

9. Arjuna defeat to karna Naga astra, krishna save him,

Nagastra wasn’t a divyastra. It was a testimony of treachery. Karna had worshipped a snake all his life. The evil man had put that snake on an arrow and fired at Arjuna. Krishna dozed it by pushing the chariot a little down. If you knew about Divyastra you would know Divyastra won’t miss if Target moves away. It can follow the target. But that evil son of Takshak missed his mark and died. Karna had earlier also fired arrows with Takshak’s other sons sitting on them. They too were diffused.

Let us summarise. An evil man fires a snake at an unsuspecting warrior. The arrow misses (because of Krishna’s intelligence). And the snake is destroyed. Now the funny part is to say that the snake and evil man won when both the treachery and the snake were destroyed.

You have a very very skewed view of victory and defeat. For you, Karna+Snake is a great team but Krishna, the charioteer, shouldn’t help his friend and the warrior! Great!

Ask yourself a simple question, Why did Krishna support Arjuna and not evil like Karna? Because had he supported a coward, a molester or a conspirator, could he have given the message — Paritranaya Saduhnama, Vinaschays cha Dushkritam?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-some-Arjuna-fans-if-Arjuna-could-defeat-Lord-Parasurama-then-why-did-Arjuna-fail-to-protect-Lord-Krishnas-wives-and-run-away-from-ordinary-Dwaraka-robbers-Why-dont-they-understand-Arjunas-power-is-Lord/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 28, 2023 06:50 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

How does it matter, if Arjuna took another bow or not? Maybe he was out of bows.

The important thing is, that he didn’t run away like some of the cowards of his era who fled leaving their best friends. He fought even for those who were not his responsibility.

Could you even not respect that noble gesture?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-some-Arjuna-fans-if-Arjuna-could-defeat-Lord-Parasurama-then-why-did-Arjuna-fail-to-protect-Lord-Krishnas-wives-and-run-away-from-ordinary-Dwaraka-robbers-Why-dont-they-understand-Arjunas-power-is-Lord/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 28, 2023 06:53 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I can see your saddish language and see how you are enjoying imagining Draupadi being stripped. No wonder you support Karna.

You said — Arjuna was about to kill Yudistir (hitting a goal in his own goal). But he had no problem when Draupadi is stripped and was enjoying.

Proves your lack of understanding of the epic. Arjuna didn’t kill Yudhishthira. For him, his vows were not greater than rational thinking. Had you read Mahabharata you would have understood Arjuna wasn’t enjoying it. But then, I see you read cheap literature. So you won’t understand.

You said — Arjuna had entire Narayana sena so what he want to achieve by fighting his own lollll. He is even mentally weak to order Narayani sena???. So arjuna is ahamkari, it means like only I should get credit if I fail everyone should fail

Again proves you haven’t read the epic. Naryanai sena was destroyed in the civil war that had preceeded. Nothing was left other than women, children old men and a few sentries. In fact, that was the reason the robbers attacked. But you need to read to understand —

Some bandits saw that the husbands of these women had been killed and that they were being led by Partha alone. They became greedy. Greed robbed those evildoers of their senses. Those abhiras47 saw the opportunity to do something wicked and consulted each other. “Arjuna is the only warrior. There are the aged and the young. The husbands have been killed. The other warriors are without energy and are passing through our territory.” Thousands of bandits descended and began to strike them with sticks.

If you can ever remove the tinted glass of ‘I am a fan’ you will see the truth in its true colours.

Next time you respond without courtesy or reference, I will delete your comment and won’t respond back.

Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me start from the end.

You asked — may I know what made u like him in past? And what made u hate him now?? What had changed?

Short answer. Realization of the truth. Like everyone I too started with abridged versions, trusted Dinkar’s Rashmirathi and believed BR Chopra to have done his due delligence. That was 30 years ago. But then I read the unabridged versions and realized how those literatures distorted the truth.

You said — u and arjuna fans are abusing karna, and entire quora platform are arjuna followers and spreading fake info.

I NEVER abused Karna. I don't use uncivic words. I didn't propagate one fake info about Karns. In all my references I quoted from authentic Vyas writing. And in authentic Vyas writing he deserves to be hated by every civilized person, including you.

In that authentic epic, Krishna calls him extremely evil soul and trunk of evil tree Duryodhana. He said, his limbs burns on the thought of his deeds.

So, yes. I hate him the way I hate all evils Ravana, Hiranyakashyap.

You said — I don't see her as same as mother sita that doesnt mean I lust for her,

You don't have to see her as mother. But that doesn't mean you should mock a woman's molestation with smilies and lolllls. That doesn't mean, you should become a fan of a lusty molester.

You said — because u are old and wise instead of spreading the message of mahabharat u are provoking fan wars, we youth are fools and we can't control ourselves it is your duty to tell us good things,

That is what I am doing, dear. My mission is not about Arjuna or Karna. It's about honouring ancient Indian civilization, its about honouring authentic epic and destroying factual inaccuracies and as you suggest, tell you good things.

And if you want to learn good things based on my over 30 years of study follow my podcast https://vivek.vnc.in/m

You said — I would also like to share my views.

Do share your thoughts, but back them with authentic references not fan fiction.

Also share why do you hate Arjuna?

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-some-Arjuna-fans-if-Arjuna-could-defeat-Lord-Parasurama-then-why-did-Arjuna-fail-to-protect-Lord-Krishnas-wives-and-run-away-from-ordinary-Dwaraka-robbers-Why-dont-they-understand-Arjunas-power-is-Lord/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 29, 2023 04:03 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I do not understand the reason for this long message which lacks both understanding and references.

WHAT IS VICTORY AND DEFEAT

Let me first explain to you the meaning of victory and defeat. It is NOT the same as killing. Vyasa NEVER said that. If that were the definition, a coward would be greatest victorious because he would run away and NO one would be able to kill him.

When a person achieves the goal he strived for that is victory, even if the person dies. On the other hand, if a person fails to achieve the goal or runs away instead of trying to achieve the goal that is defeat, worse than death.

If a person got injured, got on the back foot had to retreat, but doesn’t give up his mission, that is NOT defeat. The consequences matters.

Example 1 Draupadi’s Swyamvar

Karna fought to kill Drupada and to throw Draupadi in the fire. Arjuna fought to protect them. The battle was decisive. One won. One lost, and ran away scared. Karna was NOT undefeated.

Example 2 Ghosa Yatara

Karna fought to defeat Gandharvas. He ran away. Scared. It was NOT a retreat. It was giving up the mission. He was defeated.

Arjuna fought to secure the release of Kauravas. He was victorious even if he let Chitrasena live.

Example 3 Goharana

Kauravas wanted to rob cattles of Matasya. Arjuna’s mission was to protect. Guess who won?

Arjuna killed Karna’s brother in front of him, and the coward ran away. If this is not defeat, I don’t know what is.

And I am not alone, Drona clearly says he has seen Karna running away from every battle. He was not wrong either.

Example 4 Abhimanyu Battle

Kauravas wanted to capture Yudhishthira. Abhimanyu’s mission was to foil the plan. Abhimanyu was killed. But he was still victorious.

NOW ANSWERING YOUR POINT

You said — According veda vyasa mahabharat if arjuna is undefeated warrior, even karna too was undefeated according to him defeat means getting killed or accepting defeat, Sanjaya mentioned on 14th night karna was never vanquished.

WRONG!. Not according to Ved Vyasa Mahabharata, it is according to Sanjaya’s statement. Ved Vyas recorded, what Sanjay said. He didn’t endorse what Sanjay said. During the Matasya battle according to Ved Vyas Mahabharata Karna said, today I will bring down the ape flag and kill Arjuna. Does that mean, he really killed him? You can’t use this statement as a proof. See what really happened. And if you insist on statements here is what Dhritrashtra said to Sanjay —

Because of the wicked policies of my son, I think that the Kouravas have already been destroyed. O Sanjaya! Karna will not be able to defeat those great archers, the Parthas. Whenever Karna has fought with the sons of Pandu, the Pandavas have always defeated him in the field of battle. O son! The Pandavas are incapable of being vanquished, even by the gods, with Vasava. My wicked son, Duryodhana, does not comprehend this.

And again about Karna Bheema battle —

O Sanjaya! The hopes of my sons being victorious have always been vested in him. On seeing him retreat in the battle, what did Duryodhana say?

There is no victory for an evil man even if the kill the hero.

You said — Sir don't compare rama with arjuna, lord rama never lost to ordinary warriors meghanadha is a legend unlike Susharma,

You miss the point. Here I didn’t compare Rama and Arjuna. I reminded being injured is not a shame. It is not a parameter of defeat. Rama couldn’t save his wife from abduction because of a deceit, Arjuna couldn’t save his wife because of another deceit. Neither Rama nor Arjuna can be blamed for someone’s deceit.

I am NOT sure why are you so facinated by villains like Karna and Meghanada.

You said — Now talking about karna Nagastra, u are equating snake+karna tag team to krishna+arjuna tag team? Snake that karna used is just ordinary snake even a foot soldier can kill it, while lord krishna is Supreme god, and he used god level strength to save arjuna(match fixing),

No. I NEVER equate good and evil. First stop calling it Nagastra. Mahabharata NEVER used this word. It was a snake and an arrow. It wasn’t a divyastra. And the job of a charioteer is to navigate his chariot to protect his charioteer from danger. Here Krishna is afraid of that snake. He doesn’t take it on his own. He simply dodges it.

It was the evil of the lowest grade. This is not match-fixing. This is calling out on someone who tried to do match-fixing using a snake.

A no matter what Karna said, it was NOT the first time he had used a snake in the battle. He had done it earlier.

You said — Difference between begging and praying, begging means without doing hardwork and using influence of lord krishna and idiotic indra, praying means doing meditation for years like jayadradha.

I couldn’t understand what you mean here. Who was begging? Who used Krishna’s influence? Where?

And did you call Indra “idiotic”? Can you provide a reference from Ved Vyas that said Indra idiotic? Can you elaborate your point?

And I asked this question last time also, let me ask you once again — Why do you hate Arjuna?

(Please don’t tell you don’t, your answers, your lolls they clear says you do)

Answer: https://www.quora.com/According-to-some-Arjuna-fans-if-Arjuna-could-defeat-Lord-Parasurama-then-why-did-Arjuna-fail-to-protect-Lord-Krishnas-wives-and-run-away-from-ordinary-Dwaraka-robbers-Why-dont-they-understand-Arjunas-power-is-Lord/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 30, 2023 05:48 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I don’t know why do you use so faul language. Your language doesn’t define Arjuna, it defines you.

You said — I will start a new page on facebook and I will provoke everyone to block every arjuna quora accounts who had higher upvotes and I will bring old karna fans back.

That is an excellent idea. That is the Karna way. Try burn someone because you can’t fight them with your illogic. Do try.

You said — If we dig deep at draupadi swayamvar karna was mild because of seeing brahmin he already was cursed by 2 brahmins and arjuna had peed his pants so he lied.

If you to dig deep in the MUD. Because NO authentic reference says that Karna was mild. In fact it clearly says he fought violently to kill Arjuna. And then ran away scared. Anything else is without reference and illogic.

You said — Gandarva war was sent by moron indra, gandarva commander used illusion on karna but fought mildly against.arjuna and arjuna was hyped.

Once again, NO reference. Just illogic. The didn’t fight mildly but feebly. But you don’t understand the difference of these two words. Do you? Feeble means week. Arjuna fought mildly.

You said — Vyasa never mentioned these as defeats in kmg he himself stated on 14th day karna was never defeated. U are using your own interprolations 1st u give me cross reference where vyasa told karna was defeated. If vyasa never told we can take statements from Sanjaya and yuditir(pro pandavas).

You just proved the level of your knowledge. NO comments. By the way, Vyas didn’t write KMG. Kisari Mohan Ganguli did. And these are mentioned even in KMG

You said about Ulipi, Draupadi, Subhadra, Chitrangada

Your vulgar words doesn’t deserve a response.

You said — What I understood is u already drowned in arjuna river because of quora all quora is flowing with arjuna virus.

That is because you haven’t read Mahabharata, otherwise you would have recognized, I am floating in Mahabharata NOT in quora.

Eariler you shared a polite message that showed you wanted to learn something. Alas…

You said — finally u are even supporting Devendra everyone know how highly wicked that creature is

Actually I am not aware what he did to Karna. By the way why did Vishnu and Shiva support him? And why do you think, I would trust your more than Vishnu and Shiva? I follow Shiva and not some Asura or evil worshipper.

But then, your language is so vulgar and out of place that it does deserve a civic response and I can’t degrade myself to your level. All I can say is you are a worty Karna fan.

Content:

It is more critical in Kalayug to understand what is fair and unfair. And if you don’t trust your tainted mind, trust Krishna. He called Karna an extremely evil soul and rejoiced at the prospect of his death. And he supported Arjuna. There is no taint here.

Creation time: Mar 17, 2024 12:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Perhaps you didn’t read point 5 in my answer. I clearly mentioned this issue. But you can’t selectively read one sentence without seeing the holistic figure. Arjuna, as a kid, felt jealous of Ekalavya. Like any ordinary kid, he rejoiced that his competitor was gone. Was it good? Absolutely No. We should never rejoice in someone's tragedy.

There are few questions here.

Was Arjuna flawed as a Kid … Yes

But did Arjuna cut-off Ekalavyas finger … No.

Did Arjuna ask Drona to cut off Ekalavya’s finger … No

Did Drona ask Ekalavya’s finger because of Arjuna … Not mentioned.

Did Drona ask Karna for his finger … No

Did Arjuna show any envy for anyone in future… No

Did Arjuna show compassion to enemies… Yes

Was Krishna born to destroy evils … Yes

Was Krishna born to protect good … Yes

Did Krishna protect Arjuna … Yes

Did Krishna compare Ekalavya with Hidimb and Kimir … Yes

Did Krishna himself slay Ekalavya… Yes

Can a flawed person like Arjuna become better… You answer

Can a guru see the potential evil in a child … You answer

Has Drona right on his knowledge and wisdom …. You answer

Was it within Drona’s right to reject someone … You answer

Was it right for Ekalavya to pretend to be Drona’s desciple … You answer

Answer: https://www.quora.com/At-the-time-of-Eklavya-why-did-the-Arjun-lover-not-refer-to-the-Bori-version-of-the-Mahabharata/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Mar 17, 2024 12:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Yes, It was perfectly justified for so many reason.

Firstly, only the chariot wheel was stuck, not his weapons. His weapons weren’t broken. Prior to this day Karna himself had continuously attacked many chariotless warriors like Abhimanyu and Bhimasena. Further, many warriors fought the battle from ground bravely without asking their opponent to stop. This includes Abhimanyu on Day 13, Arjuna on Day 14.

Also, on Day 14, Bhimasena broke Drona’s chariot several times. He changed his chariot. Was Karna stupid not to change the chariot? Or was he making it a ploy to void this fatal dual?

Secondly, as per the authentic epic, Karna died fighting. He shot back Brahmastra and Varunastra. What was unjustified on a battle ground was to ask for a recess. That hadn’t happened even once in the entire battle! He could have surrendered and saved his life.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Has-Arjuna-ever-defeated-Karna-in-a-fair-fight/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 02, 2024 01:02 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

You have been overusing the word “Sychophancy” too, not in the most relevant way. Often, someone becomes great in history because they deserve that greatness. And simply calling someone who understands that greatness, as Sychophants, doesn’t serve any purpose in a debate. Belittling someone based on your agenda also doesn’t serve any purpose.

I would recommend avoiding those personal attacks and discussing the facts. If Sychophancy doesn’t work for Gandhi, how do you expect it to work for a lowly murder like Godsey?

Anyways, here is an answer for the day of 1919.

https://youtu.be/HRq8bHKqhUQ

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-didnt-Nathuram-Godse-assassinate-Jinnah-who-was-the-actual-person-responsible-for-creation-of-Pak-and-instead-killed-Gandhi/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 02, 2024 01:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I feel bad for Savarkar. People generally considered him a freedom fighter and respected him till his supporters started slinging muds at India's freedom fighters and nation builders. People started researching and some ugly truth came out.

Savarkar’s work before he was imprisoned in 1910 was commendable, at par with any revolutionary like Ram Prasad Bismil, Uddham Singh. His book on 1857 revolution and his daring to celebrate golden jubilee of India's first struggle for Independence in London is praise worthy.

But after his imprisonment, he broke down. Applied for clemency several times. Promised British government that he will never rise against their interest. And the worst part, He NEVER DID. In fact, he always sided with him. Therefore, equally is a question mark as is confinement.

Have you read Savarkar Samagra? There he mentions that his life became easy after 3 years as he was made supervisor. He didn't participate in any strike within jail. And in those 27 years you are also counting his confinement in Satara.

The fact is, you are exaggerating his sufferings more than he did. Question is, why did RSS and HMS sidelined him for the last phase of his life after 1948, where he lived a life of anonymity? Now they are ‘using’ him.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Sardar-Patel-won-the-majority-then-who-made-Nehru-as-PM/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 05, 2024 10:54 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I agree entirely; there is nothing wrong with a clemency request or asking to get out. He did fight hard for his “personal” freedom. But then, you can’t be just honoured for your struggle for “personal freedom”. Bhagat Singh didn’t ask out. Neither did Chandrashekhar Azad who chose to die by his bullet. Not did Ram Prasad Bismil and thousands of martyrs. He

The question is, what did he do with that “personal freedom”? Did he fight for India’s freedom? NO. Did he continue to support the British government? YES.

And NO. The British government didn’t consider him “most dangerous” It was the view of “one of the British officers” not the government, for which there is no record. And Savarkar, dangerous to the British government, entered cellular jail. The jail consumed that Savarkar. The one who came out of the prison was a mellowed-down British loyalist who didn’t do anything against British interests. There is no record any of his efforts for India’s freedom after his release from prison, not even in his works. And you didn’t answer why RSS an HMS sidelined him during the last 18 years of his life? Why did he became politically untouchable?

I can point out that a British government representative labelled Nehru as the most dangerous person. But I am not sure why you started this debate on a thread that was NOT related to Savarkar?

What was the need to highjack a thread with a completely off-topic-debate?

Final words — I respect Savarkar for his role in India’s freedom movement before 1910. I will continue to respect him for that. I am neutral for his efforts to gain personal freedom by whatever means necessary. I don’t condemn him for that. I don’t honour him for that either. But then, his role after coming our of British imprisonment is nothing to be proud of. He supported British government, didn’t do anything for India’s freedom movement and played divisive politics. And “his most dangerous” status was gone. In fact he became so insignificant that British government didn’t even invite him during the talks for the transfer of power. His friend Jinnah was indeed invited. And I have NO respect for that Savarkar.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Sardar-Patel-won-the-majority-then-who-made-Nehru-as-PM/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 05, 2024 11:36 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

I agree with the futility of the argument. One of the lamest arguments is “Gandhi or Nehru has never been to cellular jail.” Do you know who was put in cellular jail? Was there any murder or weapon supply charges on either Gandhi or Nehru? You can’t put everyone in cellular jail.

A life in prison is tormenting and painful but not more than death. And while you mention Bhagat Singh’s father signing pardon, I must ask who was he to sign the petition? In fact, Bhagat Singh himself asked who was he to sign? If you can’t see the difference in a man who condemned his father for signing a petition on his behalf. Signing multiple petitions, begging for a release and vouching his allegiance to the British government calling himself the British government’s prodigal son, accepting that he was wrong in standing against the British government and swearing never to rise against the British government. AND MOST IMPORTANT, HE NEVER NEVER DID ANYTHING AGAINST BRITISH GOVERNMENT AFTER HIS RELEASE. NOTHING.

If you are so focused on cellular jail, let me tell you a few more inmates. Bhagat Singh’s ally, Batukeshwar Dutta was in cellular jail. He didn’t beg. He jumped into the Quit India movement immediately after his release.

Of that 30% of his imprisonment, only 10 years was in cellular jail. Out of that 10 years 3 years was rigorous imprisonment (As per his writing).

You ridiculed my point of “one of the official” vs “government”. You asked Does it have a head, an eye a mouth of its own? Interestingly, yes. It does have a head, an eye and a mouth. You can consider British PM, parliament and viceroy of India as its head, and eye and their statements as the month. sounds coming from else were is know as gas.

Apply a little common sense and think, why would the government consider a man “most dangerous” whose sole role was to supply guns to someone else to do the shooting? There had been so many British officials murdered earlier. After prison, Savarkar did absolutely nothing for the country's freedom movement. He went along with the British government’s divide-and-rule plan. Interestingly formed a government with a Muslim league. Do you know the two parties who supported the Britishers in WW II? Do you know who called for Indian youths to join the British army against INA? Tough questions.

Britishers didn’t deny him clemency because he was convicted of murder. But eventually, when they realized that he was working for them, they first made his life easy in cellular jail, then eventually released him. He was released from cellular jail in 1921 and finally released from Ratnagiri Jail 1924.

Who is most dangerous? One who begs to you and never rises against you? Or one who galvanized the mass who can rise against you? By that margin, Nehru was so impactful that no only British officials but even the axis forces sought his support and three American presidents stood at the airport to welcome him.

Contrast against the other dangerous man who was so destructive that even his party considered him untouchable. The question is who sidelined him after Mahatma’s assassination? And why did they do that?

His party-men were unhappy with his decision to refrain from joining the Indian freedom movement.

Interesting point to conclude — Even in your long essay, you failed to mention two things.

  1. What was Savarkar’s contribution to India’s freedom movement after 1909? The answer is NOTHING.
  2. Why did you try to hijack this thread, which was not related to Savarkar?
Answer: https://www.quora.com/When-Sardar-Patel-won-the-majority-then-who-made-Nehru-as-PM/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 13, 2024 12:06 AM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Oh! I drank the same drink that the greatest minds in the world including but not limited to Albert Einstien, Martin Luthar King, Dalali Lama, Barak Obama, Steve Jobs, Ho Chi Minh, Subhash Chandra Bose, Rabindra Nath Thakoor, Dr Rajendra Prasad

And you may be surprised to see that most names in this list are those who have no association with congress.

You don’t need to be drunk to read and understand history. You need to be WhatsApp graduate not to understand it.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Gandhi-responsible-for-Indias-partition/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 19, 2024 10:41 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

The era of brainwashing started only in very recent times. Before that it was the era of enlightenment. There was a time when people used to

  • Read authentic sources
  • See with their own eyes
  • Reason with their own brain.

Now we have WhatsApp university. People don’t need to read books that tell the truth, or apply their brain. All the points that you have (or could) mention, I already know.

Can you quote any source to suggest:

  1. Gandhi’s fasts were only more Muslims and Not for Hindus?
  2. Exact reason as to how, he was responsible for the partition. Was partition Gandhi’s idea? Did he propse it? Did he immediately approve it?
  3. Yes, He couldn’t stop partition. He wasn’t God. But tell me why, didn’t anyone else stop it? Why that baffoon didn’t shot Jinnah instead of Gandhi before partition? Why HMS formed joined government with Muslim league, the party who was hell bent in dividing India?
  4. He didn’t try to rescue Muslims in Pakistan? Do you know his next plan was to go to Pakistan for precisely the same reason? And then one brainwashed stupid (The first graduate of WhatsApp University, in era when Whatsapp didn’t exit) shot him and stole the opportunity from Pakistan’s Hindu at a chance of digntify.

If you were a proud Hindu you would have condemned those radicals. Have you ever performed any pooja or Yagya or Hawan? Can you tell how that pooja ends in Hindu Santana system —

ॐ सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः । सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः । सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु । मा कश्चित् दुःख भाग्भवेत् ॥ ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥ ... हिन्दी भावार्थ: सभी सुखी होवें, सभी रोगमुक्त रहें, सभी का जीवन मंगलमय बनें और कोई भी दुःख का भागी न बने।

Are you a real Hindu? I am not a brainwashed Gandhi Bhakta. I am an enlightened one. I am a proud Hindu (and also a proud Gandhi Bhakt). Can you say the same about yourself? Do you recite teh above Mantra? Do you feel it? Do you try to adopt it? Your message doesn’t convey that.

And if you can’t establish yourself in Santana philosophy, stop lecturing about Hindu welfare

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Was-Gandhi-responsible-for-Indias-partition/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 19, 2024 10:54 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Just three questions —

  1. Is there any mention that Bhishma was considered a higher caste? I could see none. He is referred as Kshatriya. So is it just your logic?
  2. Does the authentic reference mention that Parshurama didn’t teach non-brahmins? NO. It doesn’t. You may provide a reference you have.
  3. And let us, for the sake of argument, accept your points without any reference (based on ill-propagated tv show reference), still what right did Karna have to cheat someone who clearly doesn’t want to teach him? THIS IS STILL STEALING. Consider today’s scenario. You are rejected from IIM/IIT because of your cast (NON reserved class). Does that justify you faking document to prove yourself as reservation class and take admission in the institute? What would happen if the university finds out that you have been scammed?
Answer: https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Parshuram-incarnation-of-Narayana-decline-to-teach-Karna-when-he-got-to-know-that-he-was-a-soot-putra/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 19, 2024 11:01 PM PDT
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

My friend, everytime you respond, you start with a personal attack on me as if that is a sufficient compensation for the lack of substance in the rest of the body. You didn’t answer any of my question and keeps blaming me.

NEXT TIME I SEE THIS KIND OF PERSONAL ATTACK AND BASELESS MESSAGE, I WOULD BE FORCED TO DELETE THEM.

You are on my thread and expect me to stop responding. Just say so. Because I don’t see any goal post changed.

  • It is you who ambushed a thread with an irrelevant question. Let me remind you this thread had nothing to do with Savarkar.
  • You tried to demean the sacrifices of Azad and Bhagat Singh, saying that they died but didn’t have to suffer in cellular jail as if cellular jail was the certificate of ultimate certicate of patriotism. IT IS NOT
  • You again insulted Bhagat Singh by saying that he too filed mercy plea. HE DIDN’T. HE OPPOSED IT.
  • Now, you are insulting Batukeshwar Dutt by suggesting that he spent less time in jail. Let me enlighten you. He spent 8 years in cellular jail (2 years less than Savarkar). But he protested in jail (63 days hunger strike) which Savarkar didn’t. Then, he too was transferred to imprison (proper jail not city confinement). After his release from prison he immediately joined Quit India movement and was sent to jail again. So please stop comparing.

——-

You said — “Everybody likes MK Gandhi for his non violence means but when it comes to Savarkar they want body count as compared to other revolutionaries”

WRONG. I don’t want a body count. I didn’t ask how many people he killed. Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwear Dutt dropped a bomb that didn’t kill anyone and yet it awake the masses.

Same thing happened with Gandhi, Tilak and Nehru.

—-

YOU DIDN’T TELL

  • What was the contribution of Savarkar after he secured his freedom?
  • How many anti-british article did he write?
  • How did he try to help Bhagat Singh? What was the condolence message he drafted for the martyrdom of Bhagat Singh or Chandrashekar Azad?
  • What was his contribution in INA trial?
  • Any noteworthy act of Savarkar that qualifies as struggle of Independence?
  • Why didn’t he participate in Quit India movement? Why then HM members were criticizing his stand?
  • Why did he support Muslim league?
  • Why did he support British government during WW 2?

And yes, it is futile for you to argue with me as you have no answer to any question I asked in any of my message. I know it is difficult to answer them as answers for them doesn’t exist in WhatsApp university here.

Content:

Why was Gandhiji sent to Aga Khan Palace? Did he murder someone? Did he inspire to murder someone? Did he rob someone? Cellular jail was for hardened criminals or those whom Britisher’s can prove as criminals.

Gandhiji was confined to Aga Khan Palace not for a crime but because Brithisher’s felt he would be dangerous for their political goals during war time and it would be disastrous for their law and order if Gandhi is actually sent to “Jail”. It was their explicit strategy (Not speculation) to put him some place that is not remotely connected to the word “Jail”

And that is a historical fact that whatsapp university can’t deny.
