Answer: https://www.quora.com/Has-Arjuna-ever-defeated-Karna-in-a-fair-fight/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 04, 2024 09:05 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Actually NO.

  1. Arjuna pierced Karna’s with three arrows.
  2. There is no such mention that Karna, too pierced Arjuna
  3. Karna started the battle, knowing Arjuna to be a Brahmana. He retreated, knowing the Brahamna was invincible. If he had a respect from Brahmana he wouldn’t have started the fight and wouldn’t have disrespected his gurus during Virata battle.
  4. Vyas Mahabahrata clearly mention, he ran “scared” And running scared is defeat

He didn’t lose the battle is a fantasy of Karna’s fan, with no proof.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Has-Arjuna-ever-defeated-Karna-in-a-fair-fight/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 04, 2024 09:09 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

There are three major issues with your understanding.

  1. They NEVER reached same level — neither as warrior nor as human. Arjuna was way superior in both respect.
  2. Karna had no reason to suffer inferiority. He was the son of a wealthy charioteer, had a great education and became King at an young age — all signs of being blessed.
  3. Unlike Karna, who got divine armour and kingship without any of his merit, Arjuna didn't get any freebies from Anyone. He had to prove his worth to everyone — Shiva, Indra, Drona. And the Royal lineage proved only curse — a constant threat to his life.

And most importantly, you missed is the fact that Hanuman, Krishna don't support evils. There support were not undue or unfair but a proof.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-not-have-many-advantages-over-Karna-Why-do-people-claim-that-he-was-better-when-they-never-fought-on-the-same-level/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 07, 2024 12:29 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Did Parshurama say that — Did he mean Karna would be greater than even Shri Rama and Krishna? But then, how come this greatest warrior was thrashed again and again by everyone — Arjuna during Swyamvara, Bhimasena during Rajsuy battle, Gandharva during Ghosha battle, Arjuna again during Matasya battle, Abhimanyu on day 13, Bhimasena on day 14?

What about what Shiva said about Arjuna — No one is your equal — And it was proved in Arjuna’s case.

Parshurama said no one will be your equal — he didn’t say in valour. Maybe cowardice?

Do you know in Udyoga Parva when Parshurama arrived at Hastinapur, he said, no one can defeat two Krishna’s. Karna was there. Maybe he changed his stand.

MORAL OF THE STORY: Don’t tell who said what about whom. Show what happened everytime there was a fight.

Karna’s achievement
1. Lie, Deceit and conspiracy in gurukul

2. Attempt to bribe Brahmana

3. Tried to burn Draupadi and her family during Swaymavar. Attacked Arjuna. Ran away scared. And no, he didn’t stop the battle because he thought Arjuna was Brahamana. He attacked knowing he was a Brahmana. He ran away knowing he was invincible.

4. Ran away from Gandharva leaving his friend behind to save his own life.

5. Ran away from Arjuna in Matasya battle, leaving his brother to die. Returned ran away again. Returned and got conscious.

6. Badly thrashed by Abhimanyu, Bhima and Arjuna again and again.

True. There will be no (coward) warrior like him.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-not-have-many-advantages-over-Karna-Why-do-people-claim-that-he-was-better-when-they-never-fought-on-the-same-level/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 07, 2024 01:52 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Wow! This is called blind fan — All his defeats are interpolations. Why? To make him look bad! What was the need to make him look bad? HE WAS ALREADY WORSE.

  1. Ved Vyas and Krishna called him the trunk of evil tree.
  2. He was part of every treachery of Duryodhana.
  3. He tried to burn Draupadi alive
  4. He proposed stripping of Drauapadi

He was an evil person. Period.

And for your information, none of his defeat is referred as interpolations by anyone other than fans like you. They are part of BORI CE and categorically mention it as their failure. On day 14 Bhimasena defeated him several times. He defeated Bhimasena only once before running away on seeing approaching Arjuna.

Don’t give me much information. Just tell me this — If Bharagavastra was such a powerful weapon and Arjuna had no answer, why didn’t he win the battle that day? Which great warrior did it kill? Can you name any one? Why did Krishna say that it would tire Karna?

Name one occasion in entire Kurukshetra war where a warrior pleaded for recesses because his chariot had become dysfunctional? Did Arjuna did it on Day 14? or Bhimasena? Or Drona? Or Satyaki? Or Abhimanyu? or Anyone else?

In case of Karna only his chariot was stuck, not his weapons. And he could still have fought. But he was a coward who was looking forward for an exit.

And I don’t want to go into fan wars. If you have a valid reference put it forward. Telling this is interpolation or that is interpolation is concerning defeat in a discussion where you have no answer.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-not-have-many-advantages-over-Karna-Why-do-people-claim-that-he-was-better-when-they-never-fought-on-the-same-level/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 07, 2024 04:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Let me acknowledge.

1. None of the battles where Karna was defeated is interpolation. They are part of BORI CE and have enough cross-reference to mark as interpolation.

2. Krishna did offer Arjuna Chakra to slay Karna. But have you read the chapter. Krishna was irritated as to why Arjuna is not killing him off quickly. He was irritated at lack of motivation. And you also missed the fact that Arjuna didn’t accept the offer and killed him with an ordinary arrow. Krishna didn’t call him the foremost warrior. Do you know what he said about him?

  • Karna is now like an angry snake, whose energy has been sapped through mantras. Therefore, Karna is now like a fire whose flames have been pacified.
  • In all the evil acts that Dhritarashtra’s son has done towards you, in all of these, the evil-souled and extremely wicked Karna has been present.
  • ‘O Kiriti! How is it that in every way, your weapons today are being countered by Karna’s weapons? O brave one! Why do you look like someone who is confused? The Kurus are cheerfully roaring. All of them are honouring Karna, knowing that all your weapons have been destroyed by his. In yuga after yuga, you have used fortitude to destroy the weapons of darkness and terrible rakshasas. You slew Dambhodbhava and other asuras in encounters. 778 Use that fortitude and slay the son of a suta. Or use this sudarshana chakra now and slice off his head. It is sharp at the edges and even the immortals cannot withstand it. It has been used by me earlier, like Shakra using the vajra to strike Namuchi. The illustrious one, in the form of a hunter, was pleased by your greatness and fortitude. 779 Resort to that fortitude again and slay the son of a suta, together with his relatives. (No mention of foremost warrior)
  • Partha. ‘The sun may fall down from its place. The earth may shatter into many fragments. The fire may become cold. But Karna will not be able to kill Dhananjaya.

3. I completely agree. Karna invoked Brahmastra and Varunasatra from ground. And that is a proof that only his wheel was stuck not weapons. My question is didn’t Drona, Bhima, Abhimanyu and Arjuna himself fought without chariot? Did anyone request for a recess? NO. Why Karna asked Arjuna to stop? Did he allow Bhima or Abhimanyu the same chance? As far as will power is concerned, it is clear from the fact that his Brahmastra was destroyed by an ordinary arrow.

4. You haven’t read the chapter. Or perhaps at all. No one called Arjuna a coward. It was Karna who fled on seeing approaching Arjuna. Why didn’t Can you tell, Karna fight approaching Arjuna?

BUT FEW MORE IMPORTANT QUESTION.

  1. Have you read this line — But having heard that Karna was repeatedly defeated in the battle by Bhimasena, terrible in his deeds, I have lost my senses.
  2. Do you know — Duryodhana lost 32 of his brothers trying to save the life Karna from Bhimasena? Did someone die to save Bhimasena from Duryodhana? Do you know how many times Bhimasena defeated Karna?
  3. MOST IMPORTANT — Is there a dispute that Karna was an evil man? Krishna indeed said so.
  4. Why do you hate Krishna? Didn’t Krishna called him wicked? Didn’t he want him dead? Wasn’t he repeatedly referred as evil?

The bottom line is Karna was a wicked, evil and coward who kept fleeing battle after battle. And if it wasn’t about divine weapons like Indra’s spear he was liked a extinguished flame and defanged snake.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/Did-Arjuna-not-have-many-advantages-over-Karna-Why-do-people-claim-that-he-was-better-when-they-never-fought-on-the-same-level/answer/Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Nov 09, 2024 11:03 PM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

Was Arjuna Jealous? Yes. Any perfectionist gets jealous at some feat other can perform better. But an archer is not same as warrior. And one feat can make someone jealous, but not warrior.

Did Arjuna manipulate Drona? NO. He asked a question. He didn't ask for any action against Ekalavya.

Was Drona easy to manipulate? No. It was Ekalavya who tried to manipulate. After getting rejected by Drona, he had no right to claim himself as his disciple. That was a deceit. It was between Drona and Ekalavya.

Answer: https://www.quora.com/How-would-you-rank-Arjuna-Karna-Ashwatthama-and-Eklavya-in-terms-of-archery/answer/Gokul-Renjith-5
Creation time: Nov 30, 2024 02:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Answer Comment

Content:

आपका प्रोत्साहित करने के लिए आभार। कोशिश करूँगा हिन्दी में और लिखने के लिए। अभी अभी एक और उत्तर डाला है। आपनी प्रतिक्रिया अवश्य दें। कौन बताएगा कि महाभारत का सबसे शक्तिशाली यौद्धा कौन था? के लिए विवेक दत्त मिश्रा (Vivek Dutta Mishra) का जवाब

Content:

Question Comments

Question Comment

Content:

Hi,

The article referred in this article is blatantly biased. It lays down certain unsubstantiated claims and then bases its conclusion on them. No proper conclusion or debate can work in a flawed premise. It's not a question where we are seeking answer - it's a view redrafted as question.

The article says that we celeberated body perfection of Ram - Wrong. We celebrate him as Maryada Pursottam - one with mental perfection. We also celebrate imperfection like Jagganath, Ganesh and Astavakra.

Comming to the question of securalism - we have a flawed secularism. Those whom we refer sickular think moving away from hindutvwa is secularism. And another group thinks moving away from secularism is being Hindu. Both are wrong - and interesting both have similar views - the non hindu view.

Hindutva is the very definition of secularism -

Ekam Sat Vipra Bahudha Vadanti

Unfortunately our constitution was brutalized in 42nd ammendment when we added secular word in the constitution. It was a constitutional scam, and when such a word is added people naturally search for a distinction. Hence few intellect tried to find distinction between hindu and secular. And guess who gave them answer - this scewd view of Hindutvwa!

Ram left Ayodhya for Dharma and we need to rule the land. I am not worried if we will win this war, my worry is in winning the war we may lose our Hindu way of life.

Problem is not with the leadership, problem is with lack of understanding where anyone can write anything and with the limited knowledge begin to rate our history, culture, and leaders.

And let me tell them - they are actually doing a great disservice to our great nation - Hindustan - the land of hindu civilization. And yes when someone says India is not a Hindurastra but a secular country - forgive them, for they don't know secularism is another word for Hindutvwa.

Post Comment

Content:

Hi,

I think in your long answer you missed the key question, which is expected from a learned person like you — Karna wasn’t killed unarmed. He died fighting like a warrior. Don’t disrespect Karna!

As for your glossy picture and explanations —

  • No doubt Krishna aided Arjuna confirming his victory, but
    • Did Arjuna ever really take the chakra and use it to slay Karna?
    • Krishna offered his chakra as a show of his trust in Arjuna’s dharma. This is very unlike Karna who accepted Indrastra from Indra, nay, he asked for it.
    • Since Karna was slayed by an arrow and not by a chakra he was capable for being killed using an arrow.
  • Krishna saved Karna from the trajectory of that arrow which had a snake sitting on it and Arjuna was unaware of this deceit.
    • That is what a charioteer was supposed to do.
    • Krishna was a great charioteer. Point taken.
    • That serpent wasn’t a celestial weapon but external help from someone not officially participating the war.
    • Asking why Krishna intervened is like, I was cheating why the referee blew the whistle.
    • And there is no doubt Krishna saved Arjuna on numerous occassion, but so Did Duryodhana and Ashwatthama for Karna. On day 17, Duryodhana lost several of his brothers to protect Karna from Bheem.
  • From your own quotes —
    • Thou art competent, O best of the Bharatas, to slay the mighty Karna. Even this hath ever been my thought, O mighty car-warrior, as to how, O best of men thou, wouldst slay Karna in battle.
      • Krishna always knew Arjuna was competent to kill Karna
      • There is not need to kill him unarmed.
      • Karna died as a warrior, fighting
    • I swear to thee that hero is still incapable of being slain by anybody else save thee.
      • Note Krishna is clear none other than you (save thee) can kill Karna
      • Its about killing and not defeating

Content:

Hi,

I always appreciate your writings. But since you asked if it is correct, here is my view —

I don’t believe this is correct. Neither it goes with the teachings of Gita. If we proceed with this explanation, that would mean before Kalyuga — people were either good or evil. But Santana philosophy doesn’t believe in binary system. Our good and evil are based on our Karma which is driven by our gunas — Sato, rajo and tamo.

Think Ravan good a lot of boons from Gods, that made him invincible. Now there must have been good in him. But when his other gunas dragged him, he behaved as an evil.

Was Yudhisthir all good or all bad?

Not only Gita, but even astrology suggest the effect on the universe on individual and their actions. And astronomy isn’t an invention of Kaliyug.

Even before Treta yuga, King Bali ruled on earth till the time Vamana Vishnua sent him to Patalaloka.

During the Matasya avatara, Lord instructs to save all lives on earth — good and evil.

King Daksha was punished for his evil nature — and he ruled earth in satayuga.

So, I believe this is a very sallow explanation of four yugas.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Is-this-correct-explanation-of-the-four-yugs
Creation time: Aug 03, 2020 10:09 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/Whats-wrong-with-Arjun-fans-in-Mahadevas-1
Creation time: Aug 08, 2020 09:44 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Hi,

I don’t see how your comment related to the current question or answer, neither could I understand which ultimate blessing of Krishna are you talking about that he bestowed on Karna?

Krishna indeed went to Karna and wanted him to join Pandava? But is there a real mystery here? Well if you want to understand the reason, take a few steps back. He also offered a peace deal to Duryodhana. Keep your entire kingdom, just offer 5 villiages. Does that make Duryodhana virtuous because Krishna approached him? Does it make him more virtuous than Karna, because Krishna approached him first?

Krishna approached these people in order to prevent the war. He also approached Karna to offer him a chance to redemption. If you read the section of Mahabharata, you would realize both Krishna and Karna agreed that Pandavas were winning. So it wasn’t for bettering Pandava’s chance to victory. It was to prevent the war.

What you refer as ‘Actually’ is devoid of facts and reasoning —

  • You say Karna suffered — Karna remained a king of Anga for almost entire his life. He was never exiled, his family never humiliated. He actively abused and humiliated others. And yet you call him, he suffered.
  • You say Arjuna was fortunate — He was born in exile. Lost his father. People consipired to kill him and his family alive. His wife was humiliated. He was exiled for 25 years in his life. And yet he was lucky.

Now you make a case study based on caste. How was son of Bhima deprived? How was he of lower strata? Would you have preferred Ghatotkatch left his own people and suffered exile with his father? Even son of Chitrangada and Uulipi never stayed with Pandavas? So where does strata come into picture? Talking of strata did you see —

  • Son of a fisherwoman beocming greatest scholar?
  • Sons of same fisherwoman ruling hastinapur?
  • Son of a mail becoming Prime Minister of Hastinapur?
  • A yadava becoming the God incarnate?

Please don’t dilute the greatness of our epic and culture by baseless assumptions.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/If-Karna-had-all-5-qualities-of-Pandavas-which-was-desired-by-Draupadi-then-why-didnt-Krishna-reveal-the-real-identit-1
Creation time: Aug 30, 2020 05:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Not true.

Eklavya was living and present in Yudhishthir's Rajsuy yagna that happened many years after Draupadi and his twins’ birth.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Eklavya-was-reincarnated-as-Draupadi-s-twin-brother-Dhrishtadyumna
Creation time: Sep 01, 2020 08:07 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Hi
I wish you studied even an abridged version of authentic version of Mahabharata before inviting to debate —
1. If Pandavas were not Kurus, on the same ground even Dhritarashtra was not Kuru. By that parameter even Duryodhana wasn't a Kuru. So question of Kuru or not, is settled.

2. Pandava's were not biological son of Pandu but you may call adopted sons. Adopted sons to have legal rights of Inheritance. Coming to the question of Inheritance, Pandu was the real king, Dhritrastra jus a caretaker. So Duryodhan may have ambition no legal right.

3. Now coming to capabilites. King Bharata didn't make his son the king. King Shantanu succeeded the throne bypassing his elder brother Bhalika. If you read Mahabharata, people found Pandavas so much more worthy, that Duryodhana feared they may remove Dhritrastra and install Yudhishthir as the king. This is over and above their legitimate right to the throne.

4. Duryodhana is not evil because he wanted throne, he is evil because he went to that inhuman length for his greed that only evils can go — attempt to poison, attempt to burn a family alive. This is monstrous. And to thinks such monster have any right to throne…

5. Bhima didn't abandon Ghatotkatch or Hidimba. Read that chapter and you would know, he didn't wanted to marry her and leave his family. She insisted. And he would leave them after a child birth was a Pre wedding condition, which Hidimba never resented. So why do you? Why can't you think it as an act of compassion, that he married a demon just to prevent her from killing herself?

6. Eklavya was an invited King in the Rajsuy Yagna. This rules out Eklavyas discrimination theory. He became an ally of Jarasandh and ascended to the throne in his rights. Perhaps you missed all these stories. Krishna killed him as an adversary not because he belonged to certain caste — he was an ally of Jarasandha who too was an evil man planning for sacrificing hundred of imprisoned kings. So evils and their companion would meet their fate and their caste won’t save them — neither to Drona nor to Eklavaya.

7. Now coming to your question which warriors were killed fairly lets get on with the list —

  • Bhishma — contrary to what you may watch on the TV Shows, Bhisma never gave up his arms. Neither was he alone. Neither was Shikandi no Arjuna’s chariot, nor was Bhishma alone. An entire army was guarding him. Most of them fled, but Duhsasan stayed fighting with him till the very end. So it was a many to many battle formation. All Shikhandi’s presence did, was dampen Bhishma’s mood and he couldn’t mortally hurt him, although he attacked him. Now this is not on Pandava but Bhishma who was imprisioned of his own opinion that he would not fight Shikhandi even after a gender change. If he didn’t wanted to attack, he had an option to retreat. But a warrior he was, he won’t retreat. So come out of shows, read the epic and see this greatest battle of Kurukshetra.
  • Jaidradha — He was killed in broad daylight. BORI doesn’t include Krishna’s magical sunset story. But accepting the story of Krishna’s illusion true. The illusion was to bring the rat out its hiding. When Jaidradha was killed, it was the daytime and battle time. And note the question, why everyone was keen in hiding Jaidradha? Because they were unable to handle Arjuna and thought, if they can save Jaidradha Arjuna would die. So who is deceitful here to begin with?
  • Karna — Yes. You read it right. Karna died fighting. He had only lost his chariot, not the weapons. On 14th day, Bhima broke Drona’s chariot several times. Drona didn’t request Bhima time to repair chariot. He took a new one. Also if you say earlier even Bhima’s chariot was shattered by Karna and same Karna didn’t stop attacking Bhima when he was out of chariot. So that was the accepted practice.
    Can you tell, why Karna didn’t switch his chariot? Why he wasted time in pulling it out? But it doesn’t matter. When Karna realized Arjuna is not buying this excuse, he started fighting and that is when he died.
  • All Kaurava brothers (except Duryodhana) — Bhima killed all the Kaurva brother including Duhshashan and I don’t see anything unfair written about it anywhere. Let me know if you find anything.

8. Now let us consider what may be considered as unfair wars

  • Drona — No doubt Drona was killed unarmed as he sat shocked, at the tragic death of his son, confirmed by the first lie of Yudhishthira. But It was both stupidity and actions of Drona which led to that point. In fact this applies to all warriors which I would elaborate with Duryodhana. A battle field is no place to sit and lament. Any stray arrow would have killed him. Moreover wasn’t in his command Abhimanyu was killed by several maharathi’s. And before you suggest only Duhsasana son killed him, yes the final blow was his that was preceded by serveral attacker attacking together and disarming him, and mangling him badly. You surely don’t want to say Dushshashana son was more powerful than Karna and Drona.
  • Duryodhana — He was killed on a one-to-one duel. The only unfair practice, if one may say his hitting below the waste. But didn’t Duryodhana had it coming when he tried to molest Draupadi and in a rage Bhima decided to break his thigh? That is a cosmic justice. But the most important part, every warrior that died on either side — blame Duryodhana alone. His greet, his deceit and his monster nature had pushed entire Aryavart to the war. I already describe why he wasn’t the legitimate successor to throne. Why no right to throne gives you freedom to consipire and burn a family alive and what gives you a right to back out of your promise after Pandava’s compete their exile. And yes, they completed the exile, confirmed by Bhisma and not contradicted by Duryodhana as you may see in TV shows. So Duryodhana paid the price for his lifetime of unfair play. Others paid for their alignment with him. And that, my friend, makes it a Dharma Yudha. Dharma prevails.

Now coming to women of Aryavart

Duryodhana and Draupadi

You actually close any reason to debate the moment you said — Why everyone blames suyodhana for disrobing of Draupadi...It was a big mistake

It wasn’t a big mistake my friend. It was a crime. A crime for which there would be punishment — imprisionment, exile or death. What Yudhisthira did was also a crime. He suffered a banishment for 13 years. His brothers too suffered same exile for remaining silent. You see, divine justice spares no one. It didn’t spare Pandavas. They repented. They performed penance. They even pleased Shiva, Hanuman and Dharma. So that exile was their punishment.

But Duryodhana refused to repent. He refused to accept punishment. He tried to torment Pandavas further by trying to mock them in the forest. He was captured only to be released by their generous cousins. But he still learnt no lesson, never regretted his acts — He suffered death penalty, with all his brothers and and allies — This, my friend, makes it a Dharma Yudh.

SAMBHA AND LAKSHMANA

Please share the exact reference where Sambha raped Lakshmana. Rape is a serious charge and when proved as a false accusation is liable for punishment. In this case it will break your entire claim as a house of cards, a conspiracy theory — Are you sure it was a rape?

As I know, Sambha abducted Lakshmana. He was captured. Balarama got him freed and Duryodhana married his daughter to him. I don’t see a rape or a rapist here.

So am I trying to endorse abduction? Not at all. Just establishing the difference — the two can’t be equated. Nor Lakshmana’s abduction can be equated to disrobing of Draupadi.

Abduction was a accepted marriage customary in those days. While Krishna and Arjuna abducted their bride with their consent, Duryodhana and Bhisma abudcted brides without their consent. Yes, even Duryodhana had abducted the mother of Lakshmana. But you don’t call him a rapist. Do you? He married Bhanumati after abduction, and so did Sambha.

But any abduction without the consent of the woman should be deemed as a crime and liable for punishment. Isn’t that the reason Bhisma suffered his last days on a bed of arrows? Incidently you called his killing unfair.

Justice Delayed, but not denied

In our epic justice is often delayed, but never denied. Draupadi didn’t get justice for more than 13 years. It wasn’t an immediate retribution. Bhishma was punished for abduction after say over a century. But our epic made sure we know, they were punished. So how did you deduce Sambha was spared?

Sambha is referred in Mahabharata in two occassion only, the other being the musala parva — the end of Yadu clans. In both occassion he is viewed in bad light. Being Krishna’s son doesn’t make him honorable or pure — the evil minded person he was, caused the doom of his own clan.

But he couldn’t be claimed a rapist, something he was not.

That, my friend, makes it a Dharma Yudha.

Feel free to clarify your doubts. But I would recommend do read some authentic texts before creating a narrative and do use some good spacing like paragraph to speak your mind. It becomes easy to follow your thought.

Post Comment

Content:

Just wondering, how Bhishma calling Karna Kauntaya on his death bed would save Arjuna? And why that secret should be revealed only after Bhishma was on his death bed and not before?

Everyone including Bhishma and Karna knew it was a lost war. They would be vanquished. Bhishma explicitly said so — Krishna and Arjuna together is invincible. Karna confessed the same on multiple occasions including when he met Krishna.

Bhishma like every other good people of the age including Parshurama, Pandavas, Dronacharya etc, desparately wanted to avoid the war. But warmongers like Duryodhana and Karna were in favour of the war. That was the prime reason for Bhishma’s resentment with Karna. He could see, it was become of his arrogance and support, Duryodhana wouldn’t turn away from the war and his family is on the brink mutual destruction.

When Bhishma saw that Karna ego is keeping him out of war as long as he lived, he had no reason to divulge any secret to Karna, something Karna already knew too well.

When Bhishma finally fell and he knew he would not be able to prevent the destruction, he revealed the secret to Karna, his last attempt to prevent the destruction of his entire clan. Mind it entire clan. Not Pandavas. He failed. And rest is history.

Unknown to him, however, Karna was already aware of his real identity, something which had done nothing to soften his lust for war and to prove himself the best of archer. When Krishna couldn’t persuade him, when his mother Kunti and father Surya couldn’t see him either rationale of emotion, what sway Bhishma had on him?

But to think of protecting Arjuna? Was he not aware of the outcome of Draupadi’s swaymvara? Or battle that followed? Was he not aware of the Rajsuy yagana battle? Was he not aware of the Gandharva battle? Was he not aware of the Virata battle?

Well he was well aware of not only the past, but also the future and he explicitly spoke about both present and past and in no authentic narrative he seem to be worried about Pandavas. He certainly was worried about what would befall of Kauravas.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Why-Bhishma-calls-Karna-as-Kunte-only-after-he-was-in-dead-bed-Is-it-to-save-Aruna-knowing-the-ability-of-Karna
Creation time: Dec 13, 2020 07:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

What does this article prove? It is too full of ‘maybe’ and ‘it appears’ to prove a worthy reading material to contradict the extensive research of BORI. With all its suppositions —
1. It maintains that Karna called Draupadi a whore

2. It maintains that Karna asked Dussassana to strip Draupadi

So, I am not sure —

  1. How you reached the conclusion that Karna’s dialog insluting Draupadi couldn’t have happened.
  2. Is this article written by a scholar team who has more extensive research than BORI (40+ years) to claim entire ancient text as an interpolation?
  3. Even this article indicates deems everything till shloka 40 where Dussassana begins to disrobe Draupadi is infact authentic. Like BORI (and based on BORI) it claims that Druapadi’s prayer to Krishna is interpolation.

The current article explores possible symbolism and tries to thrust their understanding of Indian society packaging their perception in maybe and it appears.

This doesn’t exonarate Karna’s henious act in Draupadi’s vastraharan failed attempt.

  • BORI and all authentic text clearly shows —
    • Karna refutes Vikarna’s compassinate plea
    • He calls Draupadi a whore
    • He asks Dussasana to disrobe Draupadi
  • There are several cross-reference to Karna’s role
    • Ashwatthama and Kripacharya in Matasya Parva
    • Karna himself while talking to Krishna
    • Krishna at the time of Draupadi’s death
  • Even this article does’t contradict these claims.

Here is the symbolism mentioned in the given article for those who are not interested in reading the entire article.

Karna orders Duh~sana to disrobe DraupadL He does not touch her or her garments himself: Between the yugas, where the Mah~bhflrata story is set, the miracle of the sarees thus symbolizes that the Earth holds the power of her own renewal. And Karna the figure of the Sun and potential agent of destruction, remains explicitly distanced from DraupadL In simplest terms, the restoration of DraupadFs sarees shows that, except at the time of the pralaya, the Earth's power to restore her garments is inexhaustible.

  • The Article mentions Karna orders Duhsassana to disrobe Draupadi
  • He is denoted as a potential agent of destruction.

Nothing in the article is suggestive that Karna had no explicit role in Disrobing Druapadi.

Content:

Arjuna made an effective use of a weapon that ensured his victory with minimal destruction. I can’t find fault with Arjuna’s strategy. But Arjuna’s weapon is not listed in most potent weapons and wasn’t comparable to the likes of Brahmastra or Narayanastra that the opposing camp had. They failed to counter in time and were routed.

Each warrior present conceded that they were vanquished. No one complained it was unfair. Should we? Can we?

Creation time: Apr 15, 2021 11:42 AM PDT

Post Comment

Content:

I agree to most of the parts of what you said. But I don’t see Drona’s decision unjust.

He had rejected Ekalayva. In response, not only he continued to learn but also continued to use his name.

There is no clear answer as to why Drona asked Eklavaya for his thumb. You may chose to think that Drona was a selfish person. But had he been so he would not have made Arjuna a great warrior than his own son Ashwathama or his patron’s son Duryodhana.

Or you can see an alternative reason. A great guru who taught all sorts of pupil could have seen his inclination and wanted to discourage him. He had seen Ekalavya wouldn’t listen to his advise. Maybe had Drona had been a more stringent option, Ekalavya wouldn’t have died on the hands of Krishna.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Because-of-Arjun-Eklavya-lost-his-talent-in-archery-Why-Dhristadyumna-next-birth-of-Eklavya-to-kill-Drona-fought-on
Creation time: Apr 27, 2021 10:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Dear Rama Mohana Reddy,

Perhaps you didn’t read Gita. Is there a point in this debate? These are exact questions answered. But since you asked me, I would certainly try to help.

What do you think is the cause of India-Pakistan conflict? A piece of land. Lac of lives are lost in past fifty years. What would you like to do? Give up your claim? And more important, condemn those people to live under a foreign regime? Most of us feel otherwise.

Pandavas war wasn’t for the land. It was for the legacy of Bharata which was tarnished by a blind king and his evil sons. Same sons, who had tried to kill them, and their mother in a heinous brutal ways.

You asked — What messages did Pandavas give to society.

Well here are some of the messages they gave to the society.

  • Try to maintain the family values even if some of them stoop so low as to try to poison you. For the sake of the family tolerate the evils.
  • Even if people try to burn you down, still try to make peace. And in the process never give up the good you can do for the society — the death of Hidimba and Bakasura are testimony.
  • When a bunch of powerful kings, blinded by their lust and angered by their failure try to throw a maiden into fire, become their shield and fight their might. People would equate you to Vishnu, Indra and Parshurama.
  • Even if your family cheats you by deceit, and your grand-father and guru decided to keep mum, you still hold your end of bargain.
  • Kauravas humiliated Pandavas and Draupadi. And then they were captured by Gandharvas. What did Pandavas do? Mock them, the way they intended? No. They protected them. Don’t you find any lesson there?
  • Even if you get the most power allies and most potent weapons capable to destroying the enemies you still hold your commitment and complete the terms of vanvaas.
  • Even when your deceit opponent refused their end of bargain, still try for peace — not because you are afraid of war — because you value peace.

But above all…

It was a lesson that you can’t value your family more than the people whom you have pledged to protect. You can let them suffer in the hands of tyrant who has no value for family, social norms, commitments or ethics. You need to fight them even if it means fighting those of your beloved ones who support them.

So my dear Rama, it wasn’t about the land it was about the duty to rule.

Now would you like to answer a few questions —

  • Why did those grand father supported the evil knowing they were evil? Why didn’t he support Dharma?
  • Why did Drona asked Panchala for Gurudakshina? Was that a piece of land or not?
  • Do you know who killed Drona? Was he a Pandava? Who killed the father of that killer?

If you don’t want to trust Krishna, Shiva at still want to argue against them and their will, at least learn the epic to debate properly.

Content:

What you consider is your personal belief and I can’t comment on that.

But I don’t understand how is this comment relevant to the question or my answer? The question was could Karna have defeated Devas? And answer is NO. There are proofs to support he couldn’t have done. He was defeated by Gandharvas and he fled. He didn’t had courage to face his adversary. Period.

If you look carefully, there is no mention of Arjuna in this question.

But still if you insist on converting it to a Karna-Arjuna debate, it’s your choice.

Do you know —

  • Arjuna defeated Karna in Draupadi Swyamvara, where Karna withdrew scared?
  • Karna fled Gandharvas who were defeated completely by Arjuna?
  • Arjuna fled thrice in Matasya battle.
  • Karna failed to protect Jaidradh from the wrath of Arjuna?
  • Karna was badly defeated on day 16.
  • Arjuna killed Karna’s brother and son in front of his own eyes and he could do nothing?

You said — Krishna saved Arjuna from a snake weapon

Do you know what was a snake weapon? It was the evidence of true evil nature of Karna. It was not a divine weapon. It was Karna colluding with a vengeful snake. And only Karna could have stooped that low to have worshipped such a vengeful snake for years to take his help in killing Arjuna. Just wondering if you have any word about this unfair battle?

You said — Arjuna killed Karna through unfair means

Could you please elaborate, what unfair means did Arjuna employee? Do you know Arjuna invoked Dharma before killing him and would have failed had he not be true to Dharma.

So please elaborate what unfair means did Arjuna employee? And please share facts not some one said so. People often say great words in obituary for those departed. So stick to fact and tell me precisely what was that unfair means?

Content:

There are two problems —

  1. Barbarik doesn’t exist in authentic versions of Mahabharata.
  2. Even if we consider his existence for a while, there was nothing in him to qualify as a great warrior, forget being in top ten. All we hear is the power of weapon.

For more information about Barbarik, you may check out some of my previous answers —

  • How did Lord Krishna stop Barbarik from using his 3 arrows? How did his head get cut off? What’s the whole story?
  • If Ghatotkacha wasn't the most powerful warrior, then how did his son Barbareeka become one undefeatable warrior? Where did he acquire all those powers from?
  • Who is barbarika in Mahabharata? And what his role in Mahabharata?
  • How did Barbarik become undefeatable in Mahabharata?
  • What is the role of Barbrik ji in Mhabharta?
  • What would have happened if Barbareek had also participated in the Mahabharata War?
  • If Barbarika (Mahabharata) could destroy all his opponents within a minute, then what is the confusion? I mean there would not be any losing side left for him to join.
  • What would have been the result of Mahabharata had Barbareek/Barbarik participated in it?
  • How many days would it take Barbareeka to end the Mahabharata battle?
Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Who-are-the-top-10-warriors-in-Dwapara-Yuga
Creation time: May 06, 2021 06:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Yes please ask. That is how people learn.

1.WHY INDRA WANT TO TAKE ARMOUR AND EARRING OF KARNA

The armour and earrings were divine artifacts and were extremely powerful. Earrings contained Amrita that would prevent the killing of evil Karna. Not only Indra, but also divinity like Krishna and Shiva wanted him vanquished.

Karna had received those artifacts because her mother demanded those protection for his son as she knew she would have to abandon him. She may have hoped Karna would grow to be a righteous person as every parent hopes. But Karna turned out to be an evil person.

Yes, Indra approached Karna to take away his armours and ear rings so that evils may not be protected by them. But in fairness can you say —

  • Was Karna happy or not with the exchange?
  • What would have happened on day 14, had Karna not exchanged his Kavacha? Entire Kuru forces would have been destroyed and Karna mangled by Ghatotkatch. He would have lived a loser’s life — immortal like Ketu. Do you know the story of Ketu? He had taken amrita so he couldn’t die. But he head was chopped. He lives just as a head. Now imagine what would have happened on day 17? Arjuna could have cut his head as Kavacha don’t protect neck. Amrita would have kept that head alive.

2.Why did Indra favor Arjuna?

Fathers generally favor their sons, just like Surya did to Karna. But did Indra really protect Arjuna? Let us check a few instances —

Did Indra save Arjuna from Lakshagriha? He may well have perished in that fire. Did he prevent that fire? Did he extinguish that fire?

Did Indra help Arjuna in Khandava Dahana? As per my understanding he fought against Arjuna. Correct me if I am wrong.

Why didn’t Indra control the dice and stopped Arjuna from getting enslaved? Surely he would have power to do so?

When yaksha killed four Pandavas, including Arjuna, why didn’t Indra protect his son?

Why didn’t Indra gave a free pass to Arjuna to swarga loka? Why did Arjuna have to win the grace of Shiva to get an entry to Swarga?

Now tell me where and how did Indra really help Arjuna? Was it by giving vasvi shakti to Karna that could have killed him?

Think again! Had Indra not exchanged kavacha for vasvi shakti, who would have benefitted more? Karna or Arjuna? Arjuna had always defeated Karna. He would have again defeated him even though he may not have been killed. But Karna would be deprived of Vasvi Shakti.

Who do you think had better chance of killing Arjuna —

  1. Karna with his kavacha?
  2. Karna with the Vasvi Shakti?

If you still don’t understand, here is what Duryodhana said —

“O supreme among kings! If you think that the Parthas cannot be countered because they are helped by the gods, throw away your fears. O descendant of the Bharata lineage! The gods obtained their divinity because they are impervious to love, hatred, malice and avarice and because they ignore all sentiments. Dvaipayana Vyasa and the immensely ascetic Narada have told us this in earlier times, and so has Rama, Jamadagni’s son.137 O bull among the Bharata lineage! The gods never act like humans, driven by love, hatred, malice and avarice. If Agni, Vayu, Dharma, Indra and the Ashvins favour the Parthas because of their affection, they would not have faced hardships.

—Debroy, Bibek. Mahabharata: Volume 4 (Kindle Locations 4998-5003).

Now can you please tell me one thing —

Even the evil Duryodhana knows Gods are impartial? What makes you guys so blind, that you can see the truth?

Creation time: May 08, 2021 08:18 AM PDT

Post Comment

Content:

Gandhari was responsible for not taking care of Duryodhana. But banishing Shakuni wasn’t going to help. Contrary to popular modern narratives, Shakuni wasn’t the mastermind of conspiracies. He was a minor stooge in Duryodhna’s consipiracies.

The only time Shakuni was mastermind was the gambling game. Before and after that he was just a follower — something like Sambha to Gabbar Singh.

Creation time: May 08, 2021 08:21 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

As per authentic epic. Once the naga was fired and Arjuna realized his existence and identity, he killed it instantly.

Also with surprise element gone and conspiracy busted, Karna had no option but to deny him. It was now a public knowledge. Important is he used a snake as a weapon to kill his enemy.

Content:

Hey why are you attacking me instead of attacking my answer? Is it because you can’t attack my answer?

Do you even know the books I am referring to? Do you know by attacking my references you are actually exposing your ignorance? The books I am referring are the authority on the subject — the most directly related to Ved Vyasa. And that narrative of Vyas didn’t call him Mrityunjya, but the trunk of an tree that was evil Duryodhana. Great people like Krishna and Parshurama called him evil and wicked. But you won’t know, because you never read those books.

Can you contradict a single Fact I mentioned in my answer? No. All you can do is blabber.

And yes, I would like people to read my book and understand our glorious cultural heritage which has been maligned by several fictions that glorify the villains. Just for your information, my book isn’t about Karna. My answer is no way related to my book.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-is-Karna-called-Mrityunjay-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 09, 2021 01:31 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

BORI clearly states Karna worshipped the snake for years and he affixed it on the arrow which Krishna saw — Having said this, he (Karna) released that arrow, the serpent which he had worshipped for many years. He said, ‘O Phalguna! You have been slain,’ and swiftly shot the arrow. On seeing that Karna had affixed the serpent, Madhava, supreme among strong ones, used his strength to press down on the chariot with his feet.

Karna was certainly boastful, but was never averse to foul play —

  1. When the Pandavas revealed themselves after Draupad’s swyamvara, Karna suggests to go for direct battle and kill them while they are weak and before Vrishini’s join them.
  2. He was the one who accepted Susharma’s idea to plunder Matasya after the death of Keechak.
  3. Don’t forget Abhimanyu’s battle.

Is there any reason to contradict BORI’s explicit reference? I don’t see.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Arjuna-countered-nagaastra-realesed-from-karna-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 10, 2021 10:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I think, you were lacking. Right? Now you have it.

You can also ignore them. They don’t understand the difference between BORI CE and Annals by a scholar.

As I said —

  • BORI CE is a 40+ years of research by number of scholars and inspecting as much as 1259 mansucripts. You can’t contradict it by an individual opinion. If someone doesn’t understand even this much, you can understand how much epic they would understand.
  • The Editor in Chief Vishnu Suthankar never said Draupadi wasn’t disrobed.
  • Even those scholars who wrote annals say they didn’t find many reference to disrobing. But none of them said, Karna wasn’t involved — there are multiple references.

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Karna-keep-quiet-when-Draupadi-was-being-disrobed-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 12, 2021 01:41 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Is your comment even relevant to this question? Or is this just a hate speech against Arjuna? Is it really necessary to push irrelevant and unrelated comments on any post?

Did you read the question. It’s about Karna, Drona, Bhishma and Ashwatthama!

But! You may be right. I understand your point.

During Matasya battle Arjuna was alone. Bhishma, Drona, Ashwatthama and Karna were supported by an army. Could we consider that a fair fight? Of course not. Arjuna was still stronger than all of them together. He along defeated armies of Gandharvas and Nivatkavacha and many Gods during Khandava. How can we expect a fair fight between Arjuna and an army of mortals who included people who would often run away?

Do you know why Drona didn’t form Chakravyuha for the first two days of his command? Do you know what changed on the 3rd day? Sure Arjuna couldn’t have defeated Drona in a fair fight, who was open to unfair fights only — forming Chakravyuha when Arjuna was away and killing a lone warrior in a pack?

Do you know how many times Ashwatthama was defeated by Arjuna in the entire epic? Do you know how many times he defeated Arjuna? But yes if someone who doesn’t know how to use Divyastra and still uses Divyastra for blind vengeance, how would you defeat him in a fair fight?

And should we really talk about Karna? No one could defeat him in fair fight. Because he never fought fair! And he always ran away thinking fleeing is not a type of defeat. How would you defeat him who doesn’t care even for his best friend? Did he not run away scared at Draupadi’s swaymvara? Then from Gandharvas? Then thrice in Matasya battle? Then fled Abhimanyu even after seeing his brother killed?

You can’t defeat someone in fair fight if they don’t know the meaning of fair fight.

And as far as the ability of Arjuna is concerned, better see what Bhima, Drona, Ashwatthama, Karna, Parshurama, and even Mahadeva said about it. Or see his feats through out the Mahabharata.

Or skip all these things, ignore what happened and keep believing what you want.

Regards,

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Can-Karna-Bhishma-Drona-and-Ashwathama-have-the-ability-to-defeat-any-Deva-in-a-single-one-on-one-battle
Creation time: May 15, 2021 09:33 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I can understand. TV show have a way of influencing people. However, my answer is based on authentic version of Mahabharata where Karna is more evil and less unlucky —

  • Drona never rejected him. He taught him the Dhanurveda
  • Karna never objected to Lakshagriha.
  • Draupadi never rejected him.
  • Karna wasn’t drunk when Gandharva attacked. In fact they went to mock Pandavas on the suggestion of Karna. And Karna fled the battle leaving his best friend behind.
  • He tried to throw Draupadi back in fire but was pushed back by Arjuna
  • Bhishma didn’t refuse to fight along side Karna. Karna refused to fight first ten days of battle under the command of Bhishma becuase he felt he won’t get the glory of his heroics.

Unfortunately modern narrative and TV Show has heavily whitewashed Karna and it natural that if someone just follows the TV Show.

Bhishma indeed suffered a lot all his life.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Karna-keep-quiet-when-Draupadi-was-being-disrobed-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: May 19, 2021 08:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Hi Rama,

you keep commenting on my POSTs without any rhyme or reason or justification and never respond back.

There is no point in this debate.

Did Karna ever defeat Arjuna?

Did he not run away from Draupadi’s swyamvara?

Did he not run away from Gandharvas?

Did he not fled Arjuna thrice in Matasya battle even after seeing his own brother killed?

Did he not fled Abhimany after seeing his own brother killed again?

Could he save Jarasandha from Arjuna?

Who was superior warrior?

  • What did Bhishma say?
  • What did Karna himself say?
  • What did Drona say?
  • What did Ashwatthama say?
  • What did Krishna say?
  • What did Shiva say?

And most important — What happened in every Karna-Arjuna face-off.

Can you quote what was the unethical method Arjuna used to kill Karna?

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Arjuna-have-to-kill-Karna-while-he-was-unarmed-when-he-could-have-easily-defeated-him-bein
Creation time: May 21, 2021 02:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

BORI doesn’t include Draupadi’s prayer to Krishna. But it does include Dussasana’s attempt to disrobe Draupadi. As per the critical edition, Dharma protected Draupadi. Anicent versions like Neelkhantha mentions Krishna saved her after she prayed.

If you ask my opinion, the two aren’t really different. Remember Krishna incarnated to establish Dharma, to protect the good and to destroy the evil. He was the divine incarnate and can be called Dharma himself. So I don’t see the difference if you call Krishna or Dharma.

Important is the core incident is maintained in the BORI CE and all know dialect of Mahabharta.

Hope that answers your question.

Although, I have not read the book under question, I would politely disagree with your absolute rejection of fictions. Here is my view — Should we reject all epic based fictions?

I would appreciate if you share your opinon.

Post: https://vyasamahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-is-your-take-on-this-book-Shikhandini-Warrior-Princess-of-the-Mahabharata-by-debutant-author
Creation time: Jun 14, 2021 12:08 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for your views.

But in my view our epics are superior to history. history or Itihaas is about past tense (Iti — what is over). But our epic is a timeless wisdom.

You said — history is immune to distortion/deviation/decoration.

And I would like to contradict your notion that history is immune to distortion/decoration/deviation. Nothing that is touched or maintained by human emotions are immune to distortion/deviation/decoration. Let me give you a food for thought — Who won Indo Pak war 1965? What does history say? Well before you respond to this question, I would recommend do a bit of research — what does Indian history say? What does Pakistan’s history say? What is international view on this matter? And you would understand the truth!

You said — If something is added or deleted from TRUTH, it no more remains TRUTH, but add or delete any amount of LIE in LIE, it will still remain a LIE

Let us understand truth is not universal. Is it day time? Now is the day time. Today is very very hot. These may be truth for me. But they are not universal. Truth varies. Therefore our epic don’t dwell on truth, but Dharma. And yes, the two things are different.

If you read through Mahabharata, many statements contradict each other. Krishna himself has contradictory statements. Events too have contradiction. So following your argument would make the entire epic a lie. But as I believe, epic doesn’t divulge on minor truth, but on Dharma. And that is perhaps an important take away.

Now few things that you said —

You said — Ramcharitmanas is a very beautiful work of devotion (bhakti) and it is good for inducing love for Shri Ram but it can't replace the history.

Weather it is history or not is not relevant. Question is should a reader read this book or not? What is your view? Should we refuse to read Ramcharit manas because you feel it is not history?

You said — So your argument that Maharishi Valmiki was not with Shri Ram all the time and hence his writing is a work of fiction to some extent, is logic less.

I must point out two things here. First, I never said Maharishi Valmiki’s work is a fiction. Second, the argument that he wasn’t with Sri Ram through all the time is not my argument. It is Maharishi Valmiki’s own argument that is mentioned in Ramayana itself. So if you find it devoid of logic, it was still Maharishi Valmiki’s concern. I only quoted him. And since it is his concern, to my understanding it is valid.

Finally as I said, Valmiki added creative detailing which don’t distort the true essence of the epic. They are not distortions. Not deviations. Not fiction. Just plain addition of detailing.

You said — If something is added or deleted from TRUTH, it no more remains TRUTH

Let me give you an example. Younger brother of Bhishma, Chitrangada died, fighting a battle with a gandharva Chitrangada. That is all that is mentioned in the epic. Does that mean, this is a complete story? Does it include why the fight happened? How did Chitrangad die? Was Bhishma sad when his brother died? Now, tell me this, does adding something here makes the epic a lie? Does it change the fact that Chitrangada died? Does it kill the essence of the epic? Or it simply adds a depth without trying to distort truth?

You said — People are fool, they prefer believing rather than knowing because it's convenient for them. If they will believe every fictional book

You are right. But the hightlight is “every fiction”. Is there a space between every fiction and no fiction? Can you stop people from going for what is convenient for them? Can we force people to read the epic?

Should we just call people as fool and move on?

Now I have a few follow up questions —

  • Would you recommend people to read Ramcharit Manas or no?
  • Can you point out which is the Itihaas book for Mahabharata? Because we don’t have any physical manuscript by Ved Vyas. Perhaps there never was. So which one do you call Itihaas?
  • Can you provide me one reference for Mahabharata which absolute truth on every moment. Which one is Itihaas?
  • Do you know the essence of Shruti-Smriti tradition? The goal is preserve the essence, the soul and the Dharma. Can truth be preserved.
  • What is your view on Lakshmana Rekha? Good or bad for epic culture?
Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Should-we-reject-all-epic-based-fictions
Creation time: Jun 15, 2021 03:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Ah! let me explain you. Here “epic” refers to popular Indian epics like Mahabharata and Ramayana. Some may call them mythology although I desist that word as they are not myth but based on events that happened in pre-historic time.

Now there is a school of thought that believes that there shouldn’t be any fictions based on those epics and should someone write such a fiction it should be rejected and not read. I hope that explains you my context. My post is a response to this school of thought and with specific context to ancient Indian epics.

Now my reference is not for fictions that are completely imaginative tales such as The Lord of the rings or Ben Hur.

Post: https://theaccursedgod.quora.com/Should-we-reject-all-epic-based-fictions
Creation time: Jun 15, 2021 04:08 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No.

My current post is related to Indian epic. The space is related to my fiction The Accursed God.

That said, I am open to any discussion.

Post: https://theaccursedgod.quora.com/Should-we-reject-all-epic-based-fictions
Creation time: Jun 15, 2021 05:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Since we generally debate on the same side, it is not just ok but even healthy to have occasional disagreement. And no, I don’t find you rude.

By the way, I read your entire post including this line — these authors distort the facts as per their own likings — this is an absolute statement giving no room for exception. First I thought to share my views as comment, but then felt compelled to write a separate article.

One of the important point I raised is — Is adaptation and distortion synonym? I would come back to this point later.

On other issue that I disagree is calling our epic as history, for I strongly believe our epics are far more superior and valuable than history, that is just a record of the past. But nothing is really ‘past’ if you realize that time is not linear but circular in nature. What is past may have little value, but the cyclic nature of time ensures that it is never irrelevant.

Another problem in your assumption is history is immutable and can’t be changed. But nothing, and I repeat nothing, that is touched by human mind can’t remain untouched by his thought process. Let me give you two examples —

1.Who won Indo-Pak war 1965?

I hope you would agree it is a historical data and nobody can be allowed to change the past. Right? So what does history say? Before you answer this question, I would advise you to do a bit of research. Not only what Indian history says, but what does the Pakistan history say? And what does International historian say?

2.Can we compare BORI and KMG?

You recommended BORI and KMG. I too recommend these books proudly for authentic reference. But since You and I carry a different view point, We both should have different justifications. Right?

Both these BORI CE and Neelkantha recitations vary on various topics which is the prime reason of absurd debate that happens on social media today. But past can be only one. Right? And even Neelkantha is not original. Right? And you would agree there are contradictions within KMG itself. Agree? How can you then recommend Neelkantha/KMG? BORI CE has many incident described shorter than other narratives. What does that mean? Other ones are distortions or BORI is distortion?

Now you may be thinking, I am discrediting the ancient scripts. Let me assure you, I am not. I too proudly recommend and refer them and would continue to do so without any ethical conflict. It is because I trust that all changes are not distortions. Because I belief that it is not change but the intent of change that we should be questioning. But since you treat them absolutely wrong, how do you justify your stand?

You said — many people have so much wrong knowledge about these epics just because the author of a book or a TV serial maker.

I completely agree with yo here. But tell me what is wrong — making of TV Show or distortion of the truth? Because they are not the same.

Let us take a couple of cases —

1.Gurudakshina battle

This is how BORI describes Gurudakshina battle —

“Capture Drupada, the king of Panchala, in a raid and bring him here to me. O fortunate ones! That will be my greatest dakshina.” Agreeing, all of them armed themselves with weapons and quickly climbed into their chariots and set out, accompanied by Drona, in order to pay the preceptor’s fee. Those bulls among men destroyed Panchala and went to the capital of the immensely powerful Drupada and attacked it. O bull of the Bharata lineage! Thus capturing Yajnasena Drupada and his advisers in battle, they brought him to Drona.

Now there are several questions here —

  • Where is the details of battle? Battle couldn’t have been so easy as to have concluded in one para. Right?
  • Who won the battle? The entire troop of Kaurava’s and Pandava’s? Based on their past history, how likely it is that they would have coordinated the attack?

Now am I disputing BORI here? Not at all. BORI itself disputes this part —

O king! Drona lived in Ahichhatra, which had towns and a countryside, and was won in battle by Partha and handed over to him.’

  • Now can you tell me why is Partha mentioned here? Can we consider Partha to have included Kauravas?
  • So who won the battle?
  • Isn’t it safe to assume that at least one of these two narrative is inaccurate?

Now if we also consider Neelkantha, there is a detailed narrative of the battle that sides more with the second reference than the first one. So should I trust Neelkantha over BORI?

That brings another question — should adding detailing to a shallow event a distortion?

2.Draupadi’s swyamvara’s target

None of the epic say what was the exact design of target that was to be shot down during Draupadi’s swyamvara. None of them says it was a revolving fish. None of them say there was suppose to see it’s image in a water. So should we call this a distortion?

Then the question is, how would you picturize this event? Because no matter what design you use for picturizing it would still be wrong as there is no exact description of the target mentioned anywhere in the epic. So would you call every depiction a distortion? Or it is simply a matter of detailing?

Can you see my point — distortion vs depiction?

3.Overglorified Karna

Now that is a distortion, because it squarely contradicts the epic narrative and in order to justify the points, people would have to provide events and narratives that goes against the true nature of the epic. It is not detailing, but distortion.

So would your reject everything just because you are worried about distortion.

As I see…

  • Epic is about Dharma and the essence of it.
  • It is superior to history and even the primitive notion of truth.
  • Not all changes are distorting. Your intent describes what is distortion and what is not.

Now finally a question for you —

  • How would you ensure that the next generation would still know epic with it’s original essence?
    • You can’t stop TV Show production
    • You can’t stop fiction books
    • You can’t influence their subscriber base
    • You can’t make everyone read BORI and KMG

Here is my solution —

  • Reach there market.
  • Give them a format that they would digest.
  • Let them feel proud of our great epic.
  • Provide counter to other popular narratives. Few will ignore, few will agree and few would be tempted to explore more.

I felt that would be better than doing nothing. And I am proudly doing my part. As quoted earlier from Ramayana, even great Maharishi Valmiki had a similar dilemma and we are blessed he decided to continue setting it aside.

If you don’t agree or subscribe to my view, I would understand.

Post: https://vyasamahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-is-your-take-on-this-book-Shikhandini-Warrior-Princess-of-the-Mahabharata-by-debutant-author

Post: https://theaccursedgod.quora.com/Should-we-reject-all-epic-based-fictions
Creation time: Jun 15, 2021 11:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Agree. I understand and agree with your concerns. I have always talked against distortions.

My concern was your Generalization of all fiction as distortion.

Similarly I don't see objection in adding character and incident to introduce depth to understanding as long as they don't betray the essence of the epic — I would add Lakshman Rekha as my favorite example here. They don't exist in Valmiki Ramayana but I don't see it a distortion.

Let me give you a narrative that didn't exist from a book that doesn't exist —

What once was a magnificent garden was a trash. Scores of bodies lying around — some were not dead. But he was dead. His master! Bhanu was immobilized. He hoped that demon monkey should have killed him instead of mocking and kicking him. Now Bhanu will have to face him. His master's big brother! How would he tell Yuvraj that his younger brother is dead? Would he live? Yuvraj would surely kill this demon. Bhanu again looked back at that huge demon monkey who was on his rampage as if taunting the might of greatest empire? He would be destroyed. Would he? Bhanu recalled his master saying the same thing. And now his master was dead. Stone dead.

Here Bhanu is fictional character. What he is thinking is imaginary. But can you say, it is wrong?What do you think someone like Bhanu might be doing in the that situation? I hope you understand the scene.

I would repeat, intent alone defines distortion or decoration — here added character and incident offer depth to epic without affecting the soul.

Nice chatting with you.

Regards,

Vivek

Post: https://vyasamahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-is-your-take-on-this-book-Shikhandini-Warrior-Princess-of-the-Mahabharata-by-debutant-author
Creation time: Jun 17, 2021 11:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Agree. It was a strategy. And he did what was right for him. This incident doesn't rate him coward. I didn't mention that either in this context.

But he certainly said here, he is terrified of Arjuna

There are other incidents that proves the kind of warrior he was — Gandharva battle, Matasya battle Swayamvara battle and Abhimanyu battle. But let us not deviate from the question.

Post: https://thoughtsonepics.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Karn-offer-his-kavach-kundal-free-of-cost-to-Indra-or-charged-something-in-return-with-strong
Creation time: Jun 21, 2021 07:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Thanks for your input.

Post: https://thoughtsonepics.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Karn-offer-his-kavach-kundal-free-of-cost-to-Indra-or-charged-something-in-return-with-strong
Creation time: Jul 14, 2021 11:16 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

The good thing about Mahabharata is it is not history but epic.

Had it been history —

  • It would have hidden the fact that Yudhishitra gambled away his own brother and family.
  • It would have hidden the fact that Karna was without chariot when he was killed.
  • It would have hidden the fact that Arjuna was aided by Shikandini while killing Bhishma.
  • It would have hidden the fact that Arjuna couldn’t save all Dwarka women from Dwarka robbers.

But since epic is not history, facts are not concealed. Hope that clears your doubt.

Few people however would go to any extent to defame our great epic. They may even create fake profile to post their answers or comment. Beware! Don’t be duped by them.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Pandavas-could-never-be-able-to-defeat-kauravas-fairly-tell-me-just-one-kuru-warrior-who-was-got-killed-by-pandavas-f
Creation time: Jul 15, 2021 10:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Indeed! It targets young generation who know little about the epic and doesn’t know what distortions it has created. Don’t trust me, do watch other shows by Mr Tiwari like Porus and Radha Krishna to see the level of distortion this guy goes for unwanted TRP.

A HD and over saturated graphics certainly provides attraction to eyes and few actors excel also. But overall it is a distortion and if you keep following would lose the original notion of what our grand epic is.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Doesnt-the-Star-Plus-version-of-Mahabharata-portray-all-characters-as-Mr-Goody-Shoes-especially-how-death-scenes-of
Creation time: Jul 22, 2021 09:20 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

It’s not mere reasoning. It’s clear. There are additional reason that you would find in my podcast.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Did-Karna-used-Rukmis-Vijay-bow-or-he-obtained-from-Parashuram-Can-we-believe-in-Karnas-statement-who-himself-lied-ma-1
Creation time: Aug 16, 2021 07:17 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Do you know your comment is entirely baseless?

What exact phrase of Manusmriti yo have in mind?

By the way if you care to read authentic versions Mahabharata you would know following points —

  1. Duryodhana, Dhritrashtra and Gandhari each accepted that throne was Pandavas both by right and by public opinion. Here I would also add a few vital information for your —
    1. Throne belonged to Pandu and NOT Dhritrashtra. He was just a caretaker King.
    2. If you look into the history of Hastinapur, elder son may not necessarily have right to throne. Shantanu was the third son of and yet was made the king. Pandu himself became King despite being younger. And Everyone (including Dhritrashtra and Duryodhana) agreed Yudhishthira would be made King by popular choice.
  2. Lakshagrih conspiracy was Duryodhana’s plan hatched in conjunction with Shakuni and Pandava. I emphasize — Shakuni wasn’t the mastermind.
  3. If you think (with emphasis) that Duryodhana would have won the war easily and fairly, you haven’t read Mahabharata and I would dare say even Duryodhana and Karna wouldn’t agree to your statement. Not only they both have expressed their inability to beat Pandavas in a fair war, it was confirmed by Bhishma, Parshurama and Krishna himself. Apart from that they were badly beaten during Swaymvara battle, Gandharva battle and Matasya battle. If he had power to win fairly, why do you think he never applied those powers?
  4. You pointed out, I called Duryodhana greedy. Well surprise! I am not alone. Duryodhana himself said so while returning from Indraprastha. He said he was unable to tolerate the prosperity of Pandavas and want to snatch everything. But he knows he couldn’t win a battle against Pandavas.

Now let us come to the war where you talked many melodramatic questions about Dharma —

Bhishma’s death

I won’t tell you — ‘It was important for Dharma to win’. I would ask you to read the epic. Authentic version. Not the TV Show crap.

Arjuna didn’t attack from behind an eunuch. It was a group fight. Pandava side had their warriors and Kaurava warriors too were armed to teeth to protect Bhishma. But most of them fled. Yet Duhshashana was one person standing till the very end.

The only reason Shikandi had to enter arena was Arjuna’s unwillingness to attack Bhishma mortally. Even Krishna had to take up arms to persuade Arjuna. Twice. Did you know that?

Drona’s death

Have you heard the word poetic justice? One day a hapless lady was pleading in front of Drona — asking not to side with her, but to define what is Dharma. Drona was silent. Was that Dharma?

You may find Dharma in killing a chariot less, weaponless warrior by surrounding him from all sides, but find it repulsive that someone who by choice gave up arm in the midst of the battle field shouldn’t be killed!

Yudhishira lied. But he didn’t kill Drona. Do you think it is a great idea to sit in the middle of a battle field and expect you won’t be killed. To think that the son of a man whom you just killed would wait idly?

But more important. Did you know Arjuna tried his best to stop Dhristduymna from killing his Guru, something his esteemed guru never tried for his son?

Karna’s death

You asked — Was it Dharma to kill a person who was extracting his chariot’s wheel?

Why not? Karna had lost his chariot not his weapons. Did you know Arjuna fought without chariot on 14th day? And he didn’t beg anyone to stop. And no one stopped.

Did you know same great Karna attacked Bhima and Abhimanyu while they too were without their chariot. Also without their weapon?

Did you know Karna also attacked and killed many foot soldiers? They too had no chariot.

Why do you have different definition for Dharama!

Duryodhana’s death

You asked — Was it Dharma to kill a person by hitting him below his thighs? You’ll have to reply!

Oh! yes I will reply.

Do you know breaking of Duryodhana’s thigh was part of Bhima’s oath? And do you know fulfilling the oath is considered Dharma?

But do you know why Bhima had taken the oath to break his thigh? Because Duryodhana asked Draupadi to sit on it. Do you think it was Dharma to humiliate someone wife? Humiliate any woman for that matter?

Duryodhana was punished for his crime. And punishing a criminal is Dharma

Do you know when a person is hanged to death for his heinous crimes, his hands are tied, and he remains without weapons. Would you call hanging that person a crime or Adharma?

Duryodhana suffered far less for his crimes.

And next time, before you seek further reply, I would advise you to —

  1. Read the epic and not argue based on distorted tales.
  2. Provide references to support your point.

Creation time: Aug 26, 2021 08:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Thanks for your comment.

Do you know at that point of time even conch shells had names? Weapons to were given names. And it so happened arrow that killed Karna was called Anjalika. But is it really mentioned as a great celestial weapon anywhere in Mahabharata? Have you read it’s description? It was a plain arrow. It wasn’t some Brahmastra or Pashupata. So yes, a single arrow killed Karna.

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-In-Mahabharata-instead-of-abandoning-Karna-couldnt-Kunti-just-have-said-that-she-found-him-in-the
Creation time: Sep 09, 2021 10:16 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

There are two points, I would reiterate from what I said earlier

  1. Duryodhana certainly showed his best performance during his last fight and Krishna suggested Arjuna that he couldn’t be won by fair means. But by no means Bhima was losing the battle. He was fighting powerfully.
  2. Krishna compared the comparative merits and demerits of the two — He said Bhima was more powerful but Duryodhana appear more terrible because of the efforts he has taken.
    1. He was more like a cornered cat with nothing left but to fight this fight.
    2. It was a battle of survival somewhere even the weaks exhibit the greatest valor.
  3. In Mahabharata people have often use superlatives. For example Sanjaya refers to Duryodhana as “King whose truth was valor”. But at most places he has been referred evil, deceitful person that he was. Can we just trust some empty words to invoke enterprise in people?
  4. Throughout the epic Duryodhana was afraid of Bhima and Arjuna. He never won against Bhima. He let his brothers fight and die at the hand of Bhima but could do nothing to protect them. He confronted Bhima in this fatal way when he was left with no one to protect him as confirmed by Dhritrashtra himself.

Finally, hitting under waist in a mace battle was prohibited, and as such it was ruled out as Adharma. If we consider on the immediate action and the rule of the wars, what Bhima did was WRONG. But let us not forget it was not an ordinary war between two very righteous people. It was also a retribution of life long wrong doing by one person against another — poisoning of Bhima, Lakshagriha, humilation of Draupadi. Bhima had taken an oath to break his thighs. And that retribution was perhaps greater than following the protocol of immediate battle.

But most certainly nothing in the large picture and complete epic reference suggest Duryodhana to be undefeatable. On the final day both were fighting with equally with no one coming out as a clear winner. No one. Perhaps he attacked Duryodhana to ensure a swift victory and also fulfil his oath.

Creation time: Sep 09, 2021 11:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Content:
