Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-themahabharataproject-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-as-per-Sanaatana-Dharma-there-is-no-heaven-or-hell-And-if-it-is
Creation time: Sep 30, 2021 04:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Is this question really relevant?

We have people who support Ravana and Karna and hate Krishna, Arjuna and Hanumana.

But when it comes to Gandhiji, these are the prime reason people hate him —

  • They know nothing about him or his ideology. They just follow the social media.
  • They think by condemning Mahatma Gandhi, they are condemning modern Congress and contemporary Gandhis. They don’t realize there is no relation.

But a more valid question should be —

  • Why people worldwide appreciate him? They didn’t read Indian history nor they have any biased for against any Indian political entity.
  • Why great men like Albert Einstien, Barak Obama, Steve Jobs, Martin Luthar King Jr, Nelson Mandela all are Gandhi fans?

Now to answer your question — People like Ambedkar insulted him, why sir?

Ambedkar differed from Gandhi’s ideology. Both of them hated caste based abuse. But Gandhiji wanted to eliminate the root of it by creating all inclusive society. Ambedkar on the other hand was looking forward for a payback for years of abuse in form of reservation.

But do you know the greatest difference between the two? If Gandhji hadn’t persuaded Patel and Nehru, Amedkar may not have found a place in constituent assembly. Gandhji didn’t consider him rival or had not political agenda against him. Ambedkar, as you yourself said insulted Gandhiji on many occasion.

But tell me should I trust Dr Rajendra Prasad, Gurudev Ravindra Nath Tagore and Shubhash Chandra Bose about greatness of Gandhji or social media for condemning him.

I would recommend you to read few books from outside Indian sub continent to understand a more unbiased picture. My first recommendation would be Freedom at Midnight.

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 07:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You don’t have to consider anyone your idol against your own soul. Gandhiji would never have wanted it.

And we live in a country where people don’t find even Rama and Krishna as perfect. So how would Gandhiji attain that perfection?

I never said he was a God. He was a great human. Like other human beings he too made mistakes. He stole gold from his brother. He even abused his wife a long time ago. But do you know what make him great despite of all these faults? You know about them because he told the world about his weaknesses. He was honest to his fault and only those honest to his fault can hope to remedy it. That was Mahatma.

Same goes true about his sleeping with naked woman. World knows because he informed the world. But do you know the true deceit of this world — They believed what they wanted to believe. Ignored what they wanted to ignore. They believed that Gandhi slept with naked woman. They had no prove. But Gandhi’s words were sufficient for them. But then the chose to ignore what he said in this context. He said that I am like a mother to her. He was 70+ and had given up sex at the age of 38. To him it was like Yog or Tapas. Have you heard the story of Vishwamitra and Menka?

People believed Gandhi is telling the truth about sleeping with a naked woman and ignored the Yog and Tapa part of it because it suited their narrative. Think! A how daring a man would be to admit such a bizarre experiment to the world, something he could have easily hidden had he really thought it to be his weakness. But we imagine things based on our own weakness. So Gandhi became a pervert. Right? Don’t you think such dual standard is just a hyporcricy?

Lastly, His bisexual relation with Kallenbach, is a great proof of social media propaganda. There is no proof of such a thing, although they shared a great personal relationship. But personal relationship isn’t same a bisexual relationship. That is a stretch of imagination.

That said, a person’s physical or private relationship shouldn’t and can not be used against him. Neither you nor I have right to comment.

Yes, you have right to chose your idol. Shubhash Chandra Bose chose one. Rajendera Prasad chose and many great men of the world did. You too have that right.

Regards,

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 08:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Unfortunate, but true. But as long as young generation have people like you, we have hope.

Many of these people haven’t read the history for they have been told history is biased. But what is really unfortunate is they don’t even trust their own ancestors. They trust no one other than those propaganda. They don’t trust Rajendra Prasad. They don’t trust Shubhash Chandra Bose, They don’t trust Rabindra Nath Thakoor, they don’t trust Narendra Modi.

All they trust is some unknown story published by an unknown author quaoting equally unverifiable evidence.

Wait for my next poetry. It counter’s many common allegations on Gandhi.

Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 08:24 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

If you have read the articles you have missed the words like “may be”. There is no letters of Mahatma Gandhi in this regard available in public domain. So all those NDTV and BBC are using conjunctures. And a conjuncture doesn’t mean truth.

And I already said, someone sexual orientation is his private business. Not mine. Not yours. Not of the world.

But glad you at least accepted the other parts of my answer.

Regards,

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 08:39 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

A correction. 99% of the ignorant don’t know anything about those and they read only social media a believe in an non-existent conspiracy theory related to coverup. Let me give you a proof.

The early mistakes of Gandhiji life is well documented by him in his autobiography — My experiments with truth. The book is available in public domain for ever and there never had been any attempt to ban this book. So there is no cover up.

The later episode, too was brought to light by none other than ‘Mahatma’ himself and has been again available in many best sellers of all times like “Freedom at Midnight”.

Why should we blame someone if I am lazy enough not to read?

So on a little scrutiny your assumption of a conspiracy theory is shattered.

Now coming to your next point, I agree every freedom fighter deserves respect, but can’t agree with your second part as to why they also deserve scrutiny?

There were not hired or paid help that you need to write their appraisal letter. You didn’t do anything for them. But whatever little they did for the country is their infinite contribution in your life. Why should you be anything but grateful? Anything less than absolute respect is being ungrateful. Remember you didn’t do anything for them. Ever. If you can’t be grateful, if you can’t be respectful, the least you can do is to abuse them.

And criticize if you must, do so with proper understanding and not half baked perspectives. Read the whole of Gandhi. Understand their context, understand the era in which they lived before being judgmental. As I pointed out earlier, you accepted Gandhi’s half truth when he said he slept with naked woman, and rejected the other half when he said it wasn’t sex but Yog and Tapa. Is this scrutiny? Or is this an pervert form of hypocrisy? Was Gandhi a pervert, or my pervert imagination projected him as pervert? Remember he had neither need nor obligation to disclose his experiment. And it was not a guarded secret even then, and the contemporary people still respected him.

You say, you don’t respect him as a person. I can’t change that. But it doesn’t even matter. Gandhi’s persona isn’t going to be shattered if few decide not to respect him or humiliate or abuse him. The world at large cherishes him. But Gandhiji is beyond both these extreme emotions.

Our views don’t define Gandhi, it defines us.

Regards,

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 11:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

If you are questioning his behavior with his wife, might I remind you that was an early phase of Gandhiji about which He wasn’t ever proud. He accepted and changed. Does change matter to you? Does it not show some higher morale?

Let me help you. Once upon a time their was a heinous robber Ratnakar. He looted and killed people. Do you know his other identity? How do you remember him?

At another juncture of history, their existed a cruel ruler whose ambition caused many devastating battles. And after one such battle, he changed for ever. Strived for peace. Have you heard about him? And the honor we gave to him? His symbols are our national symbol today.

And should I tell you the story of yet another King? He abandoned his pregnant wife. And yet, he is one of the most respectable household name in India. Do you know why? Because people didn’t just see an isolated action. They tried to see the full picture.

It is rather easy to judge things in retrospection and by sidelining the context. It is more difficult to see the context and appreciate the action realizing human limitations and emotional bounds.

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 02, 2021 11:21 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

And I have already said, I can’t change your views. I won’t even try.

And I don’t need to defend him. He doesn’t need a defense. He has been an open book.

But since you raised — Let's assume he did nothing wrong in those celibacy tests but still they are wrong at so many levels, can you imagine the trauma of those girls and implication of this on their personal life. Constant fear of “what if somebody find out”?

Do you know the girl? Have you read her views? Please do read them. And as you said about her constant fear, you must realize Gandhiji himself told it to the world. There was nothing like If someone find out! So let me ask you again (and you don’t really need to answer) who has decided that he was wrong at so many levels? You? The society? Few so called intellects who are out to bash Gandhi? Because I didn’t find woman in question, ever complaining.

Since you feel, I am ignoring, let me talk about the so called elephant in the room —

You said — I know you are ignoring this one but let me mention it again He preached truth all his life but failed to report his son even after knowing he has raped someone. I mean a righteous person would never thought about his own image or his son. Justice was denied by Father of Nation himself.

I really wished you should have done a better research before bringing this matter so many times. You are talking about some translated work, which were so bizzare in their nature and translation that they didn’t find any buyer. Do you really care about the truth, I would recommend reading this article, which definitely has a more credible source —

The truth behind news report suggesting Mahatma Gandhi accused his son Harilal of raping his own daughter: Tushar Gandhi's open letter to media

This will prove a sufficient contradiction to even the last lingering doubt in your mind about Gandhiji’s character.

Do you realize how few people with vested agenda are involved in so many baseless propaganda.

And to conclude I must reiterate — Gandhiji owes absolutely nothing to us. And all we owe to him is gratitude. Not a judgement based on unconfirmed sources and reports.

To err is human. Gandhi too was a human. He too made mistakes. But his truth, morale and achievement far exceeded any of his shortcomings. He deserves just undiluted respect from us.

But we have become such a thankless society that we condemn even Rama and Krishna for his actions. Gandhiji was a Mahatma, but still just a human.

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-The-Thought
Creation time: Oct 03, 2021 01:20 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Please read it again. Tushar Gandhi has not only cleared who was the lady but also the relationship between the two. The two people in question were to enter the wedlock and had Gandhiji’s approval. It isn’t a question of rape, but a pre-marital relationship, which may have sounded like a rape to Bapu. And therefore no further action needed other than a strict reprimand. What is important to realize is the way facts are distorted.

Yes the girls opinion is available on public domain and you just need to search for it.

I am not sure one who sees Gandhi’s action as a blatant abuse as a neutral person. I labelled them hypocrite earlier, without messing with the words. They trusted Gandhi halfway and then trusted appointed themselves to be a neutral judge.

Patel’s action was that of a political action as you can see from the word — “terrible blunder”.

And I agree, it may have pained many a follower, for none of us are Mahatma.

In my understanding a neutral person would see the some total of Mahatma, his actions and his contributions. A neutral person wouldn’t be selective. A neutral person would not pick one incident of his life to eclipse all his greatness. A neutral person would realize that if this man has always spoken truth, no matter how hard or hurting it might me, then I must trust this person when he says he is seeking Yog. A neutral person would see, he didn’t farther any of his personal family to gain from the enormous power you referred earlier. A neutral person would see Mahatma wasn’t a person in power but a person who derived is power from his spiritual and moral conduct and not to mention many is immense contribution for which even you thanked him generously. And no wonder neutral person across the world and era seek inspiration from him.

Let me finish this by sharing a peace of wisdom —

Anything you say before ‘but’ is meaningless.

Now read your last line.

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/Apne-Apne-Ram-अपने-अपने-राम
Creation time: Oct 16, 2021 10:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I tend to agree with one part of your post — Abhimanyu was born before Khandava Daha.

That said, when someone endeavors to burst the wrong assumption, it would be a critical mistake to do so by throwing lost of more assumptions. That is what your post is and that causes it to loose it’s creditability.

Let me show you the points.

1. Sixteen Years of Soma

In your reference it reads — And let him, ye best of immortals, stay on earth for sixteen years.

Now here You assume that Soma meant 16 years of heaven. Now there are few problems here —

  • It is your assumption. Soma didn’t say 16 years of heaven. He said he would stay for sixteen years on earth. The frame of reference is earth.
  • Now what is the calculation of 1 year of heaven? Is the exchange rate 2.1? According to Shiva Purana 100 years of human is 1 day of Brahma. That way Abimanyu based on Heavens age must be some thousands of years and not just 36.
  • But bottom line, You assumed Soma said heavens age. There is no explicit reference. Just what you assumed.

2. Dhritrashtra — said Thirty Three Years have passed since Khandava

You didn’t raise any doubt on this statement just the way you raised on the statement of Soma. So let us do a rough calculation.

  • Let us say Khandava Dahana was Year 1 of Abhimanyu. He was born before this period. When, isn’t mentioned. So let us not assume.
  • The preparation of Rajsuy Yagna started soon after. Let us say it took X years. (Algebric Assumption)
  • The great gamble happened soon after. Safe to say in the year X.
  • Now Udyoga Parva happened after 13 years of great gamble.
  • Now that means X+13=33. It took Yudhishthira 20 years from Khandava Dahana to Rajsuy Yagna.
  • But reading the reference doesn’t look like a 20 Years period.
  • So is it possible Dhritrashtra miscalculated the time? You didn’t raise this question.

Now can you tell me, why you assumed Soma may have been confused and not Dhritrashtra.

So let us work on two time lines —

Time Line #1

  • Abhimanyu was born soon before Khandava Dahan. Aged < 1. No reference of exact age available.
  • It took two years to prepare and complete. Abhimanyu is 2 years now. Here you assume two contradictions —
    • Abhimanyu Couldn’t have learnt weapons at a young age. You don’t have any reference to proof your point.
      • You forgot Abhimanyu learnt many thing about art of war before he was born. Three years is an infinitely long time for such a genius.
    • Abhimanyu couldn’t have gone to see off the kings.
      • A young prince may go to see off dignitaries escorted by relatives and ministers. It is a symbolism of giving respect by letting a family member see them off. You don’t need to be of some age. Akbar Participated in war at a young age. Actually his advisor led the troop, he was merely a symbol then.
    • They devoted 13 years in exile. Now Abhimanyu is 15 Years old. Let us add some rounding off —
      • Time to gamble
      • Time to negotiate final battle
    • Abhimanyu died at the age of 16 Just as Soma said.
    • But what about Dhritrashtra’s statement —
      • It contradicts a divine statement, so if I have to assume one of them to be incorrect who would it be?

Time Line #2

  • Abhimanyu was over thousand years when he died. Because his age was mentioned as per heavens years.
    • There is no reference of the fact that Soma mentioned 16 years of heaven.
    • No calculation of 16 heaven hears is mentioned.
    • All calculation everywhere is given in Earth years. So if there is one period mentioned in another unit, don’t you think, it would have been clearly mentioned.
    • NO IT IS YOUR ASSUMPTION. NO PROOF.
  • It took Pandavas 20 years after Khandava before Yudhisthira conducted his Rajsuy Yagna. Only that way Dhrirashtra calulcation of 33 will work.
    • Why is it not mentioned in Mahabharata? Where is the time line?
    • Who is more likely to be right? Some or Dhirtrashtra?
  • Abhimanyu died at an age of 36 instead of 16.
    • The divine wisdom was fake, because you assumed it to be so. Even if it doesn’t match calculation.

YOU ASSUMED THAT MY ASSUMING YOU WOULD BRUST OTHERS ASSUMPTION?

  • If you want to bust the myth, minimize assumptions and maximize Reference
  • You valid References —
    • Abhimanyu was born before Khandava
    • Abhimany was present in Rajsuy yagana and sent of the guests.
    • Dhritrashtra mentioned 33 years have passed since Khandava
    • Soma said Abhimanyu will live 16 years on earth.
  • Your Assumptions
    • Soma said 16 years of heaven … no reference. No clarification.
      • No heaven earth age reference is given anywhere in the reference. You just assumed to make it a point.
    • Abhiamnyu must have been 22 years during Rajsuy yagna … no reference, pure assumption
    • Abhimanyu couldn’t have sent of the guests at the age of 2–3 … no reference
    • Abhimanyu couldn’t have learnt weapons at an early age … no reference. just assumptions.

So except for Dhritrashtra’s statment everything else is against your assumptions. And between Dhritrashtra and Soma, I would choose Soma, specially since everything fits together.

LET US ASSUME YOU BUST THE MYTH AND ABHIMANYU WAS ACTUALLY 36 YEARS

Say your assumption is correct (I have already proven it infeasible), why are you attacking others by saying — wrong information spreaded by certain fanatics of certain characters.

Who are these fanatics? Is it a wrong information because it doesn’t match your assumption? Tell me —

  • Do you know there exists a direct reference that Abimanyu was 16 years old? Remember Soma said so.
  • Is there any direct reference that he was 33–36 years old? NO. You reached upon the calculation by making several assumptions.

Kudos! You are doing a great job of establishing your credential as someone who would understand the difference between Assumption and Realities.

By the way I patiently waited for you on the other thread but never got your response back.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Hey-Everyone-I-am-gonna-start-an-series-of-1-post-per-2-days-of-Assumptions-and-Reality-where-we-will-burst-some-wrong-i
Creation time: Oct 17, 2021 07:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Ohhh look who is here! Vivek!! Nice to see you again

Thanks for your welcome.

You said — We don't have any solid proof about whose 1 years he was mentioning. So let us calculate each and every possible outcome we have.

Let me stop you right there. What you are saying is just assumption something you want to bust. So let us stick to reference. Soma said — 16 years on earth. Mark the word on Earth. There is no mention of other wise.

You said — 1 year of Moon - 1 year of moon is approximately 28 days so it clears that he should die before even 16 years.

Again! Another assumption. No reference. Are you sure you want to fight against assumption? Anyway, this goes on to proof Soma was not talking about any other measurement of year.

You said — According to puranas and Lord Shiva himself it would cross the line of 16 years without any doubt.

Yes. It would cross 16 years well my a margin of over one hundred times! And Abhimanyu wasn’t thousands of years old.

You said — Dwapar yug is 4 time more than today's Yug (Kali yug) So 16 × 4 = 64. So according to Brahma Puran Abhimanyu's age is 64 years if we conclude today's timing.

This is by and far most ludicrous and lame argument you presented. He would have been 64 Years, by todays timing! We are not talking about todays timing. We are talking about the epic timing. And as per that time he was still 16. And it is clearly mentioned in the epic.

IMPORTANT!

All your argument about years is just your assumption. ABSOLUTELY NO REFERENCE. If you want to bring out reality, give a reference. Give me a reference that says Soma suggested 16 years based on a different calendar. He clearly says 16 years on earth.

If you have NO reference, then all you have is assumption.

You said — If Chandra dev was talking about 1 year of Earth then it contradict's the statement of Dhritarashtra's about time passed since Khandav dahan.

You are right. Dhirtrasthra may have been confused about time. His calculation any way fails to meet the reality. If we agree to his calculation there would be a gap of 20 years between Khandavadahana and Rajsuy yagna. But epic doesn’t mention such a time gap. It mentions it in quick succession. So tell me how was Dhritrashtra right about it? These events followed one after another

  • Khandavadahana (Year 1)
  • Jarasandha Vadha
  • Digvijaya
  • Rajsuy Yagna ( Within Next 2 Years)
  • Dyut Krida (Soon after Rajsuy Yagna)
  • Exile (13 years)

And all these calculations fits well with 16 years calculation. And I don’t have to make any assumption. And Soma would proof right.

You said — Abhimanyu was not as younger as we assume him.

Now you assume, that he wasn’t as young as we assume despite of a clear statement by Soma. He may have been as young, he may not have been. But he certainly didn’t die a child’s death. He was indeed younger than the rest of so called warriors, and lot braver than his adversaries. How does the age really matter?

So Question For You —

  • Give me one direct proof that Soma wasn’t talking about Earth’s age. In absence of reference, it is logical to believe local time frame and it matches with the broad timeline.
  • Give me one direct proof that there was a gap of twenty years between Khandavadahana and Rajsuy Yagna.
  • Can you honestly say that those who are saying he was 16 years have no direct proof and are spreading wrong information?
  • You are making assumptions that have no reference, Why should we not consider you spreading wrong unverified and unverifiable information?
  • Why are you branding someone fanatic for quoting direct reference from Mahabharata?
  • What do you intend to gain by the fact Abhimanyu was more than 16 years?
    • Does that justify those cowards who killed him?

May I hope you would answer these question as you are trying to bring out reality? I am hoping that you would answer my question pointwise and not throw in more assumption. If you can’t provide reference, we will assume you have none.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Hey-Everyone-I-am-gonna-start-an-series-of-1-post-per-2-days-of-Assumptions-and-Reality-where-we-will-burst-some-wrong-i
Creation time: Oct 17, 2021 09:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You asked — You give me one direct proof that he was talking about Earth's timing.

The burden of proof is not on me but on you. I have a direct reference — And let him, ye best of immortals, stay on earth for sixteen years

  • It mentions sixteen years on earth.
  • No other time frame is mentioned here.
  • There is no reference to prove otherwise.
  • It matches the timeline

You said it’s a different timeline so you need to prove. Your question back is conclusive proof that you have no reference.

You said — You are also saying He was 16 years old do you have any direct proof.

Once Again. This is not an answer. And I have already answered it earlier. See previous point. I have a direct reference in the epic. Soma said so. It also tallies the timeline

  1. Khandava —-> approx 1 Year
  2. Rajsuy → 1–2 years max
  3. Exile → 13 years

Now sum them. And don’t ask same question again unless you have prove to contradict the time line.

You failed to provide any direct reference that Abhimanyu was not 16 years as said by Soma.

You said — My each and every assumption is logical and justified.

They are still assumptions. Right? And you claim you want to bust assumptions? You think you have a logic. But where is it?

  • You quote absurd theory of a different time line for which you have no proof and no reference
  • You claimed moon year is 28 days. Where is the reference?
  • You claimed based on modern time line he would be 64 years. It is both irrelevant and illogical. No one is talking about modern calendar.
  • Where do you find the logic?
  • Do you think it is ok to propagate wild theories without any backing proof?

You asked — Where did I mention anyone as fanatic you should read my whole post again very clearly. The starting paragraph is a note.

You guessed it. In your starting paragraph which is a statement of your objective. Now do you want to say it doesn’t apply here? It is connected by “So”

You said — Obviously Duhshasana's son was a brave man who defeated One day warior (its my opinion that he is one day warior I don't want debate on it) in a fair battle and killed him bravely.

Don’t debate. Your statement is proof of how much interested you are in “reality”. You are doing a great job. Your statement is a testimony to it.

And I thank your for your mission. Our interaction like this will help reader understand the epic much better. They would surely understand the difference between reality and assumptions.

Regards,

(P.S. Your response to our previous interaction is still awaited for which you had asked two days time)

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Hey-Everyone-I-am-gonna-start-an-series-of-1-post-per-2-days-of-Assumptions-and-Reality-where-we-will-burst-some-wrong-i
Creation time: Oct 17, 2021 11:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You asked — Ok so where did he said that he is talking about Earth's timing just highlight that text.

You don’t really understand or just pretend? You don’t need to give reference for the default frame of reference for anything. All times mentioned in the epic is earth’s time. Pandavas were exiled for 13 years. Is there any mentioned that it was Earth’s thirteen year? No. It is not required.

The problem is the different time zone concept is the product of some weird imaginations that you people are carrying and you can’t expect an epic reference for it.

Bottom line is —

  • If there was a different calculation of time than what is used in rest of the epic it would have been mentioned. But it is not.
  • The calculation exactly matches the chain of events between Abhimanyu’s time of birth and death. A timeline that I mentioned.

Dhritrashtra’s statement

Dhritrashtra statement is inconsistent based on the epic timeline. Khandava Dahana was soon followed by Rajsuy Yagna which was followed by Pandava’s exile. There can’t be a gap of thirty-three years in the actual facts. Clearly Dhritrashtra was confused. I would recommend you to read the epic and you would realize Khandava Dhana and Rajsuy yagna weren’t separated by twenty years.

And this the basic flaw in your logic. Every statement said by every person can’t be considered as truth. Take for example Karna said as per Gods, any woman marrying more than one husband is unchaste. But you can’t consider it as a fact. As God of Gods himself ordained Draupadi’s marriage.

I don’t dispute Dhritrashtra said it was thirty-three years. But he was simply wrong in his assumption as his assumption doesn’t match the whole timeline.

You said — I thought you are one of the best writers on Mahabharat topic, but you definitely forgot to how to read English I said don't debate on “One day warriors statement”.

Thanks for acknowledging my knowledge of Mahabharata. I knew, you would come around someday to this realization.

And I would try my best to read English properly, only if you can use proper punctuations. For example, there is a comma or full stop missing between ‘how to read English’ and ‘I said…’. In such cases, I don’t fail to read English.

May I recommend, read my statement again. I said “don’t debate” about your “one-day-warrior” statement only. I said such statement of your

You said — Now please tell me how I am not interested in reality by that statement.

I think you know the answer. If not, see the next answer.

You said — if you are trying to debate about who is more powerful Dusashan's son or Abhimanyu then I am ready.

Ok! I guess now you realized my English is not lacking. Yes, I did point out about your view about Abhimanyu’s death when I said your are not interested in reality. I trust you are wise enough not to trust that Abhimanyu was killed fairly. And yet you put this message on a thread where you claim you care about reality.

Well you can’t fool others. And the one person you are fooling can’t be saved — you yourself.

I will not debate on this unless you can provide satisfactory answer to the current debate.

Here is a summary —

  1. You failed to prove Soma was talking about any other time frame. He clearly mentions sixteen years on earth. And no where in the epic time frame is mentioned as Earths Time or Heavens years. In absence of any specific mentions, all time unit would belong to same context. The concept of an alternative time frame is not even present in the epic. It is figment of your imagination and epic need not provide any reference. You, however, failed to provide any valid reference for your claim.
  2. You failed to provide a proof of time gap between Khandava and Rajsuy which according to the epic followed in quick succession. This time frame contradicts Dhritrashtra’s statement and conforms to Soma’s statement. You can’t change reality based on assumption or a stray statement.

Once you provide your reference, then you can debate on Abhimanyu’s death.

And please don’t repeat the bogus question asking me to prove It was earth’s time that Soma mentioned unless you prove about every other time mentioned in Mahabahrata to be Earth’s time by an explicit reference.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Hey-Everyone-I-am-gonna-start-an-series-of-1-post-per-2-days-of-Assumptions-and-Reality-where-we-will-burst-some-wrong-i
Creation time: Oct 18, 2021 11:12 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Look who is here! Me Again!

Let us get started by busting your most important misconception — Your Assumption can’t outweigh the fact.

You said — Abhimanyu didn't entered the Chakrvyuh alone he was followed by other wariors.

True. But half truth. And such half truths cast doubt on credibility of someone trying to week out misconception. Why didn’t you mention here that the quotes you have given here is from a time before Jaidradh intercepted and stopped every one? This incident is mentioned in Chapter 1016. Jaidradh enters the battle in chapter 1018. Jaidradh restrains this army in chapter 1019. There is no further mention of Abhimanyu’s army after this point.

Conclusion: Abhimanyu fought majority part of the battle alone, without any supporting army.

Quesiton — Can you contradict my point by a valid proof?

You said — Karna cuts the bow of Abhimanyu fairly. Other wariors destroyed his Chariot. Karna and Drona destroyed his weapons.

Quesiton — Define fairly. Show one reference from Mahabharata, just one reference that says it was a fair fight?

These people attacked him together. Most of them had retreated by his force. And that attack was unfair. I can quote you several references.

You said — And later they didn’t killed him.

Or couldn’t kill him. completely. You need to read the epic. But don’t worry. I am going to give you exact reference.

You said — Abhimanyu was having Brahma's Impenetrable Armour and that was the reason no one was able to pierce him.

This shows you haven’t read the epic properly. There was no Brahma’s impenetrable armor here. If there was Brahma’s impenetrable armor that would mean only two things —

  1. No one could have penetrated it and Abhimanyu wouldn’t have died.
  2. Karna’s impenetrable armor would have met the same fate. He would have died any way even with his armor on.

Let me give you an exact reference of this impenetrable armor —

The preceptor smiled and spoke gently to Karna. ‘His armour is impenetrable. He is young, swift and valiant. His father has been instructed by me on the art of donning armour. It is certain that this destroyer of enemy cities knows everything about that.

  • Making armor impenetrable was an art of donning the armor.
  • Drona taught it to his pupil.

And here is what happened to the shield — Radheya used sharp arrows to cut down his supreme shield. Deprived of his sword and shield, but still whole in his limbs, he again descended from the sky onto the ground.

  • Looks like it wasn’t that powerful at all.

Debroy, Bibek. Mahabharata Vol. 6 (Penguin Translated Texts) (Kindle Locations 869-870). Penguin Books Ltd. Kindle Edition.

You said — So let me tell group attacks were the part of War they we'rent unfair.

The group attack you (and your friends) talk about were all many to many battles where two army collided with each other.

  1. Read the last line of your first reference: At this, in that battle, Bhima pierced Bhishma, Shantanu’s son, with three arrows, Kripa with one and Kritavarma with eight.
    1. Bhisma, Kripa and Kritvarma all were fighting in this war.
  2. You forgot to highlight this part in your second reference : The battle that was fought by all the soldiers was extremely great
    • There were many soldiers.

Now in case of Abhimanyu, he was alone. And it wasn’t a group to group attack. I hope you would see the difference.

You said — And finally he started one on one Mace fight with Duhshasana's son and was killed Fairly.

Quiz — What is the most important word in your above statement?

Answer — Finally.

Imagine a cricket match. A batsman fights against a hopeless score and strikes a century. He gets out when one the team need one run to win. As the luck have it, the last batsman of no great repute scored a winning run. So the question is who actually brought the victory? One who scored the century or one who scored the last run? Didn’t understand. Never mind. I am here to help you with those reference you never read because your friends never shared them —

A little before finally —

‘“At that time, when he was bereft of his chariot, the six maharathas117 quickly and ruthlessly showered him with arrows, while the child fought single-handed. His bow had been severed and he was without a chariot.

See the reference —

  • Six Maharatha ruthlessly showered arrows.
  • Child fought single-handed. This single line busts your two mtyhs —
    • Abhimanyu was indeed alone.
    • He was a child, something you tried to refute in your last post and have not be able to give any valid reference yet.
  • He was without weapon and chariot.
    • He didn’t beg is opponent to stop.

Nearer to finally — Abhimanyu looked beautiful and seemed to replicate the deeds of Vasudeva.119 Blood flowed from his body. His face was red. His eyebrows were wrinkled and he roared like a lion.

He had been ruthlessly attacked by six Maharatha. Blood flowed from his body. And Duhasasana’s son has yet to make is entry in this fair battle.

Now coming to finally —

Abhimanyu was badly mangled by all six Maharathas. He was badly injured but not dead yet. That is when he entered his last run —

The ratha who was Duhshasana’s son, the extender of the deeds of the Kuru lineage, arose first and struck Subhadra’s son on the head with the club, as he was about to get up. Because of exhaustion and because of the great force of the club, Subhadra’s son, the destroyer of enemy heroes, lost his senses and fell down

If you still don’t understand his cause of death, read the highlighted text.

Ok. So let us ride to the conclusion —

Was Abhimanyu’s death fair or unfair?

Abhimanyu’s death is an exemplary example of how low can one stoop to exonerate their favorite evil man from the darkness of his character. Your half quotes is distorting the epic. Let us check out some direct answer to this question from the epic itself —

Sanjay — ‘“O king! Thus, in that battle, a single one was slain by many. He had agitated all the soldiers, like an elephant among lotuses. The brave one was as resplendent as a wild elephant killed by hunters.

  • Tell me why didn’t Sanjay say he died fairly in a one to one fight? Was he stupid or had less knowledge than you?

Pandavas — firmament. ‘With Drona and Karna at the forefront, this single one has been slain by six maharathas from the side of the sons of Dhritarashtra. It is our view that this is not dharma.’

‘Sanjaya replied, “I will tell you about that extremely terrible encounter that took place between one and many. It was a tumultuous battle. Mounted on his chariot, Abhimanyu exerted himself. All the rathas58 on your side were the scorchers of enemies and happily exerted themselves. There were Drona, Karna, Kripa, Shalya, Drona’s son, Bhoja, 59 Brihadbala, Duryodhana, Somadatta’s son, 60 the immensely strong Shakuni, many kings, many princes and many other soldiers. He roamed around like a circle of fire and pierced all of them with arrows.

  • Why do you think Sanjay says between one and many?

‘Dhritarashtra said, “O Sanjaya! He was the son of a lion among men. He had just become a youth. On hearing that he has been slain in battle, my mind is severely shattered. The dharma of kshatriyas, as laid down by those who have set the principles of dharma, is terrible. Desiring a kingdom, brave ones shower down their weapons on a child. He was a child and was reared in happiness. But he roamed around fearlessly.

  • Do you see breaking of Dharma?
  • Do you see brave ones shower down weapon on a child?
    • Why did he call him a child? Who was right — Soma or Dhrithrashtra?

Dhritrashtra said — Sanjaya! You are skilled in narrating. Tell me everything. O son! When Abhimanyu was slain and that wicked deed was done to Partha in the evening, what was the state of your minds? O son! Having performed that wicked deed, in battle, those on my side must have found it impossible to withstand the deeds of the wielder of Gandiva. What did Duryodhana do?

  • What wicked deed is Dhritrashtra talking about?

Krishna said — Hearing the appropriate, but cruel, words of Drona in the battle then, Karna severed his bow. When he was devoid of weapons in that battle, five maharathas, skilled in deceit, killed him with their showers of arrows.

  • Why didn’t Krishna mention Duhsasana’s son?

Gandhari — ‘You are Vasudeva’s sister ’s son. You are the son of the wielder of Gandiva. In the midst of the battle, how could those maharathas slay you? Shame on the perpetrators of that cruel deed—Kripa, Karna, Jayadratha, Drona and Dronayani.71 They have caused this hardship. Did all those bulls among rathas not possess hearts? They surrounded a child and killed him and brought me this sorrow. The Pandavas and the Panchalas were looking. Though he possessed protectors, how was that brave one killed, as if he had no protectors? On seeing that he was killed by many, as if he had no protector, how is the brave Pandava,72 tiger among men, still alive?

QUESTION — IS THERE A DIRECT REFERENCE TO PROVE ABHIMANYU DIED FAIRLY?

I gave you multiple references. And there are multiple references which I didn’t quote. Now. Your turn. Can you quote a single reference that explicitly suggests —

  • So one reference Abhimanyu died in a fair battle
  • So one reference Abhimanyu wasn’t killed due to exhaustion and group attack.
  • So one reference that doesn’t suggest Kauravas deeds were honorable.
  • So one reference that after Jaidradh’s interferences Abhimanyu had any support with him.

I would be interested in valid references and not hollow assumptions.

Post: https://www.quora.com/profile/Kushal-Tiwari-86/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Was-Abhimanyu-killed-unfairly-and-mercilessly-Lets-start-The-startin
Creation time: Oct 23, 2021 09:11 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for your support and kind words.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Who-is-the-most-complete-warrior-Karna-or-Arjuna-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 25, 2021 06:53 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for pointing out.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Who-is-the-most-complete-warrior-Karna-or-Arjuna-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 25, 2021 07:33 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Define practical.

Wasn’t my distinction between fighter and warrior practical? Didn’t I gave proper evidence in first section? What is your idea of being practical?

What is the practical importance of Mahabharata if not debating on its philosophy? What could be more practical if not the lesson for the future generation?

Is it degrading Mahabharata to a marathon debate supporting a villain because someone thinks he is a person one can corelate with?

Do you think it would be practical to glorify muscle over character? Do you think it is practical to cherish evils like Ravana, Hiranyakashayap, Tarkasura or Karna because they were powerful?

Would you find me more practical if I point out —

  • Karna can’t be compared to Arjuna. In fact he lived and died just for this comparison.
  • Arjuna won every single battle against Karna. Karna fled most of them.
  • Karna admitted he was scared of Arjuna and that there is no warrior who is equal to Arjuna.
  • No one except Karna ever suggested Karna by his own virtue stands a chance against Arjuna.
  • Lord Shiva himself proclaimed Arjuna the greatest warrior.

You may find these answer more practical as you may find something to debate on, but I would ask you to let that impulse pass for a moment and think on what you may deem as a mere philosophy —

There is a person who rushes to aid protects even his worst enemy and other abandons his best friend. Tell me whom would you have your side irrespective of their power? Duryodhana made a impractical and bad choice. I hope you don’t make a similar one. Tell me does it really matter who was more powerful?

There is a man who abducts one woman against her wishes and tries to burn down the other and then abuses her again. And the other rushes to protect. Tell me whom would you trust your family women with? Would it depend on who was more powerful?

There was a man who saw a Kingdom in distress and decided to plunder it. Another one saw the same Kingdom in distress and and the risk of plunder. He decided to protect it. Who was a better warrior?

One was admired by all warriors and even Gods and other censured by them. Tell me practically who was a more complete warrior?

Do you hope to disprove Lord Shiva — You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

You may blind yourself to ignore Shiva’s word. Who knows, in one of your series may even try to prove Lord Shiva a fool and prove that Karna was the greatest warrior, but the most practical question is —

What is the worth of a warrior, if he did no good to society or humanity? And how is a warrior different from a prize fighter?

You still don’t find it practical. Rethink! What is practical?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Who-is-the-most-complete-warrior-Karna-or-Arjuna-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Oct 25, 2021 11:33 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I guess you should invite me to your answer. It’s fun to see how you constantly misquote in the name of clearing the misconceptions.

But first things first, let me agree with you. Arjuna had the great Pashupata weapon of Shiva which had no equivalent. In fact it is the only weapon which has proven track record of the capability to destroy the universe at the end of the Kalpa. And with all greatness it was still called Pashupata and not Mahapashupata. See, I agreed with you.

But your answer doesn’t end with clarifying the fact that Arjuna had Pashupata and not some Mahapashupata. Had you ended your answer at that point, I may have even applauded it.

But as always, you drag Karna into every question related to Arjuna. You deviated from your own set agenda and and instead of busing a misconception, took it as an opportunity to further another misconception.

Let us start.

What do you want to prove by quoting Krishna’s statement?

He will shroud the king with sthunakarna, indrajala and pashupata and the maharatha will follow him.

Do you want to say Karna too had Pashupata? Seriously?

So please clear my misconceptions —

Q1.Was Shiva lying when he said — ‘The illustrious god said, “O Pandava! I will give you the great pashupata weapon, dear to me. You will be capable of bearing, releasing and withdrawing it. The great Indra, Yama, the king of the yakshas, 292 Varuna and Vayu do not know it. How can it be known to humans?

Do I remind you, Pashupata belongs to Shiva and even Krishna can’t grant this weapon to any one?

Q2.If Karna had Pashupata, why did he say — He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds. The various guardians of the world assembled and gave him weapons that have no measure.

Karna clearly says Arjuna have weapons that have no measure. Why didn’t he say he too had a Pashupata?

Even Arjuna mentioned the fact of course trusting Lord Shiva.

Now you may counter me by asking —

Was Krishna lying, when he mentioned Pashupata in the above statement?

Read the statement again, with emphasis on a different point — He will shroud the king with sthunakarna, indrajala and pashupata and the maharatha will follow him

Krishna says Karna will kill Yudhishtira. Do you agree with this assertion of Krishna? It is in the same sentence. That also means Karna was a liar and he was about to break his pledge of not killing Yudhishira? Or As I believe, Krishna was simply trying to portray the urgency to Arjuna.

Q3. Krishna says Karna is going to kill Yudhishtira. Do you agree?

Please don’t ignore this question.

Q4. Krishna said that once Karna’s Vasvi Shakti and armor is taken away, he is as good as he is dead. Why wasn’t Karna worried about his Pashupata?

LET ME BREAK THE MISCONCEPTION FOR YOU

  • Arjuna possessed the great and the terrible Pashupata given to him by Shiva himself. It wasn’t called MahaPashupata, but it was a weapon that had no measure.
  • Karna never possessed Pashupata of Shiva. It is confirmed by Shiva, Arjuna and even Karna himself. Even Krishna never worried about Karna having this weapon.
  • Because Karna fans started mistrusting Shiva and without understanding the context started attributing great weapons to Karna, Arjuna fan started emphasizing the difference by adding Maha (meaning great or terrible) in front of Pashupata. The term Mahapashupata was unofficially coined to stress the difference.

Keep writing. I get a chance to bust your misconception.

Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 28, 2021 09:22 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No Yudhisthira wasn’t lying. He was saying what he believed. And if you say what he think is truth, then you are not guilty of lying.

But let me clarify this confusion for you —

Yudhishthira replied, “Arjuna always said that he would burn down the enemy in a single day. Though he was proud of his valour, he wasn’t able to accomplish that.

Now let me take you to what Arjuna really said —

But I speak the truth when I say that you should not be tortured in your mind. When I have Vasudeva as an aide, on a single chariot, I can slay the three worlds in an instant, with the immortals, with their mobile and immobile objects, and with everything that is the past, the present and the future. That is my view. I possess the terrible and great weapon that Pashupati gave me when there was a duel with the hunter. 114 At the end of a yuga, that is employed by Pashupati to destroy all the beings. O tiger among men! I know how to use that. Gangeya does not know this, nor do Drona and Goutama. O king! Neither do Drona’s son and the son of the suta. One should not use such divine weapons to kill ordinary people in battle. Without resorting to deceit, we will vanquish our enemies in battle.

— Udyoga Parva

Now tell me, Is Arjuna arrogant here? Is he claiming that he would kill everyone by his own powers? No he gives credit to Shiva and Vasudeva. This is not called arrogance. This devotion.

Now read the last paragraph. So me, where did Arjuna said I will end the battle in one instance? He said one shouldn’t use such divine weapons to kill ordinary people. Was this arrogance?

Why don’t you quote the views of few more people —

  • Krishna called Arjuna Nishpaap.
  • Shiva said — You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

I don’t see Shiva and Krishna calling his arrogant. Krishna called Arjuna his inseparable part. Why do you hate Arjuna so much that you are constantly trying to discredit him and his son?

Why so much hate that you would ignore Krishna and Shiva and stick to the words of Yudhishira which has a clear contradictory reference?

I

Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 28, 2021 09:34 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Nice to see you started copying my style of answering. Let us continue.

You said — Yudhishthira, who said that, and you know Dharma can never say wrong or lie.

You didn’t read my answer properly. Go and read it again. If you believe something honestly and say, it is not a lie. Same thing happened with Yudhishthira. He thought, Arjuna said so. But I quoted you exact fact and Arjuna never said he would finish the war instantly. And he said that he would do it with the blessing of Shiva and Krishna. That’s not arrogance. It’s the misunderstanding of Yudhisthira. Yudhishira may be son of Dharma, Shiva is the creator of Dharma. Do you see the difference? I trust Shiva over Yudhisthira. What about you?

The other option you have is to provide exact reference where Arjuna said I will finish the battle in one day. Can you provide the exact reference?

You said — I can give many statements of Karna being praised by God's but when I provided those statements of Shri Krishna and others statement you just ignored it by saying what others think about him.

YOU ARE WRONG.

I always said Krishna praised Karna, but condemned him more times and in more strong terms. So I didn’t ignore Krishna, just reminded —

  • He wanted Karna dead.
  • He promised Draupadi her wives would weep.
  • He called him evil more times.

Has this happened with Arjuna also? Did Gods condemned him ever? Did Krishna condemn him? Did Shiva condemn him?

Please don’t misquote me.

You said — And yes I don't hate Arjun I am criticizing his actions just like what you do to Karna's actions if you can call Karna Coward and Distrust person and many other things then why couldn't I call him a boaster like Yudhi.

AGAIN YOU ARE WRONG.

There is nothing common between you and I. I don’t hesitate in accepting I hate that evil Karna. And I gave you irrefutable reasons in the past which you couldn’t counter. I gave you several opportunity to provide reasons why one should like Karna. And even as a great fan you couldn’t provide that too.

On the other hand you yourself said earlier you hate Arjuna and now you disown your own words. That is the diffrenece between you and I and that is the difference between Karna and Arjuna.

When I said Karna was a coward, there are many references I can offer. You can’t offer one reference other than Yudhishira’s opinon. An unsubstantiated opinion can’t become a fact.

You said — I respect Shri Krishna as well as Shiva but those statements said by them doesn't says that he isn't a boaster.

No you don’t respect Shiva or Krishna. Krishna called Arjuna nispaap (sinless). Shiva said Arjuna is one he gives respect to others. And there are many proof I can provide to prove this point.

But let me remind you what Krishna said — He who hates Arjuna, hates me!

In the very words of Krishna you hate him.

Do you want a reference?

Questions for you —

  • Give a direct reference where Arjuna said he would finish the war in an instant?
  • Who do you respect more Shiva and Krishna or Yudhishthira?
Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 29, 2021 09:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — I just wrote that statement what made those fake fanatics to create Mahapashuputa weapon.

So you know why Mahapashpata term was coined. Right? So who is fanatic here? Those who misquoted this line and tried to prove Karna had Pashupata.

Now why did you put that quote in your post? Are you going to clarify by editing your main post that Karna actually didn’t possess Shiva’s Pashpata? Tell me are you going to edit your post as you want to avoid misconception?

You said — You probably forgot that Shri Krishna don't know about Karna's vow.

Ha…Ha…Ha… So now Krishna doesn’t know about Karna vow! Because in your opinion he was an ordinary person like rest of us? Then tell me how is this ordinary person suppose to know if Karna had Pashupata? Is there any reference somewhere that Karna or anyone told Krishna that Karna had Pashupata? Arjuna clearly knew he didn’t have. Shiva clearly said he didn’t have.

My dear, Krishna was all knowing, all prevailing existence. He knew past, present and future. And he, without a shred of doubt, knew Karna won’t kill Yudhishthira, much in the same way he wasn’t worried from either Karna or any of his weapons other than Indra’s Vasvi Shakti.

But you can keep searching for obscure logic and calling your attempt as breaking misconception where you are doing the exact opposite.

Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 29, 2021 10:03 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — You just misunderstood my statement I was trying to say that these Arjun fans created Mahapashuputa to say that Karna had some other pashuputa and Arjun had Mahapashuputa which is greater than any other weapon.

Now do you disgree —

  • Arjuna alone had the great Pashupata of Shiva that had no measures?
  • Is it not clearly mentioned it is the most potent weapon, one that was repeatedly mentioned by everyone including Karna and Dhirtrashtra?

Tell me where are the fans of Mahadeva and Pashupata wrong?

Remember! Arjuna’s fan didn’t have to emphasize on the word Mahapashupata, had not some fools started to day dreaming that Karna too had a Pashupata equivalent to Shiva, even ignoring Shiva’s own words.

But let me break you bubble about what you call as misconception —

The Great Pashupata Weapon

This is what Shiva said —

‘The illustrious god said, “O Pandava! I will give you the great pashupata weapon, dear to me. You will be capable of bearing, releasing and withdrawing it. The great Indra, Yama, the king of the yakshas, 292 Varuna and Vayu do not know it. How can it be known to humans?

This is what Krishna said —

‘O Partha! There is a supreme and eternal weapon named pashupata.

This is what Arjuna said —

I possess the terrible and great weapon that Pashupati gave me when there was a duel with the hunter. 114

And This is what your Karna said, whom you trust above all —

He faced the immensely generous Mahadeva himself in a battle and satisfied him.683 He thus obtained the extremely terrible pashupata, the great weapon that can destroy the three worlds.

Summary —

  • Shiva — great pashupata weapon, dear to me, unknown to others.
  • Krishna — supreme and eternal
  • Arjuna — terrible and great
  • Karna — the great weapon, that has no measure

If you know even a bit of Hindi grammar, and I believe you know you must have read about sandhi-vicched (संधि-विच्छेद). So let me give you a test. But let me first give you a lesson —

महात्मा → महान + आत्मा

महावीर → महान + वीर

Now do you know the meaning of ‘महान’? It’s great.

Ok, enough of practice. Now your turn. Do संधि-विच्छेद for

  • महापशुपात

Don’t hesitate to ask if you fail. I will do it for you later. But if you do succeed, ask yourself another question — how is this word no in line with what Shiva, Krishna, Arjuna or Karna?

Assuming there existed other and not so great Pashupata, tell me how it is wrong to highlight the significance by reiterating the words of Shiva and Krishna?

You will say — You are holding on two individual words.

Yes, I am. words are important. And remember, all your posts zero down the postmortem of the same word. Right.

You said — According to actual Mahabharata Shri Krishna didn't knew about Karna's vow.

Please quote an exact reference to prove Krishna didn’t know about Karna’s vow. Now I know you will ask me to provide a direct reference to prove he knew. So let me do a preemptive strike. Read Gita. Krishna knew everything — what is past, present and future. And it is NOT A PHILOSPHY. He manifested his viratswaroop twice and clearly mentioned his knowledge. Here is a direct reference —

वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥ २६ ॥

—Bhagwat Gita Chapter 7, Shloka 26

But for non-believers like you, who think so little of Krishna, and assume he didn’t know about Karna’s vow, how was it possible that he knew Karna had a Pashupata? Remember Karna didn’t know about it himself. He never said so. And how is it he didn’t bother about Karna’s Pashupata and became too have after the Vasvi Shakti was gone that he announced that now Karna is as good as he is dead?

and still didn’t bother about it?

You didn’t answer —

  • How did Krishna knew that Karna had Pashupata when even Karna didn’t know? He never said and never used. No one else ever mentioned it.
  • Why was Krishna now worried about Karna having a Pashupata, when he was clear at unease with him possessing Vasvishakti?
  • Will you edit your post to remove the confusion and clarify that Karna didn’t have the Pashupata of Mahadeva, the one possessed by Arjuna?

PS: Here you can learn two new annotations I have used in your other comments —

  • You will say/ask
  • You didn’t answer
Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 29, 2021 10:18 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — You probably haven't read answers of other fanatics who mention Mahapashuputa itself as an another weapon.

Oh! But I have read many answers like that. For example, one that I am now answering. Read your answer. You mentioned multiple times that Karna had a different Pashupata than the great Pashupata of Shiva. Didn’t you?

So tell me, how do we differentiate between the great Pashupata of Shiva from the other inferior weapons in circulation? Do you have a solution?

And more important, is it really a misconception to call The Great Pashupata of Mahadeva and Maha Pashupata if there were lesser weapons available in circulation?

You said — I mentioned Gita and proved that Pashuputastra can be countered by Naraynsatra you just ignored it.

I don’t recall this incident when I rejected a reference from Bhagwat Gita. Would you be kind enough to provide me the reference once again? Share the chapter and shloka number.

You said — So now why should I count it as proper reference and although Gita is removed from Bori CE so what to do now according to BORI Your statement doesn't exist.

Are you sure Bhagwat Gita is removed from BORI CE? Which BORI CE did you read? One that is yet not published and exists in the figment of imaginations of Karna fan’s like you?

Sorry to break you bubble. Bhagwat Gita is NOT removed from BORI CE. Let me quote you from BORI CE —

I am not evident to everyone. I am not born and am without change. But the ignorant world does not know me. O Arjuna! I know all beings in the past, the present and the future. But no one knows me.

— BORI CE, Bhagwat Gita Parva ( Vol 5, English Translation)

I hope, now you have no problem in accepting my reference.

SUMMARY OF OUR DISCUSSION

  • You agree, if Karna had a Pashupata it was something different and inferior than what Arjuna had received from Pashupta.
  • Now since there are at least two different type of Pashupata, we need a way to differentiate between higher and lower Pashupata.

Final questions for you —

  1. How should you differentiate between to different Pashupata? Do you have recommendation?
  2. How calling the great Pashupata as Maha Pashupata a distortion or misconception?

Most importantly, You said — I will stop using your style if you don't want.

Oh No! I never said that. Please use this style. It makes the reading easy. And I would appreciate if I can contribute back something to the community in anyway I can.

I would have loved more if I could have convinced you of the truth as I did to so many people before you. For now that is my personal failure. But I still have hopes, more so because you are humble, an important trait for learners.

Please continue to use this style. I would be happy that I could help you in something.

Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 30, 2021 08:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Calling pashuputa as Mahapashuputa is not distortions at all but making Mahapashuputa as whole new weapon is distortions.

But who said, Mahapashupata is a new whole weapon? Did any one ever said —

  • It was a weapon greater than (or different from) one Mahadeva gave to Arjuna?
  • It was a weapon other than what Arjuna possessed and no one else possessed?

I don’t think so. If you find a reference, do share it with me, and I will along with you condemn such a reference.

You said — Pāshupatāstra was baffled by Nārāyanāstra

Yes. This is not a valid reference. It is not from Bhagwat Gita. It is from Bhagwatam, which is glorification of a single entity Krishna. Now you have known me as a writer and know by reverence for Krishna, but still —

  • Bhagwatam is not a part of Mahabharata — neither BORI nor any other recitation.
  • We often glorify one aspect of divinity by making them a bit superior to other.

That said, let me acknowledge this reference. But then I would urge you to read the full story. It was due to Shiva’s boon to Krishna and not because Naraynastra was more powerful than Pashupata or because Krishna is more powerful than him who is without any origin or end. When Shiva himself doesn’t want to win, Pashupata wont go against the wishes of his wielder. You need to have a clearer idea about the nature of Divyastras to understand them.

It would be a mistake to blindly quote one reference without understand the whole context.

Meanwhile, I would like you to read my detailed answer on Mahapashupata.

Vivek Dutta Mishra (विवेक दत्त मिश्रा)'s answer to What is difference between Pashupastra and MahaPashupastra?

Finally, if you follow me, soon, I would share a detailed view of Divyastra and you may find it useful.

Post: https://karnathemahanayaka.quora.com/https-mahabharat18-quora-com-Todays-Misconception-Did-Arjuna-had-Mahapashuputa-astra
Creation time: Oct 31, 2021 09:25 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Perhaps you didn’t read either my answer or the epics well enough. I have answered your first question directly and others indirectly. Let me try again —

You asked — Why didn’t she tell it in the Rangbhoomi itself, in front of everybody, as to clear any doubt?

  1. It was already too late. Karna had picked a side and had been involved in several conspiracies against Pandavas. She could see the incorrigible differences.
  2. Things had changed. She could see his son being crowned as a King and had a prosperous life ahead. His own sons always lived a life under the shadow of conspiracy.
  3. There was no point in divulging her secret that would still be frowned upon.

You asked — Why she didn’t handover to Surya?

She begged Surya not to put her in such a tight spot. She begged hard. But Surya didn’t budge. He was way too powerful for her. Surya could have take Karna with him. But he didn’t care.

You asked — Why he didn’t asked his father, or bother or some God or some one?

Remember the whole idea was to protect her family’s reputation. She was immature and afraid like any young bachelor women who is forced into a pregnancy. Call her immature, if you like, but you can’t call her a bad mother. She didn’t throw him in river to die. She didn’t poison him. She ensured her safety first from Surya. She knew his son reached someone where he would have no dearth of money, or education. Adhiratha was a friend of Dhrithrashtra. He got best possible education. Tell me how Vasudeva or Kunti bhoj would have arranged a better education than what Karna got?

You asked — Why didn’t she tell/explain Adhiratha and Radha, to give her back her child?

It would have been selfish. Adhiratha and Radha took care of her child at the time of need. They were good parent. She couldn’t have tortured them in this way, and not before talking to Karna. Moreover nothing had changed, till everything changed and she went rushing to Karna.

You asked — Why didn’t she pray to any god, such as Mahadev, to bring him up?

Tell me is their any reference anywhere that Mahadeva adopted someone son? Then there would be no need of orphanage.

You asked — Why didn’t she tell any other god, like Vayu, Agni, or some sage, to bring him up as a scholar, a king, or a warrior?

Why do you think she had all Gods at her call to do his bidding? Are you ralistic?

You asked — Why didn’t she tell Madhav about it? He could have tried something…

Krishna did try, you know. But as I said, Karna had gone far deep in evil conspiracies to hear the words of reasoning from Kunti, Surya or even Krishna.

You asked — Hell, why didn’t she tell the ‘wicked’, ‘jealous’ and ‘greedy’ Suyodhana about Karna’s real birth?

Because as you said he was wicked, jealous and greedy. He could have blackmailed Karna to do his dirty work by joining Pandavas and backstabbing them. Or else he could have backstabbed Karna. Either way no good was to come from that ‘wicked’, ‘jealous’, ‘greedy’ evil Duryodhana.

I think I answered all your questions. Now here are few of my questions. Hope you would also try to answer them.

  1. All of your questions show what you would have expected Kunti to do. But you were not in Kunti’s place. Why do you think your solutions were better than her own?
  2. What do you want to prove with all these questions? Was she a bad mother or a selfish person? Please answer clearly and when you do, please quote a reference to prove your point from Neelkantha and KMG that you have read.
  3. Why didn’t you question, Surya’s attitude in the whole affair? Why did he impregnator Kunti for his own ego? Why did he threaten Kunti of dire consequences? Why do you hold only woman responsible?
  4. Why didn’t you ask, why Surya didn’t take Karna with him. He knew Kunti can’t raise him. He may have taken him into his fold once he realized Kunti has abandoned him?
  5. What Kind of father was Surya? Why didn’t he raise Karna into some scholar? Why didn’t he tell Karna to put an end to his evil designs?
  6. Why didn’t Karna understand his mother’s plight and helplessness? Didn’t Krishna accepted Devaki as her mother. Didn’t she too had abandoned Krishna? Didn’t Ved Vyas accepted her mother’s command? Didn’t she too had abandoned him? Why only Karna found it difficult to forgive her mother?
  7. How did Kunti’s decision worked against Karna? Didn’t he get a good education? Didn’t he become a great King?
  8. Had Vasudeva or Kunti’s father raised Karna, what was the assurance that he wouldn’t have met Duryodhana in gurukul and wouldn’t be swayed into bad company and become an evil person?

Now I gave you just 8 questions instead of your 10. Will wait for your reply.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Kunti-have-real-love-for-Karna-or-was-it-just-a-fake-show-off-to-save-her-all-5-sons-answer-Vi
Creation time: Nov 01, 2021 11:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I agree with you partially. Karna’s armor and earrings weren’t weak. They were made up of Amrita. It wasn’t armor but Karna who turned out to be weaker.

I don’t agree with you when you say by defeat, Krishna meant killing. Because to defeat a warrior you don’t need to kill a warrior. Bhishma was removed from the battle field without killing him.

Do you know the story of Rahu? He had consumed Amrita. He couldn’t have been killed. But Vishnu beheaded him. He lived on just as a head, without a body. Would you have wanted such an immortality for Karna? Remember Kavacha didn’t protect his head.

What Krishna said is — Had that bull among men possessed those, you with your Gandiva and I with my sudarshana chakra would not have had the capacity to defeat him in a battle.

If you read carefully, it is the greatness of Kavacha. And theoretically anyone possessing such a divine artifact that completely shields from death should always be victorious. But Krishna just narrates a possibility — what may have happened rather than what actually happened. You should also read the next line of what Krishna said that would be enlightening —

It is because he sliced off the armour and the sparkling earrings and gave them to Shakra that Karna came to be known as Vaikartana. Karna is now like an angry snake, whose energy has been sapped through mantras. Therefore, Karna is now like a fire whose flames have been pacified.

WAS KARNA UNDEFEATABLE WITH KAVACHA AND KUNDALA

Neither Surya nor Krishna nor anyone else ever said that Kavacha or Kundala can make someone brave. They didn’t had the capacity to protect someone from getting afraid or preventing them from running away.

These artifacts may save your life but can’t ensure your victory.

By the way what is your view about these two statements —

‘Seeing Vaikartana Karna rush towards him, the wise Arjuna stretched his mighty bow and pierced him with three arrows. The impact of these sharp arrows stunned Radheya31

— Draupadi Swyamvar Parva

While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away.

— Ghosha Yatra Pava

How do you think Karna was sorely wounded and pierced by arrows despite of having invincible Kavacha? I could see only two explanations —

First, Karna could sustain arrow injuries despite having the Kavacha irrespective of whatever Surya say is theory.

Or second, Karna got terrified of minor scratches and considered them as grave wounds, found excuse and ran away. His fear made him think his wounds were grave.

What is your view?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karnas-Armor-and-Earrings-were-weak
Creation time: Nov 01, 2021 11:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — I always looked at you as an learned man, I don't doubt you but

Anything you say before but is meaningless. If you change your views one every sentence, you wont go far. Don’t make anyone great without evaluating him first. You made such a hasty mistake with Karna. Don’t do it again. First evaluate me. And then decide if you think, I am learned or not. There is no hurry.

You said — Why do you want to state things which nor me neither anyone else has mentioned. I haven't claimed him to be an undefeatable warrior,

It’s nice we both agree, Karna wasn’t undefeatable. Unfortnately not many people realize it. Many are fooled by half understanding of Krishna’s statement into believing Krishna called him undefeatable. In fact you too mention by defeat Krishna meant death. It wasn’t true.

You said — There is no shame loosing. But yes bouncing back is the thing which matters.

Agree. Again. There is no shame in loosing. I am an admirer of people like Maharana Pratap and Chandrashekar Azad. They didn’t succeed in their honorable goals and there are fewer people whom I find more honorable.

But there is same in running away. Again and again and again. There is a shame in deserting your friends and family and women folks. And that is not bouncing back. That is bouncing away.

You said — I think you clearly know what is the exact meaning of Armour and you may also know how Armour looks like. Armour doesn't covers every part of our body, it only covers chest, stomach and some shoulder area and remaining hand, neck and other organs are disclosed so those wounds would be at his legs or hands

Oh! I know what armor is. I wanted to hear from you. I wanted you to realize that armor wouldn’t have protected Karna from losing his limbs or getting beheaded, the exact way he died. That makes armors valuable and yet not a complete solution.

And yes, I wanted to quote the Rahu’s story myself. Karna could have met the safe fate. As you know armor does’t protect your throat. Arjuna beheaded Karna. How was armor going to work? The Kundala and its amrita would keep him alive — only the head not the body.

Now that you yourself so well explained the role or armor, I am sure you would realize there was a great probability of what I said. Isn’t that?

You said — Karna was never a weak person he defeated everyone on single chariot in his digvijay.

Really! Which brave persons runs away deserting his best friend behind? Which brave person runs away seeing his brothers killed? He defeated many Kings. He did his expedition knowing the absence of Pandavas. When one Pandava came out of a single chariot, he ran away leaving his brother to die. Remember an earlier digvijay where Bhima defeated Karna. Please don’t tell He was different Karna.

You said — He also spared life of four Pandavas

Thanks to thirty one Kauravas who died to protect his life. Else he might have been dead long before he could have spared anyone’s life.

You said — He also almost beheaded Arjuna with his special Nag Arrow.

Speechless, that you glorify the most hideous act of that coward. Do you know what was Naag arrow? It was a an evil snake sitting on an arrow. It was a joint conspiracy of two snakes — one in human form, who had worshipped the other for many years. There was nothing heroic about it. It was one of the most shameful chapter in the life your spineless archer. And yet sometimes ago were telling he was not a weak person. Tell me what was Karna without armor or vasvi shakti? What was his personal glory?

You didn’t give your view about — While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away.

I understand armor doesn’t protect entire body. But by any chance he mean to say he got in his own opinion sorely wounded just by arrows on his legs and hands with most vital parts protected? How do you expect such warriors to win a battle when he gets sorely wounded despite of having a divine armor?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karnas-Armor-and-Earrings-were-weak
Creation time: Nov 03, 2021 11:14 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/चलो-दिया-जलाएं-दीपावली-की-शुभकामनाएं
Creation time: Nov 04, 2021 10:46 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You asked — First you tell me what was Arjuna without Gandiva and Shri Krishna.

Glad you asked. And I will be happy to answer your curiosity. Here is what Arjuna was with Gandiva or Shri Krishna.

  • A warrior whose opponent compared him to great Vishnu, Indra and Parshurama, the personification of the knowledge of weapon. Arjuna had no Gandiva or Krishna with him then.
  • He was the only compassionate warrior to have questioned the need of war and was ready to give up his Gandiva and right for others.
  • He was a great human being who would move forward to face a group of ruthless Kings who were trying to burn down a bride and her family.
  • A warrior so powerful that the King whom he defeated wished him to be his son in law.
  • A victor who would not abusive a defeated or a retreating opponents like Chitrasena of Bhagdatta. No Gandiva is needed to be modest in victory.
  • A warrior who could stand even for his worst enemy. You again don’t need Gandiva to standup.
  • He is known as the reincarnation of ancient sage Nara.

And above all

  • A warrior brave enough to face Shiva and not retreat. A warrior whom Shiva himself graced.
  • A person great enough that Krishna should call him his inseparable existence.

Now which of these did you miss? Now tell me this —

Question for you — Is there a single reference in Mahabharata which says —

  • Arjuna was great only because he had Gandiva?
  • He would be good as dead without Gandiva?

In case you fail to provide the answer, you would have your answer.

You said — I think you watch T.V serials too much…

This is not an answer to my question. But to answer yours. Yes, I do watch TV shows. But I watch to see distortions that I can warn others. Had I trusted them I may have thought —
