  • Karna was drunk during Gandharva battle
  • Or Gandharva battle never happened.

But this remark is not an answer to my question.

You said — Can you please requote these lines again because it's hard for me to understand it.

Sure. Let me break it in points.

  1. You said armour couldn’t have protected his entire body.
  2. There is clear proof that attack could have sorely wounded Karna.
  3. Since the throat is not protected Arjuna could have beheaded Karna even with he head on. He could have severed his limbs.
  4. Because of amrita in kundala his head may have lived on like that of Rahu.
  5. Karna would have a bodiless existence.

Is it now clear?

You asked — Was he really willing to run away? he was simply outnumbered.

Does it matter? As I told you earlier, everyone seems brave when there is no danger around. But true strength of character is seen during adversity. Karna couldn’t have won the war. But he could have shared his friend’s misfortune. But he chose to run. Duryodhana wasn’t as powerful, but he chose not to run. Mark the word — he chose not to run.

And that was not the first time Karna chose to run away. Nor was it the last time. He ran away scared after Draupadi’s swyamvar. He also run away in Matasya leaving his brother to die. He ran away several times from Abhimanyu, again leaving his other brother to die. Was he overwhelmed in these cases as well?

No. He simply had a tendency to run away. Here is what Drona said — ‘It is exactly as you have said and there is no falsehood in this at all. In battle after battle, I have seen him retreat. Karna is generous. But he is also distracted. It is my view that he is half a ratha.’

You said — he didn't used any divyastra which shows his capability of not relying on Divyastras.

No only capability he showed, was that of running away. period. As far as not relying to Divyastra, unlike his role model, he used every possible divyastra in his arsenal during Kurkushetra battle — Vasvishakti, Bhargavastra, Varunastra, and Brahmastra. He even used a serpent in a battle against a human. That was his capability.

You said — That incident itself is an interpolation with no specific logic… doesn’t that sound stupid.

What sounds stupid, is calling every incident that you don’t agree as interpolation. The incident is clearly stated in BORI after forty years of research. So, no, it is not an interpolation. I wonder, why only Karna fans find every incident as interpolation? Are you really that terrified of seeing the original face of Karna that you would rather close your eyes and say interpolation… interpolation? And based on what? Some wild hypothesis, without any reference? Let us see that as well.

You asked — Anga was a part of Kauravas empire then why anyone would attack Karna's state when that area belong to Kauravas only.

This is a myth spread by some Karna fans. There is not a single reference to prove it. Can you prove it? Do you mean Duryodhana didn’t make Karna a true king, rather made him his regent? Then we shouldn’t call him Angaraj. Right?

Kaurava’s were not emperors. They ruled Hastinapur. Anga may have been a friendly kingdom but not under them.

But most important, the incident happened. So instead of calling it stupid, look at the possible reasons. Duryodhana may not have wanted the yagna to be success and couldn’t have fought against Pandavas themselves so he asked Karna to? Do I have a proof. No. I don’t need one. The battle between Karna and Bhima is well documented.

Now is it not stupid to call it interpolation without any reference and just on your own conjuncture?

You said — Thanks to Karna for not killing Yudhishthira, Bhima and twins and also Arjuna when he was unconscious on 17th day.

Duryodhana saved Karna’s life by sacrificing his 31 brothers on day 14 — three days before Karna had a chance to reach Yudhisthira. And in sparing Yudhishtira, he favored no one other than himself. It was his own pledge, no one asked for.

You said — It would have been better if you wouldn't have written those words.

Is it that painful, to relive your hero's, villainy? How would you clear my misconceptions?

You said — First of all it wasn't any type of joint conspiracy Karna was unaware that Ashwasena was there on his arrow. (Read Mahabharat )

Ok Let me read Mahabharata —

Karna affixed an extremely sharp and flaming arrow that was capable of slaying the enemy. This had a serpent in its mouth.786 That terrible arrow had been carefully preserved and washed well, protected well for Partha’s destruction. It had been worshipped and laid down on a bed of sandalwood paste. That immensely virulent weapon was lying down in a golden quiver. It was generated from the lineage of Airavata787 and flamed. Wishing to kill Phalguna in the battle, he aimed at his head.

Having said this, he released that arrow, the serpent which he had worshipped for many years. He said, ‘O Phalguna! You have been slain,’ and swiftly shot the arrow. On seeing that Karna had affixed the serpent, Madhava, supreme among strong ones, used his strength to press down on the chariot with his feet. The chariot sank down on the ground and the horses sank down on their knees.

Note:

  • Karna had worshipped that serpent for years.
  • Krishna saw him affix it on his arrow
  • In case you are wondering. Airavata is a snake lineage in which Ashwasena was born

But this is no the only reference. There are more. Earlier also Karna had tried a similar trick —

Wishing to slay Achyuta, he next shot five arrows that were like flaming serpents. They were decorated with gold and were aimed well. They pierced Purushottama’s armour with great force and passing through, entered the earth. Having bathed there, they returned to Karna. Quickly, with well-aimed and broad-headed arrows, Dhananjaya sliced each of them into three fragments and they fell down on the ground. They were mighty serpents that were on the side of Takshaka’s son.782

Note:

  • Karna used not only Ashwasena but also five other sons of Takshaka

But that is not all, Karna himself boasted of its existence to Shalya earlier —

I possess this arrow. It is well tufted and will drink blood. It has been washed excellently in oil and has been decorated well. It is lying alone in this quiver. It is lying down in sandalwood powder and has been worshipped by me for many years. This is extremely poisonous and is a serpent.379 It is capable of slaying large numbers of men, horses and elephants. It is powerful and extremely terrible and is capable of shattering body armour and bones. In my wrath, I can use this to shatter the giant Mount Meru. Listen to me. I am saying this truthfully. I will never release this at anyone other than Phalguna and Krishna, the son of Devaki. O Shalya! I will use this arrow against Vasudeva and Dhananjaya. I will be extremely angry and fight with them. That will be an act that is worthy of me.

Note:

  • Karna knew about this arrow.
  • He worshipped the snake
  • He preserved it for Arjuna
  • He thought such an act is worthy of him.

And not only Karna, even Bhishma knew about it’s existence —

“The great-souled, illustrious and great Indra, the lord of the thirty gods, bestowed a spear on you. In battle, you will see it shattered and reduced to ashes, struck down by Keshava’s chakra. There is a radiant arrow with the mouth of a serpent.145 You have always honored and protected it with the best of garlands. It will be struck by the arrows of Pandu’s sons. O Karna! Together with you, it will be destroyed.

So, now you see, I did read Mahabharata. And look what did I find?

But like always, let me humor you. Let us for the time being say (not believe) Karna didn’t know Ashwasena’s presence on the arrow, doesn’t still still raises these questions —

  • What kind of stupid warrior won’t know what arrow is he firing? Was Karna that stupid not to realize presence of a snake on his arrow?
  • Since when ignorance became proof of innocence. If Karna fired Ashwasena knowing or unknowing, he was still responsible.
  • But that also means the credit of killing Arjuna, if it were a success would have gone to Ashwasena and not Karna as he wasn’t even aware of it. Right? Krishna protected Arjuna from great Ashwasena and not a stupid Karna who couldn’t even see a snake on his arrow? Right?
  • Shouldn’t Karna and his fans be thankful to Krishna for saving Karna? Yes, It wasn’t Arjuna but Karna whom Krishna saved from the most sinful act committed by Karna?

You said — Ashwasen wasn't evil he was taking revenge from a man who killed his Mother and his family mercilessly.

Ashwasena was a snake used in human war. Weather Ashwasena was evil or not, using a snake in human battle was truly evil.

But if you read Mahabharata, Khandava was an abode of enemies of Gods. And Arjuna’s act was an act of war against evil. Krishna himself sided with him. But for you Krishna’s presence was no guarantee of Dharma. Right?

But let me tell you this — Everytime Khandava is referred in Mahabharata, it is referred as one of the greatest heroics. Not once it has been referred as merciless. Can you quote me a single reference to proof, Arjuna’s act was deemed as merciless or evil?

But tell me, do you vindicate

You asked — Yes and why would it be heroic for you because your great hero was almost going to live a life without head by Radheya.

No Arjuna wouldn’t have lived without head. He had no free gift of Amrita by her mother. He would have died. His death would be that of a Valliant warrior brought down by a wicked man with the help of a serpent! He would still have been honorable in his death. He wouldn’t have begged anyone for his life. But you know even God protect such precious life. And wishful thinkers keep regretting why a hero didn’t die at the hand of evil.

You said — Now I am telling you what was Karna without Kavacha and Shakti.

He was Commander in chief of Kauravas.

Third commander. And he remained for a period of two days. During this time he misused Bhargavastra, Brahmastra, Varunastra, conspired with a snake to kill Arjuna. Then begged Arjuna to not kill him with a lame excuse of chariot stuck in ground. Naturally he hadn’t seen a warrior fight without a chariot. Right?

He spared life of Yudhishira.

To fulfil his own pledge, that no one asked and in doing so, committed a treason against his benefactor and friend, with whom you never shared his secret. Why do you think, he didn’t share his secret with Duryodhana.

He overpowered Dhananjaya thanks to Shri Krishna for saving him. (And what's better than overpowering the one who overpower others)

When did he overpower Arjuna? Can you share a reference? Do you know when a bowler baffles a batsman with a bouncer, that doesn’t mean he bowled out the batsman or became better than him. Especially if that batsman has a record of knocking out every bowl of that batsman. Do you know who is the bowler and batsman in this story.

Doesn’t mean you shouldn’t share the reference where Karna overpowered Arjuna.

Just to be clear, in your opinion Karna’s greatness was —

  • He became a commander of an army after Bhishma and Drona were dead.
  • He spared the life of Yudhishira.
  • He baffled Arjuna at some point, not defeated him, not made him retreat, just baffled him.

But I like your reverence for Arjuna — Overpowering him, he overpowers others. Yes that would be an achievement and a lifetime dream coming true, if it were true.

I never counted Arjuna vanquishing Karna multiple times as one of his great achievements. Karna was no one compared to Arjuna and defeating him was no great act of heroic.

I guess that is the difference between Arjuna and Karna.

May I offer you a friendly advise — Please clear your misconceptions before trying to clear it for others —

  • You don’t bother to read epic and conclude Gita doesn’t exist in BORI CE, simply because someone on Quora said so!
  • You don’t know number of references that exists to prove Karna’s conspiracy with Ashwasena and claim that others don’t know Mahabharata?

Why not give sometime to reading the original epic. That may change your perception. Or are you exactly afraid of this possibility? I see a great potential in you. I see subtle changes. There is no shame in it.

To tell you the truth — I grew reading Rashmirathi by Ramdhari Singh Dinkar. I too felt excited at Karna pushing the chariot of Krishna by few steps, too liked the story of Karna donating his tooth. But then we both now know, what they were — made up stories. There is no real shame in growing up. As you said, there is no shame in losing. The shame is not in realizing your mistake and not bouncing back.

I told you about my mistake of trusting Karna many many years ago, in the hope that you too get the courage to do the right thing.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karnas-Armor-and-Earrings-were-weak
Creation time: Nov 05, 2021 01:00 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks. Wait for a long discussion on Divyastra tomorrow.

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/शक्ति-कविता
Creation time: Nov 05, 2021 09:04 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Yes, we have seen that on 17th day when this Arjuna tried to Kill Dharmaraj for a secret Vow.

Great at least you agreed with other points about what made Arjuna great.

Now coming to the 17th day of the war, did you really see? He didn’t try to kill. He said he will kill. He got angry because of Yudhishitra’s allegations on him.

But didn’t you see the main difference here? Arjuna followed Krishna’s advise. For him Dhrama was above his own oath, something in complete reverse with Karna. Krishna could talk out Arjuna from committing a mistake, he couldn’t do that with Karna.

Now your answer clearly show, you hate Arjuna. Why?

You said — So much sorrowness for Karna he retreated because he thought that Brahman was invincible and in past also he gained curse because of Brahmanas only so why repeat an mistake again.

Please provide a direct reference. I can provide you a clear reference that he retreated scared. The Brahman that was invincible was Arjuna.

That he won’t repeat mistake again is your presumptions which is baseless. Karna fought Arjuna knowing he was a Brahmana. He fought vigorously wishing to vanquish that Brahmana. So your assumptions fails against the real hard evidence.

Don’t trust everything you read on social media read the epic or follow exact reference here —The First Arjuna-Karna Battle — Draupadi’s Swyamvara Part 2

I have given point wise and reference wise.

You said — And although warriors clapped for Karna not for Arjuna.

In a cricket match I always applaud for Indian cricket time even if they lose. I never applauded for Pakistan. Do you?

Read my articles above and try to find out what great feat Karna had performed when the applauded. He didn’t hit Arjuna. He didn’t made pierced him. He didn’t make him circumspect.

It was Karna who saw Indra, Vishnu and Parshurama in Arjuna. Now do you want to say Karna was as stupid as his fans to compare a losing warrior to the greatest ones in the world?

You said — Ohh so that same Drona said that Arjuna was coward

You don’t have a habbit of reading the full reference. Read what Drona said on 11th day, 13th day, 14th day. Read why he couldn’t form chakravyuha in the face of Arjuna.

In this context he was trying to stop Satyaki from crossing him. He was trying to provoke him and engage him to waste precious time. He got his response from Bhima.

Now, A word of advice — Don’t just blindly trust words. Weight them against actions and give more value to actions than words —

  • What is more important, what Arjuna said to Yudhisthira or what he actually did?
  • What Drona said vs what really happened? It was Drona who refrained from forming charkravyuh. It was Drona who failed to protect Jaidradh.

In case of Karna, I gave you his words to remind that not only I but people of that era to knew Karna’s tendency to flee. I just didn’t give you hollow words, but many evidence substantiated by those words. Do you understand the difference.

You said — I can't understand your hatred towards snakes. If one can use animals to Kill Drona then why other can't.

I am surprised you don’t know that horses and elephants have been used in wars as respectable resources from ancient times. Can you say the same for snakes?

The problem is not just snakes but the misuse. It wasn’t a divine weapon.

But let me tell you another difference between Karna and Arjuna and between you and I.

I condemn Drona’s death. Arjuna condemned Drona’s execution. Arjuna didn’t participate in it. He rushed to protect him. Did Karna protect Abhimanyu?

You said — Ohh so before making assumptions do you have any reference that Anga wasn't a part of Hastinpur.

It was till the time Duryodhana gifted Karna the Kingom. Duryodhana says he make Karna the King of Anga. He doesn’t say a regent of Anga. He didn’t say Anga will remain part of Hastinapur and Karna would be a dummy King. I can quote Duryodhana made him King. Can you quote he made him a dummy?

And I have proof. It is clearly written Bhima defeated Karna during the Rajsuy yagna. In case you forgot, you had to provide a reference to disprove this statement which has a clear reference.

Don’t waste our time in asking for reference that exists and which you want to prove doesn’t exist. Give concrete reference to contradict it.

You said — So Dwarka was also an friendly kingdom so Pandavas attack Dwarka?

No they didn’t attack Dwarka. Is there a reference given to that effect? NO

Is there a direct reference that Bhima defeated Karna? YES.

You said — If Karna would try to stop Pandavas then wouldn't Bhishma question Karna wouldn't he should have questioned Duryodhana?

NO. This is in fact a proof that Anga was an independent Kingdom and Bhishma had no jurisdiction. And I don’t see Bhishma asking many questions.

You said — It's totally an nonsense incident. And you also don't have any proof that Anga wasn't part of Kauravas.

It is your perception. But forty years of research didn’t rule out nonsense. May I recommend you to work on your senses?

Let me repeat Duryodhana made Karna a King. I have references. And Kings are independent rulers. He didn’t made him a regent. I have proof — Duryodhana’s statement. No your turn to give a prove Duryodhana was wrong.

Do you want to say Bhima was right, when he said Karna doesn’t deserve to be a throne? Is that why Duryodhana kept him under his foot?

Doesn’t matter, Bhima did defeat him in Rajsuy yagna and there is a clear reference for it.

You said — Ohh now saying truth becomes Boastful.

I take my words back. It was not boastful. It was just the truth. Part of me still considered him human — evil but human.

And thanks for agreeing with the truth —

  • Karna knew about Ashwasena.
  • Using a poisonous snake in a battle was worthy of him.

You said — Ohhh really, just see your hatred towards Karna how distorting man you have become. Ashwasena was killed by Arjun so rather than calling the great Ashwasena on normal arrow it should be Great arrow with normal Ashwasena it wasn't any snake that made it special it was Special arrow that made Ashwasen Special. It was blazing arrow just read the description by Sanjaya about that Arrow

Oh! I hate Karna. He was evil. An I never ever tried to cover that face.

I distorted nothing. I proved with several references that “the great arrow” was Ashwasena that Karna had worshipped for years. I already gave you several references.

And since you have agreed to that, the matter ends there. Your attempt to glorify hideous act of Karna failed.

The answer you are refering was to bust your assumptions and not part of my belief system which is firmly rooted in the epic. You didn’t quote one part of my answer that would have clarified the matter —

I mentioned the above part to convience you that if you think Karna didn’t know about presence of Ashwasena on his arrow, he must have been a stupid warrior. But this conclusion comes from your assumption not on the fact — But like always, let me humor you. Let us for the time being say (not believe) Karna didn’t know Ashwasena’s presence on the arrow

Question for you — Now tell me honestly, did I distort anything here?

You said — Whatever you say but as per Ashwasena it was unfair and unjustified. It's like you killed someone's father and mother just to kill evils.

I would say this — casualty in an war is not considered as killing. By no court of law. If we follow your thought, all army officials should be tried in court as a petty criminal. And Indian judges should be hanged for awarding death penalty to Kasab. It may be you wishful thinking, it is not the epic perception.

You didn’t answer my earlier question — Can you provide a single reference from epic (not your perception) that condones Arjuna’s burning of Khandava or any associated actions? Everyone talked about it in superlative terms glorifying Arjuna’s and Krishna’s action.

Question for you — Why do you hate Arjuna so much that it reflects in every of your answer? Do you have courage to admit it openly?

You said — Yes he was the best commander in chief of Kauravas with least number of resources still performed great feats.

Please provide the references of those great feats —

  • How much losses did Pandava army suffered during the tenure of each of the commanders?
  • How many great warriors died in the tenure of each of the commanders.
  • What were the great feats of Karna as the commander?

You said — He spared life of his Brother for Dharma.

Please explain and justify.

You said — The first victory of Karna over Shri Krishna and Arjuna was with Vasvi Shakti. That single astra made them avoid Karna till 14th day.

Even if I had to accept this, it would be the victory of Vasvi Shakti and not Karna. You don’t become brave because you have a great weapon. There is a difference in having power and being brave. But a greater difference in being powerful on someone else’s energy that you didn’t earn rightfully but by trading of another freebee you had.

But more important, you need to get your facts straight. Arjuna did fight Karna on day 11 and ay 14. He defeated him on both the occasions, so there is no question of avoiding.

You said — Th second victory was with Bhargavastra

Again wrong! Bhargavastra wasn’t fired on Arjuna. And Krishna couldn’t care less for Bhargavastra. He didn’t consider it worth fighting. And yes, read the epic to know I am telling the truth.

Bhargavastra couldn’t kill one Maharatha of repute. So this is no win. At least Mahabahrata never called in a win.

But tell me if Karna won, why didn’t Kaurava won the battle that moment? Why didn’t Pandava’s army got completely routed when no one bothered to counter the weapon?

If you don’t have the answer, check out my podcast on that issue that is releasing tomorrow.

You said — The third victory was of Special arrow with Ashwasena.( He Almost ended war with behading Arjuna, Thanks to Shri Krishna)

You have a strange notion of victory. You cherish what may have happened rather than what did happen. Forget morality that you are not going to understand. How did you miss that the arrow failed and was completed destroyed? One arrow don’t define victory or defeat. And one arrow had to chose victory or defeat, it was Karna’s failure.

Do you know what victory and defeat looks like?

  • Victory is moving forward to protect someone in distress and actually protecting them.
  • Losing is losing all your hope of victory and running away so scared that capture and killing of your loved ones too can’t energies you to fight.
  • Victory is being so caring as to get of your horses to give them rest and still defeat your opponent from the ground.
  • Losing is begging your opponent not to kill you because your chariot is stuck on the ground, too afraid to fight a one to one battle.
  • Victory is announcing that you would kill your opponents son in front of him in advance and fulfilling your promise. Defeat is trying to kill your enemy in a cowardly fashion with the help of other like minded cowards.
  • Victory is dying in a just cause, defeat is killing a just enemy by deceit.

You said — it shows your sorrowness and misery.

You are right. I feel sorry and miserable. When people like you close your eyes and start to support an evil man so defiantly that even you can’t justify why you support him other than saying — he is more real an co-relatable. I think you remember you last answer. Right?

You couldn’t point out —

  • Anytime that evil man tried to protect someone beyond his own selfish friend circle?
  • Any time he protected a kingdom/ a village / a woman from heinous criminals / demons/ abusers/ looters?
  • Why you would honor that treacherous soul who would ditch even his best friend for his self preservation and ego?

S U M M A R Y

WE BOTH AGREED

  • Karna worshipped Ashwasena and tried to kill Arjuna with the help Ashwasena.
  • Karna retreated scared from Draupadi’s swaymver because he thought Brahmana was invincible.

THE PROVES ABOUT KARNA BEING KING OF ANGA

  • Direct reference in Adi Parva, Duryodhana made him King of Anga. No reference available that Anga was a subbordinate Kingdom and not an independent state.
  • Direct reference given that Bhima defeated Karna, the king of Anga. This incident is included in BORI and is not marked as an interpolation. BORI has over 40 years of research. There is no reference to contradict this fact other than suppositions by Karna fans.

The two references act as cross reference for each other.

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR YOU

  1. Give any reference that condemns Arjuna’s act of burning of Khadnava?
  2. Please clarify, why do you hate Arjuna?
  3. Do you have any direct reference that Bhima didn’t defeat Karna in Rajsuy Yagna?
  4. Please clarify how Karna spared Yudhishthira for Dharma?

P.S. I am glad, you are open to change. Light comes to those who seeks for it.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Kunti-have-real-love-for-Karna-or-was-it-just-a-fake-show-off-to-save-her-all-5-sons-answer-Vi
Creation time: Nov 06, 2021 11:07 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for sharing my answer and great it gave you a good laugh. Laugh is healthy. Frown isn’t so much.

I wish you had read a bit of Mahabharata before starting a crusade of personal abuse —

Shiva — You are the one who shows respect. There is no one on earth who is your equal. Nor is there anyone in heaven. O conqueror of enemies! The kshatriyas find their chief in you.”

If this is not admiration, I don’t know what is. He granted Arjuna a weapon that in his own words was known to none.

As for Krishna, he sent a message even for you —

My wives, my friends, my relatives and my kin are not dearer to me than Arjuna, Kunti’s son, is.

Tomorrow, the gods, the gandharvas, the pishachas, the serpents, the rakshasas and all the worlds will know me as Savyasachi’s well-wisher. He who hates him, hates me too. He who follows him, follows me too. Use your intelligence to comprehend that Arjuna is half of my body.

Now please go and laugh. But remember Krishna’s words — He who hates Arjuna, hates him.

Post: https://neil.quora.com/According-to-this-stupid-Arjuna-fan-Mahadeva-and-Lord-Krishna-admired-Arjuna-https-www-quora-com-Who-ha
Creation time: Nov 07, 2021 11:43 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I agree.

But Krishna doesn’t say everything is me. He says All are equal before me. But then he also says no one is dearer to me than Arjuna.

As I see, God isn’t partial, we are. He doesn’t chose us. We choose him. The onus is on us and not Gods.

A great symbolism exist in Mahabharata. Krishna lets Kaurava and Pandavas chose between him and the material wealth (large army). Both got what they desired. Duryodhana was pleased to receive army, he would have anyway chosen. It is not Krishna but Duryodhna abandoned Krishna.

Same goes true for Karna. Krishna himself offered Karna to be on his side. But he abandoned Krishna to be on the side of Duryodhana knowing well that because of them a great destruction would follow, knowing well Krishna was divine, and knowing well he would be destroyed.

I wonder, how can we blame Krishna for partiality?

Post: https://neil.quora.com/According-to-this-stupid-Arjuna-fan-Mahadeva-and-Lord-Krishna-admired-Arjuna-https-www-quora-com-Who-ha
Creation time: Nov 09, 2021 07:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I have seen such transformations a lot. Long long ago (few decades), I too had admired Karna. I was a big Rashmirathi fan. Though I won’t call myself a crazy Karna fan.

I tried to share my experience on the forum with a number of people in the hope they realize there is no shame in recognizing the error and do a course correction. Few learnt. Many remained lost.

We are humans, not stones that we can’t change.

Welcome to the group of evolved.

Post: https://neil.quora.com/According-to-this-stupid-Arjuna-fan-Mahadeva-and-Lord-Krishna-admired-Arjuna-https-www-quora-com-Who-ha
Creation time: Nov 10, 2021 09:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

पापी कौन? मनुष्य से उसका न्याय चुराने वाला?

अथवा न्याय खोजता, विघ्न का शीश उड़ाने वाला?

Who is the sinner — one who mocks the sense of justice, or one who in pursuit of justice destroys them?

You asked many questions. But do you have an exact reference for your claims?

Can you give me a direct reference where —

  • Arjuna stated that no one will be closer to him?
  • Bhima who will always find happiness on others finding?
    • Although I don’t even understand what does this line mean.

Now tell me —

A judge sentences someone for capital punishment. The condemned is hanged, without giving him an opportunity to fight back. His hands are tied. He is not given a chance to fight and kill policemen who are his captors. Do you think it is Adharma? Does that make judge and executioners criminals?

Why do you say Krishna was the reason, bhima was able to destroy Duryodhana?

Did Krishna help Bhima kill Duryodhana? Did he use is Sudarshana Chakra? How did Krishna help him?

Content:

Agree with you.

On more than one occasion, Karna was badly injured despite of having his armor and fled the battle.

Karna’s life is a wonderful example that divine artifacts may make you strong, but not brave. It may extend protection, but can’t make you brave, can’t stop you from fleeing the battle.

Unfortunately not many have time to read the epic. It is far easier to trust those TVShows with great HD graphics as the gospel of truth than to take the pain in reading. It is easier to think they are just stories and different people may believe whatever they like.
