Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-How-could-Arjun-defeat-Karna-when-he-has-divine-armours-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 07, 2021 04:36 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I think you see too much TV and read too little.

If you read the authentic epic, you would fine vow or not Karna had refused to give away his armor unless Indra offered him his Vasvi shakti. Indra didn’t give him vasvi shakti for his principles but because Karna decide to exchange it.

And yes, Indra did came to ask Karna for his Vasvi shakti. Afterall no other warrior had a divine help like that for free. Indra made the ground equal.

And NO ONE EVER SAID KARNA WAS THE GREATEST WARRIOR OF THE ERA.

On the other hand everyone including Karna, Bhishma, Drona, Parshurama, Indra, Krishna and even God of the Gods Mahadeva Shiva proclaimed Arjuna to be the greatest warrior of the era. Arjuna defeated Karna in every single encounter that happened between them.

Karna was defeated not only by Arjuna but also by Bhima, Abhimanyu, Satyaki and Gandharvas.

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-How-could-Arjun-defeat-Karna-when-he-has-divine-armours-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 17, 2021 04:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Finally this answer of yours proves that you accepted the fact that Draupadi never insulted Karna. Because if he never participated he couldn’t have been insulted. And thus all you older debate of Karna’s molestations of Draupadi was on his own devil nature and not some sort of retribution.

That said, like always your answer is devoid of logical references and just lame attempt at distortion of epic in favor of that evil creature — It is unfortunate and treacherous that you destort the whole epic to prove villains right.

You said — He translated the word यत्कर्ण wrongly it's original meaning is to perform the task if you are knowledgeable in Sanskrit you will automatically ge

I had earlier clarified this doubt of yours. My no obscure translation — यत्कर्ण means “perform the task” “कर्ण” doesn’t translated to “task”. And don’t just google everything. Learn basic sanskrit words. Show me one reputed Sanskrit dictionary where “कर्ण” translates to “task”. Also note in your own screen shot “यत” and “कर्ण” are two distinct words. Karna is a person like like Shalya.

And while Bibek Debroy had often blundered in translation, this is not one of the case. Let me give you a couple of proofs. The same shloka also exists in Neelkantha commentary which is translated by the Gita Press and here they explicitly mentioned —

This translation is done by Sanskrit scholars and can’t be doubted by coining illogical translations and distortions that has become the common trend these days to vindicate an evil creature.

But this is not the only shloka. There is another and explicit cross-reference. Here it is —

You said — Many people don't know that Duryodhana has two brothers named as Karna and this one is probably either of them. There is no probabilty of anyone else participating in swaymvar —

  1. Dhristduymna explicitly mentioned all Kauravas who participated in swaymver. There was not mention of any other Karna.
  2. The Karna who failed was a famous warrior, famous enough to be explicitly named. Do you know any other Karna in mahabharata who was a famous warrior?
  3. The word “पार्थिव” doesn’t translate only to Kshatriya but also as King. Unlike you, I will give you proper references —

You said — because Karna was never a kshatriya, he was called sun of suta throughout his life.

To prove the point let me take you to another shloka —

इदं धनुर्लक्ष्यमिमे च बाणाः; शृण्वन्तु मे पार्थिवाः सर्व एव |
यन्त्रच्छिद्रेणाभ्यतिक्रम्य लक्ष्यं; समर्पयध्वं खगमैर्दशार्धैः ||३४||

—Shloka 34 of chapter 176, Adi Parva (BORI)

O assembled kings! Hear that this is the bow, that is the target and these are the arrows. With these five arrows, shoot the target through the hole in the centre of the machine.

You can see here the पार्थिवाः is translated as King not Kshatriya.

This is a very lame excuse. But I must tell you it is not your lamest excuse. The award for lamest excuse would go to —

You said — Karna went there with Duryodhana to give him company

Is there any explicit reference that Karna had not gone there to participate? Is it mentioned. Who said some one who had gone accompanied can’t be a participant? By the way a scholar of much greater repute than you, prof Suthankar clearly mentions Karna was an invited guest.

Even this point is another great contender for lame excuse.

You said — Dristdyumn mentioned the one who are born in noble family can participate in Swayamwara

Perhaps you don’t realize that what Dhristdyumna said was just his wish for his sister and not a term for swaymvar. Read his statement again — I truly say that the handsome and strong one, born in a noble family, who accomplishes this great feat today, will obtain as his wife my sister Krishna.

Now answer these quesitons —

  • Does that mean people who were not handsome were barred from the swaymvar? Who was judging which of them were handsome?
  • Why was Arjuna disguised as a beggar allowed in the swaymvar? Did he look like a noble born?

Let me bust the myth for you. Nobility of being Kshatriya was no criteria. While Arjuna’s participation is a comprehensive proof, there are two more proofs —

पाञ्चालराजस्तु विषण्णरूप; स्तान्पाण्डवानप्रतिविन्दमानः |
धृष्टद्युम्नं पर्यपृच्छन्महात्मा; क्व सा गता केन नीता च कृष्णा ||१४||
कच्चिन्न शूद्रेण न हीनजेन; वैश्येन वा करदेनोपपन्ना |
कच्चित्पदं मूर्ध्नि न मे निदिग्धं; कच्चिन्माला पतिता न श्मशाने ||१५||

— shloka 14 and 15 of chapter 185 of Adi Parva (BORI)

“Where has Krishna gone? Who has taken her away? Is it a Shudra or one of low birth? Has a Vaishya who pays taxes placed his feet on my head? Has a garland been thrown away on a cremation ground? O son! Or is it a foremost man from our own varna, or is it one from a higher varna? Or has a lower being placed his foot on my head and defiled Krishna?

Here Drupada doesn’t know the identity of Arjuna who may have been a lower being or a Shudra or Vaishya. Still he couldn’t stop his participation. Now how can anyone have stopped Karna a reputed King from participation?

Here is a second reference. Yudhishtira refuses to divulge his identity to the priest of Drupad saying —

प्रदिष्टशुल्का द्रुपदेन राज्ञा; सानेन वीरेण तथानुवृत्ता |
न तत्र वर्णेषु कृता विवक्षा; न जीवशिल्पे न कुले न गोत्रे ||२३||

— Shloka 23 of chapter 185 of Adi Parva (BORI)

Drupad set a price and this brave one has won her in accordance with that. Therefore, no questions can be asked about his varna, action, intention, means of living, lineage or gotra.

Once again, you see two things —

  • There is no caste mentioned anywhere in Mahabharata.
  • No questions can be asked for varna, action, lienage.

These are explicit references rather than suppositions that you are trying to make.

You said — As per sacred texts only Khastriyas have right to participate in Swayamwara then how Can a suta participate in it.

You are talking in the same way as those devils who tried to burn down Draupadi. Do you think they knew anything about sacred text? Devil people often justify their villainy by refering to obscure text. Didn’t evil Karna said that as per Gods, a woman can have only one husband? Which God? When God of Gods blessed Draupadi with five? They are the same people who first said Brahmana’s shouldn’t be killed and minutes later changed their stance and said it is permissible to kill Brahmana willing to fight. It looks like they were writing Shastras. It is like Nirbhaya's rapist justifying that constitution permits rape and then the judge accepting their words as constitution. Fools may justify them or quote them, a sane person will check constitution, apply common sense and believe only experts. Those losers were bragging, not quoting sacred text.

But if it were true, even Arjuna would have been barred from the swaymvar. But he was allowed. Druapad’s statement and Yudhishthira’s statement contradicts these baseless words.

You said — How can Karna the wielder of Vijay Dhanush (which is considered to be better than Gandiva) can't string some bow ?

I think in the same way, he ran away from Arjuna soon after. And in the same way he ran away from Arjuna, Bhimasena, Gandharvas, Abhimanyu. You see the problem is Karna had to participate in the swyamvar not Vijaya bow.

As far as Vijay bow is concerned. There is no evidence other than Karna’s own bragging that he had Vijaya bow. No one ever collaborated Karna’s statement. Parshurama himself proclaimed Gandiva as the best of bows and never mentioned Karna had that bow. Vaisampanya clearly indicated that the bow actually belonged to Rukmi. For a detailed discussion on this refer to my podcast — https://anchor.fm/storian-hindi/...

P.S — You abandoned our previous discussion where you had to provide valid reference that Karna was not part of Yakshagriha consipiracy. I had given two valid reference that he was.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 17, 2021 07:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Yes, I have written an epic fiction on Mahabharata, but at the same time I always differentiate between fact and fiction. Fact is what is written in Vyas Mahabharata.

You said — Although he was not allowed to fight in the Mahabharata war until Bhishma was defeated, he was never afraid of war or lacked in courage.

What you said is Fiction

Fact — It was Karna who refused to fight under Bhishma’s command because of his egos.

Ref — ‘Karna said, “O king! As long as Gangeya7 is alive, I will never fight. I will fight with the one who wields the Gandiva only after Bhishma has been killed.”’ — Bhisma Avischen Parva

Why did Karna refused to fight the war? He answered that too — O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the commander is credited with qualities, never the warriors. O king! I will never fight as long as Gangeya is alive. But once Bhishma has been slain, I will fight with all the maharathas.’”’

Do you see, what I say is not fiction, but Fact

Question for you — Can your contradict my reference?

You said — In fact he was so honorable that once he found out he was Kunti’s son, he still allied himself with Duryodhana.

And yet he refused to fight for Duryodhana for first ten days? Is that why he never told Duryodhana that he won’t kill Pandavas? In fact he deliberately lied (see above reference).

Do you know he ran away from Gandharvas leaving Duryodhana to get captured. Was that honorable?

You said — His only problem was that he made friendship with an evil person (Duryodhana).

Ashwatthama too was a friend. He never conspired. Vikarna was his brother yet he opposed his crime. What is a good friend? He who pushes his friend to become more evil or he who tries to make his friend a better person?

But you may not know it was Karna who pushed Duryodhana to Ghosha yatra and Goharana. And It was Karna who ordered to disrobe Draupadi not Duryodhana.

You said — If he made a bargain with Indra like you falsely claimed, he would be a fool; since the

By calling my claim ‘false’ you proved your ignorance. By adding ‘if’ you called Karna a fool. Here is the direct reference —

O god! If I give you my earrings and armour, I will be liable to be killed. O Shakra! You will become an object of ridicule. O Shakra! Therefore, take my earrings and supreme armour, if you so wish. But take them in exchange. Otherwise, I will not give them.” Shakra replied, “Ravi36 had earlier got to know about my intended arrival. There is no doubt that he has told you everything. O son! O Karna! Therefore, ask for what you want. With the exception of my vajra, you can ask for whatever else you wish.” At that, Karna happily approached Vasava. ‘Content in his mind, he asked for the invincible shakti. Karna said, “O Vasava! In exchange for my armour and earrings, give me the invincible shakti that kills large numbers of enemies on the field of battle.”

—Kundala Harana Parva,BORI

Now if you want to call Karna a fool, do so, but you can’t falsify my direct references. Karna himself asked for exchange and refused to give the armor without exchange.

You said — Kavacha was superior in tactical terms than Indra’s weapon that could only be used once.

Now you know that my claims were not false, you must be thinking Karna was a fool. But no. Karna was an arrogant evil but shrewd not fool. He understood something you don’t

Kavacha wasn’t tactically superior. It was a protective gear. It could save Karna’s life, but it could never help him win the war, because Kavacha don’t attack enemy. And they don’t make a coward brave. Do you know why Karna was happy and content in his mind for this exchange? Read on…

You said — Lord Krishna actually said that if Karna had his armor, no one could beat him in warfare fairly.

Lord Krishna explained a probability. NOT FACT. And he praised armour and vasvi shakti. NOT KARNA. Do you know why Krishna’s statement was just a probability? Because Karna lost battles after battle despite of having a Kavacha with him.

  1. He Lost to Arjuna in Draupadi’s swaymvar battle and fled scared.
  2. He lost to Bhimasena during Rasjuy yagna battle
  3. He lost to Gandharvas and fled again deserting his best friend and his family.

You said —Each of the Pandavas had characteristics of the Devas they were born from…

Yes. But none of them were born with a kavacha. And they worked hard to achieve whatever they achieved in life. Arjuna had to compete with rest of them to prove himself better and come in good grace of his guru. He didn’t deceive his guru. He had to fight Lord Shiva to prove his worth. He was not given anything on a silver platter like Karna who, in contradiction to pulp ficiton, let a life of luxary and deceit.

There is nothing honorable about the character of Karna which has been white washed by latest fictions.

Next time you want to say something, please share a valid reference to prove your point or contradict mine.

I would not be answering unsubstantiated claims. Every single statement I made has the backing of the authentic epic. By calling them “fan fiction” and “false claims” you only expose your ignorance about the epic. Neither you nor any one else can contradict the authentic references I have presented.

Post: https://mahabharat.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-How-could-Arjun-defeat-Karna-when-he-has-divine-armours-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Dec 18, 2021 04:58 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Okk so again I need to remind you it was you who left the debate not once but thrice. If you are doing this then you don't question me for doing it.

I never left any debate. And I did ask you to share the link, where I left is answered. I asked it again and again and you couldn’t provide them. But let me provide you the link where you left without answering —

Kushal Tiwari's answer to Are the classifications of warriors done by Bhishma in the Ratha-Atiratha Parva appropriate?

You accepted — No, it's not like that as you have said it was assumption and wish of Dristdyumn and wasn't a rule.

This contradicts your point that Swayver was for Kshatirya. And Karna’s participation had no trouble.

You said — Dhrishtadyumna even thought that Krishna will participate in Swayamwara but he doesn't. Same goes for Karna.

No. Because Karna’s failure is mentioned explicitly. Krishna’s participation is not mentioned. And I have given your proof both KMG, BORI and Gita Press.

You said — Here यत्कर्ण means TO PERFORM TASK. If you want anything else you are free to ask.

May I remind you here we have two word — यत and कर्ण। Can you please tell me which of these two words mean task? Can you provide any reference as I provided?

You said — The word “पार्थिव” is translated as Kshatriya in almost every version in the version which you have mentioned.

You don’t pay attention. I gave you exact reference where “पार्थिव” is translated as King.

To prove the point let me take you to another shloka —

इदं धनुर्लक्ष्यमिमे च बाणाः; शृण्वन्तु मे पार्थिवाः सर्व एव |
यन्त्रच्छिद्रेणाभ्यतिक्रम्य लक्ष्यं; समर्पयध्वं खगमैर्दशार्धैः ||३४||

—Shloka 34 of chapter 176, Adi Parva (BORI)

O assembled kings! Hear that this is the bow, that is the target and these are the arrows. With these five arrows, shoot the target through the hole in the centre of the machine.

You asked about Gita Press translation — Why can't it be questioned ?

You may question if you have expertise. But here is three reasons I would give —

  1. It matches with the translation of Bibek Debroy. That is cross reference.
  2. Because कर्ण doesn’t translate to “task”. PC Roy and MN Dutt didn’t translate it that way. The task part is mentioned here in the shloka — नानृतं बलवद्भिर.
  3. There is a cross reference of Karna’s failure mentioned in KMG and Neelkantha which I already shared with you.

You said —If this is lame excuse then you are also doing the same you are also questioning the translation problem which I was also doing I even proved that.

No I am not challenging translation problem. I am reminding Parthiva doesn’t mean only Kshatirya. It also means King. And Karna was a King. And I am giving your reference. I am not misquoting a translation.

Can you give a reference that says कर्ण also means task?

You said — If you would have used Brahmana instead of beggar it would be better because begger is not important but being Brahman is.

That is the point. Drupada wasn’t sure he was a Brahmana. And yet he was allowed to participate. He was no noble born.

You said — I was alo confused because of Draupadi reaction. When whole Sabha and every warior present there was knowing that he was Brhamin. Literally everyone was knowing that, then how can Draupad failed to recognize him.

No one was sure. They assumed based on his appearance. At least Drupada wasn’t sure. His priest wasnt’ sure. And there is an explicit reference that overrides what you think.

When you are confused, don’t try to confuse others.

You said — The biggest evidence is Karna's performance on 17th day, he thrashed every warior on that day he was once defeated but he again defeated that Bheem badly and Arjuna ran away from Karna.

So you agree, Karna had no capability of his own he could have performed only on because of Viajaya bow?

Show a reference that Arjuna ran away from Karna. As I remember, even with a special bow he failed to protect the life of his son or his own.

You said — It is so because Karna never used that bow before but he had that from very starting.

Give me a proof that Karna had Vijaya bow other than Karna’s own statement which was a brag.

Parshurama never said he had given the bow of Karna.

In response to — The Karna who failed was a famous warrior, famous enough to be explicitly named. Do you know any other Karna in mahabharata who was a famous warrior? You said — As I said earlier, the word was not translated properly so how can you repeat this thing again. I already proved that.

Can you please clarify, what you already proved here? Was there any other famous Karna who participated in swyamver? Can you give the reference? Don’t say you proved it without giving reference.

Present the prove of concede. Just by telling I proved it, proves nothing.

You said — And the one Bow which you are talking about is Rukmis bow which was give by Drona (the other Drona) Karna’s bow was industrictible as written in Puranas and proofed in Mahabharata.

You don’t know Mahabharata. Read the original text. Rukmi had Viajaya bow and that was confirmed by Vaisampanyadas —

Those who reside in heaven possess three divine bows—Varuna’s Gandiva, the great Indra’s bow Vijaya and Vishnu’s Sharnga, which is said to be a celestial bow that is full of energy. Krishna wields it and strikes terror in the soldiers of enemies. The son of the chastiser of Paka19 obtained Gandiva from the fire god in Khandava. The immensely energetic Rukmi obtained Vijaya from Druma. Hrishikesha obtained the supreme bow Sharnga after destroying the nooses of Mura, killing the energetic Mura, killing Naraka, the son of the earth, and obtaining sixteen thousand women and many jewels. 20 ‘Rukmi obtained the bow Vijaya, which roared like a cloud and was capable of terrifying the universe.

Karna had a habit of boasting. The fact is —

  • No one ever mentioned Karna had Vijaya bow other than Karna himself.
  • Krishna was worried about Karna’s kavacha and vasvi shakti but not about his vijaya bow
  • Indra came disguised to take away his kavach. Why didn’t he also took away Vijay bow?
  • Parshurama mentioned Gandiva as the best among bows. He doesn’t say Karna had a similar powerful bow.
  • Parshurama couldn’t have given the bow to Karna as if he had the bow it would have gone to Drona when he donated all his weapons to Drona.

You said — I never accepted anything. This post was for those who follow and read BORI CE.

Question For You — What Do you think — Karna participated in the swaymvar and Draupadi rejected him or he never participated in the swyamver?

DON’T IGNORE THIS QUESTION

If you say, Draupadi rejected Karna that mean this post of yours is a farce and irrelevant.

You said : PLEASE REPLY MY OTHER COMMENTS. AND I WOULD LIKE TO REPLY YOUR COMMENT JUST MENTION LINK AND MY NEXT POST IS ON THE SAME TOPIC.

I have replied to all your comments. Every single of them. If there is any which I haven’t replied share the link of that comment and I will reply.

I have already shared the link in this post about your most recent skip. Here it is — Kushal Tiwari's answer to Are the classifications of warriors done by Bhishma in the Ratha-Atiratha Parva appropriate?

You said — AND THINK TWICE BEFORE SAYING ME ANTHING.

Yes. You are right. I would think twice and try to avoid commenting to your post as your language becomes quite abusive at times, again and again. You have apologized for this earlier. But you keep repeating the same.

My purpose of these engagements are to show you relevant reference and realize your mistakes like —

  • Karna did participate in lakshagriha conspiracy.
  • Dhristduymna didn’t say Suta’s can’t participate
  • Mistranslation can’t hide Karna’s failure. यतकर्ण doesn’t mean to perform task.
  • Draupadi never rejected Karna.

But in order to prove an evil right you get yourself tangled in that web of deceit where fictions counter each other.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 19, 2021 04:28 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Your confidence is good but check that answer again I have already replied you but for your sake I am attaching the screenshot of it.

I wished you checked better before taking the screen shot. I had already responded to all your queries below this point. You still never gave a proof that Karna wasn’t involved in Lakshagriha. Explicit Proof. There are two explicit proof of his presence.

And you didn’t share any link, where I left the debate unanswered.

You said — Karna’s failure is an translation eror made by Bibek Debroy which is translated correctly by KM Ganguly, PC Roy and MN Dutta.

That is your opinion and baselss. Baseless because it is you who has mistranslated the shloka. यतकर्ण doesn’t tranlate to “perform a task”.

PC Roy never mistranslated it.

Since you believe KM Ganuly, He clearly mentioned Karna’s failure.

You said — Karna doesn't means task but यत्कर्ण does mean th same.

Can you share any explicit reference where the work “यत्कर्ण “ means “to perform a task?” I checked sanskrit dictionary. But there is no such word. It is a composite word made up on “यत्” +”कर्ण”।

Neither the words individually or as composite means “to perform a task”

More so it would break the whole sentence as the task is mentioned separately in the shloka which I did mention to you.

You said — PC Roy and MN Dutta are well known translators they would have also looked at that scenario.

NO Niether PC Roy no MN Dutta has said “यतकर्ण” means to perform a task. It is a quora distortion. Don’t blame them. Can you show me the reference?

You said — But who asked you to give the translation of “पार्थिव” I am not being rude, I want get the reason behind this translation of yours.

Check the shloka. It doesn’t use the word Kshatriya. It uses the word Parthiva. So don’t go by translation. Check the actual word. Karna mahy or may not be called a Kshatirya but he certainly was a Parthivah (King).

You said — I do not agree on anything. But now I can evidently say you are master in distorting others. My statement was a reply and proof of my statement that Vijaya bow was better than Gandiva. My statement was to justify the power of Vijay bow.

This is what you said — The biggest evidence is Karna's performance on 17th day

Am I destorting? Who said Vijaya bow was better than Gandiva? Let me guess some arrogant boasting fool.

For I know a greater expert who said something like this —

discard your vanity and go to Dhananjaya82 before a weapon has been affixed to Gandiva, the best of bows.

Was Parshurama unaware of Vijaya bow? Was Parshurama a liar? Karna certainly was a liar. Vaisampanya clearly mentioned Vijay bow belonged to Rukmi. Parshurama couldn’t have given a bow to Karna as he had already given away all his weapons except his ax to Dronacharya long before Karna reached him. Neither Krishna nor Indra is worried about Viajay bow. There is more than explicit reference to prove the fact.

You said — It doesn't matter that I am expert or not. I can ask questions until and unless it is relevant and correct.

Oh it does matter. If you are not an expect you can ask questions. You can’t distort it. And can’t claim to clear the doubt without knowing it yourself.

Your reference of Bhargavastra — as a proof of Arjuna running away.

I take it you don’t know the difference between ‘fleeing’ and ‘leaving’. Why don’t you check it more closely with a different highlight —

At this, Kunti’s son, Dhananjaya, spoke to Vasudeva. ‘Behold the extremely terrible bhargava weapon. O Krishna! O mighty-armed one! Behold the valour of the bhargava weapon. There is no means of countering that weapon in a battle. O Krishna! Behold the angry son of a suta in the great battle. The brave one is like Yama and has performed a terrible deed. He is repeatedly casting extremely terrible glances towards me. I do not see any means of running away from Karna in this battle. If a man remains alive in a battle, there may be victory, or there may be defeat. O Hrishikesha! For the sake of victory, how can one be victorious if one is dead?’ O venerable one! Janardana wished to leave to see Yudhishthira and thought that Karna would be overcome with exhaustion in the*

  • Arjuna is worried about the misuse of Bharva weapon.
  • But Krishna isn’t worried. He knows Karna would be overcome with exhaustion.
  • He leaves to see Yudhishira knowing the weapon would not be much effective or much longer.

It is called strategy. Do you see the words like “fled” or “scared”? Can’t you differentiate between the two? Don’t tell me what you think. Give me explicit reference where Arjuna fled. Here Arjuna doesn’t even want to go. Krishna takes him.

I don’t see Arjuna’s brothers killed when he left. I don’t see Karna killing his son in front of his eyes.

You said — Karna had Vijay bow is a well known fact everyone knew it and it's divinity was shown on 17th day it was industrictible. If you want to know more about Vijay bow you can go through Puranas they will give you better description of it.

Who is “everyone”? People at Quora?

Krishna doesn’t know. Parshurama doesn’t. Indra doesn’t. Bhishma doesn’t. Drona doesn’t. Arjuna doesn’t. Vaisampanya doesn’t. So who is “everyone”

You haven’t provided any proof to this effect. It’s just a propaganda created by Karna and his followers. There is no valid reference.

No I would rather stick to Mahabharata. 17th day Karna was completely destroyed with his head severed off. Karna focused more on a chariot wheel than his so called Vijay bow. Because he couldn’t have fled on Vijay bow, but on wheel.

And there is no reference other than Karna’s boastful word that he owned Vijay bow. NOT ONE REFERENCE. Can you provide any explicit reference cross-reference other than his own words? His words are often lies and boastful.

You said — Bow is not a thing to be scared of.

Yes one should be scared of a warrior. But a great warrior with great bow is even more scary.

But same way even armor is not something to be scared of. Krishna was anyway worried. Right?

And if bow is nothing to be scared off, then Karna’s performance on 17th day doesn’t really prove his possession of Vijay bow. Right? If you say a great bow can kill more people, then it is something that you should be worried about.

Make up your mind.

In answer to my quesiton —No one ever mentioned Karna had Vijaya bow other than Karna himself — You said — It was so because no one else knew about that.

Earlier you said “everyone” knew. Now you say no one knew. Can you be specific — everyone knew or no one knew?

Ok Let us check, who may have known —

  • Did Parshurama know? Or Karna stole it while he was sleeping? He said Gandiva is the best of bows and never mentioned Karna having anything similar.
  • Did All knowing Krishna knew? I already gave you a reference, he knew everything.
  • Did Indra know? After all it was his bow.

Consider the alternative. Know one knew what was a lie spread by Karna all of a sudden on day 17.

You said — He didn't talked about Vijay bow because there was no need. He didn't had any reason to bring Vijay bow in that conversation. And Parshuram ji is man of respect from Sat yug. He is mature enough to praise others rather than bringing Vijay bow to start a debate.

As per reference — To cut the lost story short, Parshurama never said he gave Vijay bow to Karna. He had every reason to mention Vijay bow as he was talking about a mega battle and had reason to mention Gandiva. And yes, he was matured to know praising Vijay bow was still praising others and not himself. It wasn’t his.

You said — I don't know why he didn't gave that bow to Drona. He might have given it to someone else when Drona came and would have got it back afterwards.

That is a just a supposition. NO REFERENCE. But the explicit reference is clear. Parshurama gave all his weapons to Drona. Now once he gives up even if he had given to some one else (again no reference), it still becomes Drona property by verbal commitement.

Alternative — Parshurama didn’t have Vijay bow. Durma had. And he had given it to Rukmi. There is a explicit mention of it. And it fits well with rest of the narrative.

You said — Even if Karna didn't had Vijay bow, he was alone enough for Arjuna. He didn't need his father's aid of weapon, help from God and a divine bow to defeat Arjuna.

Oh yes. He was enough for Arjuna. Haven’t we seen that in Draupadi’s swyamver battle and Gandharva battle and Matasya battle and Kurukshetra battle.

And needing your father is NOT A BAD THING. Nothing to be ashamed of. And when it comes to father’s help, Karna had more of it than Arjuna. Karna got a free armor. Arjuna never got anything for free. He had to prove his worth at every stage. He didn’t get to reach heaven till he proved his worth to Shiva.

So without any valid reference you want to belive he was enough for Arjuna, something even he realized wasn’t true, it’s your wish. Or shall I say wishful thinking?

You said — According to BORI Karna didn’t participated in swyamvar. According to KMG Karna was rejected.

You will never understand what BORI said, if you think you know more about BORI than the editor-in-chief of BORI. And in KMG.

My question is what do you believe? He participated and got rejected or never participated?

Be explicit. JUST your views.

The fact is — Karna failed at swaymvar. It is explicitly mentioned in both BORI and KMG.

Forget the fact. Just your views if you have courage to give sincerely.

You said — I like your confidence but you are wrong, refer to the link which you have given me I have already replied you there long time ago and also do refer to the screenshot above.

See below your own screenshot which is from this link —Kushal Tiwari's answer to Are the classifications of warriors done by Bhishma in the Ratha-Atiratha Parva appropriate?

I had replied on the very same day December 7. Two answers. But you didn’t respond later.

YOU NEVER ANSWERED — Give me explicit proof that Karna was not involved in Lakshagriha conspiracy. I gave you two explicit reference that he was involved.

I can tell you. There is ZERO contradiction. ZERO.

QUESTION FOR YOU — Karna participated in swaymvar or he was rejected — What do you believe?

I read what you said about BORI and KMG. I asked your opinion.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 24, 2021 03:04 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Oh! YES. I do get tired. But that doesn’t mean I should let it slip. I would be guilty of not doing enough to stop the distortion of our epic.

Do you know why we needed a critical edition?

Do you know the difference between BORI and BORI Mahabharata CE?

Is current PM of India well qualified to respond to challenges of the earlier PM just because he occupies the same seat?

Do you know the true reason for variation in the manuscript?

It was in the oral tradition. And with every generation the story spread farther and farther. Each recitation also included some additional creative elements which were recited. Now Kanakprayag Brahmanas have recited the manuscript which they inherited by oral tradition but it is quite different from what people in different places inherited. And that indicates that certain stories are added in certain places.

Now say an eighth century manuscript is found out with rejection story (I highly doubt it), it would still be a sixth against many. Do you understand? One or few isolated incidents may be mistaken. NOT everyone.

Do you know about the idea of cross reference?

Someone may inject a story at one place, but can’t update the entire epic and may not realize that such additions are easily detected. Take Neelkantha, the most popular version that includes the rejection story. But it is the greatest proof of interpolation. The story includes Karna explicit failure as well.

And there is zero cross-reference rejection anywhere. No one ever mentioned it anywhere in the epic. Now there may be few other incidents that don’t find a mention at other places, but then they find a mention in every other manuscript. And that itself becomes cross-reference.

We need to critically examine cross-rerference only when there is a conflict and not if it is universally available in almost all ancient script. Say Karna-Arjuna battle after Draupadi swaymvar. It is mentioned in all prominent manuscript from Neelkantha, to Sharda to southern recitation and included in BORI. Why do we need a cross-reference?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 24, 2021 03:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You asked — We don't know what mss Nilkantha used. Do we?

Actually we do need the MSS Neelkantha used. Yes. We do.

Well I am sure, now you would go and actually do some research.

You said — Ye koi dalda hai kya jo kichuri ke saath mix kar diya? It has crept in.

And I don’t compare epic with dalda and kichuri (I don’t know what that is). But yes People often introduce their own idea and mix with what the obtain from authentic source. If you want to understand it better, you need look no further than quora, or modern fictions and TVShows. They bring ideas on their own and then build up on each other.

And this tradition is NOT knew. Every idea that you mentioned ‘crept in’ came the exact same way — someone decided to push in his own idea while narrating it and slowly it became part of that recitation, while was completely absent in other places.

You said — Secondly I didn't say it's not an interpolation. It is an interpolation

I believe you are talking about “Draupadi Rejecting Karna” is an interpolation, in which case we agree.

You said — I said why don't you challenge the text and make your own deduction?

Please explain the reference. Who challenged the text?

You said — Sukthankar wrote there can be hundreds of mss which we don't know even.

Agree with Suthankar. Many MSS were destroyed over ages as well. And foundation of any critical work must be based on available sources. Fortunately enough survived. And most of importantly, it survived in so varied geographical and tradition boundaries to provide a robust framework to sustain addition of additional MSS without needing to change the broad narrative by new additions.

Question — How does your Dalda and Kichuri theory explain that Neelkhantha includes both rejection and failure narrative at a gap of just one chapter?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 25, 2021 04:44 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — You are absolutely wrong. The volume doesn't matter. Read the prolegomena again.

AM I Wrong? Or are you captive of your imagination? Interestingly you repeated exactly what I said. Let me re-quote what I said and what you said

I had said — Now say an eighth century manuscript is found out with rejection story (I highly doubt it), it would still be a sixth against many. Do you understand? One or few isolated incidents may be mistaken. NOT everyone.

After calling me wrong you conceded — Sukthankar clearly said importance will be given to ms which are old. That is why an 6–8th century ms would make a massive difference, although in the grand scale of things, not much.

You have answered your own question and I would point out it didn’t vary much from my answer.

Now let me give you an example — Imagine an old third century manuscript is found that says it was Karna who killed Arjuna. Do you think, it would replace all other references?

Now how do we prove that the old manuscript related to original epic recitation and not from some stage play someone planned and at that age and the manuscript survived.

So let me reiterate — We have a robust framework now after over four decades of research of centuries of varied manuscript. Older manuscript most likely will confirm the supposition that CE is in the right direction. And if is too off the narrative, as we both agreed will not have much of impact.

You said — And I do have access to the original BORI Mbh & not translated by Deb Roy.

Glad to know you read original BORI MBH and have access to it. But what makes you believe that this access makes you so unique that gives you right to laugh on other?

Why do you think, I don’t have an access to the original BORI?

It’s great that you know things. It’s not great to believe that only you know the things.

So instead of trying to ridicule others, it would be great if you debate on the subject matter.

In your entire response I find it completely missing. You invested your entire energy in ridiculing a debate rather than contributing any constructive energy there. Why is that?

You said — In fact, I haven't read Deb Roy.

If you read something, it is a matter of price. Where is the price in telling I haven’t read something? I read Debroy. And I read Neelkantha. I also read KMG. I also read many mythological fictions. And I watched TV Shows. And I think, I can proudly say, that gives me an understanding of multi-dimensional issue.

Do read even fictions. That is important to understand what is brainwashing youths and distorting our epic. And you will find many dalda+kichuri for your understanding.

You said — No academic scholars read Deb Roy.

Surprise! surprise. There are not many academic scholars on Quora. And what academic scholars debate in the confines of their room doesn’t matter most in the public opinion.

How many people do you think even know about the word BORI and Neelkantha, out there? How many of them know what makes Mahabharata ‘authentic’?

Do you know the problem? Since you have access to scholars would you pass my messages to your mentors — Academic debate won’t help the epic unless it can be brought to public in simpler way.

BRChopra today is more popular associated with Mahabharata than BORI in the masses. And when BRChopra said that Karna was rejected, it became a public opinion despite of explicit BORI research that marks it an interpolation. Does that make BRChopra more knowledgeable that BORI? Nah! But whose version would people believe?

You can’t beat public opinion by sitting in academic debate alone.

So I would recommend you this — Read Bibek Debroy. Because that is what many have done partially to preserve or distort the debate. People around here don’t understand sufficient sanskrit and copy-paste each other to say “यत्कर्ण” means “to perform a task”.

If you make understanding Sanskrit mandatory for learning Mahabhrata, it would kill Mahabharata.

For me, I am content to be degraded by you. You can laugh at me and say — Ho! He is no scholar. He reads Bibek Debroy.

I am content at my effort to counter the distortion of Mahabharat in the language people understand here, giving the reference that would accept.

Regards,

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 25, 2021 05:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — My viewpoint on Indology & the epics especially doesn’t match yours.

This is a paradox — You say our ideology don’t match and if I agree with you, then it matches. Right?

You have a indirect way of matching my view point. We both agreed —

  • On the theory of cross referencing and what route CE follows. You tried to counter me and finally agreed as you said — eighth century manuscript if proposes a completely opposing theory may not make much sense in big picture.
  • I never said one reading of anything is sufficient. I have been reading it for many years. And only arrogant cease to be students.

Now we disagree too —

  • I don’t agree to the word “Hinduism”. This is one point where people get into a spiral winding confusion. We have a way of life that accepts even Buddha as an avatar. No “ism” is capable of such a feat.

You missed the essence of my previous comment, which you should have if you really appreciate importance of interpolation (but not the interpolations itself) —

  • Even fictions tell a tale. So not only Debroy (or his mistakes in translations) but also fiction write with complete garbage still gives us at a glimpse of interpolations and how they find their way.
  • While I understand the importance of interpolations let us understand two important points —
    • You said — That’s why I said interpolations are good. It reveals the socio-political-religious aspect of history.
      • I would differ. Interpolation may not always reveal religious aspect of history.
      • They often mean seer distortion to propagate one political view without rhyme or reason. Making Karna a victim is a great case study.

To Sum Up —

The idea of interpolation is not bad, the purpose of it determines good or bad. When it tries to degrade Indian epic, culture and legacy, it is certainly bad. Not all interpolation are reflection of society.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 25, 2021 11:10 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — What you’re saying is - volume. That is 6 against 250 odd MSS. Wrong. It depends on - “Ms’s position in the “family tree” of recensions”.

I believe you agreed. And understood. What you are not ready is to agree that you agreed. I never insisted on numbers alone. I continuously emphasized on the process adopted and the importance of cross-references within script and in comparison to other script. And I guess you missed the part where I strongly suggested — I don’t believe a sixth century manuscript would contain this crap. That defines your family tree of recensions.

Let me once again sum my thought for you lest you don’t feel tempted to put your words in my mouth —

The epic for thousands of years followed an oral tradition of propagation. Let us start with someone like Vaisampanya who narrated the epic in a gathering from all over the area. People present their becomes second generation who would propagate it in their regions and their followers would do the like wise.

Now let us assume 20 people carried the tradition in different parts — eastern India, nothern India, western India and so on. Now one of the twenty added a narrative to make it more interesting while reciting the narrative and two other added a different narratives in their epic.

Now none of those additional are know to 17 regions. And what they added new is also not known to each other. But interpolations added by one of them would be retold by their followers and their follower for generations creating a very large population who would begin to trust that interpolated story as sacrosanct.

So how do we know which one is an interpolation and which one is not? It would be understood only when we can collate all the manuscript and find what are the interpolations that are prevalent only in few regional manuscript, but completely missing in other places. What could be the reason?

Now let me repeat my question — Say we get a very very old manuscript say 100BC manuscript. And it says Rama killed Karna. Do you think any CE team would be reconsidering revising the manuscript?

Age is no guarantee of wisdom. People would check how this manuscript fits in big picture and then first they would validate it’s authenticity rather than blindly accepting it because it is ‘old’

That is what I said — 6 against many. An old manuscript can vindicate the view point already ascertained. It in itself can’t displace entire research if it is in complete contradiction to well researched framework.

But most important — this debate which you started was based on a completely hypothetical scenario which I pointed out in the very discussion. I don’t believe you would get an ancient manuscript with such a clearly prejudiced interpolation which fails completely on multiple fronts —

  • It goes against the foundation of characters as understood at different point of time. Consider Suthankar’s remark here —
    • Draupadi couldn’t have much say in the matter. Her nature is in contrast to what is present at just one place. She without question not only accepts Arjuna but also five husbands. No protest whatsoever.
    • No one stopped Arjuna from a participation despite of the fact that Drupad wasn’t even sure if Arjuna was a Brahamna
    • Yudhisthira too says the same thing.
    • There is zero cross reference.

Controversy and Interpolation

You know the famous and one of the most recent interpolation — Andhe Ka Putra Andha. It’s one of the recent. What was the socio-political compulsion for inserting this story?

Now when we say a particular incident is full of controversy, the first answer should we seeking is — What is the source of this controversy? Do we have a controversy because of discrepancy in MSS or because some ‘scholar’ decided to propagate an hypothetical idea and wrote and essay full of ‘maybe’ and ‘it seems’ or ‘it appears’ and ‘if their exist a manuscript’?

You know some scholar even challenge existence of five Pandavas. Now is their a single know manuscript or oral tradition that casts even an iota of doubt on their existence? But it is those ideas which germinates in few minds and becomes breeding ground of interpolations. Many people get caught up in such web of lies.

Take our current example — you are trying to contradict me by making a supposition — assuming a eighth century manuscript that includes rejection. Is there one? Now I may understand what you raised is a scholarly debate. But our friend here, on whose thread we are debating, may assume that their exists such an MSS which is most ancient and includes…

And thus propagates interpolation — People often try to read the text without those crucial — ‘if’, ‘maybe’ , ‘perhaps’

So while realizing significance of manuscript I can agree that they always represent socio political compulsions and as such naturally become something good.

My focus is not scholarly. It’s to resist degradation of our epic culture either in the name of creativity or in the name of being scholar.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 26, 2021 12:06 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — “…eighth century manuscript if proposes a completely opposing theory may not make much sense in big picture”

Exactly what I have been telling all these while. Good we agreed.

You said — The second group study Indology as “totality” - that is encompassing everything including local myths. It gives a rich texture and understanding of the story. No matter how different it can be.

Fire burns brightly. It has great texture. I would love it’s light. But can’t allow it to destroy something good to see it’s bright texture. Lights are constructive parts are welcome. Destruction no so much.

And I am not comfortable degrading the epic to a mere story just to keep scholars happy or enjoy rich texture.

You said — Local myths, prejudices, vices, and even good customs have crept into an old oral text that is what makes the epic unique. That is totality. I see it from that perspective.

I am not against interpolations. I love the idea of Laksmana Rekha. If any descriptive addons are in sync with the epic lesson and adds value to it, should be welcomed. Those which contradicts the epic and devalues it should be condemned. That’s my perspective.

How I see Swayamvar?

  1. Current and extensive research has ruled out Draupadi rejecting Karna as an interpolation.
  2. Neelkhantha one of the prominent reference that Karna’s rejection also carries Karna’s failure further diluting it’s own credibility at this point.
  3. All character references and cross-references clearly indicate that Draupadi rejecting Karna is an irrational story as stated even by Prof. Suthankar.
  4. We both agreed that even if a eighth century manuscript includes this story may not have big impact on big picture. And most important, this is a theoretical debate —
    1. There is no eighth century manuscript that carries such a story.
    2. All current manuscript has been categorized inferior.

So till the time miraculously a hundred old manuscript is discovered which completely contradicts point 1–3, we can safely stick to CE’s recommendation that it is an interpolation.

Whatever else we say, is just supposition prefixed with ‘may be’, ‘if’ …

Regards,

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 27, 2021 09:08 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Let me sum up this debate as we are repeating the same thing. And then let me ask you a few question.

KARNA’S INVOLVEMENT IN LAKSHAGRIHA

BORI and prof Suthankar never removed Karna’s involvement in Lakshagriha conspiracy. BORI has two explicit reference.

First in Adi parva, Vaisampanya das clearly mentions Duryodhana consulted Karna. In conspiracy master minds consult to form a plan, they never execute it themselves. Here at the very least Karna had full knowledge of the plan. And no one said Karna set the fire himself.

Second Krishna himself explicitly mentions Karna’s role in Lakshagriha in Karna Vadha Parva. Both these references are from BORI.

On the other hand there is NOT A SINGLE REFERENCE IN BORI OR KMG that negates the fact that Karna was not involved.

Now unless you can provide an explicit proof that Karna was not involved, we both know Karna was involved in the conspiracy.

DRAUPADI’S SWYAMVER

You are non-committal. You don’t have a clear answer and would like to accept any of the two contradictory answers —

  1. Karna didn’t participate or
  2. Draupadi rejected him.

This is just a common emotion — you don’t want to see him fail. That is understandable. But that doesn’t mean you get to choose both. If Karna didn’t participate you must agree Draupadi couldn’t have rejected him. If Draupadi rejected him you must admit Karna was allowed to particpate.

The only reason you can’t agree to one of them is the fact that deep within you know both of them to be fake.

For your Information

  1. Prof Suthankar very clearly mentioned that Karna was invited and Draupadi couldn’t have rejected him.
  2. Karna failure is mentioned in BORI explictly. Prof Bibek Debroy translation coincide with Gita Press translation. Forget translator यत्कर्ण doesn’t translate to “to perform a task”. This is not a mistranslation by any scholar but ignorant fools on Quora.
  3. Even KMG and Neelkantha clearly mentions Radheya Karna failed the swaymver.

So whatever you want to believe only Quora driven mistranslation is responsible for this confusion. All authentic source mentions Karna failure.

And you are not even sure what you believe so we can’t debate on it.

BUT TELL ME THIS, ARE YOU NOT BEING DISHONEST, WHEN YOU CLAIM TO ELIMINATE A MISCONCEPTION AND SAY SOMTHING YOU DON’T YOURSELF BELIEVE?

This is the difference between us. I say what I believe. You say something even if you don’t believe it. I base my discussion on references. You base it on quora theory.

QUESTION FOR YOU

Share exact reference where PC Roy or MN Dutta translated “यत्कर्ण” as you said. Now don’t ask me to share a reference. You said he has translated. Now it is your responsibility to share the reference.

BHARGAVASTRA AND ARJUNA

Everytime a warrior retreated a battle in Mahabharata and explicit reason is attributed, and also a consequence followed. Let us take examples —

  • Karna retreated swaymvar battle because he was scared. Result — Kings withdrew.
  • Karna fled from Gandharavas afraid of his life. Result — Duryodhana was captured.
  • Karna fled thrice in Matasya battle. Result — Kauravas were defeated. Karna’s brother was killed.
  • Karna fled Abhimanyu. Result — his brother was killed again.

Now let us come to Bhargava. A powerful weapon. But Krishna isn’t worried. He wants to go to Yudhishthira. Why? Was he afraid? NO. He wants Arjuna to convince Yudhisthira that he will kill Karna today. Now tell me —

  • How would Arjuna kill Karna if Bharagastra was so powerful?
  • Let me humor you. Arjuna fled. Mahabharata doesn’t say fled. Which great warrior did it kill?
  • If Arjuna fled, why didn’t Karna win the war? Why the war didn’t end?
  • What happened to Bharaga weapon?
  • Why did Krishna say it would exhaust Karna? Was Krishna wrong?

Now Answers to your related question —

You asked - Give me a proof that didn’t wanted to leave battle.

No proof. Arjuna wanted to leave. He always heeded to Krishna advice. He knew leaving Karna with his Bharavastra was best course of action. It would be more clear in the next answer.

Question for you - Give me some incident where Arjuna really counters great and unique asyras by himself ?

What do you think countering a divyastra really mean? Is it destroying it? You are mistaken. Countering a divyastra means rendering it ineffective. A wise strategy is rendering a weapon ineffective with least damage.

Only fools and evils seek destruction in everything. Arjuna doesn’t destroy Karna after swayvar battle. He spares the life all warriors after Matasya war. He spares the life of Bhagdatta after Rajsuy yagna. Arjuna is not blood thirsty.

Each of the great weapon initiated against Pandavas were neutralized.

You said — Here Krishna wished to see Yudhishthira when he knew incapability of Arjuna.

Think again. Krishna saved Arjuna from Narayanastra and Vaishnavastra. He didn’t leave the battle. Did he?

Then by your supposition (WITHOUT REFERENCE), was even Krishna afraid of Bhargavastra? Let us say Arjuna was incapable. Was even Krishna incapable of countering Bhargavstra? Think. Even if you can’t reply, give a sincere thought. And you will know the correct answer.

VIJAY BOW

You agreed Parshurama didn’t lie. He clearly said Gandiva is best among the bows.

You also said Parshurama had no reason to talk about Vijay bow. Why? He did talked about Gandiva. The only reason he had no reason to talk about it was the fact that he knew Vijay bow won’t matter.

Sanjay repeated Karna’s statement. He didn’t made his own statement. Think. You say know one knew about Vijay bow. If Indra, Krishna and Parshurama didn’t know how could Sanjay.

But the fact remains unchanged —

  1. Vaisampanya clearly mentioned Vijay bow of Indra belonged to Rukmi.
  2. Only Karna, a confirmed liar, said he owned Vijay bow. The lie was apparent as he never used it. Not even to from Jaidradh. Why? Was he again cheating on Duryodhana? No. he didn’t have it.
  3. Krishna and Indra were worried about armours and vasvi shakti but not Vijay bow. And both are divinity. Both knew the truth.

You asked — Did anyone ever asked Krishna about that bow? Did anyone asked Indra about that bow ?

NO. No one asked. But then no one asked them about divine armors also. Had anyone asked about them? Yet, they talked about it proactively. Yet Krishna was worried about them and was happy when Karna lost them. Krishna said with Kavacha, Kundala and Vasvi Shakti gone consider Karna as good as dead. Now he is lost. My question was why were Krishna and Indra not worried about Karna having Vijay bow. Either having that bow didn’t matter or he didn’t have it. Actually both statements are true.

You see Indra came to snatch kavacha and Kundla. Had he known Karna had Vijay bow he would have taken it. And it was impossible for him not to know.

With all your arguments the facts don’t change —

  • No one ever mentions Karna possessing Vijay bow.
  • Karna never used Vijay bow. He must have been stupid to have a great bow and not use it. And before you use favorite K-Fan remark that he was proud not to use it, Karna used up all his grand weapons.
  • There is no reference to prove Parshurama ever had Vijay bow. That too is not mentioned anywhere.

You said — By everyone who knows that Karna had Vijay bow you mean quora users.

I can understand it without you telling. Because the epic tells a contradictory tale. And this is the difference between you and I. I trust authentic reference and not the popular gossip on social media. Today’s gossip can’t change the past.

You asked — Was Vijay bow as popular as Karna’s Kavacha Kundala ?

Actually it was more popular. It was one of the top three bows of the era. And it was Indra’s brow.

You said — Arjuna was helped so many times by Krishna and Indra.

Can you give a reference where Arjuna was helped by Indra? Indra fought against Arjuna. Arjuna helped Indra.

And think. Why did Krishna help Arjuna? You think of it as a weakness. The fact is, it is the greatest proof of his strength.

You said — Every battle except Kurukshetra wasn't a do or die war.

YOU ARE WRONG.

Every battle is always a do or die battle. Every one. No exception.

Had Arjuna not performed his ‘do’, Drupada’s family would have died.

Had Arjuna not performed Karna’s ‘do’, Duryodhana and his family would have been captured.

Had Karna performed his ‘do’, his brothers would still have been saved.

Karna survived because of his ability to run away. Everytime. And that was the reason, he trusted his chariot’s wheel more than his so called Vijay bow. He could have fought bravely like Arjuna did on 14th day without chariot. But he knew he can’t fight for ever. He said that to Shalya. Sooner or later he would need his chariot wheels to flee.

YOU SAID — Due to technical glitch I can’t see my answer.

You may try incognito window. But I can present the poof. See the date on the screenshot.

You shared another link where I left a debate

I think you are facing a lot of technical glitch. I was waiting for your answer on that thread. The last answer was given by me on November 12. There after you left that debate. You may check it again. Here is the last screenshot —

Check out the dates.

S U M M A R Y

  • You couldn’t provide a single reference that Karna wasn’t invovled in Lakshagriha. You assertion that BORI removed this is WRONG. There are two references in BORI and I have provided both.
  • You couldn’t provide a single reference that यत्कर्ण means “to perform task”. I gave you exact word that MN Dutt, PC Roy have used to represent “to perform task”. Even Neelkantha represents यत्कर्ण the same way as Bibek Debroy.
  • You didn’t answer why KMG and Neelkantha mentions Radheya Karna failed in the swaymver?
  • What is the proof of Draupadi rejected Karna?
Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Dec 30, 2021 10:08 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Laksha Grih Conspiracy

You said — Duryodhana consulted or talked with Karna and Dusashan before making that house. But does that mean Karna was involved in Conspiracy.

Yes it exactly means he was involved in the conspiracy. At least he knew about the conspiracy. And that knowledge make him complicit.

You said — Krishna was talking in generalized form. Was Krishna present at the time when it all was executed.

NO. Krishna didn’t said generalized. He specifically mentioned Lakshagriha incident. And you forget — Krishna knew everything from past, present and future —

O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

You said — Has Vaisampayana mentioned it anywhere.

YES. The Adiparva statement is his.

You said — In a same way Duryodhana, Shakuni and others were blamed for that conspiracy but Karna wasn't involved. so there was no need of saying this that he was not involved.

Krishna squarely blamed Karna. And he knew everything. Vaisampanya blamed Karna. Now can you give me references where only Duryodhana and Shakuni is blamed and not Karna?

And since there are at least two references to indicate Karna’s involvement, one by Krishna himself, you need an explicit reference to proof he wasn’t involved. Krishna condemned him. Your hollow words mean nothing.

Cross Reference: Karna is mentioned in all his conspiracy earlier also.

Final Question For you — Instead of dragging the argument, is there a single reference that mentions Karna was not involved in Lakshagriha. I gave you two reference. I dare you to provide one. I know you can keep arguing but without proof. Because there is NONE.

You may degrade Krishna in saying he didn’t know. You may ignore Vaisampanya. But can you prove? Can you contradict my reference?

Draupadi’s swyamver

Karna’s failure

You said — Yes, you are right, according to me both are correct because I nor you nor anyone else has original copy of Mahabharata so we both don't know which one of either one is right.

Do you want to call it interpolation? I know it’s a favorite word of Karna’s fans. But tell me if you don’t trust Mahabharata what is your source?

Question for you — Can you give me one version of Mahabharata that doesn’t mentions Karna’s failure in Draupadi’s swyamver?

You said — Ok let me trust you for a moment but can you please explain the reason why other scholars who I have mentioned before have not translated in a same way that Bibek Debroy has translated. And please provide proof for your answer if not proof the please provide an acceptable logic.

NOT A SINGLE SCHOLAR TRANSLATED यत्कर्ण as to perform a task. NOT ONE. It is just a rumor. Some just mentioned Shalya’s name. Can you provide a direct reference where यत्कर्ण is mentioned as to perform a task?

If you want logic here it is. I don’t know if you would accept.

यत्कर्ण — The meaning of word “यत्” alone is to take effort. so what is “कर्ण” here? The person to took effort. This is elementary sanskrit and any sanskrit teacher will confirm you that.

If you read Gita Press translation you can see word by word meaning and you would realize that your translation is impossible —

You should also see that translation says “prominent warriors like Shalya and Karna and other Kashatirya…”

Now there was no other prominent Karna mentioned in entire chapter. No Dhritrashtra’s son was not a prominent warrior. Also see Karna is mentioned prominent, Kshatirya applied to other warriors.

Most important, I repeat, no scholar translated यत्कर्ण as to perform task. Can you provide exact reference?

You asked — So was Shri Krishna so according to you he has also failed that's what you are trying to say ?

  • Did Suthankar said Krishna failed? Suthankar replied on controversy created for whitewashing evil’s character. There is no controversy about Krishna.
  • Is there any mention of Krishna’s failure in any of the version? There are many that mentions Karna’s failure.
  • Does KMG or Neelkantha mention Krishna’s failure? It does mention Karna’s failure.
  • Did Krishna try to kill Drupad’s family like other losers?

Karna’s defeat

You said —Here Karna and others were fighting lightly and carelessly as Brahmans and specially Arjuna is described as weaklings in the above paragraph.

Did you read it properly? Let me show you the text.

PART 1 — Refers to Karna and Shalya. You will see there is no light fight here.

Then the mighty Vaikartana30 Karna rushed at Arjuna, eager to fight, like an elephant rushes at a rival over a female elephant. Shalya, king of the Madras, rushed at Bhimasena.

PART 2 — Explicitly mentions Duryodhana and other King (other than Karna and Shalya) who attacked Brahmanas

Duryodhana and the others rushed at the Brahmanas, but they fought with them lightly and carelessly.

PART 3 — Karna Arjuna Fight. See last hightlight to see if they fought mildly.

‘Seeing Vaikartana Karna rush towards him, the wise Arjuna stretched his mighty bow and pierced him with three arrows. The impact of these sharp arrows stunned Radheya31 and he approached with greater circumspection. Then those two invincible warriors fought violently, each eager to vanquish the other.

Do you know the meaning of circumspection? It is “the quality of being wary and unwilling to take risks;”

It is a show of weakness as you are not willing to take risk. And it refers to Karna not Arjuna. Apply common sense or read hindi translation.

Think! Arjuna had no reason to fight. To protect people in distress was his call. If he was afraid he wouldn’t have come. He hit Karna with three arrows. Karna was stunned. Arjuna was never hit. He was never stunned. Why would Arjuna be circumspect? And why would he continue to fight and not retreat? But Arjuna continues to fight. Karna sees Vishnu, Indra and Rama in him. Arjuna sees just a warrior he could vanquish. He asks him to stay. Karna retreats because he thinks Brahmana who was like Indra, Rama and Vishnu is invincible. And yes. He was scared away. Every word I mentioned comes directly from the epic.

You said — Again it's a typical Arjun fan thing. You think that Karna was scared but I think and presumably belive and that's true that Karna was surprised rather that being scared.

WRONG. There is no question of what you or I think. It is mentioned in the epic in clear term —

When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened and surrounded Vrikodara.

What is typical about Arjuna fan is they never call Mahabharata or it’s part as interpolation. Believe what you like, but reference says he was scared.

But don’t let your believe blind you so much that you can’t read text properly.

You said — Here he withdrew from the battle because he thought Brahmans strength was invincible.

That is the definition of defeat. You retreat because you think your opponent is invincible and you can’t defeat him. And see above reference. He is explicitly mentioned scared.

You said — Karna wasn’t afraid of his life or something like that he was chariotless because of Maya used by Gandharvas on the contrary to your statement it was shortage of Maharathis that led to defeat of Kauravas but don't forget the first wave of Gandharvas was routed by Karna only.

How many Maharathi’s they were short of? Were they fewer in number than Pandavas? How many of those didn’t flee? And why did Karna flee? Let me give you the reference —

Holding a sword and a shield in his hand, the son of the suta jumped down from the chariot. He leapt onto Vikarna’s chariot and whipped the horses, so that he might escape.’

And here is what the despicable person himself admitted

While you looked on, I was worsted by all those gandharvas. My own army was routed and I was incapable of ensuring that they remained there. I was sorely wounded by the arrows and hard-pressed. I ran away.

Only a blind fan can claim that Karna wasn’t afraid.

You said — Oh my gosh !! Typical Arjuna fan. Why do you turned that question in a physlosophical answer. I expected a practical answer but let me aske you again with specification. and asked — Give me some incident where Arjuna really countered some great and Unique Divyastras? ( please answer it practically with names of weapon)

Sorry I can’t answer. Because you won’t understand. You don’t understand my answer was not philosophical it was practical strategy. You don’t destroy a nuclear weapon by another nucelar weapon. You diffuse them. Only fools would dare to destroy one great weapon with another.

You asked — First of all provide me references where Karna lied?

  1. Karna lied to Parshurama that he is a Bhargava Brahmana.
  2. Karna lied in shabha that God said a woman can have only one husband. There is no such reference. Wiser man than him confirmed by solemnizing Draupadi’s marriage. Shiva himself had decreed this marriage.
  3. He never told Duryodhana that he wouldn’t kill Pandavas. He made Duryodhana believe that Karna would kill them
  4. He made Duryodhana believe that he would would kill all Pandavas — O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the
  5. He said he had Viajay bow, a story which he invented on the last day as Vijay bow of Indra belonged to Rukmi as confirmed by Vaisampanya.

Vijay Bow

  • Parshruama didn’t give his axe to Drona. It wasn’t a weapon. It was something given to him as a blessing by his guru Shiva. But he did donate all his weapons to Drona.
  • There is no reference that Karna had a mighty bow other than his words.
  • Vijay bow of Indra was present with Rukmi

You asked — First you said He didn't wanted to leave but now you are saying he wanted to leave. First confirm yourself then write.

Please read my previous reference clearly. You asked me can you proof Arjuna didn’t want to leave? To that I replied No. I can’t prove. Because Arjuna never said I don’t want to leave. He followed Krishna asvise and realized that Karna would be exhausted by himself and there is no need to wait for him.

You said — Was Krishna a baby sitter of Arjuna that he will care for each and everything for Arjuna.

You people certainly wanted to believe. So what happened here? Did he stop baby sitting. Why? Didn’t he say Arjuna is his inseparable existence. So why would he abandon him?

You said — He wants to see Yudhishthira because he knew Arjuna’s inability in countering Bhargava weapon.

Are you sure? Is there any reference that Krishna thought Arjuna couldn’t counter Bhargava? Did Krishna forget that Arjuna possess more powerful weapons?

Ok. So Krishna thought Arjuna couldn’t counter Bhargava. And he decided that he wouldn’t baby sit him. But does this theory answer —

  • Why he wanted to tell Yudhisthira that Arjuna will kill Karna today? Why didn’t he try to take him out of Kurukshetra to hide some where?
  • Why did he say Karna would be exhausted.

You know whatever you are saying is a wishful thinking of Karna’s fan. There is no proof. Do you really want to know what Krishna thought about Karna and Arjuna on the seventeenth day —

Krishna laughed and spoke these supreme words to Partha. ‘The sun may fall down from its place. The earth may shatter into many fragments. The fire may become cold. But Karna will not be able to kill Dhananjaya.

Now you still don’t understand what Krishna thought. Do you? So let me ask you again, do you have any explicit proof that Krishna thought Arjuna couldn’t defeat Karna?

You said — It is so because bow is not a thing to be scared off.

Then why drag this debate?

But then if a bow can shot more arrows, it is certainly something to be afraid of. May be Krishna and Indra were not afraid because they knew a great bow in a weak hand is nothing to be afraid of.

You said — Arjuna also possesed Gandiv yet he ran away. Was anyone afraid of Arjuna. NO.

Asked and answered. Arjuna didn’t run away. And you know it. You have not given a single proof that Arjuna didn’t leave to convince Yudhishthira that he would kill Karna. How would you kill someone if you are afraid.

Coming to your question — Was anyone afraid of Arjuna? I know at least one person. Want to hear what he says —

Karna replied, “I wish to kill only one enemy37 in a great battle. He roars and blazes and is the source of fear for me.”

And not just words. He fled leaving his brother to die.

You said — It's called strategy not stupidity. He saved his best weapons for last day.

Oh it is called stupidity — when you loose battle after battle. When you have to flee leaving your friends to die. When you flee leaving your brothers to die. A true warrior knows any day could be his last.

Matasya battle — Really! Karna never fled in Matsay battle do you even know the meaning of fleeing and recovering. Karna retreated from battle from recovering and for your kind information Bhishma had also gone to recover in Matasaya battle.

Read the epic. He fled thrice. And yes, I know the meaning of fleeing specifically when it is mentioned exactly. When your brother is dead because you fled, there is nothing left to recover.

And yes, Bhishma too had gone. It happened to best of them when pitted against Arjuna.

You said —

Advantages of Arjuna.

  1. Industrictible and Celestial Bow. — You claim even Karna had one. Thus this advantage is negated. If he doesn’t use, he was stupid.
  2. Celestial and Industrictible Chariot. — Chariots don’t fight battle. And Karna’s chariot wasn’t destrodyed. Thats how he fled, remember.
  3. Inexhaustible quiver . Karna never ran out of arrow. He ran out of courage.
  4. Ape's banner made by Vishwakarma. Ape banner too never fought. So no tactical advantage. It’s presence only proves that Arjuna was the good guy who was blessed.

Advantages of Karna

  • None, → Agree. Adharmi’s don’t have any advantage. Never.
  • He had Armour and Earrings that to was begged by Arjuna’s Dad (Indra)
    • Begged is WRONG word. Karna himself preferred exchange for which he was immensely happy.
    • Arjuna wasn’t responsible for his dad’s action. Dad accepted the proposal of Karna for a trade. You can’t hold him guilty
  • Divine Weapons — He had all his divine weapons as Karna confirmed right in the Matasya battle. He claimed he has sufficient power to destory Arjuna. What he didn’t count was his lack of courage.

Creation time: Jan 05, 2022 10:26 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for your answer. I have read Ghosha yatra multiple times.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-How-many-warriors-defeated-Radheye-and-How-many-warriors-Radheye-defeated-not-in-the-Kurukshetra-o
