Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-How-many-warriors-defeated-Radheye-and-How-many-warriors-Radheye-defeated-not-in-the-Kurukshetra-o
Creation time: Jan 10, 2022 06:28 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Where is you answer?

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 15, 2022 11:58 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

While I don’t understand most part of your answer as I don’t follow Mad Max and you lack any reference from the epic of Mahabahrata itself, but I would like to answer the parts which I can —

You said — about Karna, I think he was wrongly accused of wickedness.

NO. Karna was wicked to the core. Wicked at the heart. Few people were wicked for the sake of being wicked. Duryodhana had a reason of enmity with Pandavas. Karna hatred. He was inovled in every consipiracy against Pandavas although there is zero evidence that Pandava ever carried such a ill will toward him.

He was the instigator for vastraharana. He was the brain behind the misadventure leading to gosha yatra and go-harana. He was lustful and wanted to molest Draupadi and see her in tree barks.

And he was a war monger. There is nothing wrong in accusing him of being devil and wicked.

You said — God cannot help him, so he lives a life misunderstood, lacks in divine protection, he gets cursed here and there,

WRONG! Karna had a divine protection like no other. But you forfeit such protection if you are devil and want to strip a woman naked in public. He did get cursed, but can you count one single ocassion where the curse was unjustified.

You said — he gets little chances in life.

Really? How? How many gets a chance to be trained by the best teachers in the world? How many people not belonging to a royal lineage gets to become the King at such a young age? Can you point what what chance was denied to him? I would daresay Karna was one of the luck few who got lot more than he ever deserved.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 16, 2022 12:09 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — You said Karna was wicked - This is only opinion and people can make similar opinions about Pandavas and even Lord Krishna.

What you said is a ‘mere’ perception. What I said is a perception endorsed by sanatana references. And that is a big difference.

You may blame Krishna and Pandavas. That would be your opinion. Just opinion based on arguments or debates. But not backed by epic references.

We may find philosophical and so called deeper meaning. But how deeper or how true or how useful that philosophy would be that would be based on distortion of facts rather than facts themselves? Have you ever given a thought about it?

I am ok to debate on the philosophy and deeper meaning. But, let me remind you, no true conclusion can be drawn with fake references —

REFLECTIVE STORY OF KARNA

I agree with you, Karna had a shadow of the Kaliyuga. You may consider him as the first personification of the Kaligyuga. He represents a breed of undeserving people who expected to lay easy hand on everything and blame others for his failures.

Guru Drona teaches everyone together. Karna had no reason of enmity with Pandavas. Yet he sides with Duryodhana in his conspiracy. That represents the common mindset of a stooge and not of a friend. He wants same weapon as Arjuna although he had shown not the same level of brilliance. Guru hadn’t granted that weapon to even other princess. This represented the modern mindset where someone finds himself entitled for everything.

He was denied what he was unworthy of. Even today you don’t get degree because you want it. You have to be deserving.

Then the wicked fellow, goes on to cheat another guru. He gets cursed because of his wickedness and unnecessary arrogance. And in Kalyuga people find pity for his mindset, perhaps, because they too feel untitled despite not being worthy. So what do they do? They invent the story of a society trying to crush a so called low born and his innocent ambition.

Should we find out an inner meaning? What meaning it would be when it is based on total fabrication?

  • Karna was no low born.
  • He was never denied any education.
  • He was cursed because he deserved it.

It is ok to find the deeper meaning that Karna was a disease that was to plague the future Kalpa. But making him a victim is justifying his act.

I can go on… But I think, you got the point.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 16, 2022 07:01 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for the long answer. Mostly agree, but fail to see which of my questions you tried to answer.

More importantly, those questions are specifically for Karna fans and as such you don’t qualify. 😉

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 16, 2022 07:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You “Simple” is void of references.

Parshurama decided Karna was unsuitable because he was a liar. He resorted to deceit. This is explicitly mentioned not only by Parshurama but even by Karna.

Where your arguments fail flat is —

  • Parshurama taught Bhishma who was a Kshatirya. This shatters your theory.
  • Since Karna was a Suta (Brahman Mother, Kshatriya Father) there was no caste biased. In fact no ‘caste’ is mentioned anywhere in the epic.
  • A Nishada was a gurukul friend of Shri Rama. Again no caste biase.
  • Daughter of a fisherwomen was both King and Rishi. No caste biase
  • A low born Vidur was Prime Minister. No caste biase
  • Same Suta who was denied by Parshurama became a King and no one object. No caste biase.

If biase exists. It is in your head.

You said — As for Drona, he could have asked his pupil (without an iota of contribution towards his pupil) to never use the knowledge gained! Eklavya would have happily agreed.

You are WRONG! Drona had already refused him teaching. Did Eklavya happily agreed and abstained for the knowledge? No. He continued to learn it. When guru asked for his thumb, he must have got the message. Did he stop then? No he continued towards his destiny — to be killed by Lord Krishna, who incarnated to destroy evils.

You said — But alas he decided to cut off the part of his body, to ensure his favourite ward is not challenged in the future

His favorite pupil was ambidextrous. He could perform with both hands. That means even with a thumb chopped off. Eklavaya was no match. Have you paused and thought, Drona might wanted to save Eklavya?

He had seen him unfit for milletary education. He continued to study despite his explicit rejection. Such people often become terrorists. He was one, who attacked Dwarka and died.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 17, 2022 09:11 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

During his life time Karna, Duryodhana are called wicked and evil repeatedly. And you don’t need a court case. There are so many references to convince a common man.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 17, 2022 09:26 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Do you think Rama killed Ravana because he was not wicked or evil? Did his act not speak for themselves?

But I will not draw myself into a fresh debate here. We were talking about Karna and I answered your question — Karna was called evil. Now can we agree here?

And to answer you question — Yes. I think we should strongly point out — They were evil and should we condemned. And that is the only way we can teach our young generation, what they should aspire to avoid.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 18, 2022 09:30 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No matter what way you look if you are trying to find a justification for an evil, wicked, molester, you are seeing it wrong.

It is not like stoning Mary. It is like defending the stone pelter and acid attacker.

It is not crying thief… thief… thief against an innocent person. It is horrifying a gory evil man.

Yes we can go pointless to debate of Bhisma or Mary or someone else of you chosing. But that doesn’t change what Karna was unless of course you chose to write your fiction and make a hero or a victim out of it. There are many such fan fictions. But as per the authentic epic Karna was neither.

And with a fake narrative you can experience anything useful — neither fate nor fact nor any philosophy.

Karna wasn’t a victim. He was a perpetrator. And no philosophy of the world says that evil men don’t exist. And if there ever existed an evil man, Karna was one of them.

You are free to spawn any ideology around it.

Regards,

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 19, 2022 05:22 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Parshuram took up Bhishma’s tutorship, on the insistence of Ganga, who is a celestial being.

First things first. There is NO such reference. Second there is no reference that Parshurama taught only Brahmana.

Irrespective Parshurama taught Bhishma. Once again, like always, you argument is void of reference.

You said — If indeed there was no bias, there would have been no need for Karn to lie about his caste origins.

Once again, you have just theory without any backing reference. Karna was a wicked liar. He lied on several occasion. He needed an easy way out. He not only introduced himself just as any Brahmana but also a Bhargava Brahmana.

Actions of an evil is no proof of the bias from the sixth incarnation of Shri Vishnu. It is just a proof of a depraved wicked mind who invents excuse to strip a woman in public.

You said — Shri Ram is Maryada Purushottam incarnate of Shri Vishnu. No chance of caste bias.

But Shri Rama didn’t admit a Nishada to a gurukul. The Guru did. And that is a general reflection to the society not just to Shri Rama. You missed the point.

By the way eighth incarnation of same Rama and Vishnu (for they are same) was instrumental in killing both Ekalavya.

Also may I remind you, you are calling “Parshurama” bias who too is incarnation of same Vishnu. No chance of cast bias.

You said — If you observe, he was most suited to be the emperor of Hastinapur, after death of Pandu.

Once again, just an opinion. And there would have been many more people. But they were not sons of the King. Neither was Vidur. But he still got to be Prime Minister. And that says something about the society.

You said — Who would dare to oppose the de -facto king of Hastinapur. Dhritrashtra was always doing his son’s bidding. It was just an acceptance by royal clans due to the power of a mighty empire.

The same King couldn’t push his own son openly for being the KIng. He was afraid of social pressure. And there is a very clear reference in Jatugrih Parva to that effect. Unlike you, I don’t talk without valid reference.

So if King couldn’t go against public opinion and had to conspire and kill, he couldn’t have gone against public opinion about Karna. There is no reference that the society objected to his being the King. Kripacharaya didn’t object. Bhishma didn’t and the public didn’t.

You didn’t understand —“Daughter of a fisherwomen was both King and Rishi. No caste biase”

Satyavati. One of his son was Ved Vyas, Other two were the Kings of Hastianpur before they died.

You said — It was indeed a pity that such a fellow decides on who should get military education!!

Once again, just an opinion. You have low opinion of Drona. That’s your choice. But that doesn’t change the fact that both Karna and Eklavya vindicated his stand by proving that both were unworthy of millitary education.

Even Parshurama seconded his idea. Lord Krishna himself was instrumental in killing them both.

You seem to biased by you hatred for particular cast and that makes you ignore every reference and cling to unverifiable folklores.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 19, 2022 07:54 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

We are not talking about Napoleon and Hitler. Are we?

We are talking about Karna. And there is more than enough evidence that he was an evil.

What’s next? Would you bring that there is a certain confusion weather a rapist was good or not in his deeds? Weather a serial killer deserves to be looked with sympathy or not?

If you want to continue the debate, let us focus. Karna was evil. Both references and deeds prove that beyond doubt. He was a victim, there is not enough supporting evidence.

Your turn. Go search some more character and bring them on. And then think, what would I say? You guessed it — They are not Karna. And Karan was… (guess)

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Karna-and-Eklavya-were-denied-of-learning-weapons-because-they-were-low-born-answe
Creation time: Jan 19, 2022 07:57 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

The name of Indra is a superlative term in the epic. His weapon was one of the most potent weapon of the era.

So if something was lacking, it was surely not Indra’s credential or caliber. On the other hand not only the guru but even disciple need to be worthy and Karna had certainly proved himself other wise.

So what appears as the most probable, is the fact that Karna found a shortcut to earn the weapon that doesn’t require to learn. He knew learning is beyond his capability. As you can see, he doesn’t ask for Brahmsira. He asks for something automatic, something that works on Indra’s power and not his own.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-didnt-Karna-ask-Brahmashira-from-Lord-Indra-even-when-he-had-a-chance-Why-did-he-ask-weak-Vasa
Creation time: Jan 21, 2022 06:08 AM PST

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-couldn-t-drones-capture-Dharmraj-as-all-others-are-fighting-Abhimanu-Could-Pandavas-be-able-to
Creation time: Jan 21, 2022 07:31 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — I would like to request you to write or reply in short rather than replying in tons of paragraph.

I wish you followed your own advice. But sure. I will take care. And this is my concluding response as we are looping again.

You said about Lakshagriha — Your first reference which I saw of Krishna is in generalized form I have explained it in first reply. And Vaisampayana's statement doesn't even talks about conspiracy.

Only a very blind Karna fan would find no conspiracy in this statement —

When the Pandavas were not killed through all these means, open and hidden, since they were protected by fate and destiny, he16 consulted his advisers: Vrisha, 17 Duhshasana and the others. With Dhritarashtra’s consent, he had a house of lac built.

“House of lac” itself is conspiracy as houses are never made of inflamable material.

And only a Krishna hater would mistrust Krishna’s explicit statement —

O Arjuna! Whether it was the attempt to burn down your mother with her sons in the night, 646 or whatever Suyodhana attempted towards you in the course of the gambling match, the evil-souled Karna was the root of all that.

Here there is no question or confusion. The statement is very clear.

Interesting you doubt statements of Krishna and Vaisampanya based on your blind trust. You can wake a sleeping one not one who is pretending.

I will drop out of this debate. People who would come and read this discussion will understand the context and make their own opinion.

You said — You can answer that but you won't because, it would show the real image of so called great Arjuna who hasn't countered any great weapon.

See I told you, you won’t answer. You proved me right. Period.

You said about Draupadi’s swyamver — In reply to this can you please tell me why KM Ganguly and PC Roy didn't translated in the same way in which Bibek debroy did ?

KM GAnguly simply censored the name of Karna and included him in others. Read this statement —

'Ye Brahmanas, how can a Brahmana stripling unpractised in arms and weak in strength, string that bow which such celebrated Kshatriyas as Salya and others endued with might and accomplished in the science and practice of arms could not?

  • You can see he didn’t translate Karna as “to perform a task”.

And KMG also explicitly mentioned his failure, so there is no question of doubt —

And that bow which Rukma, Sunitha, Vakra, Radha's son, Duryodhana, Salya, and many other kings accomplished in the science and practice of arms, could not even with great exertion, string, Arjuna, the son of Indra, that foremost of all persons endued with energy and like unto the younger brother of Indra (Vishnu) in might, strung in the twinkling of an eye. And taking up the five arrows he shot the mark and caused it to fall down on the ground

Similarly even PC Roy didn’t translated “यत्कर्ण” as to perform the task. Please share authentic reference (not screenshot) and I will so you the details.

More important, not only Bibek Debroy translated it as such, the translation matches Gita Press and Southern recitations. You can also check Sri Rajgopalachari’s version.

Arjuna - Karna battle after Swyamver

You said — So first of all Arjuna was described as weakling Brahmana and the reason why Karna was stunned because it was a surprise from Arjuna who was looking weak. So it's obvious Karna compared him with Parshuram and others out of surprise but not by fear.

I understand you have zero knowledge of Sanskrit. But is you English also weak or are you just pretending? Two questions —

Was Arjuna looking weak?

Let me see how weak Arjuna was looking —

“
Seeing the valour in Arjuna’s arms, unparalleled on earth, Vaikartana Karna fought even more vigorously.
”

You must have a strange notion of weakness. A person who retreats scared was powerful and a person whose valour is mentioned unparalleled on earth was looking weak. Great understanding.

But even if you fail to understand English, how do you think Karna to be so stupid to compare a weakening opponent with Vishnu and Indra. Once again I have no further comments.

You said — Can you please provide me the details about that reference. Like Parva or chapter no.

Sure. Here is the reference — When Shalya was thus hurled down by Bhima and Karna scared away, all the other kings were frightened and surrounded Vrikodara.

— Swyamver Parva, Chapter 181

Gandharva war

You said — Karna ran because his army ran away and the reason why he ran was because there was no other prominent warior on Kauravas side while Arjuna was along with his three brothers. Who all are great wariors while Karna had Duryodhana who was not a good archer while Shakuni was not even a good warior.

Karna ran away because he was incapable of fighting (Karna’s own word).

Interesting Duryodhana was not a good archer didn’t flee. Karna who was a good archer fled. Guess being a good archer isn’t important. Being capable and brave is.

And finally you agreed All Pandavas were great warriors. None of them fled. Not even the twins. But Karna fled. And he said he was incapable of fighting. I don’t think, I need to add anything further.

Bhargava weapon

To my question — Are you sure? Is there any reference that Krishna thought Arjuna couldn’t counter Bhargava?

You Replied — Yes words of Arjuna confirms that.

WRONG ANSWER. I asked a proof for what Krishna thought and not what Arjuna said.

You know you have no answer and that is why you are presenting lousy argument. We both know you can’t provide valid reference.

Till you provide a direct reference, we agree you have none.

About Vijay Bow

You said — Yes Sanjaya described Karna's bow as ancient

Sanjay repeated what Karna said. He had no first hand knowledge.

You asked — Do you have any reference where it is said that Karna didn’t possessed Vijaya bow?

Yes. I have already shared the reference. Vaisampanya clearly mentions Vijaya bow of Indra, which is one of the three prominent bows, belonged to Rukmi. There is no confusion. And Karna was a known liar.

Defending Liar Karna, You said — Not telling anything isn't a lie. Karna's main aim was to kill Arjuna while death of Arjuna would lead them to victory.

Making some one believe something that is not true is a lie. And in the matters of war it is treason. But if you are looking forward for explicit lie of Karna here it is —

“
O tiger among kings! In an excellent fight, there is no doubt that I will kill the Pandavas alone, though the fame for this will accrue to Bhishma. O lord of men! You have appointed Bhishma your commander and the commander is credited with qualities, never the warriors. O king! I will never fight as long as Gangeya is alive. But once Bhishma has been slain, I will fight with all the maharathas.

“

This incident takes place after Karna had promised Kunti to spare the lives of four Pandavas. You see he promsies to kill Pandavas not just one Pandava. THIS IS AN EXPLICIT EXAMPLE OF HIS LIES TO DURYODHANA

You said — Ok so Krishna and Indra were not afraid because Arjuna also had a bow like that. So why afraid when you also have a powerful bow

Just your opinion. NO reference. In fact there is just no reference.

Since you continued to provide lousy argument on everything without providing a single reference, I end the debate from my side.

I won’t answer on this thread till you respond to my questions with valid references —

  • You find explicit references fake and no reference an evidence.
  • You find a explicit mention of unparalleled valor as weakness.
  • You find clearly mentioned “scared” as not scared
  • You find clear admission of “incapability” as capability.
  • You see references that don’t exist and ignore ones that exists.
  • You find Krishna a liar.

I can’t continue longer with such illogic. Reader of this thread will see clearly who is right here.

By not giving logical answer you have already conceded.

Regards,

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Jan 24, 2022 07:00 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Great. I said I don’t want to debate if you don’t have a reference. And we both know, you don’t have.

But now at least you have proof of treacherous Karna’s explicit lies to Duryodhana. We can’t say discussion was a complete waste. At least you uncovered on more proof about your favorite hero’s greatness.

So let us conclude.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/Todays-Misconception-Karna-failed-in-Draupadi-Swayamwara-Many-people-say-that-Karna-failed-in-stringing-the-bow-pre
Creation time: Jan 24, 2022 09:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for pointing out the typo. I corrected them.

Karna’s arrogance didn’t play any role on day 13 and as such it is irrelevant to the topic.

But even if we take that into account, I would still say, it had a small part. Krishna knew Karna’s oath. Had that not been the case killing Karna would have been a priority. And anyway he was no match to Arjuna

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-couldn-t-drones-capture-Dharmraj-as-all-others-are-fighting-Abhimanu-Could-Pandavas-be-able-to
Creation time: Jan 24, 2022 10:00 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Oh so long and so absurd answer full of arrogance. Do you copy and paste it every where? While it is full of hate speech it totally lacks merit. I wont have time to answer all your baseless point but I would point out a few to so their baselessness —

You said — "A satyagrahi will always wish to be killed by an attacker, but will not wish to kill anyone". Whereas even the smallest creature on earth fights for self-defense and tries to survive on the earth, the futility of Gandhiji's policy is easily conceivable.

The problem is you don’t understand human are created to be better than lowly insects. Only human, and I repeat, only humans are capable of non violence and compassion. But if you wanted to live like beast that is your call.

Gandhi may appear to be uneducated to you. But I can see you certainly don’t know Gandhi’s theory of non-violence. It is different from cowardice. It is one of the character strength which is a legacy of Lord Rama and Lord Krishna and few are capable to emulate it. Did you know even Rama and Krishna strived for peace before jumping for the war?

Now let me show your lack of understanding about Gandhi and his non-violence in your own words.

You said — The Congress and Mahatma Gandhi supported the British in the Second World War. As far as I know, no soldier on the battlefield comes to give a sweet gift to a soldier on the other side out of love, there is a reflection of violence. Surprisingly, our Mahatma Gandhi was a non-violent leader!

You contradicted yourself. Read your previous comment and the new one. First you blame Gandhi for supporting non-violence. Now you are blaming him to support violence. The problem is your lack of understanding about Gandhian philosophy which was closer to Shri Rama and Krishna. Gandhi supported India’s participation even in first world war, as he found it the easiest way for Indians to get trained in military's. He was a visionary. Had Indian not participated in world war I and II, Netaji won’t have soldiers to form INA and independent India won’t have a strong millatry from the day #1

Gandhi considered non violence greater than violence which was still preferable over cowardice. But you are so blinded by your hatred that you refuse to see the truth.

You said — Justice PB Chakraborty said — Clement Attlee Said —

Stop there. That is the classical definition of heresy. No proof. No record. No evidence. Just he said that some one said that…

Who was Clement Attlee. A one time Prime Minister of Britain who had to give up one of the finest colonies of British empire. Do you know what Winston Churchill said about him? What is his significance on the international stage or history? How long was his association with India? He came to power in 1945 long after the disappearance of Subhash Chandra Bose. How reliable do you think his words are?

Do you what Lord Mount Batten the man who saw Gandhi from close quarter said about him? Gandhi will go down in history at par with Gautam Buddha and Jeasus Christ.

Do you know what Rajendra Prasad said about Gandhiji? Was Rabindra Nath Tagore said about him? Do you think these people knew Gandhi less than Attlee?

You said — Attlee Said (heresy) — He told me, "Because of Netaji's military activities, a revolt against the British Raj is brewing between the Indian Army and Navy." They are no longer loyal.

Was Attlee unaware that Subhash Chandra Bose has died or disappeared? Was he unaware that INA had be disbanded and all members arrested?

You Attlee perhaps mistook the revolt against British Raj in Indian Army and Navy. Indian Army and Navy weren’t commanded by Netaji. Entire nation has pride and sympathy or INA and anger against British Raj. But the seeds of those revolt lied in civil disobedience. And that too didn’t uproot British rule. British government didn’t surrender before army or navy.

And did you know who fought and created mass movement in favor of captured INA soldier so that they needed to discharge them? It was Gandhi and his congress.

You said — So I wish a happy birthday to the forced father of the nation.

This line proves that you have a copy-paste text here.

But do you know who first called Gandhiji, the father of the nation? Subhash Chandra Bose. And unlike your Attlee thing, it is not a heresy. There is a historic evidence. Do you think Subhash Chandra Bose was a fool to call Gandhiji the father of the nation after he sidelined him from congress?

And you didn’t mention why Bose, decided to leave his presidency after a landslide victory? Perhaps if you read history you would understand Bose had defeated a Gandhi follower and not Gandhi himself.

You said — Gandhi on Bhagat Singh!

Stop. You know nothing of the episode. Gandhiji both in person and via official letter tried for Bhagat Singh’s release with none other than viceroy of India.

You have gathered an army of Gandhi critics. Here is my team of Gandhi followers. Can you match them —

  • Subhash Chandra Bose
  • Rabindra Nath Tagore
  • Dr Rajendra Prasad
  • Dalali Lama
  • Lord Mount Batten
  • Albert Eintien
  • Barak Obama
  • Martin Luthar King Jr
  • Narendra Modi
  • Acharya Shirram Sharma Shukla
  • Paramahansa
  • Steve Jobs
  • Millions of People around the world

You quoted Nirad C Chowdhary

Can you explain the credential of this man? What is his contribution to Indias’s freedom movement? Why should you trust him more than Subhash Chandra Bose, Dr Rajendra Prasad and Gurudev Rabindra Nath Tagore? Just because he said something that you liked? Or because he migrated away from India, made him an authority about India? Chose your gurus wisely.

Looks like a few references won’t really work. And heresy won’t replace the well documented international history.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-kille
Creation time: Jan 24, 2022 10:37 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

To add: There is no credible proof that Clement Atlee ever said that. There is no official record. It is jusy a heresy about one time Prime Minister of Britain who had limited knowledge of Indian subcontinent.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Is-it-true-that-Gandhi-contributed-minimally-to-the-independence-of-India-Was-Subash-Chandra-Bose
Creation time: Jan 25, 2022 01:31 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Such people are just spammers. No original thought. No understanding. Just learn a few names on Quora and start spamming. The irony is, they don’t realize that they contradict themselves.

I generally don’t remove such post. They let others see the absurdity of their arguments. Total hollowness and full of hate. You may disagree on somepoint, that is understandable. But how can you hate a universally liked and admired persona.

The secret is they don’t even have respect for people like Bose or Bhagat Singh. They never understood even their ideology. They just use them as stones to throw at Mahatma Gandhi. I pity them.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Mahatma-Gandhis-teaching-was-to-forgive-those-who-harm-you-or-attack-you-So-then-why-was-his-kille

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 26, 2022 04:02 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

If I have learnt one thing going Sanatan Dharm, it is the fact that human actions are not controlled as a puppet by the God, but by his three gunas, Sato,rajo, and tamo. And god is a legislator of this rule. The vishwaroop manifestation goes on to demonstrate that nothing escapes the gods mighty system.

Let us take the example of great Shri Ram. He was exiled. But It didn't write his destiny. it threw the challenges. How Shri Ram tackled the challenge, defined who he was. Same applies to Pandavas in vanwas. They had the price for their mistakes. But once in exile they repented, made the best use of their time, and even helped their worst enemy in their time of turmoil and peril. That defines a hero. What defines a hero is the fact that he goes beyond his mere selfish interest and egos. Now imagine Karna in that place. Had he been there, instead of Shri Ram or Pandava, he would have run away. This is not a mere speculation. We have a valid reference from the Epic itself.

We have debated earlier. You were, even then, trying to justify the evil. First You said, no one said Karna was evil. I gave you valid references. When the references couldn't be invalidated, you started providing irrelevant analogy. But nothing, and I repeat, nothing can prove otherwise.

I strongly recommend that one should take the difference between good and evil very seriously otherwise the society would break into chaos. If you fail to differentiate between the good and the evil then the entire epic loses worth.

Remember Krishna's mission —

paritranaya sadhunam

vinasaya ca duskrtam

dharma-samsthapanarthaya

sambhavami yuge yuge

Does this really tell you, not to take good and evil seriously?

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 26, 2022 04:29 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for your appreciation. That said, I would still disagree with you on a few points

You said— But we still have to realise all is a play of God and not take too literary that he was evil.

I am not sure, how do you suggest, not to take the words of God lightly? Krishna himself called him extremely evil.

You said — Real evil is that which hurts

Agree. Karna is evil even on this parameter. His followship is hurting our cultural heritage. It is projecting our epic in the wrong light.

You said — Satwa, Rajo and Tamo are also controlled by the divine will.

But Krishna says they are in our control of we get controlled by them. What if that is God's will? God created an highly evolved species and set it free to shape it's own destiny. It is like morden AI systems which often surprises it's own creator. And God interferes only for course correction.

On the other hand, if your theory is correct, then we shouldn't consider even most brutal murderer and criminals and rapists as evil, as it was God who orchestrated the rape. Extending the argument killing Ravana should be a wicked act of God — punishing someone else for executing God's plan. Is that what you want to say? I would urge you to reevaluate your theory.

You said — This is what Karna was, who thought his goodness came from Duryodhana hence in debt to him and not Krishna.

I wish it were true. I wish he had taken even that debt seriously and I may have appreciated him for his loyalty, if nothing else. But Karna fails even here —

  • He deserts the same benefactor, leaving him to perish of captured by Gandharvas.
  • He did talk about his debt to Krishna. He wasn't willing to not fight for the welfare of humanity. But he didn't fight for 10 days for his wounded who. Guess his ego was greater than both his loyalty and humanity.
  • He without any reason decided to spare the life of four Pandavas. No one had asked for it. Was it loyalty? Bhimasena was arch nemasis or Duryodhana. How sparing his life was loyalty. He made an exception for Arjuna— his own ego.
  • Worst, he never told Duryodhana about his own. Can you honestly say it was not reason?

There are fewer example of greater evil than Karna — wicked, treacherous, deceitful, lustrous, molester, conspirator, coward, deserter. You name the vices. If Karna was not an evil, perhaps no one else is or ever will be.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 28, 2022 11:01 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I won't debate on your analogy of comparing Karna with western civilization or culture. It is your theory and it does not define the Karna of the Epic Mahabharata.

But I would like to remind you of your own statement, a true evil is one that hurts. And by that parameter Karna an example of that true evil. Karna was not a good person and neither had he any sorry life. He was an evil monster deceitful and a traitor.

You said Karna intended good, now I fail to understand with what good intention did Karna ask the Duhsashan to disrobe Draupadi, and why did Krishna called him extremely evil soul?

You did not answer to the questions I asked you in my previous discussion. Do you consider God to be an evil for punishing Ravan and curl for his own misdeeds.

I again fail to see, because of a complete lack of evidence, how you concluded that can was a very good person and we treated him bad. You might believe whatever you want to, but we never failed because we were guilty of treating Karna badly. We didn't fail because we trusted Krishna's wisdom.

But I can assure you with certainty that we will fail badly if we don't stop the evil worshipping Mantra. Considering Karna as a good person is like endorsing all those rapist molesters and acid attackers. It is like handing over the gallantry awards to the escapists and deserters of a war. He attacked Draupadi during the swayamvar and tried to throw him back in the yagna fire because he failed to secure a religious that is is the textbook definition of modern acid attakers.

The only true reason I should feel sorry for Karna is the fact that he destroyed the immense potential of good he was born with and that is perhaps the greatest lesson one can derive from his story. That lineage and the Goodwill of the parent is no guarantee that you will become equally great and virtuous. your evil deeds will be your downfall.

Please do not unnecessarily glorify wicked and evil person.

If you want to continue with this debate it becomes your obligation to provide references to prove that Karna was a very good person who was treated badly by the society. Tell me how was he treated badly? Tell me why do you consider a wicked monster and a conspirator to be a very good person? Do you have a proof for it? Do you have a rational reasoning for it? Do you think an advance society should treat such kind of henious villains as a hero?

Unless you can provide some answers to explain yourself while staying in the premise of the Epic Mahabharata itself, and not digressing into analogies that have no valid rationale, I may not be inclined to continue this debate which continuously digresses without any rhyme or reason.

So far everything I have said in this discussion is endorsed by Krishna and by valid references from the Epic. So far you have not been able to negate any of my points. So far not been able to give any references to your claims that Karna was very good man who was treated badly. We can either conclude with this, or come up with valid responses and references.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 28, 2022 11:19 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

In your entire writing, you took care of steering clear and ignoring the two points request I made —

  1. Giving direct answer to my queries.
  2. Not digressing from the main topic.

This proves one point without reasonable doubt — you have no convincing argument supporting Karna to be anything but a worthless evil.

Now having mutually agreed to this, let me begin by thanking you for so generously offering me to use your theory in my book. However, I will not exploit your generosity. You see, I am not Karna. I don't steal.

Now let's debate on your theory. You are right to question who practices Sanatan Dharma today? You are right to point people like Mahatma Gandhi and Prithviraj Chauhan, and might I add Maharana Pratap can be compared to Arjuna, an adherent follower of sanatana Dharma. But honestly sorry hasn't changed as much as you want to believe. Let me explain.

Forget modern era, tell me how many Arjuna existed even then? Even that era was full of Duryodhana, Karna, Jaidradha and Jarasandh. There was just one just one Arjuna. Become powerfull like Karna was easy even then, you just need the art of boot-licking or cheating. Unscrupulous Karna became powerful even then. Arjun didn't get support from mute spectators even then. Even in that era path of Arjuna was no easy path. It was full of exile, apathy and hostility. He got lesser support from humans. But then he won. And he won, not because he rejected the night army of Krishna. He won despite he rejected the army. Because he chose Dharma. Had he not been installed in Dharma, he won't have been able to get Krishna's grace. Remember, without his explicit wish, Ashwatthama could lift Sudarshan chakra, although Krishna granted him the permission. But you think, this doesn't work in today's world. Let me show you, that it does.

First, I must point out Bush can't be compared to Karna. Duryodhana, may be, but not Karna. Karna was just a boot-licking stooge. His power was his flattery and willingness to stoop to any depth. A more accurate incarnation of Karna would be King Maan Singh, who retained and extended his power by becoming a subordinate to Akbar. He too faced the Arjuna of his era Maharana Pratap. Maharana, just like Arjuna faced exile and at one point appeared to be even vanquished. But ultimately he won. Akbar had to give up his quest to subdue him. He won back 32 of his 33 fort territory. And even if he had died without winning, he would still be our inspiration, like Chandrashekhar Azad and Bhagat Singh. We never seek inspiration from Maan Singh and Jaichand. We desist them.

Let me give you another example. Who had thought you could share the world with venture gesture of non violence? Who could have thought that it could cause an Empire, where sun never say, crumble? But it did happen. Isn't it? And the echo of his ideas lives in.

Arjuna doesn't come in hoards. People always sought shortcuts. Only few were capable of following Dharma. And those few always leave their mark. Always.

You call him Alexander. Tell me what's the legacy of Alexander? What did he achieve in life? Tormented civilization for his hollow ambition. Compare with Karna’s Digvijaya. But could be ever achieve his life goal of being world conqueror? He failed in his mission, just like Karna. And both died disgraceful hapless death, their ego and glory couldn't save them. What is the legacy of Alexander in modern time? Do you see any imprint on our society? Any society? Karna an Alexander are destined to die the same death. In every era.

The legacy of Karna is, he is the disease of every era. He represents the rotten mindset that you are entitled even if you are unworthy and unwilling to work hard to prove your worth. You can use deceitful ways, unethical means, and hope to control narrative. Karna is a poison that wishers in the ears of disranged youths, that if you can't secure a woman's love, rape her or throw acid on her!

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 29, 2022 11:39 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for finding my ideas inspiring.

Your answers, unfortunately, are highly prejudiced by a web of theories that you have woven with one central point — Karna is the base character for the start of western civilization. But this theory doesn’t hold good and I tried to show you in the previous answer as well.

You can’t compare Karna to Napoleon or George Bush. These people weren’t stooges. They didn’t prove their worth on charity or alms. They proved it on their merit. They weren’t awarded a divine armor for the sake of their mother whom they forsook. They weren’t given a Kingdom as a gift by a friend who was back stabbed. They fought battles to to build their nation the greatest one. Karna can’t be base of anything ‘civil’.

Next problem is, in order to justify your theory you need to mold the facts. Let me give you a few instances —

You asked — If Karna is the oldest son of Kunti, did we steal from Karna?

The answer is NO. Kunti didn’t steal anything from Karna. She gave her everything. A divine armor to protect him as long as he didn’t vacillate from Dharma. An opportunity like everyone else, to prove his worth to the world without any prejudice.

Had Kunti not separated Karna from herself, his life would have spiraled for the worst. Do you think Pandu would have married the mother of a child? Is there any such reference?

Two more child were abandoned by their biological mothers — Krishna and Krishna Dwayapan Ved Vyas. Did Ved Vyas became the King being Satyavati’s eldest son?

And if you think something was stolen, wasn’t it also restored? What Karna could have hoped to become being Kunti’s son? A King? His other sons had to struggle for the entire life to become one. Karna became a King without going through any hardship. All he had to prove was he could follow Dharma. He failed.

Karna’s debt was paid in full. No one stole anything from him. He was given more than what he deserved and you must have realized that by Shalya’s remark you quoted.

You said — The difference is Prithviraj struggled with Sanatana Dharma with Sanyogita continuously rejecting him.

Where did you get this? Sanyogita NEVER rejected Prithviraj.

This is what I was mentioning earlier. When you weave your own web of theories first and then put it to test, you will encounter strong obstacles and one way would be to invent more theories like the one above. But doesn’t that itself proves your theories don’t have foundation?

You said — This is why it is our turn we are Karna. As India in the modern day is being drained of its spiritual past, educated go abroad depriving India.

You may speak for yourself. I AM NOT KARNA. I am not a cheat. I am not a molester. I never betrayed my friend. I never humiliated a woman. I follow Dharma.

As far as spiritual draining is concerned, it’s not a problem of our era alone. There always have been more spiritually drained person than followers of Dharma. Karna had always been more popular because of his unethical adherence to self promotion with whatever shortcut necessary — flattery, deceit, or desertion. As I said, path of Arjuna wasn’t an easy on then. It won’t be easy.

But in every era Arjuna will destroy Karna. That is divine legacy.

You said — Hence the term came “all is fair in love and war”.

Know this term is not a mantra of any follower of Dharma. It is a term people use moment before they are left with no ethical argument to cover for their misdeeds. This is what an acid thrower must be thinking before expressing his supposed love.

Only fair is fair is love and war — Dharmao Rakshati Rakshitah!

You said — Like Mata Kunti and Indra dev in disguise of Brahmana asking Karna for something.

Let us take them one by one in the order of their visit —

Indra Dev — He indeed, came disguised. But the disguise was broken as Karna was well informed of his intended arrival. He indeed came to ask something. But to give or not to give or to give for free or to give for price was for Karna to decide. And Karna decided. He refused to give for free. He asked for an exchange. An exchange which pleased Karna. He was content and happy. Who are we to cry foul?

Take the incident from a different perspective. Karna was given a divine protection which was unequalled. So this action was to set a parity and to make the playing field even. What’s wrong here?

Mata Kunti — She was the one who had ensured divine protection for Karna. When she approaches Karna, she approaches with an offer — join the sides of Dharma. She didn’t ask her to spare the life of her son. That would be stupid. Karna had continuously lost at the hand of his two sons Arjuna and Bhimasena. Everyone including Karna knew with Krishna on Pandavas side, odds are against him and not Pandavas. So why did Kunti reached out? To protect his only son who was without protection of either Krishna nor Dharma.

Karna’s legacy is never becoming Bush, Brithishers or Napoleon. It is becoming Jaychanda, Maan Singh and Mir Jafar. It is becoming a deceitful cowardly and greedy creature who wouldn’t hesitate a bit before backstabbing his own family and friend for furthering his own greed and other selfish agenda. Nations and civilizations fall because of Karna, Mir Jafar and Maan Singh of every era.

People who use them may or may not rise, but character like Karna are destined to be stooges and never the master of model of the land.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Jan 29, 2022 08:56 PM PST
Content language: English

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-Awadya-गाँधी-अवध्य
Creation time: Jan 30, 2022 07:55 AM PST

Post: https://theindiannation.quora.com/Gandhi-Awadya-गाँधी-अवध्य
Creation time: Jan 30, 2022 07:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — First, you're trying to match Karna with Alexander and Napoleon.

Wrong. I don't compare corn with Napoleon or George Bush. You did those comparison. I just responded to your comparisons by trying to show the flaws and suggesting corrections. Karna was pure evil and can be compared only with Alexander in the modern era. But I am really happy that you finally realised the time gap between these people. This is exactly what I have been trying to explain you since the beginning. Your energy feels badly because you do not consider the time gap and simply creative web around your own ideas. In fact in the past I have requested you so many times not to digress from the main issue and don't bring in analogy which cannot stand the acid test for these characters. For example let me reiterate that Karna who was a marriage tools cannot be compared with a monarch like Napoleon or a powerful president like Bush.

You said — therefore all his actions become worldly.

I think you are a bit too sympathetic for karna. What you are trying to pass as a worldly act or action was actually a pure evil action of a criminal wicked deceitful molester. Since when body has become a synonym for evil? I have never considered being worldly or even being selfish to achieve your own comfort and luxury, as evil, as long as it does not involve you becoming a criminal of the last degree.

You said — about Prithviraj and Sanyogita

I am pretty sure you must have a very solid historical evidence to claim what you have in your post. I would love if you can share the exact references. I do don't work with romantic fictions and have my share of study in history and I haven't come across any valid reference for your claims. To me that you are fabbles unless of course you can provide verifiable references.

You said — This stuff is only enmity, vengeance, and not a molester like Ravana would be. This part you need to not take far.

I have no any enimity or vengeance against Karna. I am not trying to be politically correct here. There is no vengeance or animated because he was already avenged and punished by Arjun all those years ago. But yes extreme trust on the words of Krishna and genuine hate against levels like Karna, I do not see how you can call him lesser evil or a lesser molesters then Ravan. A monster is a molester, and there is no better or worse there

I stand on my point. I find it both incredulous and insulting to be compared with Karna. As mentioned by Krishna as mentioned by ved Vyas he was an extremely evil soul any suggestion that you may give with your wild and analogies, won't change that fact

You said— Symbolically answer to Karna is he is simply Arjuna without Brahmagyan.

I must disagree with you, once again. You don't really need Brahma Gyan to become good and lack of it does not make you an evil. Brahma Gyan aur not Arjun was a good man with a kind heart. On the other hand karna understood by now was an evil person. Brahma Gyan plays no role here.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Feb 01, 2022 07:42 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — I would not go off track with Karna as acid attacker etc… this is way off.

That is your choice. But references are clear. Karna, after having failed at Draupadi’s swyamver (authentic version) rushed along with other failed Kings to throw Draupadi in Yagna fire. Now tell me how is it different from acid attacker?

Now, you may chose to follow interpolated version where Draupadi rejected Karna. But that too doesn’t change the fact. A girl rejected a boy. He tried to kill her. That is the text book definition of acid attack.

Now please tell me how am I off track? Before you go on your wild expedition of Alexandre or Bush or Roman mythology, I would like you to answer this question in this context alone. Can you do that?

You said — And again your not including the time gap.

I think, you don’t read my answer as carefully as you should before say the the point again. I do consider the time gap and let me explain you once again this time gap so that you don’t have any confusion and you may not put same rhetoric question again.

Is it not you who is trying to ignore time gap while trying to make Karna a icon of west or modern civilization? You have no rationale and I pointed that out. A stooge can’t be compared to monarch irrespective of time gap. It shows you biase in comparision. Period.

Now let us see another angle. Neither you nor I belong to that time frame where Karna existed. So the most authentic version of Karna was known to the people of that era. Krishna called him evil. Ved Vyas called him trunk of evil tree. Parshuram called him wicked. Dronacharya called him wicked. Krispacharya and Bhisma called him boastful. Incident after incident it is referred that he was foul-mouthed wicked coward and conspirator. Now these are the people and authentic reference that coincides with the same time zone.

If you really wanted to consider time gap you won’t be running on wild goose chase. And if you want to consider time gap at least be reasonable and try to explain how can monarch like Napoleon be compared with side kick like Karna? You said so many things, but they are your views. There is no references.

Now you want to believe you alone understand time gap and others don’t. That is your wishful thinking.

If you are uncomfortable, I would switch to original point — let us focus on Karna of Mahabarata based on Mahabharata era. Can you do that?

You said — now it is very developed and you're trying to compare with Bush. Of course not like the president, because of different time gaps and you're not using this.

NO. I said that in previous answer. Let me repeat it again. I don’t compare Karna and Bush. I don’t find them comparable. It is you who started these absurd comparison. I showed you the flaw in your argument. Don’t put this comparison on me.

In every time gap there are few leaders and few followers. Karna was no leader. So time gap theory which you repeat doesn’t hold water.

You said — You're now on debate pointless without any explanation that can convince Karna is pure evil. You are just calling names. You have to be careful if logic lacks.

I am sure you didn’t read the text — neither mine nor Mahabharata if you still need explanation that Karna was pure evil. Let me give you pointers to prove my point —

  • From the childhood Karna carried a grudge against Pandava for no reason. He conspired to kill a fellow student who had no enmity with Karna. Does it qualify evil?
  • He wanted a powerful weapon to fight Arjuna in a battle. Arjuna had now shown any such enmity. Does that qualify as an evil?
  • He deceives his guru with a fake profile and was cursed for his deceit. Karna himself called this his ‘crime’. Interestingly after all this time gap such acts are still punishable crime under modern law. Can we call him a forger?
  • He was one of the conspirators in Lakshagrih consipiracy. This is confirmed with cross-references. The plan was to kill six unsuspecting person including a old lady. Still not evil by your definition?
  • He tried to burn down Draupadi by throwing her in Yagna fire for having failed to secure her hand. I wonder what more he would have done to qualify as an acid attacker?
  • He commanded Duhasashana to strip Draupadi in public. Duhasasana was the hand, Karna was the brain. This act is strongly condemned in the epic. After a time gap, what do you think the current law says such a person. I think a molester. What do you say?
  • For him seeing Draupadi in tree bark and in misery was a greater source of happiness that his own prosperity and that of his son. In modern times I would called such a mentality as depraved and sick and certainly not iconic. Do you see any other character with a similar mindset in the epic era? I guess Ravana or Keechaka. Can you suggest someone else?
  • When his plan back fired and Gandharvas attacked them, he fled, deserting his best friend and benefactor to die or get captured. I see Bhishma and Drona looking down at Karna for his desertions in that era. But you think, I don’t consider time gap. So tell me what would be a modern correlation of this event. What do you think Indian army would do with deserters? What do you think any army of the world would do? I think you love talking about timeless ideas.
  • Karna leaves his brother to die while he fled Arjuna and Abhimanyu on both occasions. Both his brothers paid the price. Killed. Do you think modern world is so thankless and and apathetic to their own family relationship. I would say you are mistaken.
  • Karna decides to opt out of the most crucial battle, of his so called best friends life, for first ten days. Reason? He ego. But I think this ungrateful attitude doesn’t count as evil to you!
  • Karna never informed Duryodhana about his secret pack with the enemy. In fact he made him believe that he would try to kill All Pandavas. This is and act of treachery. In Modern society such person would be tried for treason.
  • Krishna counted all his misdeeds and deemed him extremely evil souled. And it is not just about his words. You have a list of points.

And if you still don’t find the explanation sufficient, at least you would see who lacks the logic and who debate on illogic.

While we are talking about the illogic, let me show one example right now —

You said — you don’t mistake in real life seeing Rama is only an ascetic and does not deserve Sita.

See your own illogic clearly. When did I say I see Rama only as ascetic? When did I say he didn’t deserve Sita? It is just an illogical idea you coined, may be to create another diversion.

You said — Prithviraj - Yes it's all in the books but people don’t see the failed relationship

Exact reference please. All the books doesn’t qualify as reference. The history I read doesn’t include it.

You said — Can you reference where Karna is said he was an evil soul and then again he called a hero and charitable, Krishan tells Karna who his mother is?

Krishna called hi evil on multiple occasions. And being charitable and evil and not mutually exclusive. And act of charity can’t atone for the sins. Else every criminal would chose charity over punishment.

You said —Yet you're turning this character into a monster. His just simple on the wrong side.

You are simply turning a monster in to a right person on the wrong side. See my list including some of his crimes. A good person doesn’t try to burn down a woman, or a family or try to molest his on his own accord. A good person doesn’t betray his friend, family or his teacher. He wasn’t just on the wrong side — he was the trunk of everything that was wrong.

You said — so there would be vengeance against his foes the Pandavas. Not your vengeance.

I said, I don’t have vengeance. He has been avenged. I have hate and aversion against evil/

You said — You need Brahmagyan to stand firm with Truth and then comes dharma.

Here question was not stand firm with truth or against evil. You don’t need Brahmagyan not to abuse others or molest a woman.

Q U E S T I O N F O R YOU

(I will not be surprised if you don’t provide a straight answer without digressing)

  • On what parameter do you find Karna a good man other than charity? We all know many evil politicians who do regular donations.
  • Did he thwart any conspiracy to burn down innocent family?
  • What was contribution of Karna to society that qualifies him as a good man? Name any thing he did for society beyond his own selfish circle of his friend and family?
  • Did he protect any woman in distress when others were trying to burn or her down or molest her?
  • Did he protect any village or kingdom in distress from some demon of bunch of plunderers.

You say Karna was a good man. There must be something that justifies that adjective for him. Can you provide some reference from Mahabharata?

A humble request — Please do don’t start on western icon theory or Prithviraj or a new tale. Can you answer these questions?

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Feb 02, 2022 01:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I think by now you have understood You have no reference to prove —

  1. Karna had no good quality that you can refer.
  2. Any of his social contribution.

You just tried to mask this fact by again throwing up a lot of wild theory that even you know are irrelevant and baseless.

Your best defence in Karna’s favour — As a part of the crowd, although Karna had not only debated, but actually rushed trying to burn down Draupadi, but we should not judge him as criminal, because perhaps he was not as bad as Ravan. Is that how you judge a criminal?

Now, you also quoted several things with shows your biased based on popular pulp fictions rather than on true authentic references —

  1. You said — Karna did by the rules and Krishna said he did not shoot arrow at Arjuna after sunset.

Really? When did that happen? I mean, when did it happen before BR Chopra created an episode about it? For your reference it was Kauravas, on the day 14, who decided to extend the battle beyond the sunset and on that occasion you are karna I had no reservation on fighting the war on the sunset. At the end of the 16th day of the battle, where BR Chopra shows karna stopping the battle because of sunset is actually a fake incident shown. The reality is, that on 16th day, the entire Kaurava army along with karna their battle defeated by Arjun and they all fled together.

Now you can see the foundation of your belief more clearly.

2. You said— Simply he is blamed for not doing much wrong.Then being blamed and hated of course he would retaliate and create enemies.

Did you read my previous answer completely? I did quote several points each of which in itself should be sufficient ground to condemn en evil. Bank involved in plot of murder molestation deceit, desertions and treason. No tell me how much evil should a man do, in order to qualify AS evil as per your standard? Can you imagine of one Nation, that can thrive on the basis of traitors and criminals being prominent role models of the society? Do you know any Nation on the world, who idolizes their criminals? Name one Nation?

3. You said — Here then your picking the bad in Karna. Not getting to the root cause of why.

I gave you the opportunity to pick “good” Karna. But you failed. You couldn't point out anything good. Would you try one more time? Sincerely. Without trying to mask his failure with irrelevant digression.

Now, world you also take an attempt to explain the root cause, which you blame I tried to ignore? Again, a hint, answer from the epic not involve China or Greek mythology.

4 You said - He was simply not happy with inequality of that age (Caste).

This statement once again proves that you are not counting the age gap between today and the era we are talking. There is not a single reference of caste system mentioned anywhere in the entire epic of Mahabharata. Not one.

Can you quote me a single reference or incident that proves that karna was not happy with the inequality of that age? In fact if there were few promoters of inequality of any type Karna’s name is again higher on the rank. Karna announced that women are merely properties of their husbands. I don't recall any other person in the entire Epic using such kind of condemning word for anyone. It wasn't a biase against a single person who may have done you some harm. It was a generic misogynist thought, which has cross references in the way he talks to Shalya about madra women how to officers own wives I didn't wait to find out the whereabouts of Arjun during the battle.

I cannot say his views represented the views of the society as a large, because once again I don't find anyone else this is similar view. Contrast with Pandavas, who tried to protect the modesty of the Kauravas women during the Gandharva war forgetting the way I was treated them and their wife. Which kind of people would you like to see in the society abusers and molesters like karna on protectors like Pandavas?

You said — You talk of contribution to society! It is all this silly curses he is evil etc… and vengeance, it is narrow ideals of individual against individual.

You know I disagree with your views. I strongly believe a society must understand what is good and what is level who should be the role model and who should not be. Without this distinction we will surely be leading the life of beasts for animals, not a human society.

You said — He kept his word to his Mother Kunti he will not kill her other sons.

Have you considered for a moment why karna gives such a promise to Kunti? Kunti never asked karna to spare the life of his sons. Kunti asked him come on the sides of the Dharma, to prevent the war that was in no one's favour specifically not in the favour of his friend Duryodhan. Even Karna new this fact for sure. His action was pure selfish and it reason against his king and best friend Duryodhan.

You said — All the plotting were from Kauravas but the blame on Karna it seems the curse on him still exists.

Your observation is baseless on two grounds.

  1. No one exonarates Duryodhan from his evil deeds. He was unable person and he has been called a evil throughout the Epic. There are no two views about it.
  2. But when you say all the plotting were from Kauravas it is actually you who's trying to shield Karna from his own evil acts. Usse Duryodhan never deceived is Guru. Duryodhan didn't try to strip dropadi in public. Duryodhan was the mastermind of Ghosh Yatra. He was not the mastermind of of goharan. He never desserted or deceived his friend. Tell me whom I should be blaming for all these things?

Since the beginning of this debate I have been asking you for a few straight answers based on the references from the Epic Mahabharata itself. But you have chosen to ignore my request and continue to digress giving scenarios which are neither relevant nor have just justifiable reasoning behind them. The address represents your incoherent thoughts.

If I don't receive a proper answer for this answer, I will assume you are incapable of providing one, and wouldn't bother to respond back.

Regards

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive

Content:

No comments. You proved you are incapable of rationale discussion. You don't trusted valid references and claims to understand deeper meaning by analogy! Sorry to bust your myth. Nothing rationale can come if you base your arguments on fables. Any philosophy is a joke if it's foundation is a lie. Being a King was no licence for being evil.

As I said I won't debate if you know nothing about the core belief. You failed to provide any valid references either to prove your point or contradict mine. You couldn't even offer one reason to proof Karna was not evil. Not one.

So let's end here. There is no point in debating without valid references or reasoning.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-If-Arjun-is-the-conventional-hero-of-the-epic-do-you-agree-that-Karna-is-a-tragic-hero-answer-Vive
Creation time: Feb 06, 2022 08:46 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

See learning happens if there is a rationality behind it. And clearly we disagree. For you throwing a woman in fire is no big deal. Western thinking? I highly doubt it is even a human thinking.

How can you point anyone to right direction when you are not ready to talk about direction. instead of talking about left and right turns you are talking how you go to Mars, put a mirror on Jupiter and in that reflection search for a direction between two lanes in Delhi?

What actually qualifies as madness is to think western historians will give you a correct perspective of characters and we and our thousands years of studies qualifies to nothing.

But let me try to reason with you once again based on your answer —

You said — King v King battle compared it with King v Subject

There is no King vs King or King vs Subject when Karna tried to burn down Draupadi (and yes, it was a big deal). Karna was not the King of Panchal. He was an invited guest. And those Kings had no right to misinterpret the protocol and attack their host. The aggression was not a kingdom-vs-kingdom, but between frustrated losers and their humiliation by defeat. And this was personal. Not King vs King. And this was like an acid attack — destroy the object of your lust if you can’t have it. And so far you have failed to give one good reason, why it shouldn’t be called acid attack other than simply saying it is too far fetched. Do you call this discussion? You expect to people trust your irrational thinking just because you say so?

You said — When we know all men at one time have become angry with a woman and vice versa, so why call such acid attacker?

YES. And that is the main point. All men get angry with someone. But then they all don’t try to burn down the woman. They all don’t want to strip the woman in public. And those who do should be called acid attackers and molesters. Do you get the difference NOW? If you can’t understand this simple concept, you wont understand higher philosophy.

Before becoming philosopher or teacher, we need to become good humans. The biggest evil attached “Karna” is when people try to defend him, they too need to give up human values. They will be forced to consider attempt to burn down a women or treason to your own king and friend as minor offence. That is why we must condemn evil. It draws society towards it.

You said — your intention seems to be that like a political debate,

Actually you turned it into a debate. But you couldn’t stick even to that. Because debate too needs rationality and acceptable evidences. So far —

  • You haven’t given one convincible argument or evidence to prove Karna was a good human being. NOT ONE.
  • You didn’t answer any question directly. All you gave is analogies which were flawed and I pointed them everytime.

You blindly made assumptions that society harmed Karna rather than otherwise and so we all owe to Karna.

I think we can leave this thread here as it is and leave on the future readers to read and draw conclusions.
