Creation time: Feb 10, 2022 01:06 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Answer is simple. Modi is an admirer of Gandhiji.

Starting his first tenure he started preparing on the lines of Mahatma Gandhi and often expressed his views as such.

Moreover when one of his ‘cheap party worker’ praised Godsey, he categorically mentioned that he hates such mindset and may never forgive her.

In fact any rationale person would be his admirer.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-we-have-so-much-confusion-about-Mahatma-Gandhi-One-group-of-intellectual-say-Gandhi-was-perfec-1
Creation time: Feb 14, 2022 11:30 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I don’t double your capability to have multiple perspective. So let me give you one more — You comment is irrelevant to the question and my answer.

Question was —

  1. Was Kunti unaffectionate?
    1. NO. There are clear proofs and references
  2. Does Karna’s vow make him the best character?
    1. NO. It makes him boasting traitor. period.

Now let me try answering rest of your doubts.

You said — Dude… if Virgin Mary did the same with Jesus out of her love for him or to protest infamy or whatever, the world would have been poorer by an entire religion.

NOT TRUE. Jesus would still be Jesus. Just like Krishna still became God despite the fact that her mother, too, had to abandon him. Karna didn’t know her birth mother. That doesn’t mean he didn’t get a caring parent. It can’t be an excuse for his evil temperament.

And we are talking about a different culture from an era that predates Jesus by thousands of years. You must take that in account.

Can you point out what was an ideal scenario for Kunti and how that would have helped Karna?

By the way, did Virgin Mary have a husband before the birth of Jesus? Did he support her? Did Kunti have a husband before Karna’s birth to support her? Ask and you will get yet another perspective.

You said — People do what she did out of guilt. PERIOD. If you are proud of something, you don't treat it with disrespect.

Disagree. People often have do something called. That is known as tradeoff and not disrespecting. A soldiers dies for country. That doesn’t mean he doesn’t respect his life. Few things becomes bigger than ones own self.

A person choses to get his hand or leg amputed. Does that mean he is guilty or respects his leg lesser than his whole body? Neither. All it means is the person had no way of keeping his body being alive.

Kunti faced a similar situation. She couldn’t have kept Karna without bringing disgrace to his father, his family, herself and even Karna.

Had Kunti really disrespected Karna, she would have drowned her in river and he would have been dead. She won’t have asked for a divine protection of her child. She willed him to live.

You say you are capable of having multiple perspectives. Right? So why are you stuck up with the popular pulp fiction perspective that calls Kunti guilty. Why not try a broader perspective?

You said — Oh, and nobody gave a shit about her or her son like they gave to Devaki and Krishna.

Again WRONG. Does WRONG perspective also count as a perspective?

Krishna struggled his entire youth, not only protecting himself but his entire village. No body really cared for him. He didn’t even get a decent guru. He was left to fend for himself and his family. His family dotted on him.

On the other hand, Karna was grew up in a loving and prosperous family. He was so rich that he was called Vasusena. He became friend with the son of King and got the education under great gurus like Drona and Kripa.

I believe you are capable of multiple perspective. I wish you include a few authentic reference while building them.

You said — It wasn't a protest at all… it was patli gali pakad ke nikal jaana.

I couldn’t get the context here.

You said — Sun of Surya… who would have insulted her after listening to her story, if she mustered up the courage to speak up? (Back then it was even easier to get away with anything in the name of God.)

Oh No! He would still have been insulted. And no one would have married Kunti. He wouldn’t have got any recognition. That is for sure. There is not a single reference where unwed mother revealed his son’s identity. Satyavati never revealed the identity of Ved Vyas. Ved Vyas wasn’t given the right to throne.

Even other sons of Gods like Yudhishthira, Bhima or Arjuna didn’t get a easy life. They didnt’ become honorable because being sons of Dhrama or Indra. Their life wasn’t easier. They had to prove themselves at each stage of life. Everyday.

Did my answer added some new perspective?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Isnt-Karna-the-best-character-of-Mahabharata-because-he-vowed-to-spare-Pandavas-for-the-sake-of-Ku
Creation time: Feb 14, 2022 11:55 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I agree with you when you say that answers lies in Bhagwat Gita.

But I must disagree with you taking the basis of Bhawat Gita and the parent compilation that is Mahabharata when you say — The beauty of the epic is that it isn’t painting people as Good or Bad, Virtuous or Evil.

Bhagwat Gita never said there was no good or Bad. In fact it provided a detailed description of what constitute Good and what constitute bad. Had there been no good or bad, Krishna won’t need to say —

paritranaya sadhunam vinashaya cha dushkritam. dharma sansthapanarthaya sambhavami yuge yuge

To protect the good and to destroy the evil and to establish the Dharama I shall manifest again and again!

Now if you start from the beginning of Mahabahrata, it describes two trees and Karna was the trunk of evil tree ‘Duryodhana’. And the tree was evil as it’s root was mindless foolish Dhritrashtra.

When Krishna himself called Karna as extremely evil soul. It is hard to agree with your interpretation that Gita or Mahabharata suggest there was no good or evil. Can you quote a single shloka where it says there is no good or evil?

In absence of what is good or what is bad, the epic looses its essence.

Now coming to lesser significant mistakes in your answers —

You said — Karna noble as he maybe but was sitting through the process of unfair gambling, gleeful like a spiteful suitor at being once rejected as a suitor by Draupadi.

The basic assumption is wrong that Karna was noble. He was wicked, treacherous, conniving, coward and a molester. Not only Krishna called him extremely evil and consipirator, Parshurama, Drona, Bhishma, Dhirtrashtra, Sanjay everyone at points called him with valid references.

Also it may surprise you if you come out of modern fables, Draupadi never rejected Karna. Karna failed. At this is a conclusion not only from BORI as product of over 4 decades of research based on over a thousand manuscript, but all the available ancient scripts.

So Karna actually was spiteful not because he was rejected but because he failed as he failed on so many other occasions.

You said — The letter of the law or Dharma is about action they were responsible and not what aboutery or reasoning another inappropriate or wrong action.

Dharma is not law. You are taking a very shallow definition of Dharma. And it is never about raw action. It is about intention behind action. Dharma understands the difference between duty as solider vs crime as a barbarian. The act of Abhimanyu was heroic, the acts of Kauravas in Matasya were barbaric. Hope that helps.

The moment you remove intention, the action loses it’s meaning and Dharma is to seek meaning or the essence.

You said — Mahabharata is beyond this victory which is in the just the 9th Parva out of 18 Parva where the Mahabharata ends for most people. So, most people read till the end of the war, poetries, prose, stories, comics have been written, movies and TV serials made till the point the Great War is over, But it is only halfway through the great epic Mahabharata.

I agree when you say people should move beyond movies and TV series. You see our actions are same. We wish the same thing. But the reason is different.

I wish people see the true essence and authentic reference. I wish people read the ‘original’ epic to realize that —

  • Karna was no tragic hero.
  • Draupadi never rejected Karna
  • Karna never tried to prevent Vastraharana, He masterminded it.
  • Karna wasn’t averse to conspiracy. He was all in and not just as supporter but also as master mind in several.

Now time to disagree — If you think TV Series end with 9th Parva of the war, you are mistaken. You see, Mahabharata isn’t narrated in a straight forward manner. There are several flashbacks and several stories narrated in haphazard fashion. TV Shows, in essence, covers some of those stories also. Let me give you an example —

  • Bhishma’s battle with Amba isn’t available in Adi Parva but in Bhishma Vadha Parva although as per chronology it occurred much earlier
  • Parshuram’s curse to Karna which is depicted in every TV Show comes from Shanti Parva which is one of the later Parva’s you wish TVShows should show.
  • Swargamana is the last part and few TV Shows have ventured even in Musal Parva.

That said, I too wish a detailed work be done on this side too, but I am afraid the TVShows will distort even this part the way the did on the other part.

I really wish people stop defending villains on different pretext. The very act of such defense destroys the essence of the epic.

Regards,

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Isnt-Karna-the-best-character-of-Mahabharata-because-he-vowed-to-spare-Pandavas-for-the-sake-of-Ku
Creation time: Feb 25, 2022 03:29 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks Kavana,

for such a glaring review. Really appreciate it. I am happy that you liked the retelling. One of the core aspect for writing this narrative was search for the true reason why Krishna called Kurukshetra a Dharmyudh and why it was not just a family feud.

While being fiction, it was a solemn promise to myself that I won’t distort or destroy the core spirit of Mahabharata. The idea is highlight glorious Indian tradition and not mock it.

And I strongly believe such fictions are woven in to the core fabric of Indian Shruti Smriti tradition. You may read my views on this here — Should we reject all epic based fictions?

Thanks again for your review.

Regards,

Vivek

Content:

Agreed. This Karna is completely unknown and directly out of modern fantasy tales.

The known and authentic version of Mahabharata doesn’t have this Karna. The Vyas Karna was a wicked student who cheated his guru, had no credible achievement as a just King, had bartered his Kavacha and deserted his King, family and friend on more than one occasion.

Post: https://worldofsanatandharma.quora.com/The-unsung-hero-KARNA
Creation time: Feb 28, 2022 04:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You may have trust or respect for congress and Mamata?

I have no respect for either congress or Mamata. But I respect our ancient Hindu philosophy.

Why do you think, it is perfect? And how is it adequate? Can breaching an international boundary be justified? Can world afford another war? Have we done anything (forget adequate) to denounce such an activity? Can you please elaborate?

For a country eying to be a world leader supporting a country who is breaching other’s boundary in neither correct on humanitarian ground nor prudent.

Who will help India if tomorrow China does what Russia did to Ukraine. I can tell you who won’t. Russia.

We followed a similar strategy when China breached into Tibet. And what did we get in return? Let me give you a hint — 1961.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Hey-Shiva-Poetry
Creation time: Mar 01, 2022 05:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Well, I am aware of India’s association with Russia and historical ties. But then I also know Russia remained mute during 1961 conflict. But here I am not talking about selfish interest. It is not even about Russia or America or even Ukraine. It is about India and it’s responsibility towards humanity. If we want to remain ‘human’ silence is not an option. If we want to exist against such international border breach, once again silence is not an option.

And please don’t say Russia is not completely at fault. What a sovereign nation does in their country and whom it allies can’t be a justification for attacking it out of paranoia. You mean if I make friends with someone you don’t like, you get a right to barge in my house? Can you see, how ridiculous it sounds?

Tell me what happens if China does the same thing to India tomorrow by unilaterally declaring few Indian states independent and land their troop? You would still say they are not entirely wrong… Would you still say they did it because we became friends with USA or some Xyz?

Please reply. I would like to hear your opinoin.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Hey-Shiva-Poetry
Creation time: Mar 01, 2022 08:06 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Nothing in your long article explains how can we survive taking such an in-humanitarian stand? And you didn’t answer how Russia is NOT COMPLETELY WRONG?

How come we are so meek that we are afraid to even condemn something so blatantly wrong?

India’s decision is meek and selfish. There is nothing honorable in it.

I have been a BJP voter for as long as I remember. But for me India comes first and I can’t keep party over country. And current action has only shammed India.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/Hey-Shiva-Poetry
Creation time: Mar 03, 2022 12:36 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You don't try to search sun by lightning a torch. Gandhiji doesn't need endorsement from some Ambedkar.

Ambedkar wanted a separate electorate on the basis of caste. Gandhiji was against it. So who is casteist? One who divides in the name of caste or one who opposes it?

Gandhiji wanted an undivided, inclusive society not freebies that is divisive. He just didn't give them name, but tried to brings people together. But he gives freebie wins heart. Period.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Will-I-end-up-in-jail-if-I-refused-to-call-Gandhi-as-Mahatma-or-the-father-of-the-nation-answer-Vi
Creation time: Apr 17, 2022 01:44 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

People like Gandhiji don’t do lip service. His voice had the capacity to energize. His words had their impact. And one thing he never was, was insincere.

His movements didn’t distinguish caste. People who won’t sit together, worked together. He brought uniformity even through uniforms that Gandhians wore. The greatest service that could have been done was to create an all inclusive society which he was creating — where no one is belittled on the caste. And that was a long term vision. Alas! people never realized that the counter doctrine too belittled them.

Tell me what separate electorate or reservation did? Did it create an all inclusive India? Did it unite India? No It created further rift. You may say I don’t care if it served down trodden. But question is did it serve them all? No. Only a fraction got jobs in the name of reservation. Rest still suffer in same pain and poverty. This won’t have happened had Gandhian plan of all inclusivity was adopted than the divide and divide and rule which our is shamelessly followed even today by our current politicians, where benefits rulers alone and people become pawn.

You asked society was already divided. But don’t you agree it was further divided, with a wider cut and it harms them more whom it is supposed to benefit.

When you meet an accident and cut yourself a doctor is supposed to stitch your wound not rip it further. Gandhji was trying to bring together that society which was divided.

What I fail to see is how your question and follow-up relevant to the parent question?

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Will-I-end-up-in-jail-if-I-refused-to-call-Gandhi-as-Mahatma-or-the-father-of-the-nation-answer-Vi
Creation time: Apr 17, 2022 08:48 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Have you heard about nation building? Self respect? Holistic growth? If no, you won't understand.

He was giving them long term dignity through equality, where no one need to carry a badge of deprived class. He wanted a nation where people feel proud of being Indian. He wanted to give them a future of equality.

I can tell you, what he won't have provided. He won't have made anyone beggar.

The key differences — Ambedkar's policy was to favour individual at the cost of nation. What great nation would be created by someone with 10–15 percent marks and fast track promotion? In the long run, it helped no one. And the cost of such help was extremely high.

In Gandhi's vision, individual would grow together developing a powerful country. He did focus on individual empowerment, just not on the basis of birth.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Gandhi-oppose-separate-electorates-for-depressed-classes-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Apr 20, 2022 05:42 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I think Jaydradh himself would disagree with your statement.😊

I understand your point of view which is more based on modern legal system where punishment is based on crime and not on the psychology of the criminal. Indeed, ancient system was superior in this respect.

Killing Jaydradh would not have been a punishment. He would have attainted Veergati in a battle. Remember, even Duryodhana was glorified as he died in the battle field. Because people didn’t consider it as punishment for his crimes, but death in a battle field.

On the other hand vanquishing a King, making a public mockery and let him live was the worse punishment that could be given. Why else do you think, a wretched like Jaydradh performed such a penance for vengeance if he was indeed left un-punished?

During Gandharva battle, Duryodhana didn’t run. He was ready to die in the battle against Gandharvas following Kshatriya honor. What really humiliated him to the extent that he contemplated suicide? That he was freed by his worse enemy.

Now let us go back. Shri Rama offers Ravana a peace deal. Return Sita and Lanka won’t be harmed. Where was the punishment in that? First, Rama must have known that a King like Ravana would accept death over humiliation. And if he at all agreed, Ravana would be humilated, exposed, punished and Lanka would remain unharmed.

So you would find it as a recurring theme about the crime and the punishment.

You might ask why was Keechak killed then?

As I mentioned, punishments were for the criminals and not just flat on the basis crime. Keechak was not a King and hardly had a honor that would be wounded if he would be freed like Jaydradh. He was an animal that needed to be hunted down.

On seeing the reaction of Jaidradh, it is more than apparent, Jaidradh was punished. Harshly.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Do-you-think-Draupadi-should-have-taken-divorce-from-her-five-husbands-after-they-gambled-her-and
Creation time: Apr 26, 2022 07:16 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I agree with most of your point. But we must realize only fact can be fact. Opinions can’t replace facts

You said — There is a fact without Gandhi We would have got Independence Earlier. And I definitely agree with it.

It is not a fact. Just an opinion. Period.

You said — There's no way British Raj could have controllled 350 million Indians in their Own Home Soil. Even with Gandhi we could have Won Independence if he hadn't stopped the Civil disobedience Movement, after Chauri Chaura Incident.

Disagree. Britishers would have controlled it easily. They did control 1857. The reason non-cooperation (not civil disobedience) movement succeeded was it was non-violent executed masterfully by Mahatma Gandhi and Britisher’s had no idea how to control such a movement. Take a simple example — Even after a hundred years British government is apologistic about Jalianwala Bagh. Have you heard an apology for hanging Bhagat Singh or Ramprasad Bismil or for Chandrashekar Azad or for 1857? What is the reason?

In a gun fight, big gun wins. Almost always. And Brithisher’s had bigger gun. Recall what happened with INA. With a non-violent movement, they didn’t had righteous shield to use them. They were morally bound and the world was watching. With Chauri Chaura incident, it was set to change. Gandhiji was visionary. He protected Indian from going in the violent path and prevented British from exploiting the situation in their favor. Had the movement not be stopped, there would have been no civil disobedience, no Quit India.

Yes, I agree Britisher’s may still have left India. Before or after, can’t be stated as a fact. Just as projection. What can be stated as fact that if and wherever public becomes judge, jury and executioner, civic society collapses in a chaos.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/Generations-to-come-will-scarce-believe-such-a-one-as-this-ever-in-flesh-and-blood-walked-on-this-earth-Einstein-http
Creation time: May 20, 2022 11:22 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No. I read the full answer and as I said I agreed with MOST part of the answer and liked the view on internal and external freedom as well. I completely agree on all those points.

What I disagreed on is the absolute notion that even external freedom could have been achieved had Gandhiji not aborted non-cooperation movement. I believe it was almost impossible. Chauri Chaura had given Britishers a moral ground to deploy force something that was diffused by brilliant move by Mahatma Gandhi. With muscle twisting it was difficult to achieve freedom.

There is a problem when we assert —

“There's no way British Raj could have controlled 350 million Indians in their Own Home Soil.”

The problem, is they were controlling those 350 million Indians before non-cooperation movement and continued to do after that. Not everyone agreed to Mahatma Gandhi. Many adopted the path of revolution. Did we get our Independence? Tell me, how did Britishers continued to controller 350 millions even if one man decided to call of non-cooperation? Why didn’t 350 million took charge? Because 350 million individuals are still individuals till they are galvanized as one single entity. One country. One Leader. Without that one leader they were still individuals.

How is it that a few lions in jungle control thousands of deer in their home soil? How do you think that 350 millions would have worked against the government together without a proper direction and guidance. It doesn’t work that way. And pray to God it doesn’t work that way, because even if 1% decide to rebel against government there way, it leads to militancy and chaos.

Finally when we quote the example of a failed state like Afghanistan, we must realize that even that external freedom (forget internal) wasn’t possible for them till their colony ruler (USA) allowed them to form a rule.

It is simple. Even with colony rulers, they would have bigger guns. An army. The entire government mechanism and resources. Legislation. Just or unjust, they represent the government. The only possible way was to make them realize that governing is no more feasible and let them withdraw.

Another thing many people say and I disagree is — XYZ got Independence without Gandhi.

Are we sure, Indian freedom movement has no role in XYZ countries freedom? Gandhiji wasn’t just a political personality. As a politician he held no post in last decade or more of his life. In that case we may also say even India got freedom without him. But Gandhiji was beyond a political personality. He was an idea, a notion, I may say a virus attacking the core aspects of colony, making it’s existence extremely painful for the colonizer. You don’t need physical Gandhi once you adopted his ideals in your life. Why do you think Subhash Chandra Bose called Gandhiji as the father of the nation? Why is Nelson Mandela called Black Gandhi? Was his surname Gandhi?

Why did Ho Chi Minn, the father of the nation of Vietnam said — "I and others may be revolutionaries, but we are disciples of Mahatma Gandhi, directly or indirectly, nothing more nothing less"

Did Gandhi take his class as a professor in some college.

A little reflection and we can understand the role of Gandhiji even beyond the political boundry of India and that is one reason his name still resonates as the identity of India.

Hope that helps. I have had no indention to condemn the beautiful post you shared. I just wanted to augment it with my views.

Creation time: Jun 01, 2022 06:37 AM PDT

Post Comment

Content:

Oh!

And here I thought, we were trying to free India from British and their divisive politics and creating the constitution of a new democratic free India, eyeing towards future and not the past.

And I never said, Ambedkar was the original author of the idea of reservation. No, there wasn't anything original about him. But he was a strong proponent of the idea and strong handed the idea of reservation in our newly formed constitution, continuing the British divide and rule policy which is so effectively utilised even by our contemporary politicians.

And before you rob Ambedkar of his that only glory that keeps him relevant today, you may want to check out the speech of Ambedkar in the parliament after successful completion of the compilation of Indian constitution under the leadership of Dr Rajendra Prasad.

Post: https://unofficialgandhi.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Gandhi-oppose-separate-electorates-for-depressed-classes-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jun 03, 2022 09:00 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You used the right words — controversy.

And controversy is propagated by the help of half truths, distorted statements and taking the advantage of population who has stopped reading books and is content to be happy with social propaganda.

You said — His mute silence on hanging of Bhagat Singh?

Tell me, when you said this, should I consider you ignorant or dishonest? Gandhiji appealed to the viceroy for Bhagat Singh both in person and by official letter authentic copy of this correspondence still exists.

If you are ignorant of the fact, you proved my point about controversy. If you knew and suppressed the fact, you still prove me right about dishonest distortions.

But let me humour you and for a moment take your statement, Gandhiji was silent. Tell me, why was it Gandhiji's duty to protect Bhagat Singh? Bhagat Singh following Mahatma Gandhi order assembly hall? Why was he expected to protect somebody books recently goes against his ideology and in turn jeopardizing his own policies. Also let me ask you why you should blame only Mahatma Gandhi for not trying to save Bhagat Singh. Why don't you put the same question for the other leaders like Subhash Chandra Bose or sardar Vallabhbhai Patel?

The fact is you trust Mahatma Gandhi more than I do. You consider him a God capable of overt turning British ruling against Bhagat Singh. Had Gandhiji had such a profound fluence on British policies we would have been freed decades ago.

I would also have tried to explain you the significance of Gandhi's statement to which you alluded, but I am sure with your biased mindset you are not going to understand either Gandhian thought or statement which was meant to curtail the right and not to put the in the fire.

What your social media may not tell you is all the leaders it was only Gandhi who tried to go to Pakistan to prevent the Hindu genocide. No so called Hindu leader dare to do that.

Content:

And I am proud to be as Andhabhakta as Subhash Chandra Bose was, who named him ‘The Father of the Nation’ or Dr. Rajendra Prasad who compared him with ‘Ganga’ or Lord Mount Batten who compared him Buddha or Jesus Christ or other great world leaders.

But since you came to me, I would still try to help you. And today’s lesson for you — “calling something as fact” doesn’t make it a fact. Calling Gandhi as British stooge shows your ignorance. It doesn’t become fact.

Creation time: Jun 25, 2022 06:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Nice approach.

Your explanation into why Gandhari couldn't blame those who alone were to be squarely blamed shows Gandhari wasn't thinking rationally.

As per her thought process, the only way Krishna could have prevented the war was to go to Pandavs and asked them to shut up. Because she knew his son or brother or husband who are hell bent on war would never buzz.

Now I think Krishna essentially rejected the solution. Sometimes it is better to be exiled or cursed or suffer than succum to injustice and psudo peace. Even if Pandavs had agreed to peace without getting nothing, can anyone be certain there won't be another Ghosha yatra or Matasya expedition? No peace without justice is illusion.

And I am sure Krishha would have done the same even if he knew (and all knowing Krishna must have known) Gandhari would curse him, he still would have chosen the same.

As I see Gandhari's curse was driven by tragedy alone without a shred of rationality in it.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Was-Karna-killed-when-he-was-weaponless-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 05, 2022 07:46 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Why are you so abusive? Do as a Karna fan you feel compelled to be be abusive like him? Couldn't you be a bit polite?

You said — Karna was the best desciple of Parshuram.

Can you quote any reference where it is mentioned? Why did Parshurama called Arjuna better than every other warrior in Kuru forces?

If you can't show a direct prove it would prove who has half baked knowledge. Isn't it?

You said -Karna faced injustice all his life .

Can you point out, what injustice did Karna face all his life? Being trained by best gurus? Becoming King of a Kingdom that wasn't his?

Karna himself called his life fortunate. He led is life in lust and luxury.

Are you sure you read Mahabharata?

You said — but still went on to become best warrior in Mahabharata

Who said that? Why did that best warrior ran away from Arjuna in Swyamvar battle? Or Matasya battle? Or from Gandharvas?

Why didn't no one found him best including he himself? Why did he say Arjuna is best? Why did Parshurama say that? Why Bhishma or Drona or Ashwatthama? Why Shiva called Arjuna the best?

Why did that best warrior couldn't win a Single time against Arjuna? Why did Shalya had to save his life from Bhima? Why couldn't he stand against Abhimanyu? Why did he flee when his brother was killed by Abhiamnyu?

You said -If karna had bow in his hand it would have been impossible to kill karna

Did Karna have the Vijay bow when Arjuna killed his son infront of his eyes? What did he do? What did he do in Matasya battle when Arjuna killed his brother? He fled. Yes, it was impossible to kill a coward who runs away.

You said — Go and study some real book of epic Mahabharata and come back kid

Great advice. Did you ever follow it yourself? My signature would reveal What real books I have read. Can you tell which one have you read? Your comment not only lacks politeness but also lacks substance and is based on folklores and not authentic version. People reading authentic versions don't say Karna died unarmed. They have read the actual account. Yes TV show viewers do carry this misinformed view.

And please be civil next time you respond.

Creation time: Aug 20, 2022 08:35 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I don't think my words could convince you if the words of Drona, Bhishma, Parshuram, Krishna and even Shiva failed to make you believe, Arjuna was unparalleled. How could I hope that you would believe me, if you don't believe Matasya battle? Or Kurukshetra?

But I would recommend you to read the valid references to before giving your remarks on the battle with Babruvahana. Maybe you see the truth.

Post: https://legendsofsanatandharma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Agreed-Pandavas-could-not-have-killed-Bhishma-Drona-etc-without-tricks-or-without-breaking-any-law
Creation time: Aug 20, 2022 08:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Let me agree with you for a minute.

People are destined to do what they did. But that makes them heroes and Villains. What Karna did was no different than what Ravana did, or Hiranyakashyarp did. They became the the curse of humanity.

Now let me disagree with you…

People are not predestined to be evil. Everyone is born by the grace of God, not some saitan. But God doesn't micromanage them. The attribute that controls them is their own gunas Sato, Rajo, and Tamo. That is what Krishna said.

Karna was born with same potential as anyone else, perhaps greater potential — son of Surya, the light and life giver, protected by divine armour. But his guna caused his doom. Shiva didn't will him to die when he was born. He survived in river even as an infant. So, why did he finally chose that he must die?

If you blame Shiva or destiny for Karna's failure, you miss the most valuable lesson of the epic — You control your destiny, not your lust. When you control your lust, you become a hero, when you get controlled you fall. That is Krishna's karmayog.

But, If you think everything is predestined there is no lesson and you can chose to do despicable things hiding yourself behind destiny.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-was-the-reason-Karna-did-not-use-his-divine-weapon-during-the-war-especially-against-Arjuna-a
Creation time: Aug 21, 2022 12:18 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You haven't read BORI CE then. Karna had worshipped the snake for years and he knowingly fired the it. Here is a direct BORI CE reference

Shalya! Karna will not affix a second arrow. Someone like me does not engage in deceit. Having said this. 5 he released that arrow, the serpent which he had worshipped for many years .He said, O Phalguna! You have been slain, and swiftly shot the arrow On seeing that Karna had affixed the serpent, Madhava, supreme among strong ones, used his strength to press down on the chariot with his feet The chariot sank down on the ground and the horses sank down on their knees.

—BORI CE

And you are right Karna didn't run away from Bhargavastra. He got tired of holding it and failed to kill even a single Maharathi. But Karna did run away multiple times from Arjuna, Bhima, Abhimanyu, Gandharvas. Oh thats a long list. But then you have to read authentic epic.

And if you do, you won't be able to say Karna didn't know about the snake.

Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No. On multiple occasions, he ran away. Do not misquote. He fled scared during swyamvar battle. No charioteer. He jumped off and ran away during Gandharva battle. Not his charioteer. He fled twice in matasya battle. He fled Arjuna on Ashwatthama chariot on 14th day.

He was an interior warrior who also fled Bhima, Abhimanyu etc.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-was-the-reason-Karna-did-not-use-his-divine-weapon-during-the-war-especially-against-Arjuna-a
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 05:51 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Please tell me, Which version do you want to refer? Karna failed in every single version. Show me the reference you want to use. Can you provide a reference where Krishna asked Draupadi to refuse? Please come back with valid reference before calling someone WRONG.

By the way critical edition is NOT THE SHORT VERSION. ITS CRITICAL VERSION. It removed baseless interpolation that makes it shorter.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Did-Draupadi-reject-Karna-because-he-was-the-son-of-a-charioteer-Or-was-it-because-he-was-Duryodha
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 05:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You mentioned source BORI CE. But you didn’t quote anything. That is not called source. Let me give you an example of how you quote —

Karna is evil-minded and extremely injurious towards the Pandavas. O best of men! Kill him today with your sharp arrows. This is your supreme task and there is nothing wrong in it.

— source bori CE

You said — Karn used bhargavastra onced and firing divyastra on footsoldier is not wrong and not against the rule as per mahabharath

Did you read anything in BORI CE? Did you see what Drona said about Divyastra? What Indra said? What Shiva said? If you had really read you won’t be talking about this. Hint: Check what Shiva said to Arjuna while granting his Pashupata. Check what Drona said while granting Arjuna Brahmashira and what Indra said to Karna while granting Vasvi Shakti.

You said — But your coward arjun instead of saving them run away from bhargavastra for save his life

Can you quote from BORI CE, that Arjuna run away like “coward”? Is it mentioned? That he ran away? That he was a coward? Did you read anything about Divyastra?

Ok, let us assume Arjuna ran away. Why didn’t Karna won the battle? Why the weapon didn’t kill any famous warrior? Why did Krishna said it will tire Karna? Why was he not worried about Bharavastra the way he was about Vasvi Shakti?

You said — And still you ask if karn have many wepons then why he didn't use qt war

No I dont’ ask this question. Karna did use all his weapons in the war. Every single of them. He used them all against Ghatotkatch before using Vasvi Shakti.

You said — And for your kind information Arjuna's chariot is blasted in ashesh by karna's brahamastra

So was Brahmastra powerful enough just to burn the chariot? Krishna doesn’t give credit to Karna alone but also Drona and Bhishma. Now tell me this, was Krishna sitting day in and day out on the chariot for 18 days. Didn’t he get off during the night? Not to pay his last respect to fallen warriors like Abhimanyu? Why didn’t it burn to ashes then? And so what if it burnt to ashes? Arjuna had been through fire before. Right? And he was a warrior who didn’t need chariot to fight. When his horses got tired, I don’t see him begging other warriors to stop. Did you?

You said —You arjun did not succed to counter Karna's first brahamastra he invoked in fight but shree krishna postponed effect of brahamastra other wise your arjuna was dead.

Yes Brahmastras are powerful and divine. There is no shame in not countering it. The shame is in being evil. Shame is in trying to strip as woman in public. The shame is trying to burn down a bride.

By the way, tell me this why do you hate Arjuna? Do you know what Krishna said about Arjuna haters like you? Refer BORI CE.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-was-the-reason-Karna-did-not-use-his-divine-weapon-during-the-war-especially-against-Arjuna-a
Creation time: Sep 30, 2022 08:51 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Here is what Karna said before Matasya Battle —

I have obtained weapons from Jamadagni’s son, supreme among rishis. 92 Using their power, I can fight with Vasava himself. With an arrow, 93 I will kill the monkey that is stationed on the top of his standard. It will fall down on the ground today, uttering a terrible roar. The other beings who are stationed on the enemy’s flag will be tormented. Oppressed by me, they will fly in all the directions and their wails will rise up into the sky. Today I will uproot with its roots the stake that has been impaled in Duryodhana’s heart for a long time. I will bring down Bibhatsu from his chariot.

You are right, one of us has read Mahabharata like Gandhari. But then only one of us is a fan of Gandhari’s son and his extremely evil and wicked friend. And we both know who is that.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-was-the-reason-Karna-did-not-use-his-divine-weapon-during-the-war-especially-against-Arjuna-a

Content:

Post Comment

Content:

Seeking truth should we welcomed. But slandering isn't. Did you read the question?

Second part has absolute conviction that Kunti was WRONG and merciless and she “dumped” his son. Tell me, do you find that as the tone of a truth seeker? Is the question trying to seek the truth about the acts of Kunti? Which truth seeker are you talking about?

Questioner isn't seeking the truth about Kunti or reasons for her action. No they don't matter.

The questioner has already condemned Kunti and is expecting harshest punishment for her! Is seeking punishment with prejudice same as seeking truth? This question is trying to condemn not only Kunti but also our society for not condemning Kunti. Condemn Kunti, not for what she had done but because of the biase of the questioner.

And in your unbiased opinion, you don't see flaw in the question but in my criticism of a abuser?

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-wasnt-Kunti-given-punishment-for-dumping-an-infant-Karna-whereas-Ashwathama-was-given-punishme-2
Creation time: Oct 20, 2022 06:49 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Well that is what TV shows make you believe. But fact as per authentic epic is different. As per authentic references, these are the facts.

  1. Kunti never wanted to concieve. Surya forced her threatening dire consequences not for her but for her entire family. She yielded.
  2. She yielded but not before forcing her terms on Surya that her son should have divine protection. She yielded making sure her son has protection and you want to believe that she had no role in divine gift! Fact is she was the prime reason for the same.
  3. If she wanted Karna dead there were easier ways known to people even in that era — poison, drawing in river etc. You don't stuff the box with wealth to kill people. You don't ensure divine protection for people you don't care. You don't put people to guard someone you don't care. Kunti did all that.
  4. No one. I repeat no one ever called Karna Shudra. He was called Suta, offspring of Kshatriya-Brahmana union.Karna himself explained this to Parshurama.
  5. It is a wrongly propagated notion that Bhishma didn't allow Karna to participate in the war. Karna declined to fight under Bhishma command. He didn't want his glory taken away. He resented Bhishma's command.

I would recommend do read the authentic epic sometime. You would find it more interesting and informative.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-wasnt-Kunti-given-punishment-for-dumping-an-infant-Karna-whereas-Ashwathama-was-given-punishme-2
Creation time: Oct 20, 2022 07:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Thanks. I also hope you agree.

Post: https://epicfictionsfolktales.quora.com/I-am-happy-to-announce-that-my-book-Naimisha-Gods-Own-Story-is-published-and-is-now-available-with-Amazon-and-Flipcar
Creation time: Feb 25, 2023 07:11 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I have had the opportunity to read this book and must say that the book is detailed. Story so far is more in line with Bhagwatam but is surely turning towards Mahbahrata.

While Mr Rao may disclaim it as work of fiction and have certainly added a few narratives that are not referred in the epic, it doesn't betray the core philosophy and remain close to the epic. And divinations, where exists, too doesn't betray the essence.

Coming to fictional characters, who are we to say, they didn't exist. They had no direct impact on the epic so Vyas had no reason to include them. We may not make headlines in some newspaper, but that doesn't make our life less valuable.

The discussion around Vedas, specially atharva veda and debate on sakara and nirakara form of God can be considered highlight of this book.

Go and read it

Post: https://epicfictionsfolktales.quora.com/Dear-friends-You-are-aware-that-I-wrote-a-mythological-fiction-cimbiling-both-Bhagavatha-and-Mahabharata-The-book-was
Creation time: Apr 09, 2023 10:37 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No. Draupadi didn't appeal to all men. She didn't appeal to Duryodhana or Duhshashaha or Shakuni. She appealed only to men of honour, apart from the King of Hastinapur.

And the swayamvar incident you mentioned is acknowledged interpolation of Mahabharata. You don't feel guilty for what never happened.

She didn't ask her help, as she knew, he was the one who had commanded her disrobing. And perhaps, she may also have recalled he was the one who had attempted to kill her during the swayamvar.

Post: https://ramayanandmahabharata.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Karna-keep-quiet-when-Draupadi-was-being-disrobed-answer-Surabhi-Maheshwari-14
Creation time: Apr 20, 2023 02:21 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks sir,

For such a nice and detailed review of my work.

You said — If the name reminds you Ramacharitamanas, you are thinking like me, but it is actually an abbreviation for Mahabharat Nyaya Samiti.

Good Point. The name is actually meant to remind reader of Ram Charit Manas. And the beauty of the language is it each word can have several meaning that can all be relevant at once.

If we talk about Ram Charit Manas, the word Manas refers to Mansarovar and the entire title implies the great resevoir (or treasure) of Ramas great deeds. But Manas can also represent “within mind (or thought)” thus implying the character of Ram as visualized.

The main reason I used the title as “मानस” is because questions raised herein represents current mindset of the people (मानस) raising the contemporary question and accusations. The answer, however, is closely based on authentic references. I am sure you may have concluded the same.

So, Indeed, Manas is an attempt to connect it with Ram Charit Manas.

I would appeal to my other quoran family members to read the book and share your reviews.

The book is available on

https://vivek.vnc.in/get-manas

Content:

Few points –

3. Karna refused to fight under Bhisma’s command, so he couldn't have helped Bhishma.

4. Karna's formidable abilities were repeatedly evident as he continued to flee battles after battle —Draupadi’s swyamvar, Gandharva battle, Matasya battle and almost every single day at Kurukshetra.

5. Was Karna really loyal? Why did he not fight for first 10 days? Why did he desert Duryodhana during Ghosha battle? Why did he not inform Duryodhana that he won't kill his arch nemesis?

There is a lot of modern retelling that clouds people's judgement in favour of this over glorified character.

Post: https://mahabharat18.quora.com/What-if-Karna-had-not-joined-the-Kaurava-army-If-Karna-had-not-joined-the-Kaurava-army-in-the-Hindu-epic-the-Mahabhar
Creation time: Jul 07, 2023 07:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Thanks for your comment. But there are too many ifs.

First things first, Arjuna wasn't sent on any mission by Pandavas team. It was Kauravas plan to remove him from the scene so that they can capture Yudhishthira. There is no if or supposition here.

Second, no one on either side knew that Abhimanyu had the knowledge of Chakravyuh. Drona miscalculated and that miscalculation costed them dearly. They paid much heavier price even on the 13th day with Abhimanyu slaughtering Duryodhana’s son, Karna brother, Shalya’s brother, Bhoja… long list.

If You want to consider that Abhimanyu was sacrificed to ensure Pandavas should win, then the question is who deviced this plan?

  • Pandavas? Without consulting Arjuna and Krishna?
    • Do you think Yudhishthira would have done such treacherous deed against Arjuna?
  • Was it Krishna without consulting Arjuna? Arjuna himself?
    • Do you think Krishna cheated Arjuna, his best friend and his own sister?
    • Krishna was worried that Arjuna got furious and took an nearly impossible oath of Killing Jarasandh. Why would he be worried if it were part of his plan? Did he miscalculate? Was he a fool?
  • Was Arjuna aware of the conspiracy against his own son?
    • Arjuna who didn't agree to be a part of conspiracy against Drona, could he have agreed to the killing of his own son? Can such a cold blooded murderer fight so passionately against enemy knowing he himself engineered killing his son?
  • Was it Drona who planned Abhimanyu’s killing to destroy his own army? Was he a traitor?
    • Abhimanyu did great carnage to Kauravas army and Drona himself lost all hopes of winning against him. Don't you think, it would be a foolish thing to self destruct your army.
    • If he really wanted to self destruct, why did he kill Drupada and Virata?

Did I miss anyone? Please suggest. And tell me this if any of them planned such a henious backstabbing, were they really defending Dharma?

No this theory doesn't make sense…

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-What-would-have-been-the-result-of-the-war-if-Abhimanyu-had-not-died-in-the-Chakravyuh-due-to-unri
Creation time: Jul 14, 2023 07:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Post: https://vivekduttamishra.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Who-is-the-owner-of-the-Vijaya-bow-after-Guru-Parshuram-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Jul 16, 2023 08:41 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

What is really hypocritic is to quote according to your convinience. If you don’t belive Agni asked him to burn Khandava, then why should you belive that Khandava was ever burnt? Is this any less hyporcritical?

Arjuna didn’t say Agni asked him to burn the Khandava. Ved Vyas narrated it. And there was no reason for Ved Vyas to be politically correct. If he had to be politically correct he would have censored Yudhishthira’s gamble match.

Do you know about Australian wild fire 2019–20? 3 billion animals perished. No Arjuna didn’t burn down the forest, this time. Agni did it himself. Balance of Nature!

What is hypocritical is to apply forest conversation standards of 2023 on a civilization dating more than 5000 thousand years, when there were more forest and wild life than room for civilization.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-didnt-Brahma-recommend-Agnidev-to-ask-for-Karnas-help-to-get-back-his-strength-by-burning-Khan
Creation time: Jul 20, 2023 01:03 PM PDT
Content language: English

Content:

Guess you didn’t check the epic reference. Khandava was burnt by Arjuna and Krishna at the suggestion of Agni and Brahma. And through out the epic it had been hailed as heroic even by those who didn’t get selfish benefit out of it.

And Pandavas and Arjuna did a lot of things which were not for their selfish interest like Vakasura Vadha, protecing Matasya from looters, protecting evils like Duryodhana from from Gandharvas. None of these acts were really selfish.

But yes, I agree with you. For Karna there was no selfish interest. And that is what I said — he may not have helped.

My answer elaborated why Arjuna was asked to accomplish this task and not Karna. Finally Neelkanth recitation indicates that Agni acted on the suggestion of Brahma. And Krishna's partition leaves no room for doubt.

Hope that helps.

Post: https://themahabharataproject.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-Why-did-Karna-s-death-make-me-cry-answer-Vivek-Dutta-Mishra-1
Creation time: Aug 22, 2023 12:29 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Whom did Nehru and Gandhi killed? You don't give same punishment to everyone. That's not how justice works.

Still, if you compare Gandhiji lived 2338 days in jail, his wife died their. He didn't write mercy petition. Nehruji spent more than 10 years in jail. Neither received or accepted pension from British government. And these were the peope who fought for Bhagat Singh and argued cases on behalf of INA.

I don't see Mr Savarkar attempting to save Bhagat Singh or fighting for INA or participating in any freedom struggle after accepting pension from British government.

So, yes those who fought for country’s freedom and for other patriots are freedom fighters, those who supported British government openly were cowards.

Post: https://the-real-history-of-india.quora.com/https-mukeshsharmadevlisspace1-quora-com-Kala-Sach-1
Creation time: Sep 15, 2023 01:41 PM PDT
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I think you should read the entire epic yourself (or follow me). There are many distortative and selective narrative that is trying to manipulate facts in favour of Karna. And since, I see, in you, the sincerety to learn, here is the answer. Both of these are examples of partial quotes.

1. KRISHNA ABOUT KARNA

You asked — Is it written in BORI CE where Krishna says on one hand that In fair fight in three worlds even God's can't defeat karna. He could even defeat me

This half baked narrative is based on the references present in Drona Vadha Parva (Chapter 155–157).

Ghatotkatcha was dead and Krishna was very happy that Karna lost the great spear (Vasvi Shakti) from Indra. He had already lost his Kavacha in exchange of Vasvi Shakti. And he was happy that now Karna would be killed. Here is what he said —

Had he possessed the armour and had he possessed the earrings, the powerful Karna would have been able to defeat everyone in the three worlds, even the immortals. Vasava, Kubera, Varuna, lord of the waters, and Yama would not have ventured against Karna in an encounter. Had that bull among men possessed those, you with your Gandiva and I with my sudarshana chakra would not have had the capacity to defeat him in a battle. —Drona Parva, Chapter 155

NOTE:

  • There is NO debate about a fair fight. It was on a precondition if he had the divine armour.
  • It was a praise of the divine armour and not of Karna.
  • It was a probability and not a fact.

What was Krishna’s view about Karna without the spear and armour and earring?

Because of Ghatotkacha, the spear has been used up. O Dhananjaya! Therefore, know that Karna has already been slain. Had Karna possessed the spear in his hand, no man in the world would have been able to stand before him. — Drona Vadha Parva, Chapter 155

And again —

Karna is now like an angry snake, whose energy has been sapped through mantras. Therefore, Karna is now like a fire whose flames have been pacified. O mighty-armed one! Karna obtained a spear from the great-souled Vasava, the one that has now been used against Ghatotkacha. This was obtained in exchange for the earrings and the celestial armour. Since the time he obtained it, Vrisha has always thought that you have been killed in the battle. O unblemished one! O tiger among men! However, though that spear has now gone, I swear to you truthfully that he is incapable of being killed by anyone other than you. — Drona Vadha Parva, Chapter 155

NOTE:

  • Without those divine artifact (In a fair fight),
    • Karna is as if already slain
    • Snake whose energy is sapped
    • A fire that is extinguished.

WAS KARNA REALLY THAT POWERFUL WITH THOSE ARMOUR?

What Krishna said was a possibility, not a fact. Krishna knew very well that Karna had been defeated on a number of occasion before this point in time —

  1. Arjuna defeated him during Draupadi swyamvar where he retreated scared. He had his armour with him.
  2. He was defeated by Bhimasena during his Digvijaya.
  3. Gandharvas defeated him and he fled for his life abandoning his friend and family behind. He still had his armour.
  4. He was defeated by Aruna again in Matasya battle. Thrice.
  5. He was defeated by Abhimanyu on 13th day of the battle. Twice.

So, why did Krishna said, he couldn’t be defeated?

As I see, Krishna was highlighting the signfiicance of the fact that Karna’s armour and divine artifects were used up. And when someone brave used them, he would be invincible. Krishna won’t violate the sacred virtues attached with the earrings of Devi Aditi (Mother of Gods).

But there is a big difference between strong and brave. Divine armours can be you strong, they can’t make you brave. They can protect you from weapons. They can’t protect you from you fears.

Had Karna fought Gandarvas without fear, he might have won becuase he had armours and earrings and he was unkillable. That is how Ravana became the lord of the earth. He knew he can’t be killed to he battled fearlessly. But Karna, got afraid and ran away. He wasn’t ready to test his divine artificats to fullest. He lost the wars, again and again. Everytime for the same reason. He Got afraid and ran.

But if he had the artifacts and if he could have won over his fears, he would be formiddble. That’s what Krishna said. An alternative possiblity. NOT Fact.

2. KRISHNA ABOUT ARJUNA

You asked — On the the other hand Krishna saying to Arjuna that if you die i will slain Karna and Shalya with his bare arms. What do u you think?

This extremely selective narratives comes from Karna Vadha Parva (Chapter 63). Here as Karna and Arjuna faces each other for their final face-off, Karna asks a question to Shalya —

At this, the son of a suta smiled and told Shalya, ‘O friend! If through some means, Partha slays me in the battle today, what will you do after that? Tell me truthfully.’ Shalya replied, ‘O Karna! If the one with the white horses kills you in the battle today, I will slay both Madhava and Pandava.’ — Karna Vadha Parva, Chapter 63

Now Arjuna asks the same question to Krishna. Perhaps, he wanted to hear Krishna’s response. Perhaps, he wanted to be sure that in the eventuality of him dying, Pandavas would still have victory. And here is the full answer of Krishna to that question.

In that way, Arjuna also asked Govinda. However, Krishna laughed and spoke these supreme words to Partha. ‘The sun may fall down from its place. The earth may shatter into many fragments. The fire may become cold. But Karna will not be able to kill Dhananjaya. However, if this does happen, the world will be destroyed. Using my arms in the battle, I will kill Karna and Shalya.’ — Karnva Vadha Parva, Chapter 63

NOTE:

  • Krishna laughs on Arjuna’s question, which he knows is an impossibility — sun may fall down, earth may shatter, fire may become cold, But karna will not be able to kill Dhananjay (Arjuna)
  • However, he wanted to ease Arjuna’s concern about family and fate of battle without him. So he adds that he would kill Karna and Shalya with his bare hand. Bare Hand! Not Sudarshana. Because that was the importance of Karna for Krishna.

Hope this authentic reference gives you insight into what Krishna felt about Karna and Arjuna

Content:

You proved my point. You just have opinions. No facts. No references.

Do you know, why Ganga put seven of her son to eternal sleep? What does the authentic references say? Are they inline with your opinions?

Everyone gets old and decay. That's law of nature. Is modern day Ganga polluted because of her own free will or because of our uncivic behaviour? Don't forget she still is pure at origin.

Are you really trying to justify your foul language? You tried to defile one who resides in Shiva's matted hair! No wonder you are stuck with that name.

Now do you have any direct reference that Drona and Bhagdatt were more powerful than Bhishma. Please quote facts, not opinions.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 18, 2023 08:35 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

No. Unfortunately You didn't prove anything other than the lack of adherence to facts. Just more opinions. Here are more opinions you shared

  1. Regarding her being a spouse of Shiva i think that Shiva himself would praise Parvati, Kalika, Sati and his other spouses over ganga.
    • Ganga is NOT Shiva's spouse.
    • Shiva himself would praise… is your opinion, not fact

2. If you do not know, as per authentic references she freed Vasus from mortal existence as per their wishes… releasing them from prison not Murdering.

3. She kept Bhishma away from Shantanu — That was an agreement between couples. Neither you nor I have a locus here. Again, you can have your opinion and as I said, I wont debate on your views which is part of your name.

Can you give a direct reference to any of your claims —

  1. Ganga was a characterless lady.
  2. Bhagdatt and Drona were more powerful than Bhishma.

I never debated on Ganga’s curse. Just a reminder, a lowly person has no power to curse anyone.

A word of advise — instead of bestowing favour of your opinion, learn something. That would help you define yourself better. Your answer proves you haven't read or understood the essence of Mahabharata.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 19, 2023 07:31 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

I NEVER disputed the curse. But look closely it was a curse by Vasu and not Ganga. But that doesn't matter. She had her sanction. She was anguished for her son like every mother. That proves her to be a grieving mother. And if someone curses they don't become characterless. All it proves she had the power to curse that comes from purity and strength of character. A week can't curse anyone.

And you didn't answer any other question I raised. Go through my previous message and reply what I asked.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 20, 2023 10:38 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Are these references? No. They are mere opinions based on astray incidents. You are comparing a warrior unwilling to fight to someone willing to use all means to win.

Drona could fight Arjuna or counter his weapon only because Arjuna wasn't willing to attack him fatally. A lone Abhimanyu, Satyaki, Bhima everyone could defeat him when he was in his full aggressive form. Arjuna defeated him in Matasya battle easily. He was rendered unconcious. There was only one warrior that was immune to sammohanastra.

Bhagdatt is a modern day overhyped propaganda raised by Kaurava fan committee. He was so insignificant that he was NEVER even considered to be the commander of Kuru Army. Arjuna defeated him in Rajsuy yagna where he pleaded for cease fire. There is not a single reference where Pandavas army were over worried about him.

Now tell me —

  • Who amongst the warriors had a record of defeating someone as powerful as Parshurama. References please.
  • Which warrior forced Krishna to break his own oath and take up arms?
  • Which was the only warrior in Matasya against whom Arjuna moved with caution?
  • Who was the longest servings general of Kuru Army.

When you talk, use references not baseless opinions.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 20, 2023 11:38 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Again, you fail to understand the key features and facts and continue to be lost in your opinions.

  1. FACT: Bhishma defeated Parshurama. No one else did. And everyone in that era (as per reference) considered it a great feat. The weapon Bhishma used was not sammohanastra but Prashwapastra. This was a special weapon Bhishma was given and thats the reason Parshurama had no idea. There is no reference that Dhristdyumna had this weapon. And the divyastra’s were not weapons like guns who anyone can use. You need to own it on your merit.

    But the most important point you miss here is the fact that it was not winning but fighting that proved his might. The battle lasted 24 days. Several times Bhishma went mild. If you think Dhristduymna or Drona could have stood against Parshurama that long, that would be just the opinion and no reference.
  2. FACT: You don’t understand. Bravery is not in never falling, but in staying fearless in battle.

    Even Arjuna was overpowered few times for a small period. Arjuna and Bhimasena both mentioned this fact that any warrior could face hardship. In your reference Bhishma was overpowered and it was his charioteer who drove away when he was unconcious. And perhaps in your selecive reading you missed a few finer points.

    Had you checked the above paragraph you would have found a reference:

    The soldiers are incapable of even looking at the son of Pandu, who is like unto the midday sun blazing in the sky. So also none ventures to look at Bhishma, the son of Ganga. Both are famous for their achievements, and both are of fierce prowess. Both are equal in feats of heroism, and both are difficult of being vanquished in battle.'
    • Tell me, do you have a similar reference for Drona or Bhagdatt?

Had you read the next two chapter also, you would find Arjuna’s view about Bhishma and the rest —

And all of them stood still, their bows, from which they were never separated, dropping down from their hands. And when the Kuru army became insensible, Partha calling to mind the words of Uttara, addressed the son of the Matsya king, saying, 'O best of men, go thou among the Kurus, so long as they remain insensible, and bring away the white garments of Drona and Kripa, and the yellow and handsome ones of Karna, as also the blue ones of the king and Drona's son. Methinks, Bhishma is not stupefied, for he knoweth how to counteract this weapon of mine. So, pass thou on, keeping his steeds to thy left; for those that are sensible should thus be avoided,'

  • Drona is stupified. So is Karna, Kripa and the rest.
  • But Bhishma is not unconcious. And Arjuna is careful. That is what brave warriors do. They don’t underestimate their adversary.

Bhishma entered the battle quite late and their fight was an epic one. No one elses battle is this hightlighted. Everyone else was defeated and demolished. Now unlike you, everything I mentioned is based on reference. Not hollow opinions.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 24, 2023 10:42 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Drona clearly countered the sammohan astra on day 6 of the war.

How does that matter? Drona clearly was defeated in Matasya battle. Twice. Drona clearly was afraid of Arjuna and didn’t form Chakravyuh in his presence. Drona clearly failed against Abhimanyu. Drona clearly failed to save Jaidradh. Drona clearly was defeated by Satyaki, Bhimasena.

You said — so ur going to act like u do not know about the incidents on day 14 that involved guru drona ? the one where he was able to murder twenty five maharathis fast and a line

Enlighten me, what was the battle of 14th day about? It was saving Jaidradh not killing insignificant Maharathis. There is no denial Drona was more powerful than Drupada and King Virata. But are we comparing him to Drupada or Virata? Nah! We are comparing him to Bhisma. Tell me, those whom he killed, how many of them were really great Maharathi who had killed any great Maharathi in the battle? Think for an answer and you would know why I don’t care about those numbers. Quality and not quantitiy matters. So how many quality warrior did Drona killed?

You said — in adi parva which mentions that Bhishma failed to kill a single great warrior in his tenure as general?

Does that surprise you? Didn’t Bhishma clearly mentioned he would not kill Pandavas? Yes, Bhishma was unwilling to kill. But tell me, how much loss did Kaurava suffer during Bhishma’s command? How much did they suffer in Drona’s command? If you check the references you would understand the difference.

The difference is Bhishma said he won’t kill Pandavas. Drona tried and failed. Bhishma gave away his secret to death for the welfare of humanity. Drona died lamenting for his son and not for a greater cause.

And again, none of the reference you gave mentions Drona even comparable to Bhishma. But yes, Drona accepted Bhishma as his commander as he was loyal to him. He always talked high about Bhishma.

You said — bhagadatta’s achievements should already be known u know where u can find them, both karna fans and arjuna fans have given links to them many times.

That’s my point. These references are given by Karna’s and Arjuna’s fans. But did Duryodhana refer to him while recounting the great generals of his army in the first chapter of Gita? No. Did he ever pinned his hope for winning the war on Bhagdatt? No. Was Krishna ever worried about how to kill Bhagdatt? Nah!

He was a great warrior. But then there were many great warrior. And he did never qualify in the top tier rank of either side.

Rest is opinions.

You said — Satyaki was a great warrior too so is Abhimanyu but neither of them were considered for general position.

You are right. They were never considered. Because the position wasn’t vacant. And while Abhimanyu brave and powerful, he wasn’t matured to take the place. But the most straight forward answer, they were not considered because there were better choice over and above these two.

But Kauravas army got this opportunity again and again. And he was never considered. After Bhishma, Karna was a contender. Had Bhagdatta been alive, still he won’t have been the commander of Kuru army after Karna or even after Shalya.

You said — he proved to be far better as a warrior, character and person than bhishma ever was.

Please hightlight how he proved to be a better character and person than Bhishma. Arjuna spared his life long ago. And that thankless person battled against Arjuna trying to kill him. He had no obligation towards Kuru. It was his personal hatred that sent him to Kuru camp. And if you call him a man of strong character, why am I not surprised? It is because you have a very different perspective about what is a good character.

For you, Devi Ganga is characterless. It is only natural that people like Bhagdatt and Karna would be icon of strength.

As I said, we have nothing to discuss.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 24, 2023 11:04 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Please consider it your victory and like every other answers that too would be another opinion.

What I give you is references. And I would refer you to your multiple comments that are present on this thread that debunks your claim that I am deleting them. I NEVER delete a comment unless it uses extremely uncivic words or abusive language. My other answers too can act as reference to my facts.

My dear, fact matters.

When it comes to biase, we have a references in every of your answer.

So declare your victory the same way you declared Ganga a characterless woman and Bhishma a coward.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 25, 2023 09:56 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Ok, I take it that you haven’t read the authentic epic, so let me enlighten you here — The trick of covering the sun has been deemed interpolation.

But even if you were to believe it because of a lack of understanding, it would be debatable who was cheating. Pandavas who wanted to avenge Abhimanyu’s death or Kauravas who knew they couldn’t kill Arjuna in a direct battle fought as a pack of hyenas against one man, not trying to kill him but delayed him so that he died on its own.

For your information, the original Neelkantha edition, (source for KMG) mentions that the sunset illusion was exclusively for Jaydradha and not for others.

What can not be debated is the fact that Drona could never have saved Jaydradha. He wasn’t even around Jaydradh at that time. When the sun was uncovered, Jaydradh still had many warriors around him, but couldn’t save him.

You didn’t answer, how many soldiers died during Drona’s command?

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 25, 2023 11:52 PM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Glad you asked for evidence. I don’t talk without them —

Here is a direct from BORI CE —

Bori Critical Edition Udyoga Parva Ch 153

Bhishma said, 'O mighty-armed one, it is even so, 'O Bharata, as thou sayest. But the Pandavas are as dear to me as ye yourselves. Therefore, O king, I should certainly seek their good as well, although I shall certainly fight for thee, having given thee a pledge (before) to that effect. I do not see the warrior on earth that is equal to me, except that tiger among men, Dhananjaya, the son of Kunti. Endued with great intelligence, he is conversant with innumerable celestial weapons. That son of Pandu, however, will never fight with me openly. With the power of my weapons, I can, in a trice, destroy this universe consisting of gods, Asuras, Rakshasas, and human beings. The sons of Pandu, however, O king, are incapable of being exterminated by me. I shall, therefore, slay every day ten thousand warriors. If, indeed, they do not slay me in battle first, I will continue to slaughter their forces thus.

And since you follow only KMG, here is a direct reference from KMG

"Bhishma said, 'O mighty-armed one, it is even so, 'O Bharata, as thou sayest. But the Pandavas are as dear to me as ye yourselves. Therefore, O king, I should certainly seek their good as well, although I shall certainly fight for thee, having given thee a pledge (before) to that effect. I do not see the warrior on earth that is equal to me, except that tiger among men, Dhananjaya, the son of Kunti. Endued with great intelligence, he is conversant with innumerable celestial weapons. That son of Pandu, however, will never fight with me openly. With the power of my weapons, I can, in a trice, destroy this universe consisting of gods, Asuras, Rakshasas, and human beings. The sons of Pandu, however, O king, are incapable of being exterminated by me. I shall, therefore, slay every day ten thousand warriors. If, indeed, they do not slay me in battle first, I will continue to slaughter their forces thus. There is another understanding on which I may willingly become the commander of thy forces. It behoveth thee to listen to that. O lord of earth, either Karna

Chapter 157, Udyodha Parva.

This proves that Bhishma informed Suyodhana before the start of the battle that he would not kill Pandavas.

You said — That this screenshot is evidence that Bhishma was not showing mercy to any Pandava.

No, this screenshot simply says, you trust a Danava more. Because this statement was made by a Danava who was trying to console Suyodhana. Karna also said many times he could kill Arjuna. Can I take it as a reference that Karna actually killed Arjuna? NO. There is a difference between saying and doing. The reference only states Asura’s plan not confirmation of the success of the plan.

And if I had to trust a Danava, then that would mean even Drona was possessed. That means we can exclude every Drona’s act in Kurukshetra. And before that, he fought just one battle — Matasya. Where he was badly defeated.

Questions for you — Can you give a direct reference that proves —

  • A demon actually entered Bhishma.
  • Bheema got his immense power on day 14 due to some honey drink.
  • Drona was more powerful than Bhishma

No opinions please. Direct reference. If NOT, we can end this debate.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 26, 2023 12:23 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Great going. Now for you Parshurama was impressive or relevant. But as I pointed, it's your opinion. The Mahabharata references and all epic has a very different opinion about great Parshurama, the 6th Vishnu.

And in the long run, your opinion is what is irrelevant.

Read Mahabharata to see the importance attributed to Parshurama in the entire epic and stop reading snippet and screenshot on social media.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 26, 2023 12:38 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

Again more opinions. Zero references.

Do you know the two nuclear bomb USA dropped on Japan weren't same?

Please share a direct reference to prove that the two weapons were same. In fact allow me to contradict your understanding. Sammohanastra used to make people unconcious and they would eventually regain their consciousness. TheParshwastra used to put them to deep sleep where they won't be able to come out unless the effect is nullified by other weapon.

You said – it was easier for arjuna to defeat bhishma than it was to beat guru Drona

Again just your views. No references. But experts of battle, the celestial eye witnesses said

About Drona

Drona could fight Arjuna was an achievement. No equality. No victory. Just fight. Then Drona fled himself, saved by an attack from Ashwatthama.

Then Drona attacked in a pack with other warriors.

Now Drona impressed you

About Bhishma

Till this point Bhishma didn't fight. That proves his reluctance. But now he is the last man standing. He must defend his people.

Now what does celestial experts say about these two? They find them equal in exploits

Do you really think, your opinion matters? Bhishma didn't leave battlefield scared. He became unconcious. Drona fled.

But you are free to your opinions. Celestial experts and I don't agree

Content:

Why do you think I would agree with you when you failed to provide a single reference backing your opinions.

I AM not afraid of speaking against public opinion. You may read my posts. But I am against demeaning our culture and our legacy like you do, perhaps because you think it is safe to call Ganga who is revered as mother by Indians as characterless.

And in the beginning, looking at your mindset I told you I don't want to debate. I knew, I would give references and you will forward reasonings.

As far as delete is concerned, I don't delete commnets, else there would be no comments left. So if your comment is deleted look for alternative reasons.
