Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 28, 2023 05:40 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said — Logic - individual that is without a doubt inferior to drona did that against bheem.

Or logic becomes illogic when it ignores the facts.

FACT: Bhimasena defeated Drona. Broke eight of his chariots. Drona had his army and he was adamant NOT to let Bhimasena pass.

Illogic: Bhimasena got his power from some honey and Drona was tired.

Bhimasena performed a ceremony and felt like he had regained his power. There is NOT a single mention that because of the honey Bhimasena could defeat Dronacharya. Even Satyaki defeated Dronacharya and he had drank no honey. Drona failed to complete his mission. Bhimasena succeeded.

You said — Regarding Bhagadatta he fought Arjuna 8 days in the rajasuya yudh without being tired out.

Please also read, what happened next. Who won that? How much were Pandavas or Krishna worried about the presence of Bhagdatta?

If you read the narratives many Kings are referred as lion among men or Indra among men and died the next moment without doing much damage.

Post: https://gangeyabhishma.quora.com/https-www-quora-com-To-which-factor-does-Bhishma-attribute-the-character-weakness-of-Karna-despite-the-son-of-Surya-th
Creation time: Nov 28, 2023 06:10 AM PST
Content language: English

Post Comment

Content:

You said – How is it false to say any of those things?

Lack of reference dear. You don't read authentic versions and don't understand. One battle doesn't decide who is a great warrior. Consequences decide. Tell me if Kritvarma and Duryodhana won, why was Jaidradh killed? Why didn't they capture Bhimasena? It is easy to overpower but difficult to win. Bhimasena defeated Drona because Drona failed his task and Bhimasena succeeded his mission. Abhimanyu won, because he foiled Drona’s plan. Even in death he defeated his killers. Did you learn something — Consequences, dear. That is what decides victory.

You said – How is it false to say any of those things? Kritvarma/Duryodhana being inferior to Drona is a fact but both of them did better against Bheem than Drona did

That simply means Drona was inferior to all the three of them. Isn't that? Because Bhima certainly defeated Drona. And there is no reference to prove Drona was better than Kritvarma.

But Bhima defeated Duryodhana multiple times. Duryodhana was afraid of Bhima (his words).

You said —how can u deny the fact that Drona went through several hardships and long breathtaking battles that day before fighting bheem? His power and energy reservoir clearly became deplete. he was as active as arjuna that day.

Is there a reference that says his energy was deplated. None. Was he the only one active that day? Didn't Bhima also fought though battles that day?

So, as I said, stop propagating skewed views. Stick to references. Drona said, I won't let you pass me. He failed. Didn't say he was tired. Just got defeated. Neither could save Jaidrath nor could capture Yudhishthira.

Again you may stop if you can't give references.

You haven't given reference to any question I asked earlier.

Recipient: Ashwini Bhardwaj
Time: Jun 26, 2020 07:49 PM PDT

Inbox Message

Content:

Hi,

We are connected on quora and your references have been accurate. Can you please provide direct reference to Sukthankar clarified this ,to the best of his ability. He elaborated why they regarded Karna ‘s failure as authentic.

I am tired fighting Karna fans trying to put forward logic that

यत्कर्णशल्यप्रमुखैः पार्थिवैर्लोकविश्रुतैः |

नानतं बलवद्भिर्हि धनुर्वेदपरायणैः ||४||

As per Bibek Debroy, some other Kshatriya Karna failed. Mr Debroy translation doesn't match Gita Press.

Mr Suthankars statement would prove invaluable. Can you please share reference or links?

Regards,

Vivek Dutta Mishra

Recipient: Lakshmi Telidevara
Time: Jul 11, 2020 01:08 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content: Thanks for the references. Now I believe Karna's fan have come to realization that Draupadi forbidding Karna can't be argued much. So they started a new line. The Karna never attended the swyamber and one who failed was a Kshatriya Karna. I believe this confusion is created by Bibek Debory translation.

This contradicts Gita Press but unless we have a clarification from such source, it is difficult ot counter.

Recipient: Vinit Shah
Time: Mar 06, 2021 06:21 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Hi, I have always liked and appreciated your answer, but couldn’t agree with you on this one — Why does suthankar says that Karna was better than Arjuna in skill? Could it be considered legit claim?

I thought, instead of going on open forum, I would discuss with you in private if that is ok with you.

  1. Duryodhana wouldn’t have given such respect to Karna if he was an inferior warrior.
    1. As I see, he didn’t had many candidate to pick up for a close confidant.
    2. Karna had certainly shown great promises earlier.
    3. He had helped him in all his henious conspiracies. You don’t always chose a superior warrior, but one whom you can trust to be with you in your dirty deeds.
    4. Bhishma and Drona can’t be trusted with conspiracies like laksha grih.
  2. Kunti and Krishna would have simply ignored him if he was an inferior warrior.
    1. I don’t think they approached Karna for his powers. He had never won against Arjuna in any battle.
    2. Karna was the most unique card on the table. If you could perssuade Karna to back-off from the battle, Duryodhana would be forced to reconsider his options, if not give up altogther. It is not as much for what he would do in the battle as it is for what would be the impact of his absence.
    3. Kunti certainly also had mother’s instinct to win his son back.
  3. Krishna himself reminded Arjuna that he shouldn’t take Karna lightly as Karna could be equal or superior (Non critical edition). While the statement was meant to warn Arjuna not to take Karna lightly before the big duel, it indeed proven that Karna was a match indeed and at least to be very specific Karna was not technically inferior on his day.
    1. True. Karna wasn’t someone to be taken lightly, not espcially by Arjuna. He had a single focused mission in life — vanquish Arjuna. Such people must be not be taken lightly.
    2. But didn’t Krishna, in Ghatotkatch Parva and Uddyog Parva already announced that there is no way Karna could defeat Arjuna.
    3. Cautioning a warrior while battling with someone of equal skill is only natural.
  4. Bhishma also stated Karna to be in league of Arjuna and Krishna.
    1. Same Bhishma, earlier called him Ardharathi.
    2. While he changed is statement on his arrow bed, didn’t he gave more irrifutable reason earlier for considering him Ardharathi?

As I see, Karna was no doubt a skillful archer, par excellence with Arjuna. But he was no warrior. He had no warrior code, no perseverance.

Regards,

Vivek

Recipient: Mahabharata Researcher
Time: Mar 07, 2021 01:52 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

hello

Recipient: Aayan Atif
Time: Mar 11, 2021 06:30 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Hi Prabal,

Congrats for reaching a milestone with your space. I have witnessed a positive change in your approach towards Sanatan Dharma and that is quite appreciable. You have also added me to your Sanatan group as a contributer, and I try to share my answers with your space. However, I always feel hesitant.

The problem is you have transformed, I haven’t. I based on my study, consider Karna evil and my answers never tend to diviate from fact for being unbiased. But you already know that.

Now my hesitation is, should I share all my answers in your space? Would that be a problem? Would you fear the backlash you may face because of it as recently few people said that they would leave the space? I would really like you to suggest.

We have two choices —

a. I share only those answers which are not related to Karna.

b. I share all my answers and you may mute those answers that you don’t like.

c. I don’t share the answers. You can share the answer to your group if you like.

Kindly suggest.

Regards,

Vivek Dutta Mishra.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 06:32 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Shiva is Pashupati — The God of everyone. Everyone includes even demons. There is no power parallel to Gods and Shiva is supreme!

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 06:33 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

At best, yes.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 06:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

As per Santana philosophy, Gods don’t act as puppeteer. They don’t micro-manage humans (or anyone). They create broad rules like law of gravity — You jump and you fall off. They don’t ask you to jump or fall off.

We are governed by our gunas — Sato, Rajas and Tamas.

It is the job of gurus, well-wishers and human law to put a check on a man’s action. Guess they failed him or he failed them.

God’s intervene only when we completely collapse our system — there action is to rebalance the social order as Krishna says in Gita and not to micro-manage individuals.

Krishna didnt’ intervene when Yudhishthira gambled his family. He didn’t try to reduce their thirteen years of exile. He didn’t help Arjuna during Draupadi’s swyamvara or Gandharva battle or Matasya battle.

Hope you get the point.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 06:57 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

See, there is no direct reference that Raavan composed Shiva Tandava Stottram. But assuming the popular myth, all it proves, that He was a great composer and a Shiva devotee at some point in his life. It doesn’t absolve him from his crimes.

You may be a Shiva devotee at one point and then to worship Shiva may have just become a matter of habit rather than devotion. A true Shiva devotee won’t stoop to become Raavan.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 09:08 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It is a capital mistake to think of celestial weapons as some form of nuclear weapon or a remote controlled weapons that were easy to deploy. You need moral character to deploy them properly and the right context. Once you lose that moral character you may not deploy them effectively. That is why there misuse was prohibited. Even in Mahabharata most of the use of divine weapons were ineffective because they didn’t follow the proper ethics of using them — Narayanastra failed, Vaishnavastra failed, Brahamastra failed, Bhargavastra failed and so on.

Second, Chandrahas was a sword and can’t be used for a distant battle.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 09:11 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

On the other hand those who understand the responsibility and ethics won’t use it uncessarily.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 09:12 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Maybe. Or maybe a moral degraded Raavan couldn’t summon the divyastra. If you are interested in my interpretation of Divyastra you may read in my book The Accursed God.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jul 22, 2021 09:46 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I will send you in a couple of days

Recipient: Dushyant
Time: Jul 31, 2021 02:38 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Would a mention in Ramayana or Purana make it authentic when there are so many versions of them in circulation?

Remember, our epics were propagated via shruti-smriti tradition. And as with any mechanism Shruti-Smriti too take dust and fable on their way.

Consider India’s surgical strike after Uri attack. For few it is a fact for others an elaborate propaganda. None of us who are contemporary to this era can we sure what happened. We would choose to believe what we want.

Even if we think logically, Ramayana is not a chronology of Raavan’s regime and as such can’t mention every act of Raavan — good or bad.

So what is the base line? It is even a given folk tale is alined to the epic or not. Folk tales are essential elements of our great civilization. We don’t need to condemn each of them.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 08, 2021 10:53 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It may be true. We can never we sure. As a King Raavan may have established many temples. That still doesn’t prove if he was a great Shiva devotee.

My point is current written works too are derived from Shruti-Smriti tradition and mention just once fraction of them. Therefore we should focus on the key value of the epic and then weigh the folk tales considering the core values as the base line.

Raavan wasn’t a hero. He was an evil. But in Hindu philosphy, evils don’t have separte God hierarchy (like Bible’s Saitan or Islams Kaafir). Everything belongs to same all prevailing God of Gods — Pashupati Mahadeva Shiva.

So I won’t be surprised even the evil like Raavan worshipped him. After all even demons derived their power from the same God. Isn’t it?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 08, 2021 11:23 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Why evils worship Shiva? Who should they worship? Shiva is Pashupati — God of all beings. Even evils. They have no where to go. Even they need a power source and anchor.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 09, 2021 01:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Why Mahadeva grants boons to evil ones?

There is a cycle of Karma. He who performs austerities must be granted the fruit of their Karma. That’s divine rule. And can the God of all beings be partial to anyone? If he becomes partial won’t the whole cosmos collapse? Won’t that be injustice.

More important question is what is the origin or evil — power, ambition and lust. Most evil become evil only after their tryst with power that they got from those blessings or boons.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 09, 2021 01:55 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Pinaka was not in the play and neither was Sharang.

But more important, bows don't really matter. A good boy can be of great help to a great archer but can't make someone an archer.

Pinaka and Sharang are reputed because of their wielder and not in the reverse order. Same is Gandiva. No one ever said (except on quora) that Arjuna would win because he has Gandiva. Parshuram said try for peace before Arjuna wields Gandiva. Foucus was Arjuna.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 09, 2021 11:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

By the way, did you like my podcast?

Content language: English

  1. None to the best none
Recipient: Vijay Sardar
Time: Aug 19, 2021 08:55 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

*To the best of my knowledge

Recipient: Vijay Sardar
Time: Aug 19, 2021 08:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Yudhishthira had to go and see the suffering of hell before going to heaven. It is said to be the consequence of his lie

Recipient: Vijay Sardar
Time: Aug 19, 2021 08:56 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Bhishma was way more powerful than Karna or Drona. He was capable of defeating even Parshurama and it wasn’t without a reason he was unanimous choice for the post of commander. Even for Duryodhana it was no brainer between Bhishma and Karna. He was the only commander that made Pandava’s struggle for victory even though one reason was Arjuna’s reluctance to got fatal against him.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 31, 2021 01:43 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

So Yes, Bhishma would certainly have been more fatal if he so wanted. But then Krishna would have motivated Arjuna to respond and Bhishma may have been ousted earlier with much more carnage in the lesser time period.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 31, 2021 01:44 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I think, Bhishma was buying time so that Duryodhana could see the err of his ways and end the war. He was less lethal towards Pandavas and also a strong guard to Kauravas as he rendered even Arjuna reluctant and thus less effective.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 31, 2021 01:45 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Arjuna may have shared the dream that a compromise may be reached with least damage. And he continued to be optimistic till day 13. Abhimanyu’s death was a turning point for Arjuna.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 31, 2021 01:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Bhishma wasn't the oldest warrior on Kurukshetra. By today's parameter even Pandavas weren't exactly angry young men. Age had a different meaning in that yuga as the life expectancy was different.

So I would say, it mattered far less than the fact that Pandavas fought for Dharma and were destined to win, no matter what.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Aug 31, 2021 08:25 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

That is what you call occupational Hazard. Why should any soldier ever die? Why wars? Why URI attack? Why Kargil? Why 26/11?

Krishna answered in Gita — Do your duty without attaching yourself to a sense of duty.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 01, 2021 11:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But there was another reason many soldiers needed to die in Mahabharata. Can you guess?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 01, 2021 11:46 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Please elaborate

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 02, 2021 05:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

What is the meaning of Dharma establishment

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 04, 2021 08:48 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Crime should fit the punishment. Lesser punishment would be a mockery of justice. He was given due chance to redress. Now he had to face another charge for war crime.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 24, 2021 08:49 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Please share more details

Recipient: Bharti Pareek
Time: Sep 24, 2021 08:50 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

No. When a person is dead, he is gone. It doesn't matter to him if you give them proper rite or not. But what legacy Rama would have left by denying him a dignified last rite? Who would he be defining — Ravana or himself? Who would he be insulting — Ravana or his own ancestral culture?

Last rites don't judge the dead, the judge the living.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 24, 2021 10:42 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

My recommendation would be “Freedom at Midnight”

Recipient: Indu
Time: Sep 25, 2021 11:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It is written by a French and an American with reliable, authentic and most important unbiased perspective

Recipient: Indu
Time: Sep 25, 2021 11:04 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

He already said, what he had in mind. Do I need to add anything here? Yes, people do get exhausted in the battle and can be killed. But I can’t dare to either cross or justify the decision on Shri Rama. All I can do is to take an humble effort to interpret it. Shri Rama knew his actions would be a role model for all times to come. He may not have wanted to taint it. It may also have been his strategy to subdue to morale of enemy troops.

What I may also venture to say is, Ravaana had a strong army with great warriors. Killing Raavana on the first day wouldn’t have destroyed his equally evil army. Rather a headless army may tend to behave in an unpredictable manner.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 25, 2021 11:17 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Finally about Kumbhakarna. After watching Ramananda Sagar show, I actually felt a little sympathy for that poor fellow who was disturbed in the mid slumber only to die. But if you see his action of being Ravana’s partner in crimes, those sympathy simply fades away…

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 25, 2021 11:19 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Who would be ideal — one who tries to help his brother redress his wrong ways or who supports him into it?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 25, 2021 11:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

In that case you would consider Karna a great friend as he pushed Duryodhana to his doom.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 26, 2021 05:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But a true friend should always look for what is good for his friend.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 26, 2021 05:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But if you find both ideal, then Kubhkarana and Karna were great.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 26, 2021 05:23 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

suggest what?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Sep 26, 2021 09:04 AM PDT

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Inbox Message

Content:

The modern discovery leads to the ancient symbolism — the basic constituent of all matters — electron, protocol and neutron.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Nov 05, 2021 02:54 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Not just symbolism. Think of it as divyastra to control time, matter and behavior. And yes, Shiva doesn’t need to slay anyone. The world comes into existence from void and dissolves back into void by his mere wish. Remember when he destroyed (not killed) Tripura, he didn’t use any ordinary weapon. Did he. What you hear is just a leela. He is gunatita.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Recipient: Indu

Inbox Message

Content:

Things aren’t always black and white as we would like to believe. No one becomes adharmi simply by being on side of Kauravas. That is why the key question was — Did he always protect Dharma?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Nov 07, 2021 11:53 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Could we nor change it now sir, after all these years ? Since Samskrita is the essence of this country?

Should we really change such a beautiful song as our national anthem?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:44 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Vandenataram is already our national song thanks to great Gandhian Rajendra Prasad

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

So then Gandhi Ji was not anti hindu as people make him out to be?

Gandhiji was a staunch Hindu. All his movements and politics started in his prarthna sabha

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:46 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

He died with Rama in his heart. And few can proclaim to be a more profound hindu. Even Mohan Bhagwat agrees

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

And was Gandhi Ji anti Samkrita, I heard someone on YouTube why said Mahatma opposed Samskrita.

No. But he preferred a language, he called hinustani as general mode of communication. You can compare that with today's hinglish. It was more acceptable to all walks of life.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:51 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:52 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:53 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But why not Samskrita was an important langauge since it had its roots in India. Isn't it our mistake that we are letting such a beautiful language die and forgotten?

How many great Samkrita work has been done after 1800 AD? Do we reject Ramcharit manas because it is not in Sanskrit?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Unfortunately Sanskrit was already on decline in India. Ramcharit manas became most popular work precisely because it wasn't in Sanskrit. At that was in 16th century

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:57 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It's unfortunate to see great language dying, but what is more important? Nation or language? Can you thrust one language on whole nation by force and expect nation to be united?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 02, 2021 10:58 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

We should. We MUST.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Inbox Message

Content:

Inbox Message

Content:

See it is like this. Few understand Sanskrit today. One option we force everyone to learn Sanskrit. Let us destroy all non Sanskrit translation of Ramayana and Mahabharata. Do you think people will learn Sanskrit to keep Ramayana and Mahabharata alive?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Inbox Message

Content:

Time: Dec 03, 2021 04:41 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

During the constitutional assembly debate, the Hindi speaking legislatures presented the claim of Hindi as the national language

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 03, 2021 04:47 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 03, 2021 04:50 AM PST

Content:

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Content language: English

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 03, 2021 05:04 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You said — But why do people think that punishment for such a crime is extreme? Shouldn’t people support such punishments?

Because people are people. They will always have different opinions. They may support evil. Why should it be a surprise that they would oppose such penalty? Do Read about Dhanjaya Chakraborty, the rapist and how Human Rights fought to commute his sentence.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:39 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — Mahatma Ji make Nehru our PM? Did he do it only because Nehru knew to speak English?

Knowing international language would decidedly have been a criteria. But it was way beyond.

Let us first consider the age of three important leader. Gandhiji was 78. He chose to be out of race for PM. I hope you would agree, he would have been elected unopposed. Patel was just five years younger at 73, suffering from TV and was not in good health. He died in 1950.

Nehru was 20 years younger to Gandjiji and 15 from Patel. He was under 60, quite young by political standards in best of his health.

Apart from the age, Nehru had an international repute. In 1944, Lord Mount Batten had predicted he would be the PM of India. And his personality was such that US president not only visited India in his tenure but also offered India a place in security council which Nehru opposed because of his non-aligned policy.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:44 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Didn’t Modiji refused to give political posts to politicians above 70? Not even a ministerial birth?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

How was Gandhiji wrong in asking Patel to step aside?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:45 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Contrary to popular social media depiction, Nehruji was an idealist and honest person with profound understanding of India. His only folly was his innocence and idealistic trust in human goodwill.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:46 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You can check their birthdate on google

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:46 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Secondly If I enumerate some of the achievements of Nehru ji it would include — IIT, IIM, AIMS construction of DAMS and Canals and Nuclear project

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:47 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Absolutely NOT.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:48 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Nehruji did made a few mistake. He shouldn’t have announced plebiscite in Kashmir, shouldn’t have trusted China, shouldn’t have rejected a seat in security council

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But these were honest mistakes of an idealistic man who trusted friendship and human value. He wasn’t corrupt of power hungry politician like his daughter or descendets

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Nehruji had a charismatic personality and most of the ladies you find him with were respected women part of political circle. Like Lady Mountbatten. Nehru and Mountbatten were closed friend. More like a family friend and the relation extended between Lady Mountbatten and his wife also.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:51 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But imagine, if a man has affair with your wife, would you still be his friend? Nehru and Lady Mountbatten shared a plutonic relationship and Mountbatten often used his wife as a informal messanger to Nehru. They had respected and friendly relationship

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:52 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

YES.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You can apply your commonsense

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:54 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — And some said he was afraid of Netaji returning to India

This is stupid. Do you know the original constitution of India is hand written with several paintings in it include Rama and Krishna?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:55 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It is still present

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:58 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

The point is, it contains the drawing of only two Indian freedom fighters. One is Gandhiji. Can you guess the other?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 08:59 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

YES

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:00 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Now tell me, why would Nehruji allows such a rare honor if he hated him or was afraid of him. You can understand he had great influence on constitution. Right?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:01 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

No people weren’t that fanatic then to protest. And remember Indian constitution had no “secular” word then.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:02 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Nehru, Gandhi and Bose had political and ideological differences. But then it was NOT a personal enmity. And a great proof is Ambedkar. He was a bitter critic of Gandhiji and Gandhiji himself advocated his position in Constituent Assembly of India

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:05 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — When why was secular added?

It was added by Indira Gandhi in 42nd amendment act 1977. And yes. it was political.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:07 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

dirty politics for which we still pay the price. That was Indira Gandhi

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:07 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Was Adi Sankaracharya pro Hindu or neutral? Is a surgeon who makes you bleed under his knife a friend or foe?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:08 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — Can’t we remove it now? Since the govt has majority?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:09 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Do you know why the word secular wasn’t present in Indian constitution?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:10 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You said — Adi Shankaracharya was for Hindu culture

But he did proposed reforms in our practices. Right? Krishna did say that time to time it is required to re-energize Dharma.

Similarly Nehruji was a reformist. Some thought suggesting reforms in being Anti Hindu.

Similarly Gandhiji is blamed for being Anti Hindu because he supported Muslims as well.

Now time for your last three answers

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:12 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

We never had secular word because secularism in it’s most pure and perfect form is present nowhere other than Sanatana sanskriti

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:13 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

ekam sat vipra bahudha vadanti

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:13 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Sarve Bahwantu Sukhinah, Sarve Santu Niramaya

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:14 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

We don’t follow religion. We follow Dharma. And there is no English word for Dharma.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:15 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

So how do you remove secular word from a country that prides in Santana sanskriti? Would you still be a Hundu?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:16 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — was it wise to make Mount Batten as our governer general?

Transfer of power wasn’t an overnight job. It would take months. Most of the high ranking generals and officials were Britishers. How would you have made sure that they follow the government order implicitly? We could have delayed independence, removed all officials. A better option was to gain independence and make a Brit serve India.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:19 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — If our Sanatana Dhamra itself is secular then why add it?

You are right. That is why it wasn’t there originally. But now that it is present, what do we gain by removing it? Do we want to call that we would become a Islamic nation?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:20 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Because calling sanatana would be a synonym and following modern definition of Hindutwa would be degrading sanatana Hindu sanskriti

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:22 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I would say making him governor general was not a bad decision.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Dec 10, 2021 09:23 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It was prudent

Recipient: अभिjeet

Content:

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:36 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

“He was not a historian or a researcher. He was a contemporary and politician.”

Being a contemporary is more insightful than a researcher from next generation.

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:37 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

A contemporary like Subhash Chandra Bose and Dr Rajendera Prasad would be a greater source of authentic information than any research paper.

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:38 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Problem with Ambedkar was, he was a jealous and thankless person.

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:38 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Another point — “Both Gandhi and Ambedkar were political rivals.”

Is fundamentally WRONG. They were never rivals. Ambedkar was too small and insignificant for Gandhi to be considered rival.

Ambedkar wanted to imagine himself as Gandhiji’s rival. But had Gandhji himself not insisted Ambedkar wouldn’t even be part of constituent assembly of India.

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:41 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

So don’t glorify him by calling Gandhiji’s political rival.

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:41 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You may change as “some politicians with vested agenda may…”

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:41 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

“… and there opinion doesn’t constitute the fact”

Recipient: अभिjeet
Time: Jan 20, 2022 07:42 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

That is what I am saying. Why glorify a person like Ambedkar by calling him Gandhiji’s rival. A sun doesn’t find a lamp as it’s rival. Let the lamp carry such grand illusion.

Let Amebdkar fans say he was Gandhiji’s political rival. For him, the two never existed on the same plane to be called rival.

If you accept my suggestion, you may edit the last part of the answer completely downplaying Ambedkar’s statement on the lines I mentioned in my previous answer.

Recipient: अभिjeet

Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jan 24, 2022 10:39 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked- Sir you told that Sarvarkars idea on Hindu Dharma was like Islamization of our culture, could you explain?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jan 24, 2022 10:42 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You must first understand what is our ancient Sanatan Hindu culture. Its foundation lies in all inclusive the core philosophy ekam sat vipra bahudha vadanti. We are not a religion where we want to propagate our self with the help of violence sword and loots and killing. But with Savarkar a new of philosophy came into picture which he called as hindutva which advocated following a similar path, the Islamic way of propagation and ruthless crushing of people who do not follow your religion.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jan 24, 2022 10:48 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

No in the name of propagation of conduct we are following the same approach of Islam, by this method Hindus may become a major or a dominant population in the world, but the question is, will Sanatan civilization survive when we have left its core philosophy and moved to the same world of mindless violence which is so against its basic spirit

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jan 24, 2022 10:49 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I think you need to first decide, if you trust Mahatma or not. And then ask your self if he could have said those words.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Feb 02, 2022 01:05 AM PST

Inbox Message

Content:

As for rape assault here is what Gandhiji exactly said —

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Feb 02, 2022 01:05 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

“When a woman is assaulted she may not stoop to think in terms of himsa or ahimsa. Her primary duty is self-protection. She is at liberty to employ every method or means that come to her mind in order to defend her honour. God has given her nails and teeth. She must use them with all her strength and, if need be, die in the effort.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Feb 02, 2022 01:06 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

This is his view — the official documented one.

Time: Feb 02, 2022 01:06 AM PST

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Kavana.N
Time: Mar 04, 2022 08:22 AM PST

Inbox Message

Content:

Do you agree

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 04, 2022 08:23 AM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

No. It wasn't possible to overthrow Britishers with violence. Even Krishna didn't fight Jarasandha by training converting citizens into an Army. He relocated from Mathura to Dwarka. And Krishna was God with Divine powder. Gandhi wasn't.

Tell me how Gandhi could have chosen to be violent? Where would he train his soldiers? Where would the fund come from? Who would have trained them? Where? What would Britishers have done when they were training? What happened to Chandrashekhar Azad? Ramprasad Bismil?

A war is hardly winnable when fought on home turf. Your own civilians are exposed.

And in a gun fight bigger gun wins. period.

What happened to INA? Or LTTE? They were smashed by bigger guns.

But spiritual power can't be defeated by guns.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 04, 2022 07:11 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

None. We tried and failed. Other's tried and failed. Ask yourself, if there was a way, someone else would have been Father of the nation. They could have sidelined Gandhiji.

Have you watched The Last Samurai (Tom Cruise) ?

Do watch. You May have your answer.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 04, 2022 07:26 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Did you go through my recent podcasts?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 04, 2022 08:29 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

It is directly refusing. But Karna had already refused to fight before this incident once. And after this incident once.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:20 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

So Karna’s refusal can’t be negated.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:21 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Further, A general needs to question the loyalty of his subordinates. Bhishma wasn’t willing to be general of an Army with Karna under his command. He must have seen the outcome of Matasya battle where Karna’s insubordination during Matasya battle.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:22 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

But can a soldier chose his general?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:23 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Last Bhishma only refused to be the general. Didn’t refuse to fight.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:23 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Rama, Krishna and Gandhiji everyone said the same thing — Strive for peace and be ready for war.

Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:54 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:54 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:55 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Krishna recounted the strength of Arjuna and his certain victory and sought peace.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:55 PM PST
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

If you have power and strength and yet you talk about peace, Dharma is on your side. And then, after striving for the same if you take up arms, even that violence is Dharma.

Time: Mar 12, 2022 08:56 PM PST

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 22, 2022 08:57 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I can not tell this to everyone, but only to a staunch Hindu. How we see our culture will have a direct effect on how others see it. If WE compare them to all and sundry like characters of comic or a contemporary professional fighter, who would see them in their true glory?

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 22, 2022 08:58 PM PDT

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi

Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

My statement wasn’t a reflection to your personality, character or belief. It was on the question.

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 22, 2022 08:59 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I would disagree with your last statement once again as it contradicts Mahabharata. Karna was “extremely evil souled” and it was confirmed by Krishna himself. Ved Vyas, Parshurma, Drona, Bhishma everyone called him evil.

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 12:09 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Kansa and Karna can’t be called good just because they were born earlier. If you do so, they that would again be ‘derogatory’

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 12:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

As it would make Krishna’s action unjustifiable. Why did they kill a good man?

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 12:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

We must be candid in calling an evil and evil.

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 12:10 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Krishna called him “pure evil souled”. Parshurama and Drona called him wicked and deceitful. He commanded Draupadi’s disrobing. And you call it “not evil”?

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 01:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Who was evil then? Krishna, Parshurama and Drona?

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 01:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Can you actually tell me what do you mean by “He suffered a lot?” Can you tell me how did he suffer? and what did he suffer?

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 01:29 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 05:47 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Krishna called Karna “extremely evil soul”

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 05:47 AM PDT

Inbox Message

Content:

Inbox Message

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi

Inbox Message

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 05:48 AM PDT

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi
Time: Mar 23, 2022 05:49 AM PDT

Inbox Message

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi

Inbox Message

Recipient: Udyan Trivedi

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 24, 2022 01:40 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

And Arjuna defeated Bhishma. Shiva was common guru of Parshurama and Arjuna and he declared Arjuna as the greatest warrior in the three worlds.

Is there a room for me to pass the judgement?

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 24, 2022 09:05 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

By that defination, wasn't Parshuram fighting say a 100 generation later?

During Bhishm young age he was unequalled. He maynot have needed more weapons. And you can't be sure he didn't do tapas. Remember he had more weapons than Parshuram.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 24, 2022 01:57 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Perhaps Bhishma didn’t need the weapon. He wanted to prevent the war. His opponents were civilized ethical people. Arjuna knew war is the only way forward. And even Arjuna went for Pashupata more as a deterrent than as a weapon. It was more to instill fear in his opponents.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 24, 2022 09:15 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I wish you learn a bit more about Mahatma and his ways. He didn’t follow non-violence blindly. He condemned it as the first and only solution for every problem. No one in their right mind did.

Krishna became Ranchoda and tried non-violent means to resolve the problem. Pandavas conceded at unfavorable terms again and again. Rama sent Angada as his emissary to Ravana seeking peace. And Mahatma Gandhi advocated enlisting Indians into army.

Why a prophet of non-violence would ask people to join army and learn ways of the war?

  1. When you still with foolish, you must learn the ways of folly. But you shouldn’t become foolish in the process.
  2. No logical violent struggle would have succeeded within the country. It would have led to civil war. In case of Pandavas they weren’t fighting inside the walls of their city. Times were different, People were different.

I would recommend you reading “Kurukshetra” by Ramdhari Singh Dinkar for answer to this question.

Summary Gandhian ways worked in Gandhian era and would be the only way going forward where entire world sits judging the conduct of nations as individuals. Isn’t entire world censuring Russia? Russia may demolish Ukraine, but would destroy itself in the process by isolating itself from international community. Support of Ukraine is big. British couldn’t fight against Gandhian strategy, they would have become what Russia is becoming today.

But Mahabahrata era was different. And there was no one to show the path. For a period like that Gandhiji himself said —

When there is a choice between violence and cowardice, I would recommend violence.

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 24, 2022 09:55 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

As a trait of personality or culture violence must be condemned. It proves you have no better justification for your point. Even Gandhiji said so.

But people misinterpreted Vedas with a narrow mindset. And that is why Krishna had to provide his interpretation in Gita.

Violence for the sake of violence and to prove your point or meet the selfish agenda is bad. Violence in discharge of your duty is not violence but ‘duty’. The word ‘duty’ takes a precedence over ‘violence’

Recipient: Sūrya S A (सूर्य.एस्.ए)
Time: Jun 25, 2022 02:47 AM PDT

Content:

yes

You didn't respond to my WhatsApp

Recipient: Harshit Bhardwaj
Time: Jul 18, 2022 06:42 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Thanks for sharing the message. Honestly neither I know nor care about Pawan Gupta, Mrinal Rai or Khan Sir. They don't matter. Vyas epic matters. If you value your opinion ask them the questions I asked you. How was he not a traitor. I love Ramdhari Singh Dinkar (Rashmirathi) and Tagore. But neither had the access to critical edition. Neither claim they follow authentic epic. They give their views. I am quoting the exact references from the epic.

You are saying Kunti wanted Karna to promise he would kill her sons. That's your view but you can't give any reference to prove it. Because it is not mentioned anywhere. I can give reference she wanted Karna safe and wanted to side with her other sons.That is the difference.

You say had he killed Nakula I would have called him criminal.No. I wouldn't have called him criminal for killing Nakul. I would have called him criminal for his other crimes like being molester or conspirator. I never called Karna a traitor for killing Abhimanyu. He was plain coward. Period.

You say if he had sided with Duryodhana without promising to Kunti I wouldn't have called him traitor. If he had told Duryodhana about his promise he would still be honorable. But he couldn't do anything.

Had he switched sides I would call him a man of conscience. Don't believe me? See I don't call Vibhishan a traitor. He was Dharmatma who kept humanity above personal life. Only then he got lucky to be embraced by Shri Rama.

If you for once keep Vyas writings above Pawan Gupta and Mrinal Rai, you would see the truth —

  • If Karna was loyal why didn't he fight for first 10 days?
  • If Karna was loyal why did he promise Kunti against the welfare of Duryodhana when Kunti didn't ask such a boon?
  • If Karna was loyal why didn't he inform Duryodhana about his promises?

If you don't know the answer, you may ask Pawan Gupta or Mrinal Rai or Khan sir and let me know those views that matters to you.

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Jul 18, 2022 07:18 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Thanks for your views. Since you don’t want to discuss further and it is wasting your time, I would not say anything that you need to respond. Any way to all my questions you so far said — I don’t care or Mr X said this.

You need not reply to me. I am sure, however, you would seek the answer of those questions even if you don’t respond.

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Jul 19, 2022 11:13 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I liked what you said — I care about dharma. Dharma was with krishna and their followers.

Here we both agree. Karna ditched Dharma when he rejected to be on the side of Krishna. He was invited twice, first by Krishna then by Kunti and he rejected both.

We both agree — That wasn’t a good deed. That was a bad deed.

Now I am wondering, where do we differ. This is what I have been saying all along. He was a traitor and Adharmi.

But wait, you don’t need to answer to me. And it is now no more required because you see we both agree.

Regards,

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Jul 19, 2022 11:19 PM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Why do you have such a low opinion about our epics? Why do you think they are mere fables? Just to justify an evil creature? Is he so important that you are willing to ridicule the epic itself and think that we don’t have the right knowledge?

Recipient: Alok Gupta

Inbox Message

Content:

BORI may not be 100% accurate but is more accurate than rest of the lot and lot more accurate than people you quote.

One thing you would find about the followers of Dharma is they don't mistrust this.

Recipient: Alok Gupta

Inbox Message

Recipient: Alok Gupta

Inbox Message

Content:

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Aug 09, 2022 07:26 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

You asked — was Karna trader. Was that not admirable work.

Karna had right to trade. He traded. Nothing wrong with a trade.

As a trader, I admire him. He traded a less valuable artifact with a more valuable one. It was a great exchange.

But then I can’t admire him as a donor or daanveer that he wasn’t.

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Aug 09, 2022 07:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Do you know about Dadhichi? He donated his bones. What did he ask in exchange?

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Aug 09, 2022 07:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

Do you know King Sivi? He donated his flesh to protect a pegion. What did he ask in exchange?

Recipient: Alok Gupta
Time: Aug 09, 2022 07:28 AM PDT
Content language: English

Inbox Message

Content:

I admire Sivi and Dadhichi as donor and Karna as a trader.
